news_summary: The West Australian reports that Ardea Resources is making its Goldfields nickel-cobalt project the firm focus. The news is relevant to nickel via potential future supply, but the provided information does not indicate any immediate change in production timing, output, or constraints that would likely move prices in the next weeks. | Copper prices slipped after the U.S.-China Phase 1 trade deal as investors doubted it would materially lift metals demand. LME three-month copper eased 0.1% and ShFE copper 0.2%. LME inventories hit a 10-month low, but spreads widened, suggesting no urgent supply shortage. Aluminium rose slightly; nickel traded mixed across exchanges. | Minnesota Rep. Betty McCollum introduced H.R. 5598 to permanently withdraw about 234,000 acres upstream of the Boundary Waters Canoe Area from mineral leasing for sulfide-based copper-nickel mining. The bill faces long odds and potential veto, but it would constrain potential output from projects like Twin Metals. | Minnesota Rep. Betty McCollum introduced H.R. 5598 to permanently withdraw about 234,000 acres from federal mineral leasing for sulfide-based minerals in the Rainy River watershed upstream of the BWCAW. The measure targets copper-nickel mining plans such as Twin Metals, though passage and veto prospects are uncertain. | LME registered nickel inventories have more than doubled to about 183,000 tonnes after earlier depletion, while the cash-to-3-months premium has shifted from extreme backwardation to contango. This weakens the nickel bull narrative by easing imminent shortage concerns, supported by strong Indonesia-related ore and ferronickel imports.

Shanghai bonded refined nickel inventories rose by about 400 mt in the week to Jan 17 to 18,100 mt, while nickel briquettes slipped to 3,300 mt. Arrivals for December orders offset domestic deliveries, and several hundred tonnes of Sumitomo nickel entered domestic markets, with average import losses easing versus last week. | An early warning report states that D. Mark Appleby acquired additional shares of Tartisan Nickel Corp., increasing his stake from about 8.9% to 11.1%. The filing is for investment purposes and does not mention production, supply, or other fundamentals that would immediately move nickel prices.

London copper held steady near an eight-month high on firm China industrial output, retail sales and investment data, though volumes were thin before the Lunar New Year holiday. Copper on ShFE eased as trade slowed. LME aluminum and SHFE aluminum fell slightly; nickel declined on SHFE, implying limited net directional pressure. | Indonesia’s Energy and Mineral Resources Ministry regulation bans nickel ore exports starting in January and requires domestic processing before overseas shipments. The government also tightens oversight via digital monitoring systems. Given Indonesia’s role as a major exporter, the policy risks near-term nickel ore supply tightness and price support. | India’s stainless steel industry group ISSDA is asking the government, ahead of the FY2020-21 budget, to set zero import duty on ferro-nickel and stainless steel scrap. It argues ferro-nickel isn’t produced domestically and must be imported; current basic customs duty is 2.5%. | London copper rose to an eight-month high after solid China industrial output and retail sales data lifted demand sentiment, though volumes were light ahead of Lunar New Year. LME aluminium inched up, while LME nickel fell slightly. China’s scrap standards add detail but not an immediate shock. | Blackstone Minerals announced its best drill results to date at the Ta Khoa nickel project in Vietnam, including 60m at 1.3% nickel and additional high-grade intersections. The zone is described as near-surface and large-scale, with copper also reported within the mineralised interval, boosting nickel outlook. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous metals mostly closed lower Monday as liquidity thinned ahead of the Jan 24 Chinese New Year holiday. Copper fell 0.47% with technical softness, though PBOC reverse repos supported sentiment. Aluminium dropped 0.49% on weakened demand, and nickel declined 1.44% and stayed rangebound. | Doubleview Capital reports a new mineralized zone south of the Lisle deposit at its Hat gold-copper porphyry project. Drill results show sulphide mineralization with chalcopyrite/magnetite intercepts and higher intensity with depth; assays for copper/gold/cobalt are pending. It’s an exploration discovery without production implications.

Western Areas has won new short-term contracts to supply high-grade nickel concentrate from its Forrestania operations to BHP’s Nickel West and China’s Jinchuan. The tendering was driven by optimism that nickel prices will keep rising, supported by battery-related demand for nickel. | China’s imports of nickel ore and concentrate fell for a third straight month in December, down 27.9% from November. Imports are set to keep declining in January as Indonesia’s export ban takes effect. Demand disruption followed an earlier Indonesian export halt, while Philippines supply also remains constrained by the rainy season. | Group Ten Metals reported 2019 drill results at its Stillwater West PGE-Ni-Cu project, including 399m of continuous nickel-equivalent mineralization with associated copper values. The company says the results expand known Platreef-style PGE-Ni-Cu-Co mineralization, supporting incremental short-term optimism for nickel and copper supply potential. | Western Areas secured new contracts to sell 10,000 tpa nickel concentrate to BHP’s Nickel West and 10,000 tpa to China’s Jinchuan for two years with an extension option. It ends an existing Tsingshan supply by month-end. The agreements support smelter feedstock continuity for nickel. | Indonesia’s regulation bans nickel ore exports to force downstream processing, while monitoring is tightened via digital systems. However, the Philippines’ BARMM region has indefinitely suspended nickel extraction, potentially removing 100–150 kt of supply. With downstreaming delayed by execution, near-term nickel supply expectations look vulnerable.

London copper stayed near a two-week low as LME-approved inventories jumped 38,700 tonnes and coronavirus fears spread beyond China. The benchmark three-month contract fell as much as 0.5% and was near unchanged by early trade. Aluminium fell slightly while nickel rose on the day, reflecting mixed macro impact. | China’s nickel ore and concentrate imports declined for a third straight month in December, down 27.9% from November, as buyers’ rush to ship before Indonesia’s export ban faded after an October halt. January imports are expected to keep falling due to the ban and ongoing rainy-season disruptions in the Philippines. | London copper hovered near a two-week low as the Wuhan coronavirus outbreak in China raised travel/consumption concerns and LME-approved copper inventories jumped by 38,700 tonnes to 162,925. LME three-month copper fell up to 0.5% and had a larger prior-session drop, reflecting bearish near-term sentiment. | London copper hovered near a two-week low as LME inventories rose by 38,700 tonnes and coronavirus spread from China to other countries. LME three-month copper fell up to 0.5% and was near unchanged later, with overall metals sentiment weaker, while aluminium and nickel also moved amid risk concerns. | Copper fell to a near three-week low as China’s coronavirus death toll rose, renewing concerns about weaker economic growth and base-metals demand. The move was reinforced by rising LME copper inventories and wider signals of plentiful supply (cash discounts). Aluminium and nickel also closed lower amid the same risk sentiment.

Indonesia’s official said 2019 low-grade nickel ore output rose and exports jumped, but ore exports are expected to be cut in early 2020 after a policy change favoring higher-grade feed for smelters. Ferronickel and nickel pig iron production increased sharply while matte fell, keeping nickel supply dynamics in focus. | Reuters reports Indonesia’s 2019 nickel ore output rose to 52.8 million tonnes, more than double 2018. Nickel ore exports also increased to 30.2 million tonnes in 2019. However, the country stopped nickel ore exports at the start of 2020, likely prompting lower output from low-grade miners and tightening supply for nickel processing. | Copper prices fell to a six-week low as LME-approved inventories jumped 20% and Shanghai inventories rose, while concern over a spreading flu-like virus in China raised fears of weaker demand growth. Trading activity also weakened ahead of Lunar New Year. The broader base-metals complex fell, including aluminium and nickel. | Copper prices fell to a six-week low as coronavirus fears in China intensified, with two cities locked down and liquidity disrupted by the Lunar New Year. The drop was reinforced by rising LME warehouse inventories and technical selling. Other industrial metals also declined, including aluminium and nickel, reflecting broad risk-off demand expectations. | Private equity fund GSOL is in talks for four or five potential acquisitions to expand its ferronickel production, a stainless-steel input combining nickel and iron. It already revived and ramped operations in the Dominican Republic and North Macedonia, and targets further capacity and cost improvements. | Copper prices fell to the lowest in six weeks as coronavirus fears in China intensified, driving concerns about weaker economic activity and metal demand. The move was reinforced by speculators cutting long positions, rising LME inventories, and broader selloffs in industrial commodities, with nickel and aluminium also trading lower. | ZAF Energy Systems, with Capitol Power Group, is deploying nickel-zinc (NiZn) batteries in a data center operator in Atlanta, replacing VRLA backup power systems. The announcement highlights NiZn’s performance and economics for UPS/telecom use, implying incremental nickel demand for battery production. Battery scale is unspecified. | Russia’s Nornickel faces a crisis in Nikel as the local nickel smelter is closing and hundreds of jobs will be dismissed. Authorities plan to attract new industries, including a planned tourism investment. The smelter shutdown is the key immediate implication for nickel supply.

Copper jumped to an eight-month high after the U.S. and China agreed an initial trade deal, lifting hopes for stronger metals demand. LME copper prices rose about 0.7% with declines in warehouse inventories. The article also reports aluminium and nickel up modestly, reflecting positive broader sentiment. | Greece plans to liquidate troubled nickel producer Larco and sell its smelting plant and mines in separate chunks. The move follows long-running financial distress and Greece’s obligation to recover illegal state aid. Larco faces large unpaid bills to PPC and fines for environmental violations, raising risks to nickel processing availability. | Copper steadied but was still set for its biggest weekly drop in 19 months as China’s coronavirus controls raised fears of slower growth and weaker metals demand. LME-approved copper stocks rose sharply and China inventories increased. The broader complex also weakened, with aluminium and nickel heading for weekly declines. | Copper prices posted the biggest weekly drop in five years as coronavirus spread raised fears of weaker Chinese growth and reduced metals demand. China’s lockdown measures and holiday disruptions heightened the outlook risk, while LME-approved and China inventories rose sharply. Nickel and aluminium also fell alongside copper.

Palladium One reports anomalously high nickel (up to 238 ppm) and copper (up to 108 ppm) in fall 2019 soil sampling along the Smoke Lake EM anomaly on its Tyko Ni-Cu-PGE property in Ontario. The results confirm the target, but no immediate supply or production implications are provided. | Palladium One reports strongly anomalous nickel (up to 238 ppm) and copper (up to 108 ppm) in soil samples along an untested airborne EM anomaly at its Tyko Ni-Cu-PGE property in Ontario. Results are described as confirming a drill target; drilling is expected later in February 2020. | The release projects rising global copper demand driven by construction, infrastructure and manufacturing, and highlights Los Andes Copper’s $8m NSR royalty financing to advance its Chile Vizcachitas project (PFS and permitting). It also references company production updates forecasting higher 2020 nickel output, supporting sentiment for both metals.

Nickel fell to its lowest level since July on the London Metals Exchange, after previously trading above $8. Indonesia reversed an earlier export ban, while China-US trade war fears and, most recently, coronavirus spread raised concerns about weakening Chinese demand, a key driver for base metals including nickel. | A Reuters poll expects copper prices to rise on improving global growth and less seamless supply return, projecting a 2020 copper deficit. It also expects nickel support from Indonesia’s nickel ore export ban. Conversely, analysts foresee an aluminium surplus in 2020, implying LME prices will decline. | A Reuters poll expects copper to rise in 2020 due to improving global growth and less seamless supply returns, with a projected market deficit. It also expects nickel support from Indonesia’s nickel ore export ban, implying tighter supply. In contrast, aluminium is forecast to swing to surplus, weighing on prices.

London copper rebounded from a four-month low as investors reassessed the coronavirus outbreak’s economic impact in China, where virus containment measures extended holidays and threatened factory output. The report notes factory shutdown fears for copper demand and also shows smaller gains in aluminum and stronger gains in nickel on day-to-day sentiment shifts.

Panoramic Resources has awarded an underground mining contract for the Savannah Nickel Mine, as described in the article title. The update is directly related to nickel mining activity, but it appears operational rather than a clear, immediate supply disruption or demand catalyst. Net impact on near-term nickel prices is likely limited. | Reuters poll shows analysts expect nickel and copper to outperform on supply deficits (Indonesia export ban for nickel; copper shortfalls), but the coronavirus outbreak is weakening China demand expectations and funds are unwinding longs, with nickel and copper already down 12% and 9% since early January. Aluminium views are mixed. | A Reuters poll expects higher nickel and copper prices over the next two years, driven by projected supply deficits (Indonesia’s nickel ore export ban; copper shortfalls). However, the coronavirus outbreak is undermining demand assumptions in China, and the article notes nickel (-12%) and copper (-9%) have already dropped since early January as longs are unwound.

China’s nickel ore imports from Indonesia rose 72% year-on-year in 2019, according to the article. The increase suggests more raw material entering China’s supply chain for nickel processing, which can lift expected supply and create downward pressure on nickel prices over the short term. | London copper, though up slightly on Friday, is set for its biggest monthly fall in over four years as the coronavirus outbreak raises fears of weaker Chinese demand. WHO declared a global health emergency, factory activity softened, and prices have fallen ~10% since headlines began. Aluminium and nickel also moved lower/flat with the risk backdrop.

China significantly increased Indonesian nickel ore purchases in 2019, with imports up about 72% to 23.9 million mt as traders bought ahead of Indonesia’s nickel ore export ban from Jan. 1, 2020. December imports also surged, highlighting short-term supply-tightening expectations for nickel processing. | Indonesia’s proposed omnibus bill includes fiscal and non-fiscal incentives to help miners build downstream industries, but smaller nickel miners worry the incentives may not be scalable. With Indonesia already banning nickel ore exports since January, stalled smelter projects could affect near-term nickel supply expectations. | Shanghai copper sank to a more than three-year low as coronavirus concerns drove investors to sell futures. China’s market closure during Lunar New Year meant prices caught up with London declines. ShFE also showed aluminium and nickel falling, reflecting broad risk-off demand worries. | Shanghai base metals fell as China reopened trading after the Lunar New Year break, amid coronavirus fears that could reduce demand. ShFE copper sank 7% to a more-than-three-year low; aluminium and nickel also declined by about 3–4%. The outbreak and market reopening pushed prices lower despite some support from LME copper. | Shanghai copper fell to the lowest level since late 2016 as investors sold futures over escalating coronavirus worries in China. China’s Lunar New Year closure and virus-related uncertainty drove ShFE copper down the daily 7% limit, while ShFE aluminium and nickel also dropped sharply amid softer activity and disrupted trading. | SMM data show nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports increased to 14.43 million wmt as of Feb 3, up 42,000 wmt from Jan 23, as coronavirus and the CNY holiday slowed shipments. Stocks at seven major ports rose to 11.52 million wmt, indicating improved near-term ore availability. | Vale’s board approved $US120 million for restructuring its New Caledonia nickel plant, which is for sale, with a buyer expected by end of next month. After losses, Vale is moving away from closure fears and prioritising nickel hydroxide cake for EV battery demand, though refinery closure may cut staffing and output.

Glencore reported 2019 output with copper down 6% and cobalt up 10% after boosting production at its Katanga mine and shutting Mutanda early due to falling cobalt prices and rising costs/taxes. The firm also noted slightly lower nickel output and kept 2020 outlook unchanged, driving shares higher.

SMM reports copper extending its rally after China’s central bank pumped large liquidity and trimmed lending rates to stabilize markets amid the coronavirus outbreak. LME base metals rose broadly as stock markets rebounded. Copper, aluminium, and nickel all finished Tuesday higher, with short-term trading ranges suggesting continued positive momentum but some macro/FX limits. | SMM says China’s downstream nickel manufacturing contracted in January for the tenth consecutive month. The nickel-sector PMI fell to 48.7, with lower production and weaker new-order momentum. Stainless steel shutdowns during CNY and coronavirus-related outages weighed on output, while inventories were drawn down after pre-holiday stockpiling. | Copper rebounded to a one-week high after China’s central bank stimulus, prompting investors to unwind bearish positions on coronavirus-hit demand. LME three-month copper rose about 2%. Aluminium and nickel also increased, reflecting broader industrial metals gains as market sentiment stabilised despite ongoing virus-related headwinds. | SMM said SHFE nonferrous prices ended mixed, with copper (+1.09%) and nickel (+1.43%) rising and aluminium climbing modestly (+0.29%). The rally was linked to eased risk aversion after China’s central bank injected 400 billion yuan to support the economy and offset coronavirus disruption. | Copper rose to a one-week high after China’s central bank announced stimulus, prompting investors to unwind bearish positions tied to the coronavirus’s demand impact. Copper gained about 1.9% to $5,722/t. LME aluminium and nickel also climbed, reflecting broad support for industrial metals sentiment.

SMM says copper is on course for a third day of gains, with LME copper hitting a one-week high before settling higher. Nickel rose 1.4% on SHFE and gained strongly on LME. Aluminium also climbed, supported by Tomago smelter production cut reports and China demand offset steps. | Nickel futures edged higher as concerns rose over China NPI output and Philippine nickel-ore supply. NPI plants in Inner Mongolia are operating at low levels or shutting due to shortages and epidemic-related labor constraints. Philippine port measures restrict crews disembarking, tightening ore availability and supporting prices. | North American Nickel announced mobilization for a 2020 drill program (1,085m in three holes) at its Loveland nickel property near Timmins, Ontario. The mineralization targets higher-grade nickel with reported associated copper, using BHEM/VTEMMAX-directed targets. This is exploration activity without immediate supply impact.

SMM says copper’s rally is losing momentum as coronavirus concerns linger, with processors delaying operations and February consumption expected to fall. LME and SHFE copper face moderating gains from seasonally rising inventories. Aluminium is marginally firmer, while nickel edges up slightly despite pullbacks. | SMM data show nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 260,000 wmt from Feb 3 to 7 to 14.17 million wmt, with metal content down 2,200 mt to 120,600 mt. Inventories at seven major ports also declined by 19,000 wmt to 11.33 million wmt, indicating tighter near-term feedstock. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous metals mostly closed lower as renewed coronavirus concerns raised worries about near-term economic impact. Copper fell 0.54% after investors paused a rally amid outbreak escalation ahead of post-holiday travel. Nickel edged down 0.11%. Aluminium rose 0.48% on short-covering and downstream resumption expectations. | Shanghai bonded refined nickel stocks in the bonded area stayed unchanged at about 18,500 mt before the Chinese New Year holiday despite an extended shutdown from coronavirus-related delays. Import arbitrage remained closed with losses, and LME cash vs three-month discounts widened, signaling ample nearby supplies. Resumption of deliveries and warehouse activity is expected next week.

A company proposes an underground copper mine near Minnesota’s Boundary Waters, sparking concerns about water pollution and lawsuits. Supporters argue modern controls could protect the wilderness, but regulatory hurdles remain. The project is years from opening, limiting immediate effects on copper and nickel supply expectations. | Nickel has fallen about 8% since January as coronavirus-driven curbs weaken Chinese manufacturing and stainless steel demand, with rising LME stocks and weaker spreads reinforcing bearish sentiment. The article also notes copper has seen similarly hard hits from coronavirus positioning, keeping downside bias near term. | Poseidon Nickel says Silver Swan ore reserves increased 106% to 130,000 tonnes grading 5.2% nickel (6,800 tonnes contained nickel). The company also reports ongoing exploration at Black Swan and drilling results at Windarra, including 5m at 0.75% nickel and higher-grade intervals, supporting nickel resource prospects. | Purdue President Mitch Daniels highlighted a donation-related 1% net smelter return interest in FPX Nickel’s British Columbia mine. The article frames potential long-term output and returns for Purdue at current nickel prices, but it does not indicate any near-term operational disruption or demand shift.

Rio Tinto said coronavirus containment measures have slowed copper concentrate imports crossing the Mongolia–China border, while BHP also delayed shipments as Chinese plants shut. The disruption and heightened uncertainty about demand and supply chains triggered falls in key commodity prices, including copper, nickel, and aluminum. | Copper rose about 0.6% after China reported its lowest new COVID-19 cases since late January, improving sentiment for demand from the biggest metals consumer. LME aluminum also edged up, while nickel stocks climbed to a multi-year high. Rio Tinto flagged shipment slowdowns and potential aluminum smelter cuts, keeping caution.

Reuters columnist Andy Home says LME nickel is down sharply in early 2020 amid coronavirus concerns about weaker Chinese manufacturing and stainless steel demand. Rising LME nickel stocks and collapsing spreads worsen sentiment and price outlook. The same virus-driven hit to sentiment is said to have affected copper as well, both pressured by crowded long positions. | SMM says China’s nickel downstream sectors recovered in December, with PMIs rising for production and new orders (still below 50 overall) and inventories of raw materials rebounding. Stabilisation in nickel and stainless steel prices encouraged buyers to restock, while January PMI points to lingering weakness despite better order sentiment. | ALX Resources reported an exploration update for its Falcon Nickel Project, including plans for up to 1,000 metres of drilling starting in early March 2020 and geophysical/geochemical work to refine drill targets. Historical trenches showed grades up to 3.17% nickel and 0.40% copper, but no production or supply impact was stated. | FPX Nickel is advancing its Baptiste Nickel Project in British Columbia, aiming for low-cost, low-emission nickel concentrate production. The company highlights prefeasibility work, government funding, and partnerships, including stakeholders supporting the project’s development. The news is nickel-focused but describes ongoing development rather than near-term supply changes. | Vale’s New Caledonia nickel unit has received three bids for Vale Brazil’s 95% stake. A $120m approved restructure includes closing the refinery, job losses, prioritising production of NHC for EV batteries, and exporting two million tonnes of nickel ore annually subject to amendments to New Caledonia’s mining code.

London LME copper fell as the coronavirus death toll in China continued rising, weakening the near-term demand outlook. The article also shows declines in LME aluminium and nickel. It cites Hubei’s rising cases and notes potential disruption fears for LME’s Hong Kong event, reflecting risk to industrial activity. | Anglo American said nickel output met its 2019 full-year target: 11,700 mt in Q4 (up 3% YoY) and 42,600 mt for 2019 (up 1%). 2020 guidance remains 42,000-44,000 mt. Operations include Minas-Rio ramp-up, partially offset by Chile drought and other mine transitions.

Adamas Intelligence data show nickel usage in passenger xEV batteries rose 39% in 2019, with China up 56% despite only modest EV sales growth. The market is shifting toward high-nickel cathodes like NCM 811, and Tesla was a major driver in Europe, implying stronger nickel demand. | Pacific Rim Cobalt said Indonesia has approved the registration of its Cyclops nickel-cobalt project licence, a step toward transferring it to a foreign investment licence under central government administration. The project in Papua has extensive drilling, permits, and positive drilling and scoping test results for nickel-rich material processing.

Indonesia’s Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources issued February benchmark nickel ore prices, higher than prevailing market levels, to raise domestic nickel ore pricing after it banned exports from January. Benchmarks for various nickel grades were set at $28.37–$31.71/wmt FOB, aiming to rebalance profits toward miners. | SMM reports Chinese reopening and falling new COVID-19 cases calmed markets, lifting Shanghai nonferrous closes mostly higher. SHFE copper and nickel rose less than 0.1%, while aluminium gained about 0.3%. Lingering worries about the epidemic’s economic impact capped further gains in base metals. | EMX executed an option agreement with Pursuit Minerals over three Norwegian nickel-copper-cobalt projects, with EMX receiving a 3% NSR royalty plus advance and milestone payments. The assets are described as historic Ni-Cu-Co sulfide systems, but no near-term production or supply changes are indicated.

Copper rose modestly as China’s reported new coronavirus cases fell for a second straight day, easing concerns about demand from the world’s top metals consumer. The piece also notes updated LME/SHFE prices for aluminium and nickel, reflecting broader risk sentiment rather than a specific new supply or policy shock. | BHP reported higher copper output for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2019, and kept 2019–20 guidance unchanged. CEO Henry said coronavirus-related uncertainty is weighing on Chinese demand and could reduce near-term copper demand, though BHP sees underlying copper fundamentals as sound and expects demand growth with caveats. | Copper prices rose as hopes for additional Chinese stimulus lifted risk sentiment and as new coronavirus cases declined. The outlook for China’s economy and metal demand improved; firms in Hubei received temporary social-insurance relief. The article also notes gains in LME aluminum and nickel alongside copper. | Copper prices rose on hopes of additional Chinese stimulus and signs the virus outbreak is stabilizing, improving sentiment for the world’s top metals consumer. The piece cites policy relief for firms in affected areas and possible financial support. It also notes small concurrent moves in LME aluminium and nickel and mentions copper production/inflow fundamentals. | American Manganese begins feed testing for its RecycLiCo demonstration plant, processing lithium-ion battery cathode scrap to optimize leaching and chemical consumption and improve extraction efficiency for lithium, nickel, manganese, and cobalt. The update is focused on staged testing rather than commercial-scale output or immediate supply changes.

SMM says Shanghai base metals closed mostly higher, but gains were capped by a stronger US dollar after FOMC minutes signaled steady US rates. China’s PBOC cut LPR to support firms amid the coronavirus impact, supporting sentiment. Copper edged up, nickel fell slightly, and aluminium dropped ~0.3%. | Pacific Rim Cobalt says it received Indonesian government approvals for its Cyclops nickel and cobalt project after a revised registration process, citing favorable policy support tied to EV battery demand. The editorial link emphasizes Indonesia consolidating nickel production, implying improved supply prospects that can be bullish for nickel prices.

Vale reported a fourth-quarter net loss of $1.56 billion, citing impairment charges linked to nickel and coal mines. Such write-downs typically reflect weaker expected cash flows and can raise market concerns about nickel production economics, potentially weighing on near-term nickel price expectations. | Pursuit Minerals is pursuing additional European nickel sulphide projects in Norway, targeting battery-metal demand tied to rapidly expanding lithium-ion production for EVs. The company frames the move as enabling supply to battery manufacturers, which could reinforce bullish sentiment for nickel due to tighter future availability expectations. | Shanghai bonded refined nickel inventories were flat at about 19,000 mt with no new port arrivals. Import arbitrage remains closed, with losses widening to around 1,500 yuan/mt and warrant premiums near $200/mt. LME nickel cash discounts versus three-month expanded, pointing to sufficient nearby supply. | Shanghai base metals mostly fell as renewed coronavirus concerns in South Korea and other Asian economies revived risk aversion. On SHFE, copper dropped about 0.86% to a one-week low, aluminium fell 0.69% to the lowest close since Feb 4, and nickel slid 1.49% on short-position buildup. | Indonesia’s earlier nickel ore export ban to January 2020 is expected to disrupt Chinese NPI feedstock flows and tighten global refined nickel supply despite existing deficits. The piece also highlights COVID-19-driven logistical and mine/HPAL-related uncertainty outside China, implying additional short-term support for nickel prices. | Garibaldi Resources reported drilling intercepts at its Nickel Mountain project, including 9.83 meters of massive sulphides grading 7.2% nickel and 3.6% copper, plus additional nickel-copper zones. The company says the Lower Discovery Zone expands and thickens north, but no production impacts are mentioned. | West Musgrave is a planned open-pit copper-nickel sulphide project in Western Australia (Nebo-Babel deposits) expected to produce about 41,000t copper and 35,000t nickel annually in the first five years. It is under construction since Nov 2022 and aims to start in Q2 2026, with a processing plant for separate concentrates. | Nickel slid on the LME after earlier gains from six-month lows faded amid COVID-19 and broader market uncertainty. Copper moved sluggishly, only slightly above recent lows. Wood Mackenzie cited likely Chinese shutdowns affecting base metals supply, refining and demand, weighing sentiment across the complex. | The piece promotes a report on aluminum die casting for automotive parts, arguing EPA/CAFE pressure for lighter cars and rising EV production should boost aluminum die-cast demand. It also flags aluminum price volatility from sanctions/trade wars as a hindrance. Overall, impacts are mixed and not immediate for prices.

Indonesia’s Weda Bay nickel project plans to commission two 42,000 kVA RKEFs for nickel pig iron, targeting first output in March at the earliest. Once stabilised, the units are expected to produce around 1,800 mt of NPI per month. Indonesia’s January NPI Ni-content production rose month-on-month. | Garibaldi Resources reported that recent 2019 drill holes at its Nickel Mountain magmatic nickel sulfide system intersected significant near-surface massive sulfides. One hole returned 9.83m grading 7.2% nickel and 3.6% copper, suggesting expanding high-grade mineralization and improving future supply prospects.

Greece’s EU’s last nickel smelter, Larco, is at risk of shutting down after the state stops funding it and sets a deadline for a buyer before bankruptcy. High electricity/emissions costs and unresolved illegal state-aid repayment restrict investment, threatening reduced nickel output in the EU. | Greece’s state-owned Larco, Europe’s only nickel processor, faces closure and may halt production as it sells under financial distress. With large debt and over a thousand jobs at risk, the shutdown threatens nickel supply in Europe, supporting tighter market expectations in the near term. | Pacific Rim Cobalt is featured in coverage of Indonesia’s strategy to consolidate and expand nickel production and chemical processing capacity to feed the growing EV battery market. The piece cites government support and exploration success, implying increased nickel supply expectations that could influence near-term nickel prices. | Copper prices slipped into negative territory as investors feared coronavirus-related demand losses beyond China and potential disruptions to producer supply and labor. Copper treatment/refining charges rose amid virus-hit smelters. Nickel rebounded on Indonesia project-delay expectations, while aluminium was marginally higher as most LME prices stabilized.

Copper prices fell after coronavirus spread worsened outside China, raising fears of a bigger hit to global growth. The article frames copper as a gauge of economic health. It also notes nickel project delays in Indonesia, while LME aluminium rose marginally and nickel gained, reflecting mixed metal responses amid risk-off sentiment. | London copper edged up as investors hoped central-bank stimulus would offset coronavirus damage, though prices were capped amid accelerating spread beyond China. A Reuters poll suggests wider economic slowing. It also says nickel project development in Indonesia could be delayed; aluminium was largely flat on LME and ShFE. | Copper prices fell on coronavirus pandemic fears as the outbreak threatened global economic growth, with China—the biggest copper user—expected to slow. Hopes of stimulus partly limited losses. The same risk-off move pressured LME copper and also pulled down LME aluminium and nickel prices. | SMM reports most SHFE base metals fell amid escalating COVID-19 fears after new case spikes outside China and weak end-user consumption from travel/transport restrictions. On SHFE, nickel dropped about 1.5%, copper fell over 0.5% to two-week lows, and aluminium edged slightly lower. LME copper stocks jumped sharply. | CanAlaska entered a letter of intent with Fjordland Exploration to let it earn up to 80% in the North Thompson sulphide nickel project in Manitoba. The work involves staged exploration expenditures and payments, with CanAlaska operating early stages. The release is exploration-focused, not production-related. | Copper prices slid to 3-week lows as LME warehouse stocks jumped nearly 40% in one day, strengthening fears of weaker demand amid coronavirus spread beyond China. Global equities fell and safe havens rose. The outbreak is also expected to delay Indonesian nickel projects, while aluminium was slightly lower. | Fjordland Exploration and CanAlaska announce a non-binding LOI giving Fjordland an option to acquire up to an 80% interest in the Hunter and Strong properties of the North Thompson Nickel project. The program targets Ni-Cu-PGE sulphide deposits near Vale’s Thompson mine, with drill-ready exploration targets and planned expenditures.

China’s nickel ore and concentrate imports fell 38% in January amid Indonesia’s nickel ore export ban from Jan 1. Imports from Indonesia dropped 44%, with further declines expected in February due to wet-season effects and no Indonesian supply. Ferronickel and NPI imports rose, but ore tightening is still bearish for nickel. | Russian miner Norilsk Nickel says China’s COVID-19 outbreak is a downside risk for both nickel and copper markets. Mobility restrictions and extended holidays disrupt supply-chain operations and may delay mine/smelter staffing and Indonesia NPI ramp-ups. The firm also expects slower demand recovery in Q1, creating short-term uncertainty. | Russian miner Norilsk Nickel says the COVID-19 outbreak in China is a downside risk for early-2020 demand recovery and assumptions for both nickel and copper. It points to disruptions from mobility curbs, extended holidays and closures, slower mine/smelter ramp-ups, and possible delays to Indonesia NPI project staffing and construction.

Markets tumbled as COVID-19 uncertainty increased, raising fears of slower global growth and possible recession.
