Analysts cited tighter ore supply for China’s nickel pig iron producers, supporting nickel bullish moves. | A Reuters poll expects copper, aluminium and nickel to weaken as coronavirus lockdowns reduce industrial activity, including autos and construction. Analysts forecast copper prices grinding lower, with a large surplus. Aluminium surpluses are set to expand further, while nickel faces delayed stainless demand despite supply constraints. | Copper is set for its biggest weekly gain in 14 months as signs of slowing coronavirus spread improve risk appetite and equities. China copper import premiums rise and LME price strength follows. Offsets include higher LME warehouse inventories and a copper concentrate force majeure from Las Bambas, but near-term tone stays bullish. | A Reuters poll says coronavirus lockdowns have frozen industrial activity, hitting copper, aluminium and nickel demand. Analysts expect large surpluses—especially for aluminium—and forecast sharply lower prices, including new lows for copper in Q2. Nickel demand from stainless steel and construction is also weakening.

Panoramic Resources shut the Savannah nickel mine in response to the COVID-19 crisis, citing rising cases in the Kimberley and resulting travel bans and border closures. The shutdown is expected to disrupt near-term nickel output, tightening supply expectations in the next days to weeks. | Blackstone exercised an option to acquire 90% of the Ta Khoa nickel project in Vietnam for AUD$1m, including the mothballed Ban Phuc nickel mine and a nickel concentrator. The article cites strong drill results and plans for a maiden resource and scoping study toward nickel sulphate for lithium batteries, implying longer-term potential.  | ALX Resources reported analytical results from a 2020 winter drilling program at its Falcon nickel project in northern Saskatchewan. Nickel-bearing sulphide mineralization was intersected in two holes, with assays also including copper. Drilling was partially suspended due to COVID-19. The release is exploration-focused, not production-related. | Panoramic Resources has suspended operations at the Savannah nickel-copper-cobalt mine in Western Australia due to Covid-19 disruptions, including transport limits, workforce availability/costs, and equipment/supply accessibility. The company withdrew guidance and paused trading, citing cash preservation and planning for a safe restart. | Blackstone Minerals has exercised an option to acquire 90% of the Ta Khoa Nickel-PGE Project in northern Vietnam from AMR Nickel. The project includes an existing modern nickel mine and 450ktpa processing plant, with scoping studies and metallurgical testing aimed at restarting operations as nickel prices stabilize.

Group Ten Metals reported drill results from its Stillwater West project, highlighting wide intervals of palladium/platinum and also nickel and copper sulphide mineralization. The release supports potential future resource development but provides no immediate changes to production or supply affecting copper or nickel prices in the next days-to-weeks.

SMM reports SHFE base metals opened higher as worries eased amid tentative US steps to restart the economy. Copper rose over 2%, supported by supply disruptions and an accelerated copper inventory draw in China, plus Rio Tinto cutting 2020 copper production targets. Aluminium edged up, while nickel steadied after losses. | SMM data showed nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell further this week, dropping 50,000 wmt to 9.5 million wmt as of April 17, after a 430,000 wmt decline the prior week. Nickel-metal-content stocks fell 600 mt to 78,400 mt, suggesting tightening local availability. | LME copper was set for its best week since Feb 2019 as Trump outlined a staged U.S. reopening plan. Copper rose up to 1.9%, with reports of strong Chinese physical premiums and tight scrap/concentrates. The article also notes LME aluminium and nickel gains alongside improved market sentiment.

Battery-grade nickel sulphate prices in China have rallied, supported by improved precursor orders and low inventories, but SMM expects weaker downstream demand under COVID-19 to curb upside momentum in April. Producers limited cargoes; two plant suspensions may cut output, yet falling overseas orders could depress demand soon. | Shanghai nickel prices jumped more than 4% as Vale slashed its 2020 nickel production forecast due to the coronavirus. The cut intensified concerns about nickel supply shortages after Philippines mining disruptions, while China’s rate cuts boosted sentiment for stainless-steel demand, lifting ShFE and LME nickel to recent highs. | Vale slashed its 2020 nickel output forecast due to COVID-19, along with additional mine shutdowns, pushing LME nickel up about 2.6% to a one-month high. Vale also cut copper output estimates, though copper eased. Aluminium is weighed by a large rise in LME stocks and oversupply expectations, with prices down. | Nickel prices rose sharply after Vale slashed its 2020 nickel output forecast amid coronavirus-related mine disruptions. Other nickel producers also shut or cut production. The article cites ongoing concerns about demand weakness and a projected market surplus, but near-term supply expectations still supported nickel. Vale also trimmed copper forecasts, while copper eased. | Shanghai base metals closed mostly higher after China cut lending rates for the second time this year and stimulus expectations increased. Nickel surged 4.4% on the SHFE and LME. Copper and aluminium also posted gains, with copper supported by ongoing ore-supply concerns and aluminium stabilizing after strong weekly moves. | Fastmarkets’ live futures recap shows strong buying in LME nickel futures, with prices up 3.7%, pointing to short-term bullish pressure for nickel. In contrast, copper is reported to have dipped below $5,200/t, suggesting bearish near-term momentum for copper. Aluminium is discussed but not repriced. | The live futures report shows strong buying in LME nickel futures, lifting prices 3.7%. Copper is reported to have dipped below $5,200/t. It also notes procedural corrections and delayed European low-carbon aluminium differential assessments, alongside commentary on aluminium supply and shipping disruptions, suggesting mixed near-term effects. | Nickel jumped on the LME to a more-than-a-month high after Vale slashed its 2020 nickel production forecast due to COVID-19 impacts. Other nickel mine shutdowns were cited. Despite supply cuts, a poll expects a market surplus. Vale also lowered copper output guidance, while copper fell on the day. | BHP said its iron ore output remains on track, but it cut guidance for copper, nickel and thermal coal, citing coronavirus and other disruptions. With copper and nickel targets revised lower, market expectations for near-term supply weaken, which can pressure both copper and nickel prices over the coming weeks.

SHFE nonferrous metals opened lower as oil prices collapsed, weighing on sentiment and prompting renewed demand and cash-crunch concerns. LME base metals fell overnight; nickel led with a 2.86% drop and copper fell 2.85%, while aluminium declined 1%. Traders expect continued weakness amid crude weakness. | South32’s Cerro Matoso reported a 10% year-on-year increase in nickel output for January–March. The higher production rate implies improved near-term supply availability, which can reduce expectations of nickel tightness and weigh on prices in the coming days to weeks, assuming demand and inventories are unchanged. | Spot nickel trading in Shanghai was brisk but prices fell sharply. SHFE June nickel dropped more than 4% to around 96,560 yuan/mt after a second day of heavy losses, with 1# refined nickel slipping to 97,200-99,300 yuan/mt. Brent oil fell to a record low, dragging nonferrous prices lower. | St George Mining reports early success from a magnetotelluric (MT) geophysical survey at its Mt Alexander project, identifying deeper conductive zones that could host nickel-copper sulphide mineralisation. The company plans additional MT lines and an extensive drilling program once modelling is complete, but no near-term supply changes are stated.

Indonesian regional authorities are tightening controls on Chinese foreign workers during COVID-19, ordering some workers to return and denying others entry. The restrictions include Chinese technicians expected to repair nickel smelters at PT Gunbuster Nickel Industries. Separate cases also involve an aluminium producer denied foreign-worker access.

SMM reports SHFE nickel extending gains, driven partly by Philippines’ decision to extend capital-region lockdowns until May 15, covering Zambales—an important nickel ore mining area. SMM expects 2–3 nickel-ore vessels affected, with other regions resuming May 1, supporting a bullish nickel tone. | SMM data shows nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell sharply, down 302,200 wmt week-on-week to 9.2 million wmt as of April 24. Nickel ore stocks in seven major ports dropped 160,000 wmt to 7.81 million wmt, signaling potential near-term feedstock tightness for nickel production. | Philippines’ Surigao del Sur will resume nickel ore shipments on May 8, while Surigao del Norte (over 70% of exports) is expected to recover mid-May after a COVID-19 suspension. Quarantine extensions may still affect a few Zambales export vessels. Traders expect improved flows ahead. | Shanghai bonded inventories of refined nickel remained unchanged week-on-week, with pure nickel at 17,900 mt and cathodes at 16,200 mt as of Apr 24. The import arbitrage window stayed closed, limiting inbound flows to bonded warehouses, while some arrivals cleared customs without entering bonded storage. | Indonesia will implement a floor price for nickel ore trades from May 14, using monthly mineral benchmark prices with up to a 3% discount. The higher ore-cost base is expected to lift NPI smelting costs for Chinese-backed smelters in Indonesia, pressuring margins and near-term nickel-linked output sentiment. | Copper fell 0.6% on recession worries and negative COVID-19 drug-trial sentiment, with demand pressure expected. The report cites mine shutdown-related supply expectations but says macro fears outweighed them. Nickel eased with on-warrant inventories at a multi-year high, while aluminum remained under pressure amid a ballooning surplus. | Indonesia’s ministry regulation sets a floor for nickel ore transaction prices using the government’s monthly benchmark price (HPM) derived from refined-price averages including LME. The policy aims to protect small miners as Indonesia pushes downstream processing and has already advanced its nickel ore export ban to 2020.

London LME copper rallied up to about 2.3% toward a nearly six-week high as major economies planned to reopen after pandemic lockdowns. The article also shows nickel rising ~2.1% and aluminium up ~0.5% alongside, indicating a broad, demand-sentiment-driven lift in industrial metals. | London copper rebounded toward a six-week high as investors expected reopening and factory restarts to revive industrial metals demand, supported by stimulus news. Aluminium stocks are still rising in warehouses, while China’s stockpiling plans and trade/import data bolster sentiment. Nickel also gained on the day. | COVID-19 lockdowns and low nickel prices are causing mine shutdowns and production cuts across major nickel supply sources, including Vale, Raglan, Ambatovy, and ferronickel producers. Chinese nickel ore and NPI output is falling and inventories are tightening, reducing the risk of large stock builds and supporting nickel prices near term. | Copper rallied to a six-week high as investors bet easing lockdowns, factory restarts, and Japan stimulus would revive industrial demand. European plants and Chinese operations were resuming. LME nickel also rose, while aluminium inventories kept climbing and aluminium was marginally weaker.

Reuters column says lockdowns and low prices are creating a nickel supply shock. It cites COVID-19-related production hits at mines and plants (Vale, Raglan, Ambatovy, Savannah) plus price-related ferronickel cuts totaling ~80,000 tonnes, supporting a recovery in LME nickel. | Nickel Asia and Global Ferronickel plan to gradually resume mining and shipping in Surigao del Norte from May 1 after COVID shutdowns. Easing restrictions should restart Caraga nickel projects, lifting Philippine ore supply to China, which has recently been disrupted and supporting nickel ore prices. | Philippine nickel miners Nickel Asia and Global Ferronickel plan to gradually restart operations and shipments in Surigao del Norte from May 1 after COVID-19 restrictions ease. The prior shutdowns disrupted nickel ore supply and supported higher prices in China. Resumption should lift supply and weigh on nickel. | First Quantum says it has restarted the Ravensthorpe nickel project. A restart generally increases near-term production and supply visibility, easing concerns about reduced output. That tends to lower the immediate nickel price risk premium, leading to bearish pressure over the following days to weeks. | Nickel Asia and Global Ferronickel plan to restart operations in Surigao del Norte from May 1 as COVID restrictions ease after April 30. Previously shuttered mines in Caraga had disrupted nickel supply and supported prices. Reuters reports uncertainty around output and shipments, but resumption should restore supply. | SMM reports volatility in China’s nickel chain. Indonesia’s ore export ban has shifted supply to the Philippines, but April imports were hit after Surigao del Norte mining suspended due to COVID-19. Meanwhile, China’s downstream nickel sector PMI rose above 50 in March as restrictions eased, indicating strengthening demand.

Reuters columnist Andy Home says lockdowns and low prices are producing a “nickel supply shock.” COVID-19 disrupts operations at mines and plants (e.g., Vale guidance cut, mine closures/restarts), while low prices force ferronickel production cuts in several countries. Estimates cite large tonnes of market-driven cuts, supporting higher nickel prices. | SMM reports mixed nonferrous performance after Tuesday’s decline tied to falling oil prices. Copper rebounded, aluminium gained more than 1%, and nickel rose modestly (~0.5%). The note attributes gains mainly to macro optimism around economy reopenings and general market mood, plus some supply-related comments. | Indonesia rejected miners’ request to relax the nickel ore export ban. The coordinating ministry said processed nickel exports have grown and that allowing ore exports would likely cause world nickel prices to fall, especially amid weaker iron and steel demand. The ban began in January to expand domestic smelting. | Vale Indonesia reported a sharp Q1 profit decline alongside a production drop and cites Indonesia’s nickel ore export ban plus weaker downstream demand from COVID-19. It notes slumping nickel demand lowered nickel prices in the first quarter and the trend may continue into Q2, signaling near-term bearish nickel pressure.

Copper prices rose after China reported expansion in manufacturing activity for a second straight month in April, improving demand hopes. LME three-month copper increased and ShFE June copper also gained. The report notes aluminum edging up and nickel rising, indicating broader risk-on sentiment across industrial metals. | Glencore said Q1 2020 own-sourced copper production fell 9% to 293,300 tonnes, with no production reported from Mutanda and Mopani. Nickel output rose 4% to 28,200 tonnes, reflecting disruptions and maintenance effects. Capex guidance was also lowered versus prior plans. | SMM data shows Chinese nickel ore inventories fell sharply, dropping 347,000 wmt from Apr 24 to 8.85 million wmt as of Apr 30, after a 302,200 wmt decline the prior week. Stocks in metal content and across major ports also decreased, suggesting tighter near-term supply. | Rockcliff Metals announced a new high-grade Nickel-PGE drill intersection at its Tower Property (up to 4.46% Ni over 1.25m; 3.82–6.79% NiEq). It is near the company’s copper-rich Tower Deposit. The release is exploratory and provides no production impact, making price moves unlikely. | Glencore reduced 2020 capex and cut production guidance amid COVID-19 operational disruptions and cost pressures. It reported weaker first-quarter copper output and lowered full-year copper guidance, and also trimmed expectations for nickel. The spending/output cuts imply near-term supply pressure and bearish sentiment for copper and nickel markets.

Tsinghan and Eramet’s joint venture at Weda Bay in Indonesia has started production. The ramp-up of new Indonesian nickel supply is likely to lift market expectations for availability, increasing downside pressure on nickel prices over the near term as traders factor in additional output. | Tsinghan and Eramet’s Weda Bay nickel joint venture in Indonesia has started production, according to the announcement. The start-up of additional nickel output can expand near-term supply expectations and likely pressure nickel prices over the coming days to several weeks, barring offsetting demand or disruptions.

Roskill says Indonesia will advance its nickel ore/concentrate export ban to January 2020, reducing feedstock availability for Chinese NPI producers and tightening global nickel supply in a market already in deficit. It also highlights COVID-related logistics and mine/refinery shutdown uncertainty that could further reduce supply near term. | Roskill says Indonesia’s earlier nickel ore export ban (to January 2020) curtailed major NPI feedstock flows to China, forcing producers to draw down port stockpiles and increasing reliance on alternative sources. With COVID-19 causing further supply/logistics uncertainty and possible outside-China care-and-maintenance, nickel supply tightness risk rises. | London copper fell to a near two-week low, down about 1% on Monday, as some producers signaled they may resume operations while the coronavirus clouded global demand. Although production cuts previously supported prices, Peru eased mining restrictions in May. LME aluminium and nickel also declined. | Copper prices fell to a two-week low as coronavirus-driven demand worries resurfaced, particularly around China’s ability to sustain base-metals consumption. The piece cites weak PMI data, potential oversupply signals, and Peru easing mining restrictions. Aluminium and nickel also declined on the same risk-off demand concerns.

Fjordland Exploration entered an option agreement to earn up to 80% in CanAlaska’s North Thompson nickel project in Manitoba, with plans for staged exploration and possible joint venture participation. The belt is described as large-scale and drill-ready, near existing Vale nickel facilities. | Canada Nickel closed a $4.4 million private placement, including flow-through financing, to fund advancement of its Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide discovery and working capital. The release describes use of proceeds for exploration expenditures in Ontario. It does not announce production or supply changes, implying limited near-term impact on nickel pricing.

Rare Metal Mining says it discovered large gold and copper ore bodies near Mongolia’s Oyu Tolgoi region and signed a joint venture to develop the land. Drilling/extraction are expected mainly from 2021 onward, so the copper-supply upside is gradual. Nickel is mentioned only generally as a potential resource for Mongolia.

SMM notes base metals strengthened after the post–Chinese New Year return to trading, with nickel adding over 2% and copper also rebounding, while aluminium is mixed but supported by falling domestic inventories. LME prices were broadly higher overnight; nickel and copper led gains, and aluminium outlook hinges on demand recovery concerns.

London copper rose to an eight-week high on expectations that easing lockdowns will improve demand for metals. The article cites economic reopening in Australia, France, Spain and limited UK easing, with three-month LME copper up 1.8%. LME aluminum and nickel were also higher on the same risk-on demand outlook. | London copper rose to an 8-week high as countries began easing COVID restrictions, lifting hopes for metal demand. LME three-month copper climbed up to 1.8% and was still higher at 1.2%. LME aluminum also rose and nickel in Shanghai edged higher amid improving economic signals. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. announced it acquired additional nickel-copper claims (Glatz and Night Danger) in Ontario. Historical sampling and drilling report nickel and copper grades, including intervals with elevated Ni and Cu. The company positions this as complementing its Kenbridge nickel project development plans. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. acquired 100% interest in additional nickel-copper claims near its Kenbridge project, describing historical assay results for nickel and associated copper. The release frames the acquisition as supportive of developing an operating mine, but provides no immediate production or supply disruption. | SMM reports SHFE base metals closed mixed but copper and aluminium finished higher (copper +0.39%, aluminium +1.05%), supported by reopening-driven demand prospects and supportive technical/inventory signals. Nickel edged down slightly (-0.15%) yet remains supported by bullish indicators and a stainless-steel futures rally, limiting downside.

SMM reports mixed SHFE/LME base metals after China’s April inflation data missed expectations: CPI rose 3.3% and PPI fell 3.1% y/y. Nickel jumped over 1% on SHFE and LME, copper fell on SHFE, and aluminium gained modestly, keeping overall price impact mixed. | The West Australian reports that an ex-contractor at the Ravensthorpe nickel mine was fined for endangering workers’ lives. The case highlights safety and compliance issues at the site, which can increase the likelihood of operational disruptions or added oversight, potentially weighing on near-term nickel production expectations. | A contractor at First Quantum Minerals’ Ravensthorpe nickel mine in Western Australia was fined $11,000 after a telehandler tipped while lifting a 3+ tonne pipe assembly, exposing workers to serious risk. The court found inadequate training and supervision. FQM faces related charges, but no immediate output impact is stated.

Copper prices fell as markets worried that a second wave of COVID-19 after reopenings could further pressure the global economy. The IMF signaled potential additional growth forecast cuts. LME copper fell 0.8%; aluminium rose slightly but nickel declined 0.8%, with broad risk-off sentiment. | Copper prices extended losses as traders feared a second wave of coronavirus infections would delay global economic recovery. Three-month LME copper fell 0.6% and ShFE copper slipped 0.5%. The piece cites risk-off market moves and also shows smaller simultaneous declines in aluminum and nickel. | Group Ten Metals reported 2019 soil geochemistry results from its Stillwater West PGE-Ni-Cu project in Montana, identifying four new kilometer-scale anomalies with elevated nickel and copper, alongside PGE and gold. The update is exploratory and does not indicate imminent production or material supply changes, limiting near-term price impact. | Copper eased on fears of a second COVID-19 wave in China and South Korea, prompting fresh restrictions and expected lower demand for base metals. U.S. warnings about lockdown rollbacks reinforced demand concerns. The selloff spilled into other industrial metals, with aluminium and nickel also falling on the day.

Blackstone Minerals announced further broad disseminated nickel sulfide mineralisation at the King Cobra discovery within its Ta Khoa Nickel-PGE project in Vietnam, including multiple long intercepts of ~0.8–1.1% Ni and some higher-grade shallow intervals. PGE assays are pending and a maiden resource is due in Q3 2020, supporting potential restart. | LME and ShFE copper edged higher as traders pointed to steady demand from China and factory restarts, with arbitrage helping prices. However, the outlook remains clouded by the Fed’s warnings of prolonged weak growth and pandemic-driven demand destruction. The piece also references moves in aluminium and nickel. | Copper prices fell on LME and ShFE as fears of prolonged weak U.S. growth—stemming from the Fed’s comments on the coronavirus fallout—raised concerns over metal demand. The piece also cites pandemic-driven demand destruction and reports concurrent moves in nickel and aluminium prices, pointing to broadly bearish near-term sentiment. | Blackstone Minerals announced strong drilling results at its King Cobra nickel-PGM project in Vietnam, including ~38m at 1.12% nickel from near-surface and additional thick, higher-grade intervals. The deposit remains open and the company is progressing scoping and resource work, supporting incremental bullish sentiment for nickel. | Copper prices extended declines for a fourth session as the Fed warned of prolonged weak U.S. growth amid coronavirus fallout. Concerns about a possible second COVID wave in China and renewed U.S.-China frictions weighed on demand sentiment. LME copper inventories jumped 25% and market structure stayed in deep contango.

The LIVE FUTURES REPORT highlights LME market moves, noting that nickel is sliding by about 1.4%. While lead is also mentioned, the only clearly quantified price move tied to a tracked industrial metal is for nickel, pointing to bearish near-term momentum. | China’s NBS data show primary aluminium production of 2.97 million mt in April and 11.83 million mt in Jan–Apr, up 2.4% year-on-year. Production of 10 nonferrous metals (including copper and nickel) also rose to 4.93 million mt in April, suggesting supply is increasing, a bearish signal for prices. | SMM data shows nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell again in the week to May 15, down 430,000 wmt to 8.24 million wmt, after a 179,000 wmt drop the prior week. In metal terms, stocks fell to 67,600 mt, indicating tighter near-term ore supply. | Shanghai bonded refined nickel inventories fell by about 500 mt to 16,900 mt, driven by a temporary import arbitrage window that pulled nickel cathode product into China’s domestic market. Cathode stocks dropped to 15,200 mt, and no forward refined nickel arrivals were seen, alongside weaker SHFE/LME pricing.

SMM says nonferrous metals rose on Monday morning as oil prices jumped and the US dollar index slipped. The LME complex reversed a red week, with nickel rebounding and copper and aluminium also higher. SHFE positioning was mixed, but copper and aluminium showed recovery signals despite recent weakness. | Copper prices rose 1.3% on the LME as global equities improved with economies reopening after COVID lockdowns. However, China’s copper demand is expected to slow slightly in May. The article also notes increases in LME aluminum and nickel, signaling broader industrial metal support.

London copper rose to a two-month high on optimism over very early COVID-19 vaccine data, supporting expectations of a faster economic recovery. China stimulus hopes also added support. U.S.-China tensions and yuan/equities pullbacks capped gains, but LME and Shanghai aluminum and nickel also edged higher. | The live futures report says 26.5kt inflows are capping gains in LME aluminium, implying reduced upside pressure. It also states nickel is up 1.6%, indicating positive near-term momentum. The piece therefore signals offsetting short-term price pressure across aluminium and nickel. | The live futures report notes that LME aluminium gains are limited by a 26.5kt inflow, implying near-term supply/flow pressure weighing on upside. Nickel, by contrast, rises 1.6%, signaling stronger demand/positioning support for nickel prices over the same short horizon. | Canada Nickel Company reported multiple palladium-platinum zone discoveries and extended a PGM zone by 1.5 km, plus a second nickel discovery that doubles total nickel mineralization along strike to 3.4 km at its Crawford project. The updates are likely supportive for nickel sentiment, but not an immediate supply shift.

Coronavirus lockdowns and border closures disrupting transport have raised sulphur and granulated sulphur prices and, by extension, sulphuric-acid costs for DRC miners. With sulphuric acid used to extract copper and cobalt, delayed reagent shipments may force mines into more expensive, potentially constrained operations, weighing on copper supply expectations.

China’s nickel ore and concentrate imports fell 70.2% YoY in April as COVID-19 led to suspensions of Philippine mining operations and shipments. Imports totaled 1.36 million mt. Supplies from the Philippines, the main source, dropped sharply, with lower ore grades. Exports are expected to normalize in June. | SMM notes volatile but generally firmer base-metals trading ahead of China’s “two sessions,” with oil prices rising and a slightly stronger dollar. LME copper and aluminium gained about 1.7%-1.8% on Wednesday; SHFE copper and aluminium rose too. SHFE nickel jumped more than 2%-3% before easing.

SMM reports broad declines in base metals amid US-China tensions, China’s “two sessions” and weaker economic data. SHFE nickel hit limit down, while SHFE copper slipped and SHFE aluminium rose slightly. LME copper fell and LME aluminium gained modestly. Moves largely reflect macro sentiment and positioning, not fresh fundamentals. | The article argues nickel producers face tougher conditions as EV demand grows but near-term nickel supply is disrupted. COVID-19 delays Indonesia operations and worker access, while expectations of dwindling Chinese nickel ore stocks may pressure NPI producers. It also highlights that EV batteries increasingly need Class 1 nickel for nickel sulfate. | Copper prices fell 1.9% on the LME and 2.1% on the Shanghai Futures Exchange after China omitted an annual GDP target and U.S.-Sino tensions rose. Nickel dropped more than 4–5% and aluminium slipped slightly, reflecting risk-off sentiment, though contracts were still positioned for a weekly gain. | Copper prices slid about 2% as investors feared renewed U.S.-China tensions, possible sanctions, and renewed protests weighing on global growth sentiment. China’s lack of an annual growth target and demand concerns pressured copper. Aluminium and nickel also fell, with LME and SHFE declines reflecting broader selling and inventory signals. | Shanghai bonded refined nickel inventories fell 1,500 mt to 15,400 mt by May 22 as nickel cathode flowed out to the domestic market. A temporary import window and smaller import losses reduced replenishment, and Russian nickel arrived directly into China rather than entering bonded warehouses. Bonded stocks declined 2,000 mt month-to-date. | Canada Nickel announced it has closed property transactions with Noble Mineral Exploration, issuing 500,000 shares under a four-month hold. Both companies obtained the required disinterested shareholder approvals. The update is related to advancing nickel-cobalt projects tied to EV and stainless-steel demand, but offers no immediate supply impact.

Australian bidders are reportedly contesting Vale’s nickel plant in New Caledonia. Vale said it would close a deal by end-June and has faced losses, while restructuring includes closing the refinery, prioritizing nickel hydroxide cake for EV batteries, and exporting 2 million tonnes of nickel ore annually. Jobs are expected to be cut. | New Century Resources is in exclusive talks with Vale to buy Vale’s troubled Goro nickel-cobalt mine in New Caledonia. The proposed deal could include Vale funding and possible French government support, addressing delays and costs. Continued operation supports nickel supply expectations tied to EV battery demand. | Vale, in New Caledonia, has struck a tentative deal to sell its nickel assets to Australia’s New Century Resources, with financing due within two months. The plan includes restructuring, refinery closure, prioritising nickel hydroxide cake for EV batteries, and exporting two million tonnes of nickel ore annually. | New Century Resources agreed to buy Vale’s stake/assets in New Caledonia’s Goro nickel plant after Vale put the 95% holding up for sale due to large losses. The plan includes restructuring, closing the refinery with job losses, prioritizing nickel hydroxide cake, and exporting up to two million tonnes of nickel ore annually. | SMM reported SHFE nonferrous prices were mostly higher on May 26. Copper recovered 0.94% and aluminium gained 0.9%, with copper bouncing above the five-day moving average and briefly testing session highs. Nickel edged slightly down (-0.01%). Gains were capped as US-China tensions kept investors cautious.

Copper trades in a tight range as hopes for reopening are offset by renewed fears that U.S.-China friction could weaken global activity. Reports of possible U.S. action over Hong Kong heighten uncertainty. LME aluminium edges up, while nickel falls, indicating broadly mixed risk sentiment. | Canada Nickel said it is extending the Main Zone by 1.5 km and the East Zone by 400 m at its Crawford nickel-cobalt-palladium project. The update is directly tied to nickel resource definition, but the news lacks details on near-term production or supply changes, limiting expected price impact.

Fastmarkets issued a pricing notice saying it corrected and updated several base metal assessment entries due to procedural errors, including European low-carbon aluminium differentials and aluminium 6063 extrusion billet premium midpoint assessments. It also references nickel arbitrage methodology versus SHFE and LME, but no market-moving fundamentals were provided. | New Caledonia faces a proposed law change that would let SLN and Vale export two million tonnes of nickel ore for local processing. Critics argue the plan undervalues ore and accompanies refinery closure. Prior approvals for large exports suggest increased nickel ore availability, weighing on near-term nickel prices. | New Caledonia’s Palika party criticized a proposed law change that would let SLN and Vale export two million tonnes of nickel ore earmarked for local plants. They argue it sells ore at a fraction of value and cite refinery closures and past financing issues. Goro is suggested as an alternative processor. | Pan Global Resources announced additional drill results at Spain’s Escacena project, extending a shallow copper mineralized zone and confirming multiple copper horizons associated with EM conductors. Intersections include notable copper grades, but it is an exploration campaign with no production timing, offering limited short-term impact on metal prices. | SMM says SHFE base metals ended mixed amid reopening optimism and US-China friction. Copper fell 0.34% after Peru’s Antamina resumed operations, raising oversupply worries, though China inventories supported prices. Aluminium rose 1.47% for a fifth day as Chinese primary ingot and billet inventories fell. Nickel added 0.13%.

Indonesia listed several priority projects for 2020–2024, including major nickel processing projects in Morowali, Konawe and Weda Bay. The government also plans rail and petrochemical upgrades, but the explicit inclusion of new nickel processing capacity is supportive for near-term expectations around nickel supply and sentiment. | SMM data shows nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell for the second consecutive week, dropping 300,000 wmt to 7.85 million wmt as of May 29. Stocks in seven major ports declined 230,000 wmt to 6.77 million wmt, suggesting tighter near-term supply for nickel production. | Blackstone Minerals has started drilling massive sulfide targets at its Ta Khoa Nickel-PGE project in northern Vietnam, expanding testing of district-scale prospects generated by in-house geophysics. Historic Ban Phuc results included high-grade nickel and associated copper, and drilling is expected to progress over coming months, supporting nickel supply expectations. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous metals mostly rose on May 29. Copper gained 0.73% supported by decreasing domestic inventories; aluminium rose 0.84% amid tighter spot availability. Nickel edged down 0.13%. Broader support came from improved economic sentiment, though US-China tensions capped gains.

SMM reports China’s downstream nickel manufacturing PMI expanded for a third straight month in May, and raw-material inventory/purchasing sub-index rose on active nickel feedstock buys by stainless steel mills. NPI demand improved as fundamentals tightened, while some weakness persisted in battery/electroplating orders, suggesting net supportive near-term nickel demand.

Copper rose to an 11-week high as signs of China’s economic recovery strengthened, including higher May vehicle sales and no new COVID cases in Wuhan. Analysts cited strong physical demand and tight scrap supplies prompting short covering. Per reports, LME aluminum and nickel also edged higher.

The piece cites Morgan Stanley views that copper prices should remain resilient despite China-linked downside risk, forecasting strengthening later in 2020. For nickel, it highlights Indonesia’s ore export ban and pandemic mine closures tightening supply, but also weak end-demand, leading to a more mixed outlook. | Court finds Queensland Nickel traded insolvently in the days before administrators were called in, and voided certain intra-group transactions involving China First and Waratah Coal. The ruling is about recovering funds from directors/liquidators after the 2016 refinery collapse, not current nickel production. | Blackstone Minerals reported successful maiden drilling intersections of massive sulphide nickel at its Ban Chang prospect in Vietnam’s Ta Khoa nickel-PGE district, using EM plates to target mineralisation. The project also plans scoping work for downstream nickel sulphate and has prior copper and nickel grades, but no immediate market-moving supply effects are stated. | Blackstone Minerals says drilling at its Ban Chang prospect in Vietnam intersected new massive sulphide nickel veins near its EM targets, while a second rig tests additional EM targets at Ta Khoa. The firm is also progressing a scoping study and assessing a restart of the mothballed Ban Phuc concentrator. | Blackstone Minerals says it intersected massive nickel sulfide in two maiden drill holes at Vietnam’s Ban Chang prospect within the Ta Khoa Nickel-PGE project, with drilling continuing using EM plates. The company targets nickel-rich massive sulfide orebodies and downstream nickel sulfate production, but results are early and not immediate supply material.

Nornickel says a diesel spill on the Ambarnaya River in Norilsk can be contained and removed in about 14 days with favorable winds. The spill followed a fuel-tank incident and fire at CHPP-3, with about 21,000 tonnes released. Authorities are investigating under pollution charges, while large response teams work onsite.

Uncertainty over Vale’s sale of its New Caledonia Goro nickel plant is causing roadblocks and demands for talks from subcontractors and SAS Goro Mines. Vale agreed a deal to sell 95% to New Century Resources, while export of two million tonnes of nickel ore per year depends on changing the mining code amid opposition. | Norilsk Nickel said it will host a conference call for investors about a fuel leak at Heat and Power Plant-3 in Norilsk. While primarily a company/regulatory update, the incident at key infrastructure could disrupt power supply and output, potentially affecting near-term nickel and copper supply expectations. | SMM says Shanghai base metals mostly closed higher, with copper up 0.96% and nickel up 0.98%, while aluminium edged up 0.08%. The rebound follows afternoon market strength, improved LME performance, and mixed domestic inventory signals (copper declines narrowing, aluminium inventory downtrend). | The Kremlin dismissed calls to oust Norilsk Nickel President Potanin after an Arctic fuel spill. Shares in Nornickel have fallen amid Putin’s criticism of the state response. While no nationalization is confirmed, the episode highlights political risk to nickel operations, and could weigh on aluminium via Rusal’s dividend links. | Vladimir Putin said Norilsk Nickel will cover all costs of compensating damages from a petroleum products spill in Norilsk. Approximate emergency relief activities could total about 10 bln rubles. The president urged the CEO to address such environmental issues to avoid future costs. | Access to Vale’s nickel plant in New Caledonia has been restricted by subcontractors worried about the plant’s sale. Local officials are trying to find a solution while negotiations proceed. The deal includes possible export of two million tonnes of nickel ore annually, pending changes to the mining code opposed by pro-independence parties. | Vladimir Putin criticized Norilsk Nickel over a large Arctic fuel spill near Norilsk where 21,000 tonnes of diesel entered rivers and subsoil. He ordered changes to Russian law to prevent similar accidents. The incident elevates near-term operational and regulatory risk for Nornickel, potentially pressuring nickel supply expectations.

China’s nickel sulphate production fell 3.9% in May (9,400 mt Ni content), driven by weak demand, existing inventories, and a fierce price war that pushed battery-grade material prices toward or below costs. Producers halted, curtailed output, or switched salts after Covid-related order reductions.

Wood Mackenzie expects nickel sulfate production to peak later (2027) as EV battery demand accelerates, while surplus is anticipated this year and possibly through 2025. New Indonesian HPAL capacity will add sulfate supply, but future growth needs additional sulfate from 2028 onward, supporting nickel demand expectations. | Canada Nickel announced it has started a Preliminary Economic Assessment for its Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project and appointed a project director and engineering consultant. While still study-stage, the update can modestly support expectations for future nickel supply development. No immediate aluminium impact is indicated. | Canada Nickel said it has begun a preliminary economic assessment for the Crawford Nickel-Cobalt Sulphide Project near Timmins, with Ausenco as consultant. The work is expected to finish by year-end. The project is associated with major reported nickel resources and aims to advance toward mine development. | Norilsk Nickel said it plans to complete gathering contaminated soil from a May 29 diesel fuel tank spill at Norilsk’s CHPP-3 by August, while treating remaining fuel. The incident involved about 21,000 tonnes of spill and environmental damage. Cleanup progress may affect nickel supply operations.

Shanghai copper surged to a 20-week high on strong China demand as lockdowns eased, supported by low on-warrant LME stocks and reduced ShFE inventories. LME copper also rose. The piece cites strong ShFE aluminum prices but falling nickel, reflecting mixed cross-metal reactions. | Canada Nickel reported drill results from the East Zone at its Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project, extending palladium-platinum mineralization and delivering multiple nickel intersections. Highlights include nickel grades up to ~0.42% over 12 m and 256 m grading 0.23% nickel, reinforcing project potential.

Blackstone Minerals announced it intersected a 9.15m wide zone of massive/semi-massive nickel sulfide vein mineralisation at its Ta Khoa project in Vietnam. Management says Ban Chang could become a supplementary high-grade feed source for the Ban Phuc orebody. The news is generally supportive for near-term nickel sentiment. | Centaurus Metals reported “exceptional” shallow, thick nickel sulphide intercepts at Brazil’s Jaguar Central and Jaguar North deposits, with multiple long intervals around 1.2–1.35% Ni and higher-grade sections. A maiden JORC resource is due later this month, with ongoing drilling to extend mineralisation down dip. | Blackstone says it has extended massive nickel sulphide mineralisation at its Ban Chang prospect within the Ta Khoa nickel-PGE project in Vietnam, threading new drill holes and intersecting thick sulphide intervals, with assays expected soon. The company frames results as supporting future mineability and possible supplementary feed into operations. | The piece describes nickel’s role in stainless steel and EV batteries, highlights Indonesia’s growing dominance in nickel supply after the 2020 ore export ban, and profiles Silkroad Nickel’s mine and planned NPI/ferronickel smelting projects. It supports a supply-growth narrative but provides limited immediate catalysts for prices.

London copper was flat at $5,764.50/t and was on track for a fourth straight weekly gain, driven by hopes that central-bank stimulus could lift demand despite a gloomy global outlook. It notes a lack of Fed purchases, some USD support, and shows aluminum and nickel falling in separate moves. | SMM reports Shanghai bonded refined nickel inventories at 14,800 mt, unchanged week-on-week. However, muted trading follows worsening import losses as SHFE nickel underperforms LME nickel, with the SHFE/LME ratio near 8.0 and spreads averaging 3,350 yuan/mt. Russian nickel plates are still arriving into domestic warehouses. | SMM data show Chinese nickel ore inventories rose after prior declines: total port stocks rebounded 210,000 wmt to 8.04 million wmt as of June 12, with stocks in seven major ports up 200,000 wmt to 6.93 million wmt. Metal-content stocks rose to 65,900 mt. | Citi warns nickel faces unfavorable end-use demand as hospitality closures increase stainless steel scrap and weaken broader stainless end-use. With demand down more than supply, inventories/overhang could grow and the market may see a first surplus since 2015. Short-term stainless weakness is expected to weigh on nickel.

Vale is lobbying New Caledonia to change the mining code so low-grade nickel ore can be exported rather than processed locally, as Vale restructures and sells assets. Opposition from pro-independence politicians could limit export volumes, while refinery shutdown raises concern over local nickel supply. EV battery demand boosts sentiment. | Copper prices fell 1.9% as renewed coronavirus worries raised uncertainty about how quickly demand will recover. China’s manufacturing recovery looked fragile despite increased production. Chile miner unions also sought updated continuity plans amid rising worker cases. Aluminium and nickel also dropped on weaker sentiment and global demand concerns.

Copper rose 1.1% on the LME as investors welcomed the Fed’s widened bond-buying program, boosting risk-on sentiment. LME/Shanghai aluminium and nickel also increased alongside copper. However, China spot copper remained at a widening discount to futures, hinting weaker physical demand. | SMM says SHFE nonferrous prices mostly rose after overnight recovery and supportive macro sentiment from the Fed. LME copper gained on optimism around OPEC+ cuts and crude demand, while Chile extended a coronavirus catastrophe, raising copper-ore supply concerns. Aluminium and nickel also rebounded, with aluminium supported by positioning and fundamentals. | Copper prices rose after the Federal Reserve widened its corporate bond buying, supporting U.S. risk sentiment and coinciding with a weaker dollar. The LME and Shanghai contracts both gained. In Chile, labor union concerns add a potential supply disruption tailwind, while aluminium also firmed on tariff-quota expectations.

New Caledonia’s government faces a stalemate over proposed changes to permit nickel ore exports to Asia. Two smelter-running companies want the sales to offset losses from low nickel prices, but pro-independence parties oppose the law change, fearing waste of local resources and loss of onshore processing. | Blackstone Minerals’ maiden drilling at the Ban Chang West prospect in Vietnam returned a high-grade massive sulphide zone, including 0.66% nickel over 5m and an interval up to 2.98% nickel plus 1.22% copper. Management says results could add high-grade feed near its Ban Phuc facility, boosting nickel and copper sentiment.

London copper prices fell as rising COVID-19 cases in Beijing renewed lockdown fears that could hit demand. China also signaled monetary easing support, but risk sentiment turned cautious. The article notes concurrent easing in LME nickel and weakness in ShFE aluminium, indicating broad metal pressure. | Vale plans to restart its nickel mine in Canada after a COVID-19 related pause. The move is likely to lift near-term production/supply expectations and increase market availability, which usually weighs on nickel prices over the coming days and weeks. | The article headline says Vale plans to restart its nickel mine in Canada after a Covid-19-related pause. A restart typically increases expected near-term nickel output and supply availability, which can pressure nickel prices in the days to a few weeks following the news.

Nornickel said it has signed a definitive agreement to sell its Honeymoon Well Nickel Project in Western Australia to BHP’s Nickel West unit. The deal completes Nornickel’s exit from Australian operations and remains subject to regulatory approvals and customary conditions. The project is a greenfield nickel development. | Shanghai bonded refined nickel inventories rose by 800 mt to 15,600 mt, driven entirely by nickel cathode. The build was linked to wider US-dollar import losses of refined nickel (up to 3,500 yuan/mt), encouraging cargo holders to reroute seaborne arrivals into bonded warehouses. The SHFE/LME nickel ratio held near 8.0. | SMM data shows nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports increased for a second straight week. Total stocks rose by 50,000 wmt to 8.09 million wmt as of Jun 19, following a prior-week build. Stocks in metal content also climbed, adding 200 mt to 65,800 mt. | Norilsk Nickel said its Australian unit (MPI Nickel) agreed to sell its Honeymoon Well nickel project to BHP Billiton for an undisclosed price, marking its exit from Australian operations. The deal is subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions, affecting expectations for nickel supply and asset ownership. | BHP agreed to buy Norilsk Nickel’s Australian tenements via MPI Nickel, acquiring the Honeymoon Well nickel project in Western Australia, including the Wedgetail sulphide deposit and interests in exploration JVs. The deal takes Norilsk’s exit from Australia and expands BHP Nickel West’s resource base, supporting nickel sentiment. | BHP agreed to acquire nickel tenements in Western Australia, including the Honeymoon Well project and interests in exploration joint ventures near its existing Nickel West assets. The deal covers high-grade nickel sulphide resources and is subject to approvals. It adds prospective resources but does not signal immediate production changes. | Trigon Metals agreed to acquire Technomine Africa’s 100% interest in Morocco’s Silver Hill copper-silver project. The deal involves cash and share payments plus contingent payments based on contained copper-equivalent estimates. While it supports longer-term copper supply expectations, the article lacks near-term production impact details.

London copper fell as accelerating COVID-19 cases raised worries about a quicker recovery and near-term demand. LME three-month copper eased to $5,846/ton. China prices were supported by low inventories and potential Chile supply disruption, while LME aluminum slipped and LME nickel declined. | Copper rose on Monday as inventories drew on the LME and in China and supply disruption risks increased in Chile, including COVID-19-related labor health policy threats at Codelco. However, gains were limited by fears of weaker industrial demand as global coronavirus cases accelerated, capping upside despite a tightening market surplus. | OZ Minerals agreed to buy its joint-venture partner Cassini Resources for $3.37bn, seeking greater control over the West Musgrave nickel/copper project in Western Australia. The move reflects ongoing nickel consolidation and can bolster near-term sentiment toward nickel supply expectations, with limited secondary relevance for copper. | Copper rose as inventories fell to multi-month lows on Shanghai and LME monitors and China’s unwrought copper imports signaled robust demand. Analysts cited improved sentiment and noted that further gains likely need wider (Western) demand recovery. Supply risk from Chile’s Codelco added support. Aluminium rose; nickel fell.

Blackstone reports it intersected a 15.4m wide zone of massive nickel sulphide mineralisation at the Ban Chang prospect within Vietnam’s Ta Khoa nickel-Cu-PGE district. Results include assays with meaningful Ni and associated Cu, supporting further drilling and potential future mining studies, but no near-term production change is stated. | Copper prices fell after a U.S. official said the U.S.-China trade deal is “over,” raising concerns about the global economic outlook and worsening sentiment as the dollar firmed. The LME copper contract reversed recent gains. The article also notes declines in LME aluminium and nickel amid broader risk concerns. | Blackstone Minerals says it has intersected a new, wide zone of massive nickel sulphide at the Ban Chang prospect of its Ta Khoa nickel-PGE project in Vietnam. Multiple drill holes returned substantial nickel (with some copper) and support bulk underground mining potential, with EM technology proving effective. | Copper rose on expectations the U.S.-China trade deal remains intact and on stronger European PMIs, which boosted risk appetite and recovery hopes. The rally is supported by near-recovered Chinese demand, supply disruptions in Chile, falling LME on-warrant inventories, and higher Chinese import premiums. Aluminium tariff news is also cited. | Blackstone Minerals reported a wide 15.4 m intersection of nickel sulphides at the Ban Chang prospect in Vietnam, following earlier high-grade hits including nickel at 2.20% with copper. The company aims to fast-track resources for potential renewed mining, but no production changes are stated.

SMM reported that Shanghai bonded refined nickel inventories rose by 400 mt week-on-week to 16,000 mt as of June 24. Nickel cathode accounted for the increase, with forward cargoes entering bonded warehouses and some imports bypassing the bonded area. Trades in the bonded zone remained muted, suggesting supply overhang. | SMM reported that nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell after two weeks of gains. Total stocks dropped by 100,000 wmt from June 19 to 7.99 million wmt by June 24, with metal content down 800 mt to 65,000 mt. Inventories at seven major ports also declined to 6.95 million wmt.

The piece criticizes Antofagasta’s Twin Metals proposal for an underground copper-nickel mine in Minnesota, claiming it cannot operate profitably at current copper ($2.68/lb) and nickel ($5.68) prices. It argues uncertainty and opposition are hurting the local economy and that the project’s viability depends on much higher future metal prices. | Copper rose 0.3% as inventories tightened across LME on-warrant and ShFE-tracked warehouses, hitting multi-month lows. Supply risks also increased after Chile’s Codelco reported a third COVID-19 death. Even as macro concerns and a stronger dollar capped gains, copper sentiment remained supported by constrained stocks.

Mincor is moving closer to reviving nickel operations at Kambalda, backed by a planned $60 million investment. The report suggests renewed focus on restarting/expanding mine activity, which can influence expectations for near-term nickel supply and related market pricing over coming weeks. | Copper prices extended gains for a sixth straight week as supply fears rose with coronavirus disruptions in key producing countries. Codelco suspended refinery and foundry operations at Chuquicamata, implying larger copper losses, while Peru’s closures affected about 2 million tonnes. Nickel and aluminium also climbed. | Copper extended gains toward a sixth consecutive weekly rise as coronavirus-driven supply disruption fears grew. Codelco suspended refinery and foundry operations at Chuquicamata, suggesting potential production losses exceeding Chile’s estimate. Analysts also cite Peru capacity closures. Nickel and aluminium were also higher, supported by improving supply expectations. | Copper prices reached a five-month peak as investors feared worsening COVID-19 disruptions to Chilean supply. Codelco suspended operations at its Chuquicamata division, and analysts warned production losses could exceed government estimates. LME data also showed aluminium stocks rising, while nickel and other metals gained. | Garibaldi Resources begins its 2020 drill program at the Nickel Mountain project in British Columbia, targeting extensions of a nickel-copper-rich massive sulphide system identified by prior drilling and EM surveys. The release outlines exploration plans to expand the E&L deposit; no production or immediate supply changes are mentioned. | COVID-19 is disrupting metal supply via mine and smelter closures. The key focus shifts to copper in Chile: as Chilean cases rise, unions push safety measures and Codelco suspends smelting at Chuquicamata to redeploy manpower, raising uncertainty over refined copper output and potential second-wave closures.

Norilsk Nickel admitted improper wastewater dumping into Russia’s Arctic tundra and suspended employees after a gross violation of operational protocols. The company said it is cooperating with regulators to assess potential damage and claimed there is no threat of leakage. The report follows media allegations of heavy-metal contamination. | Russian authorities investigated Norilsk Nickel for pumping wastewater from an Arctic processing plant into tundra. The firm suspended staff and denied the presence of toxic tailings, but reporters allege heavy metals and acid. A large diesel spill involving 21,000 tonnes was also cited, raising near-term supply disruption risk. | SHFE base metals rose mostly higher after the Dragon Boat holiday despite renewed COVID-19 curbs. Copper hit a five-month peak on fears output could be disrupted in Chile, where Codelco suspended Chuquicamata refinery and foundry operations. Aluminium climbed alongside falling primary ingot inventories; nickel also ended higher.

LME copper is set for its best quarterly performance in a decade as China’s June factory activity surprised to the upside, supporting demand expectations. Tight scrap availability in China and supply risks in Chile also help. The piece notes LME aluminum and nickel rising alongside copper, aided by a weaker dollar and improving risk sentiment. | London copper is set for its best quarter in a decade as optimism over China’s demand recovery, global stimulus, low inventories and a weaker U.S. dollar lift prices. Three-month LME copper rose 0.8% and is up 21% for the quarter. China factory activity improved, supporting demand despite mixed export orders. | Nornickel says 435 people and 207 units of equipment are working to clean an Arctic spill after clarified industrial water overflowed from a sump at the Talnakh Concentrator. Large areas have been treated with sorbents and thousands of cubic meters collected. Suspended personnel and an investigation raise supply disruption and compliance risk for nickel. | Copper is set for its biggest quarterly gain in a decade as China’s demand rebounds, inventories at LME and ShFE warehouses fall, and COVID-related disruptions hit supply in major producing countries like Chile and Peru. The article also says aluminium and nickel rose in the same period, reflecting broad industrial-metal strength.

SMM reports SHFE/LME base metals were mixed, but copper and aluminium extended gains after China manufacturing PMI beat expectations, boosting demand sentiment, while the Fed signaled low rates and liquidity support. Copper rose to a multi-month high; aluminium also advanced. Nickel edged up slightly on support, implying moderate bullish bias. | SMM data show China’s downstream nickel sectors expanded in June but the pace slowed: downstream PMI eased to 50.25 and new orders slipped to 48.9, entering contraction. Stainless steel inventories stayed high, electroplating orders contracted sharply, and raw-material buying shifted to stockpiling ahead of August production. | Nornickel, one of the world’s largest nickel producers, faces a new Arctic pollution incident linked to its Talnakh nickel processing plant. Wastewater reportedly contained heavy metals and sulfuric acid while officials investigate. The episode follows a prior fuel spill and could heighten disruption and regulatory risk for nickel supply.

St George Mining began deep diamond drilling at its Mt Alexander nickel sulphide project and continues shallow resource drill-out at Stricklands. The company has completed major met work for a scoping study, with thick nickel-copper sulphide intercepts and plans to develop a nickel-concentrate flowsheet. Resource and deeper target results are upcoming. | SMM reports mixed SHFE base-metal trading as investors assess US economic data and coronavirus vaccine optimism. Copper and aluminium rose modestly, with copper reaching a multi-month high on the LME/SHFE. Nickel rebounded slightly but is described as rangebound without major fundamentals driving a sustained move. | Nornickel said a storage tank collapse at a Norilsk power plant, blamed on thawing permafrost, released over 21,000 tons of diesel into local soil and waterways. Executives and officials face charges, cleanup costs are estimated at 10 billion rubles, and environmental damage remains under review, creating near-term nickel supply risk. | Copper prices rose for a sixth session as COVID-19 disruptions in Chile, the top global producer, increased perceived supply risk. BHP plans to ramp down Cerro Colorado activity and Codelco will close refinery and foundry operations at Chuquicamata. Macro recovery signs also supported demand expectations; aluminium was flat and nickel edged higher.

The piece argues industrial metals are tight as China recovers and pandemic-related disruptions persist. It highlights falling copper inventories, LME backwardation and deficit forecasts, pointing to supported copper prices. Nickel is linked to hard-hit supply from disruptions and an expected 2021 recovery. Aluminium is only indirectly referenced via alumina producers. | SMM data shows Chinese nickel ore inventories increased this week by 85,000 wmt to 8.08 million wmt as of July 3, with Ni-content stocks up 500 mt to 65,500 mt. This was the third weekly rise in four weeks, suggesting improving ore availability. Stocks at seven major ports fell 50,000 wmt to 6.9 million wmt. | Copper prices fell as traders saw the recent rally extending too far amid worries about China demand, including a summer construction slowdown and rising Shanghai-tracked inventories. The move is reinforced by technical overbought signals and US COVID case records. Nickel was also mentioned for stainless-linked strength; aluminum slipped modestly.

Copper prices jumped to five-month highs as investors priced recovering demand from top consumer China and weighed COVID-19 risks to Chilean mine supply. LME copper rose about 1.7% to around $6,118/t. Aluminium and nickel were also higher, reflecting broader support for industrial metals. | Russia’s environmental watchdog Rosprirodnadzor has requested nearly $2 billion in damages from Nornickel’s subsidiary NTEK over an Arctic diesel spill that released 21,000 tonnes into rivers and subsoil near Norilsk. The case adds near-term legal and remediation cost risk; Nornickel shares fell 3% on the news.

London copper rose to a five-month high, up as much as 0.7% on strong U.S. services activity and a risk-on economic rebound narrative. LME copper inventories fell to the lowest since mid-March, reinforcing demand sentiment. Nickel ticked higher while aluminium edged down amid the same market shift. | Copper prices climbed to a five-month high on renewed China recovery expectations and supply concerns. LME copper inventories fell to the lowest since mid-March, and cash–to–three-month premiums rose, signaling tighter near-term supply. China policy and economic data boosted sentiment, while aluminium edged lower and nickel rose. | Blackstone Minerals bought a third diamond drill rig to accelerate drilling at Vietnam’s Ban Chang nickel prospect near the mothballed Ta Khoa nickel processing plant. Recent holes returned sulphide nickel grades with associated copper, and studies aim for possible nickel sulphate downstream production for lithium-ion batteries. | Shanghai copper rose to a 2020 high as China economic recovery hopes combined with supply fears supported sentiment. LME copper inventories fell to a multi-month low and nearby premiums widened, indicating tight physical supply. The article also mentions easing aluminum spreads and a rise in ShFE nickel, reflecting broad support.

Copper surged to the highest since January as speculators bet on further disruptions in Chile’s mines and solid demand from China. The move followed renewed COVID-19 production fears in top producer Chile. LME aluminium also rose, while nickel was flat, implying copper-led strength. | Gungnir Resources reports re-sampling of archived drill core at its Lappvattnet nickel deposit in Sweden, including an interval of 4.64% Ni over 0.65 metres. The company also provides historical Ni and minor Cu context and plans a resource update, but no production impact is described.

Copper prices rose to a 14-month high on hopes of a faster China recovery and concerns over disruptions in Chile, the world’s biggest producer. LME copper climbed on higher cash premiums and falling on-warrant inventories. China factory price softness was partly offset by improving sector signs; speculators increased net longs. | The LIVE FUTURES REPORT notes LME copper rallied to $6,300 at the close, while LME nickel declined by 1.5%. It also contains Fastmarkets/Metal Bulletin methodological and assessment correction notes and general commentary on aluminium supply conditions, but without a specific aluminium price move. | The report cites LME settlement moves: copper rallied to about $6,300 at the close, while nickel fell by roughly 1.5%. The accompanying text is largely administrative/methodology updates and does not add specific new fundamentals. Overall, the price signals are mixed across copper and nickel. | Norilsk Nickel was fined US$2.1bn by Russia’s environmental watchdog after a diesel spill near Norilsk. The leak followed a storage tank collapse likely linked to unusually warm Arctic conditions and has reached Lake Pyasino. Cleanup is ongoing, while the company contests parts of the fine amid Greenpeace/WWF scrutiny.

Nickel Industries’ Nickel Mines completed a stake increase to 80% in Indonesian Morowali nickel projects producing nickel pig iron. Analysts cite catalysts including improving nickel prices from cyclical lows, EV-related demand growth, and potential dividends in 2021, despite policy/export-ban risks in Indonesia. | Indonesia’s VDNI pledged to hire 5,000 locals for its Konawe nickel smelter after protests over 500 Chinese workers. The first batch arrived, but later arrivals were delayed due to demonstrations. Authorities say the project should proceed, albeit slower, while employment ramps up in phases. | Rockcliff initiated a new drilling program at its TGR nickel-PGE discovery and reported additional assay results with higher nickel equivalent grades, including significant rhodium and other PGMs alongside nickel. The update supports improved resource potential at Tower property, which is likely to be a near-term sentiment tailwind for nickel markets. | SMM says Shanghai bonded refined nickel inventories rose to about 16,500 mt as of July 10, with nickel cathode stocks up 400 mt week on week to 14,800 mt. Foreign trading stayed subdued and only limited cathode entered bonded warehouses, while some cathode is expected later in July and briquette shipments may decline. | SMM data shows nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 200,000 wmt from July 3 to 7.88 million wmt as of July 10. In nickel content, stocks dropped 1,700 mt to 63,800 mt. Inventories at seven major ports declined by 240,000 wmt to 6.67 million wmt.

SMM says nonferrous metals traded mostly higher, with copper leading on growing mine-supply concerns, including strike action at Antofagasta’s Zaldivar mine in Chile. Nickel and aluminium also rallied on Friday and into Monday, supported by broader risk sentiment and tight east China aluminium spot availability. | Shanghai and London copper jumped to 2-year highs on Monday due to supply concerns after workers at Antofagasta Minerals’ Zaldivar mine in Chile voted for strike action, with a potential walkout set for July 15 pending government mediation. The move coincided with gains in aluminium and nickel prices. | Shanghai and London copper surged to multi-year highs as supply worries grew. Workers at Antofagasta’s Zaldivar mine in Chile voted for strike action starting July 15. China flood warnings raised concerns over logistics and refined output, especially in Jiangxi. The move also lifted aluminium and nickel to multi-month peaks. | Copper prices jumped to multi-month highs on Monday amid supply worries from Chile after workers at Antofagasta’s Zaldivar mine voted for potential strike action. London and Shanghai copper rose sharply, with LME prices also firming for aluminium and nickel, indicating spillover across industrial metals. | Nornickel reported a second diesel pipeline burst near Norilsk, following the May spill that flooded rivers with over 20,000 tonnes of fuel. Regulators are seeking large “voluntary compensation” and the company disputes damage estimates. The escalation adds to operational and legal uncertainty for the group’s nickel supply. | The piece outlines Canada Nickel’s Crawford project in Ontario as a potential long-life, low-carbon nickel source outside Indonesia and China, with very strong stated economics. It also cites robust nickel demand growth for EV batteries and stainless steel, arguing the market could remain in deficit—supportive for nickel prices. | Canada Nickel reports it has identified seven nickel-bearing target structures on option properties acquired in May 2020. Historical drilling showed nickel and some PGM-enriched mineralization across the targets, though most drilling was decades ago and did not fully test target widths. The update supports potential future nickel supply sentiment.

London copper ended a six-day rally as coronavirus cases rose, renewing fears of further lockdowns and weaker global demand. LME three-month copper fell 1.7% to $6,457.5/tonne, while Shanghai August copper slipped. Shanghai aluminium also declined, and LME nickel fell amid risk sentiment. | Copper prices retreated from a two-year high as renewed U.S.-China tensions sparked fears of economic retaliation and prompted profit-taking. The article notes that LME three-month copper fell 1.8%. It also reports declines in LME nickel and Shanghai aluminium, despite firmer Chinese demand and a Chile supply-risk overhang. | Copper prices fell Tuesday after flaring U.S.-China tensions raised retaliation and growth worries, triggering profit-taking. Still, the correction may be short-lived because China demand is firm, physical premiums in China rose, and Chile supply disruption concerns (Centinela strike vote) remain. Nickel and aluminium also moved lower on the day. | Russian Rusal requested changes at Norilsk Nickel’s board, including relocating headquarters to Norilsk and overhauling environmental and safety policies after a fuel spill and other ecological accidents in the Arctic. The move signals heightened operational and compliance risk at nickel facilities, potentially pressuring nickel supply expectations. | Copper prices fell after a prior surge to a two-year peak as investors responded to rising coronavirus cases and new California restrictions, alongside US-China friction over South China Sea claims. LME three-month copper dropped 1.4% and LME aluminium and nickel also declined, reflecting broader risk-off sentiment despite firm demand signals. | Norilsk Nickel said about 45 tons of aviation fuel spilled from an Arctic pipeline on July 12, adding to a recent run of environmental incidents. The company previously reported a 21,000-ton diesel spill from a Norilsk-operated power plant in May, highlighting potential disruption and compliance risk for nickel output. | Canada Nickel highlights its Crawford nickel-cobalt sulfide project in Ontario’s Timmins mining camp, including a maiden resource estimate (~263 Mt at 0.31% nickel) and commencement of a PEA. The piece also links nickel demand to electric-battery use, potentially supporting near-term market sentiment for nickel. | Copper prices slipped from a two-year peak as markets turned cautious on renewed coronavirus risks and heightened US-China tensions. The move coincided with California restrictions and comments about potential sanctions. While Chile supply concerns had driven the rally, prices eased; LME aluminium and nickel also fell.

Clean Air Metals announced new drill intercepts at its Thunder Bay North Escape Lake intrusion, including 1.67% copper and 0.90% nickel over 27.9m. The company frames results as progress toward outlining mineralization for a future resource estimate, but provides no production or inventory data. | Copper steadied after earlier losses tied to U.S.-China tensions, trading up slightly on the LME ahead of Chinese manufacturing data. The piece cites strong recent demand indicators but limits further upside. It also highlights Chile COVID-related disruption/safety concerns and mentions marginal aluminium and nickel moves.

Wood Mackenzie says nickel’s YTD rebound is mostly sentiment rather than fundamentals. COVID-19 cut global demand and surplus forecasts replace prior deficits due to relatively resilient mining supply and strong Indonesian output (NPI, HPAL progress). Blackstone Minerals reported a new “Viper” zone of nickel-copper sulphide mineralisation at its Ta Khoa project in Vietnam, supported by electromagnetic anomalies and trench sampling (0.8% Ni and 0.5% Cu). Clean Air Metals reported new drill intercepts at the Thunder Bay North Escape Lake intrusion, including 96.0m grading 0.61% copper and 0.34% nickel, plus additional step-out thickness and strike extension. Nickel prices rose to a nine-month high as steady demand from stainless steel supported sentiment, while supply risks increased after flooding in Indonesia’s Weda Bay Industrial Park forced parts of PT Indonesia Weda Bay Industrial Park to halt operations. Copper prices are set for a third straight weekly gain after traders interpreted a Fed policy shift as keeping rates low and weakening the dollar. Indonesia mine/smelter disruptions ending further aided sentiment, lifting aluminium and nickel too.

The piece is mostly about sourcing sustainability, not immediate market disruption. | The article says EVs will drive a major rise in nickel use because nickel-rich battery chemistries (NCM/NCA) contain large nickel shares, especially for larger battery packs. London copper rose slightly as Reuters cited data showing China’s service sector and manufacturing recovery after COVID-19. London copper edged down while Shanghai copper rose ahead of an ECB decision, as Sino-U.S. trade tensions kept investors cautious. The piece explains how Covid-19 derailed nickel’s 2020 recovery. Lockdowns reduced Chinese stainless steel demand and increased stocks. Si6 Metals plans to begin drilling at the Maibele North nickel-copper project in Botswana after identifying a deep electromagnetic target, with additional drilling at Airstrip and Dibete where prior results included high-grade copper. The rally is linked to improving Chinese industrial activity and reduced inventories, pushing LME nickel up and lifting gains since March. | French police evacuated workers and repelled pro-independence activists attempting to storm Vale’s nickel-and-cobalt plant in New Caledonia as tensions flare over a planned sale. Shanghai nickel jumped, with the main January contract hitting its upper limit and closing up 3.8% after strong demand outlooks and speculative buying. Nearly 1,000 mt of nickel plates were packed and shipped to an LME Asia delivery warehouse, easing stocks. The duty-free import window for briquettes opened, though Russian nickel import losses remain, keeping inventory pressure. | SMM data show nickel ore inventories across all Chinese ports rose 101,000 wmt from Dec 4 to 9.41 million wmt as of Dec 11.
