China Molybdenum said it bought a 95% stake in the Kisanfu copper-cobalt mine in the DRC from Freeport for $550 million. The acquisition adds sizable copper reserves near China Moly’s Tenke Fungurume operations. It is a supply-related copper development but not a short-term production change. | Nickel surged to a 14-month peak in London and topped contract levels in Shanghai, gaining on supply crunch fears. Support comes from higher China stainless steel prices and concerns about nickel availability. The Eramet New Caledonia smelter is running low on ore due to protests, while Philippines output fell 12% year-to-date. | New Caledonia reports SLN’s Thio nickel mine is still closed as pro-independence FLNKS-related protests continue. Roadblocks were lifted, but unrest persists after Vale’s planned sale to a new consortium, amid opposition to Trafigura involvement. Talks are expected, leaving nickel output supply risks elevated.

A fire broke out at Vale’s nickel mine site in New Caledonia, following last week’s riots that already led to the site being closed. Firefighters worked overnight to contain the blaze and protect fuel and equipment. The incident comes amid ongoing protests over Vale’s planned sale, now potentially worsening nickel supply disruption. | Copper was little changed as renewed COVID-19 lockdown fears dented investor sentiment, offsetting strong China industrial output data. While most-tracked prices stayed in narrow ranges, LME aluminium and nickel also moved modestly with the broader risk-off tone. Analysts highlighted heightened downside risk during winter. | SMM says medium-grade nickel ore prices held near $75/wmt in November, but spot shipments thinned as rain affected Philippine supply. Negotiations for January FOB/CIF levels with Qingshan are at a deadlock, with a 1.5% bidding CIF price quoted at $52/wmt. Prices are expected to fall in December or January. | Copper prices rose as China’s factory data pointed to faster economic growth in the top consumer, and vaccine rollout boosted risk sentiment. Funds’ bullish copper positioning also remained elevated. The piece adds that China’s primary aluminium output hit a record, while nickel edged higher alongside the broader base-metals tone. | Talon Metals reported BHEM-guided drilling at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project, intersecting shallow massive sulphide mineralization grading 5.13% Ni over 2.71m within a wider 9.55m zone at 2.12% Ni, with accompanying copper grades. The release is exploration-focused and does not alter near-term supply materially. | Indonesia’s nickel reserves and its recently passed Jobs Creation Law are highlighted as factors to attract Tesla investment, with government officials discussing cooperation to develop EV battery supply chains. The piece also reiterates Indonesia’s nickel ore export ban aimed at expanding smelting, supporting near-term expectations for nickel demand. | Ero Copper reported drill results from its Vale do Curaçá Pilar mine, highlighting new “Superpod” high-grade intercepts including 46.5m at 4.96% copper and 20.3m at 4.76% copper, plus a potential new parallel lens at depth. The update is supportive for near-term copper production outlook and sentiment. | Talon Metals reported BHEM-guided drilling outside its current resource area at the Tamarack project, intersecting 2.71m of massive sulphide grading 5.13% Ni (plus associated copper) in shallow mineralization. The company plans further drilling to delineate resources, but no production or supply change is indicated. | BASF and Eramet signed an agreement to jointly assess a nickel and cobalt hydrometallurgical refining complex (HPAL in Weda Bay, Indonesia, plus a base metal refinery). If developed, it would process local ore and supply additional nickel (~42,000 t/year) for EV battery materials, supporting demand expectations for nickel. | FPX Nickel reported year-end progress on its Baptiste nickel-cobalt project, including a preliminary economic assessment projecting long mine life and costs, plus plans for maiden drilling at the Van target and further metallurgical testing for EV battery-grade nickel/cobalt products. It also expands carbon sequestration studies for the tailings. | Eramet and BASF signed an agreement to study feasibility of a nickel-cobalt hydrometallurgical refining complex in Weda Bay, Indonesia. The project would include an HPAL plant and base metal refinery, targeting mid-2020s startup, and could provide BASF access to an additional 42,000 tonnes of nickel annually.

Hundreds of workers protested at Indonesia’s PT Virtue Dragon Nickel Industry smelter in Konawe, turning violent and damaging equipment, with operations “paralyzed.” The disruption threatens nickel supply from the billion-dollar refining facility, where exports and nickel processing are strategically important, adding upside pressure to nickel prices. | Hundreds of workers at PT Virtue Dragon Nickel Industry’s Indonesian nickel smelter in Sulawesi protested for higher pay and permanent jobs for contractors. The protest turned violent, with workers torching vehicles and buildings, and the company said smelter activities were paralysed, disrupting nickel supply. | Canada Nickel signed a non-binding memorandum of understanding with Taykwa Tagamou Nation for the Crawford nickel-cobalt sulfide project, including plans for TTN-managed electrical transmission assets to supply power and potential future minority interest options. Ontario also publicly supports the partnership, framing it as enabling sustainable development. | Huntsman Exploration reports successful financings and acquisitions, and it says the permitting process has begun for an upcoming 18-hole drill program at its Canegrass high-grade nickel project in Western Australia, which also hosts copper. Work is expected after permits are received, but no production changes are announced. | Canada Nickel announced a fourth discovery—North Zone—at its Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project in Ontario. Two drill holes intersected mineralized dunite over long intervals, expanding a large geophysical anomaly, but assay results are pending. The update may modestly support nickel supply expectations despite no near-term production impact. | Canada Nickel signed a memorandum of understanding with the Taykwa Tagamou First Nation for electric power development tied to its Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project in Ontario. The partnership is intended to enable project progress by securing power, which could modestly support near-term nickel supply expectations.

Copper prices rose for a third straight session as investors looked for additional U.S. COVID-19 aid and the Fed pledged continued support until recovery is secure. The article also notes aluminium up on the day, while nickel fell on China’s SHFE, reflecting broadly risk-on sentiment. | BASF and Eramet will assess a nickel-cobalt HPAL refining complex fed by Eramet’s Weda Bay mining ore. The project is intended to supply intermediate and cathode active materials for EV batteries, targeting ~42,000 metric tons of nickel and 5,000 tons of cobalt annually from the mid-2020s. | Putin said Norilsk Nickel should be responsible for a major diesel spill in Russia’s Arctic, where 21,000 tonnes leaked into rivers and subsoil near Norilsk. A lawsuit by the environmental watchdog seeks $2 billion and has a Dec. 24 hearing. The case and environmental obligations add operational risk. | LG Chem and Tesla reportedly signed a deal for nickel-based batteries. LG will produce NCMA batteries (nickel, cobalt, manganese and aluminium anode materials) and provide them to Tesla next year. The arrangement links Tesla’s battery sourcing to higher demand expectations for nickel and some aluminium content. | Metso Outotec won a ~EUR 90 million contract to modernize PJSC MMC Norilsk Nickel’s Nadezhda smelting line in Norilsk. The scope includes a nickel flash smelting furnace and heat recovery boiler to boost capacity and availability, cut metal losses, and improve maintenance, supporting stronger near-term nickel production outlooks.

Protests in New Caledonia over Vale’s planned sale of its nickel assets escalated into riots, with road blockades, violence, and police action. The Vale plant was damaged and forced to shut, and the Thio mine was closed, cutting local nickel output and raising short-term supply concerns. | The piece says nickel is a top performer as Indonesia faces labor unrest that paralyzed sections of national production. With global supply concerns rising, nickel prices surged to about $17,650/mt. The article contrasts this with earlier disruption from Indonesia’s nickel-ore export ban. | SMM data show Shanghai bonded refined nickel inventories fell 800 mt from the prior week to 22,000 mt by Dec 18. Nickel briquette arrivals continued via direct customs clearance while import losses on nickel plates persisted. About 800 mt of nickel plates were packed and shipped overseas to LME Asia warehouses, reducing bonded stocks for two straight weeks. | SMM data shows nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 557,000 wmt from Dec 11 to 8.85 million wmt by Dec 18. Ni content stocks dropped 4,500 mt to 70,100 mt, while inventories at seven major ports fell 577,000 wmt to 6.55 million wmt.

Tartisan Nickel Corp. closed a $1 million flow-through financing at $0.43 per unit with proceeds intended to fund exploration and development of its Kenbridge Nickel Project in northwestern Ontario. The company plans to start work in winter 2021, which may support long-term nickel prospects but is unlikely to move near-term nickel prices materially. | Talon Metals provided an update on the Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project in Minnesota, highlighting drilling progress, metallurgical test work, and plans to issue an updated PEA and move toward pre-feasibility in 2021. The company aims to develop battery-grade nickel and potentially refined products, supporting long-dated nickel supply expectations.

Timmins praised Canada Nickel’s new MOU with Wabun Tribal Council and Taykwa Tagamou Nation, saying it advances the Crawford nickel-cobalt project toward permitting. The mayor links the agreement to job creation and progress on NetZero Metals’ planned processing facility for zero-carbon nickel, aligning with electric-vehicle demand. | The article expects nickel to stay oversupplied in 2026, citing strong Indonesian production and softer demand from stainless steel and EV/battery markets. Prices were volatile in Q1 2026 but have struggled below key highs. Even with Indonesian quota adjustments and mixed exchange inventories, oversupply risks further downside. | Timmins praised Canada Nickel’s newly announced MOU with local First Nations, saying it helps move the Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project toward permitting. The company also plans a nearby research and processing facility via NetZero Metals to produce low/zero-carbon nickel for EV demand, supporting regional mining jobs. | Nickel futures were down 1.6% in the LME complex, with the report attributing the decline to fears around a new Covid-19 variant. Such risk-off sentiment typically pressures near-term demand expectations for industrial metals. The piece references broader LME weakness but gives nickel the clearest impact detail.

Norilsk Nickel said it is shutting down its oldest smelting shop in Nikel, Russia (Murmansk region) as part of an environmental program to eliminate cross-border sulphur dioxide emissions. The closure reduces the local nickel smelting operation and implies tighter nickel supply, while copper and aluminium are not addressed. | Canada Nickel said lab locked-cycle metallurgy for its Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project achieved nickel recoveries of 46% and 51% with concentrate grades above 30% for some tests. The company will continue flowsheet optimization in 2021, which can improve expected production economics and support nickel sentiment.

Spruce Ridge reported assay results from infill drilling at the Great Burnt copper-gold VMS deposit in Newfoundland, including 22.75 m at 6.89% Cu and a higher-grade interval of 12.55 m at 10.59% Cu. Management says historic drilling may have understated copper grades, boosting exploration outlook.

London copper rose 1.4% as investors anticipated U.S. $2,000 COVID relief checks could accelerate economic recovery and boost metal consumption, with a weaker dollar supporting dollar-priced commodities. The report also shows nickel up and aluminium down, indicating mixed short-term responses across base metals. | Aluminium prices hit their lowest level in a month as investors worried that higher primary output and new capacity would add to surpluses, with Shanghai and LME contracts falling. Copper rose after the U.S. House approved larger coronavirus aid payments, supporting activity hopes. Nickel edged up, though drivers were not specified. | Fitch Solutions forecasts nickel mine production growth of 8.3% year-on-year in 2021, above the 2010–2019 average of 5.9%, though it does not fully offset a projected 23.3% contraction in 2020 output. The report’s backdrop of rising nickel prices is expected to support project development.

SMM data shows nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports rose by 40,000 wmt to 9.29 million wmt from Dec 25 to Dec 31. In nickel content, stocks increased to 73,500 mt. Stocks at seven major ports also climbed to 7.3 million wmt.

GEM, a Chinese EV battery firm, plans to double its stake in an Indonesian nickel project at Morowali Industrial Park, rising to 72% from 36%. The company says the move is meant to strengthen strategic control amid a nickel shortage, implying firmer access to future nickel supply. | Copper and most industrial metals rose Monday as the weaker U.S. dollar boosted demand for greenback-priced commodities and sentiment improved on hopes for global recovery. LME three-month copper gained to $7,910/tonne. Nickel rose sharply, topping a two-week high, alongside gains across broader commodity markets. | Copper rose 1.9% on the LME and nickel gained as much as 5% amid a weaker U.S. dollar and upbeat commodity/market sentiment. Broader gains were also linked to optimism over global economic recovery. The only offset noted is forthcoming GSCI/BCOM rebalancing that may sell copper within days. | SMM says refined nickel spot strengthened as traders faced limited available cargoes and halted operations, leaving tighter supply. A weaker U.S. dollar and stronger manufacturing activity also supported broader base metals. | Nickel prices surged to two-week highs as an earthquake in Indonesia sparked worries about nickel supply. Base metals also firmed on a weaker dollar and strong manufacturing.

London three-month copper rose 0.9% toward $8,000/t as a weaker U.S. dollar made dollar-priced metals cheaper for non-USD holders. Traders also watched U.S. Georgia Senate elections for stimulus odds and China’s yuan midpoint move. Copper inventories remain near multi-year lows; LME aluminum fell and nickel rose. | SMM’s December survey showed China’s nickel downstream sectors returned to expansion, with a downstream PMI rising to 51.11. Restocking occurred as cheaper seaborne electroplating nickel entered spot, supporting nickel demand sentiment into January. | Emirates Global Aluminium said its Al Taweelah alumina refinery has maintained monthly output at or above nameplate capacity for three months and is replacing some alumina imports. The ramp-up reached full production within 21 months, increasing local alumina supply that feeds aluminium smelters. | GEM signed a memorandum of understanding to increase its stake in an Indonesian nickel project. The update signals deeper participation in nickel production assets, which may improve market expectations for future nickel supply. This is supportive for nickel pricing in the near term, though timing and tonnage are not specified. | A Transparency Market Research note says EV adoption and government EV/battery regulations are boosting demand for nickel-based batteries, supporting growth in the carbonyl nickel powder market. Carbonyl nickel powder is used in battery and electrode applications, implying rising near-term nickel demand expectations. | Copper futures rose toward an eight-year peak as a weaker US dollar, expectations of robust Chinese demand, and falling LME inventories with high cancelled warrants spurred buying. The piece also reports gains in aluminium and nickel, indicating supportive broader industrial-metals sentiment alongside the copper-specific inventory draw. | Nickel prices jumped to two-week highs near $17,520/t on the LME as traders turned cautious after an earthquake in Indonesia, raising concern about nickel supply flows. ALX Resources agreed to acquire a 100% interest in Ontario’s Electra nickel project near Thunder Bay, covering 148 units over 3,000+ hectares. The company cited nickel grades up to ~1.0% in surface sampling and expects mining of nickel with other metals, including copper, but provides no near-term production guidance. | Shanghai stainless steel futures rose to a three-week high as brisk demand and low inventories coincided with a surge in nickel-linked feedstock prices. Analysts cited persistently high ferronickel/nickel pig iron. Nickel prices jumped after an earthquake in Indonesia’s Sulawesi raised supply concerns, boosting raw-material costs. | Fastmarkets/Metal Bulletin’s live futures report notes LME copper reaching a new high above $8,100/t and nickel nearing $18,000/t, signaling upward momentum. It also comments on aluminium’s supply and logistics backdrop—some output curtailment and shipping disruption—suggesting firmer conditions, though less explicitly quantified.

EnGold Mines reported drilling that extends its G1 copper zone at the Lac La Hache property, including hole G20-51 returning 5.05 metres at 2.75% CuEq within a wider 23.7-m interval at 0.51% CuEq. Results support continued exploration along the prospective trend.  | Fortune Nickel and Gold Inc. acquired 75 mining claims near Timmins, Ontario in a nickel-bearing area, issued shares, and granted a 1% net smelter return royalty. SMM data show nickel ore inventories across all Chinese ports fell 153,000 wmt to 9.14 million wmt as of Jan 8, with Ni content down 1,200 mt to 72,300 mt. Inventories at seven major ports also dropped to 7.12 million wmt, suggesting tighter near-term supply. | SMM data show Shanghai bonded refined nickel stocks fell 200 mt to 22,800 mt week-on-week as overseas pandemic conditions limited arrivals. New Caledonia’s Southern Province president proposed that local shareholders hold 51% of Vale’s nickel plant purchase, while Trafigura’s stake be cut to under 20%. Talks continue after prior sale efforts faced protests and arson at the Vale site, raising concerns over potential mothballing and jobs. | Canada Nickel has signed an agreement with Glencore to explore using the Kidd concentrator and metallurgical site in Timmins, Ontario. Canada Nickel signed a non-binding MOU with Glencore Canada to potentially process material from its nearby Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project at the Kidd concentrator/metallurgical site. Using existing surplus capacity could speed production and reduce retrofit costs, with a study expected in late March. | Shanghai copper fell as rising COVID-19 cases in China sparked fears of demand disruption ahead of Lunar New Year. The March contract dropped up to 2.5% to a one-week low, while ShFE aluminium and nickel also declined around 2% on weakened sentiment for the world’s biggest metals consumer. | Shanghai copper fell to a one-week low after China imposed fresh virus curbs, renewing concerns over demand from the world’s largest metals consumer. ShFE copper slid 1.3%. Aluminium fell 1.4% and nickel was slightly lower, reflecting expectations of slower industrial activity and tempered recovery. | Talon Metals reported a new drilling intercept at the Tamarack nickel-copper project in Minnesota: 8.39 meters grading 8.15% nickel and 3.01% copper (plus cobalt and PGM/Au). The company says results increase confidence in continuous thick mineralization and plans more drilling and a pre-feasibility study. | Talon Metals reports drill hole 20TK0273 intersected 8.39 meters of massive sulphide mineralization grading 8.15% nickel and 3.01% copper at the Tamarack project in Minnesota. The company says the thick, high-grade result improves confidence in continuous Ni-Cu mineralization and plans more drilling and a pre-feasibility study. | FPX Nickel says its Baptiste project could produce 63% refined nickel with very low carbon intensity by using hydro power and bypassing smelting, with an estimated 2.40 tCO2/t refined Ni (potentially lower with tailings sequestration). The note is based on a PEA and positions the product for carbon-threshold buyers.

Shanghai and LME nickel prices rose as traders feared supply disruptions in key ore-producing regions. Eramet warned its SLN unit in New Caledonia could face liquidation within weeks if protests continue. A Philippines nickel mining ban also boosted supply worries, though it excludes Caraga. | SMM reports that Shanghai nonferrous futures closed lower across the board. Copper fell 1.26% and aluminium weakened 1.35%; nickel was slightly lower. The piece links the drop to higher US Treasury yields and a stronger US dollar, with China’s financing/M2 data due and the market watching key contract levels. | Adventus and Salazar announce additional infill drilling at the El Domo copper-gold deposit in Ecuador, including intervals with high copper grades and improved CuEq highlights. Results will be used to update the Mineral Resource and feed ongoing feasibility and development planning for a potential future mine. This supports near-term copper sentiment. | Blue Rhino Capital signed a letter of intent to acquire entities controlling the Zebediela Nickel Project in South Africa. The transaction is conditional on financing and approvals and is aimed at exploration and development. Despite no near-term production, the deal can modestly improve nickel project sentiment and market expectations. | SMM reports most Shanghai nonferrous base metals rose on Jan 13. Nickel surged 3.15% and copper gained 0.72%, supported by a softer dollar and higher oil. Aluminium declined 1.11%. The note also includes contract closes, open-interest changes, and brief supply-demand commentary for other metals.

The EU is escalating a WTO case against Indonesia over nickel ore export curbs by requesting a formal dispute panel. The complaint, launched in 2019, targets Indonesia’s restrictions on raw materials used to make stainless steel. A ruling or compliance pressure could change nickel ore export availability soon. | A wind farm in western Kazakhstan will use Saft’s Uptimax nickel battery system to supply backup power during extreme winter temperatures down to -35°C. The batteries are used for monitoring and control equipment on remote turbines and are claimed to be reliable for over 20 years, supporting ongoing nickel battery demand. | Minnesota lawmakers introduced legislation to slow or ban copper-nickel mining in the northeastern Iron Range, including a proposed ban on copper mining on federal lands in the Rainy River watershed. Another bill would require proof of safe operations elsewhere. Local bipartisan opposition exists, but the policy is supply-restricting.

Copper climbed toward eight-year highs as U.S. stimulus hopes lifted equities and boosted industrial-demand sentiment. A firmer dollar and worsening coronavirus cases in China posed risks. Nickel also rose sharply amid supply support from halted Philippines mining. Aluminium was slightly lower, indicating weaker net momentum. | SMM reports that 1.5% medium-grade nickel ore prices in late December fell to about $65.5/wmt CIF, but shipments from small Philippine mines were limited as bids lagged expectations. Due to rainy season and constrained resources, mines are reluctant to cut offers, so January prices are expected to stay around $65/wmt CIF. | SMM data shows Shanghai bonded refined nickel inventories fell 1,600 mt week-on-week to 21,200 mt on Jan 15. Stocks at seven major ports also declined, suggesting tighter near-term supply. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous metals mostly higher: aluminium rose 0.92% and nickel gained 1.19%, while copper edged down 0.09%. The commentary attributes moves to pandemic/rescue-plan and macro developments, plus market focus on near-term levels for copper and aluminium, with no specific supply disruption highlighted. | Investors are turning bullish on copper and other industrial metals on expectations that Biden’s $2 trillion stimulus and EV/infrastructure plans will boost global growth and decarbonization demand. Bank of America raised 2021 price targets for copper, aluminum and nickel, while constrained supply and strong speculative positioning support the rally. | Talon Metals released updated drill results from its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project in Minnesota. The reported high-grade massive sulphide intercept includes strong nickel and meaningful copper grades. The news may boost investor sentiment about future supply, offering modest near-term support for nickel and, to a lesser extent, copper prices.

Copper prices rose in London after China reported stronger-than-expected Q4 growth, lifting confidence in metal demand. Reuters said three-month LME copper edged up, while aluminium also advanced and nickel increased. The positive macro data improved market sentiment and partially offset earlier declines. | Analysts at Macquarie forecast copper will keep outperforming in 2021, supported by buoyant Chinese demand and additional stimulus. Separately, Ord Minnett marked up several commodity price estimates after a forward-curve exercise, including nickel, implying broadly supportive sentiment for industrial metals heading into 2021. | Copper advanced after upbeat China economic data boosted demand expectations. Traders cited stronger-than-forecast growth and resilient industrial output in China. LME three-month copper rose about 0.8%, with Shanghai copper slightly higher. The broader complex also firmed, including aluminium and nickel. | The EU has requested a WTO panel over Indonesia’s nickel ore export restrictions. Indonesia is the world’s largest nickel ore exporter and has maintained the ban to spur domestic smelting, while NPI capacity is expanding rapidly. Market participants expect Indonesia will not likely relax the restriction, limiting near-term nickel price upside. | Canada Nickel filed an amended NI 43-101 technical report for its Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project. Adding a conceptual pit envelope reduced contained nickel—unchanged higher-grade measured/indicated core, but lower overall and larger cuts to inferred nickel—along with corrected Pd/Pt assays. OSC review is complete. | Copper rose on Monday after strong China economic data confirmed a demand-supportive growth trajectory for industrial metals. China’s Q4 GDP beat forecasts, while LME copper stocks fell to the lowest since September and the cash discount narrowed. The article also notes declines in aluminium stocks and small gains in nickel.

Copper rose on Tuesday after stronger-than-expected Chinese economic data supported expectations for a recovery in demand. The report also notes rising COVID-19 cases and renewed worries about curbs. Aluminium dipped slightly, while nickel was up, reflecting mixed but broadly supportive macro sentiment. | Aluminium prices fell to a two-month low as LME inventories jumped and Shanghai-listed stocks increased, alongside higher Chinese output, raising surplus concerns. Copper edged down amid global investors digesting U.S. Treasury nominee Janet Yellen’s remarks and currency sentiment, while nickel rose modestly.

Reuters reports Indonesia remained China’s second-biggest nickel ore supplier in 2020 despite a nickel ore export ban from Jan. 1. Indonesian arrivals fell sharply but continued as small monthly imports, with analysts citing possible reclassification as iron ore for customs and a rise in nickel pig iron exports.

Copper prices rose on stimulus optimism for the U.S. economy, boosting risk sentiment and weakening the U.S. dollar, making dollar-priced metals more attractive. The article also notes LME aluminum and nickel increased alongside copper, while China’s rising COVID-19 cases limited the rally and world refined copper deficits improved. | Copper prices rose on Thursday as investors bet that a Biden-era $1.9 trillion stimulus package will support the global economy and offset COVID-19 damage. The article links the move to broader equities strength. LME aluminum and nickel also increased, reflecting the same improved demand sentiment despite some China COVID risk.

Copper prices fell as rising COVID-19 cases in China raised fears of further restrictions that could dampen demand. Three-month LME copper and March Shanghai copper both declined. LME aluminum and nickel also dropped, indicating a broader metals risk-off move tied to China’s worsening outbreak and consumption outlook. | Huntsman Exploration announced preparations and the start of an MLEM ground electromagnetics survey at its Canegrass nickel project in Western Australia, followed by an 18-hole RC/diamond drilling program to test EM anomalies. The project reports high-grade Ni-Cu mineralization historically, but the release is exploration-focused with no near-term supply change. | Silver Elephant signed a definitive agreement to acquire Victory Nickel’s Minago nickel sulphide project in Manitoba for US$11.675m, including consideration shares. The deal also has a nickel-price trigger, issuing additional shares if nickel exceeds US$10/lb for 30 consecutive business days. Timing is late 2020/early 2021.

Copper prices fell as demand worries grew from rising coronavirus cases in China, raising risk of wider lockdowns and reduced industrial activity, especially ahead of the Lunar New Year holiday. Copper dipped on the LME and Shanghai. The broader risk-off tone also pressured aluminum while nickel was mixed in quoted trade. | Canada Nickel reported additional positive locked-cycle metallurgical testing at its Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project, citing 52% nickel recovery and the ability to produce a high-grade 37% nickel concentrate. The company plans further flowsheet optimization toward a feasibility study, improving near-term investor sentiment for nickel. | Sherritt forecast higher output and higher costs at its Cuba nickel mine. The increased production can improve near-term nickel supply expectations, while rising costs may weigh on profitability and future output economics. The article’s mixed signals make the immediate net impact on nickel prices unclear. | Sumitomo reported an additional $289 million impairment related to the Ambatovy nickel-cobalt asset. While impairment is an accounting measure, it signals financial deterioration and potential operational risk for nickel supply, which can support higher near-term nickel price expectations versus otherwise.

Canada Nickel announced processing test results from its Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project, reporting a 52% nickel recovery and details on how recovered nickel splits during processing. The update is aimed at demonstrating concentrate performance, which can support sentiment for nickel supply prospects. | China’s nickel ore imports fell in December 2020 (down 11.2% m/m) with shipments from the Philippines weaker due to adverse weather, and nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports declined by late January. Ferronickel imports in December were down m/m but up y/y, with November clearance delays normalizing. | Talon Metals announced a record 21.73-meter intercept of mixed massive sulphide mineralization at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project in Minnesota, with assays pending. Management says the discovery could extend a high-grade massive sulphide unit. The update is exploratory and does not affect near-term supply or costs directly. | Talon Metals announced that drill hole 20TK0278 at its Tamarack nickel-copper project intersected 21.73 meters of mixed massive sulphide mineralization, setting a new record for the western limb of the Massive Sulphide Unit. Assays are pending, but the company expects the results to extend high-grade Ni-Cu mineralization. | Copper prices edged lower for a fourth day as rally momentum faded on slow U.S. stimulus progress and concerns about China’s demand amid coronavirus disruptions and tighter liquidity. LME aluminum rose on firmer cash premiums suggesting tight nearby supply, while nickel declined slightly. Overall pressure is mixed across metals but copper softens near term. | Talon Metals announced record drilling results at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project in Minnesota, reporting 21.73 meters of mixed massive sulphide mineralization in a new step-out hole. Assays are pending, but the company expects the intercept to extend its high-grade massive sulphide unit. | Talon Metals said drill hole 20TK0278 at its Tamarack nickel-copper project intersected 21.73 meters of mixed massive sulphides, extending the western massive sulphide unit; assays remain pending. The update is exploratory and aimed at expanding resources, with no immediate supply or trade changes. | Copper rose as the dollar weakened, but gains were capped by doubts around U.S. stimulus delivery and concerns over Chinese demand amid coronavirus and liquidity tightening. The LME cash-aluminium premium rose, signaling tight nearby supply, while nickel fell, highlighting mixed near-term price pressure across industrial metals.

Nickel Mines reported record Q4 output from its Indonesian RKEF projects, with realised nickel and nickel pig iron (NPI) prices beating broker forecasts and margins expanding. The company is also increasing its Angel Nickel stake and raising capital for expansion. It noted an NPI pricing change from LME nickel to Chinese NPI benchmarks. | The piece is a share-price/financial overview for Nickel Industries/Mines, an Australian producer of nickel pig iron used in stainless steel. While it directly relates to nickel exposure, it lacks any specific news on nickel supply, demand, inventories, or pricing, making a clear short-term nickel price signal unlikely. | Citi expects focus on ex-China demand as developed-economy consumption rebounds, supporting copper demand. For nickel, Morgan Stanley highlights significant ongoing supply disruptions (e.g., Philippines issues and protests around New Caledonia operations) with rising stainless-steel and later battery-related demand. Copper faces potential labour-led and operational disruption risks, likely keeping a deficit. | Citi expects ex-China commodity demand to support metals consumption. For nickel, Morgan Stanley flags elevated supply disruption risks (Philippines issues; ongoing protests after Vale’s Goro mine sale), while stainless demand is expected to grow, supporting prices. For copper, labor disruptions and slower Chile/Peru ramp-ups imply a deficit and price support. | SMM says nickel sulphate prices remain high due to tight spot supply and strong downstream restocking ahead of CNY. China output rose in December, with most production battery-grade. Demand nearly doubled year on year, driven mainly by new-energy batteries and rising precursor orders.

Grid Metals reports drilling results from its East Bull Lake property, including a four-metre interval of nickel-rich semi-massive sulfide and magnetite with accompanying copper assays. The company plans additional drilling to delineate Pd- and Cu-rich zones and test Ni-rich targets. No near-term supply or demand impacts are stated. | DeepGreen Metals revised upward its polymetallic nodule resource estimate in the Clarion Clipperton Zone, increasing nodule quantity and boosting grades, including nickel by 6.1% (and higher manganese/cobalt). The company frames the deposit as battery-grade supply for EV demand growth, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel and some copper.

SMM reports broad declines in Shanghai nonferrous prices, with copper (-0.78%) and nickel (-0.84%) falling on the day while aluminium rose (+1.32%). It cites weaker US growth signals, tight domestic liquidity, and positioning/“capital withdrawal before CNY” as sentiment drivers, with spot-to-futures monitoring for aluminium. | SMM reports Shanghai bonded refined nickel stocks fell 2,000 mt to 16,600 mt as of Jan 29, the fourth consecutive weekly drop. Lower refined nickel inventories improved trading conditions, encouraging bonded nickel plates and some Russian/Australian arrivals to flow into China via customs clearance. | Norilsk Nickel reported preliminary Q4 and FY2020 production results, including nickel output up q/q (via refinery ramp-up using new chlorine leaching) and copper output up q/q (driven by higher ore volumes and Bystrinsky ramp-up). The release reiterates 2021 guidance; no aluminium-specific information is provided. | Nornickel announced preliminary 4Q and FY2020 production results and 2021 outlook. Nickel output rose q/q on refinery ramp-up at Kola MMC with new chlorine-leaching technology. Copper output also increased q/q, driven by ramp-up at Bystrinsky (Chita) and higher grades/shipment. Guidance reiterated for 2021. | Norilsk Nickel announced its 4Q20 and full-year 2020 production results, noting it met 2020 guidance and reiterated 2021 outlook. Nickel output increased 13% q-o-q to 68 kt, while copper rose 8% q-o-q to 129 kt, driven by ramp-ups and operational factors at its Russian assets.

Indonesia’s Antam aims to produce 50,000–100,000 tonnes/year of nickel sulphate from its planned HPAL plant in 2024, alongside 120,000–240,000 tonnes/year of battery precursors and cathodes. The metals and chemicals target domestic and export EV demand, supported by state-led ventures.

A Manitoba judge ordered Urbanmine to pay Vale for buying and reselling nickel stolen from Vale’s site in Thompson, Man. The case involves about 483,396 pounds of nickel and prior resale to other metals traders. The ruling is restitution/liability focused, with no clear immediate nickel supply-market disruption. | SMM says high-grade NPI prices rose to 1,120 yuan/mtu, while the ferronickel market was quiet. Costs jumped as nickel ore in the Philippines stayed expensive and thermal coal/coke prices increased, lifting ferronickel costs ~50 yuan/mtu. Many ferronickel plants are losing money and some may halt output due to low ore inventories. | Myanmar’s military detained senior officials and declared a one-year state of emergency, with communication disruptions. SMM notes CNICO’s Dagonshan nickel project is currently in normal production, but political uncertainty and potential logistics disruption create near-term nickel supply risk. Tin ore flows are reported as stable with no impact so far. | Myanmar’s military detained senior officials and declared a one-year state of emergency, accompanied by widespread communications interruptions. SMM notes CNICO’s Dagonshan nickel mining and ferronickel smelting project is currently producing normally, but instability could still affect near-term nickel supply expectations. Tin ore logistics appear stable for now. | Nickel Rock Resources announced it completed expansion of the Hard Nickel Project by acquiring additional mineral claims and reporting highly elevated nickel-bearing samples on nearby ground with similar geology to the FPX Decar deposit. The company plans further 2021 exploration, aiming to advance potential battery-grade nickel-iron alloy awaruite. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. announced an update to the preliminary economic assessment and mineral resource estimate for its Kenbridge nickel deposit in Ontario, with work to be completed in Q2 2021 and a future bankable feasibility study planned. The release is focused on project evaluation rather than immediate output or trade flows. | New Caledonia’s coalition government collapsed as pro-independence politicians resigned amid riots over the sale process of Vale’s local nickel business. Protesters allege a locally led offer was overlooked. As the world’s fourth-largest nickel producer, the unrest and asset-sale uncertainty raise near-term nickel supply concerns.

New Caledonia’s multi-party government collapsed amid debate over the future of nickel mining and smelting, including the planned sale of Vale Nouvelle-Calédonie. The independence movement opposes the deal, and protests delayed the process, increasing near-term uncertainty over nickel industry plans and potentially supply continuity. | Shanghai copper slid to an eight-week low and London copper also fell as traders priced weaker near-term demand ahead of China’s Lunar New Year. A coronavirus outbreak dampened January economic activity, dragging output and services. Nickel declined as well, suggesting broader risk-off in industrial metals. | Glencore said 2020 own-sourced copper production was 1.26 million tonnes, 8% lower than 2019, and nickel output fell to 110,200 tonnes, 9% lower. The company noted Mutanda is on care and maintenance and said 2021 guidance remains current, implying no immediate supply rebound.

Riots and a New Caledonia government collapse, tied to disputes over Vale’s nickel asset sale, raise the risk of disruptions at a major (~9% of global) nickel supplier. With China already tight on stocks, analysts say further supply fears and supported demand could keep LME nickel prices well-supported in coming weeks. | Copper rose as markets priced more potential U.S. COVID-19 stimulus and improving U.S. economic indicators. A firm U.S. dollar capped gains. The piece also notes LME aluminium, nickel (and zinc) moving higher, reflecting broader bullish industrial-metals sentiment tied to recovery hopes. | Talon Metals released an updated preliminary economic assessment for its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project, showing higher after-tax NPVs under three nickel offtake scenarios. The report highlights modeled nickel economics at specified nickel and copper prices and cites low costs, but it is a feasibility-stage update, not production. | Margaret Lake Diamonds announced it will acquire the Old Nick and Letain nickel-cobalt projects in British Columbia, with scheduled milestone payments and regulatory approvals. The release is exploratory and does not indicate immediate production or supply disruption, so any effect on near-term nickel prices is likely limited. | Blackstone reports drilling success at the King Snake prospect, intersecting massive sulfide nickel mineralisation across multiple holes. The company is continuing resource work at several Ta Khoa project sites in Vietnam and notes that the Ban Phuc concentrator restart follows success at Ban Chang, supporting positive nickel supply outlook sentiment. | Nornickel said it will run a 2021–2023 program to monitor water bodies and soils near a May 2020 fuel spill and to revegetate contaminated areas. It also plans to restore biological resources and dispose of contaminated sorbents and soils, with the plan filed with Rosprirodnadzor. No production impact is cited. | Nornickel said it plans to cut annual greenhouse gas emissions to below 10 million tonnes by 2030, using a technical upgrade program as part of its ecology strategy. The company is a major refined nickel producer and also produces copper. The announcement is sustainability-focused with no near-term operational change described.

Easpring (per the headline) signed an 8-year contract with a Lygend subsidiary to purchase nickel and cobalt raw materials. While the excerpt lacks volume details, the long-term procurement suggests firmer demand outlook for nickel feedstock, which may support nickel prices in the coming days to weeks. | SMM data shows nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 17,000 wmt to 8.08 million wmt between Jan 29 and Feb 5. In nickel content terms, stocks dropped 100 mt to 63,700 mt. While inventories at seven major ports rose, the all-port total declined. | Fastmarkets’ live futures report notes nickel rising to $17,900/t, implying bullish near-term price pressure for nickel. Most other text discusses publication timing changes and corrections to Fastmarkets’ aluminium-related assessment methodologies/differentials, without clear evidence of immediate aluminium price moves. Zinc is mentioned only in passing. | A Russian court in Krasnoyarsk ordered JSC Norilsk-Taymyr Energy Company, a Nornickel subsidiary, to pay 146 billion rubles (~$2 billion) for environmental damages. Rosprirodnadzor cited urgent inspections, 139 violations, and equipment age/lack of monitoring, following earlier oil/product spills near Nadezhinsky facilities. | Russia’s regulator upheld a multi-billion-ruble environmental penalty against Nornickel’s Norilsk-Taimyr Energy Company for a large fuel spill at Norilsk CHPP-3. More than 20,000 tonnes of oil products were released. The court awarded about 146.2 bln rubles, with Nornickel likely to appeal and disputed damage estimates. | Copper rose 1.4% on LME amid hopes that vaccine rollout and U.S. stimulus will lift industrial demand. The move was tempered by a stronger U.S. dollar. Copper inventories in LME warehouses are at multi-year lows. The article also notes gains in aluminium and nickel, reflecting broadly bullish sentiment. | Tesla proposed working with Indonesia to build EV battery supply chains, including potential cooperation on energy storage, and emphasized technology transfer beyond raw-materials extraction. Indonesia is highlighted as having the world’s largest nickel reserves. Such battery-supply expectations could increase near-term nickel demand sentiment. | Copper prices rose 1.9% on Friday, supported by a weaker U.S. dollar and hopes for stimulus-driven demand, alongside tight LME inventories. Warehousing stocks hit the lowest since 2008 and canceled warrants were elevated. LME aluminum and nickel also gained as broader metals sentiment turned bullish. | A Russian court ordered Norilsk Nickel to pay a record 146 billion rubles (about US$1.94 billion) for a massive Arctic fuel spill last May. The ruling adds significant financial and regulatory risk to the world’s largest nickel producer, potentially pressuring nickel sentiment, though no specific output reduction is cited. | Norilsk Nickel’s unit was fined after an oil products spill at the Norilsk TPP-3. The watchdog confirmed the company paid a 1 mln ruble administrative fine, while a court partially satisfied a much larger 148 bln ruble damages claim against NTEK. Norilsk Nickel disputes the damage estimates.

Copper prices rose around 1% after optimism over a potential $1.9 trillion U.S. COVID-19 stimulus package boosted expectations for metals demand and economic recovery. Tight nearby supply was suggested by a higher LME cash premium. Aluminium and nickel also increased modestly alongside copper moves. | Canada Nickel reportedly held talks with the U.S. government regarding supplying metal for EV batteries. Such discussions can affect near-term expectations for nickel supply and procurement demand linked to battery markets. With limited detail in the provided text, the likely market impact is modestly supportive for nickel. | Canada Nickel’s CEO said the company has held talks with U.S. government officials about supplying nickel for electric-vehicle batteries amid concern over China’s dominance. U.S. agencies have sought input on the miner’s ability to supply critical minerals and offered introductions to financing. Nickel demand is expected to rise as EV adoption grows. | UK-based Kabanga Nickel signed a landmark agreement with Tanzania to develop the world’s largest nickel deposit, forming Tembo Nickel Corporation to mine and process/refine Class 1 nickel with copper and cobalt by-products. The deal boosts investor confidence, but near-term production impacts appear limited, so nickel price effects likely small.

Talon Metals said drill hole 20TK0277 intersected 110.65 meters of semi-massive and disseminated sulphide mineralization at the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project in Minnesota, extending the semi-massive sulphide unit. Assays for this hole remain pending, though prior holes show Ni and Cu grades. | Renforth Resources reported drill results from its Surimeau district Nickel/VMS prospect, including 0.483% Ni over 1m and 0.156% Ni over 13m, plus intervals with elevated Cu (and Zn). The release is an exploration assay update, not a confirmed production change, so near-term market effects are limited. | Nickel prices have gained substantially over the past year and are rising toward the US$9/lb or US$20,000/tonne level that analysts say could stimulate investment in new supply. Despite the climb, the piece notes nickel has faced ongoing issues, implying some uncertainty about continued gains. | Nickel 28 says independent analysis confirms its Ramu nickel-cobalt operation is among the lowest GHG emitters in nickel production, with scope 1 and 2 intensity averaging 15.6 tCO2e/tonne of nickel in MHP. It also explores further reductions by eliminating on-site electricity generation with renewables. | White Metal reports progress on its reverse-circulation drilling program at the Taranis copper-silver project in Namibia, aiming to update an NI 43-101 compliant resource estimate. Management cites the recent rise in copper prices as a reason to continue exploration, but no immediate copper supply or physical flows are changed. | Canada Nickel CEO Mark Selby says the Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project near Timmins could be the largest since the 1970s, citing a large resource estimate and ongoing feasibility and processing-site studies. He frames EV and stainless demand as strong, implying future nickel supply growth.

Canada Nickel’s Crawford sulphide project near Timmins is presented as a potential global-scale nickel discovery, with large resources, planned feasibility this year, permitting thereafter, and initial production expected in 2023 and ramp-up to commercial by 2025. The project’s low-carbon positioning and EV-driven nickel demand support a bullish nickel outlook. | London copper rose to an eight-year high on Wednesday, driven by tight supplies, more buyers than sellers and low inventories, plus a reported copper concentrate shortfall. Optimism over further U.S. coronavirus stimulus improved sentiment for base metals demand. The article also notes increases in aluminum and nickel prices. | SMM data shows nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 34,000 wmt to 8.04 million wmt between Feb 5 and Feb 10. Stocks in nickel content declined 300 mt to 63,400 mt. Inventories at seven major ports also dropped 24,000 wmt to 6.44 million wmt. | Nickel Rock Resources announced it filed a notice of work application for the multi-year Hard Nickel Project, including trenching and diamond drilling. The claims are adjacent to FPX Nickel’s Decar project and target nickel-iron alloy mineralization. The release discusses EV demand and carbon-reducing processing prospects, but no output is provided. | Victory Nickel announced it sold its Minago nickel property and restructured its secured debt with Silver Elephant, including a debt reduction and equity-based consideration. The company also plans to return to active nickel exploration. The news is company-specific and does not signal immediate global supply or demand changes. | Silver Elephant Mining completed its acquisition of the Minago nickel sulphide project in Manitoba from Victory Nickel. The project hosts the Nose nickel deposit and other drilled discoveries. Deal terms include consideration shares and a nickel-linked issuance trigger if nickel exceeds US$10. No immediate production changes are stated. | Industrial metals rose as markets bet on global economic recovery, boosting demand expectations. Copper reportedly hit its highest level since 2013/ eight-year high, while platinum also surged. The piece links the rally to supply-demand balance, citing LME/Comex copper stocks near decade lows. | Gold rose for a fourth session as the U.S. dollar weakened and markets priced another U.S. stimulus package. The same optimism for economic recovery lifted industrial metals, with copper reaching an eight-year high and platinum hitting a multi-year peak, supported by expectations for stronger global demand.

ANZ Bank and Morgan Stanley expect copper tightness to persist: low inventories, declining bonded stocks, weak concentrate supply, and short-term Peru/Chile shipment disruptions could keep the market in deficit despite seasonal demand weakness. Separately, Macquarie’s EV sales recovery boosts expectations for higher nickel demand for batteries, supporting prices. | London copper hovered near an eight-year high, with prices supported by a weaker U.S. dollar tied to growth-relief hopes and softer inflation. Trading was thin due to China’s Lunar New Year. Aluminium fell, nickel slipped slightly, and nickel cathode and shipment fundamentals were also cited. | Copper is hovering near an eight-year high after several days of gains as China enters its Lunar New Year holiday, reducing trading activity. Falling LME and Shanghai inventories, a cash premium versus the three-month contract, and slowed Chile cathode shipments point to tight nearby supply and support bullish sentiment. | The Philippine Nickel Industry Association expects a strong 2021 for the local nickel sector, citing improving foreign demand despite COVID-19 disruptions in 2020. Export values rose even as output fell, attributed to steady world nickel prices led by China’s demand and growth across infrastructure, appliances and EVs.

Canada Nickel says its Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project near Timmins is the largest nickel sulphide discovery since the early 1970s and is being aggressively advanced. The company plans studies, explores use of Glencore’s Kidd concentrator for processing, and frames future demand from EVs, supporting bullish nickel sentiment. | Canada Nickel’s CEO says the Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide discovery near Timmins is being aggressively advanced, potentially feeding a large open-pit mine and processing plans using existing Timmins infrastructure. The company frames nickel demand from EVs and stainless steel as growing, supporting upbeat nickel market sentiment.

Copper surged to its highest level since 2012 as investors bought on inflation/reflation concerns and improving Chinese demand, alongside evidence of tightening supply. LME three-month prices rose, cash premiums increased, and inventories fell 44% in two months. Nickel also climbed to multi-year highs; aluminium edged down slightly.

Ivanplats (Ivanhoe Mines) signed a non-binding US$300m gold/palladium/platinum streaming term sheet and arranged up to US$120m senior project debt to fund development of the Platreef project in South Africa. Shaft 1 changeover and detailed engineering progress toward eventual production of nickel and copper. | Norilsk Nickel said 2020 net profit fell 39% after setting aside $2 billion for an environmental damage claim tied to a major Arctic fuel spill. The court fine over diesel leakage remains before being effective. Nornickel expects 2021 capex of $3.0–$3.4 billion, with final dividend later. | A Russian court upheld a record 146 billion ruble (£1.4bn) fine on Norilsk Nickel tied to a May 2020 oil spill near its Nadezhdinski Metallurgical Plant. Authorities blamed operational failures and tank depressurisation. The company plans to review the decision and may appeal before paying within a month. | Premium Nickel Resources was selected as preferred bidder to acquire Botswana BCL’s former Selebi-Phikwe, Tati Phoenix, and Selkirk Ni-Cu-Co assets after a technical and government review. The deal moves to documentation and six-month exclusivity for due diligence, implying potential future supply but no immediate production changes. | Copper rallied to $8,437/t on the LME, its highest since May 2012, with low stocks, tightening spreads, and fund positioning flattening despite price gains. Options open interest shows increasing demand for downside puts as liquidity deteriorates, signaling correction risk. Nickel is mentioned as also being hedged via put structures. | Premium Nickel Resources was selected as the preferred bidder to acquire Botswana Ni-Cu-Co assets from BCL’s liquidation process. The selection starts a six-month exclusivity period for due diligence and negotiations. This can modestly lift sentiment toward nickel and copper supply prospects, though no immediate production changes are confirmed. | Ivanplats signed a non-binding term sheet for a US$300m gold/palladium/platinum streaming facility and arranged up to US$120m of senior project debt to advance the Platreef mine. The project development includes nickel and copper and targets initial production after engineering and shaft changeovers, supporting mild bullish sentiment despite non-binding terms. | The Northern Miner Podcast episode features CIM Sudbury speakers discussing how Canadian low-grade nickel resources could help meet future electric-vehicle battery supply needs. It profiles companies and project experience around nickel-cobalt development, but provides no concrete near-term supply, policy, or inventory changes.

LME benchmark copper edged higher toward a nine-year peak as optimism over global economic recovery supported metals demand despite a firmer U.S. dollar. Analysts at Goldman Sachs and Citi lifted copper targets. Smelting activity fell in January and China’s futures market reopened after Lunar New Year, reinforcing a tight supply outlook. | Canada Nickel and Noble Mineral Exploration entered a binding LOI to option and consolidate additional properties around the MacDiarmid township nickel target. The exploration program identified a >1.8 km long, ~400 m wide target said to be similar quality to the earlier Crawford discovery and ~15% larger. | Grid Metals announced it received an NI 43-101 technical report for its Bannockburn nickel sulphide property near Timmins, Ontario. The report compiles 84 drill holes, highlights Mount Keith-type disseminated nickel mineralization and heazlewoodite-rich grades, and recommends further drilling and metallurgical work. No production or immediate supply changes were announced. | Norilsk Nickel faces a record $2.1 billion fine from Russia’s environmental regulator after a diesel spill caused damage to Arctic rivers and soil. The company says it will not appeal. The firm’s broader ecological overhaul includes shutting nickel smelting capacity and a planned copper smelter closure, affecting supply expectations.

Fastmarkets’/Metal Bulletin coverage says US and Europe nickel premiums are steady amid Chinese holidays, suggesting muted immediate price movement. It also notes procedural delays and corrections to European low-carbon aluminium differential assessments and some aluminium billet premium data, mainly addressing publication errors rather than changing fundamentals. | Glencore reported strong 2020 results and reinstated a dividend, citing strong commodity prices and possible further payouts if prices remain firm. Its Sudbury operations produce nickel and copper, and Glencore’s trading benefited from strong copper and nickel markets. No specific new supply or inventory change is announced. | SMM data show nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 169,000 wmt from Feb 10 to 7.87 million wmt by Feb 18. In nickel content, stocks dropped 1,300 mt to 62,100 mt. Stocks at seven major ports also declined to 6.27 million wmt. | SMM reports that refined nickel stocks in Shanghai bonded warehouses were unchanged at 17,600 mt as of Feb 18 during the CNY holiday. The import window stayed closed due to tepid foreign trade, and bonded warehouses suspended operations, with no arrivals under long-term contracts—supporting stable inventory levels. | Teck reported a quarterly loss driven by non-cash writedowns tied to oil sands bitumen expectations, but highlighted improving copper performance. It said Quebrada Blanca Phase 2 copper expansion reached 40% completion and copper revenues and sales volumes rose year-on-year, supporting near-term copper supply outlook versus prior expectations. | Renforth Resources reports lab results on Surimeau grab samples showing an average of 68% sulphide nickel, with most samples indicating sulphide nickel as the dominant form and conventional milling recoverability. The company frames this as a large-scale, battery-grade nickel discovery and continues related assay work. | Blackstone Minerals reports continued drilling success at its King Snake massive sulfide prospect, with all five new holes intersecting MSV mineralisation and a defined strike length over 800m. The company sees significant potential for the project to add to nickel MSV inventory and potentially support restart of the Ban Phuc concentrator. Assays are due soon.

Norilsk Nickel said it will not appeal a Krasnoyarsk arbitrazh court ruling related to a diesel fuel spill at its HPP-3 in Norilsk in May 2020. Management will comply with the judgment, citing legal advice. The release does not mention penalties or operational effects, suggesting limited immediate impact on nickel and copper markets.

London copper extended gains Monday, crossing $9,000/t for the first time since Sept 2011, with LME three-month up as much as 3.3% to $9,199/t. Moves were driven by a weaker dollar and hopes for stronger China demand after Lunar New Year, alongside limited supplies, supportive liquidity, and disruptions. | Copper surged to a nine-year high on LME above $9,000/t, up about 3% for the three-month contract, citing supply concerns and China demand hopes. Domestic copper futures rose similarly. Nickel also topped $20,000/t, while aluminium (and zinc) were higher, reflecting broad base-metal momentum. | LME copper surged above $9,000/ton and three-month nickel rose above $20,000/ton, both near multi-year highs, extending a coordinated commodity rally. The piece attributes gains to weak USD, recovery optimism, China demand, copper concentrate supply issues, and very low LME stocks. Hindalco’s investor day coincides with shares rising. | LME copper jumped above $9,000/ton and three-month nickel rose above $20,000, extending a coordinated commodity rally. The article cites a decade-largest deficit, high risk of scarcity, copper concentrate supply issues, strong Chinese demand, and very low LME stocks, supporting higher near-term prices. Hindalco’s investor day and stock strength add bullish sentiment. | Singapore froze bank accounts of Envy Global Trading and Envy Asset Management after an investigation into alleged fraud involving funds meant for nickel trading. The trader says accounts are frozen but customer funds are protected. The case follows prior scandals and comes as nickel has rallied sharply this year. | Base metals rallied as copper surged above $9,000/t at the LME, its highest since 2011, driven by supply concerns and post–Chinese New Year demand hopes from China. Nickel also rose above $20,000/t, while aluminium increased on broader growth and stimulus expectations alongside a weaker dollar.

Bitterroot Resources’ subsidiary signed a minerals lease and purchase option for additional 80 acres adjacent to its LM Project in Michigan, where drilling targets magmatic nickel-copper sulphides. The deal expands exploration acreage for mid-2021 drilling but does not change current production or confirmed supply, limiting short-term price impact. | Bitterroot Resources’ Michigan subsidiary secured an additional 80 acres of mineral rights at the LM Project in Michigan, adjoining areas drilled for magmatic nickel-copper sulphides. The company plans to drill-test the added lands mid-2021, but no production or major supply change is announced.

Copper prices in London retreated from a 9-1/2-year high after a sharp four-day rally (+9.2%). LME three-month copper slipped 0.3% and Shanghai copper rose modestly. Analysts cited tight supplies, reduced Chinese smelter output, and a recovering demand outlook. Aluminium and nickel moved slightly too. | SMM reports Shanghai nonferrous prices mostly fell as investors turned cautious, after Jerome Powell’s testimony suggested policy would remain supportive and inflation concerns were softer. Copper was the exception, rising 0.53%, while aluminium slipped 0.48% and nickel dropped 2%. Markets also watched inventories and LME SHFE copper signals. | Nickel Rock Resources closed the acquisition of two 100%-owned nickel exploration properties in British Columbia, subject to a 2% NSR, issuing shares and paying consideration to the vendor. The company filed an NI 43-101 technical report and plans detailed exploration, but no immediate production or supply disruption is indicated. | Indonesia’s nickel miners’ association says mining activity is normalizing and nickel production and sales are expected to rise in 2021 as vaccination recovery boosts operations. APNI members sold 40 million tonnes of nickel ore to smelters in 2020. With ore exports closed and output sold domestically, supplies are expected to increase. | Nornickel partially suspended operations at its Oktyabrsky and Taimyrsky mines after detecting increased groundwater inflow and water entering connected underground workings. The company is installing a cofferdam and draining to eliminate flooding, and will reassess production impacts after relief operations conclude. The disruption can reduce near-term nickel and copper supply. | Norilsk Nickel partially suspended operations at the Oktyabrsky and Taimyrsky mines after increased groundwater inflow caused flooding risk. The firm is eliminating water and installing a cofferdam at Taimyrsky to stop further inflow. It will reassess production guidance after relief operations conclude, implying near-term supply loss for nickel and copper.

Indonesia’s nickel ore market is recovering as COVID-19 vaccination and production normalization are expected to boost nickel mining output and domestic sales. APNI reports substantial ore sales to smelters and notes nickel ore export bans that keep supply within Indonesia. Authorities expect output and sales to rise in 2021 amid higher ore prices. | Shanghai copper surged toward a near 10-year high after the U.S. Fed signaled it would keep policy loose longer. The most-traded copper contract rose sharply, while LME copper hit multi-year highs. Aluminum also jumped, and the piece notes nickel gains alongside the broad base-metals rally. | Copper on Shanghai and LME jumped toward decade highs after Fed Chair Jerome Powell said it may take more than three years to meet inflation goals, implying rates will stay low longer. Aluminum and nickel also rose. The supportive monetary outlook calmed fears of earlier tightening, lifting base metals. | Minnesota’s Supreme Court reversed an appeals court decision involving PolyMet’s air emissions permit for the planned copper-nickel mine near Babbitt and Hoyt Lakes, sending the case back for further review. Tsingshan Holding signed pacts to supply a total of 100,000 tonnes of nickel matte—60,000 tonnes to Huayou and 40,000 tonnes to CNGR—within a year starting October 2021. The deals start October 2021 and build on trial nickel-matte production, supporting nickel demand expectations. | SMM reports that after the holiday, nickel ore and ferronickel prices rose sharply. Copper rose 1.68% and aluminium surged 4.16%, supported by a weaker US dollar and expectations of continued central bank stimulus. Nickel increased marginally (0.17%). Mixed oil-price pressure was noted but net moves were positive. | The article headline says Nornickel plans to stabilize water inflows at one of its mines by next week. Improving water management typically lowers the risk of production interruptions at nickel operations, supporting near-term nickel supply expectations and modestly underpinning nickel prices over coming days. | Group Ten Metals reported 2020 drilling results at the Stillwater West project, highlighting long, continuous nickel and copper sulphide mineralization with strong grade-thickness and co-products. Tsingshan Group signed agreements to supply 60,000 mt of high-grade nickel matte to Huayou Cobalt and 40,000 mt to CNGR over the following year starting October 2021. Tsingshan also expects rising Indonesia nickel-equivalent output and adjusts matte/ferronickel volumes to market demand, supporting nickel supply-demand expectations for batteries. | The article reports a “bullish return from holidays” for China’s nickel market, with Chinese nickel premiums rising 9.5%. This suggests improved short-term demand and tighter pricing expectations for nickel in the regional physical market, likely supporting near-term nickel prices. | Group Ten Metals announced drill results from the Stillwater West Chrome Mountain target in Montana, reporting continuous nickel and copper sulphide mineralization, including high-grade nickel-tenor intervals and associated copper. Preliminary metallurgical work also supports flotation recoverability. The findings strengthen expectations for future battery-metal supply from the project. | Group Ten Metals reported 2020 drill results from the Stillwater West project in Montana, intercepting wide nickel and copper sulphide mineralization with higher-grade zones, plus PGE co-products. While positive for nickel/copper sentiment, it is an exploration update without near-term supply changes, so near-term price impact is limited. | China stainless steel futures fell 6% to their daily limit as nickel prices on the Shanghai Futures Exchange dropped 6%. Nickel slid on supply glut concerns after Tsingshan claimed stable nickel matte supply, denting sentiment. Analysts expect a possible rebound later if stainless fundamentals improve. | London and Shanghai nickel prices dropped sharply to multi-week lows as investors worried about oversupply in nickel battery materials. The selloff was linked to Tsingshan’s plan to supply 100,000 tonnes of nickel matte to Huayou Cobalt and CNGR, which could ease a perceived battery nickel shortage. | Industrial metals fell sharply after nickel prices collapsed for a second day following a spike driven by speculative inflows. Copper dropped nearly 5% and aluminum was also lower as investors turned jittery with rising U.S. yields, weaker equities, a stronger dollar, and concern over supply outlook and positioning. | Canada Nickel says metallurgical tests at its Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project show a 20% increase in primary grind size with no loss in recovery, reducing energy use per tonne and enabling higher throughput. The PEA is delayed to April 30, 2021 to incorporate improvements. It does not imply near-term production changes. | Nickel Rock Resources filed an NI 43-101 technical report for its Nickel Project targeting awaruite nickel-iron alloy, with planned 2021 exploration (trenching, sampling, diamond drilling). The property is near FPX Nickel’s Decar project. The news relates to nickel resource potential but offers no immediate production changes. | Class 1 Nickel reports completion and planning of drilling and permitting activities at its Somanike project in Quebec and Alexo-Dundonald project near Timmins, Ontario. Such changes can influence expectations for nickel output and investment in the near term. However, the excerpt provides no specifics on volumes or timelines, limiting certainty on price direction. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous metals fell on Mar 4, with copper down 1.95%, aluminium down 1.64%, and nickel down 6%. Aluminium stocks rose across key consumption areas, indicating ongoing inventory pressure. The nickel drop is part of broad market weakness. Macro drivers include rising US Treasury yields.

Industrial metals fell as nickel prices collapsed for a second day, with refined nickel dropping more than 10% in two days. The sell-off followed eased shortage fears after a Chinese producer’s plan to add supply. Risk-off sentiment from higher U.S. bond yields also pressured copper, which fell up to 5% intraday. | SMM reports broad declines in SHFE nonferrous prices on Mar 5. Copper fell 1.76% and aluminium declined 1.68%, while nickel tumbled 6.7% on the day, with mixed open-interest changes. The piece ties sentiment to macro moves and market expectations, but overall closes bearish. | SMM data show Chinese nickel ore inventories fell 161,000 wmt from Feb 26 to 7.1 million wmt as of Mar 5. In nickel content terms, stocks dropped 1,300 mt to 56,000 mt, while inventories at seven major ports declined 51,000 wmt to 5.85 million wmt, implying tighter supply. | SMM data show Shanghai bonded refined nickel inventories fell 1,700 mt to 16,700 mt as of March 5. Stocks dropped after the holiday amid a sharp rise in nickel prices and an opened import window. Russian nickel and briquettes saw transactions, with further exports expected, likely keeping stocks declining near term. | Fortune Nickel and Gold begins an airborne geophysical review for drill targeting at its Beck-Ottaway nickel sulphide project near Canada Nickel’s Crawford deposit. The company expects maps and reports within four weeks and references prior drill intersections of nickel and anomalous copper associated with sulphides and veins. | Nickel prices plunged about 16% in two days to a three-month low after Tsingshan announced contracts to deliver nickel matte to Chinese battery materials suppliers. Analysts say the route could loosen balances or weaken the higher-price investment case, while positioning is stretched, amplifying investor sell-offs. | ArrowMetals announces a nickel sulfate tolling deal covering Q3 2021 production. The news implies changes to nickel sulfate processing and supply volumes, which can affect expectations for near-term availability along nickel chemical/conversion supply chains and potentially support nickel prices. | Tesla is named as a technical partner in a deal to take over Vale’s New Caledonia nickel mine after political turmoil and protests. The agreement gives local interests a 51% stake and keeps key processing rights, while Tesla aims to secure nickel for EV battery demand, which is expected to rise. | Bloomberg reports Tsingshan is preparing to deliver nickel matte made from nickel pig iron to battery-material producers, potentially enabling battery-grade performance with lower-cost feedstock. With battery demand fears already supporting nickel prices, the prospect of added supply drove a sharp selloff, with London futures down 14% in two days. | Tsingshan appears poised to supply battery-grade nickel products by refining nickel pig iron into nickel matte, potentially reducing a perceived nickel supply deficit tied to EV demand. The market reaction has been sharp: London nickel futures plunged 14% in two days, reflecting expectations of increased nickel availability. | Tesla will act as a technical adviser for New Caledonia’s nickel mine amid conflict, aiming to secure sufficient battery-metal supply. The move signals stronger involvement by an EV maker in nickel sourcing, potentially tightening perceived supply risk and supporting near-term nickel price sentiment.

Tsingshan’s announcement to sell 75kt of nickel and ramp production sparks fears of nickel/NPI oversupply, weighing on LME nickel. It also points to higher China copper wire/product inventories as a demand drag. Separately, China’s aluminium capacity-curb commitment is expected to slow supply growth, lifting premiums and supporting aluminium. | London copper prices dipped 0.1% after a strong rally to multi-year highs, with traders saying the U.S. stimulus progress was largely priced in. LME aluminium (-0.3%) and nickel (-1%) also fell. China’s copper imports rose in January-February, but investors stayed cautious at current levels. | SMM reports China’s refined nickel output rose 5.33% month-on-month to 13,700 mt in February, with most production attributed to the Gansu and Xinjiang smelters while other plants stayed suspended. March output is expected to climb further as Tianjin resumes and Jilin restores late-March lines. | Copper prices rose after the U.S. Senate approved a $1.9 trillion stimulus and China increased copper imports, even as exports hit record levels. Lower China treatment charges signaled tight concentrate feedstock, while LME copper stocks rose. Citi turned more bullish, forecasting further gains toward $10,500 within three months. | Views across commodities highlight nickel weakness after Tsingshan disclosed plans to start producing nickel-in-matte from Indonesian satellite ore for EV battery sulphate, triggering a sharp LME sell-off and making rebounds unlikely. Aluminium is described as having limited immediate physical-market impact, though China energy-control measures could raise future disruption risks. | Expert commentary covers nickel and aluminium. Nickel is pressured after Tsingshan indicated it will produce more nickel-in-matte from satellite ore for conversion to sulphate, creating uncertainty about long-run support, though supply disruptions at Norilsk cap downside. Aluminium gains are tempered by China energy control measures in Inner Mongolia affecting smelting fees and new approvals.

Copper edged down in London on Tuesday as a firm dollar, supported by higher U.S. yields and faster recovery expectations, weighed on dollar-priced metals. LME three-month copper fell 0.7% to $8,935/t. Aluminium and nickel also declined, while copper inventories rose to high levels. | SMM reports that Tsingshan’s announcement on supplying high-grade nickel matte has disrupted the nickel market, pressuring nickel futures lower. The write-up argues that if matte supply expands, nickel-briquette premiums could ease back toward precursor pricing, weakening price support from NPI and sulphate-linked feedstocks. | SMM reports a weekly drop in SHFE nickel futures, which spilled into spot NPI and stainless steel. High-grade NPI benchmark prices fell, spot transactions weakened, and NPI plants faced cost pressure and potential hedged-cargo selling. Stainless steel prices slid on weaker nickel sentiment, though a short-term rebound is possible if prices stabilize. | Fastmarkets/Fastmarkets’ live futures update reports that LME aluminium prices are down 1% on the day and that nickel is trading below $16,000/t. The report is therefore directly tied to immediate market pricing for aluminium and nickel, signaling bearish momentum over the next days. | Live futures commentary reports LME aluminium falling 1% and nickel trading below $16,000/t. The remainder of the text focuses mainly on Fastmarkets methodology/publication corrections rather than new supply-demand drivers, but the headline pricing indicates immediate bearish movement in aluminium and nickel. | Norilsk Nickel said it is continuing work to stop groundwater inflow at Oktiabrsky and Taymir mines. One mine working has been filled with hardening backfill, and regulators’ inspections are underway. The Norilsk Concentrator remains fully idled while repairs proceed, with relaunch timing to be decided after inspections around March 16. | SMM reports that most SHFE nonferrous metals fell on Tuesday. Copper slid 0.68% as a firmer US dollar weighed on prices. Aluminium dropped 2.13%. Nickel plunged 3.81% amid wider risk-off/commodity weakness. Other base metals were mixed, with zinc slightly higher. | Norilsk Nickel said it expects another week before deciding when to restart two Siberian waterlogged mines. It is also idling the Norilsk Concentrator after an accident and plans only partial restoration by March 16.
