Fitch expected softer fiscal consolidation. LME copper was down 1.4%, with aluminium and nickel also falling amid weaker near-term demand sentiment. | Norilsk Nickel said it needs about another week to stop groundwater inflow at two Siberia mines before assessing restart timing. Macquarie and Citi expect a copper deficit and calls on scrap to support higher prices, with Citi raising near-term copper targets. Gold is discussed as capped, but aluminium is not. | Macquarie upgraded its copper balance to a sizable refined deficit and lifted 2021-2023 price forecasts, citing strong demand rebound, thin inventories, and a large scrap “call.” For nickel, Tsingshan’s plan to expand contained output and convert to class 2-to-class 1 is described as triggering positioning exits and potential oversupply, pushing prices lower. | SMM reports most Shanghai base metals fell on Wednesday Mar 10. SHFE copper eased 1.95% to 65,400 yuan/mt, aluminium slipped 0.06% to 17,185 yuan/mt, and nickel dropped 2.05% to 119,770 yuan/mt, signaling broad near-term bearish price pressure in these contracts. | Norilsk Nickel confirmed it paid about $2.5 billion to Russia after a massive Arctic fuel spill in 2020, which triggered Russia’s largest environmental fine. The company did not appeal and said the incident hurt 2020 profit. Ongoing fixes and mine/plant issues could imply near-term nickel supply and cost pressure. | Norilsk Nickel has paid a record $2bn fine after a May Arctic diesel spill from its facilities in Siberia. The incident, linked to melting permafrost and safety failures, cut 2020 profits and implies significant ongoing cleanup and environmental spending. Nickel supply-cost concerns could weigh on prices in coming days.

Copper rose 0.6% on the LME and 0.5% on the Shanghai market as U.S. lawmakers moved toward passing a $1.9 trillion stimulus bill. Separately, Chilean mine workers at Antofagasta’s Los Pelambres voted to reject a contract, raising strike supply risk. | Copper prices rose sharply on Thursday toward 10-year highs on supportive macro sentiment, including U.S. stimulus approval, benign U.S. inflation, and stronger-than-expected Chinese lending and lending-linked demand indicators. The article also mentions aluminium and nickel rising alongside copper, though some analysts warn China demand may be overstated. | Playfair Mining reports a new copper target near the former Rødalen mine in Norway, with a strong copper soil anomaly (MMI) and historical dump samples containing up to 1.81% copper. The program also notes that some targets showed elevated nickel values, but no production is confirmed. | Class 1 Nickel signed an MoU with the Matachewan First Nation to support exploration and development of the Alexo-Dundonald nickel sulphide project near Timmins, Ontario. The company plans to start a Phase 1 drill program, which can improve near-term expectations for nickel supply, with some potential spillover to copper. | Tesla agreed to buy nickel from New Caledonia’s mine following political leaders’ approval of a new ownership deal after violent unrest and Vale’s shutdown. The contract aims to secure battery-grade, more ESG-compliant supply amid broader nickel supply fears and competing Chinese procurement deals, supportive of nickel prices.

Copper prices in London fell as demand concerns and higher supply pressured a recent rally to a near-10-year peak. Aluminium and nickel also edged lower. | SMM reports China’s officials emphasizing increased development and utilization of nickel (and cobalt) resources, citing past supply shortages and current high foreign dependency for nickel raw materials. Copper prices rose to a near two-week high as China’s industrial output growth beat expectations and global supply concerns resurfaced. Aluminium also advanced, but nickel fell during the same session, showing mixed cross-metal moves. | Copper rose on Monday on stronger-than-expected Chinese industrial growth, though trading volumes were thin and investors were cautious ahead of central bank meetings. The story also cites rising Chinese aluminum output and ongoing copper mine labor/supply disruptions in Chile and Peru, supporting bullish sentiment across copper and some spillover to aluminum and nickel. | Copper rose modestly on stronger-than-expected Chinese industrial growth, indicating improving near-term demand expectations, though trading volumes were thin. Aluminum surged after Baotou ordered shutdowns/production curbs to meet energy targets, tightening power-intensive supply. The piece also notes broad base-metals gains, including nickel.

SMM says February production of high-nickel precursor fell 10.8% month on month to 35,000 mt, despite some producers maintaining output and one Zhejiang producer raising high-nickel precursor output after a January supply shortage. Ternary production fell 13.1%. Demand was mixed: motive power demand was positive, but battery-company operating rates were bearish with pre-CNY restocking supporting only some uplift. | Fitch Solutions expects nickel prices to fall from multi-year highs in 2021 as supply increases, helped by the end of the rainy season in the Philippines and resumed mining that feeds China’s NPI facilities. Despite a gradual long-term uptrend, Fitch keeps a bearish near-term view. | Nornickel said water inflow forced partial suspension at the Oktyabrsky and Taimyrsky mines, downgrading 2021 output: nickel -35,000 tonnes, copper -65,000 tonnes, and PGM -710,000 ounces, with restart unlikely for 3-4 months. Aluminum hit a 9-1/2-year high on supply concerns from China power cuts. Nickel also dipped, indicating broad downside pressure across industrial metals.

SMM reports nickel sulphate inventories have been falling, with tight supply of battery-grade material despite adequate nickel briquette feedstock. Morgan Stanley says aluminium supply remains tight, supported by restocking and constrained LME scrap/tonnage despite higher production and falling alumina; shortages and elevated freight keep spot near $2150/t. For nickel, Credit Suisse says Tsingshan’s planned ramp could create a surplus (even if constraints exist), pressuring prices later. | Copper rose on Thursday after the Fed projected rapid U.S. growth while keeping stimulus, a dovish tone that weakened the dollar and made dollar-priced metals cheaper for other-currency holders. Nickel also increased, while aluminium was slightly lower. LME copper inventories hit a local high, but prices still firmed. | Clean Air Metals announced new drill assay results from the Escape Lake Deposit, intersecting Norilsk-style nickel-copper massive sulfides with grades including 4.76% copper and 2.5% nickel over 0.5m, plus meaningful platinum/palladium. Si6 Metals says drilling at its Maibele North nickel deposit in Botswana intersected sulphide mineralisation with anomalous nickel and copper assays, extending the strike length of the system. First Quantum says its Cobre Panama ramp increased production by about 50% and reiterates copper output guidance for 2021-23. The company’s Q4 results benefited from higher realized copper prices and hedges much of 2021 production. Nickel is mentioned via Ravensthorpe, but copper is the focus.

Indonesia will grant export permits for unrefined mineral ores even if refining construction is delayed, aiming to cushion COVID impacts. However, nickel ore is explicitly excluded from the relaxation, keeping the prior export ban in place to support Indonesia’s nickel supply-chain development. This may sustain tighter nickel supply expectations. | SMM reports bearish pressure on nickel, with Russian nickel premiums falling as more material arrived and importers cut prices. Jinchuan premiums rose slightly due to tighter Shanghai supply, but overall Russian premium declines and inventory movements suggest near-term downside risk for nickel prices. | Talon Metals reported record-length drill results at the Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project, intersecting 21.73 meters of mixed massive sulphides grading 6.72% Ni and 2.95% Cu (plus Co and precious metals). The company plans further drilling to extend the high-grade zone, but no production or supply changes were announced. | SMM says SHFE nonferrous metals closed mixed: copper +0.33% and nickel +2.12% rose, while aluminium fell 2.57% to a one-month low before partial recovery. SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulphate (crystal) quotations were cut to 34,000–34,500 yuan/mt, with active selling and most buyers staying sidelined. Despite tight spot availability, premiums narrowed and sellers expect further lower pricing. The article also cites shortfalls in intermediate raw materials and limited briquette dissolution capacity. | Copper prices fell as renewed European coronavirus lockdown fears boosted demand for the U.S. dollar and weighed on global growth sentiment. Nickel also eased on the day, while aluminum premiums rose, indicating regional tightness despite broader macro headwinds. | Premium Nickel Resources said it completed an exclusivity MOU for a six-month due diligence period to acquire former BCL Ni-Cu-Co assets in Botswana, with the company selected as preferred bidder. The work includes environmental, metallurgical, and legal reviews and negotiations for purchase agreements. | North American Nickel reports that Premium Nickel Resources received exclusivity for six months to complete due diligence and negotiate purchase agreements for former BCL Ni-Cu-Co assets in Botswana. The company frames redevelopment as producing critical metals, potentially supporting nickel supply expectations, though timing depends on future agreement and development. | Bitterroot Resources reports a new drill intersection (LM 21-10) of disseminated and blebby sulphides at its Michigan LM project, including chalcopyrite and associated nickel-bearing minerals, with assays expected in 8–10 weeks. It also expands the property via a memorandum. No production or inventory changes are indicated.

Grid Metals Corp. reports completion of additional drilling at its East Bull Lake palladium property and plans to commence ~2,000 meters of drilling at its Bannockburn nickel property in Timmins, targeting the B Zone. The company expects to confirm nickel grades and widths, but no assay results are provided now. | Renforth Resources begins a ~3,500m drill program targeting the Victoria nickel zone on its Surimeau property. London copper rose 1.1% after positive U.S. labor data and faster COVID-19 vaccination rollout boosted optimism for global recovery. LME aluminium and ShFE nickel also advanced, though copper inventories were rising. | SMM data show Shanghai bonded refined nickel stocks fell 1,500 mt week-on-week to 11,300 mt as of Mar 26. Shipments from the bonded zone may slow next week. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous metals mostly rose on Friday. Copper closed flat, but aluminium gained 1.52% and nickel rose 1.93%. The commentary cites supportive US policy signals alongside infrastructure/innovation rhetoric, consistent with improved sentiment for industrial metal demand in the near term. | Mincor Resources secured A$55m ($41.7m) loan funding to fully develop and restart Kambalda Nickel Operations in Western Australia. Management expects first drawdown late in the September 2021 quarter and targets early 2022 production of high-grade nickel sulphides, supporting nickel supply expectations; the project also includes some copper output. | Nornickel reports that its major shareholders agreed to a spin-off of the Bystrinsky project. The move is likely to influence investors’ expectations for the timing and structure of future nickel supply from Nornickel’s asset base, which can affect near-term nickel pricing sentiment within weeks. | Rusal is open to talks with Nornickel’s other major shareholder, Interros, over a proposed change to Nornickel’s dividend policy. The board will review investment strategy and dividends on March 29. Rusal says it needs stronger arguments and wants discussions tied to recent accidents and related risks.

London copper fell 0.7% to around $8,900/ton, driven by higher LME exchange inventories and signals of weakening demand from China, the top consumer. The report also cites LME aluminum down about 1%. Nickel direction is mixed, with ShFE nickel up and ShFE tin higher. | BTU Metals announced expanded land and ongoing IP geophysical surveying near its TNT copper-silver-gold discovery in Ontario’s Red Lake area. Early results suggest a significant sulphide-related chargeability target. The release mainly affects copper exploration sentiment, but it does not provide resource or supply changes, limiting near-term price impact. | Norilsk Nickel’s board asked shareholders to discuss proposed changes to dividend policy as the company plans additional investment in production and environmental protection following a major Arctic fuel spill. The company faced a $2 billion fine and is considering paying dividends based on free cash flow, potentially altering near-term sentiment around nickel. | Nornickel said it has stopped water inflow at the flooded Oktyabrsky mine and is resuming production. Oktyabrsky is set to fully resume in early May, while Taimyrsky is expected to restart in early June. The firm previously cut 2021 nickel and copper output forecasts but the repair timeline improves near-term supply expectations. | Nornickel stopped water flowing into two Siberian Arctic mines, saying the sites remain on track to fully resume production in the coming period. By mitigating immediate operational disruption at a key producer, the move can support nickel supply expectations in the next several weeks. | WA’s Mines Minister will open Mincor’s Cassini underground nickel mine near Kambalda, the first new nickel development in two decades after low nickel prices caused prior mine shutdowns. The $98 million restart aims for first nickel in August and could expand output, boosting nickel market sentiment.

SMM reports nickel prices swinging and then slipping as downstream purchasing stays sluggish and bonded-area demand fails to absorb imported Russian nickel. Shanghai bonded inventories fell to 11,300 mt by March 26. Russian premiums eased while Jinchuan prices rose on smelter financial closure; briquette premiums adjusted as trading remained weak. | Mincor has officially opened the Cassini nickel mine within its Kambalda Nickel Operations in Western Australia. The company says first nickel concentrate is on track for 1Q22 and production will ramp toward 16,000 tpy of nickel-in-concentrate. It supplies concentrate via BHP Nickel West for further processing into nickel sulfate. | Mincor Resources officially opened the Cassini nickel underground mine in Western Australia. The project is expected to provide about 56% of Mincor’s nickel output in its first five years, with first nickel concentrate targeted for early next year and production processed via BHP’s Kambalda concentrator. | Great Atlantic Resources completed a maiden diamond drill program at its Mascarene property in New Brunswick, intersecting narrow sulfide veins including copper sulfides plus magnetite intervals. The site is also associated with historical copper and inferred mafic intrusion-hosted copper-nickel-cobalt potential, but it remains exploratory with no immediate supply change. | North American Nickel Inc. announced a $1.5M non-brokered private placement to fund continued investments, including exploration tied to its nickel projects in Greenland and Ontario and further activities in Morocco. The release is exploratory in nature and does not indicate near-term production, supply disruption, or inventory changes for nickel.

LME copper is poised for its first monthly fall in a year, down around 0.5% on the day and nearly 3.8% month-to-date, pressured by a firmer U.S. dollar and renewed COVID-19 risks in Europe. The piece also cites copper concentrate force majeure and notes nickel weakness and flat aluminium. | Talon Metals reports consistent drilling intercepts of shallow nickel-copper sulphide mineralization in the CGO East zone outside the Tamarack resource area. Multiple drill holes intersect nickel-copper mineralization with mixed and massive sulphides, but assays are pending. The company plans further drilling to delineate additional resources. | SPC Nickel Corp. announced it acquired the remaining 30% interest in the West Graham Ni-Cu property in Ontario, taking full control subject to a 1% net smelter return royalty. The release cites historic nickel and copper resource grades and discusses future evaluation of the Lockerby East portfolio. | SMM reports Shanghai nonferrous prices fell broadly, with SHFE copper -1.1%, aluminium -1.32%, and nickel -2.19%. The decline is attributed to higher US bond yields lifting the dollar, weighing on copper futures. Contracts also saw lower or rising open interest depending on metal, indicating short-term bearish sentiment. | Renforth Resources said it intersected visible nickel, copper, and zinc sulphides in an initial 1,350m drill step-out west at its Victoria West target in Quebec. The result supports expectations for potential copper and nickel mineralization, which may modestly lift short-term sentiment in those metals despite being early-stage. | Vale has completed the sale of its nickel (and cobalt) operations in New Caledonia to a consortium led by Prony, which includes Trafigura. The transaction could affect nickel supply expectations, but the provided excerpt does not disclose production impacts or timelines, making the near-term price effect unclear. | Victory Nickel engaged a financial advisor to pursue restructuring of its unsecured debt. It also previously sold its Minago nickel property and notes a reduction of secured debt and liquidity from transactions with Silver Elephant. The release does not specify production changes, but links to the company’s nickel asset footprint.

Vale has completed the sale of its New Caledonia nickel plant to Prony Resources after political unrest over the deal. Terms were not disclosed, though $1.1 billion will be invested and Vale retains a long-term nickel supply contract. Vale cites support for EV-driven nickel demand; no output disruption is stated. | Vale has completed the sale of its nickel and cobalt operations in New Caledonia to a consortium led by Prony, which includes Trafigura. The report does not suggest any immediate operational changes, so the transaction is unlikely to materially move nickel prices in the next few weeks, though it is supply-relevant. | Vale has completed the sale of its nickel operations in New Caledonia. The announcement is directly tied to nickel supply but contains no information on changes to production levels, disruptions, or near-term supply constraints, so the net effect on nickel prices within days-to-weeks is likely limited. | Vale has sold its stake in Vale New Caledonia, covering the Goro nickel mine and processing plant, to the Prony Resources consortium. The deal includes major funding support, continued offtake for nickel production, and a long-term nickel supply agreement. The transfer is framed as preserving operational continuity and jobs, not changing output immediately.

Copper rose to a two-week high as Chile, the world’s top producer, closed its border amid rising COVID-19 cases, raising supply concerns in a tight market. Three-month LME copper gained up to 3.6%. Aluminium and nickel also increased on broadly supportive industrial-metals sentiment. | A live LME futures report shows over 2% gains for most base metals prices. It specifically mentions aluminium rewarrantings continuing, suggesting ongoing warehouse/physical market activity. Separately, Fastmarkets notes procedural corrections to certain assessment methodologies, but the headline is broadly bullish near-term price action. | Panoramic Resources approved restarting the Savannah Nickel Project in Western Australia after operational strategy reviews. Underground development and production are set to resume in August, with mobilisation in July. The mine will produce nickel and copper (and cobalt) in concentrate, supporting improved near-term supply outlook.

Copper prices fell as China premiums dropped and LME/ShFE inventories rose, pointing to a weaker-than-expected demand pickup in a key consumption season. The move was accompanied by declines in LME and ShFE aluminium and nickel prices, reflecting soft industrial-metal sentiment while traders watch upcoming data and earnings. | Canada Nickel signed an MoU with Taykwa Tagamou Nation to pursue favorable financing terms for the mine equipment fleet for the Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project and enable related training and jobs. The company also references power-grid steps to supply the mine. Better project financing visibility is modestly supportive for nickel. | Talon Metals reports that its first three drill holes in the CGO West target area at the Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project intersected massive sulphide nickel-copper mineralization at shallow depths. Assay results are pending and the work is early-stage exploration intended to potentially expand resources. | SMM reports most SHFE nonferrous metals declined after overnight Wall Street losses: copper fell 0.49% and aluminium 0.46%, while nickel rose 0.37%. It cites stable market sentiment despite IMF upgrades and US job-vacancy data; monitoring of US trade continues. Mixed moves suggest limited net pressure. | Canada Nickel signed an MOU with Taykwa Tagamou Nation to seek favourable financing for the mine’s electric heavy equipment fleet at the Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project. The partnership is intended to reduce initial capital requirements and support earlier startup alongside work with Glencore on processing routes.

London copper rose 0.7% as the dollar weakened, supporting dollar-priced commodities. Prices were also lifted by hopes for higher U.S. industrial metals demand from Biden’s $2 trillion infrastructure proposal. Chinese concerns capped sentiment, while LME aluminum edged up and ShFE nickel rose. | Canada Nickel says it has begun the Environmental and Social Impact Assessment for its Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project, hiring Golder/Wood for environmental studies and TES for community engagement. The company expects a feasibility study later and is progressing permitting, but no operational or supply update is provided. | Ivanhoe Mines’ Kamoa Copper appoints Ben Munanga as board chairman as the Kamoa-Kakula copper mine approaches first production in July 2021. The release also references hydropower plant upgrades supplying Phase 1/2 output, but provides no new disruption or capacity guidance beyond planned development timelines. | Copper rose 0.8% to about $8,988 as concerns over mine supply disruptions tightened concentrate treatment charges and a weaker U.S. dollar supported demand. LME inventories have doubled since early March, capping gains, while markets await China trade data. Aluminium and nickel also rose. | Copper prices rose about 1% as concerns over tighter available supply—driven by mine disruptions that cut treatment charges—combined with a lower U.S. dollar to boost bullish sentiment. LME inventories increased and rising cash-vs-3-month premiums capped gains, while China trade data was awaited. | CNGR said it will build a nickel matte joint venture in Indonesia, with production capacity of 30,000 tonnes per year across an initial phase of 10,000 tonnes. Nickel matte is an intermediate product for battery-grade nickel. The move adds supply expectations as Indonesian nickel ore export bans support smelting demand.

Copper was on track for a weekly rise as the dollar weakened, making greenback-priced metals cheaper. LME three-month copper rose and SHFE May copper gained, while LME aluminium edged lower and Shanghai nickel and aluminium increased. The dollar fell on European data strength, weak U.S. jobs, and a dovish Fed. | CNGR plans to produce nickel matte at a $243 million project in Indonesia. By expanding nickel upstream production capacity, the move is expected to bolster supply expectations for nickel and may support prices in the near term as markets anticipate improved availability. | Copper fell for the day on the LME, reversing some weekly gains, as China’s inflation beat expectations and raised fears that Beijing could tighten monetary/credit policy. The piece cites weaker macro sentiment and reports LME aluminum down and nickel mixed, indicating broader pressure on industrial metals. | SMM data show nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 255,000 wmt to 5.48 million wmt between April 2 and April 9, with Ni content stocks down to 43,000 mt. Stocks remain in a downward trend and are near historically low levels, implying tighter feedstock supply. | SMM data shows Shanghai bonded refined nickel inventories fell by 300 mt from the prior week to 10,000 mt as of April 9, marking the sixth consecutive weekly decline. Russian nickel imports were lower, though nickel briquettes and some LME flows continued into bonded warehouses. | Huntsman Exploration will host an investor webcast to discuss recent high-grade nickel and copper assay results from its Canegrass nickel sulphide project in Australia and plans for the next phase of drilling. The article is primarily a company update with no explicit changes to near-term production or supply flows. | Huntsman will host an investor webcast to discuss recent results from its Canegrass nickel sulphide project in Australia and plans for upcoming exploration. Project updates can affect expectations for future nickel supply, but the snippet does not include material production, cost, or reserve details. | Copper eased 0.3% on Friday as strong China inflation fueled fears of tighter monetary policy and a stronger dollar, weighing on metals. However, expected supply tightness and infrastructure/electrification demand supported a weekly gain. The piece also notes weaker China aluminum inflows and shows LME aluminum and nickel slightly lower.

The piece covers market views on nickel and copper. Citi trimmed nickel price forecasts after Tsingshan expanded conversion into “class 2,” indicating more supply and lower medium-term prices, though it still expects some near-term upside. Copper is described as rallying ~3% on stronger US growth optimism, despite rising LME stocks that could temper gains. | Citi trimmed nickel price forecasts by 6–15% after Tsingshan’s move into “class 2” conversion weakened the medium-term nickel bull case, projecting lower prices into 2022 and beyond. Separately, copper rose nearly 3% on US jobs/infrastructure optimism, though LME stockpiles rising may temper gains. | A live LME futures report notes nickel down 2.8%. It also includes Fastmarkets methodology and database updates, including corrections to certain low-carbon aluminium and aluminium 6063 billet premium assessments. While aluminium adjustments are procedural, the only clear immediate price move described is bearish for nickel. | CATL’s unit will buy a 25% stake in CMOC’s Kisanfu copper-cobalt mine in the DRC for about $137.5m, with joint plans to fund project capex. The mine is undeveloped and is expected to supply copper and cobalt long term, implying limited immediate price impact. | SMM reported broad declines in Shanghai nonferrous metals on April 12. Copper fell 1.76%, nickel dropped 3.58% and aluminium slipped 0.66%, tracking weaker global sentiment. The piece attributes bearish tone to subdued bullish market expectations amid macro factors and upcoming Eurozone retail sales data. | BHP forecasts copper demand could double and nickel could quadruple as faster EV adoption and renewable energy rollouts increase consumption of these metals. It cites favourable EV policy signals and mentions copper strength, attributing gains to growth and infrastructure/decarbonisation spending, supporting bullish sentiment for copper and nickel. | BHP says net-zero policies and EV, solar and wind expansion will drive much higher copper and nickel demand. It links EVs to higher copper intensity and infrastructure needs, and notes copper prices have surged. It also highlights BHP’s increased nickel/copper exposure and favorable policy signals. | Copper fell 0.6% as markets feared China may implement commodity price caps to contain inflation, while demand concerns and rising inventories added pressure. Chinese PPI inflation data and Premier Li’s comments reinforced regulatory fears. Nickel also eased on Nornickel restarting a flooded mine earlier than expected.

SMM says nickel rose slightly last week due to macro factors, supporting battery-grade nickel sulphate, which stabilized around 31,000–32,000 yuan/mt. Spot trades were quieter and premiums of nickel sulphate over first-grade nickel narrowed further, with profit margins for nickel sulphate produced via MHP and briquettes falling. | SMM reports nickel prices rose last week mainly on easing US Treasury yields and stronger macro sentiment, but nickel fundamentals remain weak: sluggish spot trading, NPI price softness, and expectations of continued April surplus. LME briquette inventories fell, yet domestic transactions weakened. CNGR’s supply expansion is seen bearish for forward balance. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous prices mostly fell on April 13: copper ended slightly lower, nickel dropped 2.23%, while aluminium advanced about 1.87%. It cites muted US sentiment, cautious market positioning, and contract-by-contract open interest changes. Overall moves are mixed across the three metals. | Nornickel said it will expand its NN Harjavalta nickel refinery in Finland, boosting output from 65 ktpa to 75 ktpa in 2023 and to over 100 ktpa by early 2026. The project targets growing European demand for battery metals and is powered by renewable energy and recycling. | Nornickel plans to boost production of nickel products, including nickel sulphate solution, at its Harjavalta plant in Finland to serve a growing EV battery materials cluster. Output is slated to rise from 65,000 tonnes to at least 75,000 tonnes by 2023 and over 100,000 tonnes by 2026, including higher sulphate volumes. | Talon Metals reported drill hole 20TK0277 at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Minnesota intersecting 138.18m grading 1.66% nickel and 1.02% copper, including a higher-grade core above 3% nickel. The company expects improved resource grade/length but provides no near-term production update. | Norilsk Nickel (Nornickel) plans to gradually expand its Harjavalta refinery in Finland, using renewable power and high-yield hydro-metallurgical processes to increase battery-grade nickel output. Capacity is targeted to rise from 65,000 tpa to 75,000 tpa in an initial phase and to over 100,000 tpa by 2026, supporting EU demand for low-carbon battery materials. | Talon Metals reported drill hole 20TK0277 at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project intersecting 138.18 meters grading 1.66% Ni and 1.02% Cu, including a higher-grade 39 meters at 3.12% Ni and 1.60% Cu. Management says results exceed expectations and require follow-up drilling.

SMM reports SHFE nonferrous trading on Apr 14: aluminium rose 1.82% and copper gained 0.47% on the day, while nickel fell 0.35%. Coverage notes open-interest changes but offers no major new supply/demand driver, implying only mild, already-contained price pressure for copper, aluminium, and nickel.

London copper fell as LME-approved warehouse inventories extended higher, weighing on the three-month contract. Nickel dipped on the day, while aluminium climbed to multi-year highs amid concerns about potential China output cuts for carbon-emissions reasons. A weaker dollar also capped gains. | Huntsman Exploration exercised an option to take 100% control of the Canegrass nickel sulphide project in Western Australia. The article highlights near-surface and deeper drill intercepts with high-grade nickel and associated copper, plus ongoing drilling and surveys. It also frames nickel demand/EV drivers as supportive for prices. | Huntsman Exploration hosted a webcast reviewing recent drilling on its 100% owned Canegrass nickel sulphide property in Australia and discussed upcoming activities. The company described strong results and optimism about the project. The release focuses on exploration progress, with no immediate production or inventory data provided.

Nornickel plans to ramp up production at its NN Harjavalta nickel refinery in Finland, which is powered largely by renewable energy. Capacity is expected to rise from 65 ktpa to 75 ktpa in 2023 and exceed 100 ktpa by early 2026 to meet growing European EV battery raw-material demand. | SMM data shows nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports rose 135,000 wmt to 5.62 million wmt between April 9-16, with Ni content up to 44,000 mt. It cites resumed shipments from Surigao and potentially higher Philippine flows, alongside sharply lower nickel ore prices and expectations of further stockpiling. | Live futures coverage reports that LME nickel rose on 16/04, signaling near-term bullish pressure for nickel. Copper is described as consolidating after a Thursday surge, suggesting the immediate post-rally move is slowing or stabilizing rather than trending higher or lower. | A live LME futures report notes that nickel futures are rising, while copper is consolidating following Thursday’s surge. It also includes procedural updates to Fastmarkets’ metal assessment publications, but the immediate market call is the mixed near-term price action between nickel strength and copper digestion. | Chilean Metals arranged $2 million in equity financing to fund an initial drill program at the Nisk nickel project in Quebec, aiming to expand a historical Ni-Cu resource and develop potential nickel-sulfate processing. It signals exploration progress rather than near-term production, so price impact on nickel/copper is likely minimal. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous prices mostly higher after China’s Q1 GDP slightly missed expectations. Copper closed up 1.69% and nickel up 1.25%, supported by macro sentiment and market positioning, while aluminium fell 0.36% despite higher open interest. Nickel supply signals cite increased Chinese port ore inventories. | SMM data shows Shanghai bonded refined nickel stocks fell 700 mt week-on-week to 9,300 mt as of April 16. The decline reflects nickel plates flowing out of the bonded area, even as Norwegian nickel and LME briquettes entered China after customs clearance. Stocks have dropped for seven consecutive weeks. | Chilean Metals arranged $2m financing to fund an initial drill program at its Nisk nickel-copper project in Quebec, including work toward an updated NI 43-101 resource. The company positions the project for a market improving for battery metals, but it remains exploration with no current production impact.

Norilsk Nickel (Nornickel) signed a term sheet with Johnson Matthey for long-term supply of critical metals for battery materials in Finland and plans to ramp nickel and cobalt production at its Harjavalta refinery. The agreement supports expected nickel supply into European EV cathode production, adding bullish near-term sentiment. | Johnson Matthey said it will commercialise eLNO cathode materials via a second plant in Finland with Finnish Minerals. The project includes treating sodium sulphate by-products and pre-treatment of nickel/cobalt sulphates. It also signed a term sheet for nickel and cobalt supply from Nornickel, supporting nickel demand. | Nornickel signed a long-term deal to supply nickel (and cobalt) to Johnson Matthey for EV battery precursor and cathode active materials production in Finland and Poland. The news follows Nornickel’s plan to boost nickel output at its Harjavalta refinery, alongside future supply expansion discussions. | SMM reports most Shanghai nonferrous metals rose on Apr 19 except nickel, which fell 1.28% while copper gained 1.22% and aluminium edged up 0.05%. The piece attributes moves to softer USD and strong economic data/risk sentiment, with no major new nickel or aluminium supply-demand event. | Norilsk Nickel said recent incidents in Norilsk temporarily shut its Norilsk Concentrator and two mines (Oktyabrsky and Taimyrsky) and reduced output at Kola MMC. As a result, 1Q21 nickel fell to 47 kt (-10% q-o-q) and copper to 91 kt (-21% q-o-q), signaling tighter near-term supply. | Norilsk Nickel said accidents in Norilsk—temporary suspension of the Norilsk Concentrator and flooding-related shutdowns of Oktyabrsky and Taimyrsky mines—cut 1Q21 nickel output by 10% q-o-q and copper by 21% q-o-q. Recovery is guided toward later in 2021, increasing near-term supply downside risk.

SMM’s nickel industry roundup says Nornickel restarted mining at the Oktyabrsky copper-nickel mine after an April 12 flood, targeting full production by end-April. It also highlights Norilsk/Harjavalta plans to expand nickel sulphate output and deals to supply high-grade NCA/NCM ternary precursor materials for EV batteries in 2021-2023. | Copper prices rose to near a decade high, with three-month LME copper up to around $9,453/t, driven by a weaker U.S. dollar making dollar-priced metals cheaper. The piece also notes modest moves in LME aluminum and tin, while nickel is mixed amid an expanding surplus and production updates at Nornickel. | Norilsk Nickel and Johnson Matthey agreed on long-term supply terms for nickel (and cobalt) for Johnson Matthey’s Finland plant producing ultra-high energy cathode materials for EV batteries. The arrangement supports Norilsk’s planned expansion of sustainable nickel/cobalt output at Harjavalta to meet growing European battery demand. | Singapore prosecutors filed five additional cheating charges against a businessman tied to a nickel trading scandal, alleging investors’ funds were used for bogus forward nickel contracts. The case involves Envy Global Trading and ongoing court oversight, with assets seized. Fraud and reduced trading confidence can pressure short-term nickel market sentiment. | Forum Energy Metals plans a large-scale airborne electromagnetic survey at its Love Lake nickel-copper-PGM project in Saskatchewan to refine targets for possible feeder pipes or ‘reefs.’ The company is moving toward a drill program later in summer, but the announcement is exploratory with no near-term supply impact. | North American Nickel Inc. closed an oversubscribed non-brokered private placement raising about $1.99 million. Proceeds are intended for continued investment in Premium Nickel Resources and further exploration at projects in Morocco and Greenland, plus general corporate use. The release is nickel-related but does not signal immediate production changes. | Forum Energy Metals plans a May airborne electromagnetic survey on its Love Lake nickel-copper-PGM project in Saskatchewan to refine conductive targets for potential future drilling. The release reiterates historic copper and nickel grades and aims to make the project drill-ready, but provides no immediate production or trade changes. | Forum Energy Metals plans an airborne electromagnetic survey in May at its Love Lake nickel-copper-PGM project in Saskatchewan to refine conductive target areas for possible drilling later this summer. The work is exploratory and aimed at better defining anomalies, with no production or supply impact reported. | Talon Metals presented its “Green Nickel” traceable supply-chain concept using the Tamarack Nickel Project, proposing a US-based refinery and integration from mine to EV battery materials. It argues US refinery capacity is missing for nickel destined for cathodes, especially after Eagle Mine closure, supporting tighter domestic nickel sentiment.

Copper prices fell as investors grew concerned that a COVID-19 resurgence in India and Japan could slow the global recovery and reduce metals demand. A weaker U.S. dollar limited losses. Reuters also noted LME aluminium up, while nickel slightly rose and aluminium fell on the Shanghai market.

Copper prices fell as physical buyers reduced demand after a 21% London rally this year. Three-month LME copper dropped 0.5% to $9,397.50/t. Softer USD provided limited support. Weaker-than-expected demand in China due to high prices and slower recovery pressured sentiment; nickel and aluminium moved modestly. | Talon Metals reported drill results from the CGO West area of the Tamarack nickel-copper project in Minnesota, highlighting shallow massive sulphide intercepts with grades up to about 5.86% Ni and 2.35% Cu. The company says this extends mineralization outside the current resource area and planning continues. | Talon Metals reported assay results from three shallow drill holes in the CGO West target outside its current Tamarack resource area in Minnesota. Intersections include high-grade nickel and copper massive sulphides, suggesting potential extension of nickel-copper mineralization. The announcement is exploration-focused and does not imply near-term production.

SMM data show Chinese nickel ore inventories at ports fell 322,000 wmt to 5.3 million wmt between April 16 and April 23. Seven major ports saw a 302,000 wmt drop to 3.94 million wmt. Ni-content stocks rose modestly, but near-term availability appears tighter. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous markets ending mixed: aluminium +0.99% and copper +0.35% after higher arrivals lifted Shanghai bonded copper stocks, while nickel slipped 0.22% with bonded nickel inventories unchanged. Open interest mostly rose in copper/aluminium but fell in nickel, suggesting mild, offsetting near-term pressure.

Copper prices rose toward a nearly 10-year high on a weaker U.S. dollar and renewed supply concerns. A potential strike in Chile, a leading copper producer, threatened an already tight concentrate market. The article also records gains in LME aluminum and nickel, reflecting supportive industrial-metals sentiment. | Surge Battery Metals announced it negotiated an option agreement to acquire an 80% interest in the Quesnel Nickel Project in British Columbia, targeting nickel alloy/mineralization (awaruite) in ultramafic/serpentinite rocks. The company plans to file permits and start work this summer, but no production impacts are indicated. | Australia’s Nickel Mines completed a further 20% stake acquisition in Angel Capital, bringing its interest in Indonesia’s Angel Nickel project to 50%, with rights to raise to 80%. The project includes RKEF lines and a 380MW power station under construction, but the update is mainly ownership-related and not linked to near-term output changes. | Proactive headlines include FPX Nickel Corp launching a metallurgical pilot test program at its Baptiste nickel project in British Columbia. The item is development-focused, but improved metallurgy work can slightly support expectations for nickel production potential, which may provide limited upward sentiment for nickel-related equities and prices.

Bitterroot Resources reported assays from drill hole LM 21-10 at its LM nickel-copper-PGM project, including 0.93% Ni and 0.77% Cu over 2.3 metres and a basal interval grading up to 2.74% Ni and 0.43% Cu. Drilling is expected to resume in late May. | Bitterroot Resources reported assays from a third drill hole (LM 21-10) at its LM nickel-copper-PGM project in Michigan, including disseminated sulphides grading 0.93% nickel and 0.77% copper and a basal interval with higher nickel. Drilling is expected to resume in late May; funds come from a private placement. | Grid Metals Corp. started drilling at its Bannockburn nickel sulphide project, aiming to better define nickel grades and test depth and metallurgical performance for potential high-grade nickel concentrates. The release is exploration-focused with no immediate production, export, or supply disruption, so near-term nickel price impact is likely limited.

China imported 28,800 mt of nickel hydro-metallurgy intermediate products in March, up 5% month-on-month and 79% year-on-year, with total imports up 87% to 85,500 mt. Unit import prices excluding taxes rose, and SMM expects strong Q2 demand for nickel sulphate, keeping intermediates in high demand. | SMM reports slightly weaker SHFE nickel but stabilised battery-grade nickel sulphate at 31,000–32,000 yuan/mt amid quiet trading and tight spot supply. Monthly purchasing may raise trades this week, while prices could fall if SHFE nickel stays low. Premiums over first-grade nickel rose, but are expected to shrink. | Palladium One reported multiple massive to semi-massive sulphide intersections at its Tyko Ni-Cu project in Ontario, linking upper and lower EM conductors and expanding the known nickel-copper mineralized zone. Drilling was suspended due to equipment and spring conditions, with assay results pending; no production changes are announced. | Palladium One reports multiple massive sulphide intersections and continuity along a 350-meter strike length at its Tyko Ni-Cu project in Ontario. The company links upper and lower sulphide conductors and plans further EM surveys before resuming drilling. It is early-stage exploration with assays pending. | SMM reported SHFE 2106 nickel jumped 3.61% to close at 126,560 yuan/mt, with rising open interest. The move was linked to easing financial conditions (lower US yields, softer dollar) and potential near-term Russian nickel supply disruption after a Norilsk mine accident, plus stronger nickel briquette and nickel sulphate-related demand expectations. | Singapore court documents say investors in a billion-dollar nickel trading fraud involving Envy Group are unlikely to recover losses, with large receivables owed and possible inability to pay. The scheme purportedly involved discounted nickel purchases and on-sales under allegedly forged contracts, prompting judicial management and forensic review. | CanAlaska Uranium stakes 12 new claim blocks at Resting Lake in Manitoba’s Thompson Nickel Belt, targeting ultramafic sulphide nickel horizons south of nearby Wabowden nickel mines. The release cites prior nickel drill intersections and highlights infrastructure access, positioning for upcoming exploration drilling and potential JV activity.

Sherritt reported stronger Q1 nickel production (finished nickel up 9% to 4,188 tonnes) and said this reflected improved nickel market conditions, with NDCC at the Moa JV at US$3.83/lb (lowest since Q4 2019) and better realized nickel prices. The article also reviews broader nickel volatility and inventories. | Glencore reported Q1 own-sourced copper production of 301,200 tonnes, up 3% year-on-year, citing productivity improvements and mine plan sequencing at South American operations like Collahuasi, Antamina and Antapaccay. Nickel output fell 11% (maintenance at Koniambo). Guidance remains within its stated range.

Copper prices extended their rally above $10,000 per tonne on the LME, nearing the 2011 all-time high. The surge is attributed to stimulus, near-zero rates, reopening-driven demand recovery, and supply constraints as mines struggle to meet higher consumption tied to the energy transition. | Nornickel plans to adopt a new ecology strategy in 2021 with investments exceeding $5.5 billion, including cuts to emissions and remediation after a May 2020 diesel spill. The company also reports studies and cleanup steps and notes it has paid a large environmental fine. The capex could affect nickel and copper supply expectations. | SMM data show nickel ore inventories across Chinese ports rose 77,000 wmt to 5.37 million wmt by April 30, with nickel-content stocks up 600 mt to 42,400 mt. Inventories at seven major ports also increased. Demand for low-nickel, high-iron ore fell as stainless mills shifted grades amid poor profits. | Copper rallied to above US$10,000 per tonne, nearing the 2011 all-time high, as stimulus and reopening raise demand expectations while producers struggle to keep up. Investors increased long positions and open interest. Aluminum was also cited higher, while nickel was lower, but the dominant price pressure is bullish for copper.

Talon reports additional shallow, high-grade massive sulphide intercepts in the CGO East area of its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project in Minnesota. Results show strong Ni and Cu grades and could extend the mine plan, but there is no production update, so near-term price impact is likely modest. | Talon Metals reported high-grade shallow nickel-copper sulphide intercepts in the CGO East area outside its current resource at the Tamarack project, including up to 4.99% Ni and 1.66% Cu over 5.1m. The company says results support potential inclusion in the mine plan and resource expansion, potentially improving future supply expectations.

Macquarie expects nickel to stay in deficit into the June quarter after a March production shortfall; disruptions reduced briquette output and port stocks fell, supporting nickel tightness. Morgan Stanley warns semiconductor shortages may cut vehicle demand, with aluminium exposed to automotive output declines and potential weaker demand/scrap prices. | Nickel: Macquarie expects a deficit to persist into the June quarter, citing below-expected March production, briquette supply disruptions, falling port stocks and rising briquette premiums. Aluminium: Morgan Stanley flags semiconductor shortages and vehicle-output cuts could reduce Q2 aluminium demand (~5%), with scrap demand also weakening. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. reported TDEM geophysical results at its Kenbridge North and Kenbridge Deposit areas, identifying stronger conductors and indicating nickel-hosting material continues at depth and along strike. A Singapore investor, Vickers Venture Partners, is implicated in a suspected nickel trading fraud involving Envy Global Trading and businessman Ng Yu Zhi. Authorities are reviewing the case after claims of fake nickel trades and falsified bank transfers and account statements totaling hundreds of millions. The news points to financial misconduct, not clear physical market disruption.

Copper surged to a record high on the LME, supported by industrial and speculative buying alongside rosy economic data. The piece notes supply tightness and weak warehouse stocks. It also reports LME aluminium rising to a multi-year high and nickel moving higher, signaling broad bullish momentum across industrial metals. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous metals mostly higher on May 7, with copper up 2.63%, aluminium up 2.93%, and nickel up 1.2% versus prior levels. The article explicitly says copper surged to an all-time high above $10,300/ton on Friday, attributing gains to global reopening, supply concerns, and longer-term electrification demand.

Copper surged to record highs on the LME and Shanghai as investors chase momentum and some physical buyers capitulate after waiting for lower prices. Support came from tight copper concentrate supply and reports that Chinese smelters agreed to cut 2021 concentrate purchases. Aluminium also hit fresh peaks; nickel was slightly down. | SMM says Shanghai nonferrous metals broadly rose, with SHFE copper jumping 4.81% and aluminium up 3.68% as the USD index weakened. The programs aim to convert inferred to indicated resources and test for new nickel-iron alloy (awaruite) mineralization. No near-term production or supply disruptions are indicated.

Shanghai copper prices slipped 1.5% after a rally to a record high, as tepid demand from China’s industrial buyers weighed. The Yangshan copper premium fell to the lowest since February 2016, indicating subdued appetite for imported copper. Nickel and aluminium also fell amid cautious sentiment. | London LME copper rose about 0.9% as traders bet on stronger demand from renewable energy and EV sectors alongside gradual economic recovery and stimulus. The piece also notes rising copper related supply tightness and speculative positioning. Aluminium also increased, while nickel fell on Shanghai data. | Max Resource reports expanded assay results widening the CONEJO copper-silver zone at its CESAR North project in Colombia, with multiple high-grade copper and silver intercepts and the zone remaining open in all directions. It indicates growing potential for a large copper-silver system, but remains exploration-stage. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous metals fell across the board on May 11. Copper eased 0.96%, aluminium weakened 0.72%, and nickel declined 1.35% as contracts closed lower with declining open interest.
