SMM says Shanghai base metals rose broadly on May 12. Copper increased 1.62% and nickel 1.42%, while aluminium added 0.25%. Commentary cited improving US economic expectations and suggested aluminium import arbitrage intermittently opened, which could slow inventory declines, supporting prices in the near term. | Sama Resources reported early drilling results from the Samapleu deposit, including 9.30m grading 2.68% nickel and 1.47% copper within a broader 81.35m mineralized zone. The company is also drilling at Yepleu using DHTEM targets. This is exploration-focused with no immediate production changes. | Sama Resources reported preliminary drilling results from its Samapleu deposit, including a 9.30 m intercept grading 2.68% nickel and 1.47% copper within a broader 81.35 m mineralized zone. The company is continuing drilling at Yepleu, with further assays pending for additional metals.

Nornickel said ore mining at the Oktyabrsky mine has recovered and is operating at full capacity. The update points to improved near-term nickel ore supply conditions, which can increase market expectations for greater availability and put downward pressure on nickel prices over the next days to weeks. | Copper fell 1.8% as a firmer dollar and weaker China TSF/loan growth pointed to slowing demand. Traders also worried Chinese authorities may cap commodity prices. The Fed/dollar reaction reduced purchasing power, and higher LME copper margins encouraged selling. Aluminium and nickel also dropped on the same market risk-off move.

Silver Elephant plans to spin out its nickel (Minago) and vanadium projects, returning to a pure silver vehicle focused on its Pulacayo silver asset. It notes nickel prices are near multi-year highs and details EIA renewal and prior investment, but the article does not imply near-term nickel supply changes. | Copper was set for its first weekly decline since early April as inflation fears pressured markets and the dollar, while China demand indicators weakened. Yangshan import premiums fell to record lows and LME cash copper shifted to a discount versus the three-month contract, signaling ample near-term supply. Aluminium and nickel were comparatively steadier. | SMM data show Shanghai bonded refined nickel inventories fell 600 mt from a week earlier to 8,700 mt by May 14. The import window narrowed but premiums for duty-free nickel briquettes and plating nickel plates were moderate, with some volumes entering China after customs clearance. Stocks are expected to keep declining. | Go Metals says it has mobilized crews in Quebec to start early-season exploration on its Havre-Saint-Pierre nickel and copper project, aiming to ground-truth geophysical conductors and assess subsurface continuity. It also plans follow-on work for gold claims. The update concerns exploration, not production or supply changes.

SMM reports Shanghai nonferrous markets mostly higher on May 17 on improved China economic data and ongoing monitoring of Covid conditions. SHFE copper slipped 0.07%, while nickel rose 1.98% and aluminium gained 1.39%. Commentary links aluminium support to falling ingot stocks and nickel/copper to broader sentiment. | Copper rebounded toward the prior week’s record as investors weighed strike risk at Chile’s Escondida and Spence mines against weak Chinese factory data. The LME copper price rose 0.4% near $10,283/t. LME aluminium and nickel also increased, supported by broader nonferrous output. | Blackstone Minerals reported assay results from drilling at its Ta Khoa nickel-copper massive sulfide project, including continuous mineralisation with significant nickel and copper (plus PGEs). Results from the King Snake prospect suggest potential resource extensions and support restarting a 450 ktpa concentrator, improving near-term supply expectations.

Copper prices rose on a weaker U.S. dollar. The report also mentions aluminium smelters in Yunnan being asked to test power-consumption staggering ahead of celebrations, supporting tight supply expectations, while LME and SHFE nickel both climbed. Overall, the news signals broad bullish pressure for copper, aluminium, and nickel. | Inomin Mines reports a ground magnetic survey and an airborne EM test at its Beaver nickel-cobalt project in British Columbia to delineate drill targets for later 2021. The release provides project geology and metallurgical context but no production, disruptions, or shipment changes that would directly affect near-term nickel supply expectations.

SMM reports China’s April 2021 nickel apparent consumption at 120,451 mt and expects primary nickel demand to rebound to a new Q2 high. Drivers include improved cost effectiveness of nickel briquettes/NPI, higher NPI use in 300-series stainless steel, and increased new energy demand consuming 300-series output. | Nickel prices continued to fall, but purchases of nickel plates turned active after prices dropped below 130,000 yuan/mt. Jinchuan nickel premiums rose and some traders discounted other brands to stimulate deals. Nickel briquette spot demand was soft, yet long-term demand and falling LME briquette stocks support tightening sentiment. | The article reports broad commodity rallies in 2021, linking gains to global reopening, stimulus-driven demand and inflation concerns. It states copper has surged through $10,000/ton on tight supply expectations. Brent crude also hits $70, reinforcing a “reflation trade” that typically supports industrial metals sentiment. | Talon Metals announced thick massive nickel-copper mineralization from new drilling at its Tamarack (Minnesota) project, including a 13.88 m intercept and additional nickel-copper intervals, with assays pending. The company says results expand the CGO West zone and could support a future resource estimate later this year. | Talon Metals reported thick drill intersections of massive nickel-copper mineralization at its Tamarack Project’s CGO West target outside the resource area, with assays pending. The company believes pooling may indicate larger sulphide bodies. This exploration update could affect long-term supply expectations, but no immediate supply impact is stated. | Copper fell 2.6% on the LME as inflation fears drove risk-off flows, eclipsing concerns about potential South American supply disruptions. It also notes broad downside moves across base metals, with nickel down 1.4% and aluminium down 1.2%, alongside a firmer dollar and contango implying ample supply. | SMM says Shanghai nonferrous metals fell broadly on May 19. SHFE copper dropped 2.01% and aluminium plunged 2.28%, while nickel edged down about 0.05%. The NDRC noted commodity prices should return to supply-demand fundamentals and is investigating market conditions, contributing to a pessimistic market mood and long liquidation. | First Quantum announced selling a 30% stake in its Ravensthorpe nickel operation to POSCO for $240 million and provided POSCO a long-term offtake of 7,500 tonnes per year of nickel-cobalt MHP from 2024. The deal supports sustained nickel supply for battery materials and funds debt reduction. | Talon Metals announced that drilling at its Tamarack nickel-copper project intersected thick massive sulphide mineralization in the CGO West area, including a 13.88 m intercept (assays pending). Additional holes also intersected nickel-copper mineralization near shallow depths, suggesting potential for larger bodies but no pricing-impact data yet. | First Quantum agreed to sell a 30% equity stake in its Ravensthorpe nickel operation to POSCO for $240 million, with First Quantum retaining 70% and operating the asset. POSCO will take an offtake of 7,500 tonnes/year of nickel in MHP beginning in 2024, and both firms will explore battery precursor production. | Talon Metals reports discovery of thick massive nickel-copper mineralization in the CGO West area outside its current Tamarack resource zone, including a 13.88m intercept at shallow depth and additional nickel-copper intersections. Assays are pending, and the release mainly updates exploration potential rather than production. | First Quantum agreed to sell a 30% stake in its Ravensthorpe nickel mine to POSCO for $240 million. The deal also grants POSCO a long-term offtake for 7,500 tonnes per year of nickel in mixed nickel-cobalt hydroxide starting in 2024, with proceeds used to repay debt.

POSCO said it will buy a 30% stake in First Quantum Minerals’ Ravensthorpe nickel operation for $240 million. POSCO expects about 7,500 metric tons of nickel supply annually starting 2024 to support EV battery anode materials. The deal highlights growing nickel demand expectations for the next few years. | PACRO says about 9,700 tons of highly pure nickel remains at the DOE Paducah site, worth over $175 million at roughly $18,000/ton. The group is exploring options to process the nickel to remove radioactive contaminants, aiming to turn it into a local economic opportunity. | POSCO agreed to buy a 30% stake in Australia’s Ravensthorpe Nickel Operation for $240m and obtain a long-term offtake for 7,500 tons per year of nickel in MHP starting in 2024. It also plans possible nickel-sulfate cathode precursor production, supporting EV battery-related nickel demand. | POSCO plans to buy stakes in Australia’s Ravensthorpe nickel project for US$240 million. The transaction is directly tied to expanding or securing exposure to nickel production capacity in the near-term outlook, which can be mildly supportive for nickel price expectations, though immediate supply impact is likely limited. | POSCO has agreed to buy a 30% stake in Western Australia’s Ravensthorpe nickel mine, valuing the BHP-linked asset at roughly $1 billion. POSCO will pay about $US240 million, with the deal motivated by rising battery materials demand, reinforcing bullish expectations for nickel supply and pricing. | POSCO agreed to buy a 30% stake in Australia’s Ravensthorpe nickel operation for $240m via First Quantum. The deal secures supply of mixed nickel-cobalt hydroxide (MHP) for EV battery cathodes, with 7,500 tons of nickel per year expected from 2024, supporting nickel-demand expectations. | Copper rose 0.6% on a softer U.S. dollar and worries about a potentially lengthy strike at Chile’s Escondida mine. The union is preparing to strike if BHP fails to reach a fair contract. China’s commodity-price management and other factors capped gains, limiting the rally’s strength. | SMM reports broad declines across SHFE nonferrous metals, with copper down 2.02%, aluminium down 1.85%, and nickel down 3.7%. The move is linked to Fed minutes suggesting possible earlier debt-purchase reduction and higher rate expectations, plus China’s guidance to curb unreasonable commodity price increases.

London copper is set for its biggest weekly drop since Sept 2020 as fears grow that China will curb a commodity price rally. Beijing’s plan to manage supply and prevent “unreasonable” price increases worries markets. The piece also shows lower nickel and aluminium prices, pointing to bearish broader metal sentiment. | SMM reports that new energy demand is becoming a key driver of nickel prices as pure-nickel substitution by NPI weakens. Analysts expect China’s NPI output to fall while Indonesia’s NPI and hydromet intermediate product supply to rise, with growing imports in 2021 alongside precursor production growth. | SMM said SHFE nonferrous metals closed mixed, with copper down 1.25%, aluminium down 1.76%, and nickel down 3.21% on the day. The note attributes the backdrop to US data showing very low jobless claims and a Philly Fed survey where accelerating input costs and rising prices squeeze profits, pressuring sentiment. | SMM’s analyst at the China International Lithium Cobalt Nickel Summit forecasts strong growth in battery recycling, including recycled nickel reaching 62,000 metric tons by 2030. The piece outlines bottlenecks in recycling policy and pricing but provides no near-term supply shock or policy action affecting nickel prices immediately. | First Quantum agreed to sell its 30% stake in the Ravensthorpe nickel/cobalt operation in Western Australia to POSCO for $240m, while keeping a 70% operating role. POSCO will receive 7,500 tpa of nickel in mixed nickel-cobalt hydroxide precipitate under an offtake deal.

Nikkei Asia reports copper hitting a record LME level above $10,000, driven by China’s faster post-COVID recovery and decarbonization demand for power, EVs, wind, and solar. Analysts highlight strong copper imports and limited new mine development, with nickel and aluminium also rising. | Huayou and Eve Energy plan a USD 2.1 billion joint venture in Indonesia to process laterite nickel ore on Halmahera, producing about 120,000 tons of nickel annually and also cobalt. Investors’ confidence rose after the deal, which aims to secure battery raw materials and reduce exposure to nickel price fluctuations. | Huayou, partnering with EVE Energy and others, plans a US$2.08 billion nickel and cobalt smelter in Indonesia, targeting about 120,000 tonnes of nickel annually. The investment could expand future Indonesian supply, but near-term production additions are unlikely, so immediate nickel price pressure is modest. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous metals fell broadly, led by aluminium (-3.52%) with copper (-1.36%) and nickel (-1.82%) also lower. Sentiment weakened amid inflation concerns and Fed policy taper/normalization talk that strengthened the US dollar, while domestic regulation-control commentary reduced confidence for bulk commodities. | Huayou will partner EVE Energy and others in a $2.08 billion Indonesia nickel-and-cobalt smelting venture at Weda Bay, targeting about 120,000 tonnes of nickel and 15,000 tonnes of cobalt annually. It also buys a stake in a battery materials firm, expanding its battery supply-chain exposure. | Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt confirmed a US$2.08 billion nickel and cobalt project in Indonesia, partnering with EVE Energy and others. The project targets about 120,000 tonnes of nickel per year, alongside 15,000 tonnes of cobalt, and includes additional investment in battery-materials firms. | China’s Huayou plans to invest $2.08 billion in an Indonesia nickel-and-cobalt project at Weda Bay, aiming for 120,000 tonnes of nickel annually. The project adds capacity in a key Indonesian processing hub and follows Huayou’s related nickel sulfate ventures and investments upstream in battery materials. | Global Ferronickel Holdings approved buying a 20% stake in China’s Guangdong Century Tsingshan Nickel Industry (GCTN), aiming to enhance synergy between its nickel ore supply and GCTN’s processing operations. The deal is expected to support consistent trade flows and diversification; no financial terms were disclosed.

The piece highlights nickel fundamentals: stainless-steel price strength and an International Nickel Study Group data point showing a renewed supply/demand deficit (consumption +18.9% in Q1 vs production +7.9%), implying tighter near-term conditions. Copper is only briefly referenced for valuation, while aluminium receives no mention. | Experts at an LME Asia Metals seminar said there’s unlikely to be a broad “super-cycle,” but the energy transition should support demand for industrial metals. Copper was highlighted as benefiting most, with EVs and renewable grid investment driving consumption. Nickel and low-carbon aluminium were also expected to gain from decarbonisation trends. | Zhejiang Huayou plans a $2.08bn Indonesia JV for a nickel and cobalt project, with Huayou holding 20% and partners including EVE Energy and Yongrui. The venture targets 120,000 tpa of nickel at Weda Bay, adding to Indonesia’s nickel supply pipeline and supporting nickel sentiment. | Canada Nickel reports a preliminary economic assessment for its Crawford sulphide project in Ontario, with $1.2 billion after-tax NPV8% and 16% IRR, plus low projected nickel costs (C1 ~$1.09/lb). The company is advancing to feasibility, potentially supporting bullish sentiment for nickel supply. | Roskill highlights the evolving nickel market as battery demand grows and supply routes change. Tsingshan’s plan to supply high-grade nickel matte from NPI-based production to Chinese battery cathode makers helped weaken the bull narrative, with LME nickel dropping over 8.5% on 4 March. Indonesia project ramps add more supply optionality.

Canada Nickel says it has completed a preliminary economic assessment for its Crawford nickel sulphide project in Ontario. After drilling began in mid-2022, the PEA is meant to outline project economics, which can raise market expectations for future nickel supply and support nickel-related sentiment in the near term. | KPMG interim judicial managers in Singapore recommended liquidating firms accused in a $1.1 billion bogus nickel trading scheme. The report said investor money was used for alleged fraud, not real nickel trades, and assets are insufficient for creditors. Authorities said forged documents and fake shipment inspections misled investors. | Singapore interim judicial managers at KPMG say firms linked to a $1.1 billion nickel trading scandal should be liquidated, citing insufficient assets. Authorities allege funds from about 1,000 investors were used for non-existent nickel deals, with forged documents and staged shipment inspections. | Enduro Metals reports discovery of the Havana vein system on the Cuba Trend at its Newmont Lake Project in BC, with surface samples assaying 4.51–5.34% copper alongside high silver and zinc. Induced polarization data support a chargeable conductor, and management plans expanded drilling and further study of linkages to Chachi. | SPC Nickel commenced a 2021 drill program at its Janes Ni-Cu-PGM project in Sudbury, targeting continuity of surface mineralization (Trench 1) and an IP anomaly (“Target B”). The release references historic and channel-sample Cu and Ni grades, but involves only exploration drilling with no immediate production impact. | Nickel 28 Capital Corp. announced a proposed normal course issuer bid to repurchase up to 8.7% of its shares for cancellation after TSXV approval. The company is a nickel-cobalt producer via its Ramu joint venture, but the filing does not indicate any immediate change to nickel output or market fundamentals.

KPMG interim managers backed liquidating Singapore firms tied to a $1.1 billion bogus nickel trading scheme, saying investor funds were not used for any nickel trades and documents were forged to sell “nickel shipment” stories. Investigators may recover assets; authorities say at least $1 billion was raised from nearly 1,000 investors. | SMM data show nickel ore inventories across all Chinese ports fell 78,500 wmt to 5.69 million wmt from May 21 to May 28. Stocks in nickel content were 44,500 mt. Inventories at seven major ports also declined to 4.2 million wmt, despite slower unloading and export-peak conditions. | SMM reports broad gains in SHFE nonferrous metals on May 28. Copper closed up 2.22%, aluminium rose 1.47%, and nickel jumped 4.85% on the most-traded SHFE contracts. Open interest also increased for copper and nickel, suggesting strengthening near-term bullish momentum. | SMM data shows Shanghai bonded refined nickel inventories rose 1,000 mt on the week to 9,700 mt by May 28. Nickel plates and briquettes arrived from LME Asia warehouses, and about 1,000 mt moved into bonded storage because the import window is still in a loss state, leaving domestic stocks low.

SMM data shows China’s downstream nickel-sector PMI rose to 50.67 in May, signaling renewed expansion. Production and raw-material inventory sub-indices recovered, while tighter ferronickel supply encouraged stainless steel firms to stockpile. Battery orders improved, partially offset by seasonal weakening in alloy and electroplating. | SMM reports nickel rebounded sharply after a plunge, gaining nearly 5% as a new US $6 trillion infrastructure plan boosted capital-market enthusiasm for commodities. The rally is linked to expected nickel demand from high stainless-steel production schedules and new energy products. SHFE and LME nickel are expected to fluctuate within provided ranges. | Brunswick Exploration announced new outcrop grab sample discoveries at its Fundy Gold Project, including high copper grades (up to ~12% Cu) alongside gold and silver. The company suggests potential IOCG mineralization and plans further prospecting. As this is early exploration with no production changes, near-term copper price impact is likely limited. | Talon Metals reported additional drilling at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Minnesota, intersecting nickel-copper mineralization in more than 60 holes at shallow depths, including continuous trends in CGO East and additional mineralization in CGO Bend. Assays remain pending and no production or trade changes were announced. | Talon Metals announced that drilling at the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project intersected nickel-copper mineralization in more than 60 holes, including shallow and wide intervals outside the current resource area. Assays for the latest holes remain pending, and a new resource estimate is planned later this year or early next year. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. filed an amended technical report and updated mineral resource estimate for the Kenbridge nickel project in Ontario. The update includes revised disclosures and reports pit-constrained and out-of-pit resources totaling 95 Mlb contained nickel and associated copper. It outlines expansion plans and potential depth continuity from geophysics. | Giga Metals reported University of British Columbia testwork showing CO2 mineral sequestration in tailings from its Turnagain nickel project, supporting a “carbon neutral” goal. The company cites lower expected emissions under electrified hauling and potential incentives from carbon policies, but provides no near-term production impacts.

The piece reviews bullish expert views on nickel and aluminium. For nickel, Indonesia’s new high-pressure acid leach project and existing capacity buildout raise output, while Macquarie warns of weaker fundamentals in 2H as supply ramps. For aluminium, Citi expects structurally higher prices as China adds less capacity and demand eventually catches up. | The piece highlights Indonesia’s expansion plans for nickel/cobalt and warns that rising Indonesian supply could weigh on nickel fundamentals in the second half. Separately, Citi is bullish on aluminium, arguing China’s slower supply additions may eventually tighten the market, pushing prices higher in 2022. | Fitch Solutions raised its long-term nickel price forecast to US$16,500/t from US$15,750/t for 2021, arguing the global nickel market will remain in deficit and the price will trend gradually higher. The update is supportive for nickel bulls, though effects may be more sentiment-driven than immediate.

SMM reports that SHFE nonferrous metals mostly fell on June 3 after investors reacted to China data. Copper slid 1.28% and aluminium fell 1.37% with lower open interest, while nickel edged up 0.07%. The note links moves to macro outlook and ongoing monitoring of US data.

Canadian Nickel’s CEO says the Crawford nickel deposit could be developed on aggressive timelines to supply anticipated EV-driven nickel demand and reduce reliance on Indonesia. The company discusses feasibility, permitting, and potential use of the Kidd Creek mill for early de-risking, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel. | SMM data show nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports rose 252,000 wmt to 5.95 million wmt from May 28 to June 4, with Ni content at 46,800 mt. Stocks increased further at major ports. Bad weather in the Philippines delayed shipments, though new China ferronickel lines may slow inventory growth later. | A Fitch study expects ongoing nickel price strength over time, citing a persistent global supply deficit and gradual price recovery. It notes easing spot levels ahead as demand stabilises and new production ramps up, though a possible stoppage at Vale’s Sudbury mine could alter the outlook. LME nickel remains elevated. | Norilsk Nickel said it began gradually restarting ore mining at the Taimyrsky underground mine in Russia on June 1, after pumping out water and reinforcing underground workings. The mine reached 5 kt/day (40% of design) and is expected to reach full capacity by end-June, boosting near-term nickel supply confidence.

Vale Inco nickel miners in Sudbury are entering a second week of strike after voting down a concessions contract. The walkout and reported management actions could affect mine and processing output timing, tightening near-term nickel supply expectations. The article also cites rising living costs and benefits cuts influencing wage demands. | Copper rose on Monday after the U.S. jobs report suggested economic recovery without forcing an earlier Fed policy tightening, helping sentiment. LME three-month copper edged toward $10,000. ShFE contracts for aluminium and nickel also increased, reflecting supportive broader industrial-metals market tone. | SMM reports nickel prices surged to 135,000 yuan/mt before pulling back, but says fundamentals remain supported. High production in new energy and stainless steel and firmer nickel sulphate and NPI help underpin demand/supply balance. Dollar and US data added volatility, but SMM expects a nickel rally this week. | SMM reported SHFE nonferrous metals finished mixed after investors reacted to China trade data and US macro signals. Copper rose 0.38% supported by a weaker USD and falling yields. Aluminium gained 0.74%. Nickel fell 1.12% on softer sentiment despite improving smelting supply and ongoing zinc imports context.

London copper rose slightly as a weaker dollar provided support, but it failed to break $10,000/t amid tepid demand from China. China copper imports fell 8% in May on high prices. The piece also notes LME nickel down and ShFE aluminum lower, reflecting broader market softness. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. closed the first tranche of a $4.48M flow-through financing priced at $0.57 per unit and plans to use proceeds to fund exploration and development work at its Kenbridge Nickel Project in Ontario. The company has commenced work and intends a summer 2021 drill program.

SMM reports China’s refined nickel output fell 590 mt (down 4.53%) month on month to 12,400 mt in May, with operating rates at 57%. Some smelters remained suspended (Shandong, Tianjin, Guangxi), while Gansu overhaul limited impact. June output is forecast to rise to 13,600 mt. | China’s nickel sulphate output fell in May to 97,700 mt (-5.82% m/m) but rose y/y, with limited intermediate raw-material stocks and some production halts in southwest salt plants. Overseas MHP smelter maintenance tightened supply. However, output is expected to rise in June as briquette dissolution lines expand. | Talon Metals announced seven new drill holes in the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project’s CGO West area, all intersecting nickel-copper mineralization, including intervals up to 9.81 meters of mixed massive sulphides. The company says the results expand the presumed massive sulphide pool beyond the current resource area; assays remain pending. | Talon Metals reported seven new drill holes in the CGO West area of its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project, intersecting multiple thick zones of nickel-copper mineralization, including up to 9.81 meters of massive sulphides. The company cites expanded potential for future resource growth, while assays remain pending. | Canada Nickel says its Crawford project is expected to have an “industry-leading” low carbon footprint, with CO2 intensity 93% below the industry average and potential additional offsets from spontaneous mineral carbonation. It attributes results to low strip ratio, hydroelectric power, and electrified equipment, supporting a net-zero nickel narrative. | Talon Metals reported seven new drill holes from the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Minnesota, intersecting multiple thick nickel-copper (massive sulphide) intervals, including up to 9.81 meters. Assays are pending and the release is exploration-focused, implying potential future resource expansion rather than immediate production changes.

Copper fell as markets worried China will curb commodity prices after renewed pledges to monitor spot and futures, following surging producer inflation. LME copper dropped 0.6% and ShFE copper 0.5%. The article also notes LME aluminum and nickel weakening, with attention on upcoming U.S. inflation data.

Copper was unchanged in thin trade as China was on holiday and reduced activity in the SHFE. Supply fears eased after workers at BHP’s Spence mine in Chile agreed a contract deal, avoiding a strike. Aluminium and nickel were slightly higher, indicating limited net near-term price pressure. | ALX Resources began a fully funded ~1,500-metre diamond drilling program at its Firebird nickel project in northern Saskatchewan, operated by ALX with Rio Tinto earning up to an 80% interest. The program targets VTEM anomalies where surface assays previously showed nickel-rich mineralization. | Waterton Global is reportedly seeking to sell its Dumont nickel sulphide project in Quebec for up to $1bn, appointing Goldman Sachs to run the process. The undeveloped mine is expected to produce 33,000 tpa, rising to 50,000 tpa, feeding growing EV battery nickel demand. | Reuters reports the Biden administration’s critical supply-chain review prioritizes expanding U.S. nickel refining, emphasizing Class 1 nickel shortages in 3-7 years. With limited domestic processing and rising battery demand, the push for more refining capacity and stockpiling/recycling is likely to support near-term nickel price expectations. | Copper fell on the LME as markets fretted about China taking steps to curb further industrial metals price gains and as demand fundamentals looked less supportive. The piece cites potential Chinese reserve releases for non-ferrous metals and upcoming industrial production data, reinforcing near-term downside pressure. Aluminium and nickel were also mentioned with gains.

Copper prices slid to seven-week lows as funds and traders cut long positions, driven by concern that China may curb further price rises. Copper is down sharply versus its recent record. The article also cites aluminium easing and a related June delivery dynamic, while nickel falls alongside broader nonferrous weakness. | Talon Metals announced new drilling at the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project, reporting multiple thick intersections of nickel-copper mineralization in step-outs within the Upper Semi-Massive Sulphide Unit. Assays are pending, and the release focuses on resource growth and continued drilling rather than near-term production changes. | Talon Metals reported new drill intersections at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project in Minnesota, extending thick nickel-copper mineralization in step-out holes within the Upper Semi-Massive Sulphide Unit. Assays for the new holes are pending and no production change is announced, so near-term effects on nickel and copper prices are likely limited. | CanAlaska Uranium reports staking additional claim blocks at Halfway Lake in Manitoba’s Thompson Nickel Belt, targeting folded ultramafic lenses adjacent to the Halfway Lake nickel deposit. Historic drilling reported 15.8 metres of 0.55% nickel with higher-grade segments, reinforcing exploration potential and supporting nickel sentiment. | Talon Metals reported drilling step-outs at the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project that intercepted thick nickel-copper mineralization, expanding the Upper SMSU to the northeast. The company emphasizes continued resource expansion and pending assays. No production, inventory, or near-term supply disruption is indicated for nickel or copper. | Talon Metals reported that new drill holes at the Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project in Minnesota extended thick nickel-copper mineralization in the north-east portion of the resource area. Intersections include multiple long intervals of Ni and Cu mineralization, though assay results are pending and no production impacts are provided. | Guatemala set a date for consultation regarding the Fénix nickel mine. The headline suggests an upcoming regulatory/community process that could affect the project’s timeline and future nickel supply. However, the excerpt does not specify likely outcomes, so near-term price direction remains unclear. | Giga Metals said it is studying expansion of processing at its Turnagain Ni/Co project to produce Mixed Hydroxide Precipitate (MHP), used in battery supply chains for NiSO4 and related nickel salts. The release cites rising MHP demand and payables, implying potential future incremental nickel feedstock, though timing is not immediate. | CanAlaska Uranium staked new claim blocks at Halfway Lake in Manitoba’s Thompson Nickel Belt, targeting ultramafic lenses and known nickel mineralization. Historic results cited include drill intersection 15.8m at 0.55% Ni. The release is exploratory and does not indicate imminent production or supply changes for nickel. | The White House reportedly designated battery-grade nickel as “critical” in a 100-day supply chain review and highlighted potential seafloor resources. Such moves can strengthen expectations for future nickel supply support and procurement focus, which can lift sentiment and price pressure in the nickel market over the coming weeks.

Citi says copper destocking tied to strong end-demand is unsustainable and expects a refined drawdown and higher prices over the next 3–4 months, with a possible upside from proposed Chilean taxes. Nickel sulphate is described as especially tight. The article also notes reported Chinese state reserve selling of aluminium, cautioning about speculation. | A Biden Administration 100-day critical supply chain review prioritizes investing in nickel refining capacity, citing vulnerabilities in battery-grade “Class 1” nickel and market indications of a potential shortage. The report highlights EV cathode shifts and urges domestic processing and recycling capacity, implying supportive near-term sentiment for nickel prices. | London copper edged up after a sharp sell-off to a seven-week low, but the rebound was limited by fears of U.S. policy tightening as inflation rose and retail sales weakened. Nickel and aluminium also fell across LME and Shanghai, reflecting broader macro pressure ahead of the Fed meeting. | Six workers were injured in a fire at Indonesia’s Weda Bay nickel smelter. The incident points to temporary disruption of nickel refining capacity, which can reduce near-term supply and worsen availability expectations. Such operational setbacks typically create downward pressure for nickel prices over the next days to weeks. | A fire broke out at Indonesia’s Weda Bay nickel smelter on Halmahera, injuring six workers. The incident is under control, and only one of 23 smelting lines was affected, with other lines operating normally. The operator said investigations and repairs will occur in coming days. | The post reports US export prices up 17.4% and import prices up 10.8% year-over-year, framing expectations of inflation ahead of a Fed meeting. Despite earlier commodity strength, it states copper and nickel are down 4% that day, implying a near-term bearish reaction for both metals. | The post cites a strong US export price rise (+17.4% YoY) and a higher import price index (+10.8%) ahead of the Fed. Despite the inflation backdrop, it says copper and nickel are down roughly 4% that day, while gold and silver are higher, suggesting near-term bearish pressure on industrial metals. | Sama Resources acquired 370 exploration claims for the Lac Brulé Ni-Cu project in Quebec after finding a mineralised gossan exposed over 115m strike length. Surface results show up to 0.52% nickel and 0.58% copper, plus high scandium, and follow-up airborne surveys are planned. No production timelines are provided.

Nornickel’s palladium fund launched its first nickel and copper ETCs on the LSE, broadening listed instruments for investors seeking exposure to these metals. While it is not tied to production, inventories, or trade flows, the launch may support modest near-term demand and sentiment for nickel and copper prices. | Nornickel’s palladium fund has launched its first nickel and copper exchange-traded commodities (ETCs) on the LSE. The new listings provide investors direct exposure to nickel and copper price movements, which could support near-term sentiment and flows into these metals. | Palladium One reported Phase II drill results from its Tyko Ni-Cu-PGE project in Ontario, including multiple high-grade nickel-copper sulphide intervals and extended strike continuity (e.g., up to 6.0% Ni_Eq over 5.0 meters). The update may mildly affect sentiment on future Ni and Cu supply, but no production changes are indicated. | Palladium One reported results from Phase II drilling at its Tyko Ni-Cu-PGE project in Ontario, extending strike length and confirming continuity of massive/semi-massive sulphide mineralization. Intercepts include high Ni_Eq and Cu_Eq (e.g., 6.0% Ni_Eq over 5.0 m). Early-stage results imply limited immediate market price impact. | SPC Nickel reported partial assay results from its Trench 1 drill program at the Janes Ni-Cu-PGM project in Ontario, including nickel and copper grades, and noted remaining results are expected in 3–4 weeks. The company presents nickel-equivalent using 30-day average metal prices, but no production or supply changes were announced. | Eagle Plains reported early drill results from Rockridge’s Knife Lake Project in Saskatchewan. Infill drilling at the Knife Lake Deposit intersected 1.95% Cu over 14.02m (plus Ag, Zn and Co). The release is a copper-focused exploration update, with no stated impacts to aluminium or nickel supply or demand. | Global Palladium Fund (backed by MMC Norilsk Nickel) launched physically-backed copper and nickel ETCs on the London Stock Exchange, later listing on Xetra, with the funds tracking spot prices and stored in Rotterdam. The release targets clean-energy investors but does not describe any immediate supply, demand, or inventory change. | SPC Nickel reported partial assay results from initial drill holes at its Janes Ni-Cu-PGM project in Sudbury, highlighting disseminated sulphides with nickel and copper grades. The company expects further assays over 3–4 weeks but provides no production or supply-impact update; the news is exploratory rather than market-moving. | Nornickel’s Global Palladium Fund launched exchange-traded commodities on the London Stock Exchange for nickel and copper. The ETCs track LME cash prices and are backed by metal stored in Rotterdam. Management highlights low expense ratios for investor access to base metals, with no mention of supply or demand changes. | Nornickel’s Global Palladium Fund launched exchange-traded commodities for nickel and copper on the London Stock Exchange. The ETCs have low expense ratios and are designed to track LME cash prices, with metal backing stored in Rotterdam warehouses. The article signals improved market access, not physical fundamentals.

Sudan signed nine mining concession agreements for gold and one copper concession with foreign and local firms across several states. The government highlighted gold export sales and recent steps to open precious metals trading and curb smuggling. The only copper element is a single concession, with no output figures or timing. | London three-month copper is on track for its steepest weekly fall in 15 months after China announced plans to sell state copper and other metal reserves and the dollar firmed on expectations of earlier U.S. rate hikes. The stronger dollar and reserve-selling expectations pressured copper and also weighed on aluminium; nickel showed a smaller move. | London copper is set for its worst weekly fall since March 2020 as China plans to sell state copper reserves and the dollar firms on expectations of faster U.S. rate hikes. Copper prices decline and Shanghai contracts also weaken. Aluminium and nickel move differently, but the macro/China-reserve backdrop is bearish. | SMM reports broad falls in Shanghai nonferrous metals, with copper down 2.63% and aluminium down 1.84%, citing weaker macro sentiment, a firmer US dollar and higher LME copper inventories. Nickel edged up 0.09% despite falling Chinese port nickel ore inventories, producing mixed near-term signals. | Copper is set for its biggest weekly fall since March 2020 after the Fed signaled monetary tightening and China said it will sell state reserves, lifting the dollar and pressuring metals. LME copper prices drop sharply, and LME inventories rise, suggesting increased near-term supply overhang.

Copper prices fell to a near 10-week low as expectations of sooner-than-expected U.S. Fed tightening strengthened the dollar. The dollar’s rise made greenback-priced metals more expensive. China’s copper exports edged up, partly offsetting demand, but near-term sentiment remained bearish; LME/Shanghai nickel rose. | Class 1 Nickel reported that Phase 1 drilling at its Alexo-Dundonald property intersected massive and net-textured sulphide mineralization at Alexo North and Alexo South, with hand-held XRF indicating nickeliferous material. Core samples were submitted for confirmation assays, and Phase 2 drilling targeting BHEM-generated conductors is planned. | The article argues EV battery growth will increase demand for Class 1 nickel and emphasizes Indonesia’s export ban that shifted supply toward other producers, including Canada. It cites an expected Class 1 nickel supply gap and rising EV nickel demand, supporting a bullish outlook for nickel.

Rosprirodnadzor said fines paid by Nornickel for a Taimyr Peninsula fuel spill will be used for Arctic cleanup and remediation of ecological damage. The Krasnoyarsk court ordered a 146 billion ruble fine after a 20,000-ton fuel release in May 2020, and Nornickel has settled it. | Copper prices rose as the dollar paused before U.S. Fed Chair Powell’s testimony, making greenback-priced metals cheaper. LME three-month copper and Shanghai July copper both gained slightly. The note also pointed to active downstream buying if prices stay below 70,000, while supply pressures remain. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous metals rising across the board after Fed Chair Powell’s updated inflation projections and a less aggressive path for rate hikes. Nickel led (+3.58%), while copper (+0.85%) and aluminium (+1.58%) also climbed. Aluminium support comes from production cuts in key regions and falling social inventories. | Northern Miner Podcast episode 244 features Ken Hoffman discussing the nickel market and battery makers’ worry about securing reliable, ESG-friendly nickel supply. The conversation emphasizes limited Western smelters and the challenge of ensuring steady output for EV batteries, which can influence near-term nickel supply expectations and pricing sentiment.

Ford is partnering with nonprofits to run a pilot empowering women in the DRC’s copper and cobalt artisanal mining supply chains. The program aims to improve financial access, formalize small mines, and reduce child labor. The DRC is a dominant cobalt exporter, but the article offers no direct pricing or production impact details. | SMM reports tight nickel ore supply and strong demand in China, keeping 1.5% CIF nickel ore prices elevated (around $64/wmt) and suggesting further increases to $65–70/wmt this week. Most sellers are sold out for July, while shipping rates, freight capacity constraints, and higher oil costs lift delivered costs. | Los Andes Copper reported metallurgical results for its Vizcachitas copper-molybdenum project, confirming molybdenum recovery assumptions and producing “clean” copper concentrates. The company cites projected final copper concentrate grades of ~22.9%–24.4% copper and specifies no major penalty elements, supporting copper project economics. | Los Andes Copper reported final molybdenum flotation results and copper concentrate specifications for the Vizcachitas PFS in Chile. Copper project economics sentiment improves. | SMM reports May actual cash costs for RKEF and EAF high-grade NPI producers were mixed, falling partly because raw material inventory prices dropped, but offset by higher coal/coke costs. Despite lower May costs, the article says profits likely shrink in June as ore prices follow NPI price movements. | Canada Nickel announced that drilling at its Crawford nickel sulphide project extended the Main Zone mineralization north and northwest, reported new higher-grade PGM intersections within dunite, and discovered/extended PGM and nickel zones at North and East Zones. The project lies near a Savannah nickel-copper-cobalt asset, and initial drilling and sampling are planned, but no production is imminent.

Copper prices fell as U.S. Fed officials’ comments increased fears of a sooner-than-expected rate hike, raising concerns about reduced liquidity and slower global recovery. The three-month LME copper contract dropped 1.2%. LME aluminium fell 0.9% and nickel rose 0.4%, reflecting mixed but broadly macro-driven metal sentiment. | Copper prices fell 1.5% as a firm dollar and fears of a sooner-than-expected U.S. rate hike pressured metal demand. Traders also cited expectations of more copper concentrate in coming months and rising inventories, while mentioning China’s smaller-than-expected reserve release. LME aluminium and nickel also eased. | Indonesia’s mining ministry is considering restricting new smelter construction for nickel pig iron and ferronickel to better utilize limited nickel ore reserves for higher-value products. Existing and under-construction plants are exempt. The plan could curb incremental supply of nickel intermediates, supporting near-term nickel market expectations.

China Baowu and Vale will partner to develop a nickel pig iron/smelting project in Indonesia’s Sulawesi Tengah. The plan includes eight smelting lines and expected output of 73,000 tons of nickel per year, powered by natural gas to cut emissions. The deal targets future nickel feedstock for Baowu’s stainless steel. | SMM data show nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports rose 137,000 wmt to 5.1 million wmt by June 25, with Ni content at 40,200 mt. Stocks at seven major ports also increased. The agreement covers joint ventures and FeNi smelting powered by natural gas-fired electricity to cut emissions. The supply outlook can support near-term nickel sentiment.

Copper fell as the dollar firmed on U.S. inflation fears, raising expectations for sooner Federal Reserve tightening. LME three-month copper dropped 0.6% and SHFE copper slid 0.9%. The piece also reports LME nickel and aluminium declining on the same broader dollar/risk backdrop. | Copper rose slightly after a softer dollar reduced support for the greenback. Traders also awaited U.S. employment data and progress on an infrastructure bill. Separately, LME nickel fell and aluminium slipped, while Shanghai’s aluminium edged higher, indicating mixed short-term pressure across base metals. | Singapore prosecutors filed 13 additional charges over an alleged scheme that raised over S$1 billion by promoting non-existent forward nickel contracts. Prosecutors say investors were cheated and at least S$201.2 million misappropriated from EGT’s DBS account. The case may raise nickel trading counterparty concerns but lacks confirmed physical market disruption. | RIA reports Nornickel expects about $0.5 billion impact from new Russian export duties. Russia introduced temporary export taxes on steel products plus nickel, aluminum and copper, costing producers $2.3 billion between Aug. 1 and Dec. 31. The added costs may reduce exports and weigh on prices. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. mobilized two rigs for a 10,000-meter drill program at its Kenbridge nickel project in Ontario, targeting additional nickel sulphide mineralization and a separate EM conductor at Kenbridge North. The release cites high-grade nickel and associated copper results, but no near-term production change or disruption.

SMM reports easing inflation panic after Powell’s dovish tone and cites strong nickel fundamentals. SHFE nickel backwardation widened as bullish raised positions amid low inventories and limited warrants. LME nickel also shifted to backwardation on expectations of higher Russian export tariffs, supporting prices. With SHFE near-month positions falling, nickel should still trade at high levels. | London aluminium prices rose 1.4% as Russia announced new export taxes affecting aluminium supply, on top of existing production curbs in China’s Yunnan province. US licensing requirements to import aluminium also supported demand. The report notes export taxes for nickel and copper too, with other metals weaker on a firmer dollar. | Canada Nickel reports a significant discovery at the Nesbitt project and provides additional results from regional properties. Exploration success can improve expectations for future nickel supply development. That outlook may reduce near-term nickel price pressure as markets factor in potential additional supply beyond current balances. | FPX Nickel says it has started drilling programs at its Decar Nickel District in central British Columbia. The announcement is directly related to nickel exploration progress and potential future resource development, but it is unlikely to materially change near-term nickel fundamentals within days to a few weeks. | CanAlaska provided an update on staged option arrangements, government-funded airborne magnetic surveys, and renewed permitting for nickel projects in Manitoba’s Thompson Nickel Belt. It also resumed control of Hunter/Strong projects after terminating an option deal. The release is focused on exploration progress, not production or supply changes. | Copper fell for a fourth straight session on a stronger dollar, driven by rising COVID-19 cases and concerns about slower economic recovery, alongside plentiful-supply signals (contango and still-high LME inventories). It also cites Russia preparing export taxes for copper, nickel and aluminium, while aluminium rises on supply concerns. | CanAlaska provided updates on its Manitoba nickel exploration portfolio, including a staged option agreement for the Manibridge project, completion of magnetic surveys funded via Manitoba’s mineral development fund, and resumption of control of Hunter/Strong after terminating an option deal. The news is exploration-focused without immediate production impacts. | CanAlaska provided updates on Manitoba nickel exploration projects, including a staged earn-in option for its Manibridge project, completion of funded airborne magnetic surveys at Resting Lake and Halfway Lake, and resumption of control after terminating the Hunter/Strong option. Copper is set for its weakest quarterly gain since March 2020 amid China’s efforts to cool a red-hot metals rally and a firmer US dollar. The piece also flags aluminium supply pressures and a tight nickel market, but copper sentiment remains bearish. | The headline reports that a Vale strike at a Canadian mine is straining battery nickel supply. Reduced production during the stoppage can tighten near-term supply of battery-grade material, typically supporting nickel prices in the following days and weeks as buyers anticipate shortages. | Vale plans to invest $121m to extend the life of its Manitoba nickel mines by 10 years and fund phase one of expansion at the Thompson Mine. The work includes ventilation, backfill capacity, and power upgrades targeting a 30% increase in production capacity, alongside exploration drilling. | Missouri Cobalt says it is nearing completion of a new hydrometallurgical facility that will enable large-scale production of battery-grade cobalt and nickel, quadrupling run-rate volumes. It also cites ongoing concentrate production and potential copper output from its Missouri assets, which could improve near-term supply expectations.

Copper is set for a weekly decline as a firmer U.S. dollar and weakening demand in China (a top consumer) weigh on prices. LME and Shanghai copper contracts fell. The piece notes related price weakness in other metals and cites pressure from slower destocking.

Reuters reports copper prices rose after U.S. job data showed faster hiring, improving demand expectations for metals tied to the economic recovery. It cites higher LME/SHFE copper prices, declining LME/SHFE copper stocks, and simultaneous gains in ShFE aluminium and nickel amid mixed inventory signals in China. | Copper prices rose on Monday after U.S. job data signaled faster hiring and easing labor shortages, improving demand prospects for metals used in durable goods. LME three-month copper and SHFE August copper both gained. The story also cites advances in SHFE aluminium and nickel alongside the copper rally. | Copper prices climbed 1.3% to a two-week high as the dollar’s uptrend stalled and funds bought, though China’s slowing growth, building inventories, and rising mine supply capped gains. Aluminium rose slightly. Nickel premiums for cash over the three-month contract reflected tight stocks and disruption factors.

Copper prices rose to a three-week high as fund buying gained momentum after a softer-than-expected China stocks release and strong U.S. jobs data boosted demand expectations. LME three-month copper and Shanghai August contracts climbed. China is also set to auction 20,000 tonnes of copper, adding support ahead of Fed minutes. | SMM says upbeat US non-farm payrolls strengthened the dollar and pushed nickel lower, though prices rebounded toward 136,000 yuan/mt. It expects nickel to trade in a wide range this week, with limited upside due to shifting international macro policy expectations and tighter domestic regulation. Stainless steel contracts are seen rangebound. | SMM reports NPI prices in China and Indonesia rose versus last week, while the discount of high-grade NPI to refined nickel narrowed to 140.7 yuan/mtu. NPI sentiment improved on expectations of tight supply and a sharp rise in nickel ore, though higher costs may pressure short-term profits. | After an OPEC+ production increase deadlock, oil prices jumped on expectations of tighter supply, lifting most SHFE nonferrous contracts. Copper and nickel rose (copper +1.85%, nickel +1.2%). Aluminium fell about 0.65%, with bulls’ open interest declining and attention on macro-driven price moves. | Talon Metals reported first assays from drill hole 21TK0313 in the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project’s CGO West area, intersecting thick nickel-copper sulphide mineralization (up to 5.54% Ni and 2.14% Cu over 13.92 m). Management says the mineralization pool is high-grade and remains open. | Talon Metals reported first assay results from drill hole 21TK0313 at its Tamarack nickel-copper project, intersecting thick mineralization grading 5.54% Ni and 2.14% Cu over 13.92 meters in the CGO West area. Drilling remains ongoing to define the extent of the new sulphide pool. | Talon Metals reports initial drill-hole assays from the CGO West area of the Tamarack nickel-copper project in Minnesota, including 13.92 m grading 5.54% nickel and 2.14% copper, with follow-up holes also intersecting mineralization. The company says the massive sulphide pool remains open for expansion, suggesting upside to resources. | Talon Metals reported first assays from a newly discovered zone at the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Minnesota. Drill hole 21TK0313 returned 13.92 meters grading 5.54% nickel and 2.14% copper, with additional intersections nearby. Mineralization remains open for expansion as drilling continues.

SMM reports Zijin Mining expects much higher first-half 2021 profits, driven by higher output of gold, copper and zinc. It cites commissioning of the Kamoa-Kakura copper mine and further ramp-up at other copper projects, boosting near-term copper supply outlook. It also notes growth at a Finland cobalt-nickel-copper project. | SMM says tight availability of 1.5% nickel ore pushed CIF prices to about $73.5/wmt, up $4.5/wmt week-on-week. Weather-related shipment delays and cargoes sold through early August tightened inventories at NPI plants, boosting production amid high profits. Freight remains elevated, reducing near-term downside risk. | London copper rose 0.6% as the dollar’s rally paused, easing pressure on dollar-priced metals, while investors awaited Fed minutes. China’s recycling push and state metal reserve auctions supported copper and aluminium sentiment. However, SHFE copper and aluminium declined, and nickel fell, indicating mixed near-term pricing pressure.

Reuters reports London copper edged up after the U.S. dollar retreated, but gains were limited by worries that the Delta variant could derail global recovery and demand. Shanghai copper slipped slightly. Nickel rose sharply on inventory tightness, while LME aluminium also increased modestly. | Fort St. James Nickel Corp. announced it has closed a private placement totaling $370,400, with proceeds allocated to general working capital and exploration at its property in New Brunswick. The release contains standard warrant terms and does not indicate any immediate production, supply, or trading impacts for nickel markets.

Panoramic Resources has begun underground development and ore production at its Savannah nickel project in Western Australia ahead of schedule. Mining contractor Barminco is now producing ore, with first concentrate shipment planned for later this year. The mine targets annual output of nickel and copper in concentrate under an offtake deal. | London copper fell 0.8% after China’s reserve-cutting steps to support growth raised doubts about a V-shaped rebound, the world’s top metals consumer. The prior Friday rally faded Monday. The piece also notes LME nickel easing and mixed aluminium moves across exchanges, indicating broadly softer sentiment.

Copper gained as stronger-than-expected Chinese trade data improved the near-term demand outlook. Imports and exports beat expectations, supporting sentiment even though physical demand was tepid due to record-high prices and weaker downstream margins. LME aluminum and nickel also rose, suggesting spillover strength from the China macro news. | SMM says nickel ore CIF quotes stayed high and stable, with mainstream traded prices around $72–75/wmt and some deals at $76/wmt. Tight supply and high demand keep prices elevated despite slowing NPI-profit momentum. Shipping delays and resilient freight rates from the Philippines to China also limit declines, supporting nickel prices.

Copper fell after U.S. inflation data triggered fears of faster Fed tightening, strengthening the dollar and making USD-priced metals less attractive. The article also notes concurrent declines in LME aluminium and nickel, indicating broader macro-driven downside pressure across industrial metals markets. | Copper prices eased slightly as the U.S. dollar firmed following U.S. inflation data, which increased expectations for faster monetary tightening. The article notes copper’s reaction was mild and fundamentals were unchanged, while nickel fell and aluminum dropped on trading concerns, pointing to broad macro-driven downside. | Shanghai SHFE nonferrous metals closed mixed after June US CPI hit a 30-year high, boosting expectations of Fed tightening and pressuring USD/debt conditions that weigh on copper. Aluminium rose 1.72%, nickel nearly unchanged, while copper fell 0.36%, keeping overall near-term impact mixed for the tracked metals. | Canada Nickel announced results from its Nesbitt drill program, reporting that drill hole NES21-03 intersected 302 metres of nickel mineralization with visible disseminated sulphides, consistent with prior holes. Management highlights scale potential of a 3.7-kilometre target and notes potential higher-grade feed for its Crawford mill. | Margaret Lake Diamonds reported high-grade awaruite from the Letain property and plans further SGS Davis Tube magnetic separation testing and assays for Ni, Co and related elements. The article discusses metallurgy work and potential future drilling permits, but provides no production or near-term supply changes to affect nickel prices directly. | Rockridge Resources reported final assay results from a 2,043m diamond drilling program at its Knife Lake copper project in Saskatchewan and highlighted additional copper mineralization. The company also plans a permitted summer VTEM and follow-up drilling program. The release is primarily sentiment/expectations for copper, without near-term nickel or aluminium effects.

Horizonte Minerals provides an operational update on its Araguaia nickel project in Brazil, citing progress on procurement tenders for key processing equipment and EPCM services, permits in place for construction, and senior debt credit committee approval expected in Q3 2021. It describes project team mobilization and ongoing social/environmental workstreams. | Atico Mining reported additional high-grade copper-gold massive sulphide intercepts from drilling at its La Plata VMS project in Ecuador, extending mineralization along strike and at depth (including chalcopyrite-dominated intervals). While copper grades are highlighted, the release is exploration-focused and does not indicate immediate production, inventory, or trade changes. | The piece outlines Western Australia’s role in global nickel, citing reserves and production figures and profiling key nickel assets including BHP’s Nickel West and IGO’s Nova operation. It highlights investment appeal and production volumes but provides no fresh supply disruption or policy change likely to move nickel prices immediately.

SMM reports a field trip to Yangzhou Yichuan Nickel Industry, detailing its RKEF-based NPI/10–15% nickel-iron alloy production lines, laterite ore sourcing (notably from New Caledonia via SMSP), estimated consumption and electricity use, and planned clean-energy projects. The visit is more informational than a new supply or demand shock. | Nickel prices surged to near five-month highs, supported by strong stainless-steel-linked demand and tight supply. LME three-month nickel rose up to 2.3% and SHFE August nickel up to 3.6%. Chinese nickel cathode output fell year-on-year, reinforcing the supply crunch. | Nickel rose to a five-month high as Chinese stainless steel futures surged more than 6% amid strong consumption and a raw-material supply crunch, plus concerns about output cuts. The nickel move is linked to higher demand for corrosion-resistant stainless steel, supporting near-term price expectations.

Copper prices declined as the dollar strengthened to near multi-month highs and investors grew nervous about the Delta-driven rise in coronavirus cases. This hurt demand expectations for industrial commodities. LME nickel edged higher, but LME aluminium and other metals weakened, reflecting broader risk-off sentiment. | Nornickel will invest about $1.43 billion to build a new cathode copper facility in Monchegorsk, Murmansk Region, to replace a closed shop and reportedly double copper output. It will also add a Feinstein separation facility, citing favorable subsidies and energy-cost support. Construction is due to start in 2022. | Nornickel says it has produced the first batch of certified carbon-neutral nickel. The certification may support demand from buyers seeking lower-emissions supply and could improve sentiment toward nickel supply quality. As an initial batch, the market impact is likely modest and primarily sentiment-driven. | Copper fell to four-week lows as a stronger U.S. dollar and worries about slowing China demand weighed on sentiment. Market attention included China’s continued releases from state reserves (including copper and aluminum) and rising LME and China warehouse inventories. Aluminium and nickel also declined, reflecting broad industrial metals pressure. | Silver Elephant announced the appointment of Peter Lightfoot as a technical advisor for its Minago nickel project in Canada’s Thompson Nickel Belt. The update is related to project technical management rather than production, permitting, or financing changes, so it is unlikely to drive immediate nickel price moves. | Norilsk Nickel said it has produced its first certified batch of carbon-neutral nickel and will begin shipping nickel cathode volumes. The initial lot totals 5,000 tons, with up to 10,000 tons planned by year-end. The product will be tokenized on Atomyze and listed via a Vienna Stock Exchange mechanism.

Vale said it is re-evaluating 2021 copper and nickel production guidance after strikes at its Sudbury, Canada mine and flooding at Voisey’s Bay, Labrador, alongside refinery maintenance in Wales. Q2 nickel output fell 14.3% and copper 4%, prompting lower near-term supply expectations. | Vale said it is reassessing its 2021 copper and nickel production guidance after a union strike at its Sudbury, Canada mine and flooding at Voisey’s Bay, plus maintenance at a Wales refinery that limits nickel. Q2 nickel and copper output fell quarter-on-quarter, signaling near-term supply pressure. | Shanghai copper fell to a one-month low as a surge in coronavirus cases raised fears that the Delta variant could slow the global economic recovery, weighing on demand sentiment.
