Tepid China demand due to public holiday and risk-off sentiment from Evergrande’s debt problems also weighed, with traders looking to upcoming U.S. payrolls for Fed taper timing signals. | Canada Nickel reported metallurgical improvements at its 100% owned Crawford nickel sulphide project, including 62% nickel recovery and higher iron magnetite concentrate grade. Flowsheet optimization via locked cycle and open-circuit tests increased nickel recovery and concentrate quality. While not yet production, results may boost near-term nickel sentiment. | BHP is reportedly in early talks with Ivanhoe Mines to acquire part of the Western Foreland exploration area in the DRC near the producing Kamoa-Kakula licence. While no deal is guaranteed, the potential for expanded DRC copper exposure is supportive. BHP also plans to supply nickel sulphate for an EV battery ecosystem. | Talon Metals announced assay results from 13 new drill holes at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Minnesota, highlighting shallow, thick nickel-copper sulphide intervals including grades up to 3.50% Ni and 1.40% Cu. The company expects these results to add volume to future resources, not near-term output. | Talon Metals reported assays from 13 new holes at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Minnesota, showing high-grade nickel-copper sulphide mineralization in the CGO East area outside the current resource. Results highlight wide, shallow intervals and potential resource expansion, but no immediate supply or trading impacts are described. | Vale was forced to suspend operations at its nickel mine in Pará state, Brazil. The suspension removes nickel output from the near-term market, reducing supply availability and likely tightening inventories. Such disruptions usually raise nickel price expectations over the following days to a few weeks. | Vale halted operations at its Onca Puma nickel mine in Pará, Brazil, after the state environmental authority suspended its operating license for alleged permit non-compliance, including community obligations. Vale is assessing impacts and pursuing administrative and judicial steps to overturn the order, implying near-term nickel supply loss.

Inomin Mines says diamond drilling has started at its Beaver-Lynx nickel-cobalt property in British Columbia, targeting nickel mineralization across a 5–6 km zone. The company plans about 1,200 metres of drilling, following geophysical work. It is an exploration-stage update without near-term production impact. | Inomin Mines says drilling has started at its Beaver-Lynx nickel-cobalt project in British Columbia, targeting disseminated sulphide nickel and associated cobalt across a 5–6 km area. The company plans 1,200 metres of diamond drilling after positive historic geophysics and shallow magnetic zones, potentially improving near-term nickel supply expectations.

Tartisan Nickel Corp. announced drilling assay results from its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario, reporting zones with nickel and copper grades and noting conductive sulphide likely extends below intersections based on TDEM interpretation. Additional drilling assays are pending, and no near-term production impact is provided.

Renault has signed an MoU with Terrafame to secure nickel sulphate supply for EV battery chemicals, potentially for up to 15 GWh of annual capacity. The agreement emphasizes low-carbon, traceable production but primarily boosts demand visibility for nickel sulphate, supporting nickel-related market sentiment. | Gungnir Resources reported additional nickel assay results from its Lappvattnet deposit in Sweden, including 33.15 m at 0.98% Ni and 0.85 m at 5.61% Ni, with more holes pending. The company is drilling to expand/define mineralization for future resource upgrades. | SMM says nickel plate warehousing and shipments in China’s bonded zone largely halted during the National Day holiday, with only minor arrivals. Inventories rose slightly by 400 mt. Domestic demand is expected to improve next week as nickel sulphate and stainless steel production resumes, but SHFE/LME price ratios should stay steady. | Vale resumed production at its nickel mine in Brazil, signaling a potential normalization of supply after disruption. Such restarts can quickly shift expectations for near-term nickel availability, influencing prices depending on how much output was lost and whether inventories or shipping schedules were tightened during downtime.

Copper fell 0.3% on the LME as a firmer dollar strengthened on expectations the Fed will begin tapering stimulus in November. Separately, the article cites aluminium production halts from high electricity prices and a nickel shutdown due to higher energy costs, but LME nickel and aluminium were up on the day. | Nickel prices rose to a two-week high as power shortages and higher electricity costs in China and Europe raised supply fears. Nickel inventories fell on the LME and ShFE, while a ferro-nickel producer shut output and smelters faced possible curtailments. Higher energy costs were also cited for aluminium production cuts. | Kosovo’s NewCo Ferronikeli is forced to temporarily stop ferro-nickel production because European electricity prices have surged to record highs. Separately, the Netherlands’ primary aluminium producer Aldel has idled its Delfzijl plant amid an unhedged power-cost squeeze. Both shutdowns reduce near-term supply. | Activists and the Fond du Lac Band won multiple federal and state rulings affecting PolyMet’s proposed NorthMet copper-nickel open-pit mine in Minnesota. EPA “may affect” findings and court decisions suspend permits and require further review, heightening delays and potential supply constraints for copper and nickel.

Canada Nickel says metallurgical performance at its Crawford project improved, delivering higher nickel recovery (62%, +19% vs prior) alongside better iron and cobalt recoveries. Management links nickel recovery gains to increased project value and continues feasibility work, though assay results are delayed. The news is supportive for nickel sentiment. | Prony Resources, the nickel producer in New Caledonia previously acquired from Vale, said Tesla agreed to a multi-year nickel supply deal for battery material. Prony aims to raise output to about 44,000 tonnes by 2024. Tesla is Prony’s largest customer; negotiations were via Trafigura. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. reported additional drilling results at its 100% owned Kenbridge nickel-copper project in Ontario, including strong Ni grades over 25.5 metres (with associated copper) and additional zone intercepts. TDEM surveys support conductive zone continuity, guiding further drilling toward down-dip and along-strike extensions. | Tartisan Nickel reported additional drilling results from its Kenbridge Ni-Cu project in Ontario, including 25.5 metres of 1.13% Ni with 0.61% Cu, plus higher-grade nickel-copper intervals. Interpretation is based on TDEM-conductive zones and supports ongoing down-dip/along-strike drilling, aiding sentiment for nickel and copper. | Norilsk says Botswana has not implemented an agreed settlement and the dispute over liquidation and sale of BCL’s assets remains in London arbitration. Observers warn PNRB’s plan to acquire and restart the Selebi nickel-copper-cobalt assets may fall through without a resolution, affecting near-term supply outlooks. | Fathom Nickel reported preliminary interpretation of 2021 soil geochemistry from its Albert Lake property, showing multiple anomalous Ni zones with associated Cu and other elements, and noted drilling is ongoing with assays pending. It also received DTC eligibility approval for OTC trading, mainly impacting investor access rather than metal fundamentals. | Bitterroot Resources reported assay results from two holes at its LM Project, stating massive and semi-massive sulphides are enriched in nickel and copper, with disseminated mineralization in one hole. The company continues drilling toward additional targets, but no near-term production or supply disruption is indicated. | Prony Resources said Tesla agreed to buy about 42,000 tons of nickel in a multi-year deal, positioning Prony as a key supplier to the U.S. EV maker. Prony also plans to produce around 44,000 tons of nickel by 2024, doubling its expected 2021 output.

Charger Metals announced SkyTEM aerial survey results over its Coates Ni-Cu Au-PGE project near Perth, delineating 22 potential sulphide conductor targets and anomalous Ni-Cu-Au-PGE values near Target T1. The findings support forthcoming drilling, but no production impacts are described. | Charger Metals reported SkyTEM helicopter EM survey results at the Coates Ni-Cu Au-PGE Project near Perth, delineating multiple sulphide-conductor targets, including strong Ni-Cu-Au-PGE anomalies at the northern end of target T1. The update supports drilling plans but does not indicate immediate production or supply changes. | Prony Resources said Tesla signed a multi-year nickel supply agreement to purchase over 42,000 mt of nickel. Tesla is prioritizing nickel supply for batteries, alongside prior contracts. Prony also plans to expand output after Vale’s unit sale, while acknowledging future oversupply risk. LME nickel has risen ~20% over a year. | Quebec Nickel Corp. provided an update on exploration at its 100%-owned Ducros Ni-Cu-PGE property in Quebec. It staked additional claims, completed stripping and till sampling preparation, and expanded an airborne VTEM survey to identify new nickel targets, with potential follow-up work and drilling planned for later.

Candente Copper said it reached conditional commercial terms with Gold Fields to option the Arikepay copper-gold porphyry property in southern Peru. The deal involves staged cash payments and exploration commitments toward increasing ownership interests, potentially supporting future copper development. No immediate production or trade impacts are cited. | Canadian nickel miner Noront agreed to be acquired by Wyloo Metals, controlled by Andrew Forrest, at 70 Canadian cents per share, topping BHP’s offer of 55 cents. BHP has five business days to match. The deal is an investment-level development, not a direct near-term supply or demand change for nickel. | Norilsk Nickel announced recommissioning of its Ust-Khantayskaya hydropower plant after replacing the seventh unit, raising capacity from 441 MW to 511 MW and improving reliability. The upgrade enabled delivery of the first batch of carbon-neutral nickel cathodes to an LME-approved warehouse and supports ramping up carbon-neutral production. | Wyloo Metals agreed to buy Noront, topping BHP’s offer and giving BHP five business days to match, potentially sparking a bidding war for a Canadian nickel miner with Ring of Fire assets. The competition reflects intensifying efforts to secure EV battery inputs, supporting a firmer nickel demand/supply outlook.

Prony Resources in New Caledonia agreed a multi-year contract to supply Tesla with 42,000 tons of battery-grade nickel. The deal follows earlier suspensions during violent attacks and coincides with plans to raise output at the Goro plant to 44,000 tons annually by 2024. Nickel prices have rebounded. | BHP and Andrew Forrest’s Wyloo are bidding for Canada’s Noront, which holds a major undeveloped “Ring of Fire” resource. The board prefers Wyloo’s higher offer, while BHP has a deadline to respond. The stakes are in nickel and copper potential, supporting bullish sentiment. | SMM says average 8–12% high-grade NPI and Indonesian NPI prices rose week-on-week as ferronickel demand improved with stainless steel mill restarts. Supply remained tight due to power rationing and restricted/paused production in multiple regions, while coal, coke, and power costs increased. Prices are expected to stay firm this week. | Gungnir Resources reports a completed EM survey at its Swedish Rormyrberget nickel project, outlining near-surface conductors outside the current nickel resource and confirming conductors near existing high-grade mineralization. Concurrent drilling at Lappvattnet continues with assays pending. The release is exploration-focused, not production-related. | Johnson Matthey says its nickel-rich eLNO cathode material will debut at COP26 and move from pilot production to customer trials, with new plants planned in Poland and Finland. The technology uses high nickel content with lower cobalt, implying supportive expectations for nickel demand tied to EV batteries. | Electric Royalties announced it will acquire a 1% net smelter revenue royalty on the Rana Nickel Project in Northern Norway, including the past-producing Bruvann Ni-Cu-Co mine. The company cites higher nickel prices since the 2002 shutdown and potential restart, but no near-term production impact is specified. | Vale has again halted operations at its Onça Puma nickel mine in Brazil after a higher court overruled a judge’s injunction that would have let the mine restart. The court order reinstated the licence suspension, citing non-compliance with permit terms, including community obligations, tightening expected nickel supply.

BHP raised its bid for Canadian nickel miner Noront Resources, outbidding Wyloo Metals after Noront’s board recommended Wyloo’s earlier offer. The deal is framed around securing battery-ingredient supplies for electric vehicles, with BHP seeking to expand its exposure to nickel. The competitive pursuit is supportive for nickel sentiment. | BHP raised its takeover offer for Canadian nickel miner Noront Resources, outbidding Wyloo. The bid targets Noront’s Eagle’s Nest nickel deposit in Canada’s Ring of Fire, and the article notes copper is also present. The scramble underscores efforts to secure battery-metal supply ahead of EV growth. | Metso Outotec won a ~€24 million contract to supply grinding mills and HCT thickeners for a greenfield nickel project in Indonesia. The equipment is booked in the company’s Q3 2021 orders. Metso claims the thickeners improve washing efficiency and reduce nickel losses, supporting nickel processing output expectations. | Surge Battery Metals updated its proposed nickel property work in British Columbia, confirming a significant new nickel discovery from nearby drilling at the Van Target at the FPX Nickel Corp. Baptiste Deposit. Surge also discusses its option/joint-venture plans and exploration spending to advance its adjacent nickel-iron alloy prospects. | Nickel Rock Resources reports confirmation of a significant new nickel discovery from maiden drilling at its Van Target in British Columbia, adjacent to FPX Nickel’s Baptiste/Baptiste deposit. The update emphasizes potential awaruite-hosted nickel for battery markets, aiming to support further drilling and assays. | Nickel Rock Resources announced confirmation of a significant new nickel discovery from its maiden drilling at the Van Target near FPX Nickel’s Baptiste deposit in British Columbia. The release highlights potential nickel-iron alloy (awaruite) and references steady nickel price strength and declining stockpiles, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel. | A government-ordered study in Minnesota could lead to a mining ban, threatening a proposed copper-nickel mine. If regulatory action prevents development, market expectations for future copper and nickel supply would weaken, likely pressuring prices in the coming days as investors reprice supply risk. | A government-ordered study may result in a mining ban that would significantly undermine a proposed copper-nickel mine in Minnesota. The prospect of reduced or halted development increases uncertainty around future supply, affecting market expectations in both copper and nickel in the near term. | The Biden administration ordered a mineral withdrawal study that could impose up to a 20-year ban on mining upstream from Minnesota’s Boundary Waters, dealing a blow to the proposed Twin Metals copper-nickel mine. The action blocks new prospecting permits or leases, raising risk of delayed or reduced future copper and nickel supply. | Nickel prices are reported surging to the highest level in seven years as market supply dwindles. The headline framing implies increasing shortages and tighter availability, which would raise expectations for near-term nickel fundamentals and support continued upside momentum over the next days to weeks. | The Biden administration ordered a mineral withdrawal study that could impose up to a 20-year ban on mining upstream from Minnesota’s Boundary Waters, potentially blocking the Twin Metals copper-nickel project. The move pauses new prospecting permits or leases in the watershed, while valid private rights may be affected by ongoing court challenges.

Nickel surged to a seven-year high near $10/lb as supply fears intensified. Vale’s Sudbury issues reduced production guidance and output, while Norilsk reported lower refined nickel. Philippines weather and logistics also hurt supply, and Indonesia renewed export-noise concerns. Demand improved with reopening and stainless steel strength. | The US Forest Service proposed a 20-year ban on mining in Minnesota border waters, freezing new permits and blocking Antofagasta’s bimetallic copper-nickel project. The move cuts expected domestic supply for EV-related copper and nickel, potentially tightening near-term market expectations while the approval process is reviewed. | The US Forest Service proposed a 20-year ban on mining in Minnesota border waters, freezing new mining leases for two years and effectively blocking Antofagasta’s bimetallic copper-nickel project. Environmental reviews could extend uncertainty for copper and nickel supply, weighing on price expectations for both metals. | Reuters reports the head of the Philippine Nickel Industry Association said bad weather and heavy rains could cut this year’s Philippine nickel output. Mining and shipping are usually disrupted during the rainy season, but this year’s downpour is more disruptive, and ore shipments are also hindered by a shortage of vessels. | BHP is working to make its Western Australia Nickel West nickel operations lower-carbon, including a solar power purchase agreement for part of Kwinana refinery electricity and studies on mineral carbonation of tailings. The company also seeks higher nickel supply agreements tied to EV batteries, while copper is cited as another growth priority. | Nickel prices have surged to a seven-year high as supply problems intensify. The article links the rally to worsening supply conditions, suggesting tighter near-term availability and strong expectations for continued upward price pressure in nickel markets over the coming days and weeks.

LME copper futures dropped over 3% as perceived tightness in nearby supply eased and traders shifted focus to weakening Chinese demand amid growth concerns and power shortages. The piece also notes nickel and aluminium fell more than 5% and 4%, respectively, in a broader risk-off move. | SMM says pure nickel inventories in China’s domestic bonded areas fell this week. Lower domestic spot premiums and prior inventory gains first weakened the SHFE/LME nickel ratio, but reverse arbitrage and low domestic stocks pushed the ratio higher. Over 1,000 mt arrived, and some bonded stock was moved to domestic markets.

SMM reports that global nickel ore reserves are 90.63 million mt with resources around 260 million mt. It highlights Indonesia as the largest holder of nickel ore reserves, followed by other resource-rich countries such as Australia and Russia. The piece is informational and does not mention near-term supply disruptions or policy changes. | The overseas copper industry roundup highlights Freeport’s Q3 copper output of 987 million lb versus analyst estimates near 1 billion lb, intensifying concerns over tighter global supply. It also notes BHP’s FY2022 first-quarter nickel output fell 20% y/y amid planned maintenance, potentially supporting near-term prices for copper and nickel. | SMM reports severe tightness in NPI (nickel pig iron), intensified by higher electricity costs and power rationing that reduces output. Indonesian NPI availability remains tight, and Q1 demand/supply may worsen as rainy season in the Philippines reduces China’s nickel ore imports. Overall, nickel prices are supported. | SMM reports high-grade NPI prices rose week-on-week amid tight supply, higher coal/smelting inputs, and regional electricity costs under energy consumption controls. However, refined nickel also moved higher, flipping NPI from premium to discount. The article expects NPI prices to rise slightly and the discount versus refined nickel to stay flat this week. | Giga Metals says it is progressing its Turnagain sulphide nickel-cobalt project in Canada toward carbon-neutral operations using hydro power and tailings carbon sequestration. The company also explores expanded processing to mixed hydroxide precipitate (MHP) for battery supply, potentially improving nickel project economics. This is supportive for nickel sentiment near term. | Singapore has added 18 new charges against an alleged nickel hoaxer, implying possible market manipulation involving nickel. The excerpt does not provide specifics on how the hoax affected nickel supply, trading flows, or physical availability, so any immediate price impact is uncertain and likely sentiment-driven. | FPX Nickel Corp. began trading its shares on the U.S. OTCQB (symbol FPOCF) and is DTC eligible, aiming to improve liquidity and U.S. investor access. The release also reiterates progress on its Decar Nickel District awaruite project, but provides no new production or supply-demand impact for nickel.

LME copper rebounded 1.68% to close near $9,867/t as low inventory levels and investor demand for inflation hedges supported prices. LME and SHFE copper stocks were cited as falling to multi-year lows. The article also reports higher LME nickel, reflecting tighter short-term supply. | Sibanye-Stillwater says it is in advanced talks to buy Brazilian assets including Atlantic Nickel’s Santa Rita (nickel sulphide and copper) and Serrote (copper and gold). The roughly $1 billion deal would add to battery-metal supply as miners race to secure inputs for EVs. The buy-side interest supports sentiment for nickel and copper. | Sibanye-Stillwater says it is in advanced talks to buy Brazilian assets including Santa Rita (nickel and copper) and Serrote (copper and gold). The deal could be about $1 billion. The targets expand battery-metal supply plans, but no near-term production disruption or volume change is specified. | Canada Nickel announced infill drilling at its Crawford nickel-sulphide project that identified an extensive higher-grade core in the East Zone. Results include 0.31% Ni over a core length and improved metallurgy, with 43% of recoverable nickel reporting to a 55% nickel concentrate. Additional drilling continues. | Silver Elephant announced a private placement by its planned spin-out, Flying Nickel, to raise up to C$7 million to fund exploration and working capital for the Minago nickel project in Manitoba. Funds remain in escrow until regulatory approvals and transfer arrangements are completed, with closing expected in November 2021. | Appian agreed to sell its Brazilian battery-metals portfolio—Atlantic Nickel’s Santa Rita nickel mine and MVV’s Serrote copper-gold project—to Sibanye-Stillwater for US$1.0 billion plus additional royalties. The assets are already restarting/ramping, indicating no immediate supply shock, so metal price impact is likely limited. | Sibanye Stillwater agreed to buy Brazil’s Santa Rita nickel mine and Serrote copper-gold mine for about $1 billion plus a 5% NSR royalty. The producing assets are expected to add immediate cash flows. The transaction supports sentiment toward nickel and copper used in energy-transition supply chains. | Sibanye Stillwater agreed to buy Brazil’s Santa Rita nickel mine and Serrote copper-gold mine for about $1 billion, adding producing low-cost assets to its “green metals” portfolio. The deal targets battery-metal expansion and is expected to boost cash flows, supporting sentiment for nickel and copper.

WTIA Group in South Korea says it has secured a contract to purchase 50,000 tons of cobalt- and copper-rich ore from mines in the Democratic Republic of Congo and will begin full-scale production at an appointed ore-processing plant next month. Sales are planned after inspections. The group also targets EV battery processing locally. | A Reuters poll expects copper to keep falling as China’s growth slows and mine supply ramps up, shifting the market to a surplus. Aluminium price gains are expected to ease as the deficit shrinks. Nickel is also seen sliding as Indonesia’s supply increases and the market turns to a surplus in 2022. | Russia’s nuclear-powered icebreaker Taimyr has entered the Northern Sea Route for winter escort duties, with service terms expanded to run November–May. The article links logistics to Norilsk Nickel’s winter supply plan for its Arctic operations and ice-class tankers escorted by icebreakers, potentially reducing shipment disruption risk for nickel producers. | Nickel prices jumped to a 7-year high on the London Metal Exchange, with contracts rising 4.6%. The move is linked to supply shocks including Vale production cuts after strikes and court suspensions, lower output elsewhere, and logistics disruptions. Tesla also cited higher aluminum and nickel costs.

Shanghai aluminum hit a three-month low as thermal coal prices tumbled, easing concerns about rising production costs for the energy-intensive metal. The piece also notes sharp selloffs in Shanghai copper and nickel alongside broader LME/LME contract moves, attributing the shift largely to coal policy and energy-cost relief. | The Biden administration, via the U.S. Forest Service, initiated a mineral withdrawal process that could lead to a 20-year ban on copper mining near Minnesota’s Boundary Waters. Opponents cite pollution risks, while Twin Metals argues its plan remains viable. The policy move raises short-term supply concerns for copper and nickel. | The Biden administration moves toward banning copper mining near Minnesota’s Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness. The Forest Service applied for a mineral withdrawal covering 350 square miles, starting an environmental study that could yield a 20-year ban. Twin Metals also seeks to mine copper and nickel, raising supply concerns. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. reports progress on a 10,000m drilling program at its Kenbridge Ni-Cu-Co project in Ontario, including TDEM targeting and preliminary results with notable nickel and copper intersections in one hole. Results in other holes are pending and the update remains exploration-focused without production implications. | Johnson Matthey said its nickel-rich battery cathode material eLNO will be showcased in a two-seater electric race car debuting at COP26. The technology uses high-nickel formulations with reduced cobalt content and is moving toward pilot/customer trials and planned production facilities in Poland and Finland.

Glencore reported year-to-date 2021 own-sourced copper production of 895,500 tonnes, down 4% versus last year, driven by lower mined grades and reduced copper by-product. Nickel production fell 13% to 71,100 tonnes due to maintenance at Murrin Murrin and operating issues at Koniambo. | SMM data show China’s September NPI and ferronickel imports fell 12.34% month-on-month and 10.09% year-on-year. Nickel-content imports declined as well. A Delong Indonesia NPI unit was partly shut due to power-plant failure, reducing NPI shipments, while port logistics improved but new capacity added little. | SMM reports nickel ore prices stabilised: Ni 1.5% CIF nickel ore at $82/wmt, flat vs a week earlier, with declining Philippine exports due to the rainy season. Plant inventories are adequate despite power rationing, while port stocks build. Restocking demand is weaker and smelting costs remain high, leading to expected stable prices. | SMM reports the SHFE/LME nickel price ratio continued falling as domestic nickel plate inventories declined more slowly, alongside large imported inflows. After the import window closed, some goods shifted to bonded zones. SMM expects over 500 mt of nickel briquette and more than 800 mt of nickel plate to arrive next week, impacting inventories.

SMM reports Indonesia’s Department of Energy and Mineral Resources forecast November benchmark FOB prices for nickel ore at $40.59–$55.72/wmt across Ni 1.7%–2.0% grades. The benchmark is projected to drop 2.81% versus October, reflecting falling nickel prices, which is likely to weigh on nickel market sentiment. | SMM’s overseas copper roundup cites Teck’s Quebrada Blanca Phase 2 expansion in Chile completed two-thirds but expected cost 5% higher, with start of production pushed to 2H 2022. It also notes Colombia’s regulator shelved AngloGold’s Quebradona copper-gold license application. Separately, South Africa/Sibanye discusses acquiring Brazilian nickel-copper assets. | London copper rebounded after two weeks of losses, driven by tight LME refined inventories and worsening expectations for mined supply. Jefferies cited underappreciated supply constraints despite projects ramping. China’s factory signals were mixed, supporting volatility. Aluminium edged down and nickel fell on the day. | SMM reports nickel prices fell with coal prices, weakening cost support, but argues the NPI-linked cost drag is limited and tight NPI supply should prevent major surplus. With downstream sentiment uncertain, prices are expected to fluctuate in a range this week, with possible drops if nickel sulphate output is curtailed. | Vale says its Long Harbour nickel rounds’ carbon footprint (4.4 tCO2e per tonne) has received independent assurance from Intertek. The company positions the low-carbon nickel for battery EV demand in North America and Europe. It highlights hydrometallurgy-based production and ESG targets, without changing output or supply levels. | Vale announced Intertek provided third-party limited assurance for the carbon footprint of nickel rounds from its Long Harbour refinery in Newfoundland. The rounds reportedly have a much lower CO2e footprint than typical Class 1 nickel, supporting Vale’s supply for North American and European EV battery markets, potentially boosting nickel demand expectations.

Power Nickel says it will start a ~4,000-metre drilling program at the Nisk project in James Bay to expand historic high-grade nickel-copper mineralization and prepare an NI 43-101 compliant resource estimate. It has permits and expects drilling over about four weeks. No new resource results are reported. | Power Nickel plans to begin a 4,000-metre drilling program at its key nickel project target “Nisk.” The update is relevant for nickel by informing the market about ongoing exploration/drilling progress, but it is unlikely to change near-term nickel balances within days to a few weeks, keeping the net price effect limited. | Murchison Minerals begins an inaugural helicopter-supported drill program at Quebec’s HPM project, targeting the PYC nickel-copper-cobalt prospect. The company aims to test subsurface extensions of earlier surface mineralization (reported Ni and Cu grades via NiEq/CuEq). Results are pending, with no immediate production or supply impact described.

BHP has entered talks with Wyloo Metals over a potential takeover of Canadian nickel miner Noront Resources, extending its tender amid negotiations for Wyloo’s support. BHP frames the move as expanding into battery minerals, seeking nickel supply for electric vehicles, which can be supportive for nickel sentiment near term. | Glencore’s Q3 production report cited lower mining grades across business units. Self-owned copper output fell 4% year-on-year to 895,500 tonnes, with weaker copper by-products. Nickel from owned sources declined 13% to 71,100 tonnes due to maintenance and operational problems, while other asset notes were largely unrelated.

LME copper closed down $37/t to $9,458.5 after uncertainty ahead of the Fed meeting and worries about China’s slowdown. Traders expected potential scaling back of QE and focused on the dollar outlook. Copper also saw weak warehouse positions, while nickel stocks fell to multi-year lows. | BHP and Wyloo are negotiating BHP’s acquisition of Canada’s Noront, driven by the need to secure battery-metal supply. BHP has raised its offer and extended the bid period. The Noront Eagle Nest project contains high-grade nickel (and copper), with delays from government negotiations, keeping supply expectations tight. | London copper rose 1% after the U.S. Fed signaled no immediate interest-rate hike, supporting expectations for sustained economic recovery. The piece cites resilient demand and supply uncertainty. It also notes LME aluminium and nickel gaining modestly, reinforcing a broad supportive macro backdrop for industrial metals. | Transparency Market Research expects nickel sulfate demand to grow (14.7% CAGR) as EV adoption and Li-ion batteries expand, with nickel sulfate used in battery cathodes and electroplating. It also cites growing use in surface treatment and coatings, which may lift expectations for nickel feedstock demand over time.

Nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports rose 60,000 wmt to 9.05 million wmt (71,000 mt Ni content) as of Nov 5, with the Guangxi port driving most of the increase. Despite a recent rise, nickel ore shipments may be ending with the monsoon season in the Philippines, so inventories are expected to fall. | Sherritt said it will boost production of battery-grade nickel at its Moa joint venture in Cuba by 15% to 20%, extending the mine’s life beyond 2040 to meet EV battery demand. It highlighted processing and U.S. embargo risks but expects construction/equipment orders and follow-up next quarter.

First Cobalt, renamed Electra Battery Materials, outlined expansion of battery-grade nickel and cobalt production for North American EV supply chains. It is running a nickel market study for a future battery-grade nickel sulfate plant (targeted 2024-25) and evaluating recycled feeds, potentially supporting nickel demand expectations. | Guatemala is reported to be advancing community consultation steps for the Fénix nickel mine. The notice suggests ongoing engagement tied to approvals for the project, which may affect near-term certainty around operational timing and output. This can influence market expectations for nickel supply in the coming weeks. | The article reports on a Katoro Gold update focused on the Haneti Project, where Power Metal is eager for drilling to begin. The project targets a large-scale nickel and PGE discovery. As an exploration milestone, it may modestly improve expectations for future nickel supply. | Copper rose about 1.2% as China’s export growth beat forecasts, LME-registered copper inventories fell sharply, and canceled warrants suggested further stock declines. Sentiment was reinforced by passage of a $1 trillion U.S. infrastructure bill, though traders remain cautious about potential Chinese demand weakness.

The US has updated its critical minerals list by adding nickel, highlighting its use in battery alloys, electronics, military hardware, and energy technologies. With Australia producing about 24% of global nickel supply, the article argues Australia is positioned to benefit, potentially lifting nickel sentiment. | Nickel 28 reported Q3 2021 results from its 8.56% stake in the Ramu nickel-cobalt project in Papua New Guinea. The company highlighted strong nickel and cobalt output, solid sales and cash costs per pound, improved payability, and a received cash flow payment, supporting nickel supply sentiment. | Nickel 28 reported Q3 2021 results for its 8.56% interest in the Ramu nickel-cobalt operation in Papua New Guinea. The company cited strong nickel/cobalt commodity prices, improved MHP payability, higher Q3 nickel sales, and a US$3.2 million cash-flow receipt following repayment of operating debt. | Minnesota’s DNR is accepting public comments as it reviews rules governing nonferrous (copper-nickel) mining restrictions near the BWCAW after a lawsuit sought to expand protections to the Rainy River Watershed. Comments are due Dec. 8. The process relates to the proposed Twin Metals mine near Ely.

Dang Sheng Technology signed a letter of intent with FMG and its subsidiary to form a Finland JV to build high-nickel ternary cathode capacity (100,000 mt/year total; first 50,000 mt/year by 2024). The project targets overseas demand for high-nickel batteries, supporting nickel demand expectations. | Gungnir Resources reported additional 2021 drill results from Sweden’s Lappvattnet nickel deposit, including 13.65 metres of 0.83% Ni and 8.00 metres of 1.03% Ni, with a peak of 5.12% Ni. These improved assays may modestly boost nickel market sentiment despite no near-term supply change. | Altilium Group agreed with Indo Mineral Research/Sebuku Group to promote and accelerate the DNi hydrometallurgical process in Indonesia. Plans discussed include constructing plants delivering about 20,000 tonnes/year of nickel in MHP and producing co-products such as aluminium hydroxide, supporting EV battery supply and cleaner nickel output. | Canada Nickel reports lab tests showing its Crawford nickel project tailings can mineralize CO2 at rates up to 17.5 kg per tonne, potentially exceeding the 4.6 kg/tonne needed to offset projected emissions. The work supports future carbon-credit opportunities and a NetZero initiative, with next pilot tests planned later.

London copper eased slightly as the dollar firmed on expectations of an earlier U.S. rate hike after strong inflation data, which can slow global recovery and raise dollar-priced metals. LME aluminium fell while nickel rose; losses were partly offset by tight exchange inventories. | Chinese ports’ nickel ore inventories rose by 190,000 wmt to 9.24 million wmt, with Ni content at 72,500 mt. Demand for nickel ore was weaker than normal as NPI production in some areas hasn’t fully recovered and buyers restocked only to cover until year-end. Inventories are expected to fall later. | Copper gained after U.S. inflation-related weakness in consumer confidence eased the dollar, supporting dollar-priced metals; tight LME/shanghai inventories also helped. China power curbs cut 7% of aluminium capacity, supporting aluminium. Nickel faces Indonesia export-pricing scrutiny, with nickel quoted lower on the day.

Japan and the U.S. agreed to begin talks on additional U.S. tariffs on steel and aluminium imports from Japan. No specific measures were set in the meeting, but the U.S. said talks could lead to easing tariffs, a longstanding irritant. This may support aluminium prices in the near term. | FPX Nickel announced new drill intersections at its Van target in the Decar Nickel District, extending strong awaruite nickel mineralization and reporting broad, high-grade near-surface intervals. Assays for additional holes are pending. The positive results may improve expectations for future nickel supply potential. | Gungnir Resources reported additional near-surface drilling at its Lappvattnet nickel deposit in Sweden, including a 6.94% Ni intercept over 31 metres in hole LAP21-12. Assays are pending for nearby holes and the company expects these results to feed future resource upgrades. | Copper prices fell as China’s property sector showed weakness, reducing expectations for metals demand. Coal prices dropped, easing smelter energy costs, but a stronger U.S. dollar lifted metal prices for non-dollar buyers. The LME warehouse squeeze eased. LME aluminium and nickel also fell on the same risk backdrop. | The article says Talon commends U.S. action on the battery supply chain as President Biden signs a bipartisan infrastructure bill and adds nickel to the U.S. critical mineral list. The move signals stronger policy attention to nickel sourcing and battery-related industrial activity, potentially supporting nickel prices in the near term.

Talon Metals says President Biden signed the U.S. Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act, providing funding for EV adoption and EV battery materials processing, including nickel. It also notes the administration added nickel to the U.S. Critical Minerals List, aiming to prioritize nickel mining and processing for supply security.  | Copper prices rose 0.5% on the LME and edged up on the SHFE after improved risk sentiment following a Biden-Xi summit. The meeting supported wider market confidence, with the yuan strengthening. In the same snapshot, nickel was up while aluminium and zinc fell, showing mixed effects across metals. | A research team at UIUC developed a tunable liquid electrolyte and polymer coating to recover metals from spent NMC battery waste, reporting ~94% nickel purity and ~96% cobalt purity. By making recycling more efficient and less wasteful, the method could boost expectations for secondary nickel supply, though timing and scale remain unclear. | Power Nickel started a ~4,000-metre drilling program at its Nisk project to expand a historic nickel-copper mineralization and produce a new NI 43-101 resource estimate. The company expects assay results in January and discusses potential nickel sulphate for EV batteries, but provides no new production or assay data. | Palladium One provided updates on its Tyko copper-nickel exploration project, including geophysical EM surveys, additional exploration permit applications, and expectations to resume drilling after permits are received. The release also cites recent high-grade Ni and Cu intercepts used in Ni-equivalent calculations, but offers no near-term production impact. | Palladium One provides updates on its Tyko copper-nickel project, including ongoing EM surveys, new exploration permit applications, and plans to schedule drilling after permits are received. It reiterates earlier drill intercept grades (Ni and Cu) and notes nickel-equivalent calculations, but no immediate production or supply impact is stated.

Vale has ramped down its Copper Cliff Smelter into a “hot” standby state for two weeks to optimize concentrate stock levels after an incident halted production at the Totten Mine. The move pauses ore smelting temporarily, with refinery operations continuing and no customer impact expected in the article. | A fire broke out in a ferronickel smelting furnace at a nickel smelter in Konawe, southeast Sulawesi, Indonesia. The incident affects only one smelting furnace production line, with expected capacity of about 650 mt/month in Ni content under normal operations. The article says the impact is not significant. | SPC Nickel announced staking of a large land package covering Nunavut’s Muskox Ni-Cu-PGM intrusion and acquisition of an extensive historical geoscience database. The release emphasizes future evaluation and potential for feeder-dyke mineralization, but it does not change near-term production, inventories, or trade flows for nickel or copper. | BHP’s executive said a decarbonised world will require much higher metal supply, projecting nearly double copper demand and about four times nickel demand over the next 30 years. The comments were presented as modelling results supporting the long-run investment case, potentially boosting short-term sentiment.

Surge Battery Metals announces it has closed an option acquisition to earn an 80% interest in nickel claims (HN4 and N100) in Northern British Columbia from Nickel Rock Resources. The company issued shares and plans $200,000 in exploration expenditures over two years, with further joint-venture steps pending. | Merger talks between IGO Ltd and nickel producer Western Areas showed no progress at annual meetings, with the deal now past three months. Despite the lack of signs of resolution, both companies’ shares rose modestly, implying limited near-term optimism related to nickel and some exposure to copper markets. | Fireweed Zinc reported step-out drill results at its Macmillan Pass (Yukon) Boundary Zone, including a copper-rich intersection grading 1.54% Cu over 2.0 m and 2.35% Zn over 203.05 m. The update highlights a new copper-zinc mineralization style but is unlikely to materially shift near-term metals prices beyond copper expectations. | Nornickel said it settled a dispute with Botswana’s government and BCL Group tied to its aborted Africa asset sale, including a 50% stake in the Nkomati joint venture mine. The company received agreed cash compensation and both sides waived claims. The resolution may lessen near-term nickel supply uncertainty.

Silver Elephant’s subsidiary Flying Nickel upsized its private placement to CAD 8.6 million, with Blackstone investing CAD 2.975 million. Proceeds (held in escrow pending approvals) will fund exploration and advancement of the Minago nickel project, supporting near-term nickel development prospects.

Cyprium Metals reports assay results from final diamond holes at Nanadie Well, highlighting extensive copper sulphide mineralisation with several high-grade intercepts and confirming the deposit remains open. The company also notes elevated nickel values associated with some copper intervals, but provides no near-term production or supply impact. | Nickel prices recently fell due to pessimism on real-estate demand, then rebounded as sentiment improved. Lower coal prices and weaker demand failed to drive sharp nickel losses. Discounts to NPI narrowed, nickel sulphate demand stayed steady, inventory is low, and customs tax-control issues may raise spot premiums. | The piece gives a nickel market review, arguing that Q1 2026 trends point to continued oversupply through 2026. It attributes the surplus to high nickel output from Indonesia and weaker demand from manufacturing and battery-related uses. It also discusses several nickel project and portfolio developments. | SMM says CIF nickel ore prices were flat, but FOB prices firmed as supply tightened. Philippines rainy season impeded mining and cargo handling, sharply reducing nickel ore exports, with further declines expected into December holidays. NPI plants buy cautiously due to weak NPI/stainless prices, keeping near-term volatility high but supportive for nickel. | Mosaic Minerals reports drilling intersected a 70-meter anomalous nickel zone (Pike Nickel East) and nearly 80 meters of nickeliferous mineralization, extending a nickel corridor by about 3.4 km and prompting plans for further drilling. Copper is also mentioned on the property, but aluminium appears only in risk wording. | Norilsk Nickel’s board recommended an interim dividend for nine months of 2021 totaling RUB 232.84 bn, with EGM set for Dec. 27 and dividend record date Jan. 14. The company is a major producer of nickel and copper, but the announcement does not change metal supply or demand expectations. | Cyprium Metals reported extensive copper sulphide intersections across all diamond drill holes at Nanadie Well, with mineralisation open along strike and across strike. The results will support a mineral resource estimate for a scoping study and preliminary metallurgical work. Elevated nickel and palladium values were found with key copper intervals. | Noble Mineral Exploration entered a Letter of Intent with Canada Nickel to option mining claims and sell MRO patents. The properties cover a 20 km strike length with evidence of nickel, cobalt, PGM’s, rhodium and rare earths. The plan is to move toward a joint venture and fund exploration over four years. | Canada Nickel reports acquiring 13 additional properties in the Timmins Nickel District, adding multiple ultramafic targets with historical nickel (and some copper) intercepts. The company highlights district-scale exploration potential, including twinning and updating historical resources, which can support improved expectations for future nickel supply.

Errawarra Resources agreed to acquire an 80% interest in the AndoverWest nickel project adjacent to Azure’s Andover nickel sulphide discovery. The deal supports exploration funding through a share placement and outlines planned EM reprocessing and drilling on nickel sulphide targets, conditional on shareholder and regulatory approvals. | SMM says China imported 299,000 mt of NPI and ferronickel in October, down 2.42% m/m but up 10.09% y/y, with Ni content at 46,000 mt. Despite a mid-November Indonesian plant fire, damage is limited, no major new Indonesia production lines were added, and Nov imports are expected to stay roughly unchanged. | SMM cited Chinese customs data showing nickel ore imports fell 21.3% m/m in October, with Philippines shipments down 22.8% m/m amid monsoon rainy-season disruptions to mining and shipments. Imports of nickel sulphide ore also dropped sharply. SMM expects declines to continue in November as more areas in the Philippines enter rainy season. | The article says the approved U.S. infrastructure bill will fund electrification, transit and EV charging stations, expected to increase demand for industrial metals. It links this to copper and mentions battery materials such as nickel. It also highlights Kutcho Copper’s feasibility study as supportive for future copper supply.

Morningstar cuts iron ore assumptions but lifts average copper price expectations, citing potential US infrastructure-driven demand growth. Credit Suisse estimates the infrastructure bill could add 50–250ktpa copper demand and see supply risks easing. Nickel pig iron in China is said to have softened after exchange nickel price declines. | Morningstar highlights US infrastructure stimulus as a potential demand tailwind for copper, estimating additional demand of 50–250ktpa. Separately, Credit Suisse notes nickel pig iron prices in China have softened after nickel declines on exchanges, with expectations of a surplus as production recovers—signaling mixed near-term pricing forces for both metals. | The U.S. and EU agreed to replace Section 232 tariffs on EU steel (25%) and aluminum (10%) with tariff-rate quotas effective Jan. 1, 2022. Eligible EU products enter the U.S. duty-free up to quota volumes; above-quota shipments face remaining tariffs. Quota administration and exclusions rules add some uncertainty. | Indonesia plans to stop exports of raw materials after its nickel ore shipment ban, aiming to shift trade toward semi-finished and finished goods to boost downstream investment. The explicit nickel restriction is the immediate driver, while copper export shutdown is mentioned as a next-step possibility; aluminium is not addressed.

LME copper futures closed about 1.3% higher at an one-month high, supported by China’s plan to increase real estate credit lines and signs of tighter supply, with falling warehouse inventories and premiums for fast delivery. Nickel also rose more than 2%, with receipts falling sharply, indicating tighter availability. | Weiming Group and Indigo signed an agreement to jointly develop and construct a 40,000 mt high-grade nickel matte project in Indonesia. The $400 million venture targets processing low-grade ore and tailings technology. Capacity in Indonesia would rise to 300,000 mt nickel content, reflecting expanded future nickel supply expectations. | Indonesia plans to halt exports of raw materials as it moves toward downstream processing. After implementing a nickel ore shipment ban, President Widodo suggests bauxite export limits next year, copper exports later, and further restrictions thereafter. The policy aims to force exports of semi-finished/finished products. | Horizonte Minerals reportedly arranged a US$633mn funding package to fully finance its nickel project in Brazil. The financing reduces uncertainty around project completion and can support sentiment for nickel supply prospects, potentially exerting upward or stabilizing expectations for nickel prices depending on construction timelines.

Nickel neared seven-year highs as LME on-warrant inventories fell to 62,304 tonnes from over 200,000 in April, signaling tighter supply. The rally was reinforced by expectations that China may provide fiscal support despite property-sector distress, boosting demand outlook; prices later eased slightly from peaks. | Nickel and other industrial metals rose as optimism about China’s property sector returned. The headline implies improved demand expectations for construction-related materials, providing near-term support to prices. By extension, the move is likely to affect copper and aluminium alongside nickel. | Chinese nickel ore inventories at major ports fell 114,000 wmt week-on-week to 9.35 million wmt as of Nov 26, with Ni content at 74,300 mt. The decline is linked to fewer shipments during the Philippines wet season, alongside recovering NPI production. Further near-term inventory declines are expected from slower supply and stronger demand. | TrendForce reports Indonesia intends to end exports of unprocessed nickel ores (and also mentions copper and tin), which could disrupt global raw-material supply chains for EV batteries. With rising nickel demand for NCM cathodes, potential tightening supports expectations for higher nickel prices within weeks, though copper effects are less direct.

Aitkin County’s Tamarack project in Minnesota, a planned high-grade nickel mine (with copper by-product potential), is entering the licensing process amid environmental opposition. The U.S. is investing to expand nickel refining capacity, and demand for nickel in stainless steel and EVs is rising, supporting bullish sentiment despite uncertain permitting timelines. | Norilsk Nickel’s Capital Markets Day highlights a large increase in capex, including upgrades to concentration and smelting capacity in Russia and capacity expansions for nickel refining and copper refining. The company also raised long-term production growth targets for nickel and copper, reinforcing expectations of future supply. | Nornickel raised its 2021-2030 investment estimate by $6 billion to $35 billion, citing higher construction costs and additional energy and production infrastructure upgrades. Management also pushed out the peak spending period to 2028 and increased its long-term output forecast, implying potential future nickel capacity. The note mentions energy upgrades after accidents. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. announced that drilling at its Kenbridge nickel project reached the deepest hole to date and intersected 3.18% nickel over 1.05 metres, with 0.19% copper. It also updated progress at a nickel-copper-cobalt project in NW Ontario, supporting positive sentiment for nickel. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. announced drill results at its Kenbridge Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in northwestern Ontario, including a 3.18% Ni intersection and a small associated copper grade (0.19% Cu) over 1.05 metres. The update signals exploration progress but does not mention production changes or market supply disruption. | Norilsk Nickel outlined an expanded 2021-2030 capex plan, raising investment by $6bn to $35bn with significant allocations to ESG and energy modernization. The company also raised strategic production growth targets for 2030+ including 25-35% higher nickel volumes and 25-35% copper growth, supporting demand-linked sentiment.

SMM reports DISER expects global nickel finished products consumption to reach 2.7 million mt in 2021 (+15%), rising further in 2022-2023. While stainless steel remains the main user, the electric vehicle battery industry is described as the future growth driver, supported by decarbonisation measures and rising EV demand to offset COVID impacts. | Norilsk Nickel forecasts nickel production of 205,000-215,000 tons in 2022 and higher output for 2023-2024, while estimating a global nickel surplus of 60,000 tons in 2022. This points to increased near-term nickel supply. The firm also mentions copper output guidance and sizable capex plans. | Norilsk Nickel raised its 2021-2030 investment forecast by $6 billion to $35 billion due to higher construction costs and added spending on energy and production infrastructure upgrades. The firm expects its capex peak to shift from 2026 to 2028, supporting improved nickel production plans after recent accidents. | Copper prices fell 0.9% as Omicron uncertainties raised concerns about near-term demand and growth. China manufacturing data was stronger, and LME copper stocks are falling, limiting losses. Nickel also eased with low stocks and high cash premiums, while aluminium slipped slightly amid broader logistics and supply tightness worries. | Nickel 28 reported strong Q3 2021 results from its 8.56% interest in the Ramu nickel-cobalt operation. It repaid an operating-debt tranche and triggered a cash distribution, citing higher nickel prices versus quarter economics, strong production and sales, and improved margins and payability. | Nickel 28 reported Q3 2021 results, highlighting strong performance from its 8.56% Ramu nickel-cobalt operation in Papua New Guinea. It notes operating debt repayment and a US$3.2 million cash distribution, higher quarterly nickel margins, and sales of 8,085 tonnes of contained nickel. | Talon Metals reported new assay results from 11 drill holes at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project, including thick intersections with high nickel and copper grades outside the main resource area. The company expects the results to expand the size and extent of known resources, but it provides no immediate production or supply changes. | Talon Metals reported continued high-grade nickel and copper assay intercepts from new drill holes at its Tamarack Nickel Project in Minnesota, particularly in the CGO West area outside the main resource zone. Management says results may expand nickel-copper resources, but no immediate production or supply-impact data is provided. | Copper fell to a two-week low as Omicron uncertainty hurt demand and growth expectations and Powell’s comments suggested faster Fed tapering. LME copper stocks are declining, but further selling followed the U.S. open. The piece also highlights tight nickel inventories and easing aluminium prices amid the same macro backdrop.

SMM data shows China’s nickel downstream PMI remained in contraction in November (49.92) with weak new orders (48.33). Despite improved production after power rationing eased, nickel sulphate entered short-term oversupply and purchase-price sub-index fell with weakening nickel prices, suggesting bearish near-term nickel demand. | DMCI Holdings said its Berong nickel mine in the Philippines will run out of resources and be decommissioned and repaired before end-2021. As the Philippines is a key nickel ore supplier to China, the suspension could further tighten global nickel supply amid an ongoing INSG-noted supply gap. | Copper rose about 1% on the LME as Omicron demand concerns eased and investors regained risk appetite. The piece cites low warehouse inventories as extra support. It also flags Chile royalty bill progress, nickel mine depletion, and aluminium’s tight nearby supply via 14-year-low stocks and a cash premium.

Huayou Cobalt commissioned its hydrometallurgical nickel smelting project in Indonesia at PT. Huayue Nickel Cobalt after completing equipment installation and testing. Using laterite ore, it targets 60,000 mt of nickel and 7,800 mt of cobalt annually. The first batch has been produced and performance indicators are qualified. | Copper prices slipped as Omicron fears and slowing China growth reduced demand outlooks. LME copper was down 0.3% on the day and around 4% on the week, despite strong year-to-date performance. China growth and property guidance stayed cautious, while aluminium inventories rose 16% and other base metals moved mixed. | Huayou Cobalt announced that its Indonesia Morowali laterite nickel wet-smelting project (60,000 t/y nickel) has completed installation, entered trial production, and produced a first batch of products with qualified indicators. The update is expected to improve the company’s nickel self-supply and integration, lifting nickel sentiment. | Huayou Cobalt said its Indonesian smelting joint venture reached first trial production, with product quality meeting requirements and producing mixed hydroxide precipitate that can be processed into nickel and cobalt battery chemicals. It plans large annual nickel output using HPAL, but no full-production timeline was given. | Canada Nickel reported metallurgical testwork at its Crawford nickel sulphide project, achieving 61% nickel recovery and 37% nickel concentrate grade from lower-grade samples, above PEA modeled performance. The company says the optimized flowsheet improves recovery and will proceed with variability testing for feasibility. | China’s Chengtun plans to invest US$245 million in an Indonesian nickel matte project. The move points to future processing capacity for nickel feedstock in Indonesia, which can increase expectations for supply and likely pressure nickel prices in the near term as traders price in potential output growth. | Gungnir Resources announced final 2021 drill results from its Lappvattnet nickel deposit in Sweden, reporting high-grade nickel intersections including 6.52% Ni over 0.8 metres within a wider mineralized zone. The company also notes surface EM geophysics underway and expects new assays to feed future resource updates. | Russia’s Nornickel increased its investment guidance by billions of dollars, citing construction cost inflation and expanded modernization for energy and production assets in the Arctic. It plans upgrades including new/expanded concentrators and smelter capacity, plus a new copper production facility in Monchegorsk, alongside Arctic logistics debottlenecking.

Hongsheng International Resources, a Chengtun Mining Group subsidiary, plans to form Chengmai Nickel in Indonesia via a JV. The project targets a high-grade nickel matte output of 40,000 mt/year in nickel content, with total investment of $350m. Funding will be staged based on requirements. | Shengtun Mining plans to build a high-nickel matte project in Indonesia’s Weidabe Industrial Park with 40,000 tons/year capacity, investing about $350 million. It is intended to supply battery-grade nickel sulfate for ternary batteries, with product underwritten by JV partners. This expands nickel-related output and feedstock expectations. | The piece notes that nickel is edging above $20,000/t, implying improving near-term sentiment for nickel. It also references aluminium supply staying intact despite output curtailments and mentions severe shipping disruption through the Strait of Hormuz, factors that can affect aluminium transport and pricing. | SMM reported Chinese port nickel ore inventories fell 169,000 wmt to 9.18 million wmt (72,100 mt Ni content) by Dec 3. Seven major ports combined declined 129,000 wmt. Backlogs and slow shipments at Lanshan and shipping difficulties at Jingtang reduced arrivals, and inventories are expected to continue sliding short-term. | China’s Chengtun plans a $245m investment in an Indonesian nickel matte project (PT ChengMach Nickel), producing about 40,000 tpa on a nickel-content basis. The JV, with Chengtun majority ownership, adds to Indonesia’s nickel feedstock pipeline, potentially improving supply expectations for nickel processing. | SMM reports the SHFE/LME nickel price ratio rose this week after prior weakness. Renewed COVID-19 fears have raised concerns about overseas nickel demand, while China’s demand stays stable and nickel arrivals fall. Inventories declined, but planned shipments of nickel briquette and plate could lift supply, limiting upside.

Nornickel is testing an online, real-time air quality monitoring system for Norilsk, aiming for transparency via a mobile app. The firm cites past SO2 emission reductions after a nickel plant closure and continues investment in its Northern Project to further cut emissions, but no immediate production or trade impact is specified.

LME nickel stocks have fallen sharply since April and premiums remain wide, indicating tight prompt availability. China’s refined nickel imports accelerated since April, reaching record monthly highs and strengthening demand amid constraints from maintenance, product shifts, and energy-related disruption. The market supply gap is widening, keeping nickel tight. | SMM reports Nornickel’s nickel balance update: 2021 tightened sharply due to supply cuts and strong demand recovery, but expectations shift to a nickel surplus of about 59,000 mt in 2022 (mainly low-grade). High-grade may be balanced with possible shortages, while EV battery demand supports longer-term growth. | Since Q4, primary nickel supply has been tight and prices strong, but the article anticipates an off-season slowdown. Refined nickel supply eases gradually, nickel sulfate demand faces weaker momentum, and stainless steel production trends remain under pressure, limiting upside drivers into December/1Q. | Azimut and Mont Royal report discovering a highly prospective nickel-copper ultramafic intrusion at Wapatik. The headline suggests potential future mineralization, but the report is exploratory and does not indicate immediate changes to mine output, inventories, or refining capacity. Near-term price impact on nickel and copper is therefore limited.

Canada Nickel released improved metallurgical testing for its 100%-owned Crawford Project in Ontario, reporting 61% nickel recovery and 37% nickel concentrate from lower-grade samples. The company is starting further variability testing for its feasibility study, citing recovery gains versus the earlier PEA and potential for enhanced flowsheet performance and future supply. | EV Nickel reported high-grade nickel intersections at its Langmuir Project, including 8.66% Ni over 1.11m and other significant intervals, plus calculated nickel tenors of 18% to >40% in sulphide. Metallurgical testing is pending, but the results bolster expectations for future nickel production. | Copper fell for a third day as Omicron concerns offset China’s stimulus effects. A stronger yuan reduced costs for buyers, but investors cut Comex longs and remained cautious. Tight Chinese stockpiles and strong imports were supportive, while rising LME availability and weak deliveries tempered gains. | LME nickel inventories are falling and cash premiums have risen, while Shanghai refined nickel stocks remain extremely low. China has accelerated imports of refined nickel and nickel intermediates since April, driven by tight domestic supply and booming demand (stainless and battery-grade). Analysts expect nickel to stay tight until new supply arrives, supporting higher prices. | Australia’s Nickel Mines agreed to buy a 70% stake in Indonesia’s Oracle Nickel Project for $525m, including $371m equity purchase and $154m shareholder-loan funding. The RKEF-based project targets 36,000 tpa nickel-in-NPI capacity plus a 380MW captive power plant, with operations starting in 2023.

Great Southern Mining reports newly identified bedrock electromagnetic anomalies at its East Laverton Nickel project in Western Australia, suggesting potential blind nickel sulphide mineralization. The company plans tighter-grid electromagnetic follow-ups and borehole testing to refine conductors. Coverage highlights the region’s magmatic nickel prospects, supporting nickel sentiment. | Tsingshan began adding molten low-grade nickel matte to the #1 converter in Indonesia, completing over seven hours of blowing to produce qualified high-grade nickel matte. The start of high production at Tsingshan’s industrial park increases output of a key nickel input, likely raising short-term supply expectations for nickel. | Blackstone Minerals has restarted mining at its Ta Khoa nickel project in Vietnam and is progressing ~1,000m of development at the Ban Phuc orebody. The company plans to recommission the Ban Phuc concentrator to produce nickel concentrate batches for refinery pilot programs, using the existing plant capacity. | Horizonte Minerals said its subsidiary will acquire ferronickel processing equipment to fast-track development of a second RKEF line at its Araguaia project in Brazil. The equipment is expected to lower development cost and supports a target of higher nickel output, improving sentiment toward future nickel supply. | Rockridge completed a helicopter-borne VTEM EM and magnetic survey at its Knife Lake copper project in Saskatchewan and reports encouraging identification of conductors, while field analytical results are still pending. The update supports future drilling targeting for a Cu-Zn-Co VMS deposit, with no immediate production or supply change indicated. | Copper prices fell as Omicron-related concerns eased and the dollar firmed, adding pressure despite still-low LME inventories. LME on-warrant stocks rose to a one-month high, signaling easing nearby tightness. Nickel declined as well, while aluminium rose slightly, showing mixed pressure across industrial metals. | Fathom Nickel announced updated 2021 soil geochemistry at its Albert Lake property near the historic Rottenstone mine, identifying extensive Ni-Cu-Co anomalies consistent with magmatic nickel signatures and planning winter drilling. Nickel and copper exploration potential is increased, but no immediate production changes are stated, limiting near-term price impact. | Nickel 28’s annual shareholder letter highlights strong performance from the Ramu nickel-cobalt operation, including production on target (~31,000 tonnes nickel), low costs, and the first cash distribution after debt repayment. The letter attributes nickel strength in 2021 to electrification demand and declining LME inventories, implying supportive near-term nickel pricing.

ASX-listed Nickel Mines agreed with a Tsingshan subsidiary to acquire a controlling interest in an Indonesia project covering nickel production and a power plant for $525 million. The transaction increases Nickel Mines’ exposure to Indonesian nickel output, which may support near-term nickel market sentiment. | Nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 164,000 wmt to 9.01 million wmt as of Dec 10, with Indonesian ore nearly fully utilised. However, wet-season Philippine supply shrank while weak NPI demand and falling NPI prices kept buying cautious, muting the effect of tighter ore supply expectations. | ING says nickel rebounded as supply growth lagged expectations and inventories fell, with a structural deficit concentrated in Class 1 and tighter battery-grade feedstocks. Supply was hit by disruptions and high power costs, while battery and stainless demand surprised higher. Nickel sulphate deficits keep pressure supportive.

Shanghai nickel prices fell for three consecutive sessions, with intraday weakness before narrowing by midday. The piece attributes the move to weakening demand (stainless steel and ferro-nickel), lack of import-support for domestic pricing, and expectations that nickel plate arrivals may weigh on inventory digestion. Overall, nickel downside risk persists. | Castillo Copper reported progress on exploration and drilling at its Arya copper prospect in Queensland and continued work on Zambia assets, including IP surveys targeting areas with copper anomalies. Laboratory assay delays extend some lithium option timelines, but the article contains no immediate changes to copper production or supply.

The piece provides commodity outlooks and highlights nickel. It notes Indonesia’s strategy to ban nickel ore exports, boosting value-add flows to China (nickel pig iron, stainless steel, mixed hydroxide) and expects nickel production to double, supporting strong export revenue upside and thus a bullish nickel price bias.

INSG reported that the global nickel market moved from a September shortage of 3,100 tons to a small October surplus of 5,000 tons. For the first ten months of 2021, however, the market still ran a large supply shortfall versus the same period last year. | Shanghai nickel and spot nickel in major regions fell and market sentiment remains weak. The article links the decline to soft stainless steel demand, ferro-nickel inventory and cautious shipment expectations, and pessimism from Indonesia’s Aoyama high-nickel matte commissioning. It notes possible further downside and recommends short positions. | SMM reports tight global ferronickel supply in the short term, driven by China’s ongoing power/energy consumption restrictions and declining ferronickel output. Nickel ore imports and port inventories are limited as the Philippines rainy season reduces output. With cautious trading and firmer demand from stainless steel and batteries, nickel prices are supported. | London copper was flat as investors awaited a key U.S. Fed decision on timing of tapering and potential earlier rate hikes. The dollar’s strength and possible liquidity tightening were a focus. LME aluminium eased, nickel was steady, and Shanghai contracts were slightly lower, indicating limited net near-term momentum.

The piece argues Australia has exported raw battery minerals rather than processing them, while China dominates processing of critical battery minerals. It warns that any restrictions on China’s refined battery-material exports could disrupt battery manufacturers in India, South Korea and Japan, potentially tightening supply expectations for nickel used in batteries. | Global Energy Metals signed an agreement to sell a 1% NSR on Norway’s Rana nickel-copper-cobalt project to Electric Royalties, while Metals One plans to acquire 90% and fund exploration. The deal is aimed at unlocking remaining Ni-Cu potential, but implies no immediate supply change. | Global Energy Metals signed a definitive agreement to sell a 1% net smelter royalty on Norway’s Rana Ni-Cu-Co project to Electric Royalties, with Metals One planning a funded exploration program and resource update timeline. The transaction is supportive for nickel supply expectations and, secondarily, copper via the mixed deposit grades. | Talon Metals updates its Tamarack nickel-copper project, reporting new high-grade drill intersections and outlining an aggressive 2022 exploration program to progress toward pre-feasibility and expand resource potential. It also establishes an ATM program to fund operations, citing strong US interest in domestic battery nickel supply.

SMM reports the inner metal market trading higher. Shanghai nickel rises over 2.7% and Shanghai aluminium over 2.7% (around 2%+). Copper also stays high and volatile, with spot prices up for two days, though downstream buying weakens after replenishment. Overall sentiment remains supported by a weaker USD. | SMM reports a broad metal rally in China as the dollar moved higher then the market stayed bid. Shanghai nickel rose about 2.75% and Shanghai aluminium about 2.7% on supportive spot dynamics. Copper remained high and volatile for two straight days, despite weaker downstream replenishment. | SMM reports the SHFE/LME nickel price ratio rose amid shortages of domestic cargoes. When the spot nickel import window reopened, traders moved nickel plate from bonded zones into China’s domestic market. Limited briquette imports and lower prices revived downstream buying, sharply reducing plate inventories, with premiums at a yearly high and less expected inflow next week. | Electric Royalties agreed to buy a 1% NSR for Norway’s Rana nickel project on the Ofoten Fjord, paying shares and $100,000 cash to the current owners. The project’s past production and planned exploration under Metals One are positioned as potential future nickel supply, supportive for nickel sentiment.

SMM notes early mixed trading: Shanghai aluminium rose ~1.6% on low inventories and tight supply, while nickel rose slightly as tight absolute inventories eased via limited imports, though fundamentals remain weak. Copper was pressured by a stronger dollar amid BoE/ECB tightening and Covid cases, keeping moves mixed overall. | Australian miner IGO will acquire Western Areas for A$1.1 billion, taking on debt and funding the deal partly from cash. The transaction is set to expand IGO’s nickel production capacity via Forrestania and the Odysseus mine, amid expectations of sharply rising EV battery demand for nickel. | Vedanta acquired Goa-based nickel and cobalt producer Nicomet, aiming to boost India’s domestic nickel supply. The firm says it will become India’s sole nickel producer and that Nicomet’s 7.5 KTPA capacity can meet about 50% of total demand. The move comes as nickel markets tighten on EV battery and stainless demand. | A Singaporean businessman accused of masterminding a large nickel trading scam received six additional criminal charges, bringing the total to 75. Prosecutors allege about US$124 million in cash and assets were involved. The report details courtroom filings but does not indicate changes to physical nickel supply or trade flows. | Pacific Nickel Mines received development consent from Solomon Islands authorities for its Kolosori Nickel Project, advancing the path to a mining lease and eventual production. The project targets output of 6.23m tonnes at 1.5% nickel, with early works and feasibility steps underway. Such progress is mildly supportive for nickel supply expectations.

SMM reports China imported 4.01 million mt of nickel ore and concentrate in November, down 10% month on month. Imports from the Philippines and Indonesia also fell. Copper NPI imports were slightly lower and much weaker year on year, suggesting reduced nickel feedstock inflow that may support nickel prices near term. | Norilsk Nickel plans about $8.1B (600B rubles) to 2030 to upgrade energy infrastructure in Russia’s Krasnoyarsk north, including new power units, heat and hydro plant replacements, 1,000+ km of transmission lines, and gas pipeline works. SMM cites Global Palladium Fund forecasts a 2022 global nickel surplus of about 59,000 tons, concentrated in low-grade NPI depending on Indonesian capacity ramp-ups. It notes COVID-delayed NPI expansion earlier and highlights uncertain higher-grade supply versus rising EV battery demand, suggesting softer nickel fundamentals. | An SMM DISER-linked review attributes nickel’s 2021 price rise to early demand (stainless steel rebound, EV battery demand) and later supply-side shocks.
