The deal targets Noront’s Eagle Nest nickel asset in the Ring of Fire. Both companies pursued the nickel supply for EV batteries, with the transaction likely supporting tighter near-term nickel supply expectations. | SMM reports Shanghai nickel rebounded after five consecutive down days, rising about 1.87% to 145,820 yuan/ton. Prices received some support from low pure nickel inventories and a broader non-ferrous move tied to crude oil. Yet spot transactions and stainless/new-energy nickel demand remain weak, so SMM expects range volatility.

Blackstone Minerals announced a 73% jump in Ta Khoa’s nickel resource base in Vietnam, now totaling about 485,000 tonnes of contained nickel (0.37% Ni on average) across multiple deposits. The update is resource-focused, with further drilling and study work planned, not new near-term nickel output.

China’s customs data show nickel ore and concentrate imports in November at 4.0076 Mt, down 10.47% from October but up 12.08% versus a year earlier. Shipments from the Philippines were the majority, also lower m/m, with rainy-season conditions reducing transport volume, affecting near-term supply expectations for nickel. | SMM cites Chinese customs data showing refined nickel imports of 26,069 mt in November. While imports fell 24.4% month-on-month, they more than doubled versus a year earlier (+102.5% YoY). January–November imports were also up sharply year-on-year, signaling higher nickel inflows. | Spot nickel import profits are positive (~250 yuan/mt) as traders move cargoes from bonded inventories into China. However, Shanghai inventory growth is limited because some cargoes were presold. Domestic pure nickel inventories are expected to stay low next week, with only 1,500–2,000 mt arrivals, keeping the SHFE/LME ratio high. | Chinese ports’ nickel ore inventories fell 118,000 wmt to 8.66 million wmt as of Dec 24, with Ni content at 68,000 mt. Inventories have declined for five straight weeks after reduced imports, though downstream demand remains weak due to high costs and restrictions. Further declines are expected before Chinese New Year.

Copper rallied to a one-month high after the LME reopened following a two-day holiday break, rising 1.2% to about $9,679/t, with prices supported by COMEX-LME arbitrage. The piece also notes mixed price action in aluminium and nickel on LME and Shanghai, but no metal-specific drivers beyond trading conditions. | Zhongwei signed a strategic cooperation framework with Xiamen Xiangyu to secure nickel (about 80,000 tons of nickel metal) and cobalt raw materials over 2022-2027. The deal emphasizes raw material supply and supply-chain security, supporting nickel availability expectations for the company’s precursor production.

Indonesia’s President inaugurated a $2.7 billion ferronickel plant in southeast Sulawesi, invested by a Chinese company. The project is intended to deepen the local supply chain and improve Indonesia’s role in global value chains. Added production capacity would raise expectations for nickel supply and likely support prices. | Copper traded in a narrow range ahead of the New Year holiday, slightly lower on the LME and Shanghai. Supportive factors include falling inventories and expectations of expanding demand. Copper risk comes from uncertainty over Las Bambas’ restart. Aluminium rose on supply concerns and Alcoa’s temporary Spain shutdown; nickel also ticked up. | SMM reports Shanghai nonferrous metals closed mixed. Aluminium rose sharply as European aluminium output is cut due to surging electricity prices, with falling social inventories. Nickel also gained on tight supply and lower SHFE/LME inventories, despite future Philippine ore pickup. Copper edged down slightly amid a quiet spot market. | SMM reports LME nickel inventories falling by 636 tonnes on Dec 30, led by declines at Singapore and Rotterdam warehouses. The lower visible stock levels suggest tighter near-term availability on the LME, which usually improves sentiment and can underpin nickel price performance over the following days. | The report describes early-session mixed moves across metals: Shanghai aluminium up ~1.56% and Shanghai nickel up ~1.27%, while copper fluctuates around 70,000 yuan/ton. It attributes aluminium strength to European energy price shocks and Alcoa planning a two-year Spanish electrolytic shutdown, but overall trading remains range-bound.

Biden issued proclamations replacing some Section 232 tariffs with TRQs for EU steel and aluminum. Aluminum faces in-quota treatment only up to specified TRQ volumes (and certain exclusions may count against quota), while out-of-quota volumes remain subject to tariffs. The U.S. will monitor implementation through Dec. 2023. | Indonesia’s government signals strong support for an EV battery development plan with cooperation from investors and research institutions. The article argues that rising EV procurement and manufacturing will increase nickel demand as a battery raw material, implying favorable prospects for nickel prices, given Indonesia’s dominant position in nickel production. | Nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 65,000 wmt week-on-week to 8.59 million wmt as of Dec 31. Total Ni content was 67,500 mt. Declines persisted but at a slower pace as arrivals and loading slowed and unloading pressure eased, while some NPI plants may maintain in January.

Lunnon Metals announced drill results from the Warren shoot in Western Australia’s Kambalda nickel district, including 8.72m grading 3.54% nickel and an interval of 2.33m at 4.41% nickel. The company highlights momentum for follow-up drilling. The report also references falling LME nickel inventories, reinforcing a bullish nickel tone. | Copper fell about 1% on the LME as the dollar strengthened and Treasury yields rose, with traders pricing an early U.S. Fed hike. Rising Omicron cases also weighed on sentiment. The same macro pressure pulled down LME aluminium and nickel, alongside other base metals. | Copper prices fell as a firmer U.S. dollar and uncertainty about Omicron’s impact weighed on industrial-metals sentiment. Three-month copper on the LME dropped 1%. The article also notes declines in LME aluminum and nickel on the day, reflecting broader macro-driven bearish pressure. | Aluminium prices rose as market participants worried that prolonged high European power costs could trigger further supply disruptions; LME aluminium inventories are down over 60% since mid-March. The same inventory-tightness theme and cancelled warrants point to potential further draws on nickel supply, supporting a broader bullish tone. | EV Nickel reports assay results from the second half of Phase 1 drilling at its Langmuir Nickel Project, including near-surface nickel sulphide intercepts (e.g., ~15.7m at 1.14% Ni and 19.5m at 0.96% Ni). The company says Phase 2 drilling begins next. | Aluminum prices rose about 1% on Tuesday as traders flagged supply disruptions linked to prolonged high electricity and gas costs, with low LME inventories and expectations of additional warehouse departures. Industrial metals broadly gained on a weaker dollar amid uncertainty in Europe’s energy market. Nickel also increased alongside the move.

The piece describes the Goro nickel mine in New Caledonia, potentially holding up to about one-quarter of global nickel reserves. It emphasizes Tesla’s push to secure responsible battery mineral supply chains. The mine is described as suspended, raising expectations of tighter nickel supply in the near term. | SMM reports nickel prices in China exceeding 150,000 yuan/mt as pure nickel inventories remain low and spot premiums stay firm. Traders completed long-term order activity, reducing arriving shipments, while stainless demand supports NPI and brisk nickel sulphate restocking. Despite expectations of easing premiums from arrivals, technicals suggest further upside for nickel this week. | SMM says battery-grade nickel sulphate quotes rose to 34,500–35,500 yuan/mt on higher nickel prices and higher upstream quotes as low-priced cargoes were consumed. Spot inventories declined after downstream stockpiling, easing cash flow and limiting discount sales. Prices are expected to stay within 34,500–35,500 this week, with nickel poised to rise. | Vale’s CEO said the company is in talks with US automakers and has agreed to sell 5% of its annual first-class nickel production to a carmaker, widely rumored to be Tesla. The deal supports nickel supply linked to North American battery-material demand, likely tightening sentiment. | London copper fell 0.4% from a six-week high as the U.S. dollar held firm on expectations for early Federal Reserve rate hikes, potentially reducing market liquidity. LME aluminium dipped 0.1% and nickel slipped 0.7% in tandem, reflecting broad macro pressure. Separate data points were also cited. | SMM cites LME data showing nickel inventories continued to fall, reaching about 101,256 tons—lowest in over two years—down roughly 61.7% from late April 2021. Shanghai inventories also declined, down 12.25% to 5,057 tons, reinforcing tighter near-term nickel supply expectations.

Lunnon Metals says recent drilling at its Kambalda project (old Foster mine, N75C) returned high-grade nickel, including 7.7m at 2.92% Ni and adjacent intercepts around 3% Ni. Management says historic workings likely host unmined nickel and plans to finalize an updated resource estimate.  | SMM reports December 2021 PMI for China’s nickel downstream industries stayed in contraction (composite 49.59). Production fell to 49.02 with weak readings in stainless steel and electroplating, while new orders were mostly contracting and declined sharply late in 2021. Nickel sulphate purchasing prices were stable with small fluctuations. | SMM says domestic nickel and stainless steel prices fell more than 2% as markets priced in the possibility of U.S. rate hikes as early as March, raising risk concerns. Despite low inventories supporting levels, the article expects greater downside pressure and near-term nickel weakness, partly offset by upstream supply mismatches. | Bitterroot Resources and its JV plan to resume drilling at the LM nickel-copper project in Michigan the week of Jan. 10, targeting the SE quadrant of the LM intrusion. The first hole will test for nickel and copper massive sulphides based on earlier high-grade intersections, with drilling serving exploration rather than production. | Bitterroot Resources and its JV partner plan to resume drilling at the LM nickel-copper project in Michigan, targeting the keel of a massive sulphide body with prior high-grade nickel and copper intercepts. The news is exploration-focused, with no near-term production or supply change implied. | Bitterroot Resources and Below Exploration plan to resume drilling in January 2022 at the LM nickel-copper project in Michigan, targeting massive sulphide zones for new intersections. Prior holes reported high-grade nickel and copper results. The update is exploration-focused with no production or supply disruption details. | Bitterroot Resources and Below Exploration plan to resume drilling during the week of January 10, 2022 at the LM nickel-copper property in Michigan. Drilling targets massive sulphide zones for further testing. The release cites prior intercepts with high nickel and copper grades but no production changes.

The piece reports that China’s nickel sulfate prices rebounded in the first week of 2022, implying firmer pricing for a key nickel feedstock. It also notes that Indonesia’s power tightness did not have a major market impact, suggesting limited near-term downside pressure from that factor. | SMM reports that the SHFE/LME nickel price ratio has bottomed and the import window has reopened, improving traders’ profitability. Bonded-zone nickel inventory rose mainly due to nickel plant supply, which may extend into next week and flow into the domestic market given tight local supply. Spot premiums remain high, and import data will be watched. | SMM said Chinese nickel ore inventories rose 86,000 wmt to 8.67 million wmt (6.81 million mt Ni content) as of Jan 7. Arrivals were limited by low Philippine exports in the rainy season. NPI output stayed stable but constrained by environmental limits and maintenance, reducing ore demand. Inventory may drop slightly soon. | The article headline says Guatemala lifted the suspension of the Fénix nickel mine. If operations resume, the market would likely anticipate higher near-term nickel supply, which typically weighs on nickel prices within the next days to four weeks.

The piece weighs differing views on 2022 nickel supply-demand, citing INSG surplus/shortage estimates and Commerzbank/Nornickel/ANZ scenarios. It highlights Indonesia’s expanding projects but also uncertainty from disruptions, China power cuts, and possible export bans or taxes—factors that could offset each other and leave prices range-bound. | SMM reports tight nickel supply with low spot availability and mentions global nickel inventories staying at historically low levels and LME nickel transfer fees at multi-month highs. Spot prices/rising waters supported firm sentiment, while downstream stainless steel restocking/pre-holiday demand improved. The outlook remains supported but wary of stainless steel maintenance. | SMM’s daily review says most metals rose, led by stainless steel, with Shanghai aluminium up over 1% for a fourth straight session and aluminium inventories decreasing. Shanghai nickel rose about 2.5% as spot supply remained tight and premiums increased. Copper also edged higher, supporting a bullish near-term tone. | BHP plans to invest $40 million into Kabanga Nickel’s Tanzanian nickel project and $10 million into Lifezone’s cleaner refining technology to cut emissions and improve efficiency. The project targets first nickel production in 2025, ramping later, but the news supports positive near-term sentiment for nickel supply prospects. | BHP plans to invest up to $100 million in Tanzania’s Kabanga Nickel project, including an initial $40 million, as part of its expansion into “future-facing commodities.” The deposit is described as the world’s largest development-ready nickel sulphide resource, supporting bullish sentiment toward nickel supply prospects. | BHP will invest $90m in Tanzania’s Kabanga Nickel project, with tranches converting into equity and expanding its stake subject to approvals and milestones. The project is expected to produce 65,000 tonnes nickel equivalent annually, supporting nickel supply development, alongside plans to advance low-carbon hydromet refining technology. | Talon Metals and Tesla signed a multi-year supply agreement for Tesla to buy 75,000 metric tonnes of nickel concentrate from the Tamarack Nickel Project in Minnesota, with Tesla holding a right to additional volumes. The price is linked to the LME official nickel cash settlement, subject to conditions for commercial production. | SMM’s afternoon review notes Shanghai nickel rising about 2.56% and Shanghai aluminium gaining roughly 1.1% by midday, while copper also moved higher. For copper, Tianjin epidemic-related disruption left spot trading deserted, with electrolytic copper discounts narrowing. Overall moves appear intraday and mixed across the strip. | Tesla and Talon have reportedly entered into a nickel concentrate supply agreement, signaling secured sourcing of nickel feedstock for battery-related production. Such deals can tighten perceived near-term nickel concentrate availability and improve demand visibility, usually supporting modest upward pressure in nickel prices over the next days to weeks. | BHP agreed to invest in Tanzania’s Kabanga Nickel project, expanding development and refining efforts tied to future nickel sulphide production. The piece links nickel demand to EV battery growth and policy-driven electrification, suggesting supply support and stronger demand expectations, which are modestly positive for nickel sentiment. | Indonesia’s President said the country will stop exporting bauxite ore from 2022 and copper ore in 2023 to accelerate downstream processing and exports of higher-value products. Indonesia already banned nickel ore exports in 2020, signaling a broader shift that could tighten traded ore supplies supporting bearish price pressure. | Indonesia plans to halt bauxite ore exports in 2022 and copper ore exports in 2023 to boost downstream processing and exports of higher-value products. It already banned nickel ore exports from Jan 1, 2020. These actions tighten supply of key raw inputs for aluminum and copper markets.

Tesla has signed a nickel concentrate supply deal with Talon Metals from the Tamarack Nickel Project in Minnesota. Tesla plans to buy about 75,000 tons of nickel concentrate over six years at LME-listed prices, supporting battery-material sourcing and potentially tightening near-term nickel supply expectations. | BHP will invest about $100m in Tanzania’s Kabanga nickel project, including buying an 8.9% stake with potential to raise it further. The move reflects a shift back to nickel amid green-energy demand. Kabanga also targets copper production, but output is planned from 2025 onward, limiting near-term price impact. | Tesla signed its first US nickel supply deal to buy 75,000 tonnes of nickel over six years from Talon Metals’ Tamarack mine in Minnesota, with options to increase tonnage. The contract supports nickel demand visibility and development funding, likely boosting sentiment for nickel prices in the near term. | SMM reports nickel prices pulled back but remain supported by low refined nickel inventories and continued stock declines. Spot premiums over futures expanded and the import window stayed open, easing tight supply. Nickel sulfate demand and restocking activity were strong, with fewer producers cutting output, leaving few bearish factors. | Resources & Energy reports petrographic confirmation of magmatic Ni-Fe sulphides at its Springfield (East Menzies) project, identifying nickel minerals such as pentlandite and related sulphides in RC drill samples. The company expects further multi-element and PGE/gold assays in coming weeks, but no production or supply change is announced. | Dundas Minerals says its maiden drilling at the Kokoda gold prospect will finish this week, and the rig will move to the nearby Jumbuck nickel prospect. Jumbuck has historic anomalous nickel grades (0.5% in a 2011 hole) with untested conductive anomalies, and drilling is expected to start this weekend. | Tesla signed its first U.S. nickel supply agreement with Talon Metals, tied to nickel concentrate prices on the London Metals Exchange. The deal sources from Talon’s Tamarack project in Minnesota and is aimed at more environmentally friendly battery materials. Tesla plans to buy 75,000 tonnes over six years. | Nickel prices held near two-month highs as electric-vehicle battery demand boosted offtake and reduced nickel stocks in LME-approved warehouses. LME inventory has fallen sharply since April 2021, with high cancelled warrants and a wider cash premium versus the three-month contract indicating tightening supply. Aluminium is also mentioned. | Tesla said it signed its first U.S. nickel supply deal with Talon Metals, selecting the Tamarack mine in Minnesota for its EV battery material. Tesla plans to buy 75,000 tonnes of nickel over six years and will also purchase cobalt and iron ore from Talon. | LME nickel futures gained around 3% to $21,500/tonne, the highest since May 2014. Prices rose as electric-car battery demand reduced LME nickel inventories, which fell about 62% since April 2021. Warehouse stock levels were 99,954 tonnes and nearly half were write-off receipts, signaling continued supply pressure. | SMM reports Shanghai nonferrous metals closed mixed around Jan 11. Nickel led with a 3.45% gain, supported by falling SHFE inventories, LME nickel and tight spot pure-nickel supply. Aluminium rose 1.97% with inventories down, despite weakening demand. Copper was essentially flat, though LME copper inventory fell. | SMM’s daily review reports mixed metal moves in Asia: Shanghai nickel rose more than 3% and stainless steel also jumped, while Shanghai aluminium climbed nearly 2%. Copper edged down slightly (~-0.01%). The piece attributes some nickel support to low inventories and improved spot tightness. | France plans to raise €1bn to support industries in securing battery raw materials amid soaring prices, including nickel. The government intends to reduce dependence on nickel, cobalt and lithium outside the EU and push Renault and Stellantis toward domestic battery production. It also seeks investment in processing projects such as in Dunkirk. | Talon Metals agreed to supply Tesla with 75,000MT of nickel concentrate from its Tamarack project, with pricing linked to the LME nickel cash settlement. The deal is conditional on reaching required stake and starting commercial production by 2026. Tesla also holds rights to negotiate additional supply, supporting nickel demand visibility. | Tesla signed a deal with Talon Metals to source carbon-neutral nickel from a Minnesota plant. Talon expects to sell at least 165 million pounds of nickel to Tesla over six years, with pricing linked to the LME nickel official price. The announcement signals firmer nickel demand expectations. | Tesla has signed a deal to source nickel from a Minnesota mine. The agreement links Tesla’s battery supply chain to a specific nickel source, potentially tightening expectations around available supply for the near term and supporting nickel pricing sentiment over the coming days to weeks. | United Steelworkers praised Talon Metals’ six-year nickel supply agreement for the Tamarack Project in central Minnesota. The union highlighted the deal’s role in providing customer stability and supporting domestic sourcing for battery-related nickel demand, reinforcing expectations for nickel supply continuity. | Talon Metals and Tesla agreed on a supply deal for nickel from the Tamarack project in Minnesota. Tesla will buy 75,000 tonnes of nickel in concentrate over six years, with pricing linked to the LME nickel cash settlement. The contract supports demand sentiment for EV battery nickel. | Tesla agreed to buy 75,000 tonnes of nickel concentrates from Talon Metals’ Tamarack project, with pricing linked to the LME cash-settlement nickel price. The supply contract strengthens near-term nickel demand sentiment as automakers secure battery-material inputs. Tamarack also involves copper, but impact is mainly nickel-focused.

The article notes that base metal prices on the LME are rising, highlighting that nickel gained about 4.8%. While much of the text is promotional and procedural, the headline datapoint points to immediate upward momentum for nickel prices in the near term. | GEM said its Indonesian nickel resource project’s key equipment was shipped to Indonesia and installation was completed by mid-May 2021. The project has moved from infrastructure to equipment installation, and is scheduled to begin operations in H1 2022 to secure nickel for precursor-material development, potentially supporting nickel sentiment. | Nickel prices jumped to the highest level in nearly 10 years, approaching $10 a pound, as on-warrant inventories fell to multi-year lows and an LME outage disrupted trading for several hours. Tight supplies were reinforced by lower stainless steel output during maintenance and seasonal/Beijing Olympics curbs, alongside EV-driven demand. | Nickel prices jumped to a 10-year peak as investors focused on dwindling LME/ShFE inventories, tight nearby supplies (cash premiums), and forecasts of a widening market deficit for EV batteries. The broader industrial metals complex also rose, with copper and aluminium moving higher as supply worries and China stimulus expectations improved sentiment. | Pedro Resources announced sulphide mineral indicator results from stream sediment surveys on its Voisey’s Bay West property and staked additional adjacent licenses to cover potential upstream sources. The sediments contain chalcopyrite and pentlandite consistent with Voisey’s Bay-type ore, but the release is early exploration with no production impact. | BHP’s nickel partner expects strong demand and potential premiums for “greener” battery metals after BHP agreed to invest in Tanzania’s Kabanga Nickel project. The deal targets faster development and hydrometallurgy refining with lower emissions, supporting nickel supply sentiment for EV-focused buyers seeking ESG-aligned inputs. | BHP plans to invest around $50m into the Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, with $40m already invested into Kabanga Nickel Limited. The project is described as the largest development-ready nickel sulphide deposit in the world, which can bolster nickel market sentiment on continued supply development.

Huayou Cobalt forecast a 2021 net profit jump, citing strong demand and higher prices for new-energy battery materials. It also announced an acquisition related to a lithium mine in Zimbabwe and highlights integrated nickel/cobalt-to-lithium production capacity growth. The news is company-specific and offers no clear near-term market dislocation for traded metals. | Nickel prices surged to their highest in a decade, driven by continued LME and Shanghai inventory declines, strong backwardation, and tightening warehouse stocks that signal a potential squeeze. Copper also rose to fresh peaks on renewed supply fears. Aluminium edged higher amid broader industrial metals strength and improving China sentiment. | The piece centers on Indonesia’s policy shift to keep nickel “at home,” implying less material nickel supply for overseas buyers, particularly China. By limiting export availability and potentially tightening near-term supply, the change is expected to support nickel prices over the coming days to weeks. | Indonesia is considering a progressive export levy on nickel pig iron and ferronickel that could be imposed as soon as this year. The move is expected to reduce nickel feedstock flows to China’s steel and EV supply chains, increasing cost and supply pressure that can lift nickel prices. | Nickel prices are surging to a ten-year high as EV makers rush to secure battery supplies. The London Metal Exchange reports warehouse nickel stocks have fallen for 51 straight days and China reserves are near record lows. Nickel jumped up to 4.4% on Wednesday amid strong demand expectations. | Awalé Resources announced follow-up soil and termitaria sampling at its Sceptre IOCG copper-gold target in Odienné, Côte d’Ivoire, defining new copper–gold anomalies and planning geophysics and drilling in 2022. The release highlights elevated Cu/Au footprints and compares the geology to major IOCG provinces, but provides no production or supply changes. | Nickel prices surged 4% to a decade high, supported by continued declines in global stockpiles (including record low China warehouse stocks) and strong EV-related demand growth. The piece also highlights new nickel supply arrangements and investment in nickel projects, reinforcing bullish sentiment for near-term nickel fundamentals.

SMM expects nickel prices to rise in January, citing tight spot supply and strong fundamentals. Global inventories are low, LME nickel remains in backwardation, and domestic spot premiums stay elevated. Pre-CNY importing is limited, while nickel sulphate trading is active due to stockpiling and smelter output cuts, supporting demand. | Nickel starts the year at a 10-year high, up nearly 6% YTD, driven by falling LME exchange stocks and tight Class 1 availability. Demand support comes from rapid EV and battery growth (boosting nickel sulfate needs), while supply risk persists after smelter disruption and constrained feedstocks. | SMM’s midday review shows downside pressure across most non-ferrous markets. Shanghai aluminium fell about 2.18% at midday, while Shanghai copper dropped around 0.78%. Shanghai nickel ultimately recovered and closed up about 0.97%. The US dollar eased slightly, but demand/spot activity remained soft, weighing on prices. | Indonesia’s deputy minister said the government may start taxing exports of ferronickel and nickel pig iron in 2022. A 2% tax is likely if nickel prices are above $15,000/tonne and increases with higher nickel prices, aiming to drive downstream investment. The measure could curb export supply. | Singapore’s High Court dismissed businessman Ng Yu Zhi’s bid to stay a $23m civil suit tied to an alleged $1 billion nickel trading scam. The case stems from a purported investment scheme run by his firms, while he faces 75 criminal charges. The report does not mention physical nickel supply or trading disruptions. | SMM said nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 78,000 wmt week-on-week to 8.59 million wmt as of Jan 14 (67,500 mt Ni content). Arrivals of laterite ore were low, and downstream purchasing before Chinese New Year was weak, leaving port stock levels tight. | SMM reviews 2021 nickel inventory trends: domestic stocks fell after mid-May, bonded-area nickel inflows slowed near year-end, and LME inventories slid down as nickel beans and plates flowed into China. It describes tight spot pure nickel and expects 2022 de-stocking, though at a slower pace later.

SMM reports low global nickel inventories and high LME nickel 3-month transfer fees supporting tightness and elevated nickel prices. It also highlights Indonesia’s possible export tax on nickel ore/pig iron if prices stay above $15,000, plus ongoing supply-side disturbances. Overall, near-term nickel remains firm despite volatility. | China’s 2021 aluminium production rose 4.8% to a record 38.5 million tonnes, even with energy-consumption curbs at smelters. December output was slightly up month-on-month but down year-on-year. China also produced more of a non-ferrous basket including copper and nickel, but overall the aluminium supply outlook is bearish. | China’s raw aluminum output rose 4.8% in 2021 to a record 38.5 million tons, despite energy-consumption limits. December primary aluminum production was near November but still high versus the prior year. The article also notes overall non-ferrous output, including copper and nickel, strengthening near-term supply expectations. | China’s official data shows 2021 primary aluminium output rose 4.8% to a record 38.5 million tonnes, even with factory power consumption curbs. December output edged down versus November, but remains high. The report also tracks other non-ferrous output (including copper and nickel) lower year-on-year, suggesting pressure from ample supply. | LME base metal futures were mixed at the 9am open, with nickel down 1.2%. The rest of the piece discusses Fastmarkets/Metal Bulletin changes, including delayed or corrected aluminium and steel-related assessments and methodology/publication timing updates. No comparable immediate copper or aluminium price move is specified. | Araguiaia Niquel Metias finalized a $346.2 million ECA-backed loan to fund its Horizonte Minerals Araguaia ferronickel project in Brazil. With limited near-term arrivals and high premiums likely to persist, downstream stainless and nickel sulphate buyers stocked before CNY. Risks include earlier Fed hikes or higher-than-expected arrivals.

SMM reports Shanghai nickel rising and breaking above 160,000 yuan/ton to a record level. It attributes strength to tight global/refined nickel inventories, rising nickel sulfate costs, stable downstream replenishment before the Spring Festival, and expectations of Indonesia introducing export duties on ferronickel, raising import costs. | Platinex completed the purchase of the W2 copper-nickel-PGE project from Springer Mineral Resources in Ontario’s Ring of Fire area, including potential smelter returns royalties. The deal targets known Cu-Ni-PGE mineralisation but does not indicate near-term output, so any impact on copper and nickel prices is likely limited. | Zijin Mining reported a major 2021 profit jump and said it expects 2022 copper output to rise sharply, supported by higher copper prices and contributions from several copper mines. The update reinforces near-term copper production and price optimism, though gold underperformance in 2021 is noted as a partial offset. | Fathom Nickel announced it bought back a 2% net smelter return royalty tied to its Albert Lake nickel-copper property in Saskatchewan for $175,000 and started a 5,000m Q1-2022 drill program. The release highlights improved project economics and future assays, without any stated production or supply impact. | Nickel cash contracts on the LME reportedly hit a $90/tonne premium versus the next day, the highest since 2010, driven by the biggest supply squeeze in over a decade. Plunging inventories are forcing buyers to pay for immediate metal, prompting heightened LME monitoring and possible further measures. | Nickel prices are supported by falling exchange and warehouse inventories, with LME stockpiles dropping for a record stretch and China’s official stocks near a low. The market runs a sizable deficit, while EV and stainless demand growth expectations keep buyers engaged. Aluminum inventories are also described as in deficit. | Aston Minerals reported thick nickel sulphide mineralisation at its Edleston gold project in Canada, including 282m at 0.43% nickel and higher-grade intervals up to 0.52% nickel, plus an 18m final interval at 0.66% nickel. The company plans further resource definition drilling and studies.

Boliden says nickel production at its Harjavalta smelter has resumed after a December 24 stoppage from a slag explosion. Output will be ramped back to the site’s annual feed capacity of about 370 ktonnes. No injuries were reported, signaling reduced near-term supply disruption risk for nickel. | The article reports nickel breaking above $23,000/t on the LME, signaling near-term bullish momentum. It also provides context on aluminium market tightness, stating output curtailments in parts of the Gulf and severe shipping disruption through the Strait of Hormuz, though it characterizes aluminium as “hard to move.”

Nickel prices surged to the highest since 2011 as a supply shortage cut LME and Shanghai warehouse stocks, pushing tom/next and cash premiums to multi-year highs. The piece also links higher power prices to reduced smelter output, tightening aluminium, while copper rises as well. | SMM says base metals rose overnight, with copper up ~1.5%, aluminium up ~1.1%, and nickel surging over 5% on tighter overseas inventories and supportive demand/infrastructure expectations. Copper gains were linked to stronger oil prices/IEA demand outlook, a weaker US dollar, and potential changes in premiums as market conditions evolve. | SMM reports Shanghai nickel prices rose sharply and broke key levels, with spot prices also firming. It attributes gains to possible Indonesia taxes on ferro-nickel, improving stainless-steel downstream demand, and continued declines in nickel inventories (including LME to the lowest since 2019) after Myanmar production was disrupted. | The news highlights that nickel has jumped to around $24,000 as “supply snarls” tighten availability in the market. Such delivery or supply disruptions typically reduce near-term supply and lift prices, implying continued bullish pressure on nickel in the coming days. | SunSirs reports Chinese spot nickel at 166,683.33 yuan/ton on Jan 19, down 0.34% day-on-day. It links the mild decline to a stronger dollar after rising U.S. bond yields, while noting Indonesian ferronickel output starting and pre-holiday stocking ending. A Myanmar FeNi plant was suspended after power towers were blown up.

LME nickel gained over 7% for two consecutive days and SHFE extended the rally. SMM cites low inventories and insufficient warehouse receipts as key contradictions, amplified by shocks including Myanmar’s Dagong Mountain power-plant bombing, higher European carbon prices/Indonesia carbon tax, and Indonesia’s nickel export tariffs, raising supply-side concerns. | Nickel is in the most severe squeeze in over a decade as LME inventories fall and the market scrambles for battery-grade “Class 1” nickel. LME three-month prices have surged above $22,000/t, cash premiums widen sharply, backwardation spikes, and Shanghai stocks hit multi-year lows amid rising demand and deficits. | Nickel surged to a 2011 peak on very low exchange inventories, with Russia-related sanctions threats adding supply concerns. Markets also anticipate stronger EV battery demand. LME three-month nickel rose sharply, and traders linked the broader rally to deficits ahead; aluminium was also cited as exposed to supply risk. | Nickel prices surged, helped by LME inventory drawdowns to multi-year lows and expectations of higher EV battery demand. The LME three-month contract hit the highest since 2011, while traders also flagged supply concerns, including a larger projected nickel deficit. Sanctions fears over Russia are also expected to widen aluminium supply gaps. | Indonesia’s Ministry of Energy targets higher ferronickel production in 2022 (to 1.67 million tons), including increased nickel pig iron and nickel matte. It also forecasts a slight increase in electrolytic copper production. The news is about production targets, implying limited near-term price impact.  | SMM says Shanghai nonferrous metals closed mixed amid US inflation pressure. SHFE copper rose 0.64% as copper inventories fell on the week; aluminium fell 0.28% due to resumed capacity in Yunnan/Qinghai and weaker pre-CNY demand; nickel surged 4.21% on low inventories and backwardation. | Chinese ports’ nickel ore inventories fell 142,000 wmt to 8.45 million wmt (66,400 mt Ni content) as of Jan 21, with laterite imports dropping in December due to wet-season disruptions. Despite weaker downstream buying, inventory is set to remain on a downward trend, supporting nickel prices.

Shanghai and LME nickel prices surged to multi-year/all-time highs, with the piece citing tight nickel inventory and supply constraints. It links strong EV battery demand—especially high-nickel ternary cathodes—to growing offtake agreements and Indonesia smelting capacity progressing toward production, implying continued upward pressure on nickel. | Nickel producers in New Caledonia are benefiting from a sharp rise in nickel prices, with London nickel futures up 21% over the past month to above $24,000/t amid rising demand and low inventories. The outlook is further supported by battery supply deals and concerns over potential Russian supply disruption. | Britishvolt signed a multi-million pound agreement with UKBIC to develop high-nickel, energy-dense EV battery cells for eventual mass production. It also secured government backing for a Blyth plant. The article argues nickel demand should rise as EVs mainstream, supporting bullish expectations for nickel prices. | SMM reports tightening Chinese nickel spot supply as pure nickel imports still fail to cover the shortage. Nickel plate and briquette premiums keep rising, while bonded-zone arrivals may decrease into the week. An import window could open soon, but supplies in China and overseas remain tight, keeping USD premiums elevated. | Shanghai nickel futures hit new record highs as booming NEV sales lift demand for battery-grade nickel. The most active contract rose 2.3% to a record CNY180,780/ton, with prices up sharply year-to-date amid low inventory expectations. Analysts expect sales growth to keep supporting nickel despite subsidy cuts. | Tsingshan Group has shipped its first batch of nickel matte products from Indonesia back to China, following disclosure of its nickel matte project in 2021. Three production lines at Morowali Park are completed, with an earlier agreement for 75,000 mt annually. The first shipment is about 500 mt, sent with NPI. | Nickel surged to a 10-year high as Shanghai prices rose sharply. The piece attributes the move to shrinking inventories, tight nickel supply-demand balance, and limited deliverable stock, with ferro-nickel weakness and ongoing maintenance affecting supply. It suggests the squeeze may be nearing an end, but near-term pressure remains upward. | Qingshan’s Indonesian high-nickel matte shipments to China begin for the first time beyond samples. SMM analysis compares nickel sulfate production economics: using Indonesia high matte is more profitable than dissolving nickel beans or mainstream intermediate routes. The piece expects high-matte capacity ramp-up to support sustained nickel-b����n demand short term, aiding nickel sentiment. | SMM reports Shanghai nickel hitting new highs and continuing a strong rally, driven by low LME and domestic pure nickel inventories, ongoing declines in social and bonded stocks, and persistent spot shortages that push futures up. Institutions view supply-demand tightness as supportive, though they warn about potential squeeze exhaustion and volatility. | Britishvolt agreed with UKBIC to develop and assemble next-generation EV battery cells using higher nickel content and more energy-dense materials. The firm also confirmed £1.7bn funding for a Blyth gigafactory due to open in early 2024, reinforcing expectations for increased nickel demand tied to EV growth. | The piece argues nickel faces a supply squeeze: LME nickel stocks are falling, cash prices are in backwardation, and borrowing costs briefly spiked. It cites Goldman/J.P. Morgan estimates of sizable global nickel deficits in 2021–2022 and expects EV battery demand to keep tightening supply. | The Russian mining town of Nikel has seen nickel output decline for years, with limited reinvestment and deteriorating infrastructure. A large local smelting plant has been shut down over environmental concerns, raising uncertainty for residents and signaling reduced nearby nickel supply processing capacity.

Shanghai nickel 2202 and spot nickel prices fell sharply after reaching recent highs above 180,000 yuan/ton. The night market continued to decline. Market rises were linked to shrinking inventories and forced-position expectations, but official shipments back to China have restrained enthusiasm and triggered position squeezing ahead of the festival. | SMM reports base metals fell overnight as investors took profits amid rising risk aversion, with a stronger U.S. dollar weighing on non-ferrous prices. SHFE copper fell 1.74% and nickel plunged 6.88%, pressured by high matte nickel output sentiment. Aluminium was near-flat, with weak/seasonal demand and rising inventories. | SMM reports SHFE nickel continued rising strongly as LME and SHFE inventories fell to extremely low levels, with domestic social and bonded pure nickel inventories also declining. Tight spot supply at home and abroad, and rising premiums, boosted prices. Nickel is expected to stay bullish this week despite possible short-term easing. | SMM reports SHFE nickel plunging to limit down, erasing last Thursday’s gains after longs exited ahead of the Spring Festival. The drop is driven partly by expectations of improved supply following Indonesia’s news of successful mass production of nickel matte, weighing on nickel price sentiment. | Shanghai nickel fell sharply as traders took profits after a blistering rally to record highs; March nickel hit an 8% daily limit lower on profit-taking ahead of the U.S. Fed meeting and China holidays. The move was reinforced by Shanghai exchange fee/margin changes, with nickel also falling at the LME. | The article reviews global nickel market trends, noting a 4.2% production decline in 2020 and a rebound. It attributes recovery to stainless steel and EV battery demand, while citing a forecast for global nickel mine output exceeding 2.7Mt by 2025. No explicit short-term supply or price catalysts are given. | Nickel led a broad base-metals decline as investors turned cautious ahead of a key Fed meeting and amid rising Ukraine tensions. Nickel futures and spot fell after prior rallies, driven partly by exchange fee/margin adjustments and profit-taking. Copper and aluminium also declined in Shanghai, reflecting risk-off sentiment despite low nickel inventories. | Playfair plans additional 1,300 metres of shallow drilling in 26 holes to trace MMI copper anomalies at its RKV copper project in Norway. 2021 results highlighted strong copper anomalies and a sulphide-rich block with notable copper and nickel grades. Drilling is delayed to spring for logistics and terrain. | SMM says nickel faces its worst supply shortages since 2007, with LME inventories falling daily since October and spot prices running with a premium indicating tight availability. Demand support comes from stainless steel and accelerating EV battery demand, while supply additions are uncertain.

LME three-month aluminum jumped 2.1% as markets focused on potential supply disruptions from Russia/major producer Rusal amid Ukraine-related escalation and possible sanctions. The piece links the move to past sanctions impacts on Rusal and also reports broader base-metal weakness, while nickel remained highly volatile. | SMM reports the first batch of high matte shipped from Morowali Park in Indonesia to China, easing tight domestic supply and improving expectations for increased Qingshan production. It states Shanghai nickel fundamentals have weakened and shifted toward surplus, with weak downstream demand and low yet only marginal inventory changes, supporting bearish price pressure. | Qingshan’s Indonesia Morowali Park shipped the first batch of high matte back to China, adding supply capacity (~3,000 t/month). Market participants expect higher nickel supply, prompting short-covering/bullish funds to unwind. Shanghai nickel futures hit the intraday limit lower; electric nickel fell sharply, pressuring downstream sentiment around the holiday. | SMM’s daily review says metals rose overall, with Shanghai copper rebounding strongly after two losses (+1.30%) and Shanghai aluminium ending a losing streak (+1.87%). Shanghai nickel remained slightly down (-0.57%), though the decline narrowed. LME copper futures also rose in the afternoon. | SMM’s daily review says metals rose generally, with Shanghai copper rebounding strongly (+1.30%) after two losses and LME copper also firmer. Shanghai aluminium ended a losing streak (+1.87%) on supply and Europe-energy worries, while Shanghai nickel fell slightly (−0.57%) but the drop narrowed. | The piece expects near-term nickel price adjustment as risk aversion rises with hawkish Fed expectations and the stronger dollar. It notes Indonesian tariff considerations and reduced refinery/refined-nickel supply schedules ahead of Spring Festival. Rising nickel prices weaken downstream stainless steel buying, likely pressuring nickel in coming days.

SMM reports Shanghai and spot nickel prices rebounded from Jan 26 lows but remain weak and volatile, citing shifting market sentiment. It notes low global inventory and strong near-term demand support, with import supplies still weak, suggesting limited downside near 160,000 yuan and a wait-and-see stance. | SMM reports tight nickel sulphate spot supplies after producers cut output on operating losses. PCAM makers plan to keep running and maintain raw material demand post-holiday, while nickel intermediates/matte remain limited. Nickel briquette demand stays resilient, inventories fall, and SHFE/LME briquettes decline, supporting backwardation and bullish nickel futures. | The Biden administration cancelled two mineral leases for Antofagasta’s Twin Metals proposed copper-nickel mine in Minnesota, halting the project. The Interior Department said renewals were improper under regulations. Twin Metals plans to challenge the decision. The move complements a longer Minnesota mining ban proposal, affecting expected North American supply. | Grid Metals reports further 2021 drilling results from its Bannockburn nickel sulfide property, including wide nickel-enriched ultramafic intervals (e.g., 193.5m at 0.31% Ni) and higher-grade sections near 0.40–0.41% Ni. The B Zone target is widened and remains open for further drilling; assay results for remaining holes are pending. | SMM commentary links hawkish Fed rhetoric to a stronger dollar but says nickel is starting to rise as the Philippines enters the rainy season. It highlights Indonesia considering export tariffs on nickel goods (policy still uncertain). Reduced refinery schedules and weaker stainless procurement are noted, yet expectations point to short-term higher nickel prices. | Electric Royalties Ltd. announced the closing of its Rana nickel project royalty acquisition, securing a 1% net smelter revenue royalty tied to exploration licenses in Northern Norway, including the former producing Bruvann Nickel mine. The transaction is funded with shares and cash and remains subject to customary closing conditions. | Wolfden filed an NI-43-101 technical report for its Rice Island nickel project and reiterated a mineral resource estimate using a 0.5% NiEq cut-off. The release includes upcoming expansion drilling to test extensions of nickel mineralization and references copper grades within the NiEq calculation. | The Philippines ended its 2017 open-pit mining ban, aiming to revive a mining sector hurt by the pandemic. GlobalData says the ban delayed projects, including the King-King mine. Now-allowed open-pit projects are forecast to add copper and nickel production, potentially improving near-to-medium-term supply expectations.

SMM reports Shanghai nickel futures and spot prices fluctuated, with Shanghai contract 2202 closing at 163,880 yuan/ton. Spot in Huatong rose slightly, Guangdong was flat. The piece attributes near-term volatility to fast prior gains and sustained funding/sentiment pressure, while fundamentals remain supported by weak imports and low global inventories. | Chinese ports’ nickel ore inventories fell 175,000 wmt to 8.28 million wmt as of Jan 28, with total Ni content at 65,000 mt. Inventories at seven major ports also declined. Slower arrivals ahead of CNY holidays and Philippines wet-season conditions likely sustain a near-term downward inventory trend. | Nickel imports into China remain loss-making as shipments are insufficient versus domestic demand and spot supply of nickel plates and briquettes stays tight. Import premiums continue rising, likely supported by limited bonded-zone arrivals after CNY holidays and slower customs clearance. The SHFE/LME nickel price ratio is expected to improve. | A Reuters poll expects copper to soften in 2022 as rising interest rates weaken demand and more mine output—mainly from South America—adds supply. Aluminium remains supported by past power curtailments, but tighter conditions are seen as already priced in. Nickel is forecast lower as inventories rise and Indonesia supply increases. | The Biden administration canceled two Twin Metals Minnesota leases near the Boundary Waters, where the project sought to mine sulfide ore for copper and nickel. The rollback follows earlier political blocks and years of contention over environmental harm. Twin Metals vowed to challenge the decision, but cancellation reduces near-term mine prospects.

Wolfden Commences an expansion drilling program at its Rice Island Nickel Project. The move is intended to extend or grow the project’s resource base, which can improve expectations for future nickel supply. Although it does not change current production immediately, it is sentiment-supportive for nickel in the near term. | Callinex reported additional drilling results expanding the Rainbow VMS copper-gold-zinc deposit at its Pine Bay Project, including deeper intersections and a newly defined Red Zone with high copper-equivalent grades. Assays support potential continuation at depth, but there is no near-term production or supply change, limiting immediate price impact.

The piece analyzes a surge then sharp reversal in Chinese nickel prices around the Spring Festival. It cites extremely low global and domestic nickel inventories, strong stainless-steel and new-energy demand, and supply/cost shocks (e.g., Indonesia export tariffs, incidents in Myanmar). Analysts debate whether new Indonesian supply (high matte) will loosen balances. | Norilsk Nickel’s Global Palladium Fund launched a physically-backed EV metals ETC. The tracked index weights include Copper (40%) and Nickel (18.65%) and stores metal in LME/LPPM warehouses. The news is primarily an investment product launch, without clear immediate supply-demand impact, and Aluminium is not referenced. | Nickel 28 reported Q4 2021 and full-year 2021 operating results from its Ramu nickel-cobalt joint venture in Papua New Guinea, including contained nickel production and nickel sales, plus cost guidance. It also cites LME nickel average pricing but does not announce new near-term supply disruptions or changes. | Inomin Mines will start a 118 km ground magnetic survey at its Lynx sulphide nickel property to delineate drill targets, with similar geologic success referenced from its Beaver nickel-cobalt project. The release also notes strong current nickel sentiment tied to EV-battery demand, implying supportive near-term nickel outlook. | This Proactive roundup contains several nickel and copper headlines tied to company developments: FPX Nickel begins a scoping study for low-cost, low-carbon nickel from its Baptiste project, while Talon Metals reports nickel-copper mineralization drill results and Lion Copper and Gold discusses a Nevada property option spin-out. Impacts are supportive but not immediate price-altering.

LME copper rose about 2% as euro zone, Japan, and US manufacturing expanded and the dollar weakened, improving affordability. Nickel climbed to multi-year highs because LME and Shanghai inventories fell close to record lows, tightening supply; the spot premium over three-month rose above $500/t for the first time since 2009. | LME three-month copper rose about 2% after improving manufacturing surveys in the euro zone, Japan and the US and a weaker dollar. Nickel also jumped, with exchange warehouse stocks falling markedly and spot premiums rising above $500/t, indicating tight near-term supply conditions. | Nickel prices continued their strong January rise, topping $US10.50 a pound. The report attributes gains to falling LME-registered inventories and higher premiums for prompt delivery, with cash nickel premiums above $500 a tonne for the first time since 2009. Gas prices are also rising but don’t drive nickel. | Glencore reported FY21 production with own-sourced copper down 5% to 1.2 million tonnes, attributing declines to Mopani disposal, weaker grades at Antapaccay and lower copper by-products from mature zinc/nickel mines. Cobalt rose on limited Mutanda restart. FY22 guidance was unchanged, with copper around 1.15 mt. | Copper prices rose 1.5% on a softer U.S. dollar and buying driven by concerns over low LME inventories, though gains were tempered by slowing China activity around the new year holiday. China’s smelter shutdowns for pollution controls supported supply tightness, aiding the broader base-metals tone. Aluminium shortages also remain stressed.

The piece weighs a potential Western Australia supply shock (prompted by an omicron spread) and its possible impact on WA-linked commodities, including nickel and alumina. It also notes Oxford Economics expects improved metal supply and new copper projects coming online, while nickel production rises in Indonesia/Philippines, offsetting disruption risk. | BHP, Tesla’s nickel supplier, has begun construction of the Northern Goldfields Solar Project to supply off-grid renewable power to its Nickel West operations (Mt. Keith and Leinster). Solar and battery storage will replace diesel and gas, cutting scope 2 emissions and supporting BHP’s low-carbon nickel strategy amid strong EV nickel demand. | Tesla agreed with Talon Nickel to buy 75,000 metric tons of nickel concentrate over six years for battery supply, with options to negotiate more. The deal targets a Tamarack Nickel Project in Minnesota amid US nickel supply constraints. Production is planned for 2026, but the contract supports nickel demand expectations.

EV sales growth is driving higher battery demand. The article notes nickel shortages and large draws in LME-approved warehouse stocks and briquette used to produce nickel sulphate for batteries. Analysts expect higher average nickel prices, with nickel recently at multi-year highs, though supplies may ease later in the year.

SMM’s daily review notes a general metal rise: Shanghai nickel jumped 3.25% with a “black series” strength and tight post-festival spot supply, while aluminium (Shanghai) rose about 3.03% on rapid opening gains. Shanghai copper closed nearly flat (-0.04%). Nickel’s supply/destocking outlook remains key. | Aluminium futures in Shanghai and the LME jumped after the Spring Festival, supported by supply concerns and expectations of higher demand. The move was reinforced by very low LME aluminium inventories and commentary on European production cuts, high electricity costs, and Russia-related sanctions risk. Nickel also firmed on tight inventories.

SMM reports a mixed metal tape on March 8: Shanghai copper is slightly lower while Shanghai aluminium turns up marginally after improving demand and expectations of tight spot ingot supply post-holiday. Nickel rises as pure-nickel supply remains tight and LME/d omestic inventories decline. Overall direction is mixed across tracked metals.

Overnight, macro signals were mixed (stronger dollar, CPI awaited). Shanghai aluminum rose 1.9% (and LME aluminum also up), while Shanghai nickel dropped 2.53% (LME/Shanghai nickel down sharply). Overall industrial metals were mixed, implying offsetting short-term pressure across aluminium and nickel. | Gungnir Resources says it has identified a “twin conductor” at its Lappvattnet nickel project. Such exploration results can raise expectations about the prospectivity of the deposit and support sentiment toward future nickel supply, typically providing mild near-term support to nickel prices rather than a direct supply change.

Sherritt reported strong Q4 and full-year 2021 results, including finished nickel output at the Moa JV and improved unit economics. The company attributes performance to better nickel market fundamentals and expects bullish near-term conditions linked to EV demand, positioning it as a “pure play” for rising nickel prices. | SMM reports a midday rally in China’s base metals as the US dollar weakened. Shanghai copper rose over 3% to a three-month intraday high, while Shanghai aluminium gained over 2% for the eighth day on tight domestic supply, low inventories and improving downstream spot demand. Shanghai nickel also rose. | SMM’s midday review notes a weaker US dollar and rising industrial metals. Shanghai copper climbed over 3% to a new intraday high for more than three months. Shanghai aluminum rose over 2% for eight straight sessions, supported by tight inventories and expected supply shortages versus imports in the near term. | Huayou Cobalt said on Feb 10 that Tsingshan has begun executing a 60,000 mt high-grade nickel matte supply agreement, with deliveries starting from October 2021. It also reported steady progress on Huayou’s Huake (Indonesia) nickel matte pyrometallurgy project, suggesting improving nickel supply conditions. | Los Andes Copper reported initial drill results at its Vizcachitas Chile project: 732m at 0.51% copper equivalent (with copper and molybdenum grades) and continued drilling to expand resources. The update can bolster bullish sentiment for copper given improved resource expectations, though nickel is not meaningfully addressed. | ThreeD Capital says its investee Premium Nickel Resources has completed the acquisition of the Selebi and Selebi North Ni-Cu-Co mines and expects exploration to begin immediately. Metallurgical testing supports producing separate copper and nickel-cobalt concentrates, with drilling and studies planned, suggesting improved supply outlook for nickel and copper. | North American Nickel’s affiliate Premium Nickel Resources has completed the purchase of the Selebi and Selebi North Ni-Cu-Co mines in Botswana from the liquidator, and exploration is expected to begin immediately. Metallurgical testing supports redevelopment to produce separate nickel and copper-cobalt concentrates, which is mildly bullish for nickel and copper. | Mongoose Mining agreed to acquire the Steele Run IOCG copper-cobalt-gold deposit in Nova Scotia, citing historic drill core with reported high copper grades and cobalt assays. The company plans further exploration, but the release provides no new production, financing, or near-term supply change.

SMM reports weak spot trading but firm nickel fundamentals: Indonesian ore demand is expected to rise 20–30% in 2022 to 100m tonnes, with high-matte supply deliveries and project progress in Indonesia. It also cites all-time-high LME 3-month transfer fees, low global inventories, and tight supplies, suggesting nickel prices remain supported and fluctuate. | SMM reports firm refined nickel pricing with high spot premiums due to limited supply and less active buying, while nickel briquette supply remains tight. It cites Indonesia’s 2022 nickel ore demand forecast rising 20-30% and ongoing high-grade nickel matte shipments and deliveries. Falling inventories and improving macro sentiment support high nickel prices near term. | IndexBox reports that nickel prices rose earlier in 2022 on strong stainless steel and battery demand, with prices up sharply year-on-year. It forecasts the 2022 average nickel price will fall about 4% to ~$17,750/tonne due to boosting global production, implying easing supply pressure. | Mongoose Mining agreed to buy Canada’s Steele Run IOCG copper-cobalt-gold deposit in Nova Scotia. The transaction involves issuing 200,000 shares and adds a near drill-ready exploration asset with reported high-grade copper and recognized cobalt mineralisation. The announcement is deal-related and does not imply immediate production impacts.

Inomin Mines reports a ground magnetic survey underway over its Lynx nickel property, targeting sulphide nickel zones (Bear and Skulow) and building on earlier airborne/ground magnetics work at the Beaver block. The company highlights nickel-cobalt potential but provides no drilling or production updates. | Premium Nickel Resources signed an asset purchase agreement to acquire the Selkirk nickel-copper-cobalt mine in Botswana, targeting closing within 120 days. The company plans drilling and metallurgical work to update the feasibility study and restart processing to produce separate copper and nickel-cobalt concentrates, but near-term output is not indicated. | North American Nickel announced its affiliate PNR executed an asset purchase agreement to acquire the Selkirk nickel-copper-cobalt mine in Botswana from Tati Nickel Mining’s liquidation. NAN holds a 10% interest and will support closing within 120 days, with planned drilling and updated feasibility and metallurgical work to restart concentrate production.

ThreeD Capital announced an asset purchase agreement to acquire the Selkirk nickel mine in Botswana, reporting “premium nickel resources” and signing related terms for the transaction. The news is directly tied to expected nickel production and funding momentum, which can support nickel price expectations over the next weeks. | Premium Nickel Resources agreed to acquire the Selkirk underground Ni-Cu-Co mine and nearby licences in Botswana from liquidators, with a 120-day closing and planned drilling to update feasibility work plus upgrades for tailings and a processing plant. The move supports sentiment for nickel and some copper supply prospects. | Premium Nickel Resources agreed to acquire the Selkirk Ni-Cu-Co-PGM mine and related licenses/infrastructure from the liquidator of TNMC in Botswana. The company plans further drilling, metallurgical testing, and updating feasibility/environmental work to restart development, but no immediate production changes are indicated. | Copper prices ticked up as Russia returned some troops to base after exercises near Ukraine, easing fears of invasion and improving risk sentiment. Three-month LME copper rose 0.5%. Aluminium was softer amid lingering sanctions-supply worries, while nickel was slightly higher, reflecting broader market moves. | Talon Metals agreed with Triple Flag to accelerate an option to reduce the Tamarack Nickel Project net smelter returns royalty from 3.5% to 1.85% for a US$4.5 million payment. The company frames the change as value accretive, preserving economics for future nickel supply and development.

Ningbo Liqin Resources, described as China’s largest nickel trader, filed for an HK listing and highlights its global nickel supply channels and Indonesia smelting projects (HPAL nickel-cobalt and expansion via RKEF lines). It plans further investment in nickel production and battery-related ventures, reinforcing expectations of expanding nickel supply and trading activity. | Base metals, led by aluminium, rose after tensions eased between Russia and the West and as focus shifted to energy crunches causing smelter shutdowns in China and Europe. LME three-month aluminium gained 1.1% and copper and nickel also increased, reflecting improved sentiment but still-tight power and gas markets. | Aurubis AG says it is building a facility in Belgium to recycle more nickel and copper. The announcement points to higher future secondary-metal output from recycling, which can improve supply expectations and weigh on near-term price momentum for copper and nickel. | Aurubis AG plans to build a facility in Belgium to recycle more nickel and copper. The project increases expected availability of recycled (secondary) metal supply from Aurubis, which can weigh on nickel and copper prices over the next few weeks as market expectations shift toward greater supply. | Aurubis will invest €70 million in Belgium (Olen) to build a state-of-the-art bleed treatment facility to recover nickel and copper from electrolyte streams produced at its tankhouses. The project targets higher recycling capacity and improved impurity removal, increasing expected recovered metal output from recycling materials. | Aurubis plans a €70m, state-of-the-art bleed-treatment facility in Olen, Belgium, using hydrometallurgical processing to recover nickel and copper from electrolyte streams generated in its Belgian tankhouses. The company expects commissioning benefits in fiscal 2025/26 and highlights increased recycling capacity, but the near-term price impact appears limited.

LME base metals rose Wednesday, with aluminum leading after easing Russia–West/Ukraine tensions and renewed focus on energy supply stress that reportedly shut some smelters in China and Europe. Shanghai pricing also skewed higher for aluminum and copper, while nickel and zinc were slightly lower. Fed minutes and a weaker dollar supported metals. | Andrew Forrest backed IGO’s bid to acquire nickel miner Western Areas, pledging his company Wyloo’s 9.8% stake to support the merger. The support also reinforces IGO’s plan to build a nickel processing plant in Australia, potentially improving expectations for nickel processing and supply continuity, supportive for nickel sentiment. | SMM expects ongoing destocking with LME nickel inventories declining since January. Nickel sulphate prices rebound as supply tightens after loss-making producers cut output, and domestic raw-material shortages may worsen as plants resume. Meanwhile, stainless steel mills restarting should lift NPI demand, while limited high-grade matte supply caps nickel feedstock growth. | SMM says China’s social inventory of refined nickel and bonded-area nickel both fell sharply in January to multi-year lows. Downstream buyers stocked ahead of the CNY holiday, while imports into bonded areas declined after the window opened. Nickel prices rose alongside strong inventory drawdowns, supporting bullish sentiment. | Copper prices fell on the London Metal Exchange as heightened Russia–Ukraine tensions lifted risk-off sentiment, strengthened the dollar, and raised costs for non-US buyers. The report cites easing China import premiums and rising copper stockpiles. Russia is a major producer of aluminium and nickel, though quoted moves are mixed. | Nickel is reported to be climbing after the U.S. renewed warnings about a potential Russian invasion. The renewed geopolitical risk is likely prompting market participants to price in disruptions or supply tightness related to Russia, increasing nickel’s risk premium and supporting higher prices in the near term. | SMM reports China Nonferrous Metals Industry No. 6 Metallurgical Construction won bids for two sections of Indonesia OBI Nickel-Cobalt Wet Phase III. The move targets a secure nickel supply amid surging raw materials prices and Indonesia’s expanding role in laterite processing. | Britishvolt and VKTR announced a joint venture to develop sustainable nickel refining capacity in Indonesia and supply nickel sulphate for high-performance EV batteries, potentially using renewable energy. With Indonesia a major nickel producer, the initiative and broader EV battery projects support expectations for nickel demand. | London copper edged higher for a second session, supported by bargain-buying, but China’s planned Shanghai lockdown and rising COVID cases weighed on demand expectations. In contrast, Shanghai nickel dropped more than 5%. Chile’s Codelco plans a “non-core” asset offering, a minor producer-development signal. | London copper edged higher for a second session, supported by bargain-buying, though COVID-19 spread in China’s key consumer regions and a two-stage Shanghai lockdown raised demand concerns. In contrast, Shanghai nickel dropped over 5%, reflecting stronger negative sentiment and likely weaker near-term outlook for industrial metals demand. | SMM says SHFE nickel has been correcting downward as longs cut exposure and futures pulled back. Pandemic-related delays and weak spot trading limit near-term demand, while nickel briquette and pure nickel transactions stay poor. NPI remains firm only due to high ore/smelting costs, but overall pure nickel demand is weak. | Nimy Resources reports nickel-bearing gossan uncovered during drill-site preparations at its Mons nickel project in Western Australia. Portable XRF readings show up to 0.96% nickel, with additional anomalous copper and cobalt and proximity to older nickel intersections. The softer tone on growth weighed on metals sentiment. Shanghai nickel also fell nearly 5%, reflecting the broader downturn in China-linked consumption expectations. | SMM reports a mixed midday session: Shanghai nickel fell sharply to expand its decline to 6.48%, while copper rose modestly (~0.64%) and aluminium ended slightly lower (~-0.15%) after an intraday rally.
