Shanghai nickel dropped about 4.7% on the same growth/demand outlook fears. Commentary pointed to potential impacts on Chinese industrial production. | NickelX received shareholder approval to acquire the Cosmos South nickel project in Western Australia’s Wiluna belt. The company will exercise the option, proceed with formal agreements, and earn a 100% interest by funding at least $500,000 of exploration over 24 months. Preliminary work includes airborne magnetic surveys. | Vietnam is expected to see rising demand for battery metals, with nickel benefiting from growing electric-vehicle uptake. The piece notes nickel’s current use in battery applications is set to increase and highlights Vietnam’s nickel resources and plans for additional mining and processing, including factories targeting nickel outputs. | Sumitomo Metal is reported to see global nickel demand for battery applications at 410,000 in 2022. The headline suggests improving demand expectations for battery-related nickel consumption, which can be supportive for nickel prices in the near term, though the excerpt lacks details on timing or supply response. | Inomin Mines reports significant drill results at its Beaver nickel-cobalt property: a 252m interval grading 20.6% magnesium with 0.16% nickel, plus near-surface silver and copper from another hole (0.29% copper over 3m). Discoveries remain open, supporting improved short-term sentiment for nickel and, to a lesser extent, copper supply potential. | Chinese metals recycler GEM sold a 9% stake in its Indonesian battery-grade nickel processing JV to South Korea’s EcoPro BM and agreed funding to support construction. EcoPro will supply loans to the project, which targets 150,000 tons/year nickel sulfate and supplies high-nickel ternary precursors, supporting expected nickel demand. | SMM says SHFE nickel (2204) fell on March 29, with weak trading, lower volume and open interest, and reduced long positions. It attributes pressure to pandemic-related transport and subdued spot demand, weak downstream demand (briquette/dissolution, April sulphate output), and less active stainless steel operations, while watching LME nickel and macro/geopolitics. | The piece argues Russia’s war on Ukraine is adding turmoil to nickel markets and notes Chinese nickel producer Tsingshan Holding Group faces multi-billion-dollar losses. It raises uncertainty around Tsingshan’s nickel mining interests in South Africa, which could affect short-term nickel supply expectations and price direction.

The piece examines how nickel turmoil, which included a surge after the Russia-Ukraine conflict, exposed perceived weaknesses in the London Metal Exchange and highlighted risk-control needs for Tsingshan and its banks. It also spotlights Tsingshan’s controlling owner Xiang Guangda amid scrutiny of the episode’s drivers. | SMM market tracking reports broad weakness in China’s non-ferrous complex early March 30: Shanghai nickel fell 2.75% and Shanghai aluminum dropped 1.5% (inner market), while Shanghai copper declined slightly. Overseas trade improves marginally for some contracts, but epidemic/logistics frictions and shifting macro sentiment keep pressure on metals near term. | The LME said it will run an independent review into the nickel trading chaos earlier this month after prices surged more than 50% to about $100,000/tonne and the exchange suspended nickel trading. Trading resumed with new daily price limits and OTC position reporting requirements. | SHFE nickel fell overnight as market participants withdrew capital and adjusted for weaker near-term demand. Downstream buyers stayed cautious amid sharp nickel price swings, import losses tightened briquette supply, and Wuxi stainless procurement was disrupted by pandemic closures. Nickel sulphate output signals also weakened. Nickel volatility and muted transactions persisted. | Sumitomo Metal Mining said global nickel demand for rechargeable battery use will rise over 20% in 2022 to above 410,000 mt, driven by firm EV growth. It also expects the nickel supply shortfall to narrow, as Indonesia’s nickel pig iron output increases. Overall the demand outlook is supportive for nickel. | The article challenges Indonesia’s claim that its raw nickel export ban boosts downstreaming. It argues the policy has reduced mining revenues via forced domestic selling at lower prices, distorts smelter economics through tax holidays, and risks trade retaliation after an EU WTO case. It concludes the evidence is weak. | LME aluminium rebounded after a near 5% prior-session drop as optimism around Russia-Ukraine talks faded, reducing a supply-related risk premium. A softer U.S. dollar supported metals, but China demand worries from a COVID surge capped gains. Russia sanctions remain a key supply overhang for aluminium, nickel and copper. | SMM reports COVID-impacted logistics and ongoing closures affecting ferronickel plants in northern China. Several plants have reduced output or suspended lines, expected to materially cut nickel-iron production in March. Despite high consolidated ferro-nickel supported by costs and tight supply, downstream stainless demand is poor, limiting acceptance of high prices. | Power Nickel reported final drill-hole assays from its Nisk Main nickel sulphate project showing continued high-grade nickel mineralization (with some copper in the intercepts). The company expects to deliver a NI 43-101 compliant resource in Q2 and has budgeted a fully funded 5,000-metre drill program starting later in Q2. | After LME resumed trading in nickel futures, OTC nickel derivative holders still cannot settle expired contracts using the new reference prices. The International Swaps and Derivatives Association is working on a bilateral ISDA template so counterparties can use updated prices for settlement, reducing counterparty settlement problems. | Tesla reportedly signed a secret nickel supply deal with Vale. Such an arrangement can affect nickel pricing by shaping expectations for near-term demand and supply security for battery-related metals. The article provides no deal volumes or terms, so the impact is likely moderate and uncertain. | The LME CEO said the month’s unprecedented nickel price surge threatened market stability and prompted the bourse to take action it had never done before. The report suggests the exchange intervened due to extreme volatility in nickel markets, but does not specify whether the action is intended to curb prices. | Aluminium prices jumped as markets renewed fears of Russian supply disruptions and the dollar weakened. The story cites Russia’s role in aluminium, copper, and nickel supply, potential sanctions and transport issues, and high European gas prices that have forced smelters to cut output. LME aluminium, copper, and nickel rose.

Bloomberg reports Tesla signed a secret multi-year supply deal with Vale for nickel, sourced from Voisey’s Bay in Labrador. The agreement is described as a way to avoid Russian nickel supply. Musk had previously urged demand for more environmentally sensitive nickel for EV batteries, reinforcing bullish sentiment. | LME aluminium jumped over 3% as traders re-priced renewed supply risks and a weaker US dollar. The piece links higher prices to sanctions and disrupted routes from major Russian producers of aluminium, copper and nickel, alongside higher energy costs and delayed investment, while noting possible demand uncertainty from China. | Tesla has reportedly secured a multiyear nickel supply agreement with Vale SA in Canada, aiming to ease battery-material shortages intensified by Russia-related sanctions. With Russia a major refined nickel supplier, shifting supply away from Russia could tighten expectations for nickel availability and limit upside pressure. | Panther Metals says it has found a new nickel laterite discovery near Glencore’s Murrin Murrin, with strong cobalt grades and indications of nickel sulphides. The announcement is relevant to nickel supply expectations, but as a discovery without near-term output details, immediate price pressure is likely limited. | SMM reports SHFE nickel futures moved rangebound and turned lower after LME nickel. Despite relatively tight Jinchuan/other nickel supply, pandemic-linked transport issues and weaker demand signals (muted stainless transactions, weak nickel briquette economics, possible April stock-related cuts) keep near-term downside pressure on nickel. | LME aluminium rose over 3% on renewed supply concerns and a weaker US dollar, making prices cheaper for non-US buyers. The piece links the broader move to Russia-Ukraine sanctions disrupting supply routes, and cites Russia’s key role as an aluminium, copper and nickel producer, alongside tight market signals. | FLSmidth was selected to supply rotary kiln, rotary dryer, electrical systems, and installation services for Araguaia Niquel Metais’ ferronickel mine in Brazil. The order supports processing of nickel laterite ore and is intended to improve efficiency and reduce fuel use, but the timing suggests limited immediate price pressure. | Nickel sulphate restocking has begun, but nickel prices remain volatile. Some sellers quote 45,000-49,000 yuan/mt and others above 50,000 due to higher costs, while buyers resist above 46,000. Negative feedback from NMC battery makers suggests output cuts, reducing purchases and adding downward pressure on nickel salt in April-May. | China Natural Resources said it will accelerate nickel exploration and increase 2022 capex at its Moruogu Tong Mine in Inner Mongolia, adding six additional drilling holes after limited prior work. The firm cites nickel scarcity and demand outstripping supply as rationale, aiming to improve resource capture in the near term. | SMM reports LME nickel inventory distribution across major warehouses on March 31, noting that the Singapore warehouse saw the largest increase, with total nickel inventories rising by about 30 tons. The build-up in visible stocks typically reduces near-term supply tightness, weighing on nickel prices short term. | Tesla reportedly signed multiyear nickel supply contracts with miners since 2021, including Vale, to secure nickel for EV batteries despite the Ukraine-related nickel supply shock that sent prices sharply higher in mid-March. The report suggests Tesla avoided the crisis through procurement, while Russia remains a key supplier. | Talon Metals says President Biden added nickel (and other battery materials) to Defense Production Act Title III support, enabling funding, infrastructure support, and potential offtake tools. The company expects this to bolster domestic production from its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project, supporting near-term sentiment for nickel (and some copper) supply. | Canadian North Resources reports TSX Venture listing and about $22m raised for exploration and development of the Ferguson Lake project, described as a Class-1 massive sulfide deposit containing nickel and copper. The proceeds are earmarked for resource definition and development, supporting bullish sentiment toward Ni and Cu in the near term. | Canadian North Resources announced receipt of a final prospectus, conditional TSX Venture Exchange listing, and net proceeds of ~$2.19M released plus ~$20M raised for exploration/development of its Ferguson Lake project. The project is described as a magmatic massive sulfide zone targeting nickel and copper, but no production impact is stated.

After a historic nickel short squeeze drove LME nickel to extreme levels, the exchange suspended trading and later scrapped troubled trades. Trading has reopened with limits and glitches, but volumes and spreads improved. Prices have moved back toward SHFE benchmarks, with LME nickel about 6% lower and stabilizing. | LME aluminium closed lower on the day but posted the biggest quarterly gain since 1988, driven by supply disruptions and higher smelting costs tied to Russia-Ukraine conflict and sanctions. Nickel also surged strongly after recent volatility. The piece adds supportive copper signals, including an export license and weaker Chile output. | SMM reports a contract for mining and transporting nickel laterite in Indonesia and discusses near-term nickel fundamentals. Nickel closed slightly lower and price amplitude narrowed as stainless-steel demand and downstream battery demand weaken amid logistics/logistics disruptions from the epidemic. Tight resources support nickel, implying volatile but not strongly directional moves. | The article argues that EV and high-nickel battery growth will push nickel demand into an “explosive” phase. It cites low global nickel inventories, rising market volatility, and carmakers (Tesla, BYD) signing or reporting nickel supply contracts. It also highlights constraints in producing nickel sulfate, reinforcing bullish expectations for nickel. | Copper dropped as China’s factory activity data signaled sharper deterioration, increasing near-term demand concerns. A firmer U.S. dollar further pressured dollar-priced metals. Investors stayed cautious ahead of U.S. jobs data. Other base metals weakened too, with aluminium slightly lower and nickel down on Shanghai trading. | The Nickel Institute president urged the LME to restore nickel trading to rebuild market stability. Since nickel trading was suspended, the institute says the disruption is damaging reputation, confidence, and could erode demand for nickel. It calls for clarification from the LME as trading resumes. | SMM says China’s downstream nickel industries PMI fell to 49.25 in March, signaling contraction. While stainless steel production recovered after February restrictions, demand weakened: high nickel prices suppressed purchasing volume (composite 50.24; sub-sectors below 50). Electroplating and NI-MH battery firms postponed orders and purchases stagnated. | Copper prices fell 1.1% as investors worried China’s weak factory activity and Shanghai COVID lockdowns would curb demand. LME three-month copper retreated to about $10,264/t. The article cites supply/energy and other metal moves, but copper remains the clearest downside read. | SMM says nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 230,000 wmt to 5.887 million wmt on April 1, leaving total Ni content at 1,800 mt. Port stockpiles remain at relatively low historical levels. As Philippine rains end but shipments cannot ramp quickly, imported ore build is slow, and nickel ore destocking is expected to slow. | LME nickel trading volumes dropped 28% in March, with the decline linked to a shutdown. The sharp volume contraction suggests reduced market participation and potentially disrupted supply and/or processing, which is generally bearish for nickel pricing in the near term as expectations weaken. | Tesla reportedly signed a multi-year, undisclosed nickel supply deal with Vale for nickel sourced from Canada’s Sudbury operations. With Tesla highlighting nickel as a key constraint and EV battery demand rising, the agreement is likely to bolster market expectations for nickel supply and demand, supporting nickel prices amid recent LME volatility.

The Bank of England and the FCA have launched reviews into the LME’s suspension and resumption of nickel trading after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine triggered sharp nickel volatility. The regulators will assess LME governance, clearing-house risk management, and transparency, with potential actions considered. | LME has announced nickel interim trading measures and an independent review, according to the article title. These actions can influence nickel market expectations by changing trading rules, delivery/settlement behavior, or short-term liquidity. The provided excerpt lacks details, so the likely immediate price effect is uncertain and treated as neutral. | UK regulators will probe the LME after it suspended nickel trading on 8 March due to disorderly market conditions. A fund’s large short squeeze drove nickel prices to more than double, prompting the exchange to halt trading and adjust timestamps, wiping certain trades and raising transparency concerns. | The headline says the U.K. will review LME operations after nickel market turmoil. Such regulatory or market-structure review headlines can influence near-term nickel price expectations through perceived trading/liquidity risk, but the excerpt provides no details on concrete policy steps or immediate nickel fundamentals. | UK regulators (FCA and Bank of England) will investigate LME handling of a week-long nickel trading suspension after prices doubled to a record $100,000/tonne amid chaos around Russia’s invasion. The LME canceled trades, citing orderly markets, and plans limits on daily moves and governance changes. | Kabanga Nickel CEO Chris Showalter says Tanzania’s class one nickel could supply global automakers seeking alternatives to Russia for EV lithium-ion batteries. He expects the auto sector to buy more than half of nickel production at Kabanga, a project scheduled for production in 2025. | The Bank of England and a UK city watchdog plan to investigate a suspension of nickel trading. Such probes can shift near-term expectations for nickel liquidity, market integrity, and trading operations. However, the provided text lacks details on the cause or likely resolution, limiting confidence on direction. | Great Atlantic Resources began 2022 prospecting and rock geochemical sampling at its Mascarene Property, targeting the Wheal Louisiana copper–nickel–cobalt occurrence. Results will be assayed, but no production or supply changes are indicated. | The piece flags that the LME faces a U.K. regulatory review amid “nickel market turmoil.” Such oversight can affect market structure, hedging, and investor confidence in nickel contracts. However, the excerpt provides no concrete policy changes or operational disruptions to quantify a clear bullish or bearish price effect. | UK regulators (Bank of England and FCA) are investigating the LME over nickel trading chaos that halted a market for a week after nickel surged ~250% in two days to over $100,000/t, then retraced sharply. London copper rose for a second session as traders worried about supply from Chile after February output fell 7.5%. Gains were tempered by a firmer U.S. dollar and lingering concerns over Chinese demand, with China holiday/lockdown measures affecting sentiment. LME also plans an independent review into last month’s nickel chaos.

Canada Nickel says infill drilling at its Crawford nickel sulphide project in Timmins yielded the highest-grade interval to date, with results including core lengths of 0.71% nickel and sections above 1.0% nickel. The firm positions the project for a feasibility study to advance future nickel supply. | Western Mines Group will drill nine bedrock conductor targets at its Mulga Tank nickel-copper-PGE project in Western Australia following an MLEM geophysical survey. The program targets potential nickel sulphide intersections (and associated copper/PGEs) to verify models, with mobilization planned next week. | Western Mines Group will drill nine holes at the Mulga Tank nickel-copper-PGE project in Western Australia after an MLEM survey identified bedrock conductor anomalies. Early holes target an electromagnetic plate and follow geochemical vectoring. Work is planned to start next week, with results guiding further drilling. | Aston Minerals says drilling at its Edleston project extends nickel-cobalt sulphide mineralisation to 1 km of strike, with multiple intercepts including up to ~0.44% nickel. The company also raises funds to accelerate resource-definition drilling and metallurgical testing, but no production impact is stated. | SMM says LME nickel has limited liquidity and prices remain elevated ($31,700–$33,600/mt). Russia-Ukraine tensions and China’s worsening COVID controls have prolonged pure nickel spot transport and warehouse delivery, tightening supply. It provides spot nickel ranges in yuan and notes low global inventories and ongoing cost/demand swings, suggesting continued high volatility but an unclear net price direction. | JPMorgan is reviewing its commodity client relationships after a short squeeze in LME nickel futures last month. Management has ordered renewed due diligence and risk assessments, including on metals traders/refiners and financing units. The bank is already reducing nickel exposure and may scale back commodities business, tightening financing conditions. | BHP signed a 12-year contract with Enel Green Power for wind electricity for two Nickel West operations in Western Australia. The wind farm will supply power for the Kalgoorlie smelter and concentrator from 2024. Combined with other solar deals, BHP expects nearly 60% lower Scope 2 emissions, supporting “green” nickel branding. | BHP will use electricity from Enel Green Power’s Flat Rocks Wind Farm to cover all power needs for two Nickel West operations in Western Australia. Enel will build the A$200 million wind project, with output matching power requirements from 2024. Combined renewables deals cut Nickel West Scope 2 emissions by ~60% versus 2020. | Blackstone Minerals delivered nickel-cobalt sulphide feed to ALS for Ta Khoa refinery pilot trials after restarting the Ban Phuc nickel concentrator in Vietnam. The company processed ore to produce sulphide flotation product, plans a 400kt refinery capacity, and is developing high-purity NCM811 nickel precursor products for batteries. | EU is considering sanctions on Oleg Deripaska, a major shareholder in Aughinish Alumina via Rusal, alongside broader measures targeting Russia’s financial sector and trade. Proposed bans on coal, limits on bank transactions, port access restrictions, and potential further action on fossil-fuel revenues increase supply-chain and risk concerns for industrial metals.

The piece expects nickel prices to rise, citing a potential future supply-demand gap in nickel resources around 2024. It links demand growth to high-nickel ternary lithium battery benefits (energy density and battery life), and notes beneficiaries across the ternary precursor and cathode chain, including Philippine nickel producers. | Canada Nickel raised $51.6 million via flow-through shares to advance technical studies for its Crawford nickel sulphide project and pay some debt. It also reported strong infill drilling results, including a high-grade nickel intercept. The company plans further step-out drilling, an updated resource estimate mid-year, and a feasibility study by year-end. | SMM cites CITIC Securities saying nickel prices may be influenced by non-fundamental factors in the short term, but the longer outlook is bullish. Fewer new nickel resources are expected over the next five years, and high-nickel NMC battery demand should rise, potentially leading to a supply deficit around 2024. | BHP Nickel West signed a power purchase agreement with Enel Green Power for the Flat Rocks Wind Farm, expected to supply 100% of power needs for several nickel operations in Western Australia from 2024. The deal supports decarbonization and reliability but does not imply near-term output changes for nickel. | Go Metals Corp. contracted an operator for a 2,400m summer diamond drill campaign at its Quebec nickel-copper sulphide project. Targets include near-surface nickel-copper sulphides (with reported grab-sample grades) and permitting for up to 20 holes. The release is exploratory and does not indicate immediate supply changes. | Go Metals Corp. announced a contracted 2,400m summer diamond drill program at its Quebec nickel-copper sulphide HSP project. Targets are surface-proven for nickel-copper sulphide mineralization with stated Ni (0.3–1.8%) and Cu (0.3–4.0%) grades, with permitting underway for up to 20 holes.

Azure Minerals’ managing director discusses a newly defined nickel/copper/cobalt resource in Western Australia. The piece frames the discovery as arriving while nickel and copper prices are “booming,” which could boost sentiment around future supply prospects and provide a modest near-term supportive effect for nickel and copper. | Shanghai aluminium prices fell to a three-week low as fresh China lockdowns and expectations of tighter monetary policy raised worries about weaker growth and metals demand. A stronger dollar pressured dollar-priced commodities, pulling LME and ShFE copper lower too, with nickel trading/infrastructure concerns adding sentiment drag. | SMM said nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 150,000 wmt week-on-week to 5.737 million wmt (45,100 mt Ni content). Inventories at seven major ports also declined. The decline is slowing, but as the Philippines rainy season ends, imports may rise, potentially moderating tightness. | North American Nickel Inc. upsizes a private placement to about $10m via subscription receipts that will be used for exploration, development, working capital and general purposes. IGO Group increased its bid by about 15% for nickel miner Western Areas after its takeover attempt was overtaken by soaring nickel prices. An independent expert previously said IGO’s last $3.36-per-share offer was not fair. The revised offer reflects the improved nickel-price environment. | St-Georges Eco-Mining disclosed additional core assay results from Quebec’s Manicouagan Project, including high-grade nickel (up to 9.49% Ni) and copper values (up to 2.2% Cu), along with significant palladium and other PGEs. The company is re-logging unsampled core to extend the Ni–Cu–PGE discovery corridor. | DW reports Germany relies significantly on Russian nickel, with Russia a major supplier of high-grade “class 1” nickel. With EV-driven nickel demand rising, fears of Russian export or EU/Moscow sanctions have previously sparked price spikes and suspended LME trading, keeping supply anxiety elevated. Copper is mentioned but less central. | Norden Crown reported results from 12 diamond drill holes at its Burfjord copper-gold project in Norway, including several copper intercepts (up to 1.27% Cu over 12m). The company says the follow-up drilling outlines broad copper zones and notes discrete cobalt and nickel mineralization, but no production changes. | Playfair Mining begins a review of its RKV Norway nickel-copper occurrences. The article notes nickel has surged to a 15-year high and argues EV demand growth plus an extended Russian (Norilsk) embargo likely tightens Class 1 nickel supply near term. It cites historical drilling results and plans further data review. | Playfair Mining begins reviewing its RKV nickel-copper assets in Norway, focusing on historical Vakkerlien, Kaltberget and Olkar showings. The release reiterates that nickel prices have surged and argues Class 1 nickel tightness may worsen if Russian supply is embargoed. No new drilling or production changes are announced. | Oil prices fell about 4% on concerns about weakening Chinese demand and expectations of additional IEA strategic reserve releases. The stronger dollar and macro data focus contributed to risk sentiment. Industrial metals dropped, led by aluminium (-3.64% offshore; SHFE -3.09%) and nickel (-2.37%), while copper also fell (~-1.61%). | Oil prices dropped 4% on worries about falling Chinese demand and plans to release strategic reserves. In line with weaker macro sentiment, industrial metals fell: aluminium dropped ~3–3.6% and nickel fell ~2–2.4% on demand-slowdown fears. Lunnon Metals will acquire rights to two historical nickel mines (Silver Lake and Fisher) in Western Australia from Gold Fields’ subsidiary, adding 19 mining leases to its Kambalda project. The company will raise up to $30m for exploration and re-entry plans, potentially strengthening nickel supply expectations. | Ken Research says Indonesia’s EV charging equipment market will grow rapidly, citing tax incentives and “availability of nickel.” It notes Indonesia holds over 22% of the world’s nickel reserves and government plans to expand EV infrastructure, implying stronger battery-related nickel demand as investment grows. | A Quebec province passed a controversial regulation that raises the allowable level of nickel in the atmosphere, overcoming local pushback. The regulation is set to come into effect later, implying changes to nickel emissions compliance for affected operations. Such rules can influence nickel production costs and short-term supply outlooks.

SMM reports refined nickel supply remains tight as pandemic conditions reduce China’s refined production, limit customs clearance, and disrupt south-market transport. North tightness and high SHFE–LME spread raise spot premiums and keep downstream restocking constrained. SMM expects domestic nickel prices to be hard to decline near term. | SMM reports that major battery makers increased the proportion of LFP batteries, but also saw a rise in high-nickel NMC share in Q1. Lithium price strength raised procurement costs, leading to slower LFP purchasing, while advance orders boosted high-nickel NMC allocations. NEV demand was only slightly higher. | SMM reports China’s nickel sulphate market ran a 4,825 mt (nickel content) supply deficit in March, extending three consecutive months of deficits. High raw-material costs reduced in-house briquette dissolution, forcing producers to cut output. Battery-grade prices are expected to stay supported in April as tight supply and high demand persist. | Talon Metals purchased three additional drill rigs to expand exploration outside the main resource area at its Tamarack nickel-copper project in Minnesota. The new drilling targets multiple zones to find high-grade nickel-copper mineralization for potential battery supply, but current output is not reported as changing.

SMM cites customs data showing China’s nickel sulphate imports fell 16.7% month-on-month to 681 mt in nickel content in February, despite strong year-on-year growth. Exports were very small. The article expects March net imports to drop to 440 mt (-35.3% MoM) due to lower natural days and wider market price spreads. | SMM reports that high-grade NPI prices in March rose with extreme LME and SHFE nickel moves, expanding the nickel-vs-NPI spread and causing downstream to pause purchases. After LME rules and falling nickel prices, the spread narrowed and NPI stabilized in early April. Weak demand suggests NPI may stay range-bound. | SMM’s daily review shows industrial metals mixed on LME but generally higher in China, led by Shanghai nickel (+2.39%) and Shanghai aluminium (~+1.5%). The piece attributes nickel strength to tighter pure nickel supply amid Shanghai epidemic disruptions and weaker demand, while LME copper is slightly up (~+0.45%). | The piece reviews March events in LME nickel trading, noting that trading was suspended and then resumed amid disruption. Framed as a “short squeeze,” the episode likely drove unusual price dynamics. It also describes how these disruptions affected publication and display of LME nickel data on Fastmarkets. | Victory Nickel filed a proposal to restructure unsecured debt under Canada’s Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act, aiming to continue operating. The company also received bonus shares from Silver Elephant Mining related to the Minago Project, which become freely tradable later in 2022. The update is company-specific with no direct nickel supply impact details. | SMM’s overnight wrap says oil rose on possible action to ban Russian exports and the dollar strengthened. Despite macro headwinds, the article cites expectations of China stimulus supporting metals demand. It reports Shanghai copper up about 0.3%, nickel up about 3.49%, and aluminium up about 1.73%.

Culpeo Minerals completed induced polarisation surveys at its Quelon iron oxide-copper-gold project in Chile, identifying large geophysical anomalies and stating three zones are compelling drill targets. The company is pursuing earn-in stakes and plans a maiden drilling program. The update is exploratory and does not change near-term copper supply. | SMM reports SHFE nickel has continued rising but expects prices to stay rangebound. Scarce local supply, poor transportation, and tight nickel briquette and nickel sulphate keep premiums elevated. However, pessimism over downstream demand and weak liquidity/hedging on LME cap upside gains. | SMM reports a shortage of domestic pure nickel supply and temporarily suspends certain premium/discount quotes due to futures diverging from spot. Spot prices for Jinchuan and NORNICKEL nickel rose sharply, supported by reduced production and import losses. SHFE nickel futures increased for six straight days, aiming to reopen the import window. | The IMF criticized the LME for cancelling trades during last month’s nickel “pickle” after Russia-Ukraine fears drove nickel to record highs. The cancellations limited losses for Tsingshan but removed gains for other counterparties, prompting investigations by UK regulators and calls to strengthen LME governance.

SHFE nickel rose for a sixth straight day, with refined nickel spot prices also climbing sharply. The report attributes gains to domestic smelter production reductions, declining pure nickel inventories, and weaker nickel salt plant output amid losses. Higher SHFE/LME spreads are expected to open the import window, though a pullback is possible if supply improves. | The article cites LME data showing nickel inventories declining sharply to about 72,600 mt, near a two-year low, after a mid-April peak. It also notes SHFE nickel stocks fell 11.96% week-on-week to 7,668 mt. Lower exchange inventories are generally supportive for nickel futures. | London copper edged up as hopes for additional China stimulus offset a firm dollar and growth concerns. A suspension at Peru’s Las Bambas mine added a copper supply-tightness risk. Aluminium and nickel in LME and Shanghai were broadly softer, reflecting uneven sentiment tied to China lockdown-related demand worries. | The IMF urged strengthening London Metal Exchange governance after a nickel-market short squeeze last month. The event triggered a six-day suspension, a 250% price surge in under two days, and billions of dollars in cancelled trades linked to Chinese producer Tsingshan. The IMF cites governance lessons for commodity-market volatility. | SHFE nickel reversed after consecutive gains, dropping 3.04% as spot refined nickel prices also fell. Despite tight pure nickel supply, reduced downstream production from higher raw-material costs and sluggish demand are weighing on prices. The piece expects further weakness once the import window opens and supply improves.

BHP said labour shortages forced it to downgrade output at its Australian nickel business and its Chilean copper operations. The company’s iron ore division is still tracking its full-year target, but the specific nickel and copper production cuts point to near-term supply constraints that could weigh on copper and nickel prices. | BHP said labour shortages have forced it to downgrade output from its Australian nickel division and its Chilean copper division, even as its iron ore operations remain on track. The update signals near-term supply constraints for nickel and copper, which can weigh on short-term price expectations over coming weeks. | WBMS reported that the global refined nickel market ran a supply deficit of 17,400 mt from January to February 2022, with apparent demand exceeding production. Refined nickel output totaled 447,600 mt versus 464,900 mt demand. Chinese and Indonesian smelter output increased, but demand rose faster. | BHP shares fell after the miner said Q3 copper output dropped 6% to 369.7 kt and it cut FY22 copper guidance to 1,570–1,620 kt from 1,590–1,760 kt, citing lower Escondida volumes. BHP also lowered nickel guidance to 80–85 kt due to COVID-19 labor constraints. | SMM reports that SHFE and spot nickel prices drifted lower as spot fundamentals and futures diverged. Spot Jinchuan and Norilsk nickel averages fell about 2,750 yuan/mt, while inquiries improved slightly but trades remained thin; prices may be hard to fall further without higher inventories. | Norilsk Nickel notified ADR holders that Russian federal law will require termination of depositary receipt programs. From April 27, 2022, shares underlying ADRs will become non-voting and no dividends will be paid on those shares. ADR holders face restrictions on trading their ADRs for entitlements.

Lunnon Metals announced a first-time resource estimate for its N75C surface prospect at the former Foster mine within Australia’s Kambalda nickel district: 412,700 tonnes at 2.3% nickel (about 9,500 tonnes contained nickel). The company is continuing historical core work and progressing dewatering/permitting for re-entry. | SMM says nickel prices have trended upward but imports remain unprofitable and tight supply persists due to losses and production reductions by refined nickel enterprises. Demand is mixed—transactions are weak—yet nickel salt raw material costs rose, causing losses and shutdowns. Without clear inventory build, nickel is hard to fall. | SMM reports SHFE nickel rebounded after two down days, rising over 2% intraday and closing up 1.64%. It attributes strength to falling pure nickel supply from reduced domestic refined output, losses and output cuts at nickel salt factories, and supportive high-grade NPI pricing amid low inventories. Nickel likely won’t drop soon. | LME copper is on track for a third weekly decline as China’s COVID lockdowns cloud demand, while LME-approved inventories have risen to the highest since last October. Rising inventories and a stronger U.S. dollar add pressure. Limited support comes from hopes of Chinese stimulus and supply disruptions, but overall bias remains down. | SMM says nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports rose by 90,000 wmt to 5.664 million wmt as of Apr 22, with total Ni content around 1,000 mt. Seven major ports inventory also increased week-on-week. Supply easing from southern Philippines and weak downstream demand prevented further inventory declines, weighing on nickel sentiment. | SMM reports volatile LME nickel and rising SHFE nickel, despite imports remaining at losses. Shanghai bonded nickel inventory was little changed at 8,200 mt, with nickel briquettes and plates flat. COVID-19 slowed operations, LME liquidity remains weak, but SHFE’s high prices reflect tight Chinese supply; profitable imports may follow if prices persist. | ArcWest Exploration provided an exploration update for its Oxide Peak Cu-Au project, including planned 2022 drilling of an untested porphyry target and prior geophysical/geochemical results indicating copper anomalies. The release is company/project specific with no immediate changes to mine output, inventories, or pricing mechanisms for copper, nickel, or aluminium. | Fastmarkets has opened an IOSCO audit/consultation on its nickel sulfate ex-works mainland China price assessment (MB-NI-0244). The review updates price specifications and may shift assessment timing from within seven days to within 30 days, with weekly publication. The move is methodology-focused rather than a market-balance shock.

SHFE nickel fell 6.23% to 224,000 yuan/mt. The piece links the move to limited imported nickel inflows and low refined smelter operating rates tightening supply, while high prices and pandemic conditions reduced downstream demand, with alloy/battery/electroplating output cut. Net expectation: high-level fluctuation. | Norilsk Nickel reported preliminary 1Q22 production: consolidated nickel output rose 10% y/y to 52kt, driven by recovery of Oktyabrsky and Taimyrsky mines and ramp-up of the Norilsk Concentrator. Copper production stayed roughly unchanged at 91kt, with logistics and geopolitical issues not disrupting operations. | Nornickel said Q1 nickel production rose 10% year-on-year to 51,534 tonnes after the recovery of its two mines from 2021 flooding. Copper output was stable at 91,414 tonnes. Despite Western sanctions-related logistics challenges, the company kept its 2022 nickel forecast unchanged, citing uninterrupted operations. | The piece focuses on Russia’s Norilsk Nickel, saying it continues to pump out metal. Persistent output from a major nickel producer can reduce fears of near-term supply disruption and ease market tightness expectations, which is generally supportive for inventories and mildly bearish for nickel prices in the short term. | The piece headlines that Russia’s Norilsk Nickel is “keeping” production levels steady, continuing to pump out nickel metal. Continuous supply from a major producer tends to add availability to the market and can pressure nickel prices in the near term, absent signs of disruption or demand acceleration.

London copper rebounded 1.4% after hitting a more-than-two-month low, driven by hopes for additional China economic stimulus and a slight retreat in the U.S. dollar. The piece also notes lingering COVID lockdown worries and broader macro uncertainty, with aluminium and nickel referenced via markets and related company updates. | Elon Musk met an Indonesian delegation at Tesla’s Gigafactory Texas to discuss potential cooperation on Indonesia’s nickel deposits for Tesla battery supply and processing. Officials said the goal is to involve nickel in ESG-aligned battery-cell production, which could lift near-term expectations for nickel demand.

LME copper fell as concerns grew that COVID lockdowns in top consumer China will curb demand, while expectations for large U.S. rate hikes lifted the dollar to multi-year highs. The stronger dollar increased costs for non-dollar buyers. China stimulus hopes limited declines. Global risk-off also pressured industrial metals. | SMM reports SHFE nickel rebounded slightly and spot nickel prices rose. Jinchuan nickel averaged 231,550 yuan/mt (+4,050 yuan/mt) and Nornickel averaged 227,650 yuan/mt (+5,950 yuan/mt). Nickel briquette also increased. The rise was linked to late trading recovery and higher Shanghai premiums, though inquiries and transactions were weaker. | SMM reports SHFE nickel remained high as tight domestic pure nickel supply persisted, with poor customs clearance, low operating rates, and high spot premiums. NPI and nickel sulphate supplies were also described as tight. Although pandemic-related demand weakened and stainless transactions were poor, SHFE nickel is expected to stay elevated. | SHFE nickel rebounded more than 3% after a brief slump, with SMM 1# refined nickel spot prices averaging 225,600 yuan/mt. Tight domestic refined nickel supply persists, smelter operating rates remain weak, NPI imports are short, and nickel sulphate supply is constrained by producer losses. Demand is mixed amid pandemic weakness, but tight supply supports prices at highs. | Industrial metals rose as hopes for further Chinese stimulus outweighed COVID-related demand worries and fears from potential aggressive U.S. rate hikes. LME three-month copper edged up and LME aluminium gained. Nickel also rose, with investors weighing China’s monetary support amid lockdown impacts on industrial activity. | Gungnir Resources announced the start of a second-phase drill program (about 4,000 metres) at its Lappvattnet high-grade nickel project in Sweden. Drilling aims to expand and upgrade the existing nickel resource and test an additional EM conductor target, using results from phase one drilling. | The piece promotes nickel investing, highlighting the recent surge in nickel futures on the LME and linking upside to potential EV battery demand growth. It also notes supply risks from rapid Indonesian production expansion that could pressure prices. Overall, the narrative implies bullish near-term expectations for nickel despite volatility. | Proactive lists investor-focused headlines including Fabled Copper reporting an airborne survey identified new anomalies at its Muskwa copper project in British Columbia, and Quebec Nickel mobilizing a second drill rig at its Ducros property after VTEM data revealed new targets. Other items are unrelated to industrial metals.

SMM suspended quotations for premiums and discounts of 1# Jinchuan nickel and 1# imported nickel from Mar 25, 2022 due to extreme LME nickel volatility and weak guidance for spot pricing. With SHFE nickel stabilizing and manufacturers requesting resumption, SMM will resume from Apr 28, 2022. | INSG forecasts global nickel demand to rise to 3.02 million mt in 2022 from 2.78 million mt in 2021, while production increases to 3.08 million mt from 2.61 million mt. The market is expected to move from a 168,000 mt deficit in 2021 to a 67,000 mt surplus in 2022. | Glencore reported Q1 own-sourced copper production of 257,800 tonnes, down 14% versus Q1 2021, citing temporary geotechnical constraints at Katanga and other changes. Nickel output rose 22% to 30,700 tonnes mainly from Koniambo. The mixed supply signals imply limited net near-term price pressure. | American Resources secured worldwide rights to a provisional patent from Purdue University for multi-mode chromatography to isolate and purify battery-grade lithium, cobalt and nickel (plus manganese) from various feedstocks including end-of-life batteries. The firm highlights high purity, yields, low energy and costs, and plans for a new production train. | Vale reported pressure from nickel hedging during a record nickel price spike, per the article headline. The framing implies nickel prices are rising rapidly and could stay supported by current market conditions, which may influence short-term trading sentiment for nickel.

Huayou Cobalt said it signed a framework agreement with PT Vale Indonesia to develop an HPAL project using limonite from Vale’s Pomalaa mine. Huayou will use its hydrometallurgy to produce nickel-cobalt hydroxide (MHP) with planned annual capacity up to 120,000 mt in nickel content, implying higher nickel supply expectations. | Sumitomo Metal Mining exited its planned HPAL nickel project with PT Vale Indonesia over disagreements on construction timelines and costs. The change may delay the project and worsen supply risks for already tight battery-grade nickel. The piece notes HPAL is key to expanding battery-grade supply, with capacity growth expected later this decade. | SMM reported Chinese nickel ore inventories at ports rose 213,000 wmt to 5.877 million wmt as shipments from the Philippines increased after the rainy season. Transport issues from the pandemic and weaker NPI demand slowed consumption. With sufficient supply expected, port inventories are projected to keep rising in the short term. | Copper rebounded 1.4% after China’s Politburo pledged more economic support and the dollar eased, making copper cheaper for overseas buyers. However, a renewed COVID wave and tighter lockdowns are expected to hurt Chinese factory activity, with copper still headed for a sharp monthly fall. Aluminium and nickel also fell on China demand worries. | Huayou Cobalt surged after partnering with Vale to build an Indonesia plant that processes Vale’s Pomalaa limonite into nickel products (including mixed hydroxide precipitate) for electric vehicle batteries. The project is expected to reach up to 120,000 tons annually, with construction already underway and definitive agreements forthcoming.

Blackstone Minerals appointed GRES to deliver an upstream definitive feasibility study for its Ta Khoa nickel project in Vietnam, covering process infrastructure, metallurgical testwork, and tailings storage, alongside mining studies. The project includes an existing nickel mine and planned refinery, supporting continued de-risking toward a future investment decision. | The LME chief said the exchange may end its unusual netting of initial and variation margin calls after heavy criticism of how it handled extreme price moves in nickel following Russia’s war. Removing the margin netting could increase funding costs for hedgers and weigh on nickel sentiment. | Blackstone Minerals hired GR Engineering Services to support a definitive feasibility study for its upstream Ta Khoa nickel project. The work includes process and metallurgical testing and tailings design, aiming to de-risk development and improve financing confidence. The move may modestly boost expectations for future nickel supply. | Talon Metals reported assay results from new drill holes at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project in Minnesota, highlighting thick, high-grade nickel and copper intervals in the CGO West area. The company plans continued drilling and feasibility work toward first production in 2026, with updates expected as new targets are drilled. | A European climate group (T&E) says BloombergNEF-based data show enough nickel (and lithium) to support production for up to 14 million EVs in 2023. It argues Europe should secure more raw materials to speed the shift away from oil, but the “enough supply” framing may reduce immediate nickel price tightness. | Fortune Nickel and Gold reported results from low-altitude drone magnetic surveys at its Beck-Ottaway-Lennox properties near Timmins, identifying magnetic anomalies coincident with EM conductors in mafic-ultramafic rocks. The company plans priority drilling to test potential Crawford-style nickel sulfide mineralization. | Couloir Capital announced it is starting research coverage on SPC Nickel Corp., a Canadian nickel exploration company focused on Sudbury and other projects. The report highlights favorable macro conditions supporting the nickel outlook and potential future demand growth, but provides no concrete near-term supply or trade impacts that would directly move nickel prices. | The LME’s nickel contract was suspended after March volatility drove prices above $101,000/tonne, triggering FCA and other regulatory reviews of the exchange and clearing house. The CEO will remain, and the priority is restoring credible, orderly nickel price discovery. Market impact is mostly confidence-related, not a direct nickel supply shock. | The article says QuantumScape may be “cathode-agnostic,” with CEO Jagdeep Singh suggesting a switch from nickel to iron-based cathodes alongside lithium-metal anodes. The piece frames iron as potentially cheaper and more energy efficient, implying reduced nickel usage in future battery designs and pressuring nickel sentiment.

Aranjin Resources began follow-up auger geochemical drilling at the Baavhai Uul copper-nickel discovery in Mongolia under an agreement with ION Energy. Early results reported anomalous nickel (up to 2,150 ppm) and copper (up to 677 ppm), with plans to upscale auger drilling and geophysics in coming weeks. | Copper prices steadied near 4-1/2 month lows as traders weighed rising interest rate risk, China COVID outbreaks and slowing factory activity that could weaken demand. The dollar’s strength further pressures dollar-priced metals. Speculators shifted toward price-fall bets. LME aluminium and nickel also posted declines. | Aranjin Resources commenced follow-up auger drilling at the Baavhai Uul copper-nickel discovery in Mongolia under an earn-in agreement. Early results cited up to 2,150 ppm nickel and 677 ppm copper from shallow samples, with additional auger, geophysics, and other copper project work planned.  | The piece describes the history of Britannia Mines near Vancouver, focusing on copper ore concentration, output, disasters, and later environmental remediation. While copper mining and historical production are emphasized, the article contains no present-day supply, policy, or demand event that would likely affect short-term industrial metal prices. | Reuters reports that Potanin’s Interros and its key holding in Nornickel have avoided US/EU sanctions, while Western firms exit Russia. Since Nornickel is a leading refined nickel producer, being spared from sanctions should ease fears of disruption and weigh on nickel’s near-term risk premium. | Bitterroot Resources provided results from a winter drilling program at its LM magmatic nickel-copper project in Michigan. Nickel-copper sulphide mineralization was intersected in 10 of 26 holes, including disseminated to semi-massive sulphides. The company plans further down-plunge drilling after additional land acquisition. | Bitterroot Resources updated on a completed winter drilling program at its LM nickel-copper-platinum group metals property in Michigan, reporting sulphide mineralization in 10 of 26 holes, including chalcopyrite, pyrrhotite and pentlandite. The company plans further targeting after additional land acquisition, signaling ongoing exploration progress.

NickelX received a $180,000 WA government grant to start drilling four holes at its Cosmos South nickel prospect near major producers. The project is progressing permitting and using geophysical data to define drill targets. Given the small exploration scale, it likely has limited effect on near-term nickel prices. | SMM reports LME nickel fluctuated between $32,000–$33,880/mt with weaker liquidity and SHFE nickel remaining rangebound at high levels. A pullback from macro factors was offset by persistent supply shortage, tightening nickel sulphate supply. Poor demand pressured prices, so the outlook is rangebound: SHFE 218,000–238,000 yuan/mt. | BHP says its bid for a Canadian nickel miner is only a “tip of the iceberg” as it pursues broader growth ambitions. The report implies continued focus on expanding nickel exposure and may affect near-term sentiment around nickel supply and production prospects. | Victory Nickel updated investors on its bankruptcy-related debt restructuring proposal and held a creditor vote, later adjourned to address concerns from its largest creditor. The company is a sulphide nickel resource holder, and the distress raises near-term uncertainty around its nickel operations, typically bearish for nickel sentiment. | The piece argues Potanin has largely avoided US/EU sanctions, despite Western pressure after Ukraine. It stresses Nornickel’s size in refined nickel production and notes its apparent exemption from sanctions, suggesting reduced nickel supply disruption risk versus previously sanctioned Russian producers.

SMM reports sluggish Chinese nickel trading, with SHFE nickel and spot premiums softening versus the prior day. It links the decline in pure nickel quotes mainly to China’s pandemic-driven lower manufacturing operating rates, which weakened demand. It also notes relative price differences between nickel briquette and nickel sulphate and slightly rising quotes. | SMM reports that LME nickel fell through the week while SHFE nickel stayed rangebound at high levels. Shanghai bonded nickel inventory rose to 8,800 mt, up 600 mt WoW, with nickel plate inventory increasing on long-term orders awaiting customs clearance. The buildup points to weaker near-term nickel tightness. | SMM says nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 235,000 wmt to 5.642 million wmt (44,300 mt Ni content) versus last week. Demand improved as pandemic easing let NPI plants restock. Philippines shipments rose after the rainy season, but supply gains remain limited. Port inventories are expected to stay steady short term. | Copper, aluminium, nickel and other commodities have fallen as China tightened Covid controls. The lockdowns reduce activity in end-use industries, cutting near-term metal demand and weighing on broader sentiment toward the metals complex. The price declines over the past couple of weeks reflect this demand shock. | Copper, aluminium, nickel and other metals have fallen over the past couple of weeks as China tightened Covid curbs. The lockdowns reduce demand from the world’s largest metal consumer and dampen sentiment toward the metal complex, creating negative price pressure across industrial commodities in the near term. | The headline says Tesla has confirmed a nickel supply deal with Vale, suggesting additional demand visibility for nickel in the near term. If the agreement affects contracted volumes or timing, it may tighten market expectations and provide modest upward support to nickel prices over the next few weeks. | Vale signed a long-term agreement to supply Tesla with nickel sourced from its Canada operations. The contract increases visibility of nickel demand from a major EV manufacturer, potentially tightening near-term supply expectations and supporting nickel prices. The announcement is directly linked to nickel market fundamentals. | CME Group is exploring a cash-settled nickel contract, including a possible nickel sulphate product, after the LME suspended nickel trading and cancelled billions in deals in March amid extreme price swings. LME volumes fell post-suspension. The move targets better hedging access, but the article cites no immediate supply-demand shift. | Shanghai’s Covid-19 controls affected bonded metals flows. Copper bonded stocks kept increasing in April but more slowly as transport improved and import arbitrage turned profitable. Aluminium and nickel bonded stocks declined due to closed or loss-making arbitrage windows and limited inflows/outflows, with weak trading and constrained logistics.  | BHP says its failed bid for Noront Resources is “almost the tip of the iceberg” for future Canadian nickel growth, setting up teams to seek projects in Ontario’s Ring of Fire. The executive also notes focus on copper and investing in opportunities, but provides no deal or supply disruption. | Vale said it signed a long-term deal with Tesla to supply nickel from its Canada operations. The agreement links Vale’s nickel production to EV battery demand, which can improve near-term market sentiment about nickel demand. The article does not quantify volumes or timing, limiting precision on magnitude.

SMM reports China produced 12,900 mt of refined nickel in April (+2.62% MoM, -0.65% YoY). Output matched expectations, supported by more stable LME nickel and resumed operations as raw-material import issues eased and March maintenance ended. May refined nickel production is expected to rise to ~13,400 mt. | China’s nickel sulphate output fell 12.46% on the month in April as nickel salt factories faced high costs and weak demand with near-negative profits, prompting production cuts to support prices. In May, some plants resumed and output rose, but demand for precursors and batteries remains poor, keeping production subdued. | Indonesia’s President Jokowi plans to meet Elon Musk during a US visit, as the country seeks to accelerate development of its nickel industry to supply EV batteries. Tesla representatives have visited Morawali’s nickel production hub, and Indonesia is targeting a full domestic nickel-to-batteries supply chain. The news is supportive for nickel sentiment. | Nickel 28 reported Q1 2022 Ramu production of 8,756 tonnes of contained nickel and lower cash costs of $1.48/lb net of cobalt by-product credits. Management said production is at expected levels as Asia reopens, while sales were temporarily hit by export restrictions and the Olympics, expected to normalize in Q2.

SHFE nickel has fallen for four consecutive days, with the spot refined nickel down sharply over five days amid weak downstream demand. NPI prices weakened as steel mill transactions slowed and profits were squeezed by oversupply in stainless steel. Supply remains tight for pure nickel, but demand weakness dominates near term.

New Caledonia plans more environmentally friendly nickel production, aiming to power nickel factories with increasing renewable energy and build solar generation for Prony Resources. The project is tied to cutting emissions and costs versus lower-cost Indonesian and Filipino producers. Output is scheduled over 2023–2025, implying limited immediate price impact. | SMM reports that pure nickel supply remains tight because the SHFE/LME spread keeps the nickel import window closed, and NPI output declines. However, SHFE nickel and spot prices fell, with weaker stainless steel demand after renewed COVID cases in Wuxi. Macro factors also continue to pressure nickel downward. | SMM reports that SHFE metals broadly rose on May 11, with SHFE nickel up about 3%, SHFE aluminium up 2.61%, and SHFE copper up around 0.3%. LME copper and aluminium also increased, while nickel declined, reflecting mixed global signals amid a weaker dollar and higher crude. | Copper rose 1.4% as China’s slowing COVID-19 infections eased near-term demand concerns, though “zero-COVID” restrictions remain. The report notes lockdowns and macro worries had pressured prices recently. It also cites disruptions affecting cobalt, nickel, lithium logistics and shows aluminium up while nickel falls. | Blackwolf Copper and Gold provides an exploration and permitting update for its Niblack (Cu-Au-Ag-Zn VMS) and Hyder-area projects (including Cantoo with Cu/Ag/Zn plus gold-silver). The company anticipates permits and plans multi-phase drilling, potentially expanding resources, which may modestly influence near-term copper expectations. No nickel or aluminium impacts are stated. | Copper prices rose about 1.4% as China’s COVID-19 infections slowed, easing immediate demand concerns, though partial “zero-COVID” restrictions still suppress activity. The piece notes April vehicle and cathode output weakness from lockdowns and logistics disruption, while LME aluminium also gained and nickel fell on the day. | ALX Resources said it completed six of eight planned holes in its Electra nickel-copper project in Ontario, intersecting sulphide mineralization and detecting nickel, copper and zinc via portable XRF. Additional lab results are pending and the update is exploratory, with no production or definitive resource revision.

Nickel fell alongside a broader mining sell-off, but the piece argues it is positioned to recover sooner than other battery metals. It points to EV battery demand plus existing stainless-steel use, Australian class-one premium supplies, and China’s grade dislocation where NPI and nickel sulphate trade at deep discounts to LME. | SMM reports tight pure nickel supply in April, with overseas sources driving availability and import losses persisting due to weak SHFE/LME spread. Downstream alloy and battery demand softened after renewed pandemic impacts. May is expected to keep supply and demand relatively weak, with limited upward momentum in SHFE nickel prices. | Canada Nickel announced it submitted a preliminary draft Initial Project Description for the Crawford nickel project to Canada’s Impact Assessment Agency after signing impact assessment process agreements with local Indigenous communities. The step initiates a review period and supports regulatory certainty for the project’s development. | Russia has allowed Norilsk Nickel’s ADRs to remain traded abroad. The move can improve market access and sentiment toward the company, a key nickel producer. While it does not directly change nickel supply or demand, the policy easing may provide mild near-term support to nickel pricing.

SMM says overseas pure nickel remains tight due to import losses, with premiums rising, but it also expects NPI supply to move toward surplus as Indonesia ramps up output. Weak stainless demand and reduced steel mill production are pressuring NPI, with NPI prices and nickel outlook leaning downward. | SMM reports that discounts of battery-grade nickel sulphate versus nickel briquettes narrowed in May. Nickel prices stayed relatively high in April, but nickel salt weakened on poor demand, leading some factories to cut briquette use. Entering May, tighter nickel salt supply reduced the downside more than nickel prices, supporting the spread. | SMM reports Chinese nickel ore inventories fell 272,000 wmt to 5.37 million wmt by May 13, with Ni-content stocks down to 42,200 mt. The decline is linked to poor Philippine weather affecting mines and delaying shipments, keeping port inventories historically low and likely limiting near-term supply.

Indonesia’s President Jokowi met Tesla CEO Elon Musk in Texas after working-level talks on potential investments in Indonesia’s nickel industry and EV battery supply. The Indonesian government says the discussions focused on technology and investment, which could support nickel demand/supply outlook and boost nickel prices. | Arc Minerals agreed with an Anglo American subsidiary to form a copper-cobalt JV for a project in Zambia. Anglo plans to fund exploration and pay cash totaling up to $88.5 million while retaining up to 70% ownership. The deal is subject to due diligence and definitive agreements, implying limited immediate supply impact. | Canadian North Resources reports re-sampling and re-assaying results from historic drill cores at its Ferguson Lake project, confirming high-grade copper-nickel massive sulfides and wide low-sulfide PGM mineralization. The findings support resource-estimation confidence, but do not indicate near-term supply changes, implying limited immediate impact on copper and nickel prices.

IGO’s proxy advisers urged shareholders to back its $1.26 billion bid to acquire nickel producer Western Areas. The offer was raised to $3.87 per share, though it remains below the fair-value range cited by KPMG’s independent expert. The update may modestly influence nickel sentiment but is not clearly market-moving. | SMM reports pure nickel supply stayed tight in April but downstream demand weakened due to COVID-19 disruptions. Spot activity improved only when prices fell at end-month, while nickel briquette remained scarce yet trades stagnated. For May, tight supply persists but weak demand and macro factors are expected to pressure prices downward. | EV Nickel reports extensive historical exploration at its Carman–Langmuir area near Timmins, identifying long dunite–peridotite units with nickel grades above 0.25% and thickness to at least 250m depth in records. The company plans late-spring surface sampling and expects drilling permits in coming weeks. | The article links recent nickel market “chaos” and extreme price spikes to US government actions, including increased nickel demand from war footing and sanctions reducing supply, alongside tighter credit/liquidity conditions tied to Federal Reserve policy. It frames nickel as both an industrial metal and a monetary metaphor, with no clear near-term directional call. | Leading Edge Materials says it has signed an exploration license for its Romanian nickel and cobalt project. The news is a development toward potential future supply, but as it concerns exploration rather than production, it is unlikely to materially alter near-term nickel market balances or pricing expectations within days to four weeks.

SMM reports weak spot nickel trading, with lower activity tied to declining procurement expectations from alloy plants. Jinchuan nickel premiums rise slightly, but NORNICKEL prices and briquette premiums are firmer or falling, and nickel briquette transactions are nearly absent due to weaker economy versus intermediate products. | Copper fell 1.1% as markets renewed worries about global slowdown after hawkish Fed signals and weak China data tied to Shanghai COVID lockdown impacts. The stronger dollar pressured dollar-priced metals. LME aluminum and nickel also declined, reflecting broad risk-off sentiment toward industrial metal demand. | Talon Metals announced additional infill drilling results at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Minnesota, including a record 23.44 m massive sulphide intercept of nickel and copper (assays pending). The company plans to complete infill drilling for a pre-feasibility study and references a Tesla nickel supply agreement. | CNGR will invest $1.26 billion in three nickel-matte projects in Indonesia’s Weda Bay, raising annual capacity by 120,000 tonnes (40,000 tonnes nickel content each). Nickel matte is processed into battery-grade nickel for EV batteries. Investments are meant to secure inputs and lower costs, likely affecting nickel supply expectations. | The page content appears to be a privacy/cookie notice rather than the operational update itself. The title references an update to mining activities at TVIRD’s Agata nickel/iron mine. Operational changes at a nickel asset can influence short-term nickel supply expectations, but no directional production details are shown here.

CNGR and Rigqueza plan to invest $1.3 billion in Indonesia to build three nickel-processing plants at Weda Bay, producing 40,000 tons of high-grade nickel matte annually each. The matte is used to make nickel sulfate for battery precursors. No construction timetable was provided. | SMM reports that high raw-material costs are stressing nickel sulphate manufacturers using dissolving nickel briquette and imported MHP. When nickel briquette prices trade above nickel sulphate, some sulphate plants cut briquette sales, lowering briquette spot premiums versus NORNICKEL nickel. Demand use in stainless/alloys appears limited, muting price signals.

The article says nickel demand surged 16.2% in 2021, creating a 168,000-tonne supply shortfall. For 2022, usage is expected to rise 8.6% but global production is forecast to jump 18.2% on Indonesia’s new capacity, moving toward a modest surplus and likely lower LME prices, though Class I supply remains disputed. | SMM reports that in early Shanghai trading, SHFE nickel rose 5.36%, while SHFE aluminium gained 1.69% and SHFE copper rose 0.66%. The broader move coincided with a firmer US dollar, expanding Fed balance sheet, hawkish Fed comments, and crude oil weakness tied to growth concerns. | SMM said nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 7,000 wmt week-on-week to 5.363 million wmt (total Ni content -50 wmt). The decline stems mainly from reduced Philippines shipments due to poor weather, alongside faster consumption by low-inventory NPI plants. Further Philippines shipment developments are watched.

Aston Minerals says metallurgical testing of nickel-cobalt sulphide samples from its Boomerang target produced saleable concentrate using conventional flotation. The head grades were ~0.51% nickel and 0.016% cobalt, with concentrate grading up to 11.29% nickel. Further optimization work is planned as analysis continues. | SMM says nickel is likely to fall this week. Weak demand and limited bonded-zone customs clearance have kept pure nickel tight, but the import window should lead to more customs clearances. NPI and nickel-sulphate input costs are pressured by lower LME nickel, while stainless-steel downstream activity remains subdued. | China’s imports of nickel intermediaries MHP and matte hit record levels in April, helped by faster NPI-to-matte conversion and ramped MHP supply from Indonesia. With supply feeding sulphate production and incentives to convert into exchange-deliverable Class 1 nickel rising, availability could improve and LME nickel prices/spreads face downside pressure.

SMM says some spot pure nickel from SHFE 2205 deliveries eased China’s supply tightness, causing NORNICKEL premiums to drop sharply and Russian nickel prices to fall. NPI supply is also rising with Indonesia’s Delong ramp, while demand remains weak under pandemic recovery. Downward pressure persists despite low inventories. | Taruga confirms ionic adsorption clay rare earth mineralisation at its Mt Craig copper project and plans further REE drilling. Separately, at the Meekatharra nickel-copper project, Peak completed an aircore program that intersected broad, “highly encouraging” nickel zones, including 17m at 0.29% Ni and 10m at 0.32% Ni. | SMM says pure nickel spot premiums weakened: Jinchuan nickel averaged 9,500 yuan/mt, down 1,500 yuan/mt from the prior day, due to soft downstream buying. Nornickel premiums were steady. Nickel briquette quotes rose on firmer nickel futures, but cost-efficiency issues for nickel sulphate weighed on trading. | Global Battery Metals was granted four new surface and mineral leases in Michigan, expanding its Sawyer Camp nickel-copper exploration area. The company plans electromagnetic surveys to guide drilling targeting mafic-ultramafic intrusions potentially hosting nickel and copper mineralization. No production or reserve update is provided. | Tesla is studying a long-lasting nickel-based EV battery (NMC) with research results published in May, claiming longevity up to 100 years and comparable performance to LFP cells. While still experimental and not tied to immediate scaling, the work implies potential future nickel demand and supports a mild bullish bias for nickel prices. | TG Metals begins trading on the ASX to fund exploration at its Lake Johnston Greenstone Belt projects in Western Australia. The company plans aggressive geophysical work and drilling to define nickel-cobalt oxide resources, including a near-term Bremer Range laterite target and other nickel prospects over the next two years.

EU supervisor ESMA says it may request more granular exposure data from additional non-EU CCPs after the March “nickel-gate” episode on the London Metal Exchange, when all nickel contracts were cancelled. It warns current oversight could leave regulators unprepared for future LME-style disruptions affecting nickel markets. | EV Nickel says it received an exploration permit for Phase 3 drilling and started surface sampling at its Carman-Langmuir ultramafic targets near Timmins, Ontario. Historic work shows elevated nickel grades, and the company plans further drilling in summer. The update is exploration-focused with no near-term output impact stated. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. announced it has begun construction of an all-season access road into the Kenbridge Nickel Project in northwestern Ontario, with permits allowing maintenance and upgrades such as brushing, ditching, graveling, and culverts. Completion is expected by September 2022, supporting project development and future nickel operations.

SMM reports weak downstream demand and declining nickel sulphate prices due to softer raw-material costs and limited alloy buying. However, short-term pure nickel inventories remain low and support prices, while Indonesian Delong NPI commissioning is progressing and some supply restarts. Overall, nickel is expected not to drop sharply. | SMM reports CITIC Futures’ view that China’s nickel market has faced weak demand amid high LME nickel prices and pandemic-related drag. However, with improving nickel affordability and rising Indonesia NPI and nickel-salt feedstock supply, primary nickel is expected to run a ~140,000 mt Ni-content surplus, suggesting bearish near-term pricing pressure. | CNGR and RIGQUEZA plan to invest $1.26 billion to expand nickel matte production in Indonesia, targeting 40,000 tons per year, building on a prior 60,000 ton cooperation. The new battery-related energy investment focus highlights additional nickel supply capacity, likely weighing on nickel prices.

Nickel North Exploration closes a private placement tranche and announces a new flow-through private placement to fund 2022 field exploration at its Hawk Ridge Ni-Cu-PGE project. The release is about equity financing and exploration plans, not production, shipments, or disruptions affecting near-term nickel supply. | Copper rose after a three-day slump as the dollar weakened, while worries about China’s economic slowdown persisted. Nickel on the Shanghai Futures Exchange climbed up to 5% after three days of declines. Separately, a strike at Rusal’s Guinea bauxite mine raised supply disruption concerns for aluminium-linked markets. | Copper rose after a three-day slump, supported by a weaker U.S. dollar, but the article says copper was still set for weekly losses due to worries about China’s demand amid slowing growth. It also references an aluminium-related Rusal mine strike and low Chinese nickel inventories lifting nickel prices. | SMM says nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports rose 37,000 wmt to 5.4 million wmt, with Ni content up 300 mt to 42,400 mt. The increase follows improved Philippines shipments after bad weather. Demand is supported by falling NPI prices, but overall the supply build may weigh on nickel sentiment short term. | SMM reports spot pure nickel imports stayed profitable, but falling pure nickel premiums boosted customs clearance. Shanghai bonded zone nickel inventory fell to 7,900 mt, down 700 mt weekly, including 2,900 mt nickel briquettes and 5,000 mt nickel plates. Traders expect premiums to be further lowered, implying continued near-term supply tightening. | Copper rebounded after three sessions of losses as a weaker U.S. dollar and hopes of China easing COVID curbs boosted demand expectations. China’s copper futures also rose. The piece also cites an illegal strike at Rusal’s Guinea bauxite mine, supporting aluminium, while aluminium and nickel prices gained on the day. | Indonesia’s officials say Tesla’s team visited nickel processing plants and that talks over a potential battery/EV factory are still ongoing, after earlier claims of agreement were tempered. The news points to possible near-term demand for Indonesian nickel supply for the battery supply chain, supporting expectations of firmer nickel prices.

SMM reports SHFE nickel (2206) gaining and closing higher after short funds covered, with price support from tight pure nickel supply and low inventories/warrants. While nickel sulphate and NPI prices soften due to weaker demand and supply conditions, overall nickel is seen as hard to fall sharply in the near term.

LME copper held near a three-week high as China demand optimism rose. Better Chinese factory data and easing COVID-19 lockdowns, including Shanghai reopening, improved near-term consumption expectations. The U.S. dollar’s limited strength provided support, while copper was steady near $9,537/tonne and other metals were mixed to slightly higher. | SMM reports NPI prices may stay volatile around 1,500–1,550 yuan/mtu, tracking NPI plant costs that could fall with more nickel ore supply and lower ore prices. It says the NPI-to-nickel linkage is weaker due to a large spread since March, so NPI declines should not greatly pressure nickel in the short term. | SMM reports sharp declines in Shanghai spot nickel. Jinchuan nickel averages about 4,000 yuan/mt (down sharply), while NORNICKEL averages 5,400 yuan/mt (down 850). The drop is linked to traders shipping as the pure nickel import window opened, falling premiums, and weaker nickel briquette quotes as futures fell. | SMM reports that LME nickel inventories have fallen to a two-year low, with May 20 seeing a new low around 71,898 mt. SHFE data show nickel inventories dropped further last week, down 9.61% to 2,877 mt on May 27, a record low. | Cordoba Minerals begins feasibility-study technical work and a 40,000-metre infill drilling program at its San Matias copper-gold-silver project in Colombia. The company cites prior PFS economics using US$3.60/lb copper and notes the current spot copper price, but provides no new supply or demand changes that would move prices. Nickel/aluminium not mentioned. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. says it has started environmental baseline studies for the Kenbridge Nickel Project to support permitting and approvals. It retained consulting firms for time-sensitive Spring 2022 work and plans further updates on a summer work program. The release is a development/permitting step, not imminent nickel production.

Sama reports four holes at the Grata prospect in Ivory Coast returning long, thick intersections of nickel-copper sulphide mineralization, including GR-08 (297.6m) and GR-11 (212m). Grades include ~0.2–0.3% Ni and ~0.2–0.3% Cu. Assays for additional holes remain pending, with no near-term production impact stated. | Seahawk-like news highlights new drilling at the Grata prospect in Ivory Coast, with multiple holes intersecting long widths of Ni-Cu rich sulphide mineralization (notably high Cu/Ni in GR-06 zones) and additional narrow stringers.
