Results for further holes are pending; the company aims to fast-track resource definition. | SMM reports pure nickel spot premiums shifted to trade over the SHFE 2207 nickel contract, but premiums for Jinchuan and NORNICKEL fell versus the prior day. It attributes declines to spot inflows into the market and higher supply, with nickel briquette prices dropping on weaker trading and cost-efficiency. | Resource Mining Corporation plans a six-week maiden RC drilling program at its Kabulwanyele nickel-cobalt project in Tanzania, targeting massive sulphides beneath nickel laterite and testing a 2km strike area. The company is also progressing due diligence for its Massive Nickel acquisition, citing a sharply stronger nickel market and competition for assets. | Nickel 28 reported results and significant debt repayment linked to cash generation from its Ramu nickel-cobalt operation. The release highlights strong nickel production and notes market tightness, with LME nickel trading above $12.50/lb. These factors are supportive for near-term nickel sentiment and expectations. | LME Clear reported a record $2 billion initial margin breach in March tied to soaring nickel prices after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. The peak breach reflects a worst-case scenario for the largest clearing member default. The event underscores the severity of nickel market volatility and stressed clearing finances.

Elliott Associates has sued the London Metal Exchange for $456 million after the LME suspended and cancelled nickel trades in March. HKEX said LME and LME Clear were named in a UK judicial review filed in June. The case centers on the trading halt’s handling rather than nickel supply or demand. | SMM reports nickel prices stayed rangebound at high levels, with spot premiums drifting down and trading volume improving. Nickel sulphate cost differentials pressured market expectations, while weak NPI terminal demand and limited stainless steel consumption restrained gains. Downstream alloy purchasing improved, but primary supply/demand recovery is still absent; low inventory supports prices. | Elliott Associates sued the LME and LME Clear for $456m after the exchange cancelled nickel trades on 8 March when nickel prices surged. HKEX said the market became disorderly and trading was suspended for over a week. The case and wider regulatory scrutiny could weigh on near-term nickel sentiment. | Elliott Management sued the London Metal Exchange for more than $456 million over the exchange’s suspension and cancellation of nickel trades earlier this year, after dramatic nickel price swings. The claim alleges unlawful conduct and human-rights violations, with the case filed in England’s High Court. | SHFE nickel extended gains, at one point rising more than 3%, while LME nickel was up after opening. SMM notes premiums narrowing amid higher absolute prices and weaker near-term transactions, but still-low nickel inventories and gradual downstream restocking are expected to keep nickel prices supported and elevated. | Elliott Management has sued the London Metal Exchange for $456 million, alleging losses tied to nickel trades being suspended and canceled earlier this year. The dispute highlights market-function disruptions around nickel contracts, which can pressure short-term nickel pricing through liquidity, hedging uncertainty, and investor sentiment. | The news reports that the LME faces a US$456 million lawsuit from Elliott International after it allegedly suspended nickel futures trading and cancelled trades. The dispute highlights trading-market disruption and potential legal/operational uncertainty, which can shift near-term nickel price expectations through liquidity and risk-premium effects. | Elliott Management sued the London Metal Exchange over its March 2022 suspension of nickel trading and cancellation of nickel trades after the price surged above $100,000/ton during a Russian-sanctions-driven short squeeze. The LME and its owners dispute wrongdoing and regulators launched a nickel market review. Nickel later traded far lower. | U.S. hedge fund Elliott Associates is suing the LME for $456 million after the exchange cancelled nickel trades and suspended its nickel market during chaotic trading in March. The lawsuit adds pressure amid regulator probes and efforts to restore volumes, potentially worsening near-term nickel liquidity and sentiment. | Nickel prices on the SHFE Ni 2207 contract rose about 1.33% on June 6 after an intraday high. The report links the move to improved pure nickel supply from Nornickel circulation, higher domestic inventories, better spot premiums, and a gradual demand recovery, suggesting nickel remains supported this week. | Elliott Associates sued the LME for $456 million over the exchange cancelling nickel trades during March chaos that led to an LME nickel suspension. The dispute comes as regulators probe the LME and it struggles to rebuild nickel volumes and trust. Nickel prices had earlier surged on short-cover expectations. | Elliott Management has filed a £363m lawsuit against the LME, alleging the exchange’s cancellation of nickel trades on March 8 was unlawful. The dispute followed a disorderly nickel surge, with regulators and the IMF criticizing conflicts of interest. The case reintroduces uncertainty for nickel trading and market structure. | Infinity Stone Ventures begins a reconnaissance exploration program at its Zen-Whoberi copper-cobalt-(plus platinum/palladium) project in Quebec. The work aims to confirm historical results, including reported copper intercepts, before potential drill work later in 2022. The release does not suggest immediate production effects for metals markets.

Jane Street Global sued the LME for $15.3 million after the exchange cancelled nickel trades in March amid severe volatility when nickel prices doubled to over $100,000/t within hours. The LME is also facing a larger lawsuit from Elliott Associates and regulator probes over its suspension and cancellations. | SMM reported that China’s nickel spot market stayed at a high level but prices edged down: Jinchuan and NORNICKEL nickel were quoted around 6,500–7,000 yuan/mt, averaging 6,750, down 500 yuan from the previous day. Premiums narrowed slightly as SHFE remained high, but spot trades were poor. | SMM reports pure nickel spot import premiums turned profitable from May 16 to June 1, prompting a drop in Shanghai bonded nickel inventories despite ongoing concern about weak downstream demand. Demand is improving after customs clearance and easing COVID impacts. With tight visible supply and warrant shortages, near-term nickel prices are expected to keep rising. | SKRR Exploration Inc. has signed a definitive agreement to acquire full ownership of the Nickel Peak Claim Group in British Columbia’s Omineca mining district. The asset is described as a nickel prospect, which can modestly improve sentiment on future nickel supply, though it is not immediate production. | SMM reports Tsingshan Group plans to commission a project in Indonesia producing 50,000 mt of refined nickel. The refinery uses Tsingshan nickel matte as raw material to make nickel plate and refined output. Start-up timing is not confirmed, but the project adds upside to supply expectations for nickel. | Canada Nickel announced it has agreed to acquire a 100% interest in the Bannockburn Nickel Property near Matachewan, Ontario, and to share more details on other recently acquired properties. The news is limited to land acquisitions and does not indicate near-term changes to nickel output or supply. | Granite Creek Copper announced it is starting a 2022 Induced Polarization (IP) survey at its Carmacks Cu-Au-Ag project in Yukon, Canada, to improve targeting of deeper and offset mineralization. The release focuses on exploration planning and resource/PEA work; it does not indicate production or supply changes. | Grid Metals and Canada Nickel announced a deal under which Canada Nickel will acquire the Bannockburn nickel property, paying Grid with 2 million shares. The company highlights drilling intersections and processing work indicating potential to produce a nickel concentrate, improving near-term perceptions of nickel resource development prospects. | SHFE nickel (ni 2207) rose modestly on June 7, supported by historically low LME and SHFE nickel inventories despite slight customs clearance improving supply. Domestic supply remained tight, and improving expectations for new-energy vehicle demand boosted nickel sulfate demand, though downstream remained cautious, capping upside. | US traders sued the LME over how it handled the March nickel disruption, alleging unlawful cancellation of trades before suspension. The nickel market had spiked after a short position by Tsingshan-linked activity amplified the move. The FCA is investigating the episode, adding uncertainty for nickel pricing. | ICE Futures Europe president Stuart Williams criticizes the LME’s March closure of the nickel market and cancellation of trades following extreme price swings. He argues circuit breakers might have prevented the shutdown, warning of knock-on effects for participants. An LME executive defends the action as a “tough decision.”

A second US trading firm, Jane Street, sues the LME over the exchange’s cancellation of nickel trades in March, alongside a larger Elliott Management claim. The cancellations followed a nickel price surge amid Ukraine invasion-related volatility, and the lawsuits target the legality of the cancellations. | SMM reports Jinchuan and Nornickel nickel in Shanghai quoted at 5,000-5,500 yuan/mt, averaging 5,250, down 1,500 yuan from the prior day. Premiums narrowed and nickel briquette prices fell to 222,500-223,500 yuan/mt, but transactions remained poor because futures prices were still high. | LG Energy Solution broke ground on nickel processing plants in Indonesia, including a $3.5 billion smelter producing nickel sulfate and a $2.4 billion cathode/precursor facility for EV batteries. It also plans a joint $300 million nickel mine with Antam. Indonesia’s ongoing nickel downstream push bolsters demand outlook. | CanAlaska, via partner Metal Energy, has started a 10,000-metre phase two drill program at the Manibridge nickel project in Manitoba, targeting nickel-copper sulphide mineralization near a past-producing mine. The release cites prior drilling success and notes a strengthening nickel market, but no assays or production changes are provided. | CanAlaska’s partner has started a 10,000-metre phase-two drilling program at the Manibridge high-grade nickel project in Manitoba, targeting extensions near a past-producing mine. The program aims to define nickel-copper sulphide mineralization; earlier drilling intersected nickel mineralization and copper/nickel were confirmed with assays pending.

EV Nickel Phase 2 drilling returned 48.6 metres grading 1.31% nickel (from W4). Samples are submitted for further met testing. The reported assay results strengthen confidence in the project’s potential resource quality and prospective nickel supply, which is typically supportive for short-term nickel sentiment. | Fitch Solutions expects miners to increase investments in copper and nickel projects because demand is rising from renewable energy, batteries, and electric vehicles. While companies focus more on developing existing assets than launching new ones, the outlook supports constructive near-term sentiment for copper and nickel supply-demand fundamentals.

LG Energy Solution has begun building two nickel processing plants in Indonesia, including a smelter targeting 150,000 tonnes of nickel sulfate annually, plus precursor and cathode facilities. The investment also includes a joint nickel mine with Antam, supporting nickel supply-chain demand as Indonesia pushes downstream development. | Canada Nickel’s CEO says the Timmins area could become a major nickel district, building on the Crawford Project—described as the largest nickel sulphide discovery since the early 1970s. The company expects to advance a feasibility study, seek permitting, and potentially find additional deposits. The narrative supports nickel supply growth for EV demand. | SMM reports China produced 14,300 mt of refined nickel in May, up 10.59% month-on-month and 15.11% year-on-year. Output rose as smelters and some salt factories resumed or started production due to low LME nickel prices and attractive profits. June production is expected to increase further. | SMM reports Chinese nickel ore port inventories fell 188,000 wmt WoW to 4.869 million wmt as Ni content dropped to 38,300 mt. Seven major ports also saw declines. Although shipments from the Philippines resumed after rains eased, ship supply is tight and NPI plants drew down stocks, keeping inventories low. | China Moly denies the DRC has taken control of its Tenke Fungurume copper-cobalt mine. Congo authorities said a court-appointed administrator assumed charge amid a shareholder dispute tied to suspected reserve underreporting and royalty payments. China Moly says management and production continue normally as talks proceed.

LME aluminium slid to a six-month low as investors feared weaker Chinese demand from COVID lockdowns, higher inflation and interest-rate hikes, and a stronger U.S. dollar. Inventories are at low levels, but macro-driven demand concerns outweighed support. Copper and nickel also declined during the sell-off. | China’s nickel sulphate output rose in May as some producers restarted and tolling increased. Demand stayed weak, but raw material costs fell, easing nickel salt prices and improving plant margins. For June-July, recovery in precursor production should lift nickel sulphate prices from lows, though abundant feed supply may cap upside. | Manitou Gold announced discovery of a nickel-bearing ultramafic complex at its Goudreau project and reported diamond drilling results including a 0.25% Ni intersection over 48 m. It also notes ultramafic intrusions along a deformation zone analogous to Crawford/Dumont nickel deposits. The release is early exploration and not production-related. | Anteros Metals updates that Phase One VTEM airborne electromagnetic surveying is underway at its Hopedale nickel project in Labrador, aiming to detect deep conductivity anomalies tied to nickel mineralization and possibly Ni-Cu-Co sulphides. The company is awaiting results to guide future surface work and potential drilling, with no production impacts stated. | Lunnon Metals reported a maiden JORC mineral resource at the Baker Shoot target in its Kambalda nickel project: 568,000 tonnes at 2.8% nickel for 15,800t contained nickel. The discovery lifts the project’s total contained nickel to 64,300t and prompts further drilling, studies, and permitting.

SMM reports that spot premiums for pure nickel increased as the future price corrected sharply and spot supply tightened. Traders bought on dips when premiums were low, lifting pre-nickel premiums. Nickel briquette prices fell mainly because premiums narrowed, and demand/transactions were weaker versus mainstream nickel plate feedstocks.

The Reuters opinion says lawsuits over the LME nickel meltdown highlight disputes over the exchange’s handling of an “orderly market” during March 8’s trading halt and trade cancellations. It argues confidence may be damaged and liquidity may shift to OTC, pressuring nickel participation and sentiment soon. | SMM reports that after May’s Fed-driven rate hikes, SHFE nickel fell while LME liquidity remained thin and prices could stay volatile. Domestic policies and improving downstream operations boosted mid-May prices, but strong pure nickel supply and higher refined output in China should limit gains in June. Warrant scarcity warrants caution for shorts. | The LME cancelled roughly $4 billion of nickel trades in early March after a disorderly move following Russia’s invasion, prompting lawsuits and criticism of the clearing house’s decision-making. The piece also highlights margin behavior, lack of circuit breakers, and UK supervisory scrutiny—raising near-term nickel market uncertainty. | Russia’s invasion sparked a rapid nickel market disruption, sending LME prices up about 250% and leading the LME to suspend trading after a major short squeeze and margin calls. The piece also argues buyers may shun Russian nickel for EVs, increasing reliance on Chinese/Indonesian supply amid energy-transition demand growth. | EV Nickel Inc. announced a non-brokered private placement of up to C$2.0 million to fund Phase 3 exploration on its Langmuir and Shaw Dome projects in Ontario. The flow-through proceeds are earmarked for “Canadian exploration expenses” and mining expenditures, with no direct production or supply change disclosed. | Elliot Management filed a lawsuit against the LME in English High Court seeking $456 million after the exchange suspended nickel futures contracts and cancelled trades in early March. The case highlights controversy around LME handling of nickel contracts, which can contribute to near-term uncertainty and downside pressure for nickel pricing.

Fastmarkets reports China nickel sulfate prices falling and likely pressured by increased availability of feedstocks such as MHP and nickel matte, including higher imports and expanded Indonesian production. Weak domestic demand and some contract renegotiations are weighing on payables and supporting further downward risk, with LME nickel also declining.

SMM reports nickel prices falling sharply: SHFE nickel dropped for five days and spot refined #1 nickel fell about 8% since June 8 to 207,200 yuan/mt. The declines are linked to US Fed rate-hike concerns. Despite some premium strength from rare sources, demand remains slack and SMM expects nickel to stay rangebound at low levels. | SMM says Chinese nickel ore inventories fell 11,000 wmt week-on-week to 4.88 million wmt as of June 17. Ni content totals 38,000 wmt, with declines across seven major ports. The supply gap persists after Philippines weather disruptions, keeping nickel ore tight and supporting nickel demand and prices. | SMM reports SHFE nickel dropped below 200,000 yuan/mt despite a brief rebound after the Fed rate hike boosted sentiment. However, growth worries persisted and prices weakened. LEM and SHFE nickel fell on increased short selling, with demand for alloys and stainless steel still poor and supply expected to ease early next week. | Copper prices edged up as traders priced in strike risk at Chile’s Codelco, the world’s largest copper producer, alongside slightly better China activity data. LME copper rose 0.4% on the day, while aluminium weakened on a firmer dollar and softer China prospects; nickel rose modestly.

SMM reports nickel prices continued to fall, with spot supply easing from higher purchases and sufficient NPI supply. NPI prices weakened as steel mills pressured raw material costs. Nickel sulphate saw more long-term orders but slack spot trading. Sluggish downstream demand and Fed rate-hike macro pressure keep SHFE nickel rangebound with downward potential. | Ronbay’s chairman says nickel prices will drop rapidly as the battery supply chain stabilises, forecasting a nickel metal surplus above 400,000 mt in 2023. He expects nickel to fall from about $20,000/mt to below $15,000/mt next year. Battery mix and recycling could affect future costs. | Huayou Cobalt said it will raise up to 17.7 billion yuan to fund a nickel-cobalt hydroxide hydrometallurgy project in Indonesia, targeting 120,000 mt/year of nickel metal, along with a lithium salt project. The financing also includes working-capital replenishment, signaling expanded nickel processing capacity. | Huayou Cobalt plans a CNY17.7 billion private placement, allocating CNY12.2 billion to its Indonesia JV to build a nickel-cobalt hydroxide production base using nickel laterite ore. The expansion targets 326,000 tons of hydroxide products annually from 2025, reinforcing nickel supply prospects. | Gungnir Resources reported progress on drilling at its Lappvattnet high-grade nickel project in northern Sweden, completing eight holes and sending core for assay, with results expected in late July/early August. The company cites prior high-grade nickel intercepts and existing inferred nickel resources, but provides no new market-moving assay data.

SMM says nickel supply remained balanced to slightly surplus: Indonesia NPI imports grew and arrivals in China kept taking share from domestic NPI, while deliveries eased spot tightness. Downstream stainless demand is still weak and macro factors are bearish. The report expects near-term nickel prices to keep falling and remain volatile. | China’s ferronickel imports rose to 492,059 mt in May, up 17.6% month-on-month and 63.65% year-on-year, according to customs data. Indonesia supplied most volumes (463,718 mt), also up sharply. The larger inflow suggests increased nickel availability, potentially weighing on nickel prices in the near term. | SMM reports SHFE nickel rebounded sharply, and spot prices rose: Jinchuan nickel averaged 11,250 yuan/mt, up 5,400 yuan/mt, while premiums declined slightly as futures had surged. Nickel briquette prices fell on narrower premiums and weaker demand, but overall spot nickel levels increased. | Flying Nickel reports completion of its first drilling program at the 100% owned Minago nickel sulphide project in Manitoba, totaling 2,834 meters. Infill holes intersected disseminated nickel mineralization and metallurgical testing will support a feasibility study. Initial drill results are expected in early July.

Customs data show China’s May nickel ore imports rose MoM but remain below previous years. Full-year-to-date imports are still down YoY, and port nickel ore stocks are at historical lows. Philippine weather disruptions slowed mine shipments, and supply-demand is unlikely to build a surplus, keeping nickel ore—and nickel—relatively supported. | China’s May nickel ore and concentrate imports rose sharply, with Philippines shipments recovering but still below last year. Despite sluggish domestic stainless/NPI markets and mild declines in ore prices, SMM expects nickel ore prices to hold near current levels. Tight Chinese port inventories and later-than-expected Philippines rainy-season impacts sustain supply. | SMM reports nickel spot prices weakening on June 22, with Jinchuan and NORNICKEL quotes down by 250 yuan/mt and nickel briquette prices falling sharply as premiums narrowed. The decline is attributed to softer nickel futures, weaker premiums, and poor spot transaction activity, indicating near-term bearish sentiment for nickel. | SMM reports SHFE Ni 2207 traded lower on June 22, with volume and open interest falling. Pure nickel supply tightened slightly improved due to overseas plate arrivals, but demand remained weak and alloy transactions were poor. SHFE warrants hit a historical low, providing some support, yet macro factors point to a slight near-term decline in nickel prices. | Nickel prices on SHFE and LME continued to fall after June 9 amid negative macro factors including US Fed rate hikes and weak demand/transactions. Spot nickel weakened as premiums and futures eased. Although SHFE warrants inventories are at a historical low, improved pure nickel supply from overseas arrivals keeps pressure bearish in the short term.

INSG data reported by SMM shows the global nickel market deficit shrank dramatically to about 200 mt in April 2022 from 8,900 mt in March. The overall January–April deficit also remains sizable versus last year, but the sharp narrowing implies easing tightness and potential support for nickel sentiment. | SMM reports nickel prices correcting down since June 9, with SHFE nickel falling over 4% in a day to 190,010 yuan/mt and LME nickel around $25,000/mt. Deliverable-brand and imported nickel inflows eased supply tightness, while poor demand reduced premiums. With inventories at lows, support exists, but macro factors are expected to pressure short-term prices. | Global markets declined as investors feared further interest-rate hikes to fight decades-high inflation could push economies into recession. Germany’s and France’s recent Purchasing Managers’ Index data showed weaker activity, reinforcing slowdown concerns. This demand outlook weighed on risk assets and industrial commodities such as copper and likely other metals.

SMM reports that SHFE nickel and related spot prices declined further, citing weak macro sentiment and the arrival of overseas pure nickel. Jinchuan nickel averaged 10,500 yuan/mt (with spot plate correcting) while Nornickel was flat at 5,500 yuan/mt. Nickel briquette premiums narrowed and transactions weakened. | HKEX’s London Metal Exchange appointed Oliver Wyman to review nickel trading “chaos” following investor lawsuits. The announcement is directly tied to nickel market functioning and potential reforms, which can affect short-term trading expectations, but no clear supply/demand or production impacts are described in the provided text. | SMM says Chinese nickel ore port inventories rose by 283,000 wmt to 5.163 million wmt (Ni content up 2,000 mt). While imports from the Philippines are recovering, total inventories remain historically low due to earlier rainy-season import shortfalls. Near term inventories should rise gradually, but tight supply likely persists. | SMM says macro factors kept nickel prices falling in domestic and overseas markets, with profitable imports supporting activity. Bonded zone inventory for pure nickel rose to 7,700 mt, while nickel briquette and plate inventories fell WoW. SMM expects profitable spot imports to continue, bringing more overseas nickel plate into bonded warehouses next week.

SMM analyzes why nickel sulphate feedstock prices and nickel briquette demand persist despite falling nickel prices. It compares cost performance across raw materials (MHP, crude sulphate, black powder, high-grade nickel matte, briquette), discusses smelter operating rates and Indonesia imports, and notes capacity bottlenecks and inventory that may keep briquette usage stable into the second half.

SMM reports SHFE nickel rebounded after several days of declines, but overall demand remains weak with poor downstream spot transactions. Supply appears to be improving due to resumed refined nickel production and profitable overseas imports, while Indonesia NPI output may add pressure. The article expects longer-term nickel prices to keep falling.

SHFE nickel rebounded after a four-day losing streak, but refined nickel spot prices continued falling. The report cites ample supply of high-grade nickel matte and MHP, weaker demand from stainless steel and alloy sectors, and conversion of some lines to refined nickel, leading SMM to expect volatile nickel prices near term. | LME data shows nickel inventories have trended lower since end-May, falling again last week to 66,936 mt by June 28, a lowest level in more than two years. SHFE inventories also dropped, down 21.18% weekly to 2,021 mt on June 24, setting a new record low. | SMM reports that on June 28 Jinchuan nickel averaged 10,000 yuan/mt, down 1,500 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL averaged 5,850 yuan/mt, down 400 yuan/mt. Jinchuan premiums fell due to high futures prices and weak downstream buying. Nickel briquette premiums narrowed but trading remained poor.

LME copper stayed near a 16-month low as investors weighed hawkish central bank signals and potential rapid rate hikes that could hurt growth and demand. LME nickel futures rose after UK sanctions involving Norilsk Nickel’s major shareholder. Aluminium premiums for July–September were agreed lower, suggesting softer near-term aluminium pricing. | SMM reports SHFE nickel rising and spot refined nickel higher, while nickel premiums compress. The supply outlook improves as pure nickel capacity resumes and some imports may arrive, but demand remains muted with quiet buying in Wuxi/Foshan. Overall, SMM expects nickel to stay rangebound with limited further change. | SMM reports sufficient nickel intermediate supply from Indonesia (nickel matte and hydrometallurgy intermediates) with inventories rising slightly as consumption lags. Nickel sulphate cost is expected to fall due to supply surplus, and NPI prices are projected to keep declining, dragging stainless steel costs. Nickel prices are expected to decline short term. | Elliott Associates and Jane Street are suing the LME after it cancelled nickel trades on March 8 during chaotic spikes above $100,000/t. The LME cited systemic risk from large margin calls. The explainer details margin mechanics, Tsingshan’s cash constraints on a large nickel short, and potential default losses.

China Molybdenum plans to invest over $1.8 billion in the Kisanfu/KFM copper-cobalt project in the DRC, including spending on engineering and construction. The mine is expected to supply about 90,000 tons of cathode copper annually after full output, starting production in the first half of next year. | SMM reports Shanghai bonded-zone nickel inventory at about 7,700 mt, unchanged week-on-week. The price ratio tightened as overseas nickel prices fell while USD premiums rose in the bonded zone. Weak downstream demand for pure nickel and delays in overseas arrivals supported the flat inventory level. | SMM says Chinese port inventories of nickel ore fell 120,000 wmt week-on-week to 5.044 million wmt on July 1. Total Ni content dropped to 39,700 mt. Supply is described as tight and likely driven by NPI plant activity, keeping short-term inventories at low levels. | London copper dropped as much as 3.6% to around $7,959/ton, extending a bear market and marking the lowest level since early 2021. It fell nearly 20% in the quarter amid recession and growth fears, with similar weakness cited for aluminium and nickel.

Brazilian Nickel Plc announced first continuous production from its Piauí Nickel laterite heap-leach project, producing nickel hydroxide product (NHP). Initial output is set to ramp toward guidance of 300 t nickel in 2022 and 1,400 t nickel in 2023 (plus small cobalt volumes), marking the company’s entry as a producer. | CMOC says its KFM copper-cobalt mine in the DRC will start producing in 1H23, targeting average output of about 90,000 tpy copper metal (and 30,000 tpy cobalt) at full production. The company argues the additional cobalt supply helps EV demand, but near-term price effects are limited.

Lunnon Metals says drilling at its Warren nickel target in Western Australia returned stronger nickel mineralisation, including assays up to 5.28% Ni, plus smaller copper and cobalt grades. The results are expected to show Warren may host substantially more contained nickel than estimated, leading to a revised mineral estimate in coming quarters. | The title says Russian tycoon Potanin plans to begin merger talks between Nornickel and Rusal. Nornickel is closely tied to nickel output and Rusal to aluminium. While deal talks can affect market expectations for supply and corporate risk, the snippet lacks details, limiting clear direction in the near term. | The piece says Russian tycoon Potanin plans to begin merger talks between Nornickel and Rusal. These companies are major producers of nickel and aluminium, respectively, so consolidation could affect expectations for supply. But without details on timing or capacity changes, the short-term price direction is unclear. | Vladimir Potanin, largest shareholder in Nornickel, said he is ready to discuss a potential merger with aluminium producer Rusal. While Nornickel has not been directly targeted by sanctions, Britain recently sanctioned Potanin, and Potanin cited diversification and possible state support as motives for talks, Reuters reported. | Vladimir Potanin, largest shareholder in Nornickel, said he is ready to discuss merging Nornickel with aluminium producer Rusal. The idea is driven by shareholder diversification to withstand sanctions; Britain recently sanctioned Potanin. The proposal comes from Rusal’s management, with no immediate response from Rusal. | SMM reports refined nickel spot prices rising, with higher Jinchuan and NORNICKEL premiums due to scarce Jinchuan supply in Shanghai and weaker expectations of sustained US rate hikes. However, NPI transaction prices fall, NPI plants face losses, and alloy demand remains weak amid Wuxi pandemic disruption, keeping nickel rangebound. | UK sanctions expanded to include Vladimir Potanin and cover entities linked to Norilsk Nickel. The CEO says the company’s stability should not be harmed, but it may need to repay some loans early due to exposure to UK banks. He also says supplies will be redirected without leaving core markets. | Vladimir Potanin said Norilsk Nickel is ready to begin talks to merge with Rusal, citing shareholder diversification, sustainability/green agenda, and increased resistance to sanctions. Norilsk produces nickel and copper, while Rusal produces aluminium with low-carbon power. The update is only about initiating merger discussions. | Copper prices plunged below $8,000/ton on the London Metal Exchange to a 17-month low, down about 2.3% Tuesday and nearly 20% in the prior quarter. The drop is attributed to global recession fears and rate hikes. Aluminum and nickel also declined amid the broader metals rout.

NickelX completed a close-spaced magnetic survey at its Cosmos South nickel project in Western Australia and is processing the data to refine subsurface lithology and targets. It has commenced permitting for 1,500m diamond drilling at CS1, with results expected in coming weeks and support from a state grant. | Satellite surveillance indicated global copper smelting activity fell in June, driven by a maintenance shutdown at Codelco’s Ventanas smelter/refinery and reduced output at other Chilean sites, plus weaker activity in China and Europe. Nickel smelting also declined due to downtime at major smelters in China, South Africa and Canada. | Satellite surveillance shows global copper and nickel smelting activity declined in June. Copper was hit by Codelco’s Ventanas smelter and refinery shutdown for maintenance and below-capacity operation at other Chile smelters, plus weak activity in China and Europe. Nickel downtime at key smelters including Jinchuan and Sudbury reduced output. | SMM reports SHFE nickel declining further, with refined nickel prices falling on the day. The weakness is linked to increased pure nickel supply, profitable imports, and oversupplied nickel pig iron pressured by weak demand. Alloy purchases also missed expectations as pandemic resurgence caused steel market operations to suspend.

SMM reports rising Indonesia nickel sulphate supply via new high-grade nickel matte and hydromet intermediate capacity, while China demand for intermediates remains below incoming volumes, risking stock accumulation. With intermediate operating rates near full and NPI prices falling, the article expects nickel sulphate costs and overall nickel prices to trend lower. | Canada Nickel Co. updated the mineral resource estimate for its 100% owned Crawford nickel sulphide project in Ontario. Total measured and indicated resources more than doubled to 1.43 billion tonnes at 0.24% nickel, with contained nickel rising to 3.48 million tonnes. Company says the feasibility study remains on track. | Copper prices jumped 4.5% to $7,858/t after Bloomberg reported China is considering $220bn in stimulus, helping offset bets on lower prices and forcing position buybacks. Analysts still warn rising rates and recession risks could push copper further down, with aluminium higher too. | The article headline says an EV nickel-related company has closed an oversubscribed private placement raising $2.2M. While details are limited, the implication is stronger investor demand and potential acceleration of activities linked to nickel for EV applications, which can provide modest near-term support to nickel prices.

Commodities rebounded after reports China may let local governments issue 1.5 trillion yuan of special bonds to support growth. The rally lifted copper by about 4.5% to around $7,860/t. The stimulus narrative typically boosts near-term industrial demand expectations, supporting base metals. | Benchmark LME copper fell about 1.7% and was set for a fifth straight weekly loss as fresh China COVID-19 restrictions raised fears of slower infrastructure spending and weaker metal demand. The piece also notes declines in LME aluminium and nickel, reflecting broader recession/risk-off sentiment toward industrial metals. | SMM reports Shanghai bonded zone nickel inventory at 7,600 mt, slightly lower week-on-week, including decreases in nickel briquette and nickel plate stocks. The drop is attributed to poor downstream demand and weaker-than-expected port arrivals, as the nickel price ratio narrowed due to lower overseas nickel prices and higher US dollar premiums. | SMM said Chinese nickel ore inventories rose 227,000 wmt to 5.271 million wmt as Indonesian NPI arrivals and weak stainless-steel demand pressured NPI production, cutting nickel ore consumption. While earlier poor weather delayed shipments, arrivals haven’t yet caught up fully; nickel ore inventories may keep rising in coming weeks.

Copper prices fell as new COVID-19 restrictions in China and worries about faster Fed rate hikes heightened fears of slower global growth and weaker metals demand. LME three-month copper slid 1.3% and Shanghai August copper fell 2%. Aluminium also declined, reflecting a broader risk-off demand outlook; nickel was mixed. | SMM says China refined nickel output rose to 15,500 mt in June (+8.74% MoM) and is expected to reach about 15,600 mt in July, with further increases as salt-plant lines are commissioned. Demand improvements support some orders, but the overall near-term supply outlook is larger. | The article title says EV-related nickel Phase 2 drilling at the “W4 Extension” intersection returned 5.4 metres grading 2.96% nickel. Positive assay results can lift market expectations for future nickel supply, providing a modest near-term support to nickel prices, though details are limited. | Finland’s Helsinki Shipyard is waiting for an export construction license for Norilsk Nickel’s first icebreaker because of sanctions against Russia. The pending license is delaying construction and pushing key schedule milestones back. The vessel is intended to secure shipping access to Dudinka and support Norilsk Arctic logistics.

Canada Nickel says its Crawford Nickel Sulphide Project’s updated resource estimate has doubled, now indicating 1.4 billion tonnes at 0.24% nickel plus additional inferred resources. The feasibility study remains on track for year-end, and the company expects potential for further regional discoveries, targeting future nickel supply. | SMM reports China’s nickel sulphate output rose in June and is expected to increase further in July, supported by higher self-production/toll manufacturing and recovery in downstream demand. Despite early-July demand improvement, market supply is projected to grow, reinforcing the recent decline in nickel sulphate and nickel prices. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. released a positive preliminary economic assessment for its Kenbridge underground nickel project, projecting 9 years of production centered on nickel with copper as a byproduct. The PEA relies on forecast metal prices and contains project-economics metrics, but it does not indicate immediate supply disruption or large inventory changes.

SMM reports that NORNICKEL nickel premiums on the spot market fell to an average of 13,500 yuan/mt, down 500 yuan/mt day-on-day. The decline is linked to customs clearance of some supply and a recovery in the SHFE/LME price ratio. Nickel briquette prices also eased as premiums narrowed. | Copper prices fell to a 20-month low as economic slowdown fears intensified. A stronger U.S. dollar, renewed COVID-19 curbs in China, and expectations of aggressive U.S. rate hikes ahead of inflation data reduced demand outlook. China’s growth cuts and market caution before China data reinforced selling pressure. Aluminium also fell; nickel was mixed. | The US Dollar Index hit a new high overnight, and most nonferrous prices fell early, but SHFE nickel (Ni 2208) rebounded and closed up about 0.9%. Spot imports were profitable, increasing overseas pure nickel supply, while low inventories and limited stainless demand keep nickel volatile. | Manitou Gold reports finding awaruite (nickel-iron alloy) in drill core at its Goudreau project in Ontario, with 0.25% Ni over 48 m, and identifies additional ultramafic structures with nickel potential. The update is exploratory, without production or supply changes. | Talon Metals released assay results from the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project, reporting thick mineralized intervals with high nickel and copper grades in the CGO East area. The drilling suggests potential resource expansion outside the current resource boundary, but no near-term production or supply disruption is announced. | Indonesia’s commerce leadership says the country will leverage its abundant nickel reserves to attract investment in domestic supply chains for the “green energy” transition. It specifically targets nickel use in ternary lithium batteries for EVs, following recent high-profile progress on local electric vehicle launches. | South32 agreed to sell a portfolio of royalties over advanced development-stage copper and nickel projects to Anglo Pacific for $185m plus up to $15m contingent payments. Royalties include NSR and realised value stakes across projects in Australia, Chile, and the US, with quarterly cash follow-ons and share issuance. | South32 agreed to sell a $185m copper and nickel royalty portfolio to Anglo Pacific, including royalties on producing Carlota copper and Nifty copper and advanced-stage copper/nickel projects in Australia and Chile. Payments include cash, share issuance, and quarterly contingent payments. The transaction shifts ownership of royalties, not physical supply.

SMM reports non-ferrous markets under pressure after Fed tightening but notes some demand recovery from downstream alloys and batteries as pure nickel spot prices fell. It forecasts higher refined nickel supply in July as salt factories normalize output, while domestic demand remains weak, leading to relatively sufficient supply and likely further LME nickel weakness. | SMM reviews nickel futures, noting a June downward trend amid macro pressure and Fed rate hikes. Despite this, nickel is relatively resilient due to low inventories. Demand for pure nickel is mixed: military/aviation improved while civilian alloy demand weakened. Upcoming Indonesia NPI/intermediate ramp-ups may lead to future surplus and long-run pressure. | Horizonte Minerals said it has purchased US$135mn in equipment for its Brazil nickel project. The spend is directly related to developing future nickel output, but it is mainly a longer-dated capex milestone rather than an immediate production or supply disruption, so near-term nickel pricing impact is likely limited. | Archer Exploration agreed to acquire Wallbridge Mining’s nickel assets in Quebec and Ontario, including a 100% stake in the Grasset nickel sulphide project, for about C$53.6m in an all-share deal. Wallbridge will focus on its Detour-Fenelon gold properties, while Archer builds a nickel-focused exploration platform.

Class 1 Nickel reports results from a 32.85 line-km ground TDEM survey in Quebec, identifying 10 new EM anomalies near the historical Marbridge nickel mine and indicating potential continuity of nickel sulphide mineralization. The company is reviewing results for a diamond drill program; prior ore also included trace copper. | London and Shanghai copper prices are set for their biggest weekly loss in over two years, pressured by a stronger US dollar and weak demand outlook tied to slowing growth and imminent rate hikes. China’s COVID-hit activity also weighs. Nickel sharply falls too; aluminium edges up slightly. | SMM reports that spot pure nickel premiums were largely unchanged despite a significant drop in nickel futures. Jinchuan nickel premiums averaged 21,000 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL averaged 13,500 yuan/mt. Nickel briquette prices fell 12,500 yuan/mt, mainly as premiums narrowed and demand weakened versus nickel plate. | SMM says nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 61,000 wmt WoW to 5.21 million wmt (41,000 mt Ni content). Inventories were historically low, though Lianyungang rose on local pandemic impact and others fell on restocking rhythm. With weak downstream demand and falling ore prices, imports are unlikely to rise; inventories may stay rangebound. | Bloomberg reports the Tsingshan owner behind the March nickel squeeze limited his losses, helped by LME trading suspensions and canceled transactions. However, the article says the squeeze’s fallout persists: damaged LME reputation, lawsuits, lower open interest, thin liquidity, and nickel contract volumes cut roughly in half as nickel prices fall to a six-month low.

SMM reports alumina prices diverging across China as ocean FOB Western Australia prices fall on abundant supply, while north China gets partial refinery recoveries but bauxite costs constrain output. South-west prices weaken with new capacity releases. Record aluminium smelter operating rates suggest only a slight alumina surplus, limiting sharp aluminium moves. | SMM assesses nickel market conditions in Shanghai, noting steady spot import profitability despite high bonded-zone premiums and improved SHFE/LME ratio. NPI producers raise quotes but reduce output due to high earlier ore costs. Demand weakens with steel mills’ production cuts and off-season, though some special alloy and battery buying increases. Overall, prices should not rise sharply. | SMM’s report attributes nickel price volatility to extreme earlier moves and highlights ongoing weakness. It expects pure nickel to be oversupplied in H2 and also anticipates oversupply in nickel sulphate and related precursor segments. Higher Indonesia NPI output and weaker demand signals are likely to pressure nickel prices lower. | Copper fell 7.8% over the week to $3.2 per pound, its worst rate since November two years ago. Nickel declined 11% and aluminium dropped 3% (zinc also down). The move is linked to recession fears and wider commodity weakness, alongside European gas supply concerns from Gazprom. | Copper jumped 3.3% on LME after investors covered shorts following Chinese government support for the property sector and signs of monetary easing, helped by a weaker dollar. CMOC’s Tenke Fungurume suspended exports. Aluminium rose 3.8% and nickel gained 6.5%, reflecting broad industrial metals strength.

Culpeo Minerals says assays from the seventh drillhole at its Lana Corina copper project in Chile extend high-grade copper mineralisation by 100 metres to depth and shows strong copper (>2%) and molybdenum intersections. Drilling continues on nearby breccia pipes. The update is exploration-focused, with no immediate production impact. | On July 19, SMM reported nickel futures rebounded and spot nickel prices rose sharply. Jinchuan nickel premiums increased by about 500 yuan/mt to an average 21,000 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL averaged 12,650 yuan/mt. Nickel briquette prices jumped mainly due to premium narrowing, though deal activity was weaker. | SMM reports nickel fundamentals are weak as demand sentiment is muted despite improved supply conditions in parts of China. Premiums for Jinchuan nickel are slipping, NPI plants hold firm amid losses and thin transactions, and market expectations point to continued near-term spot price declines for nickel and NPI-related products. | Power Nickel released an initial NI 43-101 Mineral Resource Estimate for its Nisk nickel sulphide project near James Bay, reporting indicated and inferred tonnes based on nickel-equivalent grades. The company’s mineralization is described as nickel-copper PGE style, but the release does not imply near-term production changes.

Vale cut its 2022 iron ore output forecast citing lower market prices, but also lowered its copper production estimate and reported weaker nickel performance. Nickel output fell 16.1% in the quarter, while copper production dropped 23.9% year-on-year. Analysts expect weaker results to weigh on base metals sentiment. | Canada Nickel reports lab test results for accelerated CO2 capture at its Crawford nickel-cobalt project using in-process tailings carbonation. The company claims near-term net-zero emissions in 36 hours at lab scale and significant CO2 credit potential. It may improve future nickel project economics, but no immediate production impact is stated. | Volkswagen plans to build a nickel ore production and processing plant in Indonesia this year, with sites in Batang regency and Sulawesi. The project is still under negotiation, including incentives and reserve capacity. The news highlights Indonesia’s large nickel resources and rising nickel exports, supporting nickel demand sentiment. | Russia’s Nornickel increased deliveries of nickel matte to its Finnish unit and plans to raise production to 100,000 tonnes. The report cites a 72% rise in April export value and ongoing plant operations, while noting logistics and potential further sanctions risks. | Volkswagen said it will build its first nickel ore production and processing plant in Indonesia this year, in Batang regency. The minister noted negotiations are ongoing regarding investment value, incentives, and nickel ore reserve capacity. The move signals incremental Indonesian refining demand for nickel.

SMM says SHFE nickel spot markets stayed strong on July 21. Jinchuan nickel carried premiums of 16,000–16,500 yuan/mt versus NORNICKEL at 12,500–13,000, both up from the prior day. Nickel briquette prices also rose with futures, though nickel-sulphate cost efficiency was poorer and deal activity remained muted. | Volkswagen said it will build its first nickel ore production and processing plant in Indonesia this year. Indonesian officials cited large local nickel reserves and rising exports, with VW negotiating investment value and incentives. The news points to additional downstream processing activity, supporting near-term nickel demand expectations. | Vale Indonesia, Huayou and Ford signed a non-binding memorandum to build an Indonesia plant producing 120,000 tonnes per annum of nickel chemicals for EV batteries. The project builds on prior Huayou/Vale framework rights and comes amid Indonesia’s push to process nickel domestically after the unprocessed ore export ban.

China’s nickel ore imports totaled 4.31 million mt in June, up MoM but down YoY, with weak first-half demand. Philippine laterite imports were constrained by negative sentiment as NPI and nickel ore prices fell through transmission from a poor stainless steel market. Stainless output cuts further reduced ore demand into July-August. | BASF and TODA will expand their Japanese joint venture’s capacity for high-nickel cathode active materials at Onoda. BTBM’s capacity targets up to 60,000 tons by 2025, supported by subsidies under a supply-chain program. The project responds to fast-growing lithium-ion battery demand, boosting expected nickel consumption. | Canada Nickel Company’s Crawford nickel sulphide project in Ontario is progressing with an ongoing feasibility study and defined open-pit mining and processing plans, targeting long-term nickel supply (and other by-products). The report does not indicate imminent production or disruptions, so near-term effects on nickel pricing appear limited. | SMM said Chinese nickel ore port inventories rose 375,000 wmt WoW to 5.585 million wmt as of July 22, with Ni content at 43,900 wmt. Shipments are subdued in peak season due to weaker ore/NPI profits and lowered buying intentions, but inventories are still building. | SMM reports bonded-zone nickel inventory fell to about 6,500 mt this week, down 1,300 mt WoW, with declines in nickel briquette and nickel plate. The drop is linked to lower nickel futures, improved domestic alloy demand for overseas pure nickel, and a recovered SHFE/LME nickel price ratio.

SMM reports nickel prices fell on hotter-than-expected US June inflation, raising fears of a 100 bps Fed hike, then rebounded as officials signaled a possible 75 bps hike. Spot premiums and SHFE/LME ratio improved, aiding overseas clearance optimism. Demand remained mixed, leaving spot transactions poor last week. | SMM reports on July 25 that Jinchuan nickel spot premiums fell to 13,500–14,200 yuan/mt as some pure nickel spot supply arrived. NORNICKEL premiums also declined. Nickel briquette trading was slack because futures rose, lowering briquette cost efficiency, and alloy demand remains tied to nickel plate.

NGOs sent an open letter urging Elon Musk not to invest in Indonesia’s nickel sector due to environmental concerns about mining waste and pollution. The note follows Musk’s meetings with President Jokowi and highlights Indonesia’s export ban on unprocessed nickel ore. Tesla’s response was not provided. | Copper prices rose as supply risks from major producers outweighed demand worries. MMG suspended copper production targets after a 60% output drop at Peru’s Las Bambas mine, while Vale and Antofagasta cut 2022 outlooks. A softer U.S. dollar also boosted prices. Nickel advanced alongside copper. | SMM reports that spot nickel premiums fell further on July 26 as nickel futures remained high. Jinchuan nickel was priced lower and NORNICKEL premiums narrowed, reducing transaction volumes. Nickel briquette prices rose modestly, but trading was weak because briquette economics were worse than nickel plates, the preferred alloy feedstock. | NGOs sent an open letter to Elon Musk urging Tesla not to invest in Indonesia’s nickel industry, citing environmental damage from mining waste and deforestation. The proposal follows President Jokowi’s meeting with Musk about potential investment. Environmental criticism and potential investor pullback add downside risk to Indonesian nickel supply expectations. | Marvel Discovery says it has mobilized field crews to the Duhamel Ni-Cu-Co (and Ti-V-Cr) property in Quebec to confirm historical drill holes and conduct prospecting and surveying for new nickel-copper sulphide targets. The update is exploration-focused with no indicated production or supply changes, limiting near-term price impact. | Environmental groups are asking Tesla to halt its nickel plans in Indonesia. The request raises the risk of project delays or cancellation, which could undermine short-term assumptions about nickel supply, increasing perceived availability tightness and likely weighing on nickel prices in the coming days. | EV Nickel commissioned Blue Heron Environmental to conduct quarterly surface water baseline monitoring around the Shaw Dome Langmuir W4 Zone to support hydrology/water quality documentation for permitting. The work is scheduled to start immediately and helps advance the project, but it does not indicate near-term nickel production changes. | Gungnir Resources reported intercepting new ultramafic jack-straw peridotite about 150 metres north of the Lappvattnet nickel deposit in two 2022 drill holes, with minor sulphides and assays pending. The company plans further extensions and additional drilling nearby to test EM anomalies. No price-driving assay or production updates were provided. | Gungnir Resources announced drilling that intersected a new ultramafic (jack-straw peridotite) horizon about 150 metres north of the Lappvattnet nickel deposit in two holes, with assays pending and additional holes planned. The update reinforces exploration potential for nickel mineralization, but near-term supply impacts appear limited.

LME nickel stocks have been trending down since late April, with inventories falling further and reaching 60,168 mt as of July 25, near multi-year lows. SHFE nickel inventories also fell for six straight weeks, down 18.24% week-on-week to 1,493 mt as of July 22, a record low. | Indonesia’s official Eddy Soeparno said the country should restrict exports of BOP and FeNi nickel derivatives with nickel content below 70% to boost domestic downstream processing. The proposal follows Indonesia’s large nickel ore position and active RKEF smelters exporting NPI/FeNi/nickel matte, implying tighter export supply could lift nickel sentiment. | SMM reports that refined nickel spot premiums fell by late July: Jinchuan premiums averaged 8,750 yuan/mt (-2,750 yuan/mt) and Russian nickel averaged 7,750 yuan/mt (-2,500 yuan/mt). Nickel briquette prices also declined as traders reduced premiums for delivery and nickel futures weakened. | After seven days of gains, SHFE nickel fell sharply (intra-day down 2.59%, closing down 1.24%), while LME nickel also dropped. Spot refined nickel prices declined markedly and premiums narrowed as sellers lowered offers. Supply rose, downstream demand stayed soft, and weak macro sentiment ahead of the Fed meeting weighed on nickel. | Aztec Minerals began a 10-hole, 3,000m core drilling program at its Cervantes gold-copper project in Sonora, Mexico to expand and test oxide cap and deeper copper-gold targets. While it highlights copper-bearing intercepts, it does not change near-term production, so price impact is likely limited. | Automakers’ ESG scrutiny is increasing for nickel used in EV batteries, with NGOs urging Tesla to halt Indonesian nickel and questioning Russia-linked nickel after Ukraine’s invasion. The piece notes prior ESG controversies in key supply regions and suggests procurement may shift, potentially tightening usable nickel supply for near-term markets.

Norilsk Nickel reported 1H 2022 output: nickel nearly 100 kt (+26% y/y) but down 6% q/q to 48 kt due to Harjavalta scheduled repairs, while copper rose to 204 kt (+18% y/y) and to 113 kt in Q2 (+23% q/q) due to planned furnace maintenance. Guidance from Russian feed reiterated.

Copper prices rose Tuesday, supported by a weaker dollar and supply risk from Peru. Three-month LME copper gained 0.8% and Shanghai September copper rose 1.3% amid ongoing demand worries. The article also reports gains in LME aluminium and ShFE nickel, suggesting broadly positive metal sentiment. | SMM July 29 said pure nickel premiums continued to fall in the spot market. Jinchuan nickel averaged 6,250 yuan/mt (down 1,400 yuan) and NORNICKEL averaged 4,150 yuan/mt (down 1,450). Traders reduced premiums for end-of-month shipments, while ample overseas supply pressured domestic pure nickel. | Copper is set for a fourth straight monthly decline as recession fears, a weaker demand outlook in China, and an elevated U.S. dollar weigh on prices. Copper is down about 6% this month. Nickel production at Nornickel fell QoQ. LME aluminum is up modestly, indicating weaker bearish spillover to aluminium. | SMM reports bonded-zone nickel inventory at 6,300 mt this week, with nickel briquette down 200 mt WoW to 2,700 mt and nickel plate at 3,600 mt. Clearance of overseas pure nickel was lower due to weak downstream demand, though briquette demand improved slightly from ternary batteries and some salt plants. | SMM says Chinese port inventories of nickel ore rose 774,000 wmt week-on-week to 6.359 million wmt as of July 29. Domestic NPI demand is sluggish, keeping nickel ore demand weak and driving downward pressure on ore prices. High freight squeezes mine margins and shipping, and inventories are expected to rise slowly short term.

SMM Aug 1 noted that Jinchuan nickel spot premiums were 12,000–13,000 yuan/mt and Nornickel premiums 11,500–12,000 yuan/mt, but spot trading was slack after the contract roll to SHFE 2209. High futures prices hurt nickel briquette cost efficiency and demand, with alloy production still reliant on nickel plate. | Blackstone Minerals says it is speeding development of its Ta Khoa “green nickel” battery-metals processing refinery in Vietnam, progressing feasibility work, pilot testing, permitting, and partner/offtake negotiations. The company plans to start production in about three years, using flexible refinery feedstock options from its nickel operations. | Koba Resources completed induced polarisation (IP) surveys over its historic Blackpine cobalt-copper project ahead of maiden drilling in September, following an earlier maiden program at the nearby Colson project. The work targets extensions of copper-cobalt mineralisation, but no production changes are reported.

SMM reports SHFE nickel and spot refined nickel prices fell sharply, with spot 1# refined nickel down to about 186,300 yuan/mt. Reasons include weak alloy-sector demand, flat trades, and bearish supply dynamics from increased Indonesian NPI. Low domestic and overseas inventories offer some support, but not to offset the downturn. | SHFE and LME nickel prices fell sharply before partially rebounding (SHFE down 3.27% at one point). SMM cites lower-than-expected customs clearance, expanded overseas pure nickel profits, and increased Indonesian NPI shipments that raise NPI supply beyond demand. Pure nickel demand remains weak, though low inventories may offer some support. | Nornickel reports 1H2022 IFRS results, stating revenue was supported by higher nickel and copper prices and production volumes after earlier operational suspensions at mines and a concentrator. It also highlights logistical disruptions and inventory build-up. The company describes a nickel market in surplus overall, implying limited near-term directional price pressure. | Norilsk Nickel’s interim results note that nickel and copper prices and production volumes moved during 1H 2022, alongside logistical disruptions and macro factors. The company describes nickel price volatility, market surplus dynamics, and copper’s volatility tied to geopolitics, recession fears, and a stronger dollar. Aluminium impacts are not indicated. | Nornickel reports 1H 2022 results, noting higher nickel and copper prices and recovered volumes after prior mine/smelter disruptions, offset by logistical disruptions and inventory build. It describes nickel market surplus dynamics and copper price volatility, but provides no new operational or trade catalyst that would clearly shift near-term metal prices. | Nornickel reported 1H2022 IFRS results, noting production recovery after temporary suspensions at its Oktyabrsky and Taimyrsky mines and Norilsk concentrator, but also logistical disruptions leading to lower metal sales and higher inventories. The company discusses nickel market surplus/shortage mix and copper price volatility, reflecting near-term market conditions.

BHP’s Nickel West chief said the company will sharply boost nickel exploration spending over the next two years to supply expected EV battery demand. BHP links growth to nickel’s role in EV chemistries and notes expanding exploration activity and existing supply deals with major automakers. | LME nickel inventories have trended down since late April, falling further last week to 57,978 mt as of August 1, near multi-year lows. SHFE nickel inventories rebounded 16.61% weekly to 1,741 mt after six consecutive weeks of declines, though remaining historically low, supporting tight supply sentiment. | SMM reports that spot nickel prices fell day-on-day, with Jinchuan nickel averaging 8,750 yuan/mt and NORNICKEL averaging 8,150 yuan/mt. Premiums of pure nickel continued to decline, while high futures prices kept buyers wait-and-see. Spot transactions were poor, including for nickel briquettes due to narrower premiums. | Copper prices eased as U.S.-China tensions over Pelosi’s Taiwan visit raised fears of weaker industrial demand. Markets also discounted the prospect of more Fed rate hikes and recession risks. LME three-month copper fell 1.6%, with aluminium down 1.3% and nickel down 0.8%, reflecting broad metals pressure.

SMM discusses nickel market drivers, citing capacity bottlenecks and oversupply that have pushed MHP, NPI, and high-grade nickel matte prices lower. It expects primary nickel supply to keep increasing via imports and ongoing NPI flow into China, with near-term nickel prices rangebound but downside risks remaining for NPI and primary nickel. | SMM reports that spot premiums for pure nickel fell in Shanghai, with Jinchuan premiums dropping and NORNICKEL premiums also declining. The decreases are attributed to lower Jinchuan ex-factory prices, scarce NORNICKEL trades, and weak demand. Nickel briquette prices also slid, with limited transactions due to poorer cost efficiency. | CanAlaska announced winter 2022 drill assay results at its Manibridge nickel project in Manitoba, reporting high-grade nickel-sulphide mineralization in all drill holes and continuity beneath the former mine. The release also notes the mineralization contains copper and cobalt, but provides no production or inventory updates. | Atlantic Nickel reported record 1H22 performance from the Santa Rita nickel sulphide mine in Brazil, including concentrate production and cost figures, and stated it has hedged nickel and copper prices. The update is primarily company-specific without evidence of near-term supply disruption or major demand/policy changes. | Nickel 28 reported Q2 2022 operating performance from its Ramu (Papua New Guinea) nickel-cobalt venture, including 8,128 tonnes of contained nickel and 6,624 tonnes of nickel sales. The company links volatility to LME nickel trading suspension and conflict, while citing stable production and improving demand.

SMM reports nickel premiums further narrowing as pure nickel spot demand weakens. Jinchuan nickel and Nornickel spot quotes fell versus the prior day. The decline is attributed mainly to high nickel futures prices and weak demand, leading to poor spot trading activity. | Atlantic Nickel and Appian report record 1H22 performance from the Santa Rita nickel sulfide mine in Brazil, including 8,000 t of nickel contained in concentrate, low C1 cash costs of about $4.95/lb net of by-products, and debt-free positioning. The results suggest supportive near-term nickel supply outlook. | Green River Gold updates step-out drilling at its Quesnel nickel/magnesium/talc project, reporting the highest nickel readings to date from new XRF-analyzed drill locations, including grades over 0.2%. Drilling continues toward new targets, and assay results are pending. The news is supportive for nickel exploration sentiment. | Murchison Minerals says it has commenced Phase III diamond drilling at its HPM Quebec nickel-copper-cobalt project, targeting expansion and delineation at the Barre de Fer zone and the nearby Syrah target. The company plans ~7,500 m and references assay grades, but no production or supply changes are announced. | China’s nickel ore port inventories rose 20,000 wmt WoW to 6.32 million wmt (about 50,000 mt Ni content). Mine shipments were poor as ore prices fell and high ocean freight squeezed margins, leading to production cuts. Oversupplied NPI kept downstream pricing rangebound, pressuring ore prices.

SMM reports nickel ore (Ni 1.5% CIF) staying on a downward trend as mines resist sales, low-grade nickel ore nears cost, and plants reduce output. Weak stainless steel prices and frosty ferrochrome demand limit buying. Meanwhile, Indonesian NPI supply returning to China keeps an NPI surplus, keeping downward pressure on nickel-related prices. | China’s MIIT released H1 2022 operations for the nickel-cobalt-lithium industries. Nickel output and demand drivers from new energy stayed in growth, but nickel concentrate imports fell 8.3% to 15.1 million mt and refined nickel imports fell 3.7% to 83,000 mt year-on-year. | SMM says China produced 16,000 mt of refined nickel in May, and July output increased 2.89% MoM. The rise was driven by higher alloy military orders and pure nickel demand after low SHFE prices, but weaker steel-mill shipments reduced demand. Some salt plants curtailed production, though output still grew. August output is forecast slightly higher. | Indonesia’s minister said Tesla has signed a five-year deal worth $5 billion to buy nickel products processed in Morowali, Sulawesi, for use in lithium batteries. The announcement adds visible end-demand alongside Indonesia’s prior nickel-ore export halt, likely supporting nickel prices near term. | Indonesia says Tesla struck a $5 billion deal to buy nickel products. The announcement points to stronger nickel demand associated with Tesla’s supply chain, which can lift price expectations for nickel in the coming days as markets react to potential tightening in availability. | Indonesia’s senior minister said Tesla signed about $5 billion of contracts to buy battery materials from nickel processing companies in Morowali, Sulawesi, under a five-year deal. The news points to sustained demand for Indonesian processed nickel. Indonesia’s policies favor domestic supply, which can support nickel prices near term. | Australian shares edged higher as copper-linked stocks rose following BHP’s multibillion-dollar bid for South Australian copper and nickel miner Oz Minerals. The move boosted sentiment toward copper and nickel supply expectations, helping lift related equities, which can transmit to near-term metal price expectations. | Canada Nickel said the federal Impact Assessment permitting process for its Crawford Nickel Project has started after acceptance of the Initial Project Description and publication for a 180-day comment period. The company will respond to a Summary of Issues and advance subsequent documentation. The release does not indicate immediate production changes. | Tesla signed a $5 billion, five-year contract to buy nickel products from two Indonesian suppliers in Morowali, Central Sulawesi, for use in lithium batteries. Indonesia’s government is simultaneously pushing Tesla investment in its EV battery supply chain, highlighting strong expected nickel offtake. | Tesla signed a five-year $5B contract to buy nickel products from two Indonesian producers in Morowali for use in lithium batteries. Indonesia is a major nickel exporter and is seeking deeper EV/battery investment. The deal strengthens visible nickel demand, likely providing bullish support to nickel prices. | Indonesia’s cabinet minister said Tesla signed about $5 billion in contracts to buy materials for batteries from nickel processing companies in Indonesia. The deal signals increased near-term nickel offtake for EV supply chains, likely tightening expectations for nickel demand and supporting price sentiment over coming weeks. | Indonesia said Tesla has signed about US$5 billion in five-year contracts to buy nickel battery materials from processing firms in Morowali, Sulawesi. The contracts cover two nickel products used in lithium batteries. The news adds expected demand momentum for processed nickel amid Indonesia’s export restrictions and planned export taxes. | Tesla has signed an estimated $5 billion, five-year nickel purchasing deal with Indonesian firms, including plants in Indonesia’s Sulawesi nickel belt. The contracts strengthen expectations of near-term nickel demand for EV batteries. Environmental groups criticize Indonesia’s nickel pollution, but the announcement itself is supportive for nickel prices.

Indonesia’s senior minister said Tesla has signed contracts worth about $5 billion to buy battery-related materials from nickel processing companies in the country. Indonesia has been courting Tesla to invest, citing its major nickel reserves. The deals imply strong incremental nickel demand tied to battery production needs. | Indonesia’s senior cabinet minister said Tesla agreed to roughly $5 billion in five-year contracts to buy nickel battery materials from processing firms in Morowali, Sulawesi. The nickel feedstock will be used in Tesla’s lithium batteries, reinforcing near-term demand expectations for nickel. | Great Atlantic Resources reports drill results from its Mascarene Property in New Brunswick, including a 0.40 m intercept grading 6.74% copper (with gold and silver) and other copper-bearing sulfide veins. It also notes copper-nickel-cobalt occurrence context and anomalous nickel/cobalt, but no production impact. | Great Atlantic Resources reports new drill core results from its Mascarene Property in New Brunswick, including a 0.40m intercept grading 6.74% copper and anomalous nickel (plus cobalt) and gold/silver. The findings support copper-bearing veins, but the update is early exploration without production implications for metal prices. | SMM reports China’s nickel sulphate output rose in July, with battery-grade production increasing and inventories for battery-grade further declining, tightening retail supply. Due to strong MHP profits, August output is expected to rise ~4% month-on-month, mainly from high-grade nickel matte lines, suggesting easing nickel sulphate supply that can weigh on nickel prices. | Gungnir Resources reports first 2022 assays from continuing drilling at its Lappvattnet nickel deposit in Sweden, including wide zones up to 19m at 0.80% Ni and peak grades above 4%. Drilling is progressing to the eastern area and a new EM target, suggesting resource expansion potential.  | Tesla reportedly signed about $5 billion in five-year nickel supply contracts with Indonesian processing companies. The nickel will be used in Tesla’s lithium batteries, according to a cabinet minister. With Indonesia the largest nickel producer and export curbs intended to support downstream supply, the deal signals stronger nickel demand. | Fastmarkets says MHP (from Indonesian laterite) is increasingly the preferred feedstock for nickel sulfate producers. Despite rapidly rising MHP output, China’s conversion capacity is still tight, keeping nickel sulfate supply constrained since late July. Producers cite rising offer prices and an MHP payable uptrend as demand picks up.

Horizonte Minerals awarded Copa an industrial civil works contract for its Araguaia Nickel Project in Brazil, covering process plant foundations and concrete structures to accelerate construction toward furnace shell installation in 4Q22. The update supports ongoing project development but provides no direct near-term production or supply shock details. | Go Metals began diamond drilling at its HSP nickel-copper PGE project in Quebec, testing six EM/geophysical targets after confirmed nickel-copper sulphide mineralization at surface. The initial program plans about 2,000 metres of drilling. This is exploration with no production impact reported. | Go Metals Corp. began its 2022 diamond drilling program at the HSP nickel-copper PGE project in Quebec. Surface targets were previously confirmed with nickel-copper sulphides and PGE. The company plans about 2,000 metres of drilling for initial testing, with samples sent for independent analysis. | Copper rose about 1% after U.S. inflation came in weaker than expected, easing fears of aggressive rate hikes and supporting a weaker dollar. Comex futures also climbed. The article notes aluminium fell on the same day, while nickel gained sharply, suggesting mostly supportive macro demand sentiment for copper.

On Aug 11, SHFE nickel prices were high and spot trading was poor. Jinchuan nickel premiums rose to 7,000-8,000 yuan/mt, while Nornickel premiums fell to 4,000-4,500 yuan/mt. Nickel briquette prices increased mainly from narrowed premiums, but spot demand remained weak due to lower product appeal. | Alpha Copper reports results and ongoing drill progress at its Indata copper-gold project, targeting vein gold and porphyry copper-gold mineralization with associated molybdenum. It describes chalcopyrite mineralization and encourages copper-bearing intercepts, which can affect near-term copper market sentiment. No nickel or aluminium impacts are indicated. | Tesla has signed nickel-containing product supply contracts with two Indonesian companies, including a reported $5 billion deal for five years, as Indonesia positions itself as a hub for battery-related green industries. The announcement supports expectations of higher nickel demand linked to EV and battery growth.

SMM says China bonded zone inventory fell to 6,500 mt, with nickel briquette and plate down WoW, helped by slightly improved spot trading early in the week. However, after SHFE nickel prices rebounded, demand for pure nickel weakened, pointing to bearish near-term sentiment for nickel. | SMM reports Chinese nickel ore inventories fell by 40,000 wmt WoW to 6.289 million wmt as of Aug 12, with total Ni content at 49,000 mt. Although demand for raw materials is described as poor, tight market supply and mines holding back shipments suggest short-term nickel ore levels may stay steady, supporting nickel. | Finland’s Arctech Helsinki Shipyard plans to build a large LNG-powered icebreaker for Norilsk Nickel. An export license application under sanctions has been filed and a decision is expected in coming months, with an exception possible for a pre-sanctions contract. The vessel would escort Norilsk ships on the Yenisei River. | Sky-high European energy costs are pressuring aluminium and zinc smelters, with analysts warning of further capacity cuts this winter and already low LME inventories. The resulting tighter supply deficits could outweigh demand worries, supporting expectations of relative price outperformance, particularly for aluminium, if power curbs occur.

SMM expects SHFE nickel to remain high, citing supportive macro data: US CPI and PPI growth both came in below expectations, boosting commodity prices in China. Supply is rising (domestic pure nickel, Indonesian NPI oversupply), while demand is cautious, but demand for nickel sulfate may support briquette demand and tighten inventories. | Nickel 28 updates its 2% NSR royalty on Giga Metal’s Turnagain nickel/cobalt deposit after Giga formed a joint venture with Mitsubishi to pursue development in British Columbia. The company highlights EV demand growth support for nickel and cobalt and says the JV clears a key hurdle toward development. | Quebec’s environment minister approved a new arsenic emissions cap for Glencore’s Horne copper smelter in Rouyn-Noranda, lowering the limit to 15 nanograms per cubic metre over five years from 100. Updated rules will also address nickel and other pollutants, with no immediate closure announced.

Bravo Mining announced high-grade results from the Luanga drill hole DDH22LU047, including 11.04m at 2.04% nickel and 1.23% copper (with higher-grade inclusions). The company is starting downhole electromagnetic surveying and continuing a multi-rig Phase 1 diamond drill program, with PGM results pending. | Bravo Mining announced high-grade intercepts from diamond drilling at its Luanga project in Brazil, including 11.04m at 2.04% nickel and 1.23% copper, with additional high-grade copper/nickel sub-intervals. DHTEM surveying and further drilling are planned, with PGM results pending, supporting positive sentiment for Ni and Cu. | The LME suspended Russian nickel from approved UK warehouses unless exported before July 20, citing high additional costs from UK sanctions. It follows earlier April steps banning Russian copper and aluminium-related products in warehouses. LME said no Russian nickel was currently stored, though Liverpool held 198 tonnes of nickel. | The LME suspended Russian nickel from approved UK warehouses unless it is exported by July 20, citing significant additional costs risk under UK sanctions. It follows an April 1 ban on other Russian metals, including copper and primary aluminium/aluminium alloy, from LME warehouses.
