The piece argues that nickel has lost some value-discovery influence after March’s squeeze and notes China’s pricing method for imported nickel raw materials has shifted. It highlights oversupply of primary nickel, weak domestic consumption, and refined nickel stockpiling planned for August, suggesting near-term downward pressure on nickel prices. | Canada Nickel Company announced assay results from drilling at its Deloro property, confirming nickel mineralization across 487 metres grading 0.25% Ni, including higher-grade 91 metres at 0.28% Ni, and defining mineralization over 1.1 km strike length to 420 m depth. Management frames this as a sizeable nickel discovery. | Reuters reports Shanghai aluminium prices rallied on supply concerns tied to European and China production disruptions. Norsk Hydro will close a Slovak primary aluminium plant due to high electricity prices, and China’s heatwave power controls disrupted aluminium production. ShFE aluminium rose sharply, with nickel also edging higher. | Manitou Gold says it plans to spin out its nickel-cobalt-PGE potential from the Goudreau project into a new publicly listed subsidiary and begins a 1,000-metre drill program. The update follows a recent drill intersection of 0.25% Ni over 48 metres, supporting continued nickel exploration momentum. | Premium Nickel reported first 2022 assay results from the Selebi Ni-Cu-Co sulphide mine in Botswana, including 25.65 metres of 0.95% Ni and 2.03% Cu with higher-grade sub-intervals. The company says BHEM suggests additional mineralization beyond legacy horizons and anticipates more assays soon. | Metal Energy acquired a 70% interest in the Manibridge nickel-copper project in Manitoba and plans continued exploration, targeting milestones to complete the path to 100% ownership. The update supports expectations for future nickel and copper resource development, though it does not indicate imminent production changes.

The piece examines China’s primary nickel supply-demand balance for Q3–Q4, noting that LME nickel has lost some value-discovery influence after the March squeeze and that China’s pricing method for imported nickel raw materials has changed. It also links nickel intermediate products’ supply and costs to battery-chemical production. | CanAlaska completed a VTEM Plus and magnetic geophysical survey at its Hunter nickel project in Manitoba’s Thompson Nickel Belt. Results highlight multiple target areas, possible additional host stratigraphy, and a modeled conductor extension trending from the Mel nickel deposit. The company plans 3D inversion and Maxwell modeling to refine drilling targets. | Canada Nickel confirmed a second significant discovery at its Deloro ultramafic target in Ontario after assay results from recent drilling. Holes DEL22-01 and DEL22-09 intersected long sections of ~0.25–0.28% nickel. The findings bolster the company’s Timmins nickel district exploration plans and complement its Crawford project permitting progress.

Indonesia’s President Widodo says the country may impose a nickel export tax this year. If implemented, the measure could discourage exports and reduce near-term availability in global markets, likely tightening nickel supply and providing upward pressure on nickel prices over the coming days to weeks. | SMM says LME has barred two UK warrant warehouses (Liverpool and Hull) from exporting Russian nickel since July 20. It highlights that Russian nickel is not currently stored there and that LME pure nickel inventories have fallen since late 2021, with demand growth outpacing supply. This combination could lift nickel prices. | Indonesia’s president said the country may impose a nickel export tax this year to encourage more domestic refining, following earlier discussions of progressive levies on nickel pig iron and ferronickel. An export tax can change near-term nickel supply expectations and affect prices, with likely bearish pressure. | Indonesia’s president said the country is considering imposing a tax on nickel exports this year to shift more supply toward domestic refining for EV battery materials. The planned levy follows earlier government signals about progressive taxes on nickel pig iron and ferronickel, which could tighten export flows. | Indonesia is considering imposing an export tax on nickel this year to raise revenue and encourage higher-value downstream manufacturing. The proposed policy would likely affect nickel trade flows from a major supplier, influencing near-term market expectations for supply availability and pricing. | SMM says nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 80,000 wmt WoW to 6.21 million wmt as of Aug. 19. The draw reflects low shipments and cautious mine behavior, while sluggish stainless steel and NPI markets soften demand. Near-term inventories are expected to stay around current levels, with supply-demand balance. | SMM reports bonded zone nickel inventories at 8,600 mt this week, rising with higher nickel briquette and plate stocks versus last week. Nickel briquette inventory increased by 600 mt as new-energy peak season boosted stocks. Some pure nickel arrived as traders fixed price ratios, while downstream demand remained relatively rigid.

SMM says nickel sulphate inquiries rose but trading stayed thin. Main quotations were above 37,500 yuan/mt, with some at 40,000 yuan/mt, while buyers largely offered around 37,000 and most downstream firms refused above 36,500. Prices may remain high this week due to tight spot supply, with limited upside. | SMM reports spot premiums for Jinchuan and NORNICKEL nickel fell versus the prior day, while pure nickel premiums may keep trending lower this week. Nickel briquette prices rose, but trading remained poor due to lower cost efficiency and demand for intermediates. Futures jumped on Europe’s energy crisis and Indonesia tariffs. | Indonesia’s President Widodo said the country is considering a levy/tax on nickel exports this year, following prior plans to study progressive taxation on nickel pig iron and ferronickel. As Indonesia is a major nickel exporter and refining is a value-add focus, the prospect of tighter export flows is likely to support nickel prices. | SMM reports China’s July nickel ore imports rose slightly MoM but fell YoY, reflecting sluggish demand. Downstream stainless steel and NPI plants cut output due to losses, shrinking nickel-ore demand, while spot ore supply is still tight and mines ship less. SMM expects limited import growth into the rainy season, maintaining tight balance. | Indonesia’s President says the country is considering imposing a tax on nickel exports this year to boost local refining. Earlier officials discussed a progressive levy on nickel pig iron and ferronickel. The policy could reduce export supply and raise expectations of tighter nickel availability. | Power Metal Resources’ shares rose after drilling at its Molopo Farms Complex Project in Botswana intersected nickel-sulphide mineralisation, including 0.443% Ni over 7m and higher-grade intervals. Management said the magnetic conductor edge is mineralised and more drills are planned, boosting near-term nickel supply expectations. | Twin Metals says it will sue the Biden administration to reclaim federal mineral leases for a proposed underground copper-nickel mine near Ely, after the administration cancelled its leases earlier this year and restarted withdrawals of more than 200,000 acres from mineral development. | Twin Metals says it will sue the Biden administration to reclaim federal mineral leases for a proposed underground copper-nickel mine near Ely, Minnesota. The administration previously canceled the leases and restarted withdrawal of 200,000+ acres from exploration and development, prompting the dispute. The outcome timing is uncertain. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals subsidiary sued the Biden administration to overturn the January cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. Twin Metals argues the decision was illegal and should allow a regulatory review to demonstrate the project meets state and federal standards. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals sued the Biden administration to reverse the cancellation of Minnesota mineral leases tied to its proposed underground copper and nickel mine. The company argues the January action was illegal and it should be allowed a proper regulatory review. Near-term production changes are uncertain, limiting immediate price impact. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals unit sued the Biden administration to overturn the January cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. The firm argues the decision was illegal and seeks to re-enter regulatory review to demonstrate compliance with state and federal standards. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals unit sued the Biden administration to overturn the cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. The company argues the January decision was illegal and it should be allowed to demonstrate compliance in the regulatory review process. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals sued the Biden administration to reverse Minnesota mineral-lease cancellations for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine. The company argues the January decision was illegal and should allow it to demonstrate compliance through regulatory reviews. The move could ease supply-risk expectations for both metals. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals sued the Biden administration to overturn the January cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. Twin Metals argues the cancellation was illegal and that it should be allowed to demonstrate compliance with state and federal standards during regulatory review. The move supports supply expectations. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals subsidiary sued the Biden administration to reverse the cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. The company says the January decision was illegal and should be revisited through the regulatory process, potentially improving near-term supply expectations. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals subsidiary sued the Biden administration to reverse cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. Twin Metals argues the January decision was illegal and seeks a regulatory review where it can demonstrate the project meets state and federal standards. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals sued the Biden administration to reverse the cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. Twin Metals argues the January decision was illegal and it should be able to demonstrate compliance with state and federal standards in the regulatory review process. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals sued the Biden administration to overturn the January cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. Twin Metals argues the decision was illegal and that it should be allowed to demonstrate in regulatory review that the project meets state and federal standards. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals sued the Biden administration to overturn cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. The company argues the January cancellation was illegal and it should be allowed to prove the project can meet state and federal standards in ongoing review. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals subsidiary sued the Biden administration to reverse the January cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. Twin Metals argues the decision was illegal and that it should be allowed to demonstrate compliance during the regulatory review process.  | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals sued the Biden administration to challenge the cancellation of Minnesota mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine. Twin Metals said the January decision was illegal and should allow its project to be assessed under regulatory standards, potentially improving near-term supply expectations.

China’s ferronickel imports fell to 387,400 mt in July for a second straight monthly decline, with Indonesian-origin shipments down 16% month-on-month. Meanwhile, China’s NPI inventories are in surplus, leading to production curtailments. Indonesia is also said to consider nickel export tariffs targeting NPI and ferronickel. | SMM data show China’s customs clearance volume of pure nickel fell to 9,517 mt in July, down 660 mt vs June, driven by weaker downstream demand after late-July restocking. Import profitability was pressured by rising bonded premiums and poor terminal demand, and spot imports are expected to remain loss-making in August. | China’s nickel ore imports were 4.37 million mt in July, up slightly month-on-month for two months but at the lowest level in recent years. Imports from the Philippines increased, while Indonesia and New Caledonia declined. Gains were muted by weak Chinese demand and a lacklustre market. | SMM reports that on Aug 23, Jinchuan nickel spot premiums fell to 3,500–5,500 yuan/mt (avg 4,500), down 900 from the prior day, while Nornickel premiums rose slightly. Despite futures remaining high, downstream demand stayed weak, spot transactions remained poor, and physical pricing weakened for nickel. | High temperatures triggered stronger power consumption controls and rationing across multiple regions, including Zhejiang. SMM says power curtailment severely impacted nickel sulphate producers, cutting Zhejiang nickel sulphate output from 5,388 mt Ni in July to 4,416 mt in August (down 18% m/m), with only minimal growth domestically.

Blackstone Minerals says it has advanced its Vietnam green nickel project, completing bench tests, commissioning pilot unit operations, and converting mixed hydroxide precipitate into battery-grade nickel sulphate. The company is also progressing nickel mine pilot work and feasibility studies, which should support improving near-term sentiment for nickel processing.

South32 posted record profit, saying higher realised prices—especially aluminium and metallurgical coal—boosted earnings. The company also highlighted moves to low-carbon metals, adding copper exposure via Sierra Gorda and expanding aluminium capacity through Mozal and a restarted Brazil smelter. No direct near-term price shock is announced. | Clean Air Metals reported 2022 drill assay results from the Thunder Bay North Current deposit, including copper grades up to ~0.98% and nickel up to ~0.56%, and it is advancing a prefeasibility study with metallurgical testing and resource updates. The news is exploration-focused with no immediate production impact, limiting near-term price effects. | Aranjin Resources announced follow-up auger drilling at its Baavhai Uul copper project in Mongolia, reporting extensive geochemical anomalies at the “Victory” copper-nickel discovery, including up to 1,252 ppm copper and 494 ppm nickel. The anomaly remains open and further drilling is planned to outline extensions. | Aranjin Resources announced results from initial follow-up auger geochemical drilling at its Baavhai Uul Copper Project in Mongolia, highlighting a large copper-nickel discovery (“Victory”) under shallow alluvial cover. Reported results include up to 1,252 ppm copper and 494 ppm nickel, with the anomaly remaining open for further drilling.

On Aug 30, SHFE nickel prices fell, and nickel spot premiums largely steadied after futures weakness, with Jinchuan premiums averaging higher but still reflecting the day’s softer price tone. Nickel briquette prices dropped, attributed to narrowed premiums and weak trading versus intermediate products and nickel plate. | Granite Creek Copper announced a non-brokered private placement to raise up to $640,000 for exploration and development. Funds target its Carmacks copper-gold-silver project and a newly acquired Star copper-nickel-PGE project in Canada. The company positions these projects for future copper output, but it doesn’t indicate immediate production changes. | Proactive’s news roundup cites Québec Nickel reporting impressive assay results from step-out drilling at its Ducros project. Such positive exploration updates can lift sentiment around future nickel resource potential, supporting modest near-term bullish expectations for nickel prices. Other items mention zinc concentrates, but zinc is not tracked here.

SMM reports mixed spot pure nickel premiums in China: Jinchuan premiums rebound on rigid demand, while NORNICKEL premiums stay pressured due to weak demand. For NPI, oversupply and high inventories with ongoing Indonesia inflows cap prices. Transactions in stainless/alloys are mostly muted, leading SMM to expect nickel to fall further. | MacDonald Mines reported Phase 1 drill results at its SPJ property, including a 0.41% CuEq (with copper values and associated gold/silver) over 90.44m at the Alwyn trend and cobalt anomalies. While it highlights potential for broader polymetallic systems including nickel, no nickel/aluminium results are given.

SMM reports Chinese nickel ore port inventories rose 300,000 wmt week-on-week to 6.53 million wmt (51,300 mt Ni content). Inventories at seven major ports increased too. With weak supply-demand, sluggish transactions, slack stainless steel and NPI output, and pressured NPI prices, the buildup is seen as bearish for nickel despite limited further gains expected. | Copper prices touched a one-month low earlier amid China’s extended COVID restrictions in Shenzhen and a lockdown in Chengdu, raising recession and demand concerns; potential smelter shutdowns could reduce supply. Risk sentiment improved after U.S. jobs data eased Fed hike fears. Aluminium support came from Aldel mothballing capacity due to energy costs.

Nickel prices fell as macro sentiment worsened on hawkish Fed expectations and overseas primary nickel weakened. Fundamentals pointed to supply surplus: NPI plant inventories rose, Indonesia NPI inflows increased, and NPI prices were unlikely to rise. Demand showed only modest improvement, leaving net bearish pressure on nickel.

Copper futures rose on a weaker dollar and expectations that China will accelerate infrastructure spending. Reuters cites the shift as the main driver for improved metals demand. LME cash/three-month and Shanghai copper gained, alongside price increases in aluminium and nickel, reflecting broad bullish sentiment across industrial metals. | Proactive lists several company headlines, including Chalice’s work on a polymetallic nickel-copper deposit extension, Poseidon’s partnership while refocusing on restarting its Black Swan nickel project, and Caspin’s “significant” nickel-copper-PGE drilling results. These updates are sentiment-supportive for nickel and co-product copper near term. | EV Nickel reports final assays from Phase 2 drilling on the W4 Extension at its Shaw Dome nickel project, including 20.6m at 1.23% Ni and 8.5m at 2.14% Ni, plus additional high-grade intercepts. The company is applying for a mining lease and planning further drilling. | PNG Copper Inc. announced assay results from the first three drillholes at its Doriri nickel–palladium–platinum prospect in Papua New Guinea. Results intersected significant nickel grades and continued mineralisation, prompting plans to accelerate drilling and extend along strike. Copper and aluminium are not addressed in the release. | EU and U.S. imports of Russian aluminium and nickel rose sharply in March–June, despite wartime logistical issues and sanctions. Trade data show aluminium and nickel shipments increasing up to ~70%, with supply still reaching ports. Continued Russian export availability likely reduces supply-risk premiums and pressure for higher prices. | Reuters data show EU and U.S. boosted Russian aluminium and nickel purchases by up to ~70% in March–June despite logistical issues and sanctions threats. Ports and traders report continued deliveries because metals are largely spared. The near-term supply outlook improves, weighing on expectations for aluminium and nickel prices. | EU and U.S. boosted purchases of Russian aluminium and nickel since Russia’s invasion, with imports rising as much as 70% in March–June versus a year earlier. Russian shipments continued despite war-related logistics issues and broad sanctions, suggesting supply pressures have eased, weighing on price expectations. | EU and U.S. have increased purchases of Russian aluminium and nickel since the Ukraine war despite sanctions and logistics concerns. Reuters trade data show EU/U.S. Russian aluminium and nickel imports rising as much as 70% in March–June. Analysts attribute steady flows to workable deals mainly driven by price.

Reuters data show EU and U.S. imports of Russian aluminium and nickel rose sharply from March to June, with U.S. aluminium up 21% and nickel up 17% year-on-year and some increases up to ~70%. The shift reflects ongoing sanctions-driven trade rerouting after the Ukraine war. | EU and U.S. imports of Russian aluminium and nickel rose markedly in March–June, with values up to $1.98 billion and monthly aluminium imports up around 21% (U.S.) and nickel imports up 17% (top destinations). Analysts link the shift to lessons from disruption after earlier sanctions, suggesting increased supply into Western markets. | SMM reports Jinchuan nickel spot premiums eased to 11,000–10,500 yuan/mt (down from the prior day) and NORNICKEL premiums averaged lower as high futures prices and improved tight supply weighed on trading. Nickel briquette prices also fell. Demand may improve as steel mills resume, but near-term sentiment remains weak. | SMM reports that overseas stainless steel mills are expected to shut down in September, likely reducing overseas supply and lifting domestic stainless sentiment. NPI supply is expected to be flat, while rising stainless purchasing supports NPI firming. These factors keep nickel prices high despite weak near-term pure nickel demand. | Vale and partners (Shandong Xinhai and Baowu via TISCO) signed a JV to develop the Bahodopi nickel processing plant in Sulawesi, targeting ferronickel output of about 73,000–80,000 tonnes of nickel content annually. Cost is around $2.1bn, with completion targeted by 2025 at latest. | Gungnir Resources reported additional assays from drilling at its Lappvattnet nickel deposit in Sweden, including 2.14% Ni over 3.3 metres and 0.82% Ni over 9.8 metres, plus a high-grade silver interval. The company is defining nickel shoots and aiming to upgrade its resources. | Chinese waste handler Weiming and GEM will build a USD 448 million laterite nickel smelting JV in Indonesia, producing about 50,000 tons per year of high nickel matte for battery supply chains. The partners will prioritize purchases by stake. A similar project has been underway and additional nickel projects were announced this year. | Flying Nickel reported initial drill results from its Minago nickel sulphide project, with assays averaging 0.41% nickel over 113.55 meters and trace copper. Management says results support down-dip nickel mineralization expansion, with continuous Pt/Pd intercepts. The update is exploration-focused with no production changes. | Gungnir reports drill assay results at Lappvattnet, including multiple intervals with nickel grades (e.g., 2.14% over 3.3m, 5.15% over 0.73m, and 0.82% over 9.8m) and associated silver results. Such exploration updates can influence market expectations for future nickel supply. | Vale forecast global nickel demand to rise 44% by 2030 on strong EV-battery growth, alongside an increase in Vale’s own medium-term production. It also expects copper demand to grow about 20% by 2030 and projects a copper structural deficit due to insufficient supply, supporting bullish sentiment for both metals. | Tesla reportedly signed about a $5 billion, five-year deal to buy nickel products from Indonesia for lithium-ion batteries, with deliveries already started. Examples include an aluminium smelter shutdown and copper cathode/ mine curtailments, implying reduced near-term supply. | Multiple metals smelting and production operations are shutting down worldwide under force majeure and citing very high electricity prices and weak demand. SMM says China produced 15,500 mt refined nickel in August, down 3.13% m/m, with higher spot prices cutting transactions and increasing social inventories by 29%. Downstream demand also weakened. September output is expected to rise, and Indonesia NPI supply may weigh on nickel prices longer term. | SMM reports Jinchuan nickel premiums at 9,500–10,000 yuan/mt and NORNICKEL nickel premiums at 3,500–3,800 yuan/mt, both lower than the prior day. Pure nickel spot premiums fell, but nickel futures rose, keeping spot prices elevated and transactions scarce. Demand for nickel briquettes may improve as mills restart. | Talon Metals announced assay results from 13–14 new drill holes at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project in Minnesota, including a 29.39-meter interval grading 1.71% nickel and 2.21% nickel equivalent. The company says drilling will continue to expand resources and supports ongoing feasibility planning for 2026 production. | York Harbour Metals reports preliminary assays from Phase 3 drilling at its Newfoundland York Harbour Copper-Zinc Project, including a drill interval with 2.86% copper over 14.77 metres. Management says the discovery expands the D Zone and could extend along strike and downdip. Results for additional core are pending. | Soaring European power and gas prices have driven widespread aluminum smelter curtailments, including cuts at Neuss (Germany) and Dunkerque (France). Eurometaux warns the crisis also affects copper and nickel. | NiCAN reports “outstanding” assay results from its first two 2022 drill holes at the Wine project in Manitoba. Results include 27.3 metres grading 2.61% nickel equivalent. Hole 22-05 intersected multiple mineralized zones, including 27.3 metres grading about 2% nickel and 1.81% copper, supporting exploration optimism and mildly improving sentiment for both metals. | Vale is reportedly looking to ramp up output of copper and nickel in Brazil. If investors expect higher near-term supply, it typically reduces price support for both metals. However, the provided excerpt lacks specifics on timing, capex, and expected volumes, keeping the price impact magnitude uncertain.

ASX-listed Poseidon Nickel’s MD Peter Harold says the company can generate revenue by re-treating older, previously idle gold tailings. The declines were linked to probable September steel mill production resumption and steady purchasing by salt plants, plus continuous nickel sulphate output release. | Talon Metals and Rio Tinto’s Tamarack nickel project in central Minnesota continues to expand as explorers find thicker, higher-grade nickel-copper mineralization outside the defined resource zone. The updated results improve perceived future supply prospects for nickel and, secondarily, copper, potentially supporting prices modestly.

The article argues Indonesia is accelerating EV battery supply chains by expanding nickel mining and downstream processing (ferronickel/nickel pig iron, smelters and planned industrial capacity). It notes Indonesia already supplies about a third of global nickel and expects the market to move back toward surplus, adding bearish pressure. A potential 2% export duty is also mentioned. | Tartisan Nickel released a preliminary economic assessment for its Kenbridge underground nickel sulphide project in Ontario, citing low pre-production capital, expandable resources and potential early production in about 3.5 years. The PEA includes ore grades of ~0.97% nickel and ~0.52% copper, but no immediate supply impact.

Go Metals says initial drilling at its 100% owned HSP nickel-copper-PGE project in Quebec visually confirmed nickel-copper sulphides across five zones, including several wide intercepts. Assay results are pending, and the company plans more drilling as samples are sent for lab analysis. | Go Metals reported that drilling at its 100% owned HSP nickel-copper project in Quebec visually identified nickel and copper sulphides across five zones. The company sent initial samples for lab assays and noted wide mineralized intervals. The update is exploration-focused with no confirmed assay or production impact. | Premium Nickel Resources Ltd. released additional assay results from its 100% owned Selebi mine in Botswana, reporting 6.40 metres of 0.52% nickel and 2.63% copper. SMM reports spot nickel weakness in China. Jinchuan nickel premiums fell to about 6,750 yuan/mt and NORNICKEL premiums to around 3,100 yuan/mt. Nickel futures dipped on softer demand, while spot prices stayed near 195,000 yuan/mt. Nickel briquette prices also declined, with fewer trades as nickel sulphate economics deteriorated. | Huayou Cobalt and Vale will build a second HPAL nickel processing plant in Indonesia to produce 60,000 metric tons per year of nickel in mixed hydroxide precipitate (MHP) from Vale’s Sorowako limonite. The joint venture may expand if feed supply grows, bolstering nickel supply expectations. | LME nickel trading volumes have fallen sharply and participants are increasingly avoiding the contract after a March suspension and explosive price move. Low liquidity reinforces volatility. The shift toward cheaper Indonesian nickel pig iron (NPI) and related feedstocks reduces reliance on deliverable LME nickel, weighing on nickel sentiment. | Proactive’s article is a roundup of investor news including copper and nickel exploration updates. It notes Majuba Hill Copper’s Nevada drilling completion and Fabled Copper’s Muskwa exploration results, while Copper Fox delays drilling. It also cites Nickel North Exploration expanding mineralization and other battery-metal project work, implying mixed signals. | Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt and PT Vale Indonesia plan to build a second nickel refining plant in Sorowako, using HPAL to convert low-grade limonite laterite into MHP, an intermediate product for EV battery materials. The project expands Indonesia’s nickel processing capacity and improves refined-output prospects.

SMM reports that China’s energy-consumption controls tightened nonferrous supply, lifting nickel prices into early September, but pure nickel demand remains weak due to high futures prices. With strong macro uncertainty and weak downstream orders, prices may fall in the short term. Indonesia’s NPI/intermediate projects could add supply surplus. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulphate prices rose in August as spot supply tightened, despite slight overall oversupply, because integrated producers’ output is not fully available in spot circulation. September demand is picking up and utilization is higher, so prices should stay supported though spot tightness may ease. | Technavio’s report forecasts growth for the copper alloy wire market (about USD 18.6B incremental growth) driven by electrical/electronics demand and APAC’s large market share. It describes longer-term market expansion but provides no concrete near-term supply disruptions, inventory changes, tariffs, or contract price impacts for copper, nickel, or aluminium. | Norilsk Nickel says a fire in the cobalt section of its Kola electrowinning shop-2 was extinguished and the platinum group metals production chain remains intact. The company reports the main building, finished products storage, and nickel electrowinning sections were not affected, with a test run showing normal nickel output. | Aluminium prices rose as Europe’s power crisis forced about half of EU aluminium capacity offline and analysts warned shortages could worsen in winter. Copper fell amid recession demand fears and a sharp jump in LME on-warrant stocks; nickel dropped even more with broader risk-off sentiment and tighter economic prospects.

Shanghai aluminium rose up to 2% on fears of wider and longer China production cuts as power restrictions in Yunnan could expand and remain in place amid low rainfall and hydropower supply. Gains were capped by record August output and global macro worries, which also weighed on copper and nickel. | Infinity Stone Ventures announced new assay results from its Zen-Whoberi Project, identifying a Centauri Zone where one grab sample returned 15.5% copper, alongside anomalous silver, gold, nickel and cobalt. Additional samples show lower copper grades. The update is exploration-focused, with no production or inventory changes stated. | SMM said Chinese nickel ore port inventories rose 426,000 wmt to 7.06 million wmt as of Sep 16, driven by approaching rainy-season restocking and recovering downstream demand. Inventories increased across seven major ports as shipments improved. The outlook is further growth, with NPI plants’ nickel-ore demand expected to rise as well. | U.S. and EU imports of Russian nickel rose sharply after March, with shipments to the U.S. up 70% (March–June) and to the EU up 22%. The influx of discounted Russian material suggests overstated disruption fears and increased supply availability, which can pressure nickel prices despite some earlier upside momentum.

Battery-grade nickel sulphate prices followed nickel slightly higher last week. SMM says insufficient MHP-based nickel sulphate capacity will be hard to resolve through the rest of the year. While high-grade matte-based capacity has grown, spot supply remains tight, and despite weak restocking demand, prices are expected to stay at highs this week. | Copper prices fell as markets turned more pessimistic about global growth, raising concerns of weaker metals demand; the weak U.S. dollar limited declines. Fitch Solutions cut its copper forecast. Aluminium also weakened on lower Chinese import volumes tied to strong domestic production, while nickel moved higher amid mixed signals. | SMM reports Ni 1.5% ore averaging $67.5/wmt CIF on Sep 16, flat on week, while low-grade FOB/CIF prices rose on scarce supply ahead of the rainy season. Medium-grade deals were disputed. Limited stainless demand and high NPI inventories cap upside, but a slight seasonal rise in ore is expected ($68–72).

SMM reports Western countries continued importing nickel from Russia despite sanctions, with US Russian nickel imports rising 70% from March to June and EU up 22%. Russia supplies about 10% of global nickel and Nornickel provides key battery-grade supply. The increased inflows suggest added near-term supply pressure on nickel prices. | A nickel mining executive says Canada has fallen behind global nickel output and processing is dominated by Indonesia and China. She argues Canada needs many new mines and refineries by 2030 to meet growing battery-driven demand. China’s GEM, via its QMB unit, partnered with Australia’s Nickel Industries to secure annual limonite laterite ore supply from Hengjaya’s Indonesian mine. The deal aims to back long-term production of battery materials, with yearly pricing agreements, potentially supporting near-term expectations for nickel feedstock tightness.

Power Nickel reported its NI 43-101 technical report and mineral resource estimate for the Nisk nickel project and has started a second round of drilling (~5,500 m) to infill gaps and expand high-grade mineralization. The update is intended to grow resource confidence, with only limited copper linkage via NiEq. | SMM reports the SS contract rose sharply despite the Fed’s 75bp hike matching expectations. It attributes part of the move to strong SHFE nickel gains earlier in the week. Many samples returned over 2,000 ppm nickel, with ICP results pending and further work (including potential drilling) planned. | Power Nickel announced a NI 43-101 compliant mineral resource estimate for its Nisk nickel-copper project and has begun a second drilling round (~5,500 m) to infill and expand higher-grade zones. The disclosure may affect market expectations for future nickel/copper supply but not near-term production.

OZ Minerals has given the go-ahead and started work on a $1.7 billion copper-nickel mine in remote Western Australia. It also cites BHP profit write-downs and costs linked to a global nickel price slump, reinforcing near-term bearish nickel sentiment. | The roundup highlights several nickel developments in Western Australia, including FIRB approval for Japanese investment in Ardea’s Goongarrie Hub and Ardea starting DFS drilling and testwork, which are supportive. Offsetting bearish items include BHP shutting its WA nickel operation and Ravensthorpe moving to care and maintenance amid cheap Indonesian nickel. | SMM said bonded zone nickel inventory fell to 5,480 mt, including nickel plate at 3,230 mt and nickel briquette at 2,250 mt. London copper slid to a two-month low, falling about 1.1% to $7,352/t, as the U.S. dollar hit a two-decade peak and the Fed outlook turned more hawkish. Nimy Resources says early diamond drilling at its Mons project in WA intersected substantial nickel sulphide mineralisation at the Godley prospect, with about 368m and 427m averaging ~0.14% nickel. Analysts cite macro slowdown and power/energy issues to explain large aluminium declines and potential transitory spikes from Russian delivery and potential smelter curbs. Citi notes a recent nickel rally but expects Europe-led demand weakness to dominate. SMM reports spot pure nickel premiums in China stabilising after stopping their fall. Jinchuan nickel premiums rose to 6,000-6,500 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL premiums increased to about 1,700-1,900 yuan/mt. The market reacted quickly to tighter supply prospects, while nickel also rose and Alcoa shares jumped 7% in premarket trading. | SMM reports the alloy-sector composite PMI stayed in expansion in September and early October, supporting nickel demand expectations. It notes nickel prices had fallen after initial macro pressure from the Fed meeting but eased again as outcomes matched expectations, while spot transactions improved. Fundamentals suggest inventory may not fall sharply, limiting upside. | Aluminum surged 8.5% on the LME after Bloomberg reported the exchange may discuss blocking new Russian metal deliveries to its warehouses. Russia’s role as a major supplier raises supply-risk concerns. Nickel also jumped about 5%, while copper was cited as potentially affected if Russian flows tighten. | Ero Copper shares rose 9.4% after the company announced a discovery of a regional nickel sulphide system (Umburana System) in Brazil’s Curaçá Valley near its Caraiba mill. The news is expected to lift sentiment around nickel resources, even though near-term output is not indicated. | Ero Copper shares jump after the company announces discovery of a regional nickel sulphide system in Brazil’s Curaçá Valley, near its Caraíba mill. The find highlights potential future nickel resources, but the output timing is uncertain, suggesting limited immediate support for nickel prices over the next several weeks. | Nickel 28 reported Ramu’s production and financing updates, noting that a 7.6 earthquake triggered emergency protocols and MCC reduced refinery throughput. Ramu is expected to run at reduced capacity for at least two months, while Nickel 28 says nickel prices remain elevated.

The article headline says Indonesia’s export ban has caused nickel-related investment in Morowali to triple. The development implies faster ramp-up of domestic nickel processing and could improve supply outlook, creating downward pressure on nickel prices in the near term as markets price higher future availability. | Vale said Brazilian nickel and copper assets will be reorganised into two entities. Copper assets will move to Salobo Metais S.A, while nickel assets will be placed into a new Brazil company. Vale clarified there is no decision on new Base Metals transactions, and assets remain fully consolidated and owned by Vale. | Vale said Brazilian authorities approved a reorganisation of its base metals operations: copper assets will be transferred to Salobo Metais S.A, while nickel assets will go to a new company Vale will establish. Both sets remain consolidated and wholly owned by Vale, suggesting no immediate production shift. | Rusal denied plans to offload aluminium into LME warehouses amid LME consideration of a consultation over whether Russian aluminium, nickel and copper should remain tradable and storable. Global Ferronickel Holdings acquired a 20% indirect stake in Guangdong Century Tsingshan Nickel Industry (GCTN), a Chinese nickel alloy producer with smelters. The deal links Global Ferronickel’s nickel ore supply with GCTN’s processing capabilities, following prior maiden nickel ore exports to China.

Palladium One reports preliminary assay results from its Tyko Project West Pickle discovery in Canada, including intersections with 4.8% Ni and 3.7% Cu over 1.8m (and other high-grade Ni-Cu intervals). The company highlights exploration success and expects further EM results, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel and copper. | Manitou Gold says drilling at its Goudreau project intersected 230.6 metres grading 0.25% nickel equivalent, further confirming a significant nickel-cobalt discovery. The reported assay results can shift near-term expectations for future nickel supply, supporting market sentiment toward nickel prices. | Helsinki’s Arctech Helsinki Shipyard said Finland’s foreign ministry refused an export license needed to ship an icebreaker for Norilsk Nickel under sanctions. The icebreaker project, planned for 2023 start and winter 2025 delivery, could affect nickel-linked operational logistics. | Horizonte Minerals reported progress on its Araguaia nickel project in Brazil: construction is on schedule with ~16% overall completion and planned first nickel in Q1 2024. Falcon Gold announced it has acquired a strategic nickel and copper claim group contiguous to Go Metal’s HSP property. While the move could broaden future nickel and copper supply prospects for the issuer, the article does not indicate any immediate production start or inventory impact, limiting short-term price effects. | Falcon Gold announced the acquisition of a strategic nickel and copper claim group contiguous to Go Metals’ HSP property. Such acquisitions can strengthen expectations for future nickel and copper supply, providing a modest sentiment boost. However, without production timelines, immediate effects on prices are likely limited. | ALX Resources provided an exploration update from its Flying Vee nickel project in Saskatchewan, reporting EM-confirmed conductive trends and newly located gossan and boulder zones with sulphide mineralization, including up to ~2% chalcopyrite locally. Centaurus has postponed its construction decision for a US$288mn nickel project in Brazil. The transaction is subject to approvals and is expected to close later. While vanadium is not tracked, the news can affect nickel sentiment. | Flying Nickel Mining Corp. signed a definitive agreement to acquire Nevada Vanadium’s shares, combining nickel and vanadium project portfolios. The deal is still subject to approvals and expected to close in December 2022. The release highlights battery-metal demand but gives no immediate nickel supply or production figures.

SMM says pure nickel spot prices in Shanghai were quoted at premiums versus SHFE 2211, with Jinchuan premiums averaging about 9,250 yuan/mt and rising sharply. NORNICKEL premiums also increased. The arrangement may lift market expectations for nickel availability and battery-related demand, supporting nickel prices in the near term despite the deal’s non-binding nature. | Stellantis and GME Resources signed a non-binding memorandum for future sales of battery-grade nickel and cobalt sulphate from Western Australia’s NiWest nickel-cobalt project. Stellantis targets higher BEV mixes under its Dare Forward 2030 plan. The deal is subject to customary approvals and conditions, making timing uncertain. | Stellantis signed a non-binding preliminary agreement with GME Resources to secure supplies of nickel and cobalt sulphate for EV batteries. The supply is tied to GME’s NiWest project in Western Australia with planned battery-grade output. A feasibility study is set to start, potentially supporting nickel demand expectations. | Copper prices rose, supported by renewed concerns over copper availability in LME warehouses (higher cancelled warrants) and a wider cash premium. The dispute followed the nickel price exceeding $100,000/tonne and the LME suspending trading and voiding transactions. The ruling is procedural; the LME will file its defence within 28 days.

SMM reports that SHFE nickel futures rose in the morning, lifting spot prices roughly 8,000 yuan/mt. Jinchuan nickel premiums increased to an average of 9,750 yuan/mt, while Nornickel premiums fell. Nickel briquette prices rose but transactions weakened as demand/market effectiveness deteriorated. | The piece discusses the rationale for launching SMM’s Battery Grade Nickel Sulfate Price Index and how it may be used in market scenarios. As an exploration update, it is unlikely to materially change near-term nickel or copper supply expectations. | Base metals dropped as recession fears and rising COVID-19 cases in China raised concerns about weaker demand. General Motors will take a major stake in Queensland Pacific Metals, investing up to $69 million. The funding is intended to secure nickel and cobalt sulfate supply for GM’s electric vehicles. The move signals increased near-term demand expectations for nickel feedstock, supportive for nickel prices. | SMM reports that on Oct 12 SHFE nickel fell early, while spot nickel remained on a downward trend. Jinchuan nickel premiums rose slightly, but overall spot activity was average with weak transactions. Exploration progress can modestly support expectations for future nickel and copper supply. | Russian-origin copper has accumulated in LME warehouses in Germany, the Netherlands and Taiwan since mid-September, with stocks rising sharply. Some consumers reportedly reject Russian copper, turning LME storage into a last-resort outlet. LME also discussed possible bans on Russian aluminium, nickel and copper. | GM will invest up to $69 million in Queensland Pacific Metals’ TECH facility to produce nickel and cobalt sulfate for NMC batteries, using nickel sourced via laterite processing. The deal targets secured, cost-competitive supply for GM’s EV ramp, amid recent pressure on nickel sulfate prices. | General Motors will invest $69M in Queensland Pacific Metals to secure long-term nickel (and cobalt) supply for Ultium battery cells. The nickel laterite ore will be processed at a TECH project using the DNi process with high acid recycling and no tailings dam. The deal strengthens near-term nickel demand expectations for EV production. | Quebec Nickel Corp. plans to expand drilling activity at its Ducros Ni-Cu-PGE project in Abitibi, Quebec, adding a third drill. The update suggests ongoing progress in exploration for nickel (and related metals), generally supportive for nickel sentiment, though no immediate supply change is stated. | The piece argues Indonesia’s strong nickel mine output (up 41% YoY in the first seven months of 2022) shifted global supply from deficit to surplus (~29,000 tons). It notes this oversupply, alongside demand destruction fears and a firm U.S. dollar, should pressure nickel prices downward, though liquidity remains low and volatile.

SMM says pure nickel premiums for Jinchuan and NPI-related products rose due to tight supply and active market inquiries. Downstream demand from stainless steel producers and alloy consumers remains robust, while NPI output increases are limited by pandemic impacts and lower-than-expected Indonesia supply. Overall, nickel-chain prices are supported. | Swedish startup Nilar International began manufacturing reusable ReOX nickel metal hydride batteries for renewable energy storage. The company says aging is reversible and capacity can be restored multiple times. Production followed an order from Enequi for 800 batteries, potentially providing incremental nickel demand, though likely at a relatively small scale. | Quebec Nickel Corp. reports adding a third drill at its Ni-Cu-PGE project in Quebec’s Abitibi region, expanding work around high-grade results. The update relates to exploration rather than production, so it is unlikely to materially change nickel supply expectations in the next few weeks. | Vale Canada officially opened the first phase of its CDN$945 million Copper Cliff Complex South Mine project in Sudbury. The expansion is expected to nearly double ore production and add about 13,000 tonnes/year of copper and 10,000 tonnes/year of contained nickel, supporting supply and regional investment sentiment. | Vale Canada opened the first phase of its $945M Copper Cliff Complex South Project at Sudbury, adding tunnels and ventilation for expanded underground handling. The phase is expected to increase contained production by about 13,000 tonnes/year of copper and 10,000 tonnes/year of nickel, supporting supply expectations.

SMM said bonded-zone nickel inventories fell 400 mt to 3,900 mt, including 1,250 mt of nickel briquettes and 2,650 mt of nickel plate. Despite a weaker RMB, spot pure nickel imports still found some profit margins. Demand for briquettes stayed firm due to expected higher nickel sulphate output and bullish stainless steel. | SMM says China’s nickel ore port inventories rose by 321,000 wmt to 7.94 million wmt by Oct 14 versus pre–National Day levels, with Ni content about 62,000 mt. Further arrivals are expected next week (possibly >8 million wmt), after which inventories fall once Philippines rainy season fully starts. | Vale Canada began the first phase of its C$945m Copper Cliff Complex South Mine Project in Sudbury, adding about 13,000 tonnes of contained copper and 10,000 tonnes of contained nickel per year for Vale’s Copper Cliff operations. The phased launch supports future supply, but immediate market price effects likely limited. | Vale Canada has inaugurated phase I of its $945m Copper Cliff Complex South Mine Project in Sudbury, expanding underground ore and waste handling and ventilation. The project is expected to nearly double Copper Cliff output, adding about 13,000 tonnes of copper and 10,000 tonnes of contained nickel per annum, supporting supply sentiment. | European copper buyers face record term premiums for 2023 as they self-sanction against Russian metal following UK actions affecting UMMC-linked brands. Aurubis, Codelco and Montanwerke Brixlegg quote much higher premiums amid low visible stocks and potential benchmark concerns. The piece warns Russian copper could redirect to China, affecting regional pricing. | Vale has officially opened the initial phase of its $945m Copper Cliff Mine South expansion, involving re-opening a dormant mine and rebuilding key shafts and infrastructure. Vale forecasts nearly double output at Copper Cliff, adding about 10,000 tonnes of nickel and 13,000 tonnes of copper annually, supporting low-carbon mineral supply.

A weekly broker-rating roundup highlights Citi’s view of short-term headwinds for base-metals demand from sluggish China, higher US interest rates and a stronger USD. Citi trims near-term price forecasts for aluminium, copper and nickel (while being comparatively more bearish on copper). Some company ratings are upgraded on valuation, but metal-price expectations lean weaker. | Copper prices were subdued as demand uncertainty from China’s continued zero-COVID curbs and global recession fears offset worries over tight supply. LME and Shanghai copper prices gave back gains. Aluminium and nickel also declined as risk sentiment weakened, despite lower Chile output and tight inventories. | Copper rose on tightening global supplies: LME/COMEX/ShFE stocks fell and Chilean production dropped sharply. China demand improved at the margin via premiums, but gains were capped by ongoing zero-COVID uncertainty and global recession fears. Aluminium and nickel fell in the same session, showing mixed risk appetite. | SMM reports SHFE nickel stayed rangebound, with upside pressure from strong downstream pure nickel demand and occasional drops in stainless steel prices. US CPI and core CPI exceeding expectations weighed on nickel. Chinese refined output was slightly lower MoM, and near-term price outlook for SHFE 2211 is 172,000–192,000 yuan/mt. | SMM estimates China’s nickel ore port arrivals rose to 1.27m wmt (Sep 30 week) and 1.69m wmt (Oct 7 week). With the Philippines rainy season approaching, early-purchased ore is arriving now, keeping imports high. Increased NPI demand helped stabilize and lift nickel ore prices. | Copper prices were subdued as demand uncertainty outweighed supply woes. LME copper gave up gains and hovered around $7,535/t while inventories fell and Chile production dropped sharply. China’s reiterated zero-COVID stance and recession fears limited upside, with aluminium and nickel also slipping. | Element 29 Resources reported its first resource estimate for the Elida copper project in Peru, with inferred tonnes grading 0.32% copper and small molybdenum credits, and plans further drilling and metallurgical studies. It also plans to update resources at Flor de Cobre. Overall, it’s copper-positive for sentiment, not a direct near-term supply driver.

General Motors partnered with Queensland Pacific Metals to secure increased long-term nickel supply for its Ultium battery cells, investing up to $69 million to develop a high-purity battery materials refinery in Australia. The deal supports GM’s EV production ramp and EV tax-credit eligibility, reinforcing contracted demand for nickel. | SMM says pure nickel price declines in September were mainly driven by macro expectations around Fed hikes, but downside is now slowing. It points to rigid alloy demand, tight global pure nickel inventories supporting prices, and weaker-than-expected Indonesian high-grade nickel matte output release in Q4, implying less inventory pressure ahead. | SMM reports higher Jinchuan and NORNICKEL nickel premiums in Shanghai and stable but volatile pure nickel conditions. Nickel briquette prices fell, yet improved cost efficiency increased transactions. Tight NHP supply raised nickel sulphate prices, underpinning support for nickel-related demand and sentiment near term. | Power Nickel Inc. announced a C$3 million non-brokered private placement of flow-through and non-flow-through units to fund Canadian exploration eligible for a critical mineral tax credit, plus debt settlement and working capital. It is a nickel-exploration financing update without stated immediate supply impact on nickel markets. | SMM data show SHFE and LME nickel inventories have increased from recent lows, with mixed movements across product types. However, downstream resumed production post–National Day without prior stocking, boosting purchases of high-priced pure nickel. Demand for nickel briquette persists due to nickel sulphate orders and tight MHP supply, with improved cost efficiency after imports. | Gungnir Resources reported partial results from drill hole LAP22-19 at its Lappvattnet nickel deposit in northern Sweden, including 9.0 metres at 2.35% nickel (with 5.66 metres at 3.02% nickel). The company continues drilling toward 2022/23 resource upgrading with assays pending. | Sumitomo Metal Mining expects the global nickel market to remain in deficit in 2023 for the third straight year, though the shortfall will narrow due to higher Indonesian NPI supply. Strong demand for batteries and industrial uses should keep prices supported despite improving supply. | Canada Nickel closed a US$10 million secured loan facility with Auramet to fund post-feasibility permitting and detailed engineering over the winter. The project is focused on nickel-sulphide development at the Crawford Nickel-Cobalt site. The financing improves near-term development prospects, mildly supportive for nickel sentiment.

SMM reports spot pure nickel supply turned scarce again on Oct 19, lifting NORNICKEL premiums to about 5,000 yuan/mt (+200). Jinchuan premiums averaged 10,250 yuan/mt (flat). Nickel briquette prices rose, but liquidity weakened. Supply may improve after SHFE 2210 contract goods are released. | Copper prices fell further as soaring inflation, recession worries and expectations of more rate hikes pressured global demand sentiment, while a stronger dollar weighed on metal prices. LME aluminium also slipped amid additional inflows into approved warehouses and rising inventories. Nickel rose slightly, but the overall tone remains bearish. | North Dakota’s Mercer County will host a $433 million Talon Nickel ore processing and management facility, backed by a $114 million U.S. federal cost-share grant. The plant will process ore from Talon’s Tamarack mine for battery minerals. The policy aims to strengthen domestic supply chains, reducing reliance on foreign nickel sources. | Talon Metals announced a near-doubling of indicated resources at its Tamarack Nickel Project, including a 570% rise in high-grade massive sulphide contained nickel. The update strengthens expectations for future nickel concentrate supply potential from the Tamarack nickel-copper deposit, with limited secondary implications for copper. | North Dakota’s Mercer County will host Talon Nickel’s $433 million ore processing and management facility, funded partly by a $114 million U.S. grant. The plant will process nickel ore from Talon’s Tamarack mine to support U.S. EV and grid battery supply chains, creating about 150 jobs. | Talon Nickel will build a $433 million ore processing and management facility in Mercer County, North Dakota, funded in part by $114 million from the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law. The plant will process raw ore from Talon’s Tamarack nickel mine to support domestic battery mineral supply for EVs and the power grid.

Nearctic Nickel Mines Inc. says its Quebec affiliate, Ungava Mineral Exploration, received a Statement of Defense in an ongoing Quebec Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources action. The company notes amendments to a Motion for Damages filed in 2015 and updated in May 2022. No operational or production changes are described.

SMM reports Chinese nickel ore port inventories rose 328,000 wmt WoW to 8.26 million wmt as NPI plants restocked before the Philippines rainy season. Total nickel content was 65,000 mt. With rainy-season conditions in southern Philippines expected to lift inventories short term, ore prices get some support. | Recharge Resources plans to spin out its 100% owned Pinchi Lake nickel project in Canada into a new subsidiary, NextCharge Battery Metals Corp., subject to conditions and regulatory approval. The announcement is procedural and does not change near-term output or supply, implying limited immediate impact on nickel prices.

First Quantum Minerals says it will apply for new exploration licenses in Zambia late 2022 or early 2023, citing improved business conditions under President Hakainde Hichilema after years of stagnant copper output. The planned exploration targets copper and nickel, aimed at easing global shortages, though timing limits near-term price effects. | SMM reports that on Oct 24 spot premiums for pure nickel were sharply lower: Jinchuan nickel premiums fell to 8,000–8,500 yuan/mt (down 400). NORNICKEL premiums also declined (down 500). Month-end shipping activity and weaker spot transactions followed prior futures-driven premium spikes. | As of Oct 21, nickel ore arrivals at Chinese ports were 752,000 wmt, down 34% week-on-week. Shipments from the Philippines (Surigao) were constrained by rainy-season weather restrictions. With NPI prices supported by tight short-term supply, ports are likely to see smaller imports going forward, tightening balances. | SHFE nickel prices fell after an earlier rise. Pandemic-related transport restrictions delayed spot pure nickel arrivals. NPI shipments in October were steady and inventories eased, while Indonesia NPI supply was tight with high prices. Despite rising stainless steel demand, nickel is expected to trade volatile this week. | SMM reports September 2022 China nickel ore imports rose, but port inventory is only slightly higher and the market remains tight. With the Philippines rainy season approaching, nickel ore shipments face weather restrictions, likely reducing laterite nickel ore imports. The article expects imports to trend downward during the rainy season. | Rusal filed a London lawsuit alleging Norilsk Nickel CEO Vladimir Potanin breached a decade-old shareholder pact that was meant to protect Nornickel’s dividend formula. The dispute renews tension between Rusal and Potanin-controlled Interros, with the agreement set to expire and merger talks postponed. | EV Nickel reported initial assay results from four holes of Phase 3 drilling at its Shaw Dome CarLang A Zone, confirming thick dunite-hosted, large-scale nickel mineralization (e.g., 297 m at 0.25% Ni and 201 m at 0.22% Ni). Remaining assays are pending and expected over coming weeks.

SMM says spot Jinchuan nickel premiums averaged 8,250 yuan/mt, staying flat, while NORNICKEL premiums fell. Although pure nickel premiums continue to drop, spot Jinchuan supply is generally tight in October and the premium decline is limited due to severe pandemic conditions. Nickel briquette prices rose slightly, but trades were weak. | Falcon Gold expands its HSP South project by staking additional claims, increasing its land position by 70% in Havre St. Pierre’s anorthosite complex. The area is described as newly recognized nickel source and contiguous to Go Metals’ recent nickel-and-copper sulphide discovery, with airborne surveys planned. | Falcon Gold reported a 70% increase in its land position at the HSP South property in Quebec, adjacent to Go Metals’ drilling and discovery of wide intervals of nickel and copper sulphides. Falcon plans airborne EM surveys and expanded staking across prospective contact, potentially boosting near-term sentiment for nickel and copper. | SMM cites customs data showing China’s clearance volume of pure/nickel-related products rose about 11% MoM in September while remaining low YoY. Imports of nickel briquettes and powder jumped MoM, attributed to higher nickel sulphate demand for ternary precursors. Output and bonded inventories should stay supportive into October. | Nickel 28 reported Q3 2022 operating performance from its Ramu nickel-cobalt JV in Papua New Guinea, including higher contained nickel production and substantially higher nickel sales. It discusses earthquake-related curtailment and cash-cost impacts from input commodity pricing that is reportedly easing, which affects short-term nickel sentiment. | Talon Metals plans to move components of its proposed Tamarack, Minnesota nickel processing plant to North Dakota to address environmental and tribal concerns. The company says this reduces land disturbance and permitting scope. The project remains focused on nickel and has a $114 million DOE grant, but no production change is specified. | Battery OEMs are facing rising nickel costs as supply terms have shifted from fixed long-term off-takes to spot pricing for nickel salts. The piece cites nickel-salt premiums over LME nickel and a move toward lower-grade intermediates, increasing input cost volatility and exposure for manufacturers. | Nickel North Exploration reports 2022 sampling and backpack drilling confirming high-grade, up to 400m-long Ni-Cu sulfide mineralization at the Hawk Ridge Project’s Gamma Zone in Northern Quebec. Reported intercepts and outcrop/backpack results include strong nickel and copper grades, supporting further 2023 drilling plans.

Cyprium Metals’ reverse-circulation drilling at the Nanadie Well project in Western Australia intersected broad anomalous copper-nickel-PGE zones, including multiple strong copper intervals and sections with meaningful nickel. The company plans follow-up work to test an expanded 1.6 km corridor, improving sentiment for future supply. | SMM reports that on Oct 26 Jinchuan nickel premium fell to 8,000 yuan/mt (down 250) and NORNICKEL premium to around 2,400 yuan/mt (down 500). Despite active near-month SHFE contract shifting, spot premiums declined, while thin spot trading and higher nickel briquette prices weakened deal activity. | SMM reports resilient Jinchuan nickel spot premiums in October amid supply tightness, with imports pressured by a weaker SHFE/LME ratio. Nickel demand remains supported by downstream stainless steel, and low inventory is cited. For NPI, demand is robust and the market expects slight short-term upward room, unless Indonesian flows surge. | SMM reports SHFE nickel rising for a third consecutive day (+1.35%) with spot refined nickel up to about 195,050 yuan/mt. Tight Jinchuan supply, limited premium declines, weaker import economics, relatively strong NPI demand, low inventories, and supportive stainless-steel demand should limit downside. | Clean Air Metals reported infill drill results at its Thunder Bay North PGE-Cu-Ni project, including copper (0.61%) and nickel (0.43%) in a 10m interval, and said it is advancing prefeasibility engineering and metallurgical testing. The update is exploration-focused with no immediate production or supply disruption signals. | Clean Air Metals reported 2022 drill results from its Thunder Bay North Current deposit, including 10.0m grading 0.61% copper and 0.43% nickel, and said it is advancing a prefeasibility study with metallurgical optimization and resource validation. The update may support longer-term supply expectations, but no immediate production impact is cited.

Talon Metals is advancing the Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project in Minnesota to supply nickel for electric-vehicle batteries, citing test results and a Tesla supply deal. Local groups raise concerns about wetlands and potential runoff, but the company says processing occurs out of state. The prospect supports nickel sentiment more than near-term supply.

SMM reports SHFE nickel fell in early trading. In the spot market on Oct 28, Jinchuan nickel premiums averaged 6,250 yuan/mt, down 400 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL premiums averaged 1,550 yuan/mt, down 350 yuan/mt. Spot supply rose and shipments increased; nickel briquette prices also dropped as demand remained weak. | SMM said China’s bonded zone nickel inventory fell by 700 mt to 3,900 mt in the week to Oct 28. Nickel briquette stocks were 1,050 mt and nickel plate 2,850 mt. Spot pure nickel imports weakened, but some briquettes cleared customs on demand from nickel sulphate producers despite off-season plate demand. | SMM says nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports rose 289,000 wmt to 8.55 million wmt (67,000 mt Ni content) as October purchases before the Philippine rainy season arrived. Port stocks are expected to keep increasing short term, though growth may be capped by rainy-season disruptions in the Philippines and later decline.

SMM reports nickel faced heavy downward pressure from the macro front after US jobless claims and real GDP growth beat expectations, weakening expectations of slower Fed rate hikes. On supply, liquidity improved, but downstream demand slightly weakened: stainless spot prices fell and high nickel curbed alloy buying, limiting support. | SMM data shows pure nickel inventories in six Chinese regions rose to 7,050 mt last week, up 1,151 mt week-on-week. Nickel briquette stocks increased to 650 mt, and nickel plate inventories rose to 6,400 mt. Higher inventories reflect softer downstream demand and shifting bonded-to-domestic supplies. | In October, China’s pure nickel supply stayed tight amid renewed COVID outbreaks and low trader margins, but last week liquidity improved as SHFE nickel and spot premiums fell. Import profits weakened on surging overseas prices. Downstream stainless/alloy demand was sluggish and off-season approached, leaving sufficient supply and bearish price pressure.

SMM reports SHFE nickel prices falling as upstream sellers become more willing to offer while downstream demand remains weak. Pure nickel spot premiums and nickel briquette prices drop. For NPI, large “invisible” inventory risks future pressure. Alloy-sector civil orders fall, so nickel demand stays soft. | SMM reports that on Nov 1 spot pure nickel premiums were much higher, with Jinchuan premiums averaging 8,250 yuan/mt and NORNICKEL averaging 4,250 yuan/mt, both up sharply day-on-day. Spot prices rose in the morning on favourable news, though trade was thin and nickel briquette transactions were weak. | BHP’s CEO warns of a timing mismatch where demand for decarbonization metals rises before supply can scale up, implying an eventual copper shortage. Trafigura notes copper stockpiles cover only about 4.9 days of consumption and could fall further. Multiple research reports project large mine-supply gaps, supporting a copper price rebound view. | Fastmarkets’ first nickel MHP assessment finds strong international supply/demand with rising MHP payables (75-78% assessed; offers near ~80%). Limited MHP conversion/refining capacity and a nickel sulfate supply-demand deficit lifted nickel sulfate prices in China for a second week. Underlying LME nickel strength also supported gains. | Ero Copper reported Q3 2022 results, including 11,189 tonnes of copper production and ongoing cost pressure, noting a $10.3m impact from final settlements on provisionally priced copper concentrate sales and higher operating costs. The firm also disclosed progress and financing guidance for growth projects and ongoing nickel exploration.

Copper prices edged lower as bleak near-term manufacturing and metals demand outweighed hopes that China may ease COVID-19 controls next year. Global risk sentiment weakened ahead of a large U.S. rate hike, likely stifling activity. Tight supply and low inventories were cited as support, leaving overall bias mixed. | Base metals prices rose on Tuesday after widespread rumors that China might change/ease its zero-Covid policy. Three-month futures gained for copper (+2.7%), aluminium (+0.9%), and nickel (+8.2%). Analysts said reopening hopes lifted China risk assets and triggered short covering; nickel additionally benefited from strong EV sales.

Copper prices fell as markets digested a hawkish Fed stance signaling prolonged policy tightening, weakening risk sentiment and near-term demand expectations. China’s elevated COVID cases added pressure on industrial and construction activity. The report also notes lower tin and lead, with aluminium dipping and nickel edging up.

Fastmarkets’ LME Week coverage says nickel dominated discussions due to high demand and ongoing market transition after the LME nickel contract suspension in March. It highlights investor focus on MHP and volatility, plus tight European nickel cathode supply risk tied to Russian-origin acceptance/sanctions concerns, with premiums remaining elevated.

Jervois Global is planning a $200m equity raising with UBS to restart its Brazilian refinery. The company focuses on battery metals, including nickel group materials. Restarting refining capacity can ease near-term supply concerns, which may weigh on nickel price expectations over the coming days to weeks. | SMM reports nickel spot trading was quiet on Nov 8. Jinchuan nickel premiums eased to about 5,650 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL premiums rose slightly. Futures were stable. Imported pure nickel premiums rebounded on supply shortages, but nickel briquette trading remained slack as briquette prices stayed above nickel sulphate. | An Essential Geopolitics podcast episode discusses Indonesia’s planned ban on raw ore exports, citing analysis that the government has decided to restrict exports of nickel ore. The move is expected to affect international nickel ore availability, potentially tightening supply and supporting near-term nickel pricing sentiment. | Magna Mining says its first resource estimate at the Denison Sudbury Basin project shows “significant potential” and plans additional drilling. The project targets nickel and copper, with the company exploring a restart of a past-producing underground mine. The news is more sentiment-driven than supply-increasing immediately.

SMM reports China produced 15,400 mt of refined nickel in October, marginally higher MoM and up YoY. Despite worse pandemic conditions in north-west China, smelter operations were not disrupted and transportation impacts were limited. November output is forecast to rise slightly to 15,500 mt on resumed/new capacity. | Copper prices fell after China’s weaker economic indicators and a fresh rise in COVID-19 cases increased concerns about industrial demand. Despite some support from tight physical supplies, three-month LME copper slipped around 0.5% and SHFE copper diverged. Market focus now shifts to US inflation and politics. | Xander Resources engaged a drilling contractor to run a near-term drill program (2,500–4,000 metres) at its Timmins Nickel Project, focusing on a north block near Canada Nickel’s Crawford and Reid discoveries. The release is about exploration activity with no production or reserve updates. | Surge Battery Metals began drilling at its 80%-owned Hard Nickel 4 project in central British Columbia. Early core results show strongly altered peridotite with elevated nickel using portable XRF, with planned magnetic separation to test for awaruite. Follow-up drilling aims to confirm continuity of the nickel-bearing unit. | Participants at Antaike’s nickel and cobalt conference highlighted uncertainty for 2023 but noted bullish pressure in Chinese nickel sulfate markets. Tight current nickel sulfate supply is pushing prices higher, while expectations of increased feedstock supply from Indonesia may ease later. SHFE deliverable-brand decisions and LME Russia-material guidance add volatility.

First Quantum restarted fixed-loop electromagnetic surveys over the Money nickel-copper-PGE prospect in Western Australia and plans additional drilling targets. Prior 12 holes reported nickel and copper grades, and management highlights similarities to China’s Jinchuan deposit. Results are expected next year. | First Quantum restarts FLEM surveys at Dreadnought Resources’ Money nickel-copper-PGE prospect in Western Australia under a 70% earn-in. The survey aims to identify shallow massive sulphides after prior drilling returned meaningful nickel and copper grades. Additional drill targets and results are expected later next year. | SMM reports nickel prices are less supported as high prices significantly contain demand across the nickel industry chain. Although SHFE nickel pushed up absolute spot prices, spot premiums declined and transactions were muted. Stainless steel mills limited buying due to losses and weak terminal demand, pressuring nickel sentiment. | China’s largest nickel trader, Lygend Resources & Technology, received approval to list in Hong Kong after filing an updated IPO prospectus. The company plans to raise funds to expand production, citing expected growth in EV-related demand and sales. The development is potentially supportive for near-term nickel market sentiment.

Nickel Asia Corp. reported attributable net income of P6.90 billion for the first nine months of 2022, up 12% year on year. Revenues increased 2% to P21.51 billion, largely driven by higher nickel ore prices, indicating improved nickel-linked pricing conditions for the company.

NickelX intersected a 153-metre-wide zone of massive sulphides in its first drilling hole at the Cosmos South nickel project in Western Australia. The sulphides include pyrrhotite/marcasite with lesser chalcopyrite; intervals will be assayed for nickel and gold. Drilling targets conductive EM anomalies near existing nickel operations. | SMM reports weekly high-grade NPI discount to refined nickel at 600 yuan/mtu, widening versus last week. Spot pure nickel premiums fell as pure nickel rose and futures gained more. Military alloy demand held, civil demand weakened, and limited NPI inquiries plus losses for stainless/NPI producers support stable NPI prices and an expanding NPI–nickel spread. | Nickel prices jumped briefly to their limit as a report of an explosion or blast spooked the market. Such events raise concerns about disruptions to nickel supply or operations, prompting traders to bid prices higher over the immediate trading period. | LME nickel surged 12% (and briefly ~15% to $30,960/t) on low volumes, narrowly avoiding a 15% daily limit that would trigger a disruption event. The move lacked clear nickel-specific news, instead reflecting weaker dollar, macro optimism, and stop-loss/fund buying. Copper rose slightly; aluminium dipped modestly amid stable Russian metal acceptability. | Reports of a possible U.S. ban or sanctions on Russian aluminum imports are cited as a bullish catalyst. The article says aluminum prices spiked after the White House consideration became public and could extend gains if restrictions are enacted. It also links Russian supply share to nickel and copper risk.

A Tesla-backed nickel miner is reducing output after a waste dam leak. The incident raises short-term supply concerns by disrupting mining operations and potential remediation and regulatory response, which can tighten nickel availability expectations over the coming days and pressure nickel prices lower. | Canada Nickel reported successful pilot plant testing at its Crawford nickel project, showing improved iron and chromium recoveries and higher iron grade in magnetite concentrate, plus nickel recovery to the magnetic concentrate. The results support the project’s downstream steel/ferrroalloy value proposition and could be mildly bullish for nickel sentiment.

SMM reports ferronickel prices rose in October as stainless steel inventories depleted and mills resumed production, lifting ferronickel demand. Imports fell due to overseas smelter cuts, while Indonesian NPI supply also stayed tight. Markets fear Indonesia may soon raise ferronickel/NPI export tariffs, providing support and limiting upside. | Indonesia’s investment minister proposed an OPEC-like bloc of top nickel producers at talks with Canada, seeking coordinated policies and “fair added value” for nickel. With Indonesia also banning raw ore exports since 2020, the initiative signals potential supply-management, supporting expectations for higher nickel prices near term. | Finnish Customs data show imports of Russian nickel into Finland rose sharply after Russia’s invasion. Finland’s minister for European affairs said the government is ready to ramp up sanctions and support an EU nickel ban if consensus is reached, noting sanctions act gradually. | Xinhua reports that China-backed IMIP and Tsingshan’s Weda Bay industrial park in Indonesia are expanding nickel-focused processing and supporting ferronickel and battery-material supply. It cites large-scale investment, infrastructure improvements, and cleaner HPAL production, which boosts capacity expectations for Indonesia’s nickel industry. | Indonesia’s investment ministry says it proposed to Canada creating an OPEC-style group of major nickel producers to coordinate nickel commodity policies and value addition. The idea follows earlier comments about potentially forming a cartel to manage nickel supply.
