Indonesia is the world’s biggest nickel miner, so coordination hopes can support nickel sentiment. | The piece highlights renewed turmoil in nickel trading on the LME, noting that nickel plunges as illiquid trading conditions spark fresh market disruption. Such LME liquidity stress typically drives rapid repricing, implying clear downward pressure on nickel prices over the next days. | The article headline highlights heightened volatility in nickel prices, noting a sharp plunge after a 40% rise over the prior two weeks. The framing implies a rapid reversal and near-term downward pressure on nickel, consistent with a bearish market reaction in the first days. | The article title says the LME is stepping up scrutiny of nickel trading as nickel prices swing widely. This can influence short-term market positioning and expectations by affecting how trades are conducted or monitored. However, the provided content lacks details on policy measures or physical-market impacts. | The article examines whether nickel’s recent rally can sustain amid thin trading volumes and potential breakdown risk, alongside ongoing pressure in stainless spot pricing. It also highlights expectations for the WTO’s final findings on Indonesia’s nickel ore export ban and notes the LME’s decision not to restrict Russian metal as a key variable for sentiment.

Xinhua describes how China-invested industrial parks in Indonesia (IMIP and IWIP) are scaling ferronickel and battery-material related processing, employing tens of thousands of workers and adding infrastructure. By expanding nickel production capability in major supply regions, the article points to higher future nickel availability, weighing on prices. | Cyprium Metals reported anomalous copper and nickel mineralisation from reverse-circulation drilling at the Cue project in Western Australia, including long intercepts of copper and a nickel interval. The firm is also working to restart its Nifty copper mine, but no near-term production changes are announced.  | Cyprium Metals reported anomalous mineralisation from RC drilling at its Cue project, including significant copper and nickel intercepts, plus some gold and zinc. It also reiterated work toward financing and restarting its Nifty copper project, which has existing infrastructure and planned H1 FY2024 copper plate production. | Antilles Gold confirmed high-grade copper porphyry mineralisation at its El Pilar oxide-sulphide project in Cuba, reporting a 134m intercept with strong copper grades and open depth, plus preliminary gold results. Assays are preliminary pending replication at a certified lab, with further drilling planned; no production impact yet. | Xinhua reports that Chinese firms (including Tsingshan) have built and expanded Indonesia’s nickel industrial parks, notably IMIP and Weda Bay/WIIP, in regions like Morowali and North Maluku. It describes ferronickel/HPAL-related production lines, infrastructure, and green power/processing plans, implying increased future nickel supply. | Palladium One reports grab samples from its Canalask nickel-copper-PGE project in Yukon with assays up to 2.1% Ni and 6.17% Cu, alongside a drone magnetometer survey refining an ultramafic dyke target. Permitting is underway for systematic drilling of historic EM targets. | LME nickel trading has become highly volatile: three-month nickel briefly surged above $30,000 and breached the new daily limit before falling sharply to about $25,800. The LME raised initial margins and increased surveillance amid shrinking liquidity. Supply uncertainty around Russian nickel and market conditions are key drivers of the swings. | Canada’s government source says the country is “very unlikely” to join an OPEC-like group aimed at coordinating nickel supply. The comment suggests less probability of producer-led supply discipline, but it is not an immediate policy action, so any effect on short-term nickel prices is likely limited. | Vale and GM signed a term sheet for long-term supply of battery-grade nickel sulfate from Vale’s proposed Bécancour, Quebec plant, equivalent to 25,000 tonnes per year of contained nickel for GM’s Ultium nickel-based cathodes. Deliveries are targeted to start in 2H 2026, supporting nickel demand outlook. | General Motors agreed to purchase nickel sulfate from Vale to help power electric vehicles. The contract signals firmer demand for nickel feedstock, which can tighten expectations for near-term supply and provide upward support for nickel prices over the next several days to a few weeks.

Vale Canada will supply GM with battery-grade nickel sulfate from a proposed Quebec plant starting in 2H 2026, enough refined nickel for up to 350,000 EVs per year. GM says North American nickel helps EVs qualify for U.S. clean energy tax credits, supporting EV-driven nickel demand expectations. | Vale announced its first large-scale EV battery deal in North America with General Motors, supplying nickel for a range of GM vehicles. The agreement is described as potentially worth $760M per year and includes deliveries of 25,000 tonnes, signaling firmer nickel demand outlook. | Vale agreed to supply GM with 25,000 tonnes per year of battery-grade nickel sulfate from a proposed Quebec plant beginning in 2026. The nickel will feed GM cathodes for about 350,000 EVs annually and help GM qualify for U.S. clean-energy tax credits under the IRA, boosting near-term nickel sentiment. | Indonesia’s minister proposed an Opec-like group of nickel suppliers to coordinate policies and boost downstream refining for EV-battery nickel. Australia’s industry body opposes supply-constricting cartels, and Canada is unlikely to join. Still, the plan highlights bargaining power and potential supply coordination, supporting nickel price expectations. | Eco-Business reports China-backed investment—up to $2 billion via a green fund—aimed at expanding Indonesia’s EV battery and nickel value chain. With Indonesia poised to increase refined nickel supply to meet China’s rising EV demand, the news is supportive for nickel market expectations over the coming weeks. | Indonesia’s plan to create an OPEC-like group to coordinate nickel output and pricing faces hurdles because production is largely controlled by private companies rather than governments. Analysts say China-linked firms hold major stakes in Indonesia’s nickel industry, limiting control. Nickel demand growth is noted, but no immediate policy change is confirmed. | GM signed a term sheet with Vale to supply battery-grade nickel sulfate sourced from a proposed Canadian plant in Bécancour. The nickel sulfate equals about 25,000 metric tons per year and will be used in Ultium battery cells. The deal targets lower battery cell costs and a greener supply chain, supporting future nickel demand.

The news roundup reports progress on several mining companies, including Poseidon Nickel’s positive bankable feasibility study for the Black Swan nickel project and Blackstone Minerals’ drilling confirming continuity and high grades of nickel/PGE at Ta Khoa. It also notes Dundas Minerals exploring nickel and copper, supporting modest near-term sentiment. | Battery-grade nickel sulphate prices in China rose week-on-week, supported by higher nickel raw material costs and continued tight supply. Some nickel salt plants using LME nickel benchmarks reported losses, though MHP-linked production improved after a nickel futures dip. Terminal demand weakened, but prices are expected to remain stable.  | Wallbridge Mining has sold all its Quebec and Ontario nickel assets, rights, and obligations to Archer Exploration, including a 100% stake in the Grasset nickel sulphide project. Wallbridge will receive Archer shares and retain a 2% net smelter royalty, with the rationale to refocus on gold. | The piece argues that rising USD and easing China zero-COVID expectations are lifting copper, while aluminium gains are supported by hopes for China reopening and LME decisions on Russian metal sanctions. Nickel is described as in a short-term bull market with breakout moves amplified by LME liquidity after a short squeeze, though Indonesia supply risks loom.

SMM reports nickel spot premiums widened/rebounded as SHFE nickel rose in the morning. Jinchuan nickel premium averaged 4,250 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL averaged 2,450 yuan/mt. Nickel briquette prices increased, but spot and briquette trading were described as weak, still priced at premiums to nickel sulphate. | Universal Copper reports completion of 3D geological modeling at its Poplar copper project, identifying higher-grade copper-gold-molybdenum-silver targets and citing recent drill intercepts. The work is intended to guide exploration drilling in 2023, but the release does not imply near-term changes in copper supply or operations. | Power Nickel says it has closed new financing adding over $4 million and plans to expand its drilling program at the Nisk high-grade nickel sulfide project from about 5,000 metres to 12,500–15,000 metres. The update supports sentiment for future nickel supply visibility. | Power Nickel closed a CAD$4.19 million financing and will expand its drilling at the Nisk high-grade nickel sulfide project from 5,000 metres to an additional 7,500–10,000 metres, continuing through mid-December and resuming mid-January. Assay results are expected starting next week, supporting nickel sentiment in the near term.

SMM reports Nov 23 nickel market conditions: Jinchuan nickel spot premiums narrowed to 3,600–4,000 yuan/mt and NORNICKEL premiums to 2,300–2,500 yuan/mt. Despite lower premiums, absolute spot prices stay high, with slack spot trading. Steel mill demand is weak, limiting nickel briquette transaction activity.

Québec Nickel Corp. announced a private placement and flow-through share offering raising up to $10 million to fund working capital and Canadian exploration expenditures for its nickel projects in Québec. The release details deal terms and timing but does not indicate immediate changes to nickel output or market supply-demand conditions.

The headline reports that SK On plans to build a nickel production facility in Indonesia in partnership with EcoPro and China’s GEM. The announcement relates to future nickel supply capacity and could modestly lift market expectations for nickel availability, supporting prices in the short term while production timing remains uncertain. | Los Cerros acquired nickel-copper projects in Papua New Guinea from Footprint Resources and plans diamond drilling and geophysical work. Historical results include a trench assay with 2.6% copper and float nickel sulphide up to 45.8%. The move is exploratory and meant to attract JV partners, not to signal near-term production changes. | Los Cerros acquired five nickel-copper projects in Papua New Guinea via Footprint Resources and plans a 3,000m drill program at Kusi and geophysical work at Veri Veri, targeting high-grade nickel sulphide and historical copper-bearing assays. The news boosts exploration activity but offers no immediate supply impact for benchmark copper or nickel prices. | SK On will build a nickel MHP facility in Indonesia with EcoPro and GEM, aiming for 30,000 tons of high-purity nickel starting Q3 2024. MHP is used to produce nickel sulfate and EV battery precursors. The project also seeks oxidized nickel ore supply, potentially supportive for nickel. | Technology Minerals reports grab-sample assay results from its Aramo project in Asturias, Spain, including up to 28% copper, 1.88% cobalt, and 1.68% nickel. The company completed a 3D laser survey for underground mapping. The news is exploration-positive, potentially improving short-term sentiment for copper and nickel. | SMM reports Chinese nickel ore inventories fell by 98,000 wmt WoW to 8.94 million wmt as of Nov 25, with port inventory down to 4.68 million wmt. Low supply and reduced shipments persist, while NPI plants buy less due to lower NPI prices. Gradual further declines are expected. | GEM, EcoPro, and SK On will jointly build and operate an HPAL nickel plant in Indonesia’s Morowali. The facility targets 30,000 tonnes of nickel per year from mixed hydroxide precipitate. It aims to secure stable nickel feedstock and meet US IRA sourcing requirements, supporting market confidence in nickel supply. | Lygend Resources & Technology plans a HK IPO raising about $470m, using 56% of proceeds to develop its Indonesia HPAL nickel hydrometallurgy project. The project targets large nickel-cobalt compound capacity and has prior phase commissioning, with full lines expected by Q4 2023, potentially supporting nickel production outlook.

Power Nickel reports encouraging drill assay results from the Nisk project, including high-grade nickel-copper-cobalt sulfide and PGE-bearing mineralization. Intercepts extend mineralization further at depth and eastward, and drilling will be expanded. As exploration progresses, this can modestly support nickel and copper sentiment. | The piece links rising nickel prices to stronger demand for electric-vehicle batteries, referencing Elon Musk’s remarks on which battery minerals matter most. While details are limited, the stated demand-driven move implies higher near-term nickel pricing pressure tied to EV battery market expectations. | EV Nickel reported assay results from four additional Phase 3 drill holes at its Shaw Dome Project, confirming thick dunite-hosted nickel mineralization in the CarLang A Zone (e.g., ~0.25–0.26% Ni over long intercepts). The company expects remaining assays shortly and a preliminary resource estimate in Q1 2023. | Global Energy Metals reported encouraging assay results from initial field sampling at its Nevada Lovelock project, including copper up to 26.3% and nickel up to 0.309%, plus cobalt. The update is exploration-focused with no production or confirmed reserves, so any short-term price effect is likely limited and sentiment-driven. | Global Energy Metals released sampling results from its Nevada Lovelock project, reporting assays up to 26.3% copper and 0.309% nickel, alongside cobalt. The update is based on limited surface rock sampling and recommends further mapping and potential drilling. It signals exploration potential but does not affect near-term supply. | Metal Energy expands drilling at the Manibridge nickel sulphide mine in Manitoba, reporting prior high-grade nickel intercepts (assays pending) and planning further 10,000-metre drilling. The nickel belt is described as underexplored, supporting positive sentiment for nickel supply prospects, with minor stated copper co-product potential. | Canada’s trade minister rejects Indonesia’s proposal to join a “nickel cartel” modelled on OPEC. The piece notes Indonesia’s large nickel role in EV batteries and Canada’s lower-carbon, battery-grade nickel ambitions. While Canada will cooperate on trade, its refusal lowers the chance of supply coordination. | BNN Bloomberg reports the LME says it prevented nickel from falling into a $20 billion “death spiral.” Such an intervention would likely ease market stress and support expectations for nickel prices over the coming days as risk premia and liquidation pressure recede.

SMM reports sluggish NPI trading as nickel ore supply tightens in the rainy season, keeping ore prices firm. Weak demand from steel mills and higher processing costs lead to slight inventory build and rangebound-to-lower NPI prices. If plants can’t secure cheaper ore, they may suspend production for maintenance. | Palladium One reported high-grade intercepts at its Tyko Project’s West Pickle discovery, including 10.4% nickel and 3.4% copper over 2.3 meters, and resumed drilling with a second rig. The news supports exploration success for nickel and copper, but offers no immediate production change. | Usha Resources filed and mailed an information circular for a proposed spin-out of its Nicobat nickel-copper project into Formation Metals. The release describes recent drilling confirming high-grade nickel-copper shoots and outlines transaction conditions. While Ni and Cu are mentioned via grades, it is primarily corporate restructuring, not a market-moving supply or demand change. | Xander Resources began its first diamond drilling program at the Timmins Nickel Project in Ontario, targeting geophysical conductors near Canada Nickel’s Reid Discovery and other ultramafic-related sulphide trends. Drilling covers 2,500–4,000 metres and aims to test high-priority anomalies that could expand nickel resource expectations. | Murchison Minerals announced drillhole BDF22-002 results from its Barre de Fer zone, reporting 121.2 m at 1.36% NiEq (including higher-grade intervals) and associated copper-equivalent grades in nickel sulphide mineralization. The update supports exploration potential but does not affect near-term production or inventories. | Copper prices rose 1.1% on Tuesday as investors bet China’s COVID protests could force faster easing of curbs, boosting growth and metals demand expectations. Easier property developer financing also supported the metals-intensive sector. The yuan’s strength made dollar metals cheaper for Chinese buyers, lifting LME copper. Aluminium and nickel rose too. | Palladium One reported drill results featuring grades of 10.4% nickel and 3.4% copper over 2.3 metres. Such assay announcements can lift market expectations for the potential resource base and future supply of nickel and copper, providing modest near-term support to prices. | Palladium One reports drill results including 10.4% nickel and 3.4% copper over 2.3 meters. Such high-grade findings can support market expectations for future nickel and copper output, potentially adding modest upside pressure to prices in the near term.

Indonesia’s President Jokowi says he will appeal a WTO ruling against Indonesia’s nickel ore export ban. The outcome could influence nickel ore export flows and supply expectations for later-stage processing, weighing on nickel prices if markets expect improved availability. | SMM reports the November alloy-sector PMI stayed above 50 (50.24), with production and new orders holding up. SHFE nickel trended higher in November, supported by a softer US CPI and persistent demand for refined nickel from military alloy uses. December PMI is expected to soften slightly, though military orders stay stable. | SMM reports November’s final PMI for nickel wire and nickel mesh at 50.17, above 50, with orders slightly higher than October as downstream textile/furniture operating rates recovered after the National Day holiday. December PMI is estimated higher, supported by a 0.25% RRR cut and real-estate financing support. | Indonesia says it will appeal a WTO ruling that found it violated trade rules over its 2020 nickel ore export ban. The EU sued Indonesia, alleging the ban. Indonesia maintains its downstream policy to develop nickel processing. The appeal keeps uncertainty around ore supply tight, supporting nickel sentiment. | CanAlaska reported assay results from the first 14 holes of its Manibridge Phase 2 drill program confirming nickel-copper-cobalt mineralization. Intersections include ~0.8% Ni over 20m and ~0.6% Ni over ~20–25m near the historic Manibridge Nickel Mine. Further drilling is planned and two assays are pending. | Indonesia says it will appeal a WTO ruling against its 2020 nickel ore export ban after the EU’s complaint. The government argues downstream processing is needed for jobs and investment. The appeal keeps regulatory uncertainty over nickel ore exports, potentially constraining supply and supporting nickel prices near term. | The report says the Goro nickel mine faces tighter regulation after a tailings leak. Additional compliance requirements can increase the risk of operational disruptions, delays, or reduced output. In the near term, this would likely heighten nickel supply concerns and pressure prices lower. | Indonesia’s Jokowi said the country will appeal a WTO ruling against its 2014 nickel ore export ban. The WTO found Indonesia failed to justify the ban as preventing critical shortages. While the decision disrupted EU supply, Indonesia plans to maintain downstreaming policy to attract processing investment.

Horizonte Minerals PLC said it has been awarded a port contract for its Araguaia nickel project. The contract can improve logistics and shipping arrangements tied to nickel operations, which may slightly strengthen expectations for nickel supply readiness. The magnitude and timing of any production impact remain uncertain. | Canada Nickel reported positive drilling assays at its Reid property, including a higher-grade interval (0.29% Ni over 325.5m, with 0.35% Ni over 81.5m) and continued mineralization in multiple holes. It also announced a new discovery at Sothman with all five holes intersecting mineralization (assays pending). | Gungnir Resources reported additional near-surface drilling at its Lappvattnet nickel project in northern Sweden, including 7.0 metres of 1.16% nickel and intersections suggesting a new footwall nickel target. Assays for several holes remain pending. The release provides resource context but no immediate production changes.

SMM says nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 23,000 wmt WoW to 8.92 million wmt, with Philippines shipment outflows declining and inflows slowing. Meanwhile, NPI plants faced losses from weaker NPI prices and steel mills cut output, reducing ore demand. Stocks may decline only modestly short term, with imports elsewhere still sizable. | Poseidon Nickel Ltd said it is fully funded and progressing toward a decision to proceed with its Black Swan nickel project. While it does not indicate immediate production, moving the project forward can increase expectations of future nickel supply, which may weigh on nickel prices over the coming days to weeks.

Copper rose to a three-week high as more Chinese cities eased COVID curbs, lifting expectations for fuller economic reopening. A weaker U.S. dollar also supported LME prices. The article notes aluminium and nickel gains as well, reflecting improved short-term demand sentiment for industrial metals. | Giga Metals said it will exit copper exploration in Piaui State, Brazil, because the program has not generated enough support to justify ongoing budgets. The company plans to focus instead on its Turnagain nickel project, while previous work targeted sediment-hosted copper mineralization. Near-term price effects are likely minimal. | EV Nickel Inc. announced a up to C$1.0 million non-brokered flow-through private placement to fund exploration of its Shaw Dome nickel project in Ontario. Proceeds will be used for Canadian flow-through mining expenditures. No production changes, mine disruptions, or trade/policy measures affecting nickel prices were mentioned.

Copper prices gained as China began easing COVID-19 curbs, boosting hopes for demand recovery in the top consumer. LME three-month copper rose 0.5% and Shanghai January copper edged slightly lower. Gains were limited by signs of softer imported copper demand and slowing inventory withdrawals, while the dollar stayed steady.

EV Nickel reports assay results from three additional Phase 3 holes at its Shaw Dome “CarLang A Zone,” intersecting thick peridotite-dunite hosting nickel with grades around 0.25–0.26% Ni. The company says results confirm continuity along a portion of the trend and will feed a preliminary resource estimate in Q1 2023. | Ring of Fire Metals (Wyloo Metals’ renamed unit) signed an MoU with Webequie First Nation to collaborate on exploration and negotiations for the proposed Eagle’s Nest mine in Ontario’s Ring of Fire. The deposit is described as high-grade nickel-copper, but the agreement signals partnership framework rather than near-term output. | The article says nickel prices showed a short-term uptrend in December and could see bullish upside this month, supported by the continuation of a range breakout and improving demand expectations from China reopening. Risks include weak stainless demand, recession concerns, and downside from Indonesia’s nickel ore export ban being lifted. | The headline reports that Vale plans to split off its metals group to better capitalize on battery demand. Because the move is framed around battery-linked metals, including nickel and copper, it may boost sentiment for near-term supply and demand expectations, supporting prices even if immediate physical effects are unclear.

Vale plans to split its base metals operations into a separate entity to ensure nickel and copper assets receive dedicated funding. Management expects accelerating battery/EV demand and a premium for battery-grade nickel, projecting higher production. The restructuring aims to attract capital and optimize capacity, supporting bullish sentiment for both metals. | Vale said it will ring-fence its nickel and copper operations into a new Vale Base Metals unit, with Canadian, Indonesian and Brazilian assets, and plans to name a strategic partner in the first half of next year. The move aims to attract capital for base-metal investments as EV battery demand rises. | Vale plans to split off its base metals operations into a separate entity, a move aimed at ensuring nickel and copper assets receive sustained capital. Executives cite rising battery and EV demand, highlight North American production, and set ambitious nickel and copper output targets requiring major investment. | Vale said it will ring-fence and split out its copper and nickel assets into Vale Base Metals, including Canadian and Brazilian mines, and later select a strategic partner. The move accompanies plans for major base-metal investment as EV battery demand rises, supporting improved short-term sentiment for copper and nickel supply outlooks.

London and Shanghai copper prices rose to multi-month highs as China eased COVID-19 curbs and property support improved demand expectations. Goldman lifted its copper price forecast and cited tightening visible stocks. Non-ferrous prices broadly moved higher, with aluminium and nickel also up, supported by a weaker dollar. | SMM reports Dec 9 bonded-zone nickel inventories rising to 6,300 mt, including 1,370 mt of briquettes and 4,930 mt of nickel plate. Import spot losses climbed to 30,000 yuan/mt as domestic-outside futures spreads failed to narrow and the SHFE/LME ratio fell. With weak customs clearance and rigid domestic demand, some nickel was redirected to ports. | SMM said Chinese nickel ore inventories fell 29,000 wmt to 8.89 million wmt as the Philippines remains in rainy season and port stock declines. Total nickel content is about 70,000 mt. NPI demand is weaker and some plants will reduce output in December, keeping nickel ore prices trending lower. | Québec Nickel Corp. announced closing the first tranche of a brokered private placement, raising about $5.6m to fund working capital and nickel-related exploration at its Québec properties. The news is financing-driven with no production, supply, or inventory changes stated, limiting short-term impact on nickel prices. | The piece says LME nickel’s benchmark status remains impaired months after the March trading meltdown, with liquidity sliding, high volatility, and abnormal pricing versus Shanghai. Low deliverable nickel supplies mean the contract still matters, but investors have largely stayed away, keeping market confidence fragile. | Vale plans to separate its base metals operations from its iron ore business, covering nickel assets in Canada and copper/nickel production elsewhere. Management says the move will attract capital and better position Vale to meet expected EV and battery demand, with nickel and copper receiving more focused investment. | Vale plans to split its base metals business from its iron ore unit in mid-2023, isolating nickel assets including Sudbury and Voisey’s Bay. Executives cite surging battery-driven demand for nickel and copper, alongside major planned capex to expand output, supporting a bullish near-term sentiment for both metals.

Copper prices fell from near six-month highs as the U.S. dollar firmed on stronger-than-expected U.S. producer inflation, raising concerns about higher-for-longer Fed rates. Markets also turned cautious ahead of central bank meetings. LME aluminium and SHFE nickel also dropped alongside copper. | Copper fell on the LME and Comex as concerns grew that China’s economy will weaken due to spreading COVID cases and ongoing factory curbs. Uncertainty over whether the Fed and other central banks will slow rate hikes added pressure. The same macro concerns weighed on aluminium and nickel. | Indonesia has appealed a WTO ruling that backed the EU in a dispute over Jakarta’s ban on nickel ore exports and related domestic processing requirements. The EU argued Indonesia’s restrictions harmed its stainless-steel industry, while Indonesia banned nickel ore exports from early 2020. The appeal could prolong uncertainty over supply restrictions.

London copper edged up 0.2% to $8,393/tonne, supported by China property shares and data on weekly new home sales. However, surging COVID-19 infections raise concerns about manufacturing disruption. SHFE January copper fell. Peru protests near Las Bambas add supply uncertainty as markets watch central banks. | Indonesia has appealed a WTO ruling related to a dispute over its nickel ore export ban. The appeal keeps uncertainty around whether and when export restrictions will change, which can alter near-term nickel ore supply flows to refiners and thereby influence nickel price expectations over coming days to weeks.

SMM reports that mid- and high-grade nickel ore prices rose slightly in November on low arrivals and scarce supply, partly offset by lower ocean freight. However, poor demand and falling NPI prices have weakened momentum. For December, SMM expects nickel ore prices to fall slightly, with Ni 1.5% ranging $70–75/wmt.

SMM reports that in November nickel sulphate supply was in a small surplus despite sluggish downstream demand. A battery-grade deficit turned into a surplus due to weaker demand and delayed downstream purchases alongside ternary producers dumping inventories. For December, precursor output is set to fall and nickel salt output only slightly, leaving a surplus that pressures prices. | Canada Nickel says it has filed the Detailed Project Description and related responses to advance the federal impact assessment process for its Crawford nickel sulphide project, following prior permitting steps and consultations. The company also hired Deutsche Bank and Scotiabank to advise on equity financing alternatives, targeting permits by mid-2025. | SHFE nickel prices continued to decline, with spot refined nickel and briquettes lower as demand remained weak. Inventories rose in some areas and LME stocks increased. While macro improved modestly after weaker-than-expected US CPI, downstream stainless steel and alloy consumption stayed soft, limiting upside. | KoBold Metals plans a $150m investment in Zambia’s Mingomba copper-cobalt project, including a $115m payment tied to stakes and $35m for exploration. It aims to develop the high-grade deposit using AI-enabled drilling analysis. Zambia targets much higher copper output by 2032, but near-term production impact is unclear. | The report’s headline says the U.S. sanctioned Russia’s richest tycoon and Nornickel chief Potanin. Because Nornickel is a key nickel supplier, the sanctions could disrupt operations, financing, or trade-related access, likely tightening perceived near-term nickel availability and weighing on nickel prices.

SMM reports tight spot pure nickel supply in Shanghai as the import window remains closed. Jinchuan nickel premiums rise to 8,000–8,500 yuan/mt and NORNICKEL premiums to 6,000–6,500 yuan/mt. Nickel briquette prices also tick up, though spot trading remains weak due to relative pricing. | The press review says the US blacklisted Vladimir Potanin and related entities, but spared Norilsk Nickel via a special clause to avoid disrupting the global non-ferrous metals market. It notes that analysts expect limited near-term effects on nickel prices, with demand support from stainless and batteries in China. | SMM says bonded-zone nickel inventory rose to 8,100 mt as of Dec 16, with nickel briquette at 1,370 mt and nickel plate at 6,730 mt. From Nov 1, spot imports suffered amid a low SHFE/LME nickel ratio. The export window then opened, prompting nickel plate shipments to ports and lifting bonded stocks this week. | Norilsk Nickel’s board unanimously approved its 2023 budget, with CAPEX projected to rise 10% to about $4.7bn. The investment programme supports mining, infrastructure maintenance in the Norilsk Industrial District, equipment replacement and capital repairs, alongside initiatives to address operational challenges from partner refusals. | The report says hedge funds suffered $95 million in losses amid an LME nickel crisis, indicating significant stress in nickel trading. While the excerpt does not provide underlying fundamentals or price moves, the reference to a “crisis” implies near-term negative sentiment and risk to nickel prices.

SMM reports pure nickel futures on SHFE have declined, but spot nickel premiums stayed broadly stable because spot supply remains tight. Jinchuan and Nornickel premiums increased slightly day-on-day, while nickel briquette prices dropped and remained higher than nickel sulphate, weakening briquette spot transactions. | Nickel ore trading was flat but held at high levels as rainy-season supply tightness persisted. Philippine mines maintained high FOB asking prices, while NPI spot prices rose with raw-material tightness. Downstream nickel processors reduced output in December, tempering demand, yet Ni 1.5% ore is expected to remain around $68–72/wmt short term. | Canada Nickel announced it will acquire the past producing Texmont nickel property and reported early drilling at Texmont and higher-grade targeting at Sothman, with assays pending. Historic resources include 3.2 Mt at 0.9% nickel and Sothman at 1.24% nickel, implying potential for near-term open-pit production. | The PRNewswire piece promotes a report on global nickel mining, citing historical and forecast nickel mine production by country (e.g., growth in Indonesia, Colombia, New Caledonia) and declines in South Africa, the Philippines, and Finland. It also discusses reserves, major projects, and demand drivers, but lacks near-term market-moving events.

Nickel Creek Platinum says nickel prices should rise amid a perceived nickel supply shortage for EV growth. It highlights the Nickel Shäw Project in Yukon, recent drilling intersecting near-surface massive/semi-massive nickel-copper sulphides, and completed studies toward saleable nickel/copper concentrate. It anticipates a prefeasibility study in 2023. | Canada Nickel will acquire a 100% interest in the former Texmont nickel property near Timmins, Ontario, between its Deloro and Sothman assets. The company frames the move as providing near-term smaller scale opportunities, which can modestly improve near-term nickel supply expectations. | SMM reports that spot pure nickel supply remained tight on Dec 20, pushing Jinchuan premiums to an average of 10,250 yuan/mt (+1,900) and NORNICKEL premiums to 7,750 yuan/mt (+1,300). Despite rigid demand, transactions stayed thin, reinforcing bullish short-term nickel tightness. | LME data showed nickel inventories increased to 54,360 mt, a three-and-a-half-month high after a prior decline. SHFE nickel inventories fell sharply on Dec 16 but overall fluctuated. The piece notes that declining inventories usually support futures, implying the LME build is a short-term bearish signal for nickel. | Canada Nickel agreed to acquire the past-producing Texmont mine in Canada for cash and a promissory note, with the option to restart operations. The company is drilling to update resources and assess whether output could support a restart by 2025 or provide higher-grade feed for its Crawford nickel project, lifting nickel sentiment. | Russian miner Nornickel is considering a 10% output cut next year. Because Nornickel is a key nickel producer, the prospect of lower supply can tighten expected market balances and lift nickel price sentiment within weeks as traders price in reduced future availability.

SMM says SHFE spot premiums for Jinchuan nickel averaged 10,750 yuan/mt on Dec 21, up 500 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL premiums were flat at 7,750 yuan/mt. It attributes strength to tight pure nickel supply and traders awaiting the import window, with nickel briquette premiums also increasing. | SMM reports China’s November nickel ore imports fell 12% MoM to 4.17 million mt, with laterite nickel ore down 12.5% MoM, driven by rainy-season restrictions on Philippine mine ROM output. NPI prices slid toward cost, pressuring producers and limiting ore buying. December imports are expected to decline further. | Vior signed a letter of intent with Soquem for a 50% option interest in its Skyfall nickel project in Quebec, including $2.5 million in exploration spending and $350,000 in cash payments over two years. The update supports ongoing nickel exploration but signals no near-term production impact. | Vior signed a letter of intent with Soquem to advance its district-scale Skyfall nickel project. The update is geared toward progressing the development pipeline, which can modestly improve market expectations for future nickel supply. That typically supports nickel sentiment in the coming days as supply risks ease. | Vior Inc. signed a letter of intent with SOQUEM regarding its Skyfall nickel project in Quebec’s Abitibi Greenstone Belt. The deal suggests forward progress on developing new nickel supply. Although the announcement is at the LOI stage, it can still provide modest near-term support for nickel pricing sentiment. | EV Nickel Inc. closed a C$1.03 million non-brokered private placement, issuing flow-through units to fund exploration at its Shaw Dome nickel project in Ontario. Proceeds will be used for Canadian flow-through mining expenditures. The release signals ongoing exploration but does not indicate any near-term production or supply change.

Clive Palmer has sold Queensland’s Yabulu nickel and cobalt refinery to Swiss-based Zero Carbon Investek for an estimated $1–2 billion. The buyer plans to restart operations, aiming to employ hundreds and later add solar and battery power to decarbonize production. Sale is subject to regulatory approvals. | SMM reports China imported about 14,521 mt of refined nickel in Nov 2022, but customs clearance volumes for refined/pure nickel fell 13% month-on-month. The decline is linked to nickel import losses after the SHFE/LME ratio dropped, and rising bonded inventories. SMM expects further clearance decreases in December. | Premium Nickel Resources announced additional assay results from its Selebi Ni-Cu-Co sulphide mine in Botswana, including multiple drillhole intervals with reported nickel and copper grades and NiEq calculations based on LME prices. The update is exploratory/resource-expansion oriented and does not change near-term production or inventories, limiting short-term price impact. | Clive Palmer’s Queensland Nickel/Yabulu refinery was sold for an estimated $1–$2 billion to Zero Carbon Investek, with plans to restart operations and employ hundreds. The deal is framed as happening in more favourable conditions for nickel tied to battery demand, supporting near-term nickel sentiment.

Premium Nickel Resources reports drill results at its Selebi nickel-copper-cobalt project in Botswana support the idea that past-producing deposits connect at depth. The company is working to reopen the Selebi mine, which could improve perceived future nickel supply and modestly affect copper expectations due to co-product potential. | SMM reports SHFE nickel futures rising to a new high, tracking LME nickel’s gain after news that Norilsk Nickel may cut 2023 production by 10%. With low LME warrant inventories and the pure nickel import window still closed, supply concerns intensified and lifted nickel prices. | Reuters reports copper trading in a narrow range as recession fears and uncertain demand from China pressure prices, despite support from Chinese liquidity measures and easing COVID restrictions. China’s infection spike disrupts logistics and lowers operating rates. Strong U.S. data lifts the dollar. Aluminium and nickel also slip modestly. | SMM says Dec 23 bonded-zone nickel inventories reached 8,300 mt, with nickel briquette and plate inventories rising, while the SHFE/LME price ratio failed to improve. Import losses stayed around 10,000 yuan/mt, limiting pure nickel clearance. Salt makers dumped briquettes and demand weakened, increasing plate inventory. | Clive Palmer has sold Australia’s Yabulu nickel and cobalt refinery to Zero Carbon Investek for $1.3bn, raising funds and positioning a planned restart. The refinery has been on care-and-maintenance since 2016 and is expected to resume operations in about 18 months, with additional investment and renewable energy upgrades. | Class 1 Nickel reported progress on its Somanike drilling program in Quebec, with two active drills and assays pending. Drilling intersected sulphide mineralisation in 18 holes and identified geophysical anomalies for follow-up near the historic Marbridge nickel mine. Positive results may support near-term nickel and copper sentiment. | SMM says nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 81,000 wmt week-on-week to 9.04 million wmt, with total Ni content near 71,000 mt. Inventories at seven major ports also declined. Supply tightness from restricted Philippine shipments and cautious NPI buying are cited, with expectations for a slow inventory decline. | Copper prices edged higher as low inventories and hopes for economic recovery next year offset weaker near-term demand in China from a surge in COVID-19 cases. LME copper rose 0.5% on the day. ShFE copper stocks fell, limiting downside if demand improves. Nickel rose; aluminium fell. | The article headline says hedge funds lost a court fight seeking LME disclosure of communications tied to a nickel crisis. While this is directly connected to nickel market sentiment and transparency, the excerpt provides no specific new supply, trading, or regulatory information to indicate a clear short-term price move. | Fastmarkets says demand for nickel for EV batteries is set to rise sharply and is a key driver for the nickel sulfate battery-grade market. The article describes high nickel price volatility and production shifts in China toward intermediates like MHP, while briquette remains important for LME hedging access, reinforcing nickel’s near-term tightness expectations. | A High Court judge dismissed a case by hedge funds seeking information about the LME’s decision to cancel nickel trades in March, when nickel surged on fears related to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. The cancellations helped some shorts but hurt others, prompting ongoing lawsuits and appeal considerations.

SMM expects SHFE nickel to remain supported as pure nickel supply stays tight, keeping spot premiums high and Indonesian NPI nearly fully booked. Downstream demand for stainless steel/alloys is only slightly improving, and some restocking occurs ahead of Chinese New Year, supporting a bullish tone for nickel in the near term. | SMM reports Dec 27 spot nickel premiums: Jinchuan at about 8,500 yuan/mt and NORNICKEL around 7,150 yuan/mt, both lower than the prior day. Premiums eased as traders became more willing to ship late month, but spot transactions were thin because high futures prices and weak briquette liquidity limited buying. | Nickel 28’s chairman provides a year-end update tied to its Ramu Nickel operation and nickel sulphide royalties. It discusses 2022 nickel market dynamics, higher sulfur costs, expected nickel/cobalt production, and that debt repayment timing depends on nickel prices. It also references progress at Dumont and Turnagain projects.

PT GNI in North Morowali, Indonesia, faced a smelter incident after coal dust exploded, causing deaths and shutting two production lines for overhaul. Workers protested over safety and pay demands. The first phase of GNI is planned to reach production with multiple lines, but the accident implies short-term output losses. | The article argues nickel is likely to stay oversupplied through 2026 due to strong Indonesian output and weaker demand for stainless steel and nickel batteries. It notes quota changes have only stabilized prices, inventories are mixed (LME up, SHFE down), and soft China steel/EV trends weigh, despite risks from US-Iran energy bottlenecks. | SMM links the COVID-19 spread to softer overall demand, noting suppressed pure nickel demand because nickel prices remain high. Spot transactions are thin and Jinchuan and NORNICKEL nickel premiums fell. While NPI supply is relatively tight and SS futures rose, pure nickel demand cannot support prices. | SMM reviews 2022 nickel volatility and outlines 2023 risks. It cites falling LME inventories early in the year, later demand weakness, tight spot supply from a closed import window, and then expects a significant refined nickel and overall primary nickel surplus. Rising NPI supply and pessimistic macro could keep nickel prices downward. | SMM reports spot premiums for Jinchuan nickel fell to 7,000–8,000 yuan/mt, with the average down 1,000 yuan/mt day-on-day. Nornickel premiums also declined. Traders ship more as month-end approaches, but spot transactions stay thin due to high futures prices and weak demand for nickel briquettes. | Osisko Bermuda agreed to a revised deal involving a silver stream and a backstop copper stream to help fund Metals Acquisition Corp’s $1.1B purchase of the CSA copper-silver mine in New South Wales. The financing structure and copper-focused stream support near-term copper supply expectations and sentiment.

SMM said nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports rose by 141,000 wmt to 9.18 million wmt by Dec 30, with Ni content around 72,000 mt. Arrivals increased, and Philippine rainy-season shipments were reduced, but downstream demand remained weak. NPI plants ran at low rates, consuming ore slowly. | Clive Palmer’s Queensland Nickel refinery is set to be acquired by Switzerland-based Zero Carbon Investek for about $1.5 billion, contingent on Queensland port access and pending Foreign Investment Review Board approval. The move could enable restart-related supply improvements for nickel, supportive for near-term nickel market sentiment. | The Northern Miner reports that surging demand for battery metals is offsetting higher construction costs for PolyMet Mining’s copper-nickel NorthMet project in northeastern Minnesota. Updated estimates suggest the first phase of the open-pit operation remains economically supported despite cost inflation, which can improve near-term sentiment for both copper and nickel. | Estimates for PolyMet Mining’s NorthMet open-pit copper-nickel project in Minnesota suggest surging battery-metal demand is offsetting higher development costs. The first phase of the operation near Lake ... indicates investors see demand support for copper and nickel even as project expenses rise.

SMM reports that nickel-salt and nickel sulphate producers faced limited profits because high-cost nickel inputs and still-heavy high-priced inventories weighed on margins. Nickel sulphate firms stabilized some costs, but lower nickel sulphate prices cut profits further, while downstream ternary precursor buyers reduced purchase prices amid weak demand, leaving the market stalled. | GEM says the first phase of Qingmeibang Indonesia’s nickel resources project (30,000 mt Ni content annual capacity) has started operations. The project is ramping up and is expected to produce over 20,000 mt of nickel content in 2023. The update signals additional nickel supply entering the market.

SMM reports that imported NORNICKEL nickel losses expanded as LME nickel prices rose. Spot premiums for Jinchuan nickel fell to an average of 6,000 yuan/mt and NORNICKEL nickel to about 6,750 yuan/mt, both down sharply day-on-day. Nickel briquette trading stayed weak due to relative pricing versus nickel sulphate. | SMM reports nickel prices surged again, with SHFE and LME nickel hitting highs amid very low LME warrants and weak liquidity, driving speculation and extreme volatility. It warns that nickel-linked pricing for intermediates is becoming harder to trade. Nickel sulphate demand is weak and a surplus is expected in 2023. | Metal Energy reports completion of a 10,000-metre drill program and acquisition of full (100%) interest in the Manibridge nickel project in Manitoba. Drill holes intersected visible nickel sulphides, with some assay highlights and remaining results pending, supporting future development potential. It cites high recent nickel demand amid EV and storage growth. | Copper fell to two-month lows as traders prioritized demand fears from slowing global growth and rising COVID cases in China, outweighing dollar weakness. Aluminium also dropped on sliding energy prices that raise smelting economics. Nickel plunged alongside the broader base-metals sell-off. | Antam said its Haltim Ferronickel Plant in East Halmahera will start operating in the second semester of 2023 after power and construction phases progress. The plant’s annual capacity is 13,500 tonnes, lifting Antam’s ferronickel capacity to 40,500 tonnes per year, potentially increasing nickel supply expectations. | Global Commodities Holdings will launch a nickel trade platform with a nickel index intended to underpin future contracts competing with the LME. The initiative follows last March’s chaotic LME nickel trading suspension and seeks direct physical-market trading, potentially improving price discovery and sentiment for nickel in the coming weeks.

SHFE nickel prices fell for a second consecutive day, dropping about 5% in the session, as Tsingshan-related nickel matte supply grows and macro sentiment turns pessimistic on expectations of a Fed interest rate hike. Inventories abroad rose, while tightness in domestic stocks eased, keeping nickel weak near term. | Magna Mining reported additional drill results from the Crean Hill 101 Footwall Zone, including 31.1m at 4.0% Ni and also notable associated Cu grades. The company says results show continuity of high-grade massive sulphide mineralization and expects further assays. This is exploration-focused, not production-related. | Green River Gold Corp. reports additional assay and drilling results from the Quesnel Nickel Project, noting consistent nickel mineralization at depth at the Deep Purple Zone 2 target. Six more winter drill holes are planned, but the announcement is exploration-stage and does not indicate immediate production impacts for nickel.

Copper prices rose as expectations grow that China will support its ailing property sector, improving demand outlook and helping inventory restocking. LME copper was up 1.2%. Nickel fell sharply to a one-month low on supply expectations after Tsingshan’s new electronickel project started production. Aluminium also edged higher. | Copper prices rose on hopes China will add property-sector support measures, improving the near-term demand outlook. LME three-month copper and SHFE February copper both increased, while Chile production fell. In contrast, Shanghai nickel fell on supply expectations from Tsingshan’s new project starting production. Aluminium also edged up. | SMM says nickel ore inventories across Chinese ports fell 370,000 wmt to 8.81 million wmt as of Jan 6, with Ni content down to about 70,000 mt. Supply tightness from poor Philippines weather is cited, and further declines are expected, with demand possibly improving after Chinese New Year. | Copper rose to a three-week high on a weaker U.S. dollar and optimism that China will boost property demand. LME three-month copper climbed about 1.2%. The piece also references Chile output declines supporting copper, while nickel weakness reflects Tsingshan project-related supply expectations and aluminium advanced.

SMM reviews 2022 nickel price volatility tied to Russia-Ukraine risks, later COVID/macro effects, and changing supply-demand fundamentals. It notes lower LME/SHFE inventories but still highlights rising output and expected primary nickel surplus in 2023, mainly from oversupply of NPI, implying nickel prices likely fall overall. | Canada’s Impact Assessment Agency will require a full federal impact assessment for Canada Nickel’s Crawford Project. The review covers Indigenous and public comments and resumes a 180-day planning phase. The company highlights potential net-zero carbon capture, but the added regulatory steps introduce timeline uncertainty for nickel supply.

SMM reports sluggish spot trading in pure nickel as downstream companies stop stockpiling. SHFE nickel prices fell slightly despite higher premiums for Jinchuan and NORNICKEL nickel. Nickel briquette trading is also weak, and some traders are less willing to ship ahead of Chinese New Year logistics suspensions. | SMM projects nickel will be oversupplied from 2023 onward, especially from Indonesian NPI and refined supply increases, potentially widened by delays and possible Indonesian export tax changes. It estimates 2023 demand below supply, predicting a nickel price downtrend with volatile fluctuations. | The article headline says an LME Nickel Review recommends tighter rules to address market distortions. Such regulatory or governance changes can influence nickel contract trading conditions and expectations in the near term. The excerpt provides no details on implementation or mechanisms, so the likely immediate price impact is uncertain. | Metal Energy reported assay results from five Phase Two drill holes at its Manibridge project in Manitoba, intersecting shallow, stacked zones of high-grade nickel sulphide mineralization. Results include thick intervals and continuity along strike and dip, supporting potential resource expansion. Copper-cobalt is mentioned, but aluminium is not. | Metal Energy reports five drill holes intersecting shallow, high-grade nickel sulphide mineralization at its Manibridge project, including thick stacked zones and composite results up to 65% host rock mineralized. The announcement strengthens near-term market expectations for nickel supply potential and carries a modest positive link to copper via Ni eq reporting. | LME said it will detail by end-Q1 how it will implement recommendations from an independent report on last year’s nickel trading crisis. The broken contract, limited deliverable stocks and Russia-related supply worries led to disorderly price spikes. LME plans risk/control changes and may restore Asian nickel trading to improve liquidity. | Copper prices rose to a 6-1/2-month high on expectations that China’s reopening and stimulus-backed lending will boost metals demand. LME futures extended gains despite a holiday-related demand slowdown. Aluminium also climbed and nickel edged up, reflecting broad base-metal optimism tied to China’s improving demand outlook. | A report commissioned by the LME says the exchange must tighten volatility and risk controls to prevent another nickel market collapse after it canceled about $4 billion in nickel trades during March 2022. Consultants Oliver Wyman recommend upgrades to live trading oversight, potentially affecting near-term nickel market structure. | The LME said it will outline by end of Q1 how it will implement recommendations after a nickel trading crisis left the contract with low volumes and liquidity. Reforms include stronger risk controls, better OTC monitoring, and volatility management, plus potential return of Asian nickel trading to boost liquidity. | Sama Resources reported assay results from four additional drill holes at the Grata nickel-copper prospect in Côte d’Ivoire, with thick intervals grading around 0.30–0.33% nickel and 0.34–0.40% copper in parts of the intersections. Exploration is ongoing and no production or supply disruption is indicated. | Sama Resources announced assay results from four additional drill holes at the Grata nickel-copper prospect in Côte d’Ivoire, reporting substantial nickel and copper mineralised intervals (with palladium also mentioned). The update is exploration-focused with no production or market supply changes, making any short-term impact on nickel and copper prices likely minimal. | Copper extended gains to a 6-1/2 month peak as investors priced in a quicker China reopening and further stimulus, supported by unexpectedly higher bank lending. LME three-month copper rose 0.8% to $8,926/t. Aluminium also climbed, while nickel edged up on the same demand optimism. | Canada Nickel promotes its Crawford Timmins-area project as a low-carbon, large-volume nickel mine, using in-process tailings carbonation to generate carbon credits. The company targets EV/battery makers for financing and seeks federal support under Canada’s Critical Minerals Strategy. Permitting and feasibility are ongoing, but nickel supply sentiment may improve.

An independent Oliver Wyman review says the LME lacked sufficient tools to curb the short-term distortions during March’s nickel price “chaos.” It urges the exchange to strengthen rules and oversight. The review follows the LME’s week-long nickel trading suspension and the cancellation of large volumes of trades. | SMM reports that pure nickel spot lagged futures in December but social inventories fell sharply as spot cargoes moved to LME warehouses. In January, LME prices drop amid macro/news factors, while imports remain constrained, output rises, and alloy demand restocks ahead of Chinese New Year. Overall, nickel is expected to trade rangebound. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulphate prices continued falling in late 2022 due to reduced NEV precursor demand after subsidy cancellation, sharp precursor production cuts, and heavy inventory selling by integrated plants. With demand set to trough end-January and output reductions planned, prices are expected to keep easing in January. | SMM reports SHFE nickel fell 11.2% entering January, after December gains. The decline is linked to macro recession worries from Fed remarks, and a shift on the news front as Tsingshan’s electrodeposited/refined nickel commissioning adds supply and weighs on deliverable conditions, alongside rising global visible nickel inventories. | NiCAN says it will begin drilling at the Wine nickel property in Manitoba, Canada. The announcement is exploratory in nature, but it can slightly lift market expectations for future nickel resources and potential supply development. Near-term price impact is likely modest since drilling is not equivalent to production. | Golden Arrow commenced an IP-resistivity survey and plans its first diamond drilling program in Q1 at the San Pietro Cu-Au-Co project in Chile. The company aims to refine drill targets for copper and cobalt using geophysics and prior drilling data. It is exploration activity with no immediate production impact. | Nickel prices surged above $30,000/t in mid-November but fell sharply the next day amid low liquidity and heightened market monitoring by the LME. It also flags supply-risk events (Goro tailings leak, Indonesian plant rumour) and ongoing Russia-related uncertainty, reinforcing short-term volatility and bearish price pressure. | Britain’s FCA is blocking the LME’s plan to restart nickel trading in Asian hours, saying supervisory controls are inadequate while London is asleep. The LME previously suspended nickel activity for eight days in March 2022 after a spike. Ongoing inactivity has hurt liquidity and volumes, pressuring confidence. | Britain’s FCA is blocking the LME from restarting nickel trading in Asian hours, citing doubts over supervisory controls and orderly-market management. The LME suspended nickel activity in 2022 after sharp Asian-hour price spikes. Continued delay sustains low volumes and reduced arbitrage versus ShFE, weighing on nickel sentiment.

The UK watchdog has blocked an LME plan to restart Asian-hours nickel trading, according to trade sources. The decision could disrupt normal nickel market access and liquidity for Asia-linked participants, affecting short-term trading conditions and price expectations. However, the article provides limited detail on magnitude or timing. | SMM says China’s nickel sulphate market was in a sizable surplus in December due to slowing demand and smelter output not cutting as fast as upstream precursor plants. Year-end liquidity tightening accelerated a sell-off. January prices are forecast to fall again as the glut persists despite weak demand. | Power Nickel reports assay results extending the PN-22-009 nickel-copper-Co-PGE mineralized interval to 40.3m, with high-grade Ni-Cu values and plans to extend drilling further in Q1 2023. The update supports continuity of the Nisk resource, but provides no near-term production change. | Palladium One reported additional drill intercepts at its Tyko Project in Ontario, including high-grade massive sulphide assays with up to about 10.3% nickel and 2.9% copper over 1.8 meters. The company frames results as reinforcing West Pickle’s potential and plans further exploration to connect mineral zones in 2023. | Palladium One reported additional high-grade drill intercepts at its Tyko nickel-copper project, including zones with up to ~10.3% nickel and ~2.9% copper over drill intervals. The release focuses on exploration success and connecting mineralized zones, without any near-term production or supply-change information. | EV Nickel reported additional drill-hole assay results from its CarLang A Zone at the Shaw Dome Project, confirming thick, broad nickel mineralization in 23 reported holes, with assays pending for five more. The company expects a preliminary resource estimate in spring 2023 and highlights low overburden and development potential. | EV Nickel announced additional assay results from the CarLang area in Ontario’s Timmins camp, reinforcing the zone as a potentially large-scale nickel prospect. The update extends geological confidence around the target and can modestly improve expectations for future nickel supply, supporting nickel market sentiment.

China’s faster-than-expected reopening is described as a “game-changer” for commodity markets, driving bullish repositioning and lifting raw-material prices. Softer US inflation reduces pressure for aggressive Fed tightening, weakening the dollar and supporting expectations for global growth, which underpins rallies including copper and likely industrial metals demand. | LME expects to resume nickel trading in Asian hours but the FCA says supervisory controls are not yet adequate, requiring tighter monitoring to contain volatility. Past nickel disruptions stemmed from low LME inventories and deliverability/ warrant issues. Meanwhile, falling LME nickel and improving supply suggest price risks are limited near term. | The LME published the final Oliver Wyman report on last year’s nickel trading scandal, citing accumulated short positions as a key factor in the March suspension. UK regulators questioned the LME’s ability to carry out plans to restart Asian trading hours. The restart reportedly faced delays amid ongoing nickel chaos.

Workers clashed at PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry (GNI) in Sulawesi, killing two and damaging company property and dormitories. Police said operations at the smelter are currently suspended, with dozens detained. The incident followed protests over safety and pay, adding short-term supply risk for Indonesian nickel output. | Two workers were killed in clashes at PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry (GNI) smelter in Sulawesi, following labor protests over safety and pay. Operations at the smelter are currently suspended while police and the company investigate. Indonesia’s nickel supply tightness risk is heightened near term. | Clashes at Indonesia’s PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry (GNI) smelter left two workers dead and prompted suspending operations. The unrest stemmed from labour protests citing poor safety and pay. Damage to dormitories and vehicles occurred, dozens were detained, and authorities are investigating, potentially disrupting nickel supply. | Indonesia plans to limit nickel smelter construction, prioritizing ore processing toward higher-value products and requiring new plants to follow “green” energy principles. The government argues this is needed to maximize value addition, while existing coal-powered smelters may face constraints, potentially limiting future nickel supply growth. | Workers and locals clashed with police at the Gunbuster Nickel Industri (GNI) smelter in Morowali, Indonesia, over wages, safety, and layoffs. Multiple workers were arrested and three people died. Authorities blame work-safety and salary issues rather than foreign labor, while the company faces investigation and potential operational disruption. | Riots at PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry’s smelter in Indonesia left two workers dead and triggered a heavy security deployment after protests over pay and safety turned violent. The incident at the GNI nickel smelter suggests potential disruption to nickel refining supply in the near term. | Nickel Asia’s board approved an additional P2.92 billion investment into its renewable energy subsidiary EPI, including funding for geothermal operating expenses and related project investments. The disclosure notes the company’s revenues benefited last year from higher nickel ore prices, but the new spend is not a direct nickel production or supply change. | A riot and labor protest turned violent at PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry’s smelting facility in Indonesia’s Sulawesi, operated by a unit of China’s Jiangsu Delong Nickel Industry. Workers attempted to enter the plant, clashes with security followed, and heavy machinery was set on fire. Two workers died. Supply disruption risk is near-term.

CaNickel Mining released PEA and resource estimate results for its Bucko Lake nickel mine, outlining a 13-year project with average annual production of about 7.8 million lb of nickel. The update is a supply-side development that can influence near-term nickel market sentiment. | Nordic Nickel was selected for BHP’s inaugural Xplor program, receiving a US$500,000 non-dilutive grant and in-kind support to advance its Pulju nickel sulphide project in Finland. The company has started maiden drilling and plans additional deeper holes using the grant to test EM targets. | Two workers were killed in a riot at PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry’s nickel smelting plant on Sulawesi, Indonesia, amid protests over labor conditions. A one-day strike preceded clashes and the setting of fire to heavy machinery and vehicles. The disruption risks near-term nickel supply, weighing on prices. | Production has reportedly resumed at an Indonesian nickel smelter after a deadly clash. The restart should ease near-term supply tightness tied to the disruption, lowering expectations of reduced nickel output from the facility and weighing on nickel prices over the next days as supply risk declines. | SHFE nickel spot premiums weakened on Jan 17, with Jinchuan premiums averaging 11,500 yuan/mt (down 750 yuan/mt) and NORNICKEL premiums averaging 6,950 yuan/mt (down 200 yuan/mt). Nickel briquette prices also fell about 750 yuan/mt as trading remained subdued amid demand concerns. | Operations at PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry (GNI), Indonesia, were suspended after weekend riots at the smelter left two workers dead and damaged facilities. Police say production has resumed and hundreds of employees returned under heavy security. The restart of a major nickel processing asset should ease short-term nickel supply tightness. | Indonesia deployed security forces after clashes and rioting at the China-owned PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry smelter in Central Sulawesi. Two workers were killed amid protests over pay and safety. Authorities described broader unrest involving workers and guards, raising risks of short-term nickel refining disruption. | A riot and protest over labour conditions at PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry’s nickel smelting plant in Sulawesi left two workers dead and led to a one-day strike. Employees set fire to heavy machinery and vehicles while trying to enter premises, disrupting operations. The incident is likely to constrain nickel supply. | BHP selected seven companies for its BHP Xplor accelerator to fast-track early-stage exploration of copper and nickel. The firms receive funding up to $500,000, mentorship, and in-kind services to accelerate testing of new concepts. The programme may support longer-term supply prospects, but timing is distant. | BHP selected seven companies for its BHP Xplor programme to fast-track early-stage exploration for copper and nickel. The companies receive funding, mentorship, and services, including cash payments up to $500,000. BHP aims to speed discovery of new concepts and data to build its future pipeline. | Flying Nickel reported progress on its Minago nickel PGM project in Manitoba, including the status of an environmental act license/notice of alteration review, advancement of an impact benefit agreement, and continued feasibility study and PGM recovery optimization. The update supports expectations for future nickel project development. | Quebec plans to kick off its 2023 drilling program at the Ducros nickel project. The update relates to ongoing development and exploration activity for nickel supply prospects. While not an immediate increase in output, it can offer modest positive sentiment for nickel markets over the coming weeks. | The piece discusses nickel’s post-squeeze volatility and reports negotiations where Tsingshan may switch inputs with Chinese copper producers to boost refined nickel output, potentially raising global supply. It also cites expected higher nickel production from Indonesia efforts, implying surplus risk and possible downside pressure on prices. | Production restarted at PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry (GNI) in Indonesia after a deadly weekend riot damaged parts of the plant and followed labor unrest over wages and safety. Management reported 2,963 employees, including 350 Chinese workers, returning to work. Security remains heightened while authorities investigate causes.

Production has resumed at PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry (GNI), a nickel smelter owned by Jiangsu Delong Nickel, after operations were suspended following weekend protests and rioting that killed two workers. With production restarting, near-term nickel supply risk eases, which can be supportive for nickel prices. | BASF and Eramet are set to finalise a $2.6 billion partnership to build/operate a nickel smelter in Indonesia. The planned investment increases expectations for future nickel refining supply, which can weigh on nickel prices as markets anticipate more availability. | BASF and Eramet plan to invest about $2.6 billion in a nickel smelter in Indonesia. The project would ultimately increase nickel refining capacity and influence longer-run supply expectations, but the article provides no near-term commissioning timeline, suggesting limited immediate (days-to-weeks) price pressure. | Stellantis and Terrafame signed a five-year supply agreement beginning in 2025 for low-carbon nickel sulphate used in EV batteries. Terrafame will provide a significant portion of Stellantis’ sustainable nickel needs for its electrification plans, reinforcing demand expectations for nickel battery inputs. | Stellantis has signed a nickel sulphate supply deal with Terrafame, Reuters reports. Nickel sulphate is used to produce battery materials for EVs, so the agreement is likely to bolster expectations for near-term nickel demand and strengthen sentiment around nickel prices over the coming weeks. | BASF is nearing a $2.6bn deal with Eramet to invest in an Indonesia Weda Bay HPAL plant producing mixed hydroxide precipitate (MHP) for EV batteries. The project targets up to 67,000 tpa nickel (and ~7,000 tpa cobalt). Decisions are expected in early 2023; commissioning is planned for early 2026. | Chile’s government rejected the $2.5bn Dominga open-pit iron and copper mine and port project near the Humboldt penguin reserve, citing insufficient mitigation for marine and nature impacts. The decision removes a potential copper supply development in a key producer region, likely tightening near-term expectations. | Talon Metals, in partnership with Rio Tinto, plans to develop a nickel mine in Tamarack, Minnesota, expected to operate in 2026, while also striking a Tesla deal for nickel for EV batteries. It highlights depleting global supply and the closure of Michigan’s nickel mine, suggesting nickel tightness risk. Community objections are noted. | Stellantis said it has signed a supply agreement to secure nickel sulphate for electric vehicle batteries, sourced from Finland-based Terrafame. The deal signals additional committed demand for battery-grade nickel compounds, which can tighten market expectations and provide bullish support for nickel prices in the near term.

Canada Nickel says assay results confirm the Reid property is a major discovery, with a target footprint larger than its flagship Crawford nickel-cobalt project in Ontario’s Timmins-Cochrane camp. The news highlights expanding resource potential, which may lift near-term market sentiment for nickel supply expectations. | SMM reports a rebound in Shanghai spot nickel. SHFE nickel rose as funds supported prices; Jinchuan nickel premiums increased to about 11,500 yuan/mt. NORNICKEL premiums declined, but spot prices still rebounded. Nickel briquette prices rose sharply, though briquette trading remained weak versus nickel sulphate. | BASF and Eramet are in final stages of a roughly $2.6b partnership to build a hydro-metallurgical facility in Indonesia to produce battery-grade nickel (MHP) from Eramet’s Weda Bay mine. Officials cite potential investment approval in early 2023, boosting expectations for future nickel supply into EV batteries. | Talon Metals announced new drilling results at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project, identifying a “Raptor Zone” with high-grade nickel-copper massive sulphide intersections in multiple holes outside the current resource area. The company frames this as evidence of district-scale potential, which could affect near-term nickel sentiment and, secondarily, copper.

Talon Metals says it has identified a new nickel and copper mineralization zone about 3.2 km from its Tamarack project in central Minnesota. The discovery is expected to boost sentiment around potential future output. Near-term effects are likely limited pending further drilling, assays, and resource definition. | Talon Metals says it has discovered a new system of nickel and copper mineralization about 3.2 km from its Tamarack project in central Minnesota. The company describes the new zone’s location and potential significance, but the announcement is not tied to immediate production changes or inventories. | SMM reports SHFE nickel prices fell despite China’s GDP growth being at a normal level. Markets focus on upcoming US core PCE data and potential Fed tightening, which could strengthen the dollar and pressure commodities. Fundamentals show weak import clearance, weaker spot transactions, and tight deliverable refined nickel, suggesting limited near-term support. | SHFE nickel prices fell this week despite China’s 2022 GDP growth normalizing. Market support was limited as upstream sellers offered less and NPI producers kept low finished-product inventories. Downstream demand stayed soft due to CNY holiday rest. Ahead, potential US Fed tightening risks remain bearish for commodity prices. Nickel expected to drop further pre-CNY. | SMM says Chinese port nickel ore inventories fell 96,000 wmt WoW to 8.58 million wmt, with Ni content down 1,000 mt to about 67,000 mt. Arrivals were pressured by rainy conditions in the Philippines, while NPI and steel mills suspended output for overhauls. Inventory may decline faster after Chinese New Year.

The piece (based on the title) says that sanctions are constraining Nornickel and forcing the company to adjust its strategy. This implies potential near-term disruptions to nickel output, refining or shipments, which would likely tighten effective supply and weigh on nickel prices. | Metal Energy announced assay results from three drill holes at its Manibridge nickel project in Manitoba, reporting broad high-grade nickel intersections including 85.3m of 0.67% NiEq and additional intervals with similar NiEq grades. Results extend mineralization down dip and support improved confidence in the nickel resource at Manibridge. | Port of Townsville has approved arrangements to restart nickel ore imports and nickel product exports for the mothballed Queensland Nickel refinery in Queensland, which has been tied up in legal and financial hurdles. The renewed port access is expected to support plans to revive output, influencing nickel supply expectations.

SPC Nickel and Vale Canada agreed to consolidate adjacent Sudbury deposits, granting SPC Nickel the right to acquire full interest in the Crean Hill 3 nickel-copper property in exchange for development work and a C$1m payment at a feasibility deadline. Vale retains rights/royalties on the combined project. | SPC Nickel and Vale Canada agreed to consolidate adjacent West Graham and Crean Hill 3 deposits in Ontario’s Sudbury camp. SPC can earn full rights to Crean Hill 3 by delivering a feasibility study by June 2026, paying C$1m and granting Vale certain royalties. It targets higher-grade nickel-copper growth. | Norilsk Nickel says it has overcome complications in feedstock deliveries to its Harjavalta plant in Finland. A contracted Finnish operator is transporting by rail. The company expects possible logistical interruptions later, but has planned conservatively to meet client demand for contracted Finnish volumes versus production capacity. | SPC Nickel and Vale Canada signed a cooperation agreement to consolidate SPC Nickel’s West Graham and Crean Hill 3 nickel-copper deposits into a single property in the Sudbury camp. The deal is aimed at streamlining the asset footprint, but it does not indicate any near-term production supply impact. | BHP signed an agreement with Mundoro Capital to earn 100% interest in three exploration areas in Serbia’s Timok region: Borsko Jezero, South Timok and Trstenik, targeting porphyry copper deposits. The options require about $1.7m cash and $7.5m exploration over three years, with a 2% NSR royalty retained by Mundoro.

Nikkei Asia reports China is seeking Indonesia’s large nickel market. It notes protests in Sulawesi, Indonesia’s mineral-rich region, turned violent after disputes tied to a high share of Chinese workers in local companies. Such unrest raises risks of interruptions to nickel production or flows.

SPC Nickel agreed with Vale to consolidate the Crean Hill 3 nickel-copper property with its Lockerby East assets in Sudbury. The deal enables a combined evaluation and potential open-pit development, starting with a 2023 feasibility study. Production is not expected until years later, limiting near-term price effects. | Palladium One Mining reports staking an additional 4,520 hectares and expanding the Tyko nickel–copper district. A high-resolution airborne magnetic survey is being flown across the expanded property to refine feeder dyke/chonolith structures and improve drill targeting.
