The release describes exploration activity, not any output or supply disruption. | Stillwater Critical Minerals increased the inferred resource tonnage for its Stillwater West battery and platinum metals project in Montana, highlighting a large expansion in nickel resources. The company aims to mirror its neighbor Sibanye-Stillwater. The update is supply-related sentiment, but not immediate production, limiting short-term price impact. | Flying Nickel Mining Corp. announced a non-brokered private placement raising up to $800,000 to fund a Minago PGM assay/drilling program and general corporate purposes. The company is developing its 100% owned Minago nickel project. The release is financing-focused with no immediate supply or production impact stated. | The Biden administration ordered a 20-year closure of parts of Minnesota’s Superior National Forest to mineral and geothermal leasing, blocking the Twin Metals proposed underground copper-nickel mine near Ely. The move follows prior disputes over lease reinstatement and threatens project viability, increasing perceived supply risk for copper and nickel. | The Biden administration ordered a 20-year closure of Superior National Forest land in Minnesota to mineral and geothermal leasing, protecting the Boundary Waters and stopping the Twin Metals proposed copper-nickel mine. The move followed rescission of key mineral rights, shifting outlook toward lower future supply of copper and nickel.

Nickel premiums fell across the US, with nickel briquette and 4x4 cathode premiums dropping after China returns from Lunar New Year. Reports show demand improving but price opinions easing, leading to lower assessed ranges. In Europe, briquette premiums decreased on weak stainless demand, while uncut cathode premiums rose slightly amid tight high-purity supply.

SMM reports nickel spot premiums rising on Jan 30: Jinchuan nickel premiums against SHFE increased to around 10,500 yuan/mt (+750), while optimism over consumption after the Chinese New Year holiday supported the move. Nickel briquette prices also rose, though trading remained relatively slack. | SMM reports China’s December 2022 customs clearance for refined/pure nickel fell 13% MoM, with refined nickel imports largely flat MoM but pressured by persistent import losses. Lower nickel sulfate output and weaker 300-series stainless steel demand reduced need for nickel briquettes, weighing on near-term nickel demand. | BHP launched its Xplor accelerator to fund and mentor early-stage explorers targeting copper and nickel, receiving hundreds of applications. The article also cites BHP’s binding $9.6bn takeover of Oz Minerals and an option to take a stake in copper exploration leases in Serbia, strengthening supply outlook sentiment for both metals. | The headline says the Philippines may tax nickel exports following Indonesia’s reported success. Such export taxes can reduce outbound supply and tighten availability in the near term, potentially lifting nickel prices as market expectations shift toward lower export flows. | Residents of Sibuyan Island in the Philippines are asking authorities to pause APMC’s nickel mining exploration, arguing it threatens local ecosystems and livelihoods. The company has received an export license for bulk testing and ore shipments are reported, but protests and required environmental clearances raise the risk of delays.

Philippines officials are considering imposing an export tax on nickel ore to shift the country from exporting raw material to developing domestic nickel processing. With the Philippines a leading global nickel ore supplier, the policy could tighten upstream availability for overseas supply chains, supporting nickel price expectations in the near term. | The Philippines is considering taxes on primary nickel ore exports, or even a broader ore export ban, inspired by Indonesia’s success with export restrictions since 2020. The policy aims to drive local processing and boost downstream revenue. Philippine nickel ore shipments are expected to improve despite a 17% production fall. | SHFE nickel stayed elevated on Jan 31 and spot pure nickel prices rose. Jinchuan premiums averaged 8,500 yuan/mt (down from yesterday), while Nornickel premiums averaged 8,250 yuan/mt (up). Nickel briquette prices climbed 2,650 yuan/mt to about 228,700–229,200 yuan/mt, despite weaker spot transactions. | Philippines lawmakers are considering a tax on raw nickel ore exports (initially 10%) to push miners toward local processing. Industry groups warn it would “kill” the sector, citing low processing capacity versus ore output. The proposal follows Indonesia’s nickel ore ban model, affecting near-term nickel export supply expectations. | The Biden EPA blocked Alaska’s Pebble Mine from advancing, rejecting developers’ plan under the Clean Water Act due to unacceptable impacts on the Bristol Bay salmon ecosystem. By halting a major proposed gold and copper project, the decision reduces potential copper supply, weighing on copper price expectations. Broader mining actions are mentioned for other minerals.

Vale SA said Q4 2022 nickel production fell 1.3% to 47,400 tonnes, while nickel sales volumes jumped 30.2% to 58,200 tonnes by drawing on pent-up stocks to meet contracts. The company also noted weaker iron ore output and ongoing expectations for 2023 nickel production. | The Philippines is considering taxing exported nickel ore to attract investment in domestic nickel processing. Analysts warn tariffs would increase supply uncertainty and raise Chinese smelters’ nickel-ore procurement costs (Philippines ore is largely used). While potential future smelting capacity could expand, the near-term effect is cost pressure for primary nickel. | SMM reports Jinchuan Group boosted refined nickel production by 600 mt in January. The plan targets 6,000 mt higher refined nickel output in 2023 and relies on capacity and balance improvements across nickel smelters, using high-grade nickel matte as feedstock, with most incremental output from existing lines. | SMM reports SHFE nickel prices rising on Feb 1 while spot trade activity is slack due to high spot levels. Jinchuan and Nornickel spot premiums fell day-on-day as suppliers were keen to sell. Nickel briquette spot prices edged higher, but nickel sulphate production economics remained unattractive, limiting demand. | Atlantic Nickel reports record 2022 performance at its Santa Rita nickel-copper sulphide mine in Brazil, including higher concentrate output and details of hedging for 2022-2023. Realized prices were about US$12.23/lb Ni and US$3.70/lb Cu, but the release is mainly historical, limiting immediate price pressure. | Glencore said it will keep its 2023 output guidance despite a 12% copper production plunge in 2022, citing issues at Katanga and disruptions including an Antapaccay suspension after protests. Nickel production rose 5% on improved operations, but overall guidance stayed unchanged, limiting near-term price impact.

The Philippines is considering increasing export taxes or banning nickel ore exports to support local processing and EV battery supply chains. The piece argues this could disrupt nickel ore supply flows—especially to China, Japan, and South Korea—raising input costs and potentially supporting nickel prices in the near term despite implementation uncertainties. | Fleet Space Technologies will partner with Canada’s Power Nickel to deploy its satellite-based ExoSphere exploration system at the company’s NISK nickel project in Quebec. The technology aims to accelerate, lower-impact discovery of high-grade nickel deposits needed for EV battery supply, supporting a modest positive outlook for nickel. | SMM reports Indonesia has relaxed restrictions on foreign ownership in local nickel mines from 49% to 70%, strengthening foreign participation. With stainless-steel and intermediate-product investment, competition for nickel ore is intensifying. Indonesia is also promoting benchmark pricing for low-grade nickel ore, drawing market attention. | Umicore has signed a long-term agreement with Terrafame to supply low-carbon, high-grade nickel sulphate for EV cathode materials. Commercial deliveries have started, with volume ramp-up planned in 2023. The contract covers a substantial part of Umicore’s planned nickel sulphate needs, supporting demand expectations for nickel. | After China’s New Year, nickel prices jumped on hopes of stronger consumption and a potential Philippines nickel-ore export ban, but the high level curbed buying. The article says the ban is unlikely due to power and rainy-season constraints. With weaker fundamentals and thin spot trades, SHFE nickel prices pulled back.

The Philippines’ trade chief said the country will prioritize exporting processed nickel ore and develop downstream processing aligned with EV battery supply chains. The plan also follows earlier consideration of banning raw ore exports to force processing. Community opposition exists, but the policy direction is supportive for nickel sentiment. | SHFE nickel reversed after seven gains, dropping 3.68% on Feb 2, with LME nickel also weaker. The earlier bullish support from the Philippines considering nickel ore export taxes faded. Ample supply during CNY, massive social inventories, and cautious restocking by alloy plants due to high nickel prices point to further downside. | NORNICKEL nickel spot premiums in Shanghai rose to nearly 1,400 yuan/mt above Jinchuan, leading to poor spot shipments as downstream pure-nickel demand stayed weak. Nickel futures climbed on post–CNY production resumption, but market sentiment was less bullish and the SHFE/LME ratio improved, suggesting premiums may ease next week. | SMM forecasts 2023 refined nickel as volatile but trending lower. It expects Chinese and overseas refined nickel output to keep rising, while demand growth is uneven (stainless steel and nickel sulphate weaker; alloy demand supported). It also anticipates a supply surplus and higher inventories, with Indonesian export-tax risk possibly increasing oversupply. | SMM reports China’s port nickel ore inventories fell 484,000 wmt to 8.1 million wmt by Feb 3, with Ni content down to about 64,000 mt. The drop is linked to slack holiday trading and weaker Philippines shipments due to rainy season, supporting expectations of further declines. | Future Metals reports additional nickel-copper assay “hits” at its Panton project. The update is interpreted as improved potential resource prospects for both nickel and copper. Such discovery-related news can modestly support short-term market sentiment by raising expectations for future supply, even without immediate production details.

Indonesia’s nickel-rich profile and continued nickel ore export ban are driving large EV and battery investment deals, with Indonesia expected to capture a major share of global nickel demand growth for batteries. It also signals upcoming export bans on copper ore and bauxite, supporting near-term expectations for tighter supplies. | The article details Indonesia’s accelerating nickel-related investment and processing chain for EV batteries, including the first shipment of nickel mixed hydroxide precipitate to China from Morowali and multiple smelter/HPAL and battery-material projects. It also notes broader EV investment deals, implying improved nickel feedstock and supply expectations. | Copper prices declined as a firm U.S. dollar following a strong jobs report made dollar-priced commodities more expensive. The move was reinforced by doubts about China’s recovery pace after COVID restrictions were removed. Aluminium and nickel also fell on their respective exchanges amid the same macro and demand pressures. | Indonesia’s export ban on nickel ore and push to build EV battery supply chains underpin expectations for rapidly rising nickel demand and sustained market prominence. The article cites strong production growth, large EV/battery deals, and forecasts that EVs will drive significant nickel demand, supporting near-term bullish sentiment for nickel prices. | SMM says battery-grade nickel sulphate prices rose to 38,000–39,000 yuan/mt on supply tightness. Demand from refined nickel increased, producers restarted and expanded capacity, and nickel salt makers held low post-holiday inventories after output cuts and maintenance. Integrated firms are restocking amid concerns over future raw-material supply. | ANZ forecasts nickel will face a large supply surplus of about 55,000 mt in 2023. The bank warns nickel prices may face downward pressure if electric-vehicle sales growth disappoints, suggesting weaker demand could compound the oversupply situation and weigh on near-term pricing sentiment. | SMM reports nickel prices rose after post-CNY production resumed, supported by refined output increases and concerns over possible Philippines nickel-ore export bans. However, high nickel prices suppress downstream demand, with weak spot transactions, fewer shipments, and limited buying by electroplating and battery-related units. Fundamentals seen as offering weaker support going forward. | Indonesia, the world’s top nickel producer, is pushing EV battery investment and leveraging its 2020 nickel ore export ban to expand onshore processing. With EV demand rising, Indonesia is projected to add a large share of global nickel growth, tightening expectations for battery-grade nickel prices; nickel also faces potential sustainability-related constraints. | Indonesia is courting Tesla and other automakers to invest in its nickel-driven EV battery supply chain. It cites over $15bn in battery-material and EV production deals, including nickel export bans, HPAL refining plans, and nickel sulfate/MHP smelter and refining investments. These developments bolster nickel demand expectations short term. | Palladium One reported expanded drill intercepts at the West Pickle massive sulfide zone on the Tyko nickel–copper project in Ontario, extending mineralization to over 600 meters. Results reinforce high-grade nickel-copper potential, including a prior wide interval at RJ, but the update is exploration-stage. | Nickel ore trading was slack after the CNY holiday, with Ni 1.5% ore CIF around $71.5/wmt, flat on the pre-holiday level. Limited Philippine supply, falling ocean freight, and rising NPI prices drove wait-and-see sentiment. NPI/stainless output grew little, leaving demand weak; prices are expected $70–74/wmt. | Copper fell to a four-week low as demand-recovery doubts in China and a stronger U.S. dollar pressured base metals. Higher rate expectations lifted the dollar, while U.S.-China tensions added uncertainty. Aluminium and nickel also declined alongside copper, reflecting broad bearish sentiment in the industrial metals complex. | The article content could not be accessed (error/404), but the title states Palladium One expanded the West Pickle high-grade nickel zone to 600 meters at the Tyko nickel–copper project in Canada. Without further data, the impact on nickel and copper is uncertain and likely limited to sentiment.

Chengtun Mining said its high-nickel matte project at PT. ChengMach Nickel Indonesia is in progress, including land leveling and design work. With planned annual capacity of 40,000 mt in nickel content, the project is expected to be completed and commissioned in Q4 this year, increasing expected nickel supply. | SMM reports SHFE nickel traded lower early, but downstream restocking lifted spot transactions and inquiries. NPI sellers kept high quotes and Tsingshan offered March bids above expectations. Despite cost and demand dynamics, SMM expects nickel prices to move rangebound today. Pure nickel premiums were mostly flat and briquette trading was sluggish. | SMM reports Feb 7 pure nickel spot premiums for Jinchuan rising to 7,300–7,500 yuan/mt (+400). Nornickel premiums eased to 6,500 yuan/mt (-50). SHFE nickel opened lower but rebounded, while spot prices were slightly down and inquiries increased, supporting nickel sentiment near term. | London copper rebounded after a four-week low as the dollar weakened. Copper also faced potential near-term supply tightness after First Quantum Minerals suspended loading at a key Panama port for its Cobre Panama mine. Aluminium and nickel moved with the broader metals complex; a proposed U.S. tariff on Russian aluminium added supportive upside risk. | USGS preliminary data show global nickel mine production rose to 3.3 million tonnes in 2022, up 21% from 2021. Indonesia’s output jumped to 1.6 million tonnes (+54%), supported by commissioning of NPI and stainless steel projects. Additional developments in intermediate matte and nickel-cobalt feedstocks were also cited. | Indonesia’s nickel export ban and push to develop an onshore EV battery supply chain are accelerating investment and production. With large nickel reserves and rising EV battery demand, analysts expect Indonesia to capture much of global nickel growth. Any added confidence in constrained non-Indonesia capacity supports nickel prices. | Sky Gold Corp. acquired three properties in Ontario with exploration targets modeled on the past-producing Shebandowan Ni-Cu-Co-PGE deposit. The release highlights nickel and copper mineralization indicators (airborne VTEM conductors, till anomalies, historical drill results). No production or smelting/refining activity is mentioned, so near-term metal supply impact is limited. | Sky Gold acquired three Ni-Cu-Co-PGE exploration properties near the past-producing Shebandowan deposit, describing ultramafic/serpentinized geology, magnetic highs, and untested VTEM conductors plus historical drilling and till anomalies. The story is relevant to nickel and copper but lacks any near-term production impact.

Copper rose on Wednesday as a weaker U.S. dollar followed perceived dovish comments from Fed Chair Powell and as supply disruptions supported prices. LME three-month copper gained 0.7% and Shanghai March rose 0.7%. Chile export declines and the resumption of Peru’s Antapaccay operations were cited. | Anglo American will buy a 9.9% stake in Canada Nickel, owner of the Crawford nickel project in Ontario. The miner will contribute technology and obtain exclusive rights to buy up to 10% of recoveries from nickel concentrate and related materials, supporting expected nickel output expansion.

SMM expects nickel prices to stay rangebound with some downward potential. Increased profitability and expected flows of imported pure nickel plates should ease supply tightness in the coming weeks. Nickel ore costs are firm, but stainless steel cold-rolled demand is weaker, with some restocking by alloy makers after recent price declines. | SMM reports that on Feb 9 spot pure nickel premiums fell for both Jinchuan and NORNICKEL (each down about 600 yuan/mt day-on-day). Nickel inquiries and transactions were sluggish, with lower premiums as NORNICKEL supply arrived steadily. Nickel briquette trading was weak versus nickel sulphate prices. | USGS preliminary estimates show global nickel ore output rose 21% in 2022 to 3.3 million mt, driven by a 54% jump in Indonesia to 1.6 million mt. The increase is attributed to commissioning of integrated NPI and stainless steel projects, plus ongoing development of nickel-cobalt intermediates for battery-grade nickel sulphate feedstock. | China produced 31,200 mt Ni content of nickel sulphate in January, down 16.99% month-on-month, as precursor plant output cuts and COVID disruption reduced operating rates and shipments. High inventories dampened demand. February output is expected to rebound, but near-term supply remains pressured. | SMM reports that LME nickel inventories fell by 936 mt to 48,252 mt on Feb 9. The decline signals tighter exchange availability for nickel, which can support near-term price expectations as market participants adjust for reduced stock levels. | EV Nickel began Phase 4 drilling at its Shaw Dome W4 Zone, targeting the eastern expansion of previously defined high-grade nickel mineralization and collecting metallurgical samples. Assay results are expected in early Q2. The company says results could materially impact the upcoming updated resource estimate. | Anglo American agreed to buy a 9.9% stake in Canada Nickel for C$24m and will provide technical support for the Crawford nickel project in Ontario. The equity funding is meant to complete permitting, and Anglo gains rights related to recoveries of nickel concentrate and related carbon credits. | Trafigura says it faces a $577 million loss after discovering nickel shipment fraud, with some purchased cargoes missing the nickel they were supposed to contain. The fraud was detected through inspections and legal action has started, while most shipments remain in transit awaiting further checks. | Trafigura says it may lose up to $577mn after suing parties including TMT Metals over an alleged “systematic fraud” in containerised nickel shipments during 2022. Some containers inspected since late Dec 2022 were found not to contain nickel. Most shipments remain in transit pending inspection.

Bloomberg reports GM is in advanced negotiations with Vale for a stake in Vale’s newly established base metals division, which supplies nickel and copper for EV batteries. With automakers scaling EV production, Vale expects sizable growth in nickel and copper demand, and GM’s bids could tighten expectations despite no deal being finalized. | Aperam and Econick formed a JV, Botanickel, to produce biosourced nickel by extracting nickel from hyperaccumulator plants and feeding it into stainless-steel recycling and processing routes. The company claims pilot production has started and aims to cover part of primary nickel demand, potentially reducing exposure to nickel volatility. | Copper eased on China factory-gate price weakness and weaker U.S. employment data that raised demand and recession worries, while Las Bambas in Peru maintained reduced output after road blockades. Nickel surged after Trafigura disclosed a large nickel-fraud-related loss and rising expectations around low-stock liquidity. Aluminium also edged lower. | Copper fell as China factory-gate prices and weaker U.S. unemployment claims revived demand and recession worries. Nickel jumped after Trafigura disclosed a large charge tied to alleged systematic fraud involving nickel cargoes. Aluminium also eased on the day, indicating a mixed but mainly risk-driven tape across industrial metals. | Trafigura will record a ~$577m impairment after discovering missing nickel in containerized shipments from suppliers allegedly involved in document misrepresentation. The issue is described as isolated and under review, with inspections ongoing for remaining in-transit loads. Trafigura initiated legal proceedings against those parties. | The piece reviews the LME’s efforts to restart and stabilize nickel trading after the March 2022 volatility suspension, including possible permission to resume Asian hours, reworking margin/15% daily limits, improved OTC oversight, and clearing access proposals. Ongoing sanctions-related nerves around Russian deliverable nickel also shape sentiment. | SMM reports Chinese nickel ore inventories fell 267,000 wmt WoW to 7.83 million wmt, with Ni content down 2,000 mt to about 62,000 mt. Shipments from the Philippines were constrained by rainy-season conditions, and rising February NPI output is expected to lift ore consumption. | The piece discusses Indonesia’s dominant nickel position—21 million tonnes of proven reserves, an ongoing nickel ore export ban, and rapidly expanding processed nickel and battery/EV deals. It also notes efforts to persuade Tesla to produce EVs or batteries in Indonesia, reinforcing expectations for nickel-linked demand. | Trafigura announced a $577 million impairment after discovering that some purchased containers did not contain nickel as contracted, firing its head of nickel/cobalt trading and pursuing legal action. With a portion of shipments already inspected and failing checks, while others remain in transit, uncertainty around nickel supply quality increases.

London copper edged up 0.2% but stayed near a one-week low as traders were cautious ahead of U.S. CPI. The dollar’s rise weighed, but China bank-loan data supported demand hopes. LME aluminium also rose 0.4%, while SHFE nickel fell 3.4% amid weaker sentiment. | Fastmarkets reports nickel sulfate prices in China rebounded 3.45% week-on-week after Tsingshan began producing nickel full plate using nickel sulfate, boosting sentiment and pressuring futures. But traders remain skeptical that EV-driven demand will strengthen, citing slowing battery metal demand and expectations of ample spot supply. | Trafigura alleges large-scale containerized nickel fraud by traders connected to Prateek Gupta, involving 20,000 tonnes of uncut nickel cathode and potential $577m losses. After the release, LME three-month nickel saw sharp volatility and a temporary spike, then eased, while participants fear wider pricing and trade-finance impacts.

Bloomberg reports that Citi stopped financing Trafigura’s nickel “transit finance” trades on Oct. 27 after concerns over alleged fraud tied to a counterparty. Trafigura’s inspections found containers labelled as nickel did not contain nickel, and it previously sold some of these cargoes onward, potentially amplifying nickel market disruption fears. | China’s nickel ore imports fell to 2.76 million mt in Dec 2022, down 33.9% MoM, while port stockpiles continued declining. Philippine ore shipments dropped 22.7% MoM due to rainy-season disruptions. Sulphide ore imports also declined. January import levels are expected to stay low despite lower ocean freight and a NPI price rebound. | SMM reports China’s refined nickel imports totaled 14,441 mt in Dec 2022, down 0.55% MoM and 46.41% YoY. Most volume came from other unwrought alloy nickel, which fell sharply year-on-year. Russia was the largest supplier. Separate categories showed mixed MoM/YoY movements.

A deadly protest at Indonesia’s Gunbuster Nickel Industry (GNI) smelter in Morowali led to a strike, violent clashes, police arrests, and the suspension of production at the facility. Management and authorities investigated while heavy security presence continued, raising near-term supply disruption risk for nickel. | PolyMet and Teck closed the NewRange Copper Nickel joint venture covering the NorthMet and Mesaba deposits in Minnesota. The owners committed funding for permitting and engineering (initial work program) and PolyMet announced a ~$200m rights offering to fund its share and repay debt. This is a modest bullish signal for copper and nickel. | PolyMet and Teck closed their 50:50 joint venture, NewRange Copper Nickel LLC, covering the NorthMet and Mesaba copper-nickel deposits. PolyMet will raise about US$200 million via a rights offering to fund its share of an initial work program and repay Glencore-related debt. Permits remain partly dependent on litigation clearance. | PolyMet and Teck’s joint venture, NewRange Copper Nickel LLC, has been formed to advance the NorthMet and Mesaba projects in Minnesota. These deposits are cited as major copper and nickel resources. The company plans permitting and pre-construction studies, which can modestly lift near-term sentiment for copper and nickel supply prospects. | NewRange Copper Nickel LLC was formed via a JV between PolyMet and Teck, controlling the NorthMet and Mesaba deposits in Minnesota. The projects are geared toward developing domestic copper and nickel supply for North America’s clean-energy transition, with permits pending for NorthMet and ongoing pre-development work for Mesaba. | Teck announced it has completed the NewRange copper–nickel joint venture agreement with PolyMet. The deal headline suggests improved forward supply expectations for copper and nickel, which can support prices in the near term. The excerpt contains no project specifics, so immediate impact is uncertain.

Qingdao Zhongcheng’s Indonesian operations are said to be producing nickel ore subject to Indonesia’s export restrictions. The ore will be smelted in Indonesia, where the company’s smelters will prioritize NPI production rather than refined nickel for now, while monitoring further policy changes. | Tier One Silver reported composite chip sampling results at its Hurricane Project in Peru, highlighting copper grades (up to 1.59% Cu) and nickel grades (up to 1.86% Ni) from the new Rayanpata target within a magmatic sulphide system. The company plans further mapping and sampling to pursue extensions. | Tier One Silver reported composite chip sampling results from a new Rayanpata target at its Hurricane project in southern Peru, highlighting high grades of copper (up to ~1.43% in composites) and nickel (up to ~1.86%), with cobalt. This is exploration-stage sampling only, with no production or supply impact details. | Trafigura says containers purchased from Gupta-linked firms may not contain nickel as declared, potentially involving about 25,000 tonnes. Investigations are ongoing and the alleged fraud could shake confidence in nickel trade/warehouse integrity, adding uncertainty about physical supply quality despite no direct hedging exposure. | Caspin Resources reported drilling results from the Yarawindah Brook Project showing high-grade zones containing platinum group elements, nickel and copper within broad mineralisation. The company also outlined a gold-in-soil anomaly at Mount Squires. The update is exploration-focused, but supports incremental bullish sentiment for nickel and copper supply outlooks. | Caspin Resources’ CEO discusses early drilling results at the Yarawindah Brook Project, identifying high-grade zones of platinum group elements along with nickel and copper within broader mineralised zones. It also notes a strong gold-in-soil anomaly. The update is exploration-focused, suggesting limited immediate effects on near-term metal pricing.

Hedonova exited its 76% stake in Indonesia’s Mineralindo Morowali nickel miner, selling for $31 million to a consortium of Chinese firms. The deal includes leaving the mine’s leased equipment, smelter, and about 300 tonnes of nickel ore inventory. Hedonova cites ESG concerns and broader uncertainty in nickel demand and supply. | Hedonova exited its 76% stake in Indonesian nickel miner Mineralindo Morowali, selling it for $31 million in an all-cash deal to a Chinese consortium, INBC. The stake was bought for $10 million in 2020. The announcement details ownership change but no stated production or export changes. | The U.S. is considering a 200% import levy on Russian aluminium, potentially reducing Russian metal flows into the U.S. Analysts warn Russia could retaliate by limiting other critical metals, especially nickel, since about 11% of U.S. nickel imports come from Russia. Spillover sanctions risk could amplify price pressure. | The U.S. is considering sharply raising import levies on Russian-origin aluminium. Analysts say retaliation could follow, potentially including limits on Russian nickel exports to the U.S. Trade data show Russia is a meaningful nickel supplier to the U.S., so tariff escalation could disrupt both aluminium and nickel supply expectations. | The US is considering steep levies on Russian-origin aluminium imports. Analysts warn Russia may respond by limiting exports of other critical metals to the US, with nickel highlighted as important for aerospace and EV batteries. Russian aluminium trade is small, but retaliatory supply constraints could pressure nickel and aluminium prices. | The US is considering raising import levies on Russian-origin aluminium to 200% to pressure Russia over Ukraine. Analysts say retaliation could include limiting other critical metals shipped to the US, especially nickel (used in aerospace and EV batteries). Data show meaningful US nickel imports from Russia and smaller aluminium volumes.

London copper eased 0.1% as a firmer U.S. dollar weighed on greenback-priced metals, while rising Chinese inventories pointed to weak demand. Aluminium rose 1.4%, while nickel fell on SHFE. The piece also cites supply disruptions in mining countries as supportive for copper. Overall tone is mixed, not one-way. | SMM reports China is rapidly expanding electrowinning nickel capacity, with major new/converted lines slated for 2023–2024. It argues this will increase refined nickel supply, divert MHP/nickel sulphate feedstock, and contribute to a future surplus with rising domestic social inventories. Net effect is downward pressure on nickel prices. | The Philippines is considering an Indonesia-style raw nickel export tax (reported 10%) and possibly an outright ban, alongside proposed royalties. This would likely tighten nickel ore supply and support nickel prices, but the plan faces opposition and may take time to implement, making near-term impact more uncertain. | Trafigura appointed new co-heads of its battery metals trading after reporting it was victim of “systemic fraud” tied to nickel cargoes. The firm booked a $577 million charge in 2023 after discovering some nickel shipments did not contain the metal, prompting greater scrutiny of nickel trading risk.

The Oregon Group report predicts a 3–5 year shortage of Class I nickel needed for EV batteries, citing constrained high-grade supply, delays in Tsingshan’s ramp, and geopolitical and decarbonization pressures. Western efforts may boost domestic supply but the report expects the current Class I deficit to persist, supporting higher nickel prices. | Trafigura replaced the co-head of its nickel, cobalt and lithium trading after alleging it was a victim of nickel-related fraud. The trader says some containers bought from others were not found to contain nickel and faces potential losses of US$577mn, launching legal action against connected parties.

SMM reported that spot #1 refined nickel prices fell by 600 yuan/mt on Feb 22 to 213,300–216,400 yuan/mt. The direct movement in the reported nickel benchmark reflects short-term bearish momentum in refined nickel pricing. | SMM reports that SHFE’s most-traded nickel contract was 207,810 yuan/mt as of 14:58:37 on Feb 22, falling 1.01% over the prior 28 minutes. The move reflects short-term bearish price pressure in nickel futures. | London copper prices fell 1.1% amid U.S. rate jitters and a stronger dollar, while investors weighed China’s post-COVID demand recovery against high inventories. LME aluminium and nickel also declined (aluminium -1.4%, nickel -2.7%) as macro pressure weighed on industrial metals sentiment. | CME Group plans to launch a nickel futures contract settled using prices from a new UK platform run by Global Commodities Holdings (GCH). The initiative follows LME nickel disruption and liquidity decline after last March’s price spike, offering market participants an alternative benchmark for hedging and trading. | CME Group plans to launch a nickel contract, settled using prices from a UK-based Global Commodities Holdings (GCH) physical trading platform that could produce a benchmark index. The move targets frustration with LME nickel contract liquidity after last March’s price spike, offering an alternative reference for hedging and trading.

SMM reported that spot #1 refined nickel prices fell sharply on Feb 23, dropping by 3,650 yuan/mt to 209,600–212,800 yuan/mt. The move reflects clear near-term weakness in refined nickel pricing. | Palladium One reported drill results from its Tyko Nickel–Copper project, including high-grade intervals (e.g., up to ~3.5% Ni and 1.2% Cu over 3.4 m) and a new mineralized chonolith/feeder dyke discovery north of the RJ Zone. The findings are supportive for near-term nickel and copper sentiment.

POSCO and a Chinese firm reportedly signed a preliminary agreement for EV battery nickel production in Indonesia. The deal points to additional nickel supply coming through a new investment pipeline tied to battery demand, which can lift near-term market expectations for nickel prices. | POSCO Holdings and China’s Lygend Resources & Technology signed an MOA in Indonesia to cooperate on nickel production for secondary batteries. Plans include building a nickel MHP plant in Sulawesi with 120,000 tons annual capacity, with construction starting in 2024 and production in 2025. The move targets cost-competitive Indonesian nickel. | Posco and Ningbo Liqin signed a preliminary agreement to build a nickel processing plant in Indonesia, targeting 120,000 tons/year of mixed nickel-cobalt hydroxide (MHP) from 2025, with an initial 60,000 tons. The project supports EV battery cathode supply and adds to Indonesia’s nickel value-added processing push. | Copper prices fell as China demand appeared tepid after the Lunar New Year and markets increasingly priced higher U.S. interest rates for longer. The downturn was partially cushioned by global supply disruptions, including First Quantum’s suspension at Cobre Panama, but overall sentiment turned bearish for copper and broader industrial metals. | SMM reported Chinese nickel ore inventories at seven major ports fell 425,000 wmt week-on-week to 7.18 million wmt (56,400 mt Ni content). Inventories at the ports also declined. NPI plant demand improved somewhat, while Philippines rainy-season disruptions kept imports low, suggesting inventories may keep falling. | Premium Nickel Resources closed a CAD$7.8m private placement to fund further drilling and underground development at its Selebi and Selkirk nickel-copper-cobalt deposits in Botswana. The funding aims at advancing projects toward a NI 43-101 PEA maiden resource. No immediate production, inventory, or pricing changes are stated.

SMM reports that pure nickel spot premiums declined further at month-end: Jinchuan nickel averaged about 5,750 yuan/mt and Nornickel about 3,600 yuan/mt, both down versus the previous day. Weak demand and subdued trading coincided with further falls in SHFE nickel and spot quotes, and lower nickel briquette prices. | SMM reported that Shanghai nickel premiums weakened on Feb 27: Jinchuan averaged 5,750 yuan/mt (-400) and Nornickel averaged 3,600 yuan/mt (-300). Weak demand and SHFE 2303 nickel deliveries led to mediocre spot trading, while nickel briquette prices also fell as trading stayed poor versus sulphate prices. | London copper fell as a firmer dollar and uncertainty over China’s demand weighed on prices, even as supply disruptions in Indonesia, Peru and Chile provided some support. Analysts also cited inventory dynamics and a seasonal demand outlook. Aluminium in the region rose slightly, but SHFE aluminium and nickel declined with smelter production cuts. | Lifezone Metals announced completion of the S-K 1300 technical report summary for the Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, reporting 40.4 Mt attributable resources containing 2,325 Mlb nickel and 311 Mlb copper (plus cobalt). The TRS is preliminary and supports development progress, not immediate production or supply changes. | Nickel 28 reported Ramu Q4 2022 nickel production of 8,480 tonnes (+33% y/y) and full-year contained nickel of 34,302 tonnes (with 105% of nameplate capacity). The release also notes LME average nickel prices rose strongly in 2022 and highlights improved sales and operating momentum into 2023.

The piece describes Trafigura’s alleged $577m loss from discovering that purported nickel containers contained low-grade materials such as carbon steel instead. Credit lines were withdrawn, containers were inspected, and legal actions followed. It highlights counterparty and document-fraud risk, with potential short-term sentiment effects on nickel. | South Korea’s Ministry of Industry, Trade and Resources designated 10 “Strategic Key Minerals” for tighter management, including lithium and nickel, for semiconductor and battery supply security. It plans global mineral and supply-demand maps and an early warning system to monitor risks, which may modestly influence nickel expectations near term. | SMM reports Feb 28 spot premiums for pure nickel falling: Jinchuan premiums average 5,400 yuan/mt (down 350), while NORNICKEL averages 3,000 yuan/mt (down 600). SHFE nickel is said to have bottomed, but upstream firms shipped more toward month-end, weighing on spot demand. | Residents in Palawan, Philippines, are protesting the Ipilan Nickel Corporation (INC) mine, citing lack of free prior informed consent and operations in/near protected old-growth forest zones. Local officials and legal groups push for cease orders and even MPSA cancellation, raising the risk of near-term nickel supply disruption to China. | Fathom Nickel reports completion of drilling at its Gochager Lake project and plans to commence drilling at the Albert Lake Project by March 3, targeting ultramafic Ni-Cu sulphide systems and cobalt. The company expects assays in April but provides no new confirmed production or reserve changes. | The article headline reports that EV nickel project CarLang has defined an updated resource estimate for its A Zone, stating 1.25 million tonnes indicated and 1.16 million tonnes inferred of contained nickel. As this is an exploration/resource-definition update, it may mildly boost near-term nickel sentiment via improved supply outlook. | EV Nickel reported a maiden NI 43-101 resource estimate for the CarLang A Zone at its Shaw Dome project, totaling ~1.25M tonnes indicated and ~1.16M tonnes inferred contained nickel. The company cites nickel pricing assumptions in pit optimization and positions the deposit as major undeveloped supply, but it is not production. | An Indian tycoon sued for missing nickel cargoes told inspectors he had a heart attack shortly before planned checks in Rotterdam. Trafigura alleges shipments contained no nickel, instead carbon steel, and seeks $577m in losses. The story highlights fraud and supply-quality/counterparty risk for nickel. | The podcast features Nickel 28’s chairman discussing recent industrial metals developments in the U.S. after the Inflation Reduction Act and the push to secure domestic supply chains. It also discusses the LME’s credibility in relation to the 2022 nickel short squeeze, which could affect short-term nickel sentiment and positioning.

Power Minerals has started a multi-element resampling program at its Musgrave nickel-copper-cobalt-PGE project in South Australia, using pXRF on drill core samples and sending selected samples for lab analysis. Results will help define drill targets for upcoming drilling, but no production or confirmed resource changes are reported. | Power Minerals has started a core reanalysis and pXRF sampling program at its Musgrave nickel-copper-cobalt-PGE project in South Australia, processing over 1,000 samples. Selected samples were sent for lab analysis to define drill targets, including the Pink Slipper anomaly. No results or production impacts are reported. | Power Nickel reported high-grade Ni-Cu-Co sulphide and PGE assay results from multiple holes at its Nisk deposit and said drilling is ongoing toward late April/early May. While discoveries can support bullish sentiment for nickel and some copper supply prospects, there is no immediate production or inventory impact, limiting price moves. | SMM reports that on March 1 traders offered spot quotes versus SHFE 2304 nickel, with Jinchuan and NORNICKEL premiums rising sharply. Despite the premium increases, SHFE nickel and spot prices fell to 2023 lows. Downstream profit margins did not improve and pure nickel demand remained poor, weakening intraday transactions. | Québec Nickel Corp. reported new drilling results from its Ducros Ducros Ni-Cu-PGE project, including a 4.90-metre interval averaging 0.77% Ni and 0.56% Cu, with one-metre samples up to 1.27% Ni and 1.21% Cu. Follow-up drilling is planned; no production impacts are announced. | Leading Edge Materials says it has identified an expanded zone of cobalt-nickel mineralization at its Bihor Sud project in Romania, with new galleries and planned mapping/sampling after safety inspections and ventilation installation. Trenching proceeded early due to mild weather. This exploration progress could modestly lift expectations for future nickel supply.

Oman signed a mineral exploration agreement with the UK’s Knights Bay to explore for and extract nickel, described as the first deal of its kind with a foreign partner. Initial investment for exploration and evaluation is estimated at $25–$30 million over the first three years, with no near-term output details. | EV Nickel Inc. announces upcoming presentations at PDAC and other events and says it is awaiting assay results from its 2023 W4 drilling later in March. It also discusses clean nickel strategy and a previously released CarLang A Zone maiden resource estimate. No new production or pricing data is provided. | Oman’s Ministry of Energy and Minerals signed a mining concession with Knights Bay for Block 21, giving the UK firm rights to extract high-purity nickel with zero carbon emissions. The project includes exploration and appraisal and plans to process battery minerals (nickel and cobalt) for EV industry demand. | Sky Gold Corp. says the TSX Venture Exchange accepted its 100% acquisition of three nickel-copper-cobalt-platinum group element properties in Ontario. The deal terms include share issuance, cash, and a net smelter/royalty. No production or development timeline is provided, implying limited short-term supply impact for nickel and copper. | Sky Gold Corp. reports that TSX-V has accepted filing for Sky’s 100% acquisition of three nickel-copper-cobalt-PGE properties in northwestern Ontario. The deal terms involve share issuance and cash payments, plus potential additional consideration if a large, viable ore body is identified—no near-term production impact is stated.

London copper was set for a weekly gain as stronger-than-expected China manufacturing and services data boosted demand sentiment, while declining SHFE inventories suggested improving tightness. Markets also weighed a firmer dollar and potential additional U.S. sanctions on China. Aluminium rose slightly; nickel fell on the day. | SMM says bonded zone nickel inventory fell 1,000 mt to 5,600 mt week-on-week as of March 3. Nickel plate stock dropped to 4,130 mt, while briquettes were flat at 1,470 mt due to low stainless steel demand. Spot trading picked up slightly amid lower nickel prices. | The UK Financial Conduct Authority launched an enforcement investigation into the London Metal Exchange’s nickel trading halt decision and its systems and controls around Jan. 1 to March 8, 2022. The regulator appears focused on stopping trade, not on canceling trades. Bank of England reviews also flagged LME Clear shortcomings. | Three Chinese migrant workers filed a human-rights complaint alleging abuse and unsafe conditions at a nickel processing estate in Indonesia, including alleged forced overtime, lack of protective equipment, and withholding passports. The company denied involvement with the IMIP Morowali complex. Allegations could heighten scrutiny and disrupt nickel operations.

SMM reports Ni 1.5% ore CIF prices at about $72/wmt, steady week-on-week, with low Philippines shipments. Supply tightness is pushing mines to seek higher ore quotes, but NPI demand looks weak: traders restocked earlier, hedgers may lower prices, and NPI plants may reject higher ore costs. SMM expects further NPI declines. | SMM says Chinese nickel ore inventories fell 172,000 wmt week-on-week to 7.0 million wmt (55,000 mt Ni content) by March 3, citing low recent arrivals and rigid NPI purchasing. It expects inventories to keep trending lower, potentially around 6 million before the end of the Philippines rainy season. | Nickel ore arrivals at Chinese ports totaled 498,000 wmt as of Feb 24, up 27% week-on-week, with shipments landing at several major ports. The ore is mainly for delivery to NPI plants in Shandong, Jiangsu and Liaoning. Overall imports remain low and downstream demand is rigid, limiting stock build-up. | SMM reports pricing pressure on NORNICKEL nickel affecting nickel sulphate spreads and near-zero electrowinning profits. Ternary demand is weak and firms prefer cheaper liquid nickel sulphate, while nickel sulphate supply is tight due to delayed Indonesia raw-material line commissioning and late February restarts. Costs support prices; expected range is 39,000–40,000 yuan/mt this week. | SMM reports nickel sulphate enterprises’ margins fell further as demand rose but new MHP capacity commissioning was delayed. Nickel salt plants faced high-priced raw materials and tighter high-grade nickel matte supply (some matte lines switched to NPI). Lower downstream purchase prices pushed firms toward reduced profits or losses. | The UK FCA has opened an enforcement investigation into the LME’s 2022 suspension of nickel trading, examining conduct and controls around 1 Jan to 8 Mar 2022 after a surge in nickel prices linked to Russia-Ukraine supply concerns. The Bank of England also flagged governance and risk-management shortcomings at LME Clear. | Electric Royalties announced it will acquire a 0.5% gross revenue royalty (with options for additional royalty) on the Kenbridge nickel project in Ontario. The project is described as nickel-copper mineralized, with a preliminary economic assessment using assumed Ni and Cu prices, but no near-term production changes are stated. | The UK FCA has opened an investigation into the LME’s March 2022 suspension of nickel futures, reviewing trading activity from early 2022 until the March 8 halt. The suspension followed a brief nickel surge above $100,000/t. FCA highlights transparency and risk management as LME may adjust market structure. | The UK FCA announced an enforcement investigation into some of the LME’s conduct and controls between Jan. 1, 2022 and the nickel contract suspension on March 8, 2022. The article notes the probe relates to the period of disorderly nickel trading and adds ongoing uncertainty for LME nickel confidence. | EV Nickel Inc. announced it received CAD 500,000 from the Ontario government for its “Clean Nickel” strategy, including R&D on bioleaching and carbon capture to produce lower-carbon nickel from its Shaw Dome project near Timmins. The non-dilutive funding supports innovation for EV battery supply. | Canada Nickel reported assay results from the first 12 holes at its Texmont nickel project, finding near-surface, thick, high-grade mineralization with multiple intervals above 1% nickel and remaining open at depth and to the north. Management highlighted potential near-term, smaller open-pit production, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel supply prospects.

Hedge funds are suing the London Metal Exchange over the cancellation of billions of dollars of nickel trades after the exchange suspended nickel in March following a violent price spike. The FCA is investigating possible misconduct, while other lawsuits are ongoing, increasing nickel market uncertainty. | Goldman Sachs Commodities Research said lithium prices are expected to fall further and will move lower alongside cobalt and nickel. The note projects a downtrend over the next nine to 12 months, implying bearish sentiment for nickel prices, though the timing extends beyond the immediate weeks focus. | Copper prices edged higher as the dollar weakened and traders anticipated improving China demand from March. LME three-month copper rose slightly, and Shanghai April copper also increased. Aluminium eased on the day and nickel declined, indicating the bullish impulse was strongest for copper rather than industrial metals broadly. | PT Aneka Tambang reported improved unaudited January 2023 performance for its nickel business. Ferronickel production rose to 2,113 tons (+28% YoY) and sales to 2,013 TNi. Nickel ore production reached 1.06 million wmt (+16% YoY) with sales at 1.08 million wmt (+80% YoY). | LME data shows nickel inventories dropping to a multi-year low around 43,884 mt before a slight rebound, then easing again to 44,628 mt. SHFE inventories also widened but rose 14.72% last week to 2,884 mt, indicating tighter LME visibility while domestic stocks recovered.

FPX Nickel reportedly received funding from the Government of Canada aimed at scaling up production of nickel and cobalt for EV battery supply chains. Additional support for output expansion can shift expectations for nickel availability and demand dynamics, potentially supporting nickel prices over the next few weeks. | Harita Nickel begins a $600m investor roadshow ahead of a public listing, in a broader Indonesian push to retain nickel processing after the 2019 raw nickel export ban. The country’s EV-battery investments and growing nickel export value support nickel demand/supply expectations, with some peripheral copper IPO plans mentioned. | Aurubis is constructing the Bleed Treatment Olen Beerse (BOB) hydrometallurgical facility to recover copper and nickel from electrolytes/“bleed” streams at its recycling sites, boosting recycling capacity. Production is scheduled for late 2024 with full operations in 2025/26, so near-term price impact is limited despite involvement of both metals. | Aurubis is building the Bleed treatment Olen Beerse (BOB) hydrometallurgical plant to process copper electrolysis bleed and recover metals including copper and nickel. The facility targets higher recovery from electrolyte streams, with commissioning planned for 2H 2024 and full operation in 2025/26, supporting supply expectations.

SMM reported bonded zone nickel inventory fell 500 mt to 5,100 mt as of March 10. Nickel plate stock declined more amid weak downstream demand, reflecting dropping nickel prices. Nickel briquette demand from stainless steel stayed low, leaving briquette inventories broadly flat. Overall, the inventory move aligns with modest expectations. | Canada announced a ban on importing Russian steel and aluminum as part of sanctions over the Ukraine invasion. Reported additional curtailed trade is expected to be small because Russian shipments had already declined and imports faced prior tariffs, though the move may add some supply-chain pressure. Copper and nickel are not addressed. | Canada will ban imports of Russian steel and aluminum under its sanctions against Moscow. Foreign Affairs Minister Joly ties the policy to weakening Russia’s ability to attack Ukraine and possible regime change. Industry sources say Russia’s Canadian aluminum/steel volumes are small and the overall commercial impact is likely muted, limiting price effects.

Indonesian villagers on Wawonii Island protest nickel mining expansion, alleging threats to land rights and environmental damage. Farmers report land seizures, clashes, detentions, and attacks on or obstruction of mining equipment. Indonesia’s growing nickel processing build-out increases supply risk in the near term, potentially tightening expectations. | Indonesian farmers in Sulawesi are fighting nickel miners over land rights, with reports of detentions, protests, and equipment set on fire. Indonesia’s nickel boom involves expanding mines and processing plants, but ongoing conflict and environmental grievances may disrupt operations. Such near-term supply uncertainty is nickel-price negative.

Villagers on Indonesia’s Wawonii Island confront nickel miners as a mining boom expands Indonesia’s nickel supply chain for EV batteries. Protest actions, detentions, clashes and burned equipment raise the risk of operational disruptions and contested land, potentially affecting near-term nickel production despite growing processing capacity. | SMM reports nickel prices fluctuating with little change in spot premiums and slack intraday spot trades. NPI volumes were transacted at 1,240 yuan/mtu, but producers cut prices due to high inventories and weak markets. On demand, stainless steel sales controls are relaxed and alloy buying stays on demand, weighing on nickel. | Fathom Nickel increased its land position under the Gochager Lake option agreement by 937 hectares at Mal Lake after the optionor acquired the new disposition. The company also updated Albert Lake exploration status and expects assays later in April-May, but provides no production or reserve changes. | Flying Nickel Mining signed an Impact and Benefit Agreement with Norway House Cree Nation to advance the Minago nickel project in Manitoba. The IBA includes jobs, contracting, board participation, share subscription, and revenue-sharing based on nickel revenues, improving certainty around development. | The Mille Lacs Band of Ojibwe and allies launched the “Water Over Nickel” initiative opposing Minnesota plans for the proposed Tamarack nickel mine by Talon Metals. They cite risks to local water, watersheds, and cultural sites and urge officials to prioritize clean water over nickel mining.

SMM reports Ni 1.5% nickel ore at about $71/wmt CIF, slightly lower WoW. Philippine rainy-season conditions keep nickel ore supply tight. NPI demand from stainless producers has been weaker, pressuring NPI prices and prompting wait-and-see restocking, though limited supply suggests nickel ore may stabilize with occasional drops. | London copper fell as investors reassessed China demand prospects after early 2020s factory data grew more slowly than expected and property investment stayed weak. A steadier U.S. dollar also dampened buying interest. In parallel, LME aluminium and nickel were slightly lower on the same day. | Canada Nickel says its first metallurgical test at the Reid ultramafic nickel project near Crawford in Ontario produced a head grade of 0.35% nickel, 0.10% sulphur, and 6.2% iron, alongside reported high nickel recoveries. The results support improved prospects for future nickel output.

TMC entered a non-binding MOU with Japan’s PAMCO to evaluate toll processing 1.3 million tonnes/year of polymetallic nodules from 2025. PAMCO would use existing Hachinohe smelting infrastructure to produce nickel-copper-cobalt alloy and manganese silicate, with further study on upgrading to matte. Near-term effects are uncertain due to study-only terms. | European nickel refined premiums for uncut cathode and briquettes rose sharply, especially in-whs Rotterdam, amid tighter regional availability and higher offers/assessments. LME nickel weakness encouraged some consumer return, but liquidity remained limited at top range levels. Cathode premiums stayed high, while US premiums were mostly flat.

China imported 87,800 mt of nickel hydrometallurgy intermediate products in Dec 2022 (17,700 mt Ni content), down 13% MoM. Nickel matte imports were 30,000 mt in Dec, down 7% MoM. Tsingshan shifted some matte-based lines to NPI, cutting matte output by 9,000 mt in Jan, potentially tightening intermediate supply. | Mistango (15% interest) updates Manibridge drilling results, highlighting over 1% nickel mineralization and a highest-grade continuous intersection of 19.45m at 1.69% NiEq, with assays also incorporating Cu and Co components. These are exploration findings rather than production changes, so near-term metal price effects are likely limited. | The European Union published its Critical Raw Materials Act and designated copper and nickel as critical raw materials. The framework aims to simplify permitting and strengthen access to supply. While potentially supportive for medium-term investment certainty, the excerpt provides no immediate trade or supply shock likely to drive near-term prices. | The article argues copper should benefit from supply tightness, historically low inventories, and ongoing disruptions, reinforced by EU legislation classifying copper and nickel as critical raw materials. It also expects aluminum to remain above historical averages into early 2024. Nickel is framed as having a 2023 surplus despite EV-driven demand. | The FCA confirmed it has opened an enforcement investigation into the LME’s conduct and controls related to the LME’s 2022 suspension of nickel trading. The review follows the disorderly nickel market after Russia’s invasion and price spikes, and aims to assess governance and lessons for future market structure and risk management.

Industrial metals prices fell as investors worried that a banking crisis could worsen and hurt global economic growth. Copper eased on both LME and SHFE. Nickel also declined. Aluminium was mixed but broadly pressured, reflecting risk-off positioning as liquidity promises and a Credit Suisse rescue deal failed to calm markets. | Access World, once owned by Glencore, allegedly shipped over $1.2 million of fake nickel from Rotterdam, with LME warrants totaling 54 tonnes equivalent discovered as non-deliverable under contract specifications. The small quantity (0.14% of live stock) still raises nickel-industry fraud and liquidity concerns, likely pressuring nickel sentiment. | The article says nickel prices have fallen significantly from February to March and remain range-bound, with lower lows needed to confirm a downtrend. It notes South Africa’s load shedding threatens ferrochrome supply for stainless steel, but overall direction for nickel is unclear, with potential for stabilization. | The Mille Lacs Band of Ojibwe launched an initiative opposing a multi-billion-dollar nickel mine. By challenging the project, the action raises the risk of delays, permitting hurdles, or reduced near-term supply prospects for nickel, which may support prices. However, timing and project outcome remain uncertain.

The FCA confirmed it has opened an enforcement investigation into the LME’s decision to suspend the nickel market after disorderly conditions in March 2022, when nickel prices spiked sharply following the Russia-Ukraine invasion. The probe targets LME conduct, systems, and controls, with implications for nickel market governance. | Andrew Forrest’s Wyloo Metals has launched a $760 million takeover bid for BHP-related nickel supplier Mincor Resources, offering $1.40 per share, a 35% premium. The deal raises expectations of changes in ownership and availability of nickel feedstock, which can support nickel sentiment in the near term. | SMM says February spot ferronickel trading was dominated by supply from Indonesia and Dominica, but prices fell sharply as refined nickel and high-grade NPI slumped. Ferronickel premiums over NPI shrank, including Ni 15–20% trading near Indonesian NPI and Ni>35% premiums dropping, while Ni 25–30% premiums held up. | A Dutch warehouse mix-up led JPMorgan Chase to mistakenly buy bags believed to contain over 50 metric tons of nickel, later found to be filled with stones. The incident follows an LME nickel supply error. The amount is small versus inventories, implying limited immediate impact on nickel prices. | Reuters reports copper prices in London fell Tuesday as the dollar recovered from a five-week low and traders worried about a looming global financial crisis. While some risk appetite returned after UBS’s Credit Suisse takeover, LME three-month copper eased 0.4%. Aluminium rose slightly, while SHFE nickel fell 2.6%. | JPMorgan allegedly received bags of stones rather than the purchased nickel after the LME found inspected sacks at a Dutch warehouse failed quality standards. The misdelivered ~54 metric tons were worth about $1.3 million at current nickel prices. LME storage/checking responsibilities may face pressure. | Wyloo Metals, backed by Andrew Forrest, has bid A$760 million for Mincor Resources, prompting a 41% jump in Mincor shares. The assets feed a WA nickel smelter network tied to BHP. While it highlights growing nickel demand, it is primarily takeover-driven and not an immediate supply shock. | SMM reports that on March 21 nickel briquette prices averaged 175,350 yuan/mt, down 5,050 yuan/mt from the previous trading day. The quoted range is 175,000-175,700 yuan/mt, signaling a short-term bearish move in nickel-related pricing. | Midland Exploration begins a C$3.4m nickel-focused exploration program in Nunavik, including lake sediment and airborne ZTEM surveys targeting a crustal-scale structure favorable for Ni-Cu-Co mineralization. It builds on prior Ni-Cu discoveries and shows in grab and channel samples but provides no immediate production or supply changes. | SHFE data cited by SMM show nickel inventories dropped sharply in the week ending March 17. Stocks fell to 2,986 mt, down 31.28% from the previous week. The report implies tighter near-term availability, which can provide upward support to nickel prices. | SMM says spot nickel trading in Shanghai remained sluggish on a slight rise in Jinchuan and Nornickel premiums. Jinchuan premium averaged 7,650 yuan/mt (down 100). Nornickel averaged 2,850 yuan/mt (up 150). Nickel briquette transactions were also slack as briquette still traded at premiums over nickel sulphate. | Trafigura says bank financing for nickel trading remains robust despite fraud losses, but the LME found irregularities in nickel warrants, including non-conformant bagged briquettes discovered via inspections. The market may see more “flight to quality” and risk-off lending, heightening near-term nickel logistics and quality concerns. | Wyloo Consolidated Investments, via Wyloo Metals, launched an A$750m on-market takeover bid for Mincor Resources, offering A$1.40 cash per share for the stake it does not own. Mincor develops a nickel sulphide project in Western Australia, linking the deal to nickel supply sentiment. | Trafigura and other traders received bags of stones instead of nickel under LME contracts stored in a Rotterdam warehouse. LME found nine contracts containing material other than nickel and inspected holdings. Separately, Trafigura faces major losses from alleged nickel substitution with carbon steel, reinforcing nickel supply and quality risk.

The LME cancelled nine nickel contracts after “irregularities” at a Rotterdam warehouse, where JPMorgan was said to own the invalidated nickel warrants. The episode sparked wider inspection of LME nickel on warrant across Europe and Asia, though no further issues were found so far, keeping near-term nickel delivery uncertainty elevated. | JPMorgan owned nine LME nickel contracts that were suspended/canceled after irregularities were found at a Rotterdam warehouse, with the issue backed by stone material rather than metal. The LME and warehouses launched widespread nickel verification. While no broader problems were found, deliverability uncertainty is likely negative for nickel prices.

LME cancelled nine nickel warrants (54 tonnes) due to failures to meet contract specification after some delivered nickel was found to be stone. Concerns over additional non-conforming metal have damaged delivery confidence, widening steep discounts/contango for nearby nickel contracts versus longer-dated prices. | The LME said it found no further irregularities after cancelling nine nickel warrants (54 tonnes) for non-conforming bagged nickel containing stones. The exchange postponed nickel trading to allow warehouse inspections. Traders are rolling positions and nearby contracts show steep discounts, reflecting nervousness about LME delivery quality. | Fathom Nickel says it has acquired new mineral claims at Watts Lake in Saskatchewan through a cash-and-share deal with SKRR Exploration. The expanded land package triples coverage and connects the historic Gochager Lake nickel deposit, strengthening its nickel exploration position; the claims may also host VMS-style lead-zinc mineralization. | Rusal plans additional London lawsuits involving Nornickel CEO Vladimir Potanin, extending a long-running shareholder dispute tied to dividend protections and alleged diversion of Nornickel subsidiaries. Rusal says action protects shareholders, while sanctions apply to Potanin but not directly to Nornickel. No operational impact is indicated. | LME said that, after completing inspections of bagged nickel warrants in licensed warehouses for irregularities in stocks, Asian-hours nickel trading will resume on Monday (March 27). Trading had been postponed by about a week after the LME found nickel failing to meet contract specifications at a warehouse. | LME said Asian-hours nickel trading will resume on March 27 after a full inspection of bagged nickel warrants in LME-licensed warehouses. Trading had been postponed by a week after nickel failing to meet contract specifications was found earlier. The decision follows irregularities in warehouse stocks and LME contract compliance checks. | The LME said a 100% inspection of all bagged nickel warrants in licensed warehouses found no further irregularities. It expects to resume Asian-hours nickel trading on March 27. The update follows last week’s cancellation of some nickel warrants amid concerns about contaminated or incorrect contents. | Wood Mackenzie says China’s renewed growth would most benefit steel, aluminium and copper, with housing/property recovery lifting construction-linked demand. In a high-growth scenario it estimates additional copper and aluminium consumption and suggests that higher energy demand could trigger supply curtailments that can support nickel and other metals. | Wood Mackenzie expects China’s reopening and faster growth to raise construction-driven demand for copper and aluminium, with additional tonnes forecast for both. In a higher-growth scenario, exporters and stronger activity could support metal prices. It also warns higher energy demand may trigger supply curtailments, potentially boosting nickel as well.

SMM reports that as of 10:06:35 on Mar 24, the most-traded SHFE nickel contract was quoted at 177,670 yuan/mt, up 1.0% over 19 minutes. The short-term increase suggests bullish intraday price momentum for nickel on the Shanghai futures exchange. | ASX-listed Dynamic Metals says it has made a new nickel sulphide discovery, with drill results suggesting a largely continuous 30m mineralised hit. A 3m section returned no ore due to technical issues, but the finding could still support expectations for future nickel supply. | Copper prices eased from a three-week high as markets weighed banking-sector turbulence and the Fed’s cautious stance after a rate hike. A steadier dollar limited further gains. The article also notes mild moves in aluminium and stronger nickel on SHFE, reflecting broader risk sentiment rather than a new supply-demand shock. | Copper prices rose to a three-week high for a second day as banking-sector unease kept the U.S. dollar near seven-week lows. Reuters cites supportive FX effects for dollar-priced commodities and expectations around the Fed’s rate path. Aluminium and nickel were also higher on the day. | GEM plans a South Korea nickel battery-material joint venture, investing about 1.21 trillion won to build capacity of at least 43,000 tonnes per year by 2026. The project targets U.S. EV tax credit rules under the IRA, shifting sourcing to North America allies and boosting nickel-linked demand. | GEM, SK On and ECOPRO Materials plan a South Korea joint venture investing about 1.21 trillion won to build capacity for 43,000 tonnes of nickel-based battery materials annually. The project is designed to satisfy U.S. Inflation Reduction Act sourcing rules for EV tax credits, boosting expected nickel-related demand. | SMM reports bonded zone nickel inventory fell 300 mt week-on-week to 4,600 mt as of March 24, with briquettes at 1,370 mt and plates at 3,230 mt. Despite inventories declining in line with expectations, nickel prices remain under pressure at lows. Downstream buying is on demand, while new-energy briquette demand stays weak. | SMM reports Chinese nickel ore inventories fell 185,000 wmt week-on-week to 6.41 million wmt as of March 24, with Ni content of 50,000 mt. Port stocks also declined at seven major ports. Nickel ore prices dropped on lower NPI prices, but buyers expect further reductions as Philippines rains continue. | GlobalData says Russia is the third-largest nickel producer, with 2023 output slightly down versus 2022, and exports up about 2% in 2023. It expects Russian nickel exports to decline modestly (CAGR ~1.14%) from 2023-2027. No immediate supply shock is described.

SMM reported that pure nickel inventories in six China regions fell by 379 mt to 5,670 mt as of March 24. Nickel briquette inventories were down 50 mt to 580 mt, while nickel plate inventories declined 379 mt to 5,090 mt, driven by downstream demand for on-demand purchases. | Nickel prices rose 4.82% last week despite a Fed rate hike that was not initially bullish. The report links the rally to tightening Chinese spot supply as pure nickel imports incurred losses, while demand for pure nickel stayed weak. Stainless steel mill cut news weighed on NPI, but overall nickel support prevailed. | Reuters reports copper holding a narrow range with small gains on both LME and SHFE as investors weigh global economic headwinds and banking-system worries. LME nickel resumes Asian hours after a prior suspension and cancellations, while LME aluminium rises modestly. Overall moves are mild, implying limited net impact. | Copper traded in a tight range as investors weighed global economic headwinds and banking risks, with LME copper up 0.3%. LME nickel resumed Asian hours after a year-long suspension, but fell 0.6%. SHFE nickel rose sharply, while aluminium was up modestly. | Copper prices held in a tight range, with three-month LME copper up 0.3%. China imported 1.48 million mt of nickel ore in February, up 4.31% month-on-month and 19.26% year-on-year, with higher volumes from the Philippines as rainy-season disruption eased. No assays are finalized. | Fathom Nickel reported assay results from two drill holes at the Gochager Lake Property, intersecting high-grade nickel-bearing mineralization and testing for cobalt. Copper reached a six-week high after US dollar weakness driven by unexpectedly lower US producer prices raised expectations that the Fed is nearing the end of its rate-hike cycle. The weaker dollar supported dollar-priced metals. The article also notes gains in LME/SHFE aluminium and nickel. | Copper rose to a seven-week high as the dollar slumped after U.S. producer prices unexpectedly fell, strengthening bets that the Fed is nearing the end of its rate hikes. Nickel premiums rose (NORNICKEL), weakening transactions, though briquette demand from nickel salt factories persists. | Copper on the LME reversed from a seven-week high as the U.S. dollar rebounded on expectations of another Fed rate hike. Gains were supported by lower exchange inventories and stronger-than-expected China trade data, but analysts said upside is limited given improving supply and subdued Chinese demand. Nickel and aluminium also rose intraday. | Elliott and Jane Street seek damages after the LME suspended nickel trading and voided transactions on March 8 last year, when nickel briefly doubled above $100,000/t. A judicial review runs June 20-22 to judge whether the LME acted lawfully, with a remedies trial planned if needed. Other related lawsuits are stayed. | Mitsubishi says it is appraising investments in nickel projects, citing rapidly rising EV battery demand, and is verifying profitable options given technical and cost hurdles. SMM reports firm premiums for NORNICKEL nickel due to warrant delivery and no cleared spots flowing into China. Nickel demand improves with 300-series stainless and alloy output, supporting prices. However, projected electrowinning output growth amid recession-related macro concerns is expected to add supply and pressure nickel prices. | As of April 7, Chinese ports received 770,000 Wmt of nickel ore, up 15% week-on-week. Arrivals increased at Yangjiang, Jinzhou, Ningde and Lanshan. Nickel ore prices dipped slightly on rising NPI prices, while Philippine mines resumed operations slowly, with further arrivals expected in late April. | SMM reports Apr 17 spot nickel trading was slack. Jinchuan nickel premiums rose to an average of 6,900 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL premiums averaged 6,750 yuan/mt. SHFE nickel prices continued higher and premiums stayed firm, but intraday spot and briquette transactions were weak, keeping price momentum muted. | Nickel ore (Ni 1.5%) CIF prices in China slipped slightly to $52/wmt as Philippines supply remains limited because many mines haven’t started. NPI prices rose to 1,052 yuan/mtu WoW, but NPI plants hesitate due to unpredictable price trends and low purchase offers, keeping ore quotes firm. Short-term ore pricing likely stable. | SMM reports nickel sulphate enterprises shipping at lower levels due to slack demand and low profit margins. April production schedules are planned to dip 15.93% month-on-month, while nickel briquette premiums over nickel sulphate previously widened sharply. Slightly sufficient raw-material supply may push raw-material producers to cut quotes, weighing on nickel-linked prices. | The piece reviews nickel supply and demand for EVs, noting Indonesian production growth and muted near-term demand from a slow Chinese reopening and recession fears, which weigh on nickel prices. It also highlights 2022’s extreme nickel volatility tied to the Ukraine conflict and a longer-term EV demand outlook through 2030.

Copper rose on Tuesday after faster-than-expected Chinese GDP growth boosted expectations for near-term demand. While some indicators pointed to softer factory orders later in April/May, the data still supported prices. LME aluminium gained on warehouse inflows and March output, and nickel was also higher as industrial metals moved together. | SMM said April 18 spot nickel trading in Shanghai remained sluggish. Jinchuan nickel premiums averaged 6,150 yuan/mt, down 750 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL averaged 6,250 yuan/mt, down 500. Despite lower premiums, NORNICKEL supply was still tight and premiums remained firm. Nickel briquette transactions were slack.

Copper dipped in Asia as traders weighed the Fed’s outlook for further interest rate hikes, supporting a firmer dollar and pressuring dollar-priced industrial metals. LME tin retreated after Myanmar mining suspension concerns eased. LME aluminium and nickel also eased, with SHFE aluminium and nickel showing opposing gains. | Copper fell in Asian trading as traders weighed prospects of additional U.S. Federal Reserve rate hikes, supporting the dollar and pressuring non-ferrous prices. Three-month LME copper declined and Shanghai copper ended lower. LME aluminium dropped and nickel was little changed, reflecting overall market risk sentiment tied to Fed outlook. | SMM’s midday market wrap notes the dollar was slightly higher and oil slightly lower. SHFE base metals broadly rose, with nickel up 4.36% and aluminium also increasing. In contrast, LME base metals fell across the board. The piece is descriptive and lacks specific drivers for nickel and aluminium. | SMM reports that ferronickel prices for small orders fell as stainless-steel mill demand weakened and NPI prices dropped quickly.
