The move expands POSCO’s nickel supply chain for secondary battery demand and EV growth. | Nickel 28 reported Q1 2023 performance from its Ramu nickel-cobalt operation, including higher contained nickel production and a large jump in nickel sales versus the prior-year quarter. It also cited improved cash costs and received a US$9.7 million distribution while reducing construction debt. Overall signals supportive near-term nickel fundamentals. | FPX Nickel reports drilling and confirmatory geotechnical results for its Baptiste nickel project and outlines multiple awaruite nickel target advancements at the Klow property in British Columbia. The company also begins environmental assessment work and completes pilot testwork to produce concentrate, aiming to support discussions with EV battery supply partners. | Indonesia plans to stop tax holidays for investments into nickel pig iron (NPI), saying downstreaming should reach higher nickel content and not only intermediate products. The move targets lower-grade smelter expansion while the global nickel market faces major oversupply, with Indonesia output surging and capacity additions from TBP and Merdeka. | Indonesia’s minister says the government will cut tax breaks meant to attract nickel investment, specifically to discourage projects deemed low quality. The policy change is expected to reduce or delay some nickel-capacity development, weighing on near-term nickel supply expectations and pressuring nickel prices lower over coming weeks. | InZinc Mining outlines a fully funded 2023 exploration program at its Indy project, following 2022 drilling that intersected nickel-cobalt-copper and minor copper in oxide material, plus new nickel-cobalt-copper targets and zinc targets. The article is about exploration groundwork and drilling timing, not immediate production effects. | Copper held steady on the LME as investors awaited the Fed’s rate decision, balancing nervous macro sentiment against a tight longer-term supply outlook. On-warrant LME stocks rose to the highest in nearly four months. Aluminium slipped and nickel rose modestly amid broader commodity pressure from tighter policy fears. | EV Nickel reports preliminary bench-scale bioleaching tests on its W4 nickel sulphides, using locally sourced bacteria, achieving nickel extraction of 86.0%–90.5% over 8–12 days. The company says Tank Bioleach could reduce smelter/refiner reliance and acid use, improving prospects for domestic nickel product. | Copper is expected to drift higher but remain rangebound amid weaker-than-expected China demand and falling LME stocks, alongside mine-output disruption risks trimming surplus estimates. Aluminium faces softer sentiment from increased output and rising inventories in China. Nickel is pressured by concerns over rising Indonesian supply and broad nickel-bearing exports. | US Senator Rubio asked multiple US agencies to investigate Ford’s Chinese partnership tied to an Indonesia nickel processing plant, arguing it threatens national security. The probe could create regulatory and legal uncertainty around the venture, potentially delaying nickel processing and pressuring short-term nickel supply expectations. | U.S. Senator Marco Rubio asked the Biden administration to investigate Ford’s Indonesia nickel processing plant in partnership with PT Vale Indonesia and China’s Zhejiang Huayou. Rubio alleges national security concerns linked to China’s Belt and Road strategy. The probe could disrupt expectations for nickel processing timelines and demand/supply balance. | U.S. Senator Marcio Rubio asked the Biden administration to investigate Ford’s plan with Vale Indonesia and China’s Huayou to build a $4.5 billion nickel processing plant in Indonesia. The probe raises potential delays or disruptions to nickel supply development, adding near-term downside risk for nickel markets. | A Reuters poll expects copper to recover slightly but remain rangebound, with low inventories supporting prices while weak China physical demand and recession fears cap gains; aluminium is pressured by higher output and rising inventories; nickel is bearish on concerns of expanding Indonesian supply and growing market surpluses.

SMM reports May 4 spot pure nickel premiums versus SHFE 2306 falling, with Jinchuan and NORNICKEL premiums still high in absolute terms. SHFE nickel rose early, tracking overseas nickel futures, but premiums drifted lower and spot trades missed expectations. Nickel briquette demand was slack as prices stayed above nickel sulphate premiums. | Indonesia is cutting tax holidays for investment in low-quality nickel products like NPI, aiming to redirect spending toward higher-value downstream processing and reserve nickel for EV-battery materials. The move may slow incremental supply, but the market already faces major Indonesian oversupply, with INSG projecting a sizable surplus this year. | SMM reports nickel prices declined last week as macro factors and improving supply reduced tightness. Imported pure nickel premiums fell sharply after spot inflows, failing to boost demand, while Jinchuan demand held better. Civil/high-temperature alloy demand was soft, leaving no strong support for nickel. | Copper prices rose after the dollar weakened, but gains were capped by weaker-than-expected China demand signals, including contracting factory activity. Analysts highlighted reduced bullish positioning and bearish forecasts. Aluminium was nearly unchanged, while nickel rose modestly, reflecting limited broader momentum beyond copper. | SMM notes a rebound in industrial metals alongside weaker oil and a steady dollar. SHFE nickel jumped 2.85%, with SHFE copper up slightly and SHFE aluminium down marginally. LME copper gained 1.26% and LME nickel rose 0.65%, while LME aluminium was slightly higher, suggesting mild bullish bias. | Flying Nickel Mining Corp. released additional drilling assay results from its Minago Nickel PGM project in Manitoba, reporting multiple drill intervals with nickel grades (about 0.25% to 1.61% Ni) plus platinum and palladium. The update supports ongoing resource/resource-definition work, with no immediate change to production or market supply flows. | Fortune Nickel and Gold provides an update on exploration drilling plans for its Gowan Township project in Ontario, including recommending and collecting a helicopter-borne airborne TDEM survey to refine targets for future drilling. The company cites nickel prospects and does not report resource or production changes. | CanAlaska signed a definitive agreement to acquire the Mel nickel deposit and adjacent claims in Manitoba, subject to TSX Venture approval. The company positions the asset as part of a district-scale sulphide nickel portfolio near Vale’s Thompson nickel operations. The release frames future nickel demand but provides no immediate supply impact. | CanAlaska Uranium signed an agreement to acquire the Mel nickel deposit and adjacent mineral claims in Manitoba’s Thompson Nickel Belt. The company frames the deal as adding a high-grade nickel resource near Vale’s operations. The announcement is subject to TSX Venture approval, with no near-term output changes described.

Citi Research cut its 2023 and 2024 forecasts for nickel and aluminium, indicating weaker expectations for those markets. It simultaneously raised its copper price forecasts for 2023-2025, implying improved outlook. Overall, the revisions point to mixed metal fundamentals rather than a single immediate directional move. | Citi Research cut its 2023-2024 price forecasts for nickel and aluminium, lowering nickel and multiple aluminium annual averages. It simultaneously raised its copper forecasts for 2023-2025. The update may affect market sentiment, but it is a projection change rather than a direct near-term supply or demand disruption. | Citi Research lowered its 2023-2024 forecasts for nickel and aluminium, reducing nickel and aluminium price expectations for 2023-2024. It simultaneously raised copper forecasts for 2023-2025, increasing expected copper prices. The note is about research revisions rather than immediate supply-demand shocks. | Power Nickel closed the final tranche of its private placement, raising CAD 516,000 to fund Quebec exploration at its NISK Ni-Cu sulfide project, including planned assays and metallurgical work. The release highlights Critical Mineral incentives and expects multiple near-term results. This can modestly support nickel sentiment, with limited copper spillover. | LME nickel stocks fell 28% since early January to 39,966 tonnes after small withdrawals from Rotterdam, suggesting tighter LME-deliverable supply. The nickel forward curve remains in persistent contango and contracts show liquidity concerns and potential volatility. However, weak demand keeps physical premiums under pressure globally. | Posco will invest $440m to build a nickel smelter in Indonesia’s Halmahera, targeting 52,000 tons per year of nickel matte (70–75% nickel) for EV battery intermediates. The project follows Indonesia’s export restrictions aimed at boosting downstream processing and expands Posco’s nickel supply ambitions. | Satellite-observed smelter activity fell sharply in April, with global copper dispersion at its lowest since March 2021 and additional planned shutdowns/maintenance in Indonesia and Bulgaria. Marex/SAVANT link weaker smelting activity to higher treatment and refining charges and an inverse relationship with LME copper, pointing to near-term bearish pressure. Nickel dispersion also slipped. | SMM notes crude oil prices gained over 1% and the U.S. dollar index edged lower. In the reported session, LME metals rose across the board: copper +0.68%, nickel +0.65%, and aluminium +0.52%. SHFE showed mixed moves, with nickel sharply lower and copper/aluminium slightly down.

SHFE nickel traded rangebound as downstream firms were cautious about restocking after Labour Day. NPI holders stayed slower to ship despite profits. Mild gains in stainless steel spot and HRC aided demand, but #316L fell sharply. Overall tight domestic and abroad nickel spot supply keeps prices supported, so SHFE nickel should remain rangebound. | Brokers flag pressure across industrial raw materials. Citi turns bearish on copper, expecting slower China demand and weaker US/Europe consumption amid high inventories and disappointing orders, trimming the 0–3 month copper forecast. Macquarie expects a large global nickel surplus, with nickel pig iron and sulphate oversupply widening discounts versus LME. | Citi turns bearish on copper, citing softer global PMI readings, weaker China consumption expectations, high/rising inventories in the US and Europe, and continued order disappointment, cutting its near-term copper estimate. Separately, Macquarie expects a large nickel production surplus, with nickel pig iron and sulphate in oversupply and trading at discounts. | Copper steadied but sentiment weakened as China data pointed to softer growth and demand, with April imports down 12.5% and manufacturing activity slipping. LME-approved copper inventories rose 40% since April 18 and a firmer dollar weighed on prices. Nickel fell on rising supplies; aluminium edged up modestly. | Environmental group WALHI and others urge Tesla to stop or limit direct investment and use of Indonesia’s nickel, citing pollution, smelter emissions, and alleged rights violations affecting fishing communities. With Indonesia a major nickel supplier, the scrutiny could increase compliance or disruption risk, weighing on nickel market sentiment.

Copper prices rose on a weaker U.S. dollar ahead of U.S. inflation data, but gains were limited by expectations of subdued demand in Q2 and rising supply. Peru’s March copper output jumped 20.4% after mine stoppages. Aluminium and nickel were roughly steady to mixed as traders waited for CPI. | SMM reports China’s refined nickel output at 17,600 mt in April, slightly higher month-on-month, after offsetting shutdown/overhaul effects with production increases in Jiangsu. May output is forecast to rise to 17,900 mt, supported by continued Jiangsu ramp-ups and normal operations in Guangxi, suggesting added supply pressure. | SMM reported that May 10 spot nickel transactions were sluggish. Jinchuan nickel premiums rose slightly, but NORNICKEL premiums fell, with nickel prices moving down. Nickel briquette trades were slack and priced at premiums over nickel sulphate, reflecting weaker near-term demand conditions for nickel. | Macarthur Minerals provides an update on the Lake Giles tenements, where ultramafic komatiite-type rocks and serpentinised olivine/talc-carbonate formations are viewed as having high potential for nickel sulphides. The company plans nickel-targeted drilling, EM surveys, and assaying to refine prospects, indicating improved exploration focus. | Québec Nickel Corp. initiated scoping metallurgical tests at its Ducros project to assess whether the Fortin Sill Zone can produce a saleable Ni-Cu-Co-PGE+Au concentrate. SGS will run grindability, flotation, and separation tests using 150 kg of drill core, with results expected later in Q4 2023.

China’s GEM plans a $500 million joint project in Indonesia’s Morowali Industrial Park to produce 20,000 tonnes per year of nickel intermediate product for the new energy sector. GEM holds 51%, CAHAYA JAYA INVESTMENT 26%, and Weiming 18%. The announcement links directly to nickel supply expectations. | SMM reports May 11 nickel market weakness. Jinchuan nickel premiums averaged 8,900 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL premiums fell to about 6,100 yuan/mt. Nickel price pressure is linked to poor downstream demand and recession-hit trading, with higher nickel warrants and slack briquette transactions pushing premiums down. | Pan Global said it has secured surface access to begin drilling immediately at the Romana West target within its Escacena project in Spain, aiming to test an on-strike extension of the La Romana copper-tin-silver discovery. Two rigs will mobilize and 25–30 holes are planned, with results pending.

Mallee Resources says its Avebury nickel mine has exited care-and-maintenance and is ramping up. By end of March it had about 210 staff and produced and sold the first 10,000 tonnes of nickel-cobalt concentrate. Output is building toward nameplate capacity (900,000 tpa), supporting higher near-term supply. | SMM reports SHFE nickel weakened on poor downstream demand and recession-driven trades, with rising nickel warrants and falling spot premiums. NPI supply is increasing as more Indonesian shipments arrive, while low-inventory holders delay sales. Stainless steel futures slid with nickel, pulling spot slightly lower. SMM expects nickel to trade rangebound today. | Copper was up on Friday but set for its biggest weekly drop since November as weak global economic data darkened demand expectations and rising supplies pressured prices. LME copper inventories rose toward a two-month high and a stronger dollar hurt sentiment, weighing on the broader metals complex including nickel and aluminium. | Nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 243,000 wmt WoW to 6.36 million wmt as of May 12, with total Ni content at 50,000 mt. Ore prices rose slightly. Buyers returned to rigid-demand purchases, while mines planned higher quotes; Philippine spot shipments resume despite poor weather, keeping supply expectations tighter. | SMM said May 12 Jinchuan nickel premiums rose on supply tightness, but spot trading was average because futures prices are low. NORNICKEL premiums also edged up. Nickel briquette prices declined and deal activity was slack, with briquettes still trading at premiums over nickel sulphate. | Copper hit the lowest level in over five months as fears of slowing demand in China, rising LME warehouse inventories, and a stronger U.S. dollar pressured prices. The sell-off was reinforced by recent Chinese inflation data. Aluminium was slightly higher on the day, while nickel’s gains reflected short-covering amid expected surpluses. | Norilsk Nickel said Rotterdam has allowed it to continue selling nickel through its transshipment hub after earlier disruptions. The company is also using the Tangier port in Morocco for product logistics. Continued nickel deliveries point to easing supply constraints, likely supporting nickel prices in the coming days. | The article says the LME will require warehouses to verify (“feel”) their nickel bags following a heist. The change targets accountability and reduces uncertainty about physical nickel availability against LME warrants. Such operational disruptions can tighten deliverable supplies and support short-term nickel prices. | The LME plans tighter rules for how licensed warehouses handle nickel, including magnet/metal-detector tests, “touch inspections,” and weighing bags before delivery out of the LME system. The move follows cases where nickel delivered to traders proved to be stone and earlier irregularities in LME-warranted stocks.

Benchmark London copper prices fell in Asian trading as the U.S. dollar firmed to a five-week high and investors worried about weaker China demand ahead of industrial activity data. Rising inventories and a slowdown in global growth pressured sentiment. LME aluminium and nickel also declined. | Copper rebounded after a five-month low as China’s PBOC injected liquidity, signaling support for economic growth and improving base-metal sentiment. The weaker dollar and “bargain hunting” aided prices. Aluminium rose on lower LME on-warrant stocks, while nickel weakened on vulnerability concerns and forecast cuts. | Emerita Resources reported assay results from 21 drill holes at its Iberian Belt West La Infanta deposit, highlighting polymetallic massive sulphide intervals including ~1.4% copper over 4.2m in one hole. The company also received a permit for Infanta Sur to start exploration. No nickel or aluminium impact is indicated. | Nickel Asia reported Q1 net income down 8%, citing foreign exchange losses and a lower weighted average nickel ore price. The move suggests weaker nickel ore pricing during the quarter, which can influence short-term sentiment about nickel supply/demand and producer cost conditions.

Qianhai Mercantile Exchange plans to launch contracts for lithium carbonate, nickel sulphate, and possibly one other battery metal, alongside considering a low-carbon “green aluminium” physical contract in China. The move targets EV-battery demand and rising preferences for lower-emissions aluminium, but no near-term supply or inventory change is cited. | SMM reports that Chinese ports received 2.67 million wmt of nickel ore in April, up 42% month-on-month, while Philippine exports rose to 2.36 million wmt, up 52%. The monsoon disruption eased and mining recovered, with demand picking up at the same time. May shipments are expected to rise further. | China’s nickel ore imports rebounded in March to 2.12 million mt (+42.9% MoM), with laterite ore and higher shipments from the Philippines after monsoon disruptions. Nickel sulphide ore imports also rose. Domestic port inventories increased, and April imports are expected to grow as NPI supply tightens. | A Thomson Reuters Foundation report warns that nickel mining for EV battery supply chains in Indonesia and the Philippines involves environmental damage and alleged human-rights abuses. It notes Indonesia’s nickel ore export restrictions, Philippines mine closure/resumption history, and recent pledges to improve monitoring—raising reputational and compliance risks for nickel producers. | China’s nickel imports fell sharply in March. Refined nickel totaled 4,443 mt, down 52% MoM and 77% YoY, while nickel powder and flake reached 1,310 mt, down 35% MoM and 58% YoY. Nickel briquettes rose MoM but fell YoY; Russia nickel plate imports dropped to zero. | China imported 129,000 mt of hydrometallurgical intermediate nickel products in March, up 13% month-on-month (equivalent to 24,600 mt Ni). High-nickel matte imports reached 20,000 mt and were up year-on-year, with Indonesia driving most growth. This suggests firmer nickel material inflows for processing. | Palladium One Mining announced initial results from 2023 exploration at its Tyko nickel-copper project in Ontario, using updated airborne magnetic survey data to refine chonolith/feeder dyke targeting and begin field mapping and sampling. The release is exploratory with no immediate production or supply disruption mentioned. | Churchill Resources announced a NI 43-101 technical report for its Florence Lake nickel project, identifying 43 high-priority nickel targets and recommending a 2023 field program and later drilling. It also updates plans for the Taylor Brook project with deeper drilling under prior high-grade Ni/Cu intersections. No production or confirmed new resources.

SMM’s daily wrap shows most SHFE/LME base metals closed lower. SHFE nickel fell over 3% and LME nickel fell 0.9%, while SHFE copper and LME copper dropped about 0.7–0.9%. Aluminium was the exception: SHFE aluminium rose ~0.4% and LME aluminium edged up ~0.24%. | Wave Nickel agreed to buy Brazil’s Niquelândia nickel mine and processing plant from CBA to produce nickel MHP using a new microwave process. Output ramp-up is gradual, starting with a demonstration plant by end-2024 and full scale later, with potential cost and emissions advantages. Nickel sulfate/MHP prices have been under pressure. | Canada announced nearly CAD 360 million to support research, development and expansion in key minerals, including copper, nickel and aluminium, and plans to simplify mining licensing. It also issued a Canada-U.S. joint statement on building a key mineral supply chain, potentially improving medium-term supply expectations and sentiment. | St-Georges Eco-Mining reported partial re-assay results for drill hole MN22-03 at its Manicouagan project, including high-grade nickel and copper intersections alongside platinum-group metals. The update improves geological confidence for future resource potential, but remains exploration-focused with no immediate supply impact on near-term copper or nickel prices. | Nickel 28 reported fiscal 2023 results for its 8.56% stake in the Ramu nickel-cobalt operation in Papua New Guinea. The company cited strong production and sales, lower average cash costs, significant debt repayment, and expectations of continued strength in 2023 as EV demand for nickel-rich batteries improves. | St-Georges Eco-Mining reported re-assay results for drill hole MN22-03 at its Manicouagan project, including intervals averaging 2.43% nickel and 1.18% copper, with individual quarter-meter samples up to about 3.36% copper and 4.1% nickel. The update boosts exploration sentiment for both metals. | XCana Petroleum Corp. reports completing an 80-acre expansion to gain full ownership of the historically copper-producing Boyer Copper Deposit, adjacent to its Nevada Nickel #73 claim. The company frames the “Table Mountain Critical Mineral Project” as targeting copper and nickel among others, but offers no production timelines.

Nickel Industries is profiled as demand for nickel rises, driven by stainless steel and EV-related expectations. However, the piece warns about regulatory risk in Indonesia (processing rules, potential emissions sanctions) and ESG/low-carbon nickel requirements that could pressure its coal-based production. Net effect on near-term nickel pricing is mixed. | SMM reports spot nickel premiums rose amid low supply while nickel futures fell sharply. With economic recession limiting demand, trades remain weak even as spot activity picks up. However, expected growth in supply—driven by spot inflows for warrant delivery—should push stainless steel spot prices lower, weighing on nickel. | Copper prices fell in London as a stronger U.S. dollar reached seven-week highs and LME-registered on-warrant stocks rose to a six-month peak. The article also describes weakness across base metals, with nickel touching a fresh multi-month low and aluminium trading lower, reflecting tighter financial conditions and softer demand expectations. | Nickel 28 filed its management information circular and proxy materials for its June 12 shareholder meeting, alongside a board letter urging votes for its nominees amid a takeover attempt by Pelham. The release reiterates the company’s nickel-cobalt exposure via its Ramu joint venture and royalties, but provides no operational or supply changes. | WBMS reported March 2023 copper cathode output below consumption, leaving a supply shortage of 74,400 mt, while primary aluminium posted oversupply of 148,200 mt and refined nickel oversupply of 5,600 mt. These figures point to tight copper but substantial surplus conditions in aluminium and nickel. | WBMS reported March 2023 copper cathode output of 2.1Mt versus consumption of 2.17Mt, leaving a 74,400t supply shortage. Primary aluminium ran in oversupply (148,200t), and nickel also showed a small oversupply. Overall, the copper deficit is the clearest near-term supportive factor. | Sherritt reported a worker death at its Moa nickel/cobalt joint venture in Cuba, the second fatality in months. The company says injuries resulted from a fall and that it is implementing immediate safety measures while working with authorities. Moa mines nickel laterite for Sherritt refining, but no production downtime is stated. | The UK introduced sanctions prohibiting imports of Russian-origin copper, aluminum, and nickel, alongside a wider ban on Russian diamonds. The measure directly restricts Russian metal supply into the UK/market, raising expectations of tighter availability and supporting near-term price strength for industrial metals. | Rishi Sunak at the G7 summit in Japan announces expanded sanctions banning imports of Russian-origin copper, aluminium and nickel, alongside a wider package restricting Russian diamonds. Such measures reduce access to Russian metal supplies for the market and can tighten short-term availability, potentially lifting prices. | Rishi Sunak says G7 leaders will announce wider sanctions on Russia, including a ban on Russian-origin imports of copper, aluminium and nickel. The measures aim to restrict financing and war capacity tied to Russian supply chains, with the UK and allies expected to announce further steps over the weekend. | The UK government announced sanctions banning imports of Russian-origin copper, aluminium, and nickel, alongside Russian diamonds, as part of wider restrictions on 86 Russian individuals and firms linked to Russia’s military-industrial complex. The UK’s import ban is expected to tighten supply availability and support prices for the affected metals. | Rishi Sunak said the G7 will ban or restrict imports of Russian diamonds and also extend sanctions to Russian-origin copper, aluminium and nickel. The measures aim to pressure Russia after the invasion of Ukraine. The news signals tighter trade access for Russian metal supply into allied markets.

Reuters reports analysts expect global nickel prices to fall as Indonesia expands nickel capacity and production. Indonesia’s output growth in NPI and MHP is forecast to relieve supply constraints, while production costs decline. CRU and others project the LME three-month nickel contract dropping this year and below $20,000 in 2024. | UK Prime Minister Rishi Sunak announced an embargo on imports of Russian diamonds and also copper, aluminium and nickel. Reporting indicates additional restrictions on Russian diamond trade and sanctions targeting firms tied to Russia’s military-industrial sector. The move is aimed at cutting Russian economic inputs into global markets. | The UK Prime Minister announced an embargo banning imports of Russian diamonds and also Russian copper, aluminium, and nickel. UK reporting says sanctions include a broader market freeze and additional measures. Such trade restrictions on Russian metal supply can tighten availability and provide upward price pressure for copper, aluminium, and nickel. | The UK Prime Minister is reported to have announced an embargo on imports of Russian diamonds, including copper, aluminium, and nickel. The measure is part of sanctions related to Russia’s actions in Ukraine, alongside asset freezes and individual/entity restrictions. The EU is also considering diamond trade restrictions. | Copper is set for a fifth straight weekly decline as the U.S. dollar stays near two-month highs and markets price in additional Fed rate hikes. Weak Chinese demand dampens sentiment. Aluminium and nickel show intraday gains, but the broader macro backdrop caps industrial metals, leaving weekly momentum bearish. | SMM reported that spot premiums for pure nickel rose on May 19, with Jinchuan premiums averaging 11,650 yuan/mt and NORNICKEL averaging 6,900 yuan/mt. The publication links the premium gains to improving transactions and tighter pure nickel supply, indicating stronger near-term nickel market sentiment. | Ahead of the G7 Japan summit, the UK is set to unveil sanctions and export controls targeting Russia. Trade Data Monitor shows Britain imported little or no Russian copper, but did import unwrought aluminium and refined nickel from Russia in 2022-2023. Such curbs can reduce Russian metal flows and affect short-term pricing expectations. | Britain and other G7 states plan new sanctions and export controls against Russia. Factbox data shows UK Russian imports of refined copper were effectively absent in 2021 and tiny via 15,252 tonnes last year; aluminium imports from Russia fell versus 2021; refined nickel imports were much lower or absent in earlier periods. | The article outlines Britain’s recent imports of sanctioned Russian metals before new G7 sanctions and export controls are announced in Japan. It notes some Russian-origin refined copper was imported previously (none in 2021/2022 refined), aluminium supply from Russia, and Russian refined nickel. Curbs on such flows can tighten supply and lift prices. | UK Prime Minister Sunak plans new import bans on Russian copper, aluminium and nickel as part of fresh G7 sanctions. The factbox details limited UK prior imports from Russia (nickel none in early 2023; aluminium and copper past volumes). Reduced Russian trade is likely to tighten supply and support prices. | The UK said it will announce new import bans on Russian copper, aluminium and nickel alongside fresh G7 sanctions at a summit in Japan. Trade data show the UK imported limited Russian volumes—none for refined copper, none early-2023 for aluminium and nickel—while refined nickel and unwrought aluminium imports occurred in 2022. | The UK plans new import bans on Russian copper, aluminium and nickel, alongside new G7 sanctions targeting Russia over the war in Ukraine. Britain imported no Russian refined copper in recent years, while it bought unwrought aluminium and some refined nickel from Russia in 2022. The bans could tighten supply expectations. | Copper rebounded 1.2% on LME on optimism that U.S. debt talks will avert a default, lifting broader risk assets and supported by a slightly weaker dollar. The article also notes gains in aluminium and a strong rise in nickel, reflecting macro-driven sentiment despite headwinds from China demand and recession risks. | Copper rebounded as investors grew more optimistic that U.S. debt talks will avert a federal default. LME three-month copper rose about 1% and Comex futures gained, supported by a softer dollar and traders covering shorts. The piece also flags headwinds from weak China demand and recession risks ahead. | The article notes that LME profits have fallen to an 8-year low as a “nickel crisis” hits the exchange. While details are limited, the framing suggests significant stress in LME nickel markets, which usually implies near-term weakness for nickel prices due to trading disruptions and higher risk. | FPX Nickel says hydrometallurgical testwork at its Baptiste project produced battery-grade nickel sulphate and improved the refinery flowsheet with lower purification stages and reagent use. It also yielded cobalt and copper by-products. Results support inclusion in the feasibility study and potential future nickel supply for EV batteries.

Reuters reports copper fell on deteriorating demand expectations in China, alongside rising supplies and climbing LME stocks (up 80% since mid-April). Mine resumptions in Peru and a DRC royalty agreement supporting copper exports eased supply worries, widening the cash discount. Other metals were mixed; aluminum slid and nickel edged up. | Australian Adavale Resources secured a prospecting licence for its Kabanga Jirani nickel project in Tanzania and plans additional drilling, DHEM, and AMT surveys. The project is within the Luhuma trend with historic nickel sulphide intersections, while recent work also defined copper-bearing soil anomalies near an adjacent Kabanga deposit.

Atlantic Nickel said Santa Rita delivered strong Q1 2023 performance: higher nickel-in-concentrate production, lower C1 cash costs, and shipments with an average realized nickel price around $10.63/lb. It also holds forward coverage at >$13/lb on much of 2023 nickel output, with some copper forward exposure. | Copper touched a near six-month low as speculators boosted bearish positions over recession risks and weaker China demand. The cash–three-month spread widened sharply, signaling ample near-term supply, while a firm dollar and fragile risk sentiment weighed on industrial metals broadly, including aluminium and nickel, which also declined. | Australia’s Widgie Nickel raised about A$12m ($7.9m) via a discounted share placement to fund exploration, evaluation, and ongoing development at the Mt Edwards nickel project. The company cites nickel commercialisation and ongoing work at Mt Edwards, which hosts substantial nickel sulphide resources. | The G7/EU/US announced fresh sanctions aimed at closing Russia-related loopholes, with metals explicitly targeted. Russia’s large roles in global aluminium output and LME aluminium stocks raise warrant and delivery risks, while traders may pull non-Russian inventory. Russia’s copper and nickel supply shares add further near-term tightening expectations for LME base metals. | Goldman Sachs lowered its 2023 price forecasts for aluminium to $2,431/mt (from $2,700) and copper to $8,689/mt (from $9,750). It also cautioned that nickel could fall sharply amid surging supply from Indonesia and China, pointing to softer expected prices and downside risk for the metal complex. | Goldman Sachs cut its 2023 price forecasts, lowering aluminium to $2,431/mt from $2,700/mt and copper to $8,689/mt from $9,750/mt, while still expecting 12-month targets to materialize. It also flagged potential sharp nickel downside as supply from Indonesia and China rises.  | Goldman Sachs lowered its 2023 forecasts for aluminium and copper and reiterated 12-month targets may still materialise later. It highlighted potential sharp declines in nickel due to increased supply from Indonesia and China. Overall, the revised outlook weakens near-term price expectations across aluminium, copper and nickel.

Congo plans to boost its stake and control in the Sicomines infrastructure-for-minerals deal with China, seeking higher equity for Gecamines, a larger infrastructure loan envelope, and lump-sum compensation over alleged undervaluation of copper and cobalt sales. Negotiations with Chinese firms are expected to intensify after Tshisekedi returns from China. | Lifezone’s Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania (with BHP holding a minority stake) is in discussions to sell yet-to-be-produced nickel via offtake/prepayment contracts with EV and battery firms. Talks show strong interest and may support expanding refined output plans, but no agreements are finalized. | SPC Nickel said drilling at its West Graham Project in Ontario found a new high-grade intersection in hole WG-23-042, grading 1.71% nickel and 0.46% copper over 20 metres. Phase 1 drilling has completed over 5,000 metres across 27 holes, with 11 awaiting assay results. | Nickel 28 responds to a takeover-related proxy challenge, arguing Pelham lacks a credible plan. The company emphasizes its nickel exposure via its Ramu nickel-cobalt joint venture and mentions cash-flow leverage to nickel and copper prices. The article is mainly corporate governance, without new operational or supply developments.

NORNICKEL nickel premiums rose as market supply tightened, with SMM expecting tightness to last until next week. Yet trader quotations were lowered amid growing stocks, while stainless steel spot pricing weakened due to poor demand and high inventories. With imported inflows expected, nickel is forecast to move rangebound. | Miners argue nickel pricing mechanisms have not kept up with the shift from traditional stainless-steel buyers to EV and battery cathode demand. As a result, they see a window where nickel assets could be acquired cheaply, reflecting subdued nickel pricing conditions and potential near-term downward pressure on nickel. | EIA reported a much larger-than-expected fall in US crude inventories, while the dollar rose for several days. Metals trading closed mixed: SHFE nickel surged 3.08% and copper dipped 0.77%, with aluminium down 0.4%. LME nickel gained 2.33%, copper rose 0.34%, and aluminium ended flat. | SMM says May 25 spot pure nickel premiums for Jinchuan are 10,500-10,800 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL premiums are 7,500 yuan/mt, flat for Jinchuan vs down but still supported by shrinking supply. Nickel briquette prices rose. Tight supply is expected to persist into next week, supporting nickel sentiment. | DR Congo plans to raise its stake in the Sicomines copper-and-cobalt venture with Chinese partners to 70% and seeks more infrastructure funding and compensation, arguing the 2008 deal was lopsided and priced minerals below market. Renegotiations are expected to start after President Tshisekedi returns from China. | DR Congo plans to raise its stake in the Sicomines copper-and-cobalt JV with Chinese partners from 32% to 70% and seek greater management control, citing limited gains under the 2008 infrastructure-for-minerals deal. Reuters reports groundwork for renegotiations ahead of discussions, potentially affecting copper supply expectations.

SMM reports that Nornickel nickel premiums rose as local supply tightened, with most NPI factories pausing quotations. However, trader stocks increased due to imported spot inflows tied to long-term orders. Spot prices for warrants were below market sources as futures fell and mills reduced shipments, driving down inventory. Overall, imported inflows may pressure nickel lower. | Crude oil prices rose as expectations shifted on OPEC+ production-cut signals, while the U.S. dollar weakened. Reflecting this risk-on macro backdrop, SHFE and LME base metals increased broadly: SHFE aluminium +3.38%, copper +2.17%, nickel +1.27%; LME aluminium +0.85%, copper +1.31%, nickel +1.29%. | SMM reported that spot nickel premiums fell at month-end on May 26. Jinchuan nickel premiums averaged 10,050 yuan/mt, down 600 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL averaged 6,900 yuan/mt, also down 600 yuan/mt. Nickel briquette prices rose slightly, but transactions were slack amid continued premiums versus nickel sulphate. | Lifezone Metals has started offtake marketing for products from its Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, targeting EV companies for nickel, cobalt and copper output. The feasibility work continues and no deal is agreed, but progress toward commercialisation and processing plans supports bullish near-term sentiment for nickel. | China’s refined nickel imports fell to 3,204 tonnes in April, the lowest since 2004, with imports from Indonesia and Russia also dropping sharply. Chinese stainless steel mills are shifting toward nickel pig iron (NPI) rather than Class I refined nickel, and EV battery growth may further reduce demand for high-purity nickel. | G7 expanded sanctions on Russia, reducing Russian nickel exports to markets like the Netherlands and the US while shifting flows to other buyers (notably Canada and China). At the same time, China’s electrowinning nickel capacity is set to rise sharply, increasing competition and oversupply risk, which the article expects to keep nickel prices pressured lower. | NickelSearch Ltd says drilling at Carlingup’s high-priority B1 target in Western Australia has confirmed and extended historical nickel sulphide results, making B1 a second target for immediate follow-up. Additional assays are expected, with diamond drilling planned for early June, but no production timing is provided.

SMM reports mixed LME/SHFE performance after a U.S. debt-ceiling agreement boosted risk appetite and weakened the dollar, but inflation and expected Fed hikes limit upside. Copper rose slightly yet sentiment may fade amid weaker consumption. Aluminium fell with inventories dropping but fundamentals seen unchanged. Nickel weakened due to falling premiums, rising stocks, and softer demand. | Indonesia, the world’s top nickel producer, is putting plans for a nickel export levy on hold while it develops a nickel price index to reduce volatility linked to LME benchmarks. The index will feed into royalties and the levy calculation. Export tax delay likely supports nickel sentiment near term amid LME uncertainty. | Indonesia is considering creating a nickel price index this year and has paused plans to tax nickel product exports, a senior government official said. Because Indonesia is the world’s biggest nickel producer, these policy and pricing benchmark signals could influence near-term nickel market expectations and support prices. | Business groups and officials say northern Minnesota/Wisconsin and Michigan’s Upper Peninsula could boost domestic production of critical minerals for EVs, especially nickel. The article cites the Eagle Mine’s nickel/copper output and Tamarack’s nickel supply agreement with Tesla, alongside USGS mapping funding and supportive federal policies. | Copper fell after the U.S. dollar hit a two-month peak, making dollar-priced metal more expensive for non-USD holders. Risk sentiment improved temporarily, but the stronger dollar kept pressure on the non-ferrous complex. LME nickel and aluminium also declined, suggesting broad near-term bearish momentum for industrial metals. | Indonesia’s government plans to launch a new nickel price index this year, separate from the current HPM benchmark. It also signaled a deferral of proposed export duties on NPI and high-grade nickel matte, rather than immediate implementation, potentially supporting near-term nickel supply/export flows and weighing on prices. | Indonesia said it plans to launch a new nickel price index this year, distinct from the current HPM benchmark for Indonesian nickel ore. The government also indicated it will defer planned export duties on nickel products such as NPI and high-grade nickel matte, delaying cost pressures and potentially supporting nickel prices. | Copper prices stayed near a six-month low as investors prepared for Chinese data expected to show continued factory contraction. Demand concerns were reinforced by weak construction activity, low Yangshan import premiums, and LME stocks rising to about 100,000 tonnes. Aluminium and nickel also fell as broader metals sentiment deteriorated. | Indonesia’s export-ban policy on nickel ores, smelter buildout, and potential further restrictions (bauxite ban; possible heavy tax if WTO appeal fails) are positioned to increase processed nickel output while potentially tightening effective supply. Rising EV battery demand is cited, supporting a bullish near-term nickel outlook. | NewRange Copper Nickel began an ~$18M salvage and recycle program at the former LTV Steel site to restore electrical systems and refurbish infrastructure for the NorthMet clean-energy critical minerals project. The work will salvage steel and concrete to support safer construction readiness. It reinforces ongoing development of copper and nickel resources. | Outokumpu said it will invest EUR 11 million for a 9.9% stake in Canada’s FPX Nickel to strengthen its sustainable nickel supply chain. The Baptiste project is planned to start producing near decade-end, targeting low-carbon nickel units suited for stainless steel feedstock. | The Market Herald released interviews and highlights corporate news, including FPX Nickel closing a private placement with Outokumpu as a strategic cornerstone investor. The financing is a positive signal for FPX’s nickel-related activities and can modestly improve short-term sentiment toward nickel prices, though it lacks operational detail.

Indonesian copper miner Amman Mineral’s $880m IPO will finance debt repayment and projects, including completing a copper smelter in Sumbawa with planned cathode output. The Indonesian government’s temporary raw export exemption supports revenue needed to finish the project, reinforcing near-term copper sentiment. | White & Case highlights Indonesia’s strong Q1 deal activity, including Nickel Industries’ March equity placement to fund purchases and raise its stakes in Indonesian nickel projects (including Huayue Nickel Cobalt). It frames the strategy as shifting toward battery-grade/Class 1 nickel for EV demand, which is supportive for nickel sentiment. | Copper prices slid after China’s manufacturing PMI came in weaker than expected, below 50 indicating contraction, reinforcing concerns about demand in the world’s top consumer. Copper was set for a second monthly loss and fell 5.8% so far this month. LME aluminium and nickel also declined alongside risk sentiment. | Macquarie’s Jim Lennon argues Indonesia will supply most of global nickel growth through 2029, and that strong supply versus slowing demand created a nickel surplus in 2022. With underutilized capacity, the market could stay surplus, weighing on LME nickel. Battery demand is growing but supply effects dominate near-term. | Fathom Nickel closed the first tranche of an upsized private placement to fund exploration at the Albert Lake and Gochager Lake projects in Saskatchewan. Proceeds support Canadian flow-through mining expenditures and general corporate uses. The company targets magmatic nickel sulphides and its resource includes copper by-grade. | Copper prices edged toward six-month lows after May China factory activity contracted faster than expected, reinforcing concerns that the rebound is losing steam. Japan and South Korea output also fell. A weaker yuan pressured dollar-priced metals, while physical premiums rose and LME inventories increased. | Nornickel says the palladium market may swing to a surplus in 2024 as recycling outpaces recovering demand. While palladium is not tracked here, the company also expects the global nickel market surplus to exceed 180,000 tonnes in 2024 versus 200,000 in 2023, implying weaker nickel pricing pressure. | INSG commentary at SMM’s Indonesia nickel and cobalt conference projects the global primary nickel market to return to surplus in 2023, citing ongoing supply growth (including rising Indonesian output) and a reported 51,000 mt surplus in Q1 2023. Demand growth is viewed as slower amid macro uncertainty. | SMM’s May 31 market wrap says crude oil slipped and the U.S. dollar rose, with supply-side headlines around Iran/OPEC. Base metals mostly fell: SHFE nickel plunged 4.81%, SHFE copper was slightly down, and SHFE aluminium rose modestly. LME metals were largely lower, with aluminium nearly flat. | BHP’s Escondida in Chile will use AI and Azure-based analytics on real-time concentrator data to improve ore processing and grade recovery. The initiative aims to extract more value from existing resources as copper is expected to face future supply shortfalls from green energy and battery demand. | LME launched two consultations to reform market rules after last year’s nickel crisis, including expanded warehouse reporting on off-warrant inventories and changes to how closing prices are set. The VWAP-based closing method would include more aluminium, copper and nickel contracts. Timing is June–July, not immediate rule changes. | The article recounts major developments in the LME nickel trading crisis, including a halt and trade cancellations after prices spiked above $100,000/ton, regulatory probes and independent reviews, disputes over delivered “bagged” nickel, and later steps to resume trading and revive the nickel contract via reduced waiting times and fees.

Spot premiums of Norilsk nickel fell sharply as large arrivals and a sell-off pressured the market. Indonesia’s plan to defer export tariffs on nickel products may erode the market share of domestic NPI. With nickel supply described as ample, the article highlights downside risks for nickel pricing. | Commerzbank lowered its 2023-2024 price forecasts, reducing expected end-2023/end-2024 levels for copper, aluminium, and nickel versus prior estimates. Copper and nickel forecasts fell materially, while aluminium was also marked down. The change reflects revised expectations, not an immediate physical market disruption. | Commerzbank cut its end-2023 and end-2024 forecasts for copper (to $9,000/$9,400), aluminium (to $2,600/$2,800), and nickel (to $23,000/$20,000) versus prior estimates. The revisions imply weaker expected market conditions, typically bearish for metal sentiment and pricing. | Commerzbank cut its forecasts for industrial metal prices, including copper, aluminium, and nickel, for end-2023 and end-2024. Copper and nickel targets were reduced materially, while aluminium was lowered versus prior estimates. The revision may affect market sentiment, but it is largely a medium-term view rather than an immediate shock. | SMM’s conference outlook links NEV growth and high-nickel ternary batteries to rising nickel demand (especially via battery use). It also describes nickel sulfate supply expansion led largely by Indonesia, while noting China faces raw-material shortages and production cost pressure. Net effect favors tighter nickel fundamentals and support for prices. | Avante Mining announced it is commencing preparatory work on the TL Nickel Project, including re-logging and targeted sampling of historic core ahead of planned 2023 drilling. The release cites historic grades for nickel (and associated copper) and expects resampling results soon, but provides no production or immediate supply impact. | SMM’s daily comments show broad base-metal strength alongside firm energy and mixed FX. SHFE aluminium jumped 2.04% and SHFE copper gained 1.4%, while SHFE nickel slipped 0.17% by end of intraday. The piece suggests near-term bullish pressure for aluminium and copper. | Nickel Creek Platinum announced an updated mineral resource estimate for its 100%-owned Nickel Shäw Project in Yukon. Measured and indicated tonnage increased, with contained nickel rising to 2.47 billion lbs; copper is present at 0.13% grade. The update supports progress toward a pre-feasibility study. | Canada Nickel reported final assay results from the Texmont Project, confirming continued high-grade, near-surface nickel mineralization. Most drillholes intersected >1% Ni and several intersected higher-grade intervals close to surface. Management highlighted potential for near-term smaller-scale open-pit production and expects a resource and PEA later in 2023. | Eramet’s CEO said the LME has lost benchmark status for part of the nickel market, with Shanghai Metals Market becoming the reference for pricing ferronickel. She cited a growing mismatch between nickel stored in LME warehouses and the underlying fundamentals. LME also announced consultations after last year’s nickel trading crisis. | Eramet said LME is losing benchmark status for ferronickel pricing as Shanghai Metals Market indices increasingly set prices for nickel ore/ferronickel. It cited a disconnect between LME pricing of pure nickel and physical inventory fundamentals. The company noted limited LME indexation for its products. | The article argues copper is in a “buy zone,” citing declining LME warehouse stocks, tight supply, and rising demand tied to decarbonization (EVs, wind). It expects copper to avoid breaking key support around $3.15 and sees bullish upside despite bearish technicals. Spillover to other base metals is suggested. | Leading Edge Materials reports assay results from Bihor Sud showing high-grade Co-Ni mineralization, including ~30% nickel and supporting ~1 km Co-Ni potential, with access delays due to high radon now resolved. The company plans further underground channel sampling and drilling, reinforcing nickel supply upside expectations. | Leading Edge Materials reports positive trench and underground gallery wall assays at Bihor Sud, highlighting high-grade Co-Ni mineralization with nickel assays exceeding 30% in samples from gallery G7, alongside Zn-Pb-Ag results. Improved access after radon-related ventilation delays enables further underground sampling and drilling plans. | Minnesota regulators decided existing nonferrous mine siting rules are insufficient to prevent noise and light impacts on the Boundary Waters and will reopen rules, potentially expanding protective buffer zones. The change is another obstacle for Twin Metals’ proposed copper-nickel mine, already affected by prior federal actions. | Minnesota regulators decided existing nonferrous mine siting rules are insufficient to protect the Boundary Waters from noise and light impacts. The DNR will expand the buffer zone, potentially affecting the Twin Metals copper-nickel project. The change could delay permitting, adding supply risk for copper and nickel in the coming weeks.

SMM says Indonesia has the world’s richest nickel ore reserves (25.4 million mt nickel content in 2021) and higher-grade lateritic ore than the Philippines. It attributes Chinese mining and stainless steel investment demand to Indonesia’s ample resources and comparatively less stringent environmental rules for local production. | Eramet said LME lost benchmark status in a key nickel market segment after a major short sale, with SMM becoming the benchmark for NPI pricing. The piece also notes Commerzbank cutting price forecasts for copper, aluminium and nickel for 2023-2024, signaling a bearish outlook for these metals. | Eramet says ferronickel pricing is increasingly benchmarked to a Shanghai Metals Market index rather than the LME since last year’s LME short squeeze and trading disruption. The LME nickel contract remains challenged, with falling volumes and diverging prices across nickel intermediates, some also pressured by Indonesian supply. | SMM discusses trends in nickel structural supply tied to high-nickel ternary cathode pathways, with weighted nickel consumption from Chinese precursors rising toward 2025. It also notes Commerzbank has lowered price forecasts for copper, aluminium, zinc and nickel for 2023-2024, suggesting weaker price expectations. | SMM says China’s bonded-zone nickel inventories increased to 4,200 mt as nickel briquettes and nickel plates rose to 1,670 mt and 2,530 mt. The gain was driven by deliveries for long-term orders and intensified arrival of imported pure nickel at ports from late May through early June, adding supply and pressuring prices. | Markets ended higher: the article cites a two-day fall in the US dollar and two-day rise in crude oil, with SHFE nickel up 4.04%, SHFE aluminium up 1.71%, and SHFE copper up 1.5%. LME copper, aluminium and nickel also rose. Commerzbank lowered longer-term forecasts for copper and aluminium. | Copper prices were set for their first weekly gain in seven weeks after a surprise rise in China’s Caixin manufacturing activity boosted demand hopes. A U.S. debt-default deal, Senate debt-ceiling legislation, and expectations of no further Fed hikes weakened the dollar and lifted risk assets. Aluminium and nickel also rose.

Reuters reports copper fell on Monday due to demand worries, notably in top consumer China, and a stronger dollar. Copper later partially recovered as the dollar retreated. The piece cites rising LME warehouse stocks and supply concerns from cancellations, while aluminium and nickel also declined. | The article says Nickel in Quebec (Ducros) is suspending field work because of forest fire risk. The move points to short-term operational disruption at the project site, potentially reducing near-term nickel supply continuity and increasing availability concerns, which can pressure nickel prices downward in the coming days.

Firefighters responded to a smouldering fire at Aurizon Port Services at Carrington, where 10m-high piles of copper and lead concentrate began to self-combust. Crews cooled the material for about seven hours while staff removed the top layers. The fire did not damage buildings and was extinguished the same day. | Avebury Nickel Mine is trucking 4,500 tonnes of nickel concentrate to Burnie Port for loading on a vessel to China over Tuesday to Thursday. It discusses ramping up production while noting nickel prices are below desired levels and the mine is near break-even. | Copper prices in Shanghai and London rose toward a four-week high as investors priced in potential Chinese stimulus and supportive policy for property, while LME on-warrant copper inventories fell sharply. Nickel also increased, reflecting broader metal demand expectations, whereas aluminium was slightly lower in the session. | Japanese miners warn Chile’s newly approved mining tax overhaul will reduce profitability and deter new copper and lithium mine investment, though it won’t force exits of existing projects. Higher taxes and royalties raise development risk, potentially weighing on expectations for future copper supply and pressuring prices in the near term. | Indonesia said it will launch a domestic nickel price index for use in export-tax and domestic ore pricing formulas, aiming to reduce reliance on LME nickel. The government also proposed postponing export duties on nickel products such as NPI and high matte, which could support nickel market sentiment short term. | Indonesia’s government will ban the construction of smelters processing low-grade nickel products, citing an excess of existing RKEF projects and limited high-grade resources lasting only about 9–10 years. Officials are discussing pausing new low-grade refining capacity, which may tighten future nickel supply expectations. | The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers revoked the Section 404 Clean Water Act permit for NewRange’s (NorthMet/PolyMet) proposed copper-nickel project after concerns that it would violate downstream water quality standards tied to the Fond du Lac Band of Lake Superior Chippewa. The operator may reapply, but near-term supply outlook worsens. | The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers revoked the Clean Water Act Section 404 permit for the NewRange Copper Nickel (NorthMet) project, citing insufficient assurance of compliance with downstream water quality standards. NewRange may reapply, but the decision materially raises project delay risk and supply uncertainty for copper and nickel. | NewRange Copper Nickel said the US Army Corps of Engineers revoked the NorthMet Project’s Clean Water Act section 404 permit. The company is reviewing options to proceed, arguing the project would be environmentally protective. The permit setback increases uncertainty over future copper and nickel supply from NorthMet. | NewRange said the US Army Corps of Engineers revoked the Section 404 Clean Water Act permit for the NorthMet Project, prompting it to review options and re-engage regulators. The company argues the project is protective, but the permit reversal raises near-term development risk for its copper-nickel supply. | The U.S. Army Corps revoked a key permit needed for the NorthMet copper-nickel mining project. The decision heightens the risk of project delays, reduced output, and slower near-term supply growth, typically weighing on copper and nickel sentiment within the coming weeks. | The U.S. Army Corps revoked a key permit for the NorthMet copper-nickel mining project. The decision increases uncertainty over project progress and potential near-term supply availability, which can weigh on copper and nickel market expectations and prices over the coming days to weeks.

The U.S. Army Corps revoked a crucial Clean Water Act permit for the proposed NewRange Copper Nickel (PolyMet) mine in northeastern Minnesota after finding it would not ensure compliance with downstream water-quality requirements of the Fond du Lac Band. The permit had been suspended previously. | The article headline says the Army Corps has pulled a key permit for a copper-nickel mine near Hoyt Lakes. Losing or delaying permitting can hinder operations, reduce near-term output visibility, and raise supply risk. That tends to pressure copper and nickel prices, while aluminium is not directly implicated. | The US Army Corps revoked a permit for the NewRange copper-nickel mine. The decision raises the risk that the project will be delayed or curtailed, tightening near-term supply expectations. That dynamic can weigh on prices for both copper and nickel within coming weeks. | The US Army Corps revokes a permit for the NewRange copper-nickel mine. The decision could delay or impede development and production, reducing near-term supply expectations. That typically supports lower pricing risk for copper and nickel as markets anticipate potential output shortfalls. | DMCI Mining’s unit Zambales Diversified Metals secured an Environmental Compliance Certificate to expand nickel-ore production capacity to 2 million WMT from 1 million WMT. ZDMC produced 599,000 WMT in Q1 (up 88%) and raised inventory 16%. DMCI targets 1.5M WMT shipments in 2023, supporting nickel supply expectations. | Reuters reports nickel has underperformed, with LME three-month nickel hitting a nine-month low as markets price an impending supply glut from Indonesia’s rapidly expanding output. Despite LME and SHFE stockpiles at multi-year lows, inventories are still falling, cushioning but not reversing bearish sentiment. | The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers revoked a federal permit for PolyMet’s copper-nickel mine. The decision raises the risk of delays or cancellation of mine development, tightening near-term expectations for copper and nickel supply. That supply uncertainty is likely to weigh on prices in the coming days to weeks. | The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers revoked a federal permit for PolyMet’s copper-nickel mine. The decision raises the likelihood of delays or suspension of mining activities, worsening near-term supply expectations and increasing downside pressure for copper and nickel markets over coming weeks.

Ero Copper updated its Curaçá Valley exploration for nickel sulphide at the Umburana System, reporting new drill intercepts with up to 7.09% Ni and initial rougher metallurgical recoveries of 77–91%. While copper grades are included in NiEq, the announcement is mainly exploration progress with no production change. | Ero Copper announced updated nickel sulphide exploration results from the Curaçá Valley, including drill intercepts such as 11.2 meters at 1.86% Ni and a higher-grade interval with 3.71% Ni, plus minor copper. It also reports initial metallurgy work. The news is sentiment-supportive for nickel, with limited near-term impact. | Ero Copper updated investors on ongoing drilling of its Umburana nickel sulphide system in the Curaçá Valley, reporting multiple nickel-rich intercepts and initial metallurgical rougher recoveries (77%–91%). The company says mineralization remains open and targets are being drilled, supporting positive sentiment toward future nickel supply. | London copper fell 0.4% as expectations of higher-for-longer interest rates and slowing global growth outweighed hopes for Chinese stimulus. Analysts noted weak demand fundamentals and warned a delayed package could restart the downtrend. Nickel also slipped 0.8% while aluminium edged up 0.3% on inventory-driven factors.

Australian shares rose as mining stocks gained and expectations of a pause in U.S. rate hikes improved sentiment. The key nickel link came from Nickel Industries, which announced it would raise $633 million and adjust investment in Indonesia’s Excelsior Nickel Cobalt project, a development that could support nickel supply expectations. | Nickel Industries secured a $943m placement after United Tractors agreed to invest in its Indonesia-based nickel production base, with plans for future mining and processing initiatives. Additional funding is aimed at boosting downstream capacity, including higher output at the Excelsior project, supporting nickel supply expectations. | PT United Tractors’ subsidiary Danusa Tambang Nusantara agreed to subscribe for a 19.99% stake in Nickel Industries Limited for A$943 million. NIC operates Indonesian nickel mining and RKEF processing lines and is expanding into HPAL and battery nickel matte production, which is supportive for nickel market sentiment. | Australian markets ended mixed as weaker US jobs data boosted hopes for a Fed pause, but the index still fell for the week. Nickel Industries surged 13.3% after Indonesian partner PT United Tractors agreed to invest $943 million for a nearly 20% stake, strengthening sentiment toward nickel production. | Copper prices rose on Friday toward a second weekly gain as hopes for Chinese demand stimulus increased and LME/Shanghai inventories fell. Three-month copper gained 0.4%. Aluminium edged higher on improved Yunnan hydropower conditions. Nickel also advanced, reflecting overall supportive sentiment across base metals. | The LME received its first fast-track application to list a new nickel brand from Quzhou Huayou Cobalt. The exchange aims to boost liquidity and tackle low inventories, which are at the lowest since 2007. Nickel supply is dominated by nickel pig iron (NPI), largely undeliverable under LME terms. | LME has received its first request to list a new nickel brand from Quzhou Huayou Cobalt New Material. The move follows earlier LME actions to improve nickel liquidity after a prior contract suspension and persistently low inventories (about 37,230 tonnes). Analysts note most supply is nickel pig iron not deliverable to LME.

Appian agreed to sell its Brazilian battery-metals assets—Atlantic Nickel (Santa Rita nickel sulphide) and Mineração Vale Verde (Serrote copper-gold)—to ACG for US$1.065 billion cash. The announcement highlights long-term offtake and funding partners but provides no immediate supply disruption. | Glencore and automakers are expected to back a $1 billion nickel and copper SPAC deal in Brazil. ACG will buy the Santa Rita nickel sulphide and Serrote copper mines from funds advised by Appian Capital. The announcement follows a mining M&A spree tied to green-transition demand expectations. | Glencore and automakers are reported to back a $1 billion nickel and copper SPAC deal in Brazil. Such funding commitments can improve market expectations for future nickel and copper supply development, offering modest near-term support. The immediate impact on spot prices is likely limited and timing remains unclear. | Sources say Glencore-linked automakers and Glencore-backed SPAC structures will support a $1 billion Brazil SPAC deal to buy the Santa Rita nickel sulphide and Serrote copper mines. The mining M&A push is tied to expectations for green-transition metals demand, but the article lacks production disruption details. | Royal Gold announced a $250m commitment to buy royalty interests on Brazil’s Serrote and Santa Rita mines. The deal includes copper and nickel royalties on both mines and is effective from May 1, 2023, with royalty terms based on payable production. It is primarily a financing/royalty transaction, not a production change. | Glencore, Stellantis and PowerCo will back a $1 billion SPAC deal in Brazil to buy the Santa Rita nickel sulphide mine and the Serrote copper mine. Nickel concentrate will be refined in Glencore facilities and supplied into EV battery supply chains. The mines use hydropower and may expand. | Royal Gold said it will pay $250 million to acquire royalty interests on Brazil’s Serrote and Santa Rita mines. The royalties include copper and nickel components on both producing mines. The announcement is primarily a financing/royalty acquisition with no immediate operational change described. | Glencore, Stellantis and PowerCo back a $1 billion SPAC deal to acquire two Brazilian mines: Santa Rita nickel sulphide and Serrote copper. Nickel concentrate will be refined by Glencore and used in EV batteries by automakers. Both mines use hydropower and are considering expansions. | SMM’s daily comments note three straight days of crude oil declines and a largely flat U.S. dollar. At intraday close, SHFE copper was down 0.07% and aluminium down 0.87%, while SHFE nickel rose 2.22%. Other metals also moved, but the key tracked effects are mixed. | EV Nickel announced an updated NI 43-101 resource estimate for its W4 high-grade nickel deposit, more than doubling historical contained nickel and increasing measured/indicated resources to 31.3M pounds Ni at 0.98%. Management says the deposit remains open for expansion and will support a preliminary economic assessment. | Glencore, Stellantis and PowerCo back a $1-billion SPAC deal in Brazil to buy the Santa Rita nickel sulphide and Serrote copper mines. Nickel concentrate will be refined in Glencore facilities and supplied into EV battery production. The expanding hydropower-based mines do not signal an immediate supply shock, limiting price impact. | Glencore, Stellantis, and PowerCo back a $1-billion SPAC deal in Brazil to buy the Santa Rita nickel and Serrote copper mines. Nickel concentrate will be refined at Glencore facilities and feed EV battery production. Hydropower use and planned expansions further support investment sentiment. | Copper fell 0.7% as traders worried about China’s demand outlook and awaited U.S. Fed and inflation data. Earlier gains were linked to covering shorts and Chinese stimulus optimism. Falling LME stocks offer some support, but expectations of elevated rates and China uncertainty weigh on copper and aluminium. | Canada Nickel said it has improved in-process tailings technology to capture carbon during ore processing at its Crawford nickel project, potentially lowering capital and operating costs and qualifying for refundable carbon-capture tax credits. The integrated feasibility study is now expected in September 2023, with no change to the overall schedule. | CanAlaska Uranium proposes to spin out five nickel properties (including the Mel nickel deposit) into a new vehicle, Core Nickel, via a statutory plan of arrangement. The company says completion is conditional on approvals, financing, listings and market conditions, with no operational or supply change detailed. | Glencore and automakers back a $1bn SPAC deal to buy two Brazilian mines: a nickel sulphide site in Santa Rita and a copper mine in Serrote. The output will be refined at Glencore refineries and used for EV batteries, with $100m equity from Glencore and $100m nickel prepayment from PowerGo. | Glencore and automakers backed a $1bn SPAC deal to buy Brazilian nickel sulphide and copper mines, with Glencore handling nickel refinement at its Western and North American refineries. The metals are intended for EV battery use by Stellantis and PowerGo, reinforcing near-term supply-demand expectations. | Appian agreed to sell its Brazilian battery-metals portfolio—Atlantic Nickel (Santa Rita) and Mineração Vale Verde (Serrote copper-gold asset)—to ACG for $1.065 billion cash. The deal positions a London-listed nickel sulphide producer and targets expanded supply to Western EV partners, but it is mainly ownership transfer. | Market wrap notes metals were mixed: nickel shares surged after Nickel Industries announced a conditional capital placement with an Indonesian mining conglomerate at a premium to fund its nickel/cobalt project in Indonesia. The report also discusses macro CPI/Fed expectations, but the only metal-specific company news is nickel.

Copper and other base metals in London climbed after China cut a key borrowing rate for the first time in 10 months to support growth. Traders expected improved industrial demand, while a weaker dollar also made greenback-priced metals cheaper for non-dollar buyers. LME aluminium, nickel, and copper rose modestly. | Copper prices rose after China cut borrowing costs for the first time in 10 months, supporting growth expectations and risk sentiment. A softer dollar also helped. The Reuters snapshot showed LME aluminium and nickel moving higher alongside copper, though China’s total copper demand remained weaker month-on-month. | Appian agreed to sell Atlantic Nickel and Mineração Vale Verde to ACG Electric Metals for $1.065 billion, supported by EV and trader financing including Glencore and Volkswagen’s PowerCo. Atlantic Nickel is a nickel sulphide producer, and MVV includes the Serrote copper-gold project, but no immediate production impact is stated. | Aston Bay and American West report new high-resolution gravity anomalies at the Storm Copper Project in Nunavut, identifying large, shallow copper sulfide targets coincident with historical EM/IP signals and previous drill intercepts. The news is exploration-oriented and does not indicate near-term supply changes, so market price impact is likely minimal. | Golden Arrow reported Phase 1 drill results from its San Pietro IOCG project in Chile, including 34m at 0.41% Cu and high cobalt intercepts, plus additional shallow cobalt targets. It is exploration-focused and does not indicate near-term production changes, so near-term effects on copper prices are likely limited. | MacDonald Mines began a 1,200m drilling program at its Alwyn Cu-Au trend near Sudbury, reporting iron-silicate alteration with chalcopyrite that may indicate iron-rich IOCG-style mineralization. The release is exploratory rather than supply-related, with only limited, indirect relevance to copper and potential nickel. | Mink Ventures announced an option to acquire a 100% interest (subject to a 1.5% NSR) in the Warren copper-nickel project near Timmins, Ontario. The drill-ready property has multiple Cu-Ni zones with historical grades and reported potential targets from geophysics. No production or supply changes were stated. | Premium Nickel Resources announced CAD$33.99m of financing (equity and a CAD$15m term loan) to fund prepayments and exploration/resource development at the Selebi and Selkirk Ni-Cu-Co sulphide mines in Botswana. The company plans continued drilling and modernization to redevelop production, supporting nickel and copper sentiment.

Shanghai copper rallied to a seven-week high as China’s demand outlook improved following a central bank rate cut and as tight supply conditions emerged. Refined output fell on maintenance and import activity weakened. A softer U.S. dollar provided additional support. LME aluminium and nickel also rose. | SMM’s daily comments say base metals rose across SHFE and mostly higher on LME. The LME faces major legal claims over cancelling nickel trades after prices surged about 250% in two days in March 2022. Ongoing legal and regulatory scrutiny may weigh on nickel sentiment. | Mallee Resources reports that clay-like minerals disrupted ore processing at its Avebury nickel mine, reducing concentrate production rates in April and May and causing downtime in the tertiary crusher. Copper prices rose as low supplies outweighed tepid demand concerns. A fire at Indonesia’s Gunbuster Nickel Industry (GNI) smelter in Morowali killed one worker and injured six others. The statement says an investigation is underway. Tightening bauxite-to-alumina logistics can support aluminium input costs. No production change is announced. | Erdene reports expanded mineralization from a 12-hole program at its Zuun Mod molybdenum-copper porphyry project in Mongolia, including long Mo and Cu intervals and copper anomalism north of the deposit. The permit enables ore stockpiling and commissioning preparation before plant start-up next year. The project’s planned output of ferronickel and nickel adds to near-term supply expectations for nickel. | Norilsk Nickel said it has made “certain progress” selling accumulated metal reserves built up in 2022, according to Interfax (via Reuters). A report says China’s rapid expansion into Indonesia’s nickel operations has given it dominant control of nickel needed for EV batteries in the U.S. The Inflation Reduction Act’s mineral-sourcing requirements may exclude Indonesia and invite scrutiny of Chinese firms’ involvement, weakening U.S. supply prospects and near-term nickel demand outlook. | London copper rebounded 0.3% as the dollar eased, but gains were capped by uncertainty over Chinese demand and expectations for further U.S. Fed rate hikes. LME aluminium rose 0.6% and nickel edged up slightly, reflecting mixed sentiment across metals tied to broader risk assets. | BofA reduced its nickel price forecast for 2023 to $22,063/t from $23,222/t previously, while keeping the 2024 forecast unchanged at $21,250/t. The downgrade signals softer expected nickel market conditions, which can weigh on near-term nickel price expectations. | Copper edged up but remained in a tight range as investors weighed U.S. Fed rate expectations and a pending jobs report against China demand outlook. China’s property and new-energy activity supported demand, while expanding domestic supply from smelters and rising aluminium output capped gains. Aluminium and nickel also saw modest moves. | Indonesia’s ministry is probing allegations that nickel ore continued to be exported to China despite a ban since 2020. China imported 5.56 million tons between 2020 and May 2023, prompting investigations into misclassification and possible illegal shipments. Any crackdown could tighten nickel ore supply and support nickel prices. | Indonesia is investigating alleged illegal exports of nickel ore worth 14.5 trillion rupiah to China dating back to early 2020. Authorities say 5.3 million mt of ore were illegally shipped, causing estimated losses of royalties and export taxes. Possible enforcement tightening could reduce near-term nickel ore flows to China. | BofA Global Research lowered its commodity price forecasts, including copper and nickel. The revisions point to weaker expected market conditions for these metals. | BofA Global Research lowered its price forecasts for several commodities. Copper forecasts were reduced for 2023 and 2024, and nickel’s 2023 forecast was also cut (2024 unchanged).
