Aluminium and nickel also declined modestly, reinforcing a cautious near-term tone despite earlier intraday gains. | BHP says EV makers are not paying a “green premium” for sustainably mined Australian nickel while Indonesian laterite nickel volumes are unexpectedly large. Aluminium also fell. Nickel slipped and the piece cites a deeper supply surplus from production ramp-ups in Indonesia and China, reinforcing bearish sentiment across base metals. | Sumitomo Metal Mining said the global nickel market should remain in surplus in 2024, with the surplus widening to 36,000 metric tons from 24,000 this year. The view is based on Indonesia’s growing NPI output and rising supply outpacing demand, despite expected increases for stainless steel and batteries. | Australia’s Nickel Industries plans to enter final investment decision within weeks on an Indonesia Morowali HPAL smelter producing MHP, nickel sulfate and nickel cathode. With ~72,000 tonnes annual MHP capacity and large investment, the project strengthens expectations for future nickel output, supporting nickel market sentiment. | Québec Nickel Corp. reports AI-driven exploration results at its Ducros property, generating multiple new nickel-sulphide (and Ni-Cu-PGE) targets and updating geological interpretations to guide further work. The company also continues deep drilling at the Fortin Sill target, aiming to extend mineralization at depth. | Copper fell for a fourth straight session as investors sold on worries over China’s demand momentum, uncertainty about interest rates and growth, and rising LME stocks. The piece also cites CTA-driven selling that pressured aluminium and nickel, while nickel/lead saw losses and nickel’s surplus outlook worsened sentiment.

INSG forecasts nickel production exceeding demand for the third straight year, with the surplus rising to 239,000 tonnes next year and growing larger each year. Despite rising EV-related demand, output growth is faster, especially from Indonesia and China. Bears are heavily short, and surplus may increasingly reach LME/ShFE deliverable nickel. | Copper prices eased and were set for their steepest weekly decline in nearly 11 months as a firm dollar and high LME inventories weighed on sentiment. Aluminium rebounded slightly but remained on track for its biggest weekly drop since February. Copper opened higher after a weekly decline as China’s demand remained resilient, supporting both copper and aluminium despite property weakness. However, expectations of further U.S. Fed rate hikes weighed on overall sentiment. The piece reports nickel falling while other base metals rose slightly. | Abaxx Commodities Exchange in Singapore plans to launch a physically settled futures contract for nickel sulphate used in EV batteries and aims to add additional battery-metal contracts after nickel. London copper is set for a third straight daily gain as a weaker dollar and bullish sentiment lift LME prices, with LME Seminar showing copper as the top upside bet for 2024. Aluminium edges up; nickel is also higher in SHFE trading. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate trading around 32,100–32,800 yuan/ton, with slight week-on-week increases but weaker demand transmission from high prices. Spot activity remains weak after holidays, but inquiries persist. Tight raw-material supply and low shipping willingness support costs, while downstream demand continues, leaving potential upside for nickel sulfate prices. | Indonesia’s government says a European company is negotiating a large nickel smelter investment to supply batteries and electric vehicles. Since Indonesia’s 2020 ban on raw nickel ore exports, the policy has driven downstream investments despite an EU WTO dispute. New smelter plans are likely to support nickel supply expectations. | Copper prices fell as renewed jitters over China’s property demand intensified after Country Garden warned of significant challenges meeting offshore debt. The move was reinforced by sharply higher LME warehouse inventories, widening a surplus outlook and weakening cash premiums. Aluminium and nickel also declined amid broader industrial-metals risk sentiment. | Russian-origin aluminium on LME warrants fell to 76% in September from 81% as  cancellations occurred in Gwangyang, with Russian aluminium tonnage dropping to 132,475 from 183,650. Aluminium was up on the day, Reuters said. | Pacific Ridge reported additional drilling at its Kliyul copper-gold project, including 103.5 m of 0.63% CuEq (0.18% copper) in the Kliyul North zone and expanded known extents of the porphyry system. Copper prices rebounded as the U.S. dollar weakened and investors hoped China could restart demand via potential infrastructure-focused stimulus, including possible sovereign debt issuance. The strategy targets EV battery supply chains, potentially boosting expectations for nickel demand and downstream utilization. | Copper prices edged higher as U.S. bond yields fell from recent peaks and markets looked for China to announce more stimulus. The housing-demand outlook remains a concern and LME inventories rose toward a 17-month high, reflecting a likely surplus. Aluminium rose slightly, while nickel eased. | Canada Nickel Company will host a webcast and conference call to discuss its Definitive Feasibility Study for the Crawford nickel sulphide project. The update could affect market expectations for future nickel supply from the Timmins-Cochrane camp, providing modest near-term support to nickel pricing sentiment. | Copper edged higher on the London Metal Exchange as U.S. bond yields fell from recent highs and investors looked for more China stimulus. However, concerns persist about China’s housing slowdown, while evidence of rising copper inventories and mine ramp-ups points to a potential surplus.

Copper prices edged lower as London inventories rose to the highest since May 2022, with LME copper down and the copper premium signaling resilient Chinese imports. Nickel and aluminium moved modestly. | Copper rose on Thursday as a weaker U.S. dollar made dollar-priced commodities cheaper and as investors looked for further Chinese stimulus. However, gains were capped by sharply higher LME copper inventories, while aluminium stayed flat and nickel rose. | Canada Nickel reported a positive bankable feasibility study for its Crawford nickel sulphide project, highlighting higher after-tax NPV/IRR and production of about 48 ktpa nickel. The update strengthens expectations for future long-life development (permits/financing) and improves sentiment for nickel demand-supply balance over coming months. | Midland Exploration released new assay results from drilling at its Santos nickel-copper discovery on the Tete Nord property. The latest hole intersected mineralized zones with grades including 0.33% Ni and 0.12% Cu over 39.73 metres. Better project economics can support expectations for future nickel supply and sentiment.

SMM reports that on Oct 12 the Shanghai battery-grade nickel sulfate index was unchanged day-on-day at 32,202 yuan/ton, with the average also stable. Supply remains tight due to unresolved raw material issues, but demand is soft with weak inquiries and transactions, leaving overall nickel sulfate market conditions weak. | London copper edged up as a softer dollar supported USD-priced metals, but it was still set for a weekly decline amid weaker industrial demand sentiment tied to “higher-for-longer” rates. Reuters reports LME Week with lingering fallout from last year’s nickel crisis, including pending lawsuits over cancelled nickel trades and continued efforts to strengthen trading/clearing systems. It says nickel trading volumes and participation remain weaker than other metals and nickel lags the broader LME recovery, implying cautious near-term market sentiment. | Copper prices opened higher on resilient Chinese demand and signs of stabilizing growth, with imports at an intra-year high. However, gains were capped by rising LME inventories and a firmer dollar ahead of a Powell speech, limiting the net near-term upside. Aluminium was steady; nickel rose modestly. | SMM said China’s battery-grade nickel sulfate index rose to 32,202 yuan/ton and has stabilized under upward pressure after the holiday. Tight raw-material supply persists with low spot circulation and reluctant selling. Demand weakened on slower electro-nickel and precursor purchasing, but cost-side support remains strong, supporting nickel. | Papua New Guinea’s PM Marape urged MCC, developer of the Ramu nickel/cobalt project, to expand into downstream processing, and MCC said it would seriously consider such expansion in its PNG investment plans. The project is MCC’s biggest investment outside China, supporting nickel-focused sentiment. | Copper edged higher on Monday as the dollar weakened, reversing two sessions of losses, supported by stronger-than-expected Chinese physical demand and infrastructure spending. However, nerves over the Middle East and weaker global growth sentiment weighed on metals, with traders still net short. Aluminium dipped, while nickel rose. | Flying Nickel Mining closed a non-brokered private placement raising about $600,705 at $0.079/share, with Norway House Cree Nation as sole investor and strategic partner. Proceeds fund exploration and working capital for the Minago nickel project in Manitoba. Copper prices eased from near six-week highs as a stronger U.S. dollar—supported by retail sales and cooling inflation data—reduced demand for dollar-priced commodities and reinforced expectations that the Fed will cut rates later. In the same report, LME aluminium and nickel also fell modestly. | Talon Metals reported assay results from 116 new drill holes at the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project, including 14 exploration holes with high-grade nickel-copper massive sulphide intercepts outside the current resource. The company highlighted continued resource growth potential, particularly in CGO East and CGO West areas. | Talon Metals reported results from 116 new drill holes at the Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project, including 14 exploration holes intersecting high-grade nickel-copper mineralization outside the current resource. The company cites significant potential for resource expansion, with assays showing strong Ni and associated Cu grades in multiple new intervals. | Talon Metals reports assay results from 116 drill holes at Minnesota’s Tamarack nickel-copper project, with 14 exploration holes intersecting high-grade nickel-copper massive sulphides outside the current resource. The company cites potential for resource growth in the CGO East and CGO West areas, supporting longer-term supply expectations. | Talon Metals reported results from 116 drill holes at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project in Minnesota, including 14 exploration holes intersecting high-grade nickel-copper mineralization outside the current resource. Intercepts include several thick massive sulphide zones with elevated Ni and Cu assays, indicating potential resource growth. | Talon Metals reports that 14 of 20 newly drilled exploration holes at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project intersect high-grade massive sulphide mineralization outside the current resource. The company highlights multiple strong nickel and copper (NiEq) assays and suggests continued potential for resource expansion, improving near-term sentiment for Ni/Cu. | Talon Metals reported high-grade nickel-copper massive sulphide intercepts in 14 of 20 new exploration holes at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project, including thick, elevated NiEq intervals outside the current resource. Management highlighted continued potential to expand resources, supporting bullish market sentiment toward nickel and copper. | Talon Metals announced results from 116 drill holes at its Tamarack Nickel Project, with 14 exploration holes intersecting high-grade nickel-copper massive sulphides outside the current resource. Intercepts in several areas show strong NiEq grades, but the news is primarily exploration-oriented, not imminent production. | Colossus Resources signed an option agreement to acquire 100% interests in Chilean Calvario and Mirador porphyry projects targeting copper (± Mo ± Au), with plans for at least $2.5m of exploration over two years. The news is exploration-stage and does not indicate immediate supply changes for tracked metals. | Spruce Ridge Resources agreed to acquire nickel laterite properties in Oregon (Red Flat and Cleopatra) with reported inferred resources totaling over 1 billion pounds of nickel. The release outlines transaction terms, financing, NI 43-101 milestone payments, and planned resource upgrades, but provides no near-term production or market flow impact. | Panoramic Resources has launched a strategic review of its Savannah nickel project in Western Australia and says it is open to selling the asset in whole or in part. The review comes as battery metal markets are weak, which may influence expectations for nickel supply and near-term sentiment.

Copper prices inched higher as the weaker U.S. dollar made dollar-priced metals cheaper, but gains were capped by renewed concerns over China demand, with home prices falling again and commentary on rising Chinese refined copper output. LME aluminium also edged up, while nickel rose slightly, indicating mixed near-term pressure. | Indonesia’s state holding Mind Id is negotiating to increase control of Vale Indonesia, Indonesia’s major nickel producer, amid rising resource-nationalism and elections. The key risk is whether Vale Indonesia’s mining concession is extended by December 2025; otherwise its major nickel smelting projects with Chinese investors could be jeopardized. | Indonesia’s mining minister said the government will extend its SIMBARA online tracking system to nickel shipments starting next year, covering ore movements from mines to buyers. The policy aims to improve governance after the platform was first used on coal, potentially reducing supply uncertainty for nickel. | Panoramic Resources is undertaking a strategic review that includes potential sale or partnership options for its Savannah nickel project in Western Australia due to weak nickel/battery metal prices. The review aims to reassess capital requirements and funding in the current market environment. Savannah is a nickel-copper-cobalt mine with prior suspensions. | Reuters reports that under a contract, Trafigura will supply nickel in the range of about 20,000 to roughly 40,000 tonnes per annum. The disclosed supply commitment points to additional availability expectations for nickel, which can pressure prices unless demand or other constraints counterbalance. | Trafigura agreed to invest in a planned all-in-one nickel refinery to be established by Korea Zinc via its nickel sulfate subsidiary, KEMCO. The move increases refining/capacity expectations for nickel, which can reduce fears of near-term supply tightness and exert downward pressure on nickel prices. | Trafigura and Korea Zinc signed an KRW185 billion (about USD140 million) deal to build an all-in-one nickel refinery in Ulsan, South Korea, and secure working capital. Trafigura will supply 20,000–40,000 tonnes of nickel feedstock annually under a long-term agreement with off-take rights. | Trafigura signed a $140 million agreement with Korea Zinc to build an all-in-one nickel refinery in Ulsan, using pyro-hydro processing for nickel matte and MHP. Korea Zinc will set up the project via its nickel sulfate unit KEMCO, with Trafigura taking a stake and securing long-term feedstock supply and off-take rights. | Vale Canada and Sumitomo agreed to sell a 14% stake in their Indonesian nickel miner to state miner MIND ID as required for Indonesia to extend Vale Indonesia’s mining permit, due in 2025. The restructuring increases MIND ID’s share while stabilizing operations, supporting expectations for nickel supply continuity. | Reuters reports that an agreement has paved the way for the renewal of mining permits for Vale’s nickel operations in Indonesia beyond 2025. The development lowers uncertainty about future nickel supply from the site, which can be supportive for nickel prices as markets price in less regulatory disruption. | The article says Chinese firms have invested over $14 billion in Indonesia’s nickel sector over the past decade, building smelters and refineries and expanding related infrastructure. It also notes widespread complaints about working conditions, but does not indicate immediate operational stoppages. Net near-term impact on nickel prices appears limited.

London three-month copper rose to an eight-week high on a softer U.S. dollar and hopes that Chinese property support will improve demand. Reuters cites pledges for property financing and debt-risk resolution. LME aluminium also advanced and nickel increased, reflecting broader bullish sentiment across industrial metals. | Korea Zinc and Trafigura signed a KRW185bn (USD140m) deal to build an all-in-one nickel refinery in Ulsan via KEMCO, using an integrated pyro-hydro process for nickel matte and MHP. A long-term contract secures nickel feedstock supply of 20,000–40,000 tonnes per year, supporting nickel supply outlook. | Trafigura will invest $140m for a 12.9% stake in Korea Zinc’s KEMCO to build an all-in-one nickel refinery in Ulsan. The plant will process nickel matte and mixed hydroxide precipitate, supported by Trafigura’s long-term feedstock supply of 20,000–40,000 tpa and offtake rights. | Gama Explorations completed a SkyTEM time-domain EM survey over its 625 km2 Tyee nickel-copper project in Quebec, adding 1,187 line km and generating 66 drill targets. Results from pending analyses are expected to refine targeting for sulphide-related nickel-copper occurrences, but there is no production or supply update. | Gama Explorations says it completed a SkyTEM geophysical survey over its Tyee nickel-copper-cobalt project in Quebec. The EM data is expected to improve drill targeting and highlight additional targets after previously discovered nickel-copper and titanium-vanadium showings, with samples pending analysis.

Copper prices climbed to two-month highs as supply risks intensified. An indefinite strike at Peru’s Las Bambas mine and maintenance at First Quantum’s Cobre Panama facility—both tied to protests blocking coal—supported LME and Shanghai copper gains. A weaker U.S. dollar further boosted the base-metals complex, lifting aluminium and nickel too. | Ranchero Gold announced it can earn a 100% interest in the Pinchi Lake nickel project in British Columbia under an option agreement with Recharge Resources. The project is a prospective awaruite deposit and includes exploration funding and cash/share payments. No production or near-term supply changes are stated. | Korea Zinc and Trafigura plan to expand an all-in-one nickel refinery in Ulsan using an integrated hydro-pyro process to produce nickel sulfate via MHP feedstock supplied by Trafigura. The project targets battery-grade supply in South Korea, where MHP conversion capacity is limited, supporting nickel expectations despite price softness.

Copper prices pulled back from two-month highs as a firmer U.S. dollar and renewed growth concerns weighed on sentiment. However, declines were limited by a supportive supply-demand backdrop, including low inventories, supply disruptions, and strong Chinese demand. Aluminium and nickel also fell modestly amid the macro/dollar influence. | The article reviews Indonesia’s dominant nickel role and its raw nickel export ban since 2020. It also highlights EV-battery supply chain shifts and policy moves in the US and EU, including an anti-subsidy investigation targeting Chinese automakers sourcing Indonesian nickel. The implications are trade- and policy-driven, but timing/magnitude are uncertain.

Nickel prices plunged on the LME, with the contract hitting a 2.5-year low amid oversupply. Reuters cites a rising global nickel surplus (155,000 tons in the first nine months), driven by an Indonesia supply surge outpacing demand. Other base metals moved slightly, but nickel was worst hit. | London nickel prices fell to a more-than-2.5-year low as oversupply concerns weighed on sentiment. Reuters cites a widening global nickel surplus (155,000 tonnes in the first nine months) driven by Indonesian supply, plus inventories near the highest since March 2021, pressuring nearby and deferred nickel contracts. | SMM announced it will introduce two new benchmark price points for Indonesian domestic laterite nickel ore with 1.2% and 1.6% nickel (delivery to factory) from Dec 1, 2023. The update formalizes data collection and calculation under its methodology and is published weekly, aiming to improve pricing transparency for settlement. | Copper prices rose on the LME as a weaker U.S. dollar and a stronger yuan supported Chinese purchasing demand. The Yangshan premium hit a yearly high and front-running for potential China property stimulus boosted buying. LME nickel and aluminium were also slightly mixed in the session. | Great Southern Copper raised GBP905,000 via a placing to fund further exploration in Chile, including drilling at its Especularita copper-gold project and work at other targets, plus initial activity at its Monti Lithium project in Salar de Atacama. The funding is modest, with limited immediate implications for copper prices. | Copper rose on the LME as a firmer yuan and weaker U.S. dollar boosted Chinese buyers’ purchasing power for the dollar-priced metal. Front-running around potential China property stimulus further supported demand. Nickel was also higher and aluminium edged up, reflecting a broadly supportive base-metal tone.

Zangezur Copper and Molybdenum Combine said a sanctioned beneficial owner (Gleb Trotsenko) no longer holds a stake after selling it in 2023, and that the new large shareholder (Svetlana Ershova) acquired shares legally and avoids sanctions. Media speculate this could be sanctions circumvention, raising currency-market concerns for Armenia’s exports. | Reuters reports London copper on track for a second straight weekly gain, supported by renewed China support for the property sector boosting demand signals (higher Yangshan premium), a weaker U.S. dollar, and improved risk appetite after softer US CPI. Copper holds around $8,409/t, while aluminium and nickel drift lower. | Nornickel will pay $18 million to drop its nickel JV stake in South Africa. The Nkomati mine has been on maintenance after stopping mining amid low nickel prices, and the buyer ARM will assume environmental liabilities. Nickel prices are falling sharply, but the transaction closes in 2024, reducing near-term impact. | Canada Nickel filed a Bankable Feasibility Study for its Crawford nickel sulphide project in Ontario. The study highlights large reserves/resources, long-life average nickel output, and low cash costs, alongside strong economics. While development is still future-facing, the BFS can support bullish sentiment for nickel prices over coming weeks.

Shanghai nickel slid to a 14-month low as weakening stainless steel demand and rising supply pressured prices. SHFE December nickel fell 3.8% and LME three-month nickel extended losses. Analysts pointed to broader stainless production cuts, softer EV consumption, and stock builds with capacity ramp-up. | Nickel Industries is seeking investors for its Indonesian Morowali plant, which will produce EV-battery nickel products such as nickel sulphate, matte and MHP. The company is inviting firms like Tesla and Panasonic to its operations, expects customer deals in 2024, and targets sizeable output once completed in 2025. | Nornickel’s South African unit agreed to transfer its 50% stake in the Nkomati nickel JV to African Rainbow Minerals, subject to competition approval, with closing due next year. Nkomati has been on care and maintenance since 2021, and the deal mainly shifts ownership and liabilities rather than immediate production. | France is seeking an agreement by January to stabilize New Caledonia’s nickel industry after political tensions and high energy costs drove producers toward collapse. It aims to secure €1.5bn short-term financing, improve energy and logistics, and shift exports toward battery-grade nickel, supporting near-term supply outlook. | Premium Nickel Resources reported additional assay results from underground drilling at the past-producing Selebi North Ni-Cu-Co mine in Botswana. Intercepts show meaningful nickel and copper grades (NiEq highlights), supporting continued resource definition efforts. The release improves project outlook but does not signal immediate supply changes, limiting near-term price impact. | Premium Nickel Resources reports additional underground drilling assays from the Selebi North mine in Botswana, a Ni–Cu–Co sulphide system. Results include thick intercepts with grades such as 1.74% NiEq over 22 metres and higher-grade Ni and Cu sections, supporting optimism for defining mineralization down plunge and potential future resource growth.

Reuters reports copper edged higher as China’s central bank signaled accommodative policy and as a strike began at Peru’s Las Bambas copper mine, boosting supply-risk sentiment. Nickel rebounded from three-year lows after an industry report raised the prospect of changes to Indonesia’s nickel pricing. Aluminium slipped modestly. | Copper edged higher on hopes China’s central bank keeps policy accommodative and on a reported indefinite strike at Peru’s Las Bambas mine. Nickel rebounded from three-year lows after an industry report suggested changes to Indonesia’s nickel pricing. Aluminium slipped modestly as broader metal prices were mixed. | Benjamin Hill Mining announced two inaugural diamond drill holes at its Alotta copper-gold-molybdenum project in Yukon, confirming multiphase porphyry-style mineralization with copper and molybdenite indicators. The program is proposed to continue in May 2024, with more drilling targeting geophysical and soil geochemical anomalies. Assays are pending. | London’s High Court will rule on whether the LME unlawfully cancelled billions of dollars of nickel trades in March 2022. Elliott and Jane Street seek $472 million over voided trades after a one-week suspension during a chaotic nickel price eruption. The outcome could affect confidence and LME crisis-response expectations. | Canadian North Resources reported assay results extending sulphide mineralized zones at its Ferguson Lake nickel-copper project in Nunavut, including wide intersections grading both Ni and Cu. The release indicates targets and potential resource upgrades, but provides no production or shipment data that would immediately alter near-term supply expectations for copper or nickel.

SMM’s conference focused on developing an independent Indonesian nickel pricing mechanism. Indonesia’s ore and NPI values are said to trade at higher CIF/transaction levels than HPM benchmarks, and the gap is expected to widen as supply-demand imbalances grow. SMM plans trial Indonesian nickel ore and NPI price benchmarks from Dec 1. | BHP will trial Arca’s “air-to-rock” carbon mineralisation at the Mount Keith nickel mine in Western Australia, capturing carbon from mine waste and converting it into rock. The 18-month pilot has Canadian grant support and aims to reduce emissions, but it does not signal immediate changes in nickel supply or pricing fundamentals. | UBS’s base metals outlook for 2024 projects copper deficits widening to a larger shortage than previously expected, supportive for copper prices. It also forecasts stronger nickel oversupply and sustained downward pressure. Aluminium is expected to be broadly balanced but with a tendency to shortages later in 2024. | UBS forecasts base metals broadly rangebound short term. Copper is expected to shift to a slightly larger supply deficit (108k mt in 2023, 73k mt in 2024), which supports higher prices. Nickel faces intensifying oversupply and downside pressure. Aluminum is balanced overall but projected to tighten later in 2024. | The piece headline says the LME won a case tied to the “nickel crisis,” with a UK ruling going against Elliott. If related to nickel contract or delivery/settlement mechanics, the decision likely reduces uncertainty and supports nickel sentiment near term, though details are not provided in the text. | Orecap Invest Corp. completed an additional seed-round private placement into Cuprum, which owns the Thierry Copper Mine in Ontario. Proceeds fund working capital, going-public preparations, and further assays/drilling. Thierry is described as a primary copper resource with nickel byproduct, but no near-term production change is announced. | The London High Court upheld the LME’s decision to cancel about $12 billion in nickel trades in March 2022 after a sharp price spike. Firms including Elliott Associates and Jane Street had sought $472 million in compensation. The judgment affirms LME crisis powers, with limited direct implications for near-term nickel fundamentals. | An administrative law judge recommended Minnesota regulators not reissue a crucial “permit to mine” for the long-delayed NewRange/PolyMet copper-nickel project due to concerns that its waste-basin design won’t prevent water pollution. The permit decision is now with the DNR, keeping supply timelines uncertain. | An administrative law judge recommended Minnesota regulators not reissue a key permit for the long-delayed NewRange/PolyMet copper-nickel mine, citing insufficient waste-basin design to prevent water pollution. Further regulatory and court obstacles remain, increasing the risk of additional delays and reduced near-term supply expectations for copper and nickel. | A UK court upheld the LME’s decision to cancel $12 billion of nickel trades during the March 2022 turmoil, rejecting investors’ claims it exceeded its powers. The ruling likely affects legal/regulatory sentiment around the LME, but it does not introduce new near-term nickel supply or demand shocks. | A UK High Court upheld the LME’s decision to cancel nickel trades on March 8, 2022, rejecting investor lawsuits. The ruling highlights LME rules, clearing-house risk from margin calls, and references a dramatic prior nickel price surge. Impact is mainly about confidence and liquidity in LME nickel contracts, not fundamentals. | The LME won a £372m legal battle over its cancellation of about £11bn in nickel trades after the nickel price surged past $100,000/t in March 2024. The High Court ruled LME can void trades in exceptional circumstances. The decision should ease clearing and legal uncertainty for nickel markets.

The Advocate reports China-linked buyer interest in the Avebury nickel mine sale. The mine has resumed production and continues exporting nickel concentrate, despite Mallee Resources’ financial distress and receivership. The article cites a nickel oversupply narrative from Indonesia affecting prices, but offers no clear near-term supply cut. | London copper rose 0.6% as China’s manufacturing slowdown boosted expectations of additional government stimulus, supporting near-term demand. Prices were also supported by supply worries after Panama ordered the First Quantum Cobre Panama mine shut down following a Supreme Court contract ruling. LME aluminium edged up; nickel was slightly lower. | An administrative law judge recommended Minnesota regulators not reissue a crucial permit for the long-delayed NewRange (PolyMet) copper-nickel mine, citing concerns that the planned waste-basin design won’t adequately prevent water pollution. The ruling extends uncertainty around permitting, potentially delaying or derailing future copper and nickel supply. | An administrative law judge recommended Minnesota regulators not reissue a crucial permit for the long-delayed NewRange/PolyMet copper-nickel mine, citing inadequate waste-basin design to prevent water pollution. Further legal steps remain, but the setback adds supply uncertainty and undermines the project’s timeline, pressuring sentiment for copper and nickel. | Archer Exploration resumed drilling at its 100% owned Grasset nickel sulphide project in Quebec. Fall 2023 will test a newly identified nickel-rich massive sulphide zone from prior hole GR23-03, with follow-up EM surveys. Assays include high-grade nickel and minor copper, with no immediate production impacts. | Archer Exploration resumed drilling at its Grasset nickel project in Quebec to test shallow extensions of a newly discovered nickel-rich massive sulphide zone. Earlier results included 1.55% Ni over 5.80 metres and intervals of higher-grade nickel, with minor copper. Fall 2023 drilling and DHEM targeting continue. | Copper Road Resources announced drill results from the JR Zone, reporting long intervals of copper and molybdenum mineralization in the Jogran Porphyry and extending the vertical extent of Richards Breccia. The company emphasizes continuity and potential for additional discoveries, but the update is exploration-only and doesn’t affect near-term supply. | EnerVenue, a US-based manufacturer of metal-hydrogen batteries, is launching pre-assembled EnerVenue Energy Racks with integrated energy storage vessels (150 kWh and 102 kWh) and nickel-hydrogen battery systems. The company promotes simplified installation and modular scaling, but provides no volume figures that would clearly move nickel demand or supply soon. | A U.S. city approved Westwin Elements to build a first-of-its-kind nickel and cobalt refinery in Lawton, but residents are concerned about the company’s lack of mining/refining experience and potential environmental and health impacts. The debate and possible petition to halt progress increases near-term delay risk for nickel supply.

The US blocked Indonesia from becoming a free-trade partner for IRA battery sourcing benefits, after failed talks between Jokowi and Biden. As Indonesia is not on the US list, Indonesian materials are less likely to qualify for EV tax credits. Australian nickel miners welcomed the move, supporting nickel demand expectations. | Copper prices rose after the dollar weakened and China’s Caixin factory activity unexpectedly expanded in November, supporting demand expectations for industrial metals. The article also shows aluminium up modestly and nickel roughly unchanged, suggesting a broader positive metals tone driven mainly by macro and China data. | SMM says it has launched Indonesia’s domestic nickel ore price from Dec 1, publishing weekly assessments for Ni 1.2% and Ni 1.6% laterite delivered to factories. The average prices were $22.9/wmt and $36.7/wmt, each down $0.9 and $1.6 from the prior week. | Copper rose to a three-month high as Caixin showed stronger-than-expected China factory expansion, while exchange inventories declined and tighter supply signals emerged (Panama issues, less concentrate availability). A softer U.S. dollar and expectations of earlier Fed easing provided additional tailwinds. Nickel and aluminium were also modestly higher. | Talon Metals agreed with the U.S. Department of Energy on how to spend nearly $115 million to develop a North Dakota nickel ore processing plant supplying Tesla, with ore from a proposed Minnesota mine. The project has to pass further Minnesota permitting and faces environmental opposition, but DOE funding signals support for critical-minerals supply. | Developers of a Talon Metals nickel ore processing plant backed by the U.S. Department of Energy have reached an agreement on how to spend nearly $115 million. Funding would be released as permits are obtained, improving the project’s execution timeline and supporting domestic nickel supply prospects.

Premium Nickel Resources Ltd. announced a proposed $20 million equity and debt financing, including a brokered private placement and an amended term loan, to fund exploration and development of its mineral assets in Botswana. The article does not cite any imminent production or supply disruption, so near-term nickel pricing impact is likely limited. | Copper prices retreated Monday as the dollar strengthened, LME warehouse stocks increased, and doubts re-emerged over China’s demand prospects. Copper was down 1.3%. The report also mentions nickel pressured by surplus expectations and aluminium lower, reflecting weaker industrial-metals sentiment. | CaNickel signed a 12-month option agreement to sell its Bucko Lake Mine in Manitoba, a nickel project, for up to C$80 million, subject to approvals. The option includes an upfront payment and additional tranches tied to permitting and commercial production, implying potential future nickel supply. | Premium Nickel Resources announced C$20m ($14.7m) equity and debt financing, including funds to advance exploration and development at its Selkirk mine in Botswana. Selkirk produces nickel and copper (among other metals), but the company describes use of proceeds for development and working capital, implying limited immediate supply impact. | Producers in China and South Korea are shifting NCM cathode production toward mid- to low-nickel chemistries to reduce battery costs as EV demand weakens and auto-makers intensify price discounts. High-nickel NCM output is reported cut by 20–30%, implying softer near-term nickel demand despite longer-term plans to return to high-nickel. | Marvel says it is mobilizing drill crews to the Duhamel nickel project. The move suggests progress in exploration/development toward potential future nickel output, which can modestly lift sentiment for nickel supply expectations. The press-release excerpt provides limited operational detail, constraining how strongly it may move prices soon. | Marvel Discovery Corp. says it has retained a contractor to run a 2023 drilling campaign at its Duhamel Ni-Cu-Co (and Fe-Ti-V) property, targeting massive sulphide zones with airborne and TDEM geophysical trends. Historical results include nickel and copper grades, but no production or supply-change details are given. | The piece argues nickel’s underperformance will persist as Indonesia’s output and smelting expand after the 2020 ore export ban, adding to global surplus. It forecasts surpluses widening in 2024, notes weaker stainless-steel demand, and highlights very bearish LME positioning, leaving nickel prices under pressure near term.

Premium Nickel Resources Ltd. upsizes a previously announced private placement and amends its term loan, raising total financing to about $22 million. The company expects closing around Dec. 14, subject to approvals. The news relates to potential project funding but does not cite production volumes or nickel supply changes. | Si6 Metals in Botswana says recent drilling at Maibele North intersected strong visible nickel-copper sulphides (pentlandite, chalcopyrite) and expects assays in 6–8 weeks. The company plans a mineral resource review and open-pit mining study. While relevant for nickel and copper sentiment, no immediate supply impact is indicated. | The US Treasury’s FEOC definition will disqualify nickel projects with more than 25% foreign-government control—especially many Indonesian operations with Chinese stakes—from IRA battery-related tax credits. This likely reduces near-term expectations for Indonesian nickel entering the US EV supply chain, tightening demand prospects. | Pacific Ridge reported diamond drilling results at its Kliyul copper-gold project, including an interval of 111.0 m grading 0.45% CuEq. The company says drilling expands the known KMZ mineralized footprint and that mineralization remains open. The release is copper-focused and provides no near-term nickel or aluminium drivers. | Kutcho Copper Corp. reports target generation for its Kutcho copper-zinc project, highlighting high-priority drill areas such as Esso West, with historical intercepts including 7.2m at 2.0% Cu and plans for ground and drill testing in 2024. The release is exploration-focused with no clear near-term market supply impacts. | Cobalt One (Blackstone Minerals) agreed to acquire CaNickel’s Bucko Lake nickel mine in Manitoba for about C$80m. The past-producing asset is outside Wabowden and requires tranches, permitting and eventual commercial production. The buyer is described as well-resourced to advance the project, supporting nickel supply sentiment. | Trafigura seeks to keep a freezing order against businessman Prateek Gupta, arguing he orchestrated a $600 million fraud involving nickel trades. The dispute alleges nickel was substituted with lower-value materials, leading to a major impairment. The ruling could affect Trafigura’s finances, but it does not indicate new near-term nickel market disruptions. | Arya Resources Ltd. said drilling has begun on its Dunlop nickel-copper project in Saskatchewan, after permits were obtained and crews were mobilized to test identified targets. No resource estimates or production changes were provided, implying limited near-term implications for nickel and copper supply expectations and prices. | Arya Resources Ltd. announced the start of drilling on its Dunlop Nickel-Copper Project in Saskatchewan, targeting identified nickel-copper targets. The company says it has permits and is mobilizing crews to begin around Dec. 6, 2023. The news concerns exploration activity, not production or shipments. | Arya Resources announced mobilization and planned commencement of a drill program starting around Dec. 6, 2023 on its Dunlop Nickel-Copper project in Saskatchewan, targeting nickel-copper targets. Permits have been received; however, no drilling results or production changes are reported, limiting near-term price impact. | A London court document alleges that Trafigura used shipping receipts (bills of lading) from nickel trades to raise financing by lending them to uninvolved firms and potentially substituting low-value metals for pure nickel. The dispute focuses on trade documentation/arbitrage, with no stated immediate effect on nickel supply or official stocks.

Blackstone Minerals executed a 12-month option to acquire 100% of the Wabowden nickel sulphide project in Manitoba, which includes the Bucko mine and processing facility. The firm says the asset could supply its Ta Khoa refinery, reducing reliance on third-party feed, with low-cost, renewable power and supportive policy alignment. | The LME is researching a possible nickel sulphate contract amid rising EV battery demand. Nickel sulphate is a lower-purity nickel input for batteries, and Indonesia’s dominance in nickel supply increases focus on this product. A new Singapore exchange also plans a nickel sulphate futures contract. | Blackstone executed a 12-month option to acquire 100% of the Wabowden nickel sulphide project in Manitoba, including the Bucko mine and processing facility. The company says Wabowden could supply Ta Khoa refinery, improve nickel feed security, and benefit from low-cost hydropower and IRA compliance as battery demand grows. | Indonesia expects 2023 nickel product output at 1.65-1.75 million tonnes and highlights 2.2 million tonnes of current capacity, including NPI/matte and MHP. The government is not issuing new NPI permits but supports HPAL/MHP expansion. Commentary on smelters facing tighter ore supply adds near-term supply-market tension. | Green River Gold reported assay results from three drill holes at its Quesnel Nickel Project, confirming consistent nickel mineralization starting at bedrock surface and continuing across 50 consecutive holes. The company also plans further drilling (about 6,000 meters) to test depth and expand the known zones, supporting project upside but not near-term supply. | Green River Gold reported assay results from three holes at its Quesnel Nickel/Magnesium/Talc Project, with average nickel grade of 0.18% across eight received holes and evidence of nickel-bearing minerals starting at bedrock. The company is continuing drilling and awaiting permits for additional 6,000 m of NQ drilling. | The report highlights two early-stage proposed nickel projects in B.C. (FPX Baptiste and Giga Metals Turnagain), with pre-feasibility studies and major investor backing. It notes potential output, processing approaches, and permitting hurdles, but emphasizes early development and long timelines, limiting immediate nickel price impact.

London copper prices were flat as traders waited for catalysts. A nearly 1% weekly gain in the U.S. dollar index weighed on dollar-priced copper. LME aluminium slipped slightly, while nickel rose sharply; however, overall moves suggest limited new directional impetus. China export growth helped cushion sentiment. | Lifezone Metals announced an updated Mineral Resource estimate for its Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, increasing measured/indicated resources and contained nickel (and also copper) and adding ultramafic material via a revised processing flowsheet. The release is based on resource modeling, not reserves or near-term production. | Lifezone Metals reported a significant Mineral Resource update for its 69.7%-owned Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, increasing Measured & Indicated resources and contained nickel (and also copper) versus the prior estimate. It strengthens the technical basis for a Definitive Feasibility Study, improving sentiment on future battery-metal supply. | Lifezone Metals reported a 69% increase in measured and indicated resources at the Kabanga Nickel Project in Tanzania, with updated grades including nickel and copper. The company cites ongoing work toward a definitive feasibility study, but no production change, making near-term effects on nickel and copper prices likely limited. | Nickelex Resource Corporation requested a 30-day extension to close a non-brokered private placement to fund working capital and early exploration on its Strong Project in Manitoba’s Thompson Nickel Belt. Net proceeds target detailed geophysics and drill planning, with the offering subject to TSX Venture Exchange approval. | Nornickel (Norilsk Nickel) announced shareholder approval of an interim dividend for nine months of 2023 (RUB 915.33 per share) and a 100-to-1 ordinary share split. The announcement does not cite any operational changes affecting nickel or copper supply, demand, or inventories in the near term. | Ford said it will take a direct stake in Indonesia’s Pomalaa battery-nickel chemicals project with PT Vale and Huayou Cobalt. The venture targets 120,000 tons of nickel chemicals annually, with production starting in 2026. Ford also agreed to procure battery materials from Pomalaa to secure EV supply.

Pampa Metals contracted Eco Minera to conduct a ~2,500m diamond drilling program at its Piuquenes porphyry copper-gold project in Argentina, aimed at testing depth and lateral extension of known mineralization. Drilling starts in January 2024, with assays expected progressively from March. Historical intervals reported included high copper grades. | Anglo American said it will cut near-term production and capital spending to lower costs amid weak Chinese demand and macro uncertainty. The firm will reduce output at its Los Bronces copper operation and trim iron ore at Kumba due to logistics constraints. Indonesia believes its ban on exporting unprocessed nickel is boosting domestic value-added. Copper prices rose in London as a weaker dollar improved currency buying power and tighter supply prospects next year were reinforced by recent mine closures/disruptions. Copper prices fell after China’s Central Economic Work Conference readout disappointed investors by omitting large fiscal stimulus measures, worsening expectations for demand from the world’s top consumer. Panoramic Resources was placed into administration and its Savannah nickel mine is expected to be sold or recapitalised, with Trafigura positioned to take control. Analysts cite recession fears and increased Chinese-linked Indonesian nickel supply weighing on prices. | Panoramic Resources, an Australian nickel producer, has called in administrators as nickel prices hit three-year lows. The company cites repeated setbacks at its ageing Savannah project and years of poor acquisitions, reflecting continued financial stress in the nickel market. | Canada One Mining reported SGS lab results from an MMI soil survey at its Abitibi East project, identifying anomalous nickel and copper ion zones and planning follow-up surveys and electromagnetic work. The findings suggest potential nickel and copper mineralization, but no production or supply impact is stated. | Canada One Mining reported MMI soil survey results at its Abitibi East project, identifying anomalous nickel and copper ion responses in multiple target areas and planning follow-up surveys and drilling. While positive for potential future discovery, the release provides no production or supply-change details. | Copper rallied sharply, gaining 2.8% to around $8,565/t on Thursday, the biggest daily jump in nearly a year, driven by a weaker dollar and expectations of Fed rate cuts. While the discovery is supportive for future supply expectations, it offers no near-term production or inventory implications for copper or nickel prices.

London copper was steady after a sharp prior-session jump as dovish Fed expectations pressured the dollar near four-month lows. Three-month LME copper was almost flat, while January SHFE copper rose. In the same session, aluminium fell and nickel declined, though the week trend was mixed and copper ended stronger weekly. | The UK announced new sanctions restricting imports of certain Russian-made metals and banning exports of dual-use goods. With Indonesia producing a large share of global nickel, continued export limits could tighten supply dynamics and support nickel prices near term.

Reuters reports copper eased on worries about weak Chinese demand, but sentiment was supported by mine closures/disruptions suggesting tighter supplies and supply concerns around Panama’s Cobre mine. It also notes aluminium fell after LME warehouse stock jumps, then rebounded. Nickel dropped as other base metals moved with risk appetite. | Canada Nickel said it extended the repayment date of a US$12 million loan facility with Auramet to January 18, 2024, paying an extension fee and issuing warrants. The update is intended to advance permitting, engineering, and financing/offtake initiatives during the extension period, supporting near-term project continuity. | The article title says a long-delayed Minnesota copper-nickel mining project won a court round after several setbacks. That outcome improves the project’s prospects to proceed, which can modestly raise near-term expectations for future supply and support copper and nickel prices. | Minnesota’s Court of Appeals upheld the MPCA air permit for NewRange Copper Nickel’s proposed copper-nickel mine and processing at the former LTV site in Hoyt Lakes. Environmental groups had challenged the permit, but the decision reduces regulatory uncertainty for development, with no immediate production timing stated. | Minnesota’s Court of Appeals upheld the state’s air permit for NewRange Copper Nickel’s planned copper-nickel mine at the former LTV site in Hoyt Lakes. The ruling removes a regulatory hurdle after environmental groups challenged the permit, but it doesn’t signal near-term production, limiting immediate price effects. | A court upheld the air permit for a proposed copper-nickel mine, lowering regulatory uncertainty. By improving the prospects that the project can proceed toward development and eventual production, the decision is likely to support market expectations for increased future supply. This can be bullish for copper and nickel prices. | A court upheld the air permit for the proposed NewRange copper-nickel mine, removing a permitting obstacle and lowering the risk of further development delays. The decision improves the project’s outlook and can affect near-term sentiment by increasing expectations of future supply for both copper and nickel.

Copper prices were largely flat as traders stayed cautious ahead of a Bank of Japan decision and assessed US rate-cut prospects. Strong Chinese refined copper production and rising imports weighed on the Yangshan premium. Aluminium weakened on rising LME inventories and nickel also declined. | Si6 Metals completed its first-phase drilling in Botswana, reporting high-grade copper-silver intercepts at Dibete and strong visual nickel-copper sulphide mineralisation at Maibele North, with assays pending in three to four weeks. The project remains delayed with other permits still contested, but the ruling reduces one near-term regulatory hurdle for copper and nickel supply expectations. | Minnesota’s Court of Appeals upheld a state agency decision granting an air emissions permit for the proposed NewRange/PolyMet copper-nickel mine. The permit win is partial, but it lowers near-term regulatory risk for potential copper and nickel output, subject to remaining court and regulator steps. | Silver One Resources announced additional staking at its Phoenix Silver Project in Arizona to cover areas tied to magnetic anomalies along a 30+ km porphyry copper belt. The decision is a win for Glencore/Teck, but other major permits remain unresolved, keeping near-term output uncertain for copper and nickel markets. | A Minnesota appeals court affirmed regulators’ decision granting an air emissions permit for the proposed NewRange copper-nickel mine near Babbitt, overturning claims of “sham permitting.” The project is still stalled by other court and regulatory issues, but the ruling lowers uncertainty for potential future copper and nickel supply.

Nickel 28 reported Q3 results for its 8.56% stake in the Ramu nickel-cobalt operation in Papua New Guinea. Ramu produced 8,979 tonnes of contained nickel in MHP, with cash costs net of by-product credits falling 29% to $2.77/lb and distribution proceeds supporting strong quarter cash generation.

Minnesota’s Court of Appeals upheld NewRange Copper’s air emissions permit, rejecting claims the company overstated the scale of its proposed copper-nickel mining operation. But other permits remain suspended or withdrawn, including wetlands approval, so the mine still can’t be built without further changes, limiting immediate supply impact. | Nickel prices fell on subdued consumption alongside increasing production and higher inventories in exchange warehouses. The report notes expanding supply from downstream investments and cathode output while demand has not improved. | Reuters reports copper prices pulled back as bullish investors reduced positions ahead of the holidays after copper failed to hold above recent highs. Nickel was little changed, weighed by growing supply and weak consumption, while aluminium fell modestly. Overall, the moves reflect short-term positioning and demand/supply expectations.

Shanghai aluminium (Feb) and zinc rose to November highs as markets priced potential further Chinese rate cuts and expected Red Sea shipping delays. The same session saw copper at multiweek highs and nickel higher, reflecting broad metal risk-on and logistics supply friction. | Reuters reports aluminium prices in London rose to the highest since Nov 6 on Friday, driven by supply concerns after a fuel depot blast in Guinea (key bauxite supplier) raised fears of bauxite shortages for Chinese alumina smelters. Chinese alumina futures hit record highs, alongside a weaker dollar, technical buying and logistics disruption risk via the Red Sea/Suez. | A DOE-led study presents a cost-effective solid-state method to produce smooth single-crystal, nickel-rich NMC811 cathodes, scaling to kilogram levels with industry collaboration. Copper prices were steady to slightly higher as a weaker U.S. dollar supported demand and tight global raw-material supply concerns persisted after recent mine closures. Unrest centers on safety, furnace maintenance, and working conditions at the Morowali Industrial Park, a key nickel hub. | Hundreds of Indonesian nickel workers rallied demanding stronger workplace safety standards after a fire killed 18 people. Labour groups threatened strikes if demands aren’t met. A spokesperson said operations at the Morowali industrial park, the world’s biggest nickel ore producer, have not been affected and talks are ongoing. | Workers in Indonesia protested after an explosion killed 18 at a Chinese-funded nickel processing plant in Morowali. The manpower ministry pledged tighter safety laws amid ongoing hospital treatment. | Hundreds of nickel workers in Indonesia rallied for improved safety after a fire killed 19 employees. Authorities also pledged stronger safety enforcement, and unions threatened further strikes if demands aren’t met. | Indonesia’s police are investigating a deadly explosion at the Chinese-owned Indonesia Tsingshan Stainless Steel nickel plant in Sulawesi. The fire killed 18 workers and injured 40+, adding disruption risk for near-term nickel output in a key producing region. | Base metals rose in London on improved Chinese industrial profits. Aluminium hit an eight-month high on technical buying by CTAs and supply concerns after a Dec. 18 blast at Guinea’s main fuel terminal handling bauxite-related inputs. Copper and nickel also increased as the dollar eased and Indonesia-related supply focus strengthened. | An explosion at a Chinese nickel smelter in Indonesia killed at least 19 workers and injured dozens, with fire and hot steam disrupting operations during routine maintenance. The incident highlights safety concerns in Indonesia’s rapidly expanding nickel industry, raising risks of near-term supply disruption for nickel used in stainless steel and EV batteries. | A fire at Indonesia Tsingshan’s nickel smelter in Morowali has killed 18 people and prompted authorities to investigate, with smelter operations paused. Indonesia is a major nickel producer and export ore bans push feed into smelting. The shutdown threatens near-term nickel supply, pressuring prices lower. | A furnace explosion at a Chinese-owned nickel plant (PT Indonesia Tsingshan Stainless Steel within PT IMIP) in Indonesia’s Sulawesi killed 18 workers. Copper prices in London were steady near a five-month high as a softer U.S. dollar—driven by expectations for potential Fed interest rate cuts—supported dollar-priced metals. A fire at an Indonesian nickel smelter in the Morowali industrial park killed at least 19 workers and injured others. The piece cites firm Chinese demand despite property weakness. LME aluminium and nickel were mostly flat or slightly lower, suggesting the strongest near-term upside is for copper rather than aluminium or nickel. | Avebury nickel mine continues operations under a new owner as Mallee Resources moved into administration and receivership this year. Restarted production last year after years in care and maintenance. | The IMF warns that after the Russia-Ukraine conflict pushed metals higher, nickel and copper (along with other EV battery minerals) could rise further. It echoes IEA demand forecasts and notes supply-chain concentration risks, such as cobalt dependence on Congo, which may tighten expectations for critical metals. | The IMF warned that following Russia-Ukraine-related increases, prices of metals used in EV batteries—especially nickel and copper—could rise further. London copper edged higher as China manufacturing improved and investors looked for fresh stimulus, while SHFE copper benefited from low warehouse inventories. Aluminium and nickel were weaker on the firm dollar. Copper prices eased as the U.S. dollar firmed on reduced expectations for early, aggressive rate cuts and as Chinese buying slowed with seasonal weak demand and strong domestic output. Aluminium also slipped; nickel rose. | Copper fell to a two-week low as the U.S. dollar rose and China’s industrial demand worries intensified after weak December manufacturing PMI data. Indonesian nickel miner AKP launches an IPO and after listing will sell 60% to South Korean buyer LX International via Energi Battery Indonesia. Copper fell on the LME as the firm U.S. dollar pressured dollar-priced metals and traders awaited Chinese data for demand signals. Panoramic Resources says it will make 140 on-site roles redundant at its Savannah nickel mine in Western Australia after entering voluntary administration, citing a low prospect of near-term turnaround. The World Bank’s forecast of slower growth adds demand concerns. Aluminium edged up modestly, while nickel slipped slightly, reflecting mixed risk sentiment beyond copper. | Australia’s Resources Minister Madeleine King supports a pricing structure that pays a premium for nickel meeting higher ESG standards in Australia versus “dirty nickel” from Indonesia. The move is aimed at rewarding compliant producers amid industry turmoil and job losses, potentially lifting expectations for cleaner nickel pricing. | Copper prices fell as a steady U.S. dollar and worsening demand sentiment, reinforced by World Bank warnings on global and China growth, pressured risk appetite. Investors awaited U.S. inflation data for Fed rate-cut timing. LME aluminium and nickel also eased, reflecting broader macro-driven weakness in industrial metals. | Reuters reports copper edged higher as the dollar softened and investors paused after repeated bearish sessions.
