The geology targets magmatic sulfide Ni+Cu±PGE mineralization, which may support bullish sentiment for nickel and copper. | Alaska Energy Metals secured an option to acquire the Bambino nickel-copper-PGE property in western Quebec and will begin exploration with soil sampling and a VTEM survey. The geology targets magmatic sulfide Ni+Cu±PGE hosted in gabbro sills. The announcement is exploration-stage, implying limited immediate supply effects. | Riots in New Caledonia, a major nickel producer, have halted mining and damaged SLN infrastructure, forcing mines to idle and the processing plant to run at minimum capacity. The disruption supports higher LME nickel prices by reducing output and potentially eroding a global surplus, despite some offset from growing Indonesian supply. | New Caledonia produces about 7% of global nickel, but the article cites near-term disruption risks: metallurgical plants teetering on collapse, Glencore stopping financing for a loss-making mine, and Goro reducing output after a tailings dam leak. With a global nickel glut already weighing on prices, disruptions add bearish sentiment. | Class 1 Nickel announced an updated mineral resource estimate for the Alexo North nickel sulphide deposit, with indicated resources rising to 42,600 tonnes at 0.92% Ni and a higher contained nickel figure versus the 2020 estimate. The company plans further drilling to upgrade and expand resources across the project. | Class 1 Nickel announced an updated NI 43-101 mineral resource estimate for the Alexo North nickel sulphide deposit, combining new and historical drilling data. The update significantly raises indicated resources versus the 2020 estimate and supports continued exploration at the Alexo-Dundonald Nickel Project. This is generally supportive for nickel sentiment. | Civil unrest in New Caledonia has halted mining and damaged/curbed output at Societe Le Nickel’s operations, while Koniambo idled its mine and processing plant. The disruption supported a rally in London Metal Exchange nickel prices to nine-month highs as analysts warned reduced supply could erode a global surplus. | SMM reports nickel pig iron prices (SMM8–12% high-nickel) holding steady but under pressure from heavier upstream sentiment. Limited Indonesian ore approvals and persistent raw-material shortages keep smelter output weak and operating rates low. Demand from steel mills may rise near the procurement cycle, supporting stable-to-strong ferronickel in the short term. | Nickel prices continued to ease in the morning, with the spot market difficult to trade. Jinchuan premium was 600-800 yuan/ton (avg 700), while the Russian nickel premium ranged -600 to -300 yuan/ton (avg -450). Downstream demand did not rise materially, keeping sentiment cautious despite recent-high levels. | Glencore and Rusal extended their long-term aluminium supply contract into 2025 despite previously low traded volumes. The extension is enabled because Rusal is not directly targeted by Western sanctions. The report also notes an LME ban on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel from April 13, reinforcing near-term supply-tight sentiment. | Deadly unrest in New Caledonia has raised concerns about nickel supply disruptions from a region holding 20–30% of global reserves. LME nickel prices jumped from about $18,510/t in early May to above $21,275/t, with broader worries of future shortages for battery and industrial materials. | Nickel prices surged after political riots in French-ruled New Caledonia, where the country is a top global nickel producer. The unrest led to deaths, airport closures, curfews, and troop reinforcements, raising fears of supply and logistics disruption. Nickel jumped about 5% then added further gains to a multi-month high.

Violent unrest in France’s Pacific territory of New Caledonia has followed constitutional voting changes and a 12-day state of emergency. New Caledonia supplies roughly 20–30% of world nickel reserves and most local exports, raising fears of disrupted production. The article says global nickel prices have surged. | Copper prices fell after hawkish Fed minutes supported a firmer U.S. dollar, making dollar-priced commodities more expensive. The drop was reinforced by weakening Chinese demand as earlier price gains reduced end-user consumption and lifted inventories. LME and SHFE aluminium and nickel also declined, reflecting broad risk-off pressure. | Riots in New Caledonia have halted nickel mining operations and damaged/limited processing at SLN, reducing output to minimum levels. As New Caledonia is a major nickel supplier, analysts say disrupted production could erode a global surplus and supported a rally in LME nickel prices, though prices later eased. | Nornickel’s board recommends not paying dividends for FY2023, citing sanctions and geopolitical challenges that hit revenue and EBITDA and reduced free cash flow. The company already paid interim dividends in 9M 2023 and says it will not increase debt for dividends. Nornickel is a major nickel and copper producer. | Copper on the LME fell for a third straight session due to profit-taking and consumers shunning near-record prices. It notes elevated China inventories and a refined market surplus in March. The piece also shows aluminium and nickel easing on the session, alongside caution ahead of U.S. jobs data. | Indonesia approved 204 nickel RKAB work plans totaling 220.7mn t, but participants expect production approvals may not meet 2024 ore demand of about 220mn t. Supply concerns include lower approval rates outside Morowali and New Caledonia unrest disrupting nickel value-chain operations and transportation. Nickel prices have risen on tighter availability. | May 23 SMM battery-grade nickel sulfate averaged higher (index 32,389 yuan/ton). The report attributes the rebound to limited nickel-salt supply, low finished-product inventories, and higher raw-material costs from elevated nickel prices. Downstream buying continued despite weaker demand due to replenishment needs and warehouse-building constraints. | SMM’s May 23 nickel spot review says prices fell sharply: nickel beans dropped to 149,000–149,800 yuan/ton (down 5,050 yuan/ton). Nickel sulfate traded about 2,582 yuan/ton below beans, narrowing the beans–sulfate spread. Jinchuan and Russian premiums also shifted, reflecting bearish market sentiment. | SMM reports that SMM 8–12% high-nickel pig iron averaged 978.5 yuan per nickel point, up slightly. Higher Indonesian ore approvals vs March are still far below demand; Indonesian smelter output remains weak, and China supply is low. Weak stainless steel demand limits acceptance, but cost-driven tight circulation supports a stable-to-slightly-firm near-term outlook. | French President Macron visited Nouméa amid clashes between separatists and loyalists in New Caledonia, hinting the state of emergency could be lifted. The unrest is tied to New Caledonia’s nickel-dependent economy and an industry downturn, with stalled rescue talks. Disruption risks likely weigh on nickel prices soon.

Copper prices were nearly unchanged as investors reassessed the rally that recently pushed LME prices to record highs, supported by fund buying and a U.S. Comex short squeeze but offset by dampened Chinese physical demand. LME aluminium and nickel fell slightly, indicating limited immediate directional impact beyond copper’s cooling momentum. | Talon Mining begins nickel exploration in Michigan’s Upper Peninsula, backed by about $20 million in U.S. federal funding. The company acquired a large land package and cites prior findings of up to 7.4% nickel, aiming to drill once targets are identified. It frames results for domestic EV-related supply chains. | Reuters reports BHP is racing to secure Anglo American’s copper assets in Chile and Peru, with Anglo investors seeking higher cash/price or a simpler deal structure. The copper mines targeted are expected to produce ~532,000 tons this year, but the dispute is about valuation and execution details, not immediate output changes. | Macron postponed voting reform in New Caledonia after yielding to rioters, in a territory described as nickel-rich. The unrest could disrupt local mining or logistics feeding nickel production, tightening near-term supply expectations. That scenario is generally supportive for nickel prices over the next days to four weeks.

Gladiator Metals reports assay results from previously unassayed historic drill core at the Cowley Park prospect, extending and confirming high-grade copper skarn continuity (notably several meters grading around ~1–2% Cu) with mineralization open under cover and at depth. It does not indicate imminent production changes impacting copper markets immediately. | Australia and the EU signed a critical minerals memorandum intended to smooth investment in new projects and build momentum for a “green premium” market. The article specifically cites nickel among targeted resources, which should support expectations for nickel demand and potentially tighten supply outlooks over coming weeks. | Canadian Copper Inc. said New Brunswick approved the transfer of the Murray Brook mining lease and title. The company frames the event as supporting a potential Caribou restart by providing a nearby ore resource. The release cites spot pricing used in its resource valuation, but no immediate production impact is stated. | Fathom Nickel reported new drill results from the Gochager Lake project, including multiple high-grade nickel intercepts with associated copper and cobalt, plus strong BHEM conductor evidence suggesting expansion to depth and along strike. The update is exploration-focused and does not indicate imminent production changes for nickel or copper supply. | Electric Royalties reports updates across its royalty portfolio, including Zonia copper-oxide progress toward a feasibility study and Kenbridge nickel project baseline studies and permitting steps. While these developments may support longer-run supply expectations, the article does not indicate near-term production changes, implying minimal immediate impact on copper and nickel prices. | The Fastmarkets podcast interview focuses on battery raw materials, including nickel. It explains how Indonesian nickel matte can feed nickel sulfate for China’s battery sector and notes that mixed hydroxide precipitate (MHP) production expansions depend on whether nickel economics remain viable. The piece links supply chains to sentiment but gives no definitive short-term nickel price direction. | Unrest in New Caledonia since mid-May has halted nickel mining and processing, stopping operations at SLN and Prony, idling Koniambo, and depleting ore reserves. Fuel blockages and possible shipping risk premiums further threaten supply. Given New Caledonia’s large global reserve share, the disruption should support higher nickel prices.

Copper prices rose as expectations of stronger Chinese demand improved, supported by new property-sector measures and an IMF upgrade to China’s growth outlook. Gains were capped by a steady/firm U.S. dollar and higher Chinese inventories. Aluminium and nickel also ticked up on the broader metals sentiment. | SPC Nickel reported high-grade channel sampling from its West Graham Ni-Cu project in Sudbury, including results of 0.94% Ni and 0.44% Cu over 17 metres and 0.62% Ni and 0.31% Cu over 38 metres. The company says results correlate with prior drilling and support development potential, but no immediate production impact is stated. | Copper Lake Resources reported winter drilling results from its Marshall Lake copper-zinc-silver VMS property in Ontario, highlighting high-grade copper and zinc intercepts and a persistent EM conductor with the deposit remaining open at depth. The update supports potential resource expansion but does not indicate near-term production. | SGS says it was contracted by The Metals Company to test and optimize onshore processing flowsheets producing high-purity nickel sulfate from seafloor polymetallic nodules. Bench-scale work also targets cobalt sulfate and mentions copper cathode production from nickel matte, supporting future supply expectations for nickel battery chemicals. | LME approved the first Indonesian refined-nickel brand (“DX-zwdx”) for delivery, adding a new source of Class I nickel. With Russia’s Norilsk constrained by sanctions and LME stocks rising to a two-year high, the shift toward Indonesian/Chinese deliverable supply is likely to weigh on nickel prices.

BHP published its Statement 1087 compliance assessment report for the Mt Keith Satellite Project, covering 28 Dec 2020 to 31 Dec 2021. The report states BHP Nickel West was compliant with all conditions, describing ongoing satellite mining operations as an extension to the Mt Keith Nickel Mine. No near-term operational disruption is indicated. | Shanghai aluminium hit a two-year high on early trade, supported by supply constraints linked to western sanctions on Russian metals, tight availability (including higher premiums to Japan), and robust demand from solar and EV sectors. Meanwhile, copper and nickel slipped as a firmer U.S. dollar weighed on base metals priced in dollars. | LX Pantos and Indonesian shipping firm KSA plan a joint venture to transport coal and other minerals from Indonesia, including nickel. KSA already moves large volumes and the venture expects to secure barges and expand vessels and transshipment facilities in stages. This could improve Indonesian nickel export capacity and near-term supply expectations. | Copper fell 2.7% on the LME as funds unwound bullish positions amid concerns that high interest rates will damp metals demand, especially from China. A firmer dollar and higher yields added pressure. Aluminium and nickel also declined, reflecting broader industrial-metals risk-off sentiment. | Eramet’s CEO said SLN’s New Caledonia nickel furnaces need ore supplies in the coming days because mining has been halted by riots and the Doniambo plant is running at minimum capacity. Without re-supply within about 10 days, operations face risk, tightening near-term nickel supply expectations. | Norilsk Nickel expects the global nickel market to remain in surplus, forecasting roughly 100,000 metric tons for 2024 and 2025. It says low-grade nickel will dominate the surplus. The company attributes persistence to supply growth from new Indonesian facilities and rising offerings of nickel and chemical compounds. | Anglo American said it has started formal processes to divest coal and explore options for its nickel operations after rejecting BHP takeover bids. Management indicated nickel mines could be mothballed and processes will follow regulatory steps. Such actions may tighten near-term nickel supply expectations and support nickel prices.

Weaker US economic data revised GDP down and reinforced “higher-for-longer” rate concerns, dragging US risk assets. The report says base metals traded lower. It also mentions Rio Tinto keeping a New Zealand aluminium smelter running with new electricity supply agreements, but overall the tone is bearish for industrial metals. | Copper rebounded on the LME, but gains were capped by profit-taking after May 20 record highs and by weaker physical demand in China. China’s May manufacturing PMI fell below 50. Aluminium and nickel also rose on the day, reflecting broader market moves rather than a single new metal-specific shock. | Reuters reports copper under pressure after China’s May manufacturing PMI fell below 50, worsening demand expectations for the top consumer. Despite earlier speculative gains and a modest monthly rise, profit-taking could push copper back below $10,000. LME aluminium eased, nickel edged up slightly.  | Amaroq Minerals reported updated results from South Greenland exploration, confirming a copper-molybdenum porphyry system and identifying 24 additional copper targets for its 2024 field season. The company also references copper-nickel sulphide targets within the same belt, but provides no near-term production or supply impacts. | Posco Group broke ground on a KRW 1.5 tn project with CNGR to build a nickel refining plant and a precursor cathode-material plant for EV batteries. The refining unit targets 50,000 mt/year of ~99.9% nickel and the precursor unit 110,000 tons/year. Production starts in 2026 to reduce import dependence. | POSCO and China’s CNGR held a groundbreaking ceremony for POSCO CNGR Nickel Solution’s nickel refinery and a C&P Energy precursor plant in Pohang. The joint ventures are intended to secure stable raw materials for rechargeable batteries, potentially supporting improved near-term expectations for nickel supply.

London copper rose 1.1% on supportive China and US data: China’s private manufacturing survey showed the fastest activity growth in about two years, and US inflation stabilization kept the Fed’s planned rate cuts on track. A tighter China copper import discount suggested improved physical demand, lifting copper and supporting aluminium and nickel. | Cobalt Blue Holdings describes virtual tour coverage of pilot-scale feedstock testing at its technology centre for a planned cobalt-nickel refinery in Australia. The company reports high cobalt extraction recovery and ongoing optimisation toward producing cobalt and nickel sulphate. Policy support for critical minerals is cited, but commercial timelines are not provided. | Nickel 28 Capital Corp. announced it received a management cease trade order from the Ontario Securities Commission after a voluntary application tied to delays in filing audited annual financial statements. The order restricts certain insiders from trading the company’s securities but does not affect non-insider shareholders’ trading. No production impacts are stated. | A Conference Board of Canada outlook says Sudbury’s economy is being weighed by soft nickel prices. It links the fall in nickel to worries about electric-car demand and, importantly, rising Indonesian supply, noting nickel prices are about half of March 2022 levels. Lower prices have hurt Vale and Glencore profits.

The article claims Power Nickel in Quebec has delivered major “miracle drill results” pointing to large deposits of strategic metals, explicitly including nickel and copper. It frames the discovery as a potential supply solution amid critical metal shortages, geopolitical risk, and high tariffs affecting EV-related supply chains, supporting bullish market sentiment. | Power Nickel reported high-grade drill assays from its Lion Zone, including intervals with 9.88% copper over 5.59m and 0.49% nickel over the same run, plus broader polymetallic results and CuEq context. The news supports project development but does not imply immediate supply changes. | Power Nickel reported high-grade assay results from its Lion Zone drill program, including 9.88% copper and 0.49% nickel over 5.59m (plus additional Cu and Ni-rich intercepts). The news supports project potential but provides no production, permitting, or supply/inventory change, so near-term metal price impact is likely limited. | Copper prices fell below $10,000 per tonne on the LME, down 1.5%, driven by weak demand expectations from China and persistently high Shanghai physical inventories. Softer U.S. data and rate-cut expectations supported a stronger dollar, adding near-term pressure. Aluminium and nickel also edged lower. | Canada Nickel said it completed infill drilling at its Deloro nickel project near Timmins, reporting nickel-bearing intervals and noting an initial resource estimate expected in July 2024. The update improves the project’s resource definition but does not indicate any near-term production or supply impact on nickel markets. | Talon Metals said it extended nickel-copper mineralization in Minnesota’s Raptor Zone by drilling 110 meters more strike, including 8.91 meters of nickel mineralization with 1.77 meters of massive sulphides in a new hole. Assays are pending, and exploration will continue with step-out drilling in 2024. | Talon Metals reported that follow-up drilling at Minnesota’s Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project intersected 8.91 meters of nickel mineralization, including 1.77 meters of massive sulphides, extending the Raptor Zone strike length. Assays are pending, but results support expectations of additional nickel-copper resources. | Talon Metals says it extended the Raptor Zone nickel-copper mineralization by 110 meters and intersected 8.91 meters of nickel mineralization, including 1.77 meters of massive sulphides, with assays pending. The update supports potential future nickel and copper supply, but it is exploratory. | Talon Metals reported that new drilling in Minnesota’s Raptor Zone extended nickel-copper mineralization and intersected 8.91m of nickel mineralization including 1.77m massive sulphides, with assays pending. The company plans further step-out exploration, signaling potential for a larger mineralized system but no immediate output change.

An Indonesian economist argues that nickel downstreaming largely benefits China, with most added value flowing abroad. The piece also alleges continued nickel ore flows to Chinese processors despite Indonesia’s 2020 export ban. These points could influence near-term nickel supply expectations, but no direct immediate price driver is quantified. | Copper prices fell after a strong rally as weak physical demand in China weighed on the market. LME three-month copper slipped below $10,000 and SHFE July copper dropped, while SHFE and LME copper stocks rose. Aluminium and nickel also eased, suggesting broadly bearish sentiment in base metals. | Copper prices near a one-month low as real demand fails to match strong electrification-driven expectations. Three-month LME copper slipped below $10,000, with sellers paying China to destock. Funds reportedly sold copper and bought aluminium. Aluminium was also down on the day; nickel fell alongside broader metals weakness. | Merdeka Battery Materials (MBMA) cut 1Q output at its Sulawesi SCM mine due to wet weather and limited contractor equipment, with limonite and saprolite supplies falling. Its RKEF smelters also produced less nickel in NPI and matte. Guidance for 2024 is steady, but near-term supply is lower. | Norilsk Nickel is upgrading its Nadezhda Metallurgical Plant in Norilsk, including replacing a flash smelting furnace and implementing a Sulfur Program to cut SO2 emissions. The work uses demolition robots and spider cranes to improve safety and efficiency, and management expects production volumes to rise after repairs. | Alaska Energy Metals said TSX Venture Exchange accepted an option agreement granting it the right to acquire 100% of the Bambino nickel-copper property in western Quebec and will commence exploration programs. The release is an early-stage acquisition/exploration update without any near-term production or supply impact. | Alaska Energy Metals said TSX-V accepted an option agreement giving it the right to acquire 100% of the Bambino nickel-copper property in western Quebec and to start exploration. The update is about future project development, with no immediate production or inventory impact for nickel or copper.

Copper futures rebounded above $10,000/t as the dollar softened on expectations of Fed easing and as signs of firmer China demand emerged. LME three-month copper rose 1.6%. LME aluminum and nickel also advanced, with Reuters citing renewed copper buying activity in China. | Silver One says it found additional copper and silver showings at its Phoenix Silver Project in Arizona, with select samples up to 7.79% Cu. The area is near Freeport McMoRan’s Miami copper mine and BHP’s Ocelot porphyry project, and drilling is targeted for Q3 2024. | Copper prices rebounded 1% to around $10,027/ton ahead of the ECB’s expected first rate cut, supported by optimism that lower rates may improve growth. Gains were capped by concerns over sluggish demand, especially in China where inventories rose and producer operating rates fell. LME aluminium and nickel also rose. | EV Nickel reported assay results from 148 surface samples at its Shaw Dome Project’s CarLang B target, finding that most samples grade around 0.24% Ni or higher, with grades up to 0.34% Ni. The company plans further summer sampling, but no production timeline is provided.

London copper eased as softer-than-expected physical demand from China weighed on prices. Markets also awaited U.S. jobs data for guidance on when Fed rate cuts may begin, lifting expectations of cuts after recent labor-market cooling signals. Other LME metals, including aluminium and nickel, were modestly higher. | China’s May unwrought copper imports rose 15.8% year-on-year to 514,000 tonnes, beating expectations despite weak physical consumption. Imports were up 17.4% from April. Analysts cited potential additional Russia-origin flows into China following Western sanctions in April, with DRC shipments also improving. | London copper fell after mixed China trade data. Better exports contrasted with slower imports and higher-than-expected unwrought copper imports, raising concern that Chinese inventories will grow; SHFE stocks hit a four-year high. Broader macro cues from an upcoming US jobs report and Fed cut expectations also influenced pricing; aluminium and nickel moved mildly. | Copper on the LME is poised for a third weekly decline, breaking below $10,000/t after mixed Chinese trade data. Better exports but slower import growth and higher unwrought copper imports raise concerns about rising inventories. Traders also await a U.S. jobs report for rate-cut timing, weighing on growth-linked metals. | Canada Nickel’s CEO Mark Selby argues the world demand for nickel is resilient, with growth mainly driven by stainless steel and some battery use. The piece also updates progress on exploration, permits, and plans for processing plants at Crawford, and suggests nickel prices may ramp later this year.

Copper fell to its lowest in over five weeks on a firm dollar and shifting expectations for Fed rate cuts after stronger U.S. jobs data. Physical demand constraints were noted, including a continued discount in China import premiums. Aluminium declined about 1%; nickel was nearly unchanged. | Copper rebounded from five-week lows, rising 0.6% after U.S. jobs data-driven selloff shifted focus to improving fundamentals. Traders expected support from China returning from holiday and cited better China PMI activity, while concerns remain from rising warehouse inventories and weaker Yangshan premium. Aluminium and nickel saw only modest moves. | Copper rebounded 0.6% after dipping to the lowest since May 2, supported by shifting focus to improving fundamentals and expected Tuesday buying as China returns from holiday. The prior sell-off was triggered by strong US jobs data and a stronger dollar. China stocks remain high and premiums weak. Aluminium and nickel saw modest declines/gains. | Power Nickel begins a 2024 summer drilling program of 20 holes and over 8,000 metres at its Nisk property in Quebec, targeting extensions of high-grade polymetallic sulphide lenses. The program focuses on both the polymetallic Lion Zone (PGE/Pd/Au/Ag/Cu) and nickel-rich Nisk Main. No production or shipments are mentioned.

Base metals fell as the yuan weakened, reducing Chinese buying power. LME/SHFE copper dropped, while aluminium eased amid high inventories, weak physical demand, and a wider LME cash-to-three-month discount reflecting non-tight nearby supply. Nickel moved less consistently, amid broader downside pressure. | Nornickel is reconstructing the pyrometallurgical line at its Nadezhda Smelter by dismantling flash smelting furnace No. 2 and installing a new unit. The smelter processes nickel concentrates and produces copper-nickel matte sent for further processing. The repair is planned to take ~60 days while improving reliability. | Indonesia’s government is reviewing whether to terminate permits for RKEF smelters producing ferronickel and nickel pig iron. Officials said policy aims to reserve limited nickel ore for EV-battery nickel products. Permit termination risk could reduce current nickel supply, affecting short-term nickel price expectations. | Orecap updates on Cuprum’s Thierry copper project in Ontario, highlighting recent flow-through financing, commissioning of remaining assays from Summer 2023, completion of an updated NI 43-101, and historical resource/PEA details. The project’s CuEq calculations assume copper and nickel prices, but no new supply or production impact is indicated. | Copper prices hit a seven-week low as the weaker yuan reduced purchasing power for China and as LME copper inventories rose after fresh inflows. The piece also describes a contango structure with no imminent shortages, while aluminium and nickel fell amid broader base-metal pressure. | Premium Nickel upsized a C$15m private placement to about C$27.5m to fund exploration and development of its Botswana assets, including the Selkirk nickel-copper-cobalt-PGM mine acquired in 2022. The offering is fully subscribed at C$0.78 per unit, with proceeds also for general corporate and working capital.

POSCO Group and CNGR broke ground in Pohang on a nickel refining joint venture producing 50,000 tons/year of 99.9% high-purity nickel and a precursor plant producing 110,000 tons/year. The complex is meant to supply cathode materials via POSCO Future M, with mass production slated for 2026. | Tsingshan plans to register and list an additional 50,000 tonnes of nickel cathode on the LME in the second half of 2024, following its earlier Dingxing listings. The move is aimed at boosting deliverable high-grade supply amid rising LME nickel inventories and growing surplus that weigh on nickel prices. | Tsingshan plans to register another 50,000 metric tons of nickel cathode on the LME in the second half of 2024, after listing 50,000 tons in Q2 from its Dingxing plant. The company expects to apply for 50,000 tons from another smelter this year, adding potential supply. | Kalla Group says it has concluded phase one of its nickel smelter and will begin gradual operations this year. Phase two of a Rp 10 trillion nickel processing and refining facility in South Sulawesi is targeted for completion in 2027, adding capacity and increasing ferronickel and nickel sulfate output, supported by renewable power. | Copper rebounded as the dollar steadied ahead of U.S. CPI and the Fed decision. Traders cited expectations of a dovish statement and lower currency pressure supporting dollar-priced commodities. LME copper rose 0.8% from recent lows, while aluminium was slightly lower and nickel flat, reflecting broad industrial-metal reaction. | New Caledonia’s nickel processing is effectively shut after weeks of unrest. Eramet’s SLN runs smelter furnaces at minimum capacity due to disrupted ore logistics, while Prony halts both its plant and nearby mine for security. Mining is suspended and a rescue package is uncertain, keeping output constrained. | Norilsk Nickel says sanctions have cut revenues and disrupted payments, deliveries and pricing, limiting copper plant sulfur-equipment upgrades. Separately, the US and UK have banned Russian copper, nickel and aluminium from LME/CME trading. Norilsk plans to move some copper smelting to China later, but the trading bans are immediate.

An “over-night” market note focuses on US CPI driving risk-on sentiment, but the key metals link is nickel-specific: reports say Indonesia is considering terminating permits for Rotary Kiln Electric Furnace (RKEF) smelters. That raises near-term supply risk for nickel refining, supporting expectations of firmer nickel prices. | Power Nickel Inc. plans a flow-through offering of up to $20 million to fund exploration on its Nisk property in Quebec, aimed at expanding its high-grade nickel mineralization. The financing is expected to close before end-June and supports drilling over the next 12–18 months, which may boost nickel sentiment. | Ardea Resources says its mineralised neutraliser (MN) testwork has improved nickel-cobalt inventory by about 16% at the Goongarrie Hub project and received a $1.1m federal grant. A Japanese consortium also committed funds for a definitive feasibility study, supporting development progress that may help nickel supply sentiment. | Copper prices paused after rebounding, pressured by the Fed’s delayed rate-cut timeline, which steadied the U.S. dollar. Reuters notes profit-taking and highlights rising LME inventories in Taiwan and South Korea. LME aluminium and nickel also fell on the same macro-driven risk/FX move. | Draganfly was selected by First Atlantic Nickel to deploy drone-based survey technology to explore the Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland, targeting awaruite (a nickel-iron alloy). The stated goal is to rapidly identify prospective zones to support 2024 exploration and drilling, potentially strengthening future nickel supply expectations. | Macquarie’s Jim Lennon says global nickel prices likely hit a floor as Indonesian nickel ore supply tightens after slower approval of mining permits and RKAB quotas fall short of demand. He expects excess to narrow, with nickel support from stronger steel demand and improving battery-sector growth. | First Atlantic Nickel selected Draganfly drones for aerial and magnetic geophysical surveys at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland. The drone-based sensing aims to identify high-priority nickel (awaruite) targets and speed exploration ahead of a 2024 drilling program, but it does not change near-term supply. | Fastmarkets’ nickel market round-up discusses the ongoing “relief rally” in LME nickel during H1 2024, but highlights resistance near the 50-month/50-week moving averages and the $20,000/t psychological level. It expects short-term rebounds to attract buyers, while maintaining a broader bearish tendency into mid-2024. | GT Resources completed a C$1.84m non-brokered private placement (flow-through shares) and mobilized exploration drill rigs for the Canalask copper-nickel project in the Yukon. The program targets EM Maxwell plate and downhole surveys to locate massive sulphide mineralization, including historical nickel and up to 6.1% copper assays.

Kevin Rudd criticized the London Metals Exchange for not supporting new nickel standards to counter China’s cheap dumping. He warned the practice could trigger a 20% reduction in global nickel supply sources, with officials suggesting current “doing business as usual” is harmful for industry and geopolitics. | Macquarie analyst Jim Lennon said global nickel prices may have hit a floor. He expects a recovery supported by strong demand from the steel sector and improving supply dynamics, as Indonesia’s slowdown in nickel ore production permits reduces the existing ore surplus. | Nickel prices on the LME edged up on Friday but were set to fall for a fourth straight week as LME inventories rose. Stock build is linked to Chinese producers ramping high-grade cathode output in Indonesia and China that can be delivered on the LME. | Copper prices fell as a stronger dollar and rising LME-registered inventories signaled a supply surplus, widening contango. Nickel dropped to its lowest in over two months, pressured by higher LME and SHFE stocks and increased China high-grade cathode output. Aluminium also eased amid the same bearish complex tone. | Reuters reports copper prices slipping and nickel hitting a two-month low, driven by a firmer U.S. dollar and rising LME inventories suggesting a supply surplus. Copper LME stocks jumped 28% in a month. Nickel is pressured by high warehouse stocks and China cathode output increases. Aluminium also fell. | Power Nickel announced up to a $20m flow-through offering to fund exploration at its Nisk property in Quebec, targeting eligibility for Canada’s 30% critical mineral exploration tax credit. The raise involves issuing 16m flow-through units at $1.25 with warrants. Closing is pending TSX Venture approval.

China’s primary aluminium production rose 7.2% in May to the highest level in nearly a decade, with output also up year-to-date. Producers cited strong demand growth prospects for solar and EVs and speculative buying. LME and Shanghai aluminium prices hit multi-month highs, implying bullish near-term pressure. | Copper fell to its lowest in almost eight weeks as weak Chinese industrial data clouded demand expectations. LME three-month copper slid to about $9,631/ton and LME copper stocks rose. The same China-focused demand worries coincided with declines in aluminium and nickel prices. | Indonesia’s energy and mineral resources ministry will investigate a second work accident at PT Indonesia Tsingshan Stainless Steel’s nickel processing plant in Morowali. Two workers were scalded during furnace slag cleaning, and a prior explosion killed 18 workers. The probe and possible sanctions raise near-term nickel production uncertainty. | The piece is an interview with Lifezone Metals’ CEO about the company’s Hydromet technology and its Kabanga Nickel Project in Tanzania. It claims the process could unlock new LME-grade nickel and also copper supply, but offers no near-term operational updates. Copper futures were down on Friday due to a stronger dollar but were poised for a first weekly gain in five as physical demand picked up after prices hit a two-month low. Copper futures inched lower as traders weighed a firmer dollar and concerns over dull demand from China. The piece cites rising SHFE copper stocks to 322,910 tons, suggesting ample supply. Reuters reports copper futures edged lower as a firmer dollar and weak demand expectations from China weighed on the market. It also lifted 2024 contained nickel guidance to 17,000–20,000 tonnes, signaling stronger near-term nickel supply prospects.

The liquidator of Queensland Nickel says creditors have been paid in full after the Yabulu nickel refinery collapse in 2016. While most coverage is legal wind-down, it also cites BHP idling WA nickel mines because of global oversupply and volatile pricing, implying near-term bearish pressure on nickel sentiment. | London copper fell below $9,000/t for the first time since early April, down 1.9% to $8,933.50, amid worries over China’s demand after a political meeting produced no targeted support for growth or property. Physical demand stayed weak with high inventories, pressuring also nickel and aluminium. | Centaurus Metals’ managing director discusses the company’s recent feasibility study for its nickel sulphide project. The article emphasizes a projected A$175m annual cash flow for 18 years and frames the project as positioned to benefit from the current “battery metals” cycle, supporting positive nickel sentiment in the market. | Northern Star’s CEO says labor-cost pressures in Australian resources may plateau as the nickel industry in Western Australia runs into trouble following BHP’s nickel collapse. He also cites weaker lithium and shutdowns of high-cost iron ore operations. The remarks imply nickel supply disruption could support nickel pricing. | London copper fell below $9,000 for the first time since early April, down 1.4% to $8,981.5/t, driven by investor risk-off sentiment and weaker China demand expectations after no supportive policy measures. Weak physical demand and high inventories were cited, alongside equity losses. Nickel and aluminium also fell. | Brixton Metals reported initial 2024 drill assay results from its Thorn Project, highlighting long broad copper-dominant porphyry intervals with associated Au, Ag and Mo, and noting mineralization remains open at depth. The company is awaiting additional hole results; no nickel or aluminium supply-demand drivers were cited. | Brixton Metals announced initial 2024 drill assays from its Thorn Project in NW British Columbia, describing copper-dominant porphyry Cu-Au-Ag-Mo mineralization, with multiple higher-grade sub-intervals and extended mineralization in hole THN24-290. The company is running two rigs and continuing geophysics and geochemistry. | Eramet raised its full-year earnings outlook on a manganese ore rally but warned the nickel market remains tough. The firm cited a 27% first-half EBITDA drop pressured by weaker nickel prices and a supply surplus. Violence in New Caledonia disrupted SLN nickel output, adding downside risk for nickel sentiment. | The EPA flagged parts of Huntington, West Virginia, as an area of concern for toxic air pollutants tied to nickel emissions from Special Metals’ Huntington division. Officials highlighted health risks from long-term nickel exposure and workers’ exposure. WVDEP will hold a public meeting to discuss the findings, potentially prompting tighter controls. | Flying Nickel Mining Corp. announces strong shareholder approval of its arrangement with Nevada Vanadium, subject to court, stock exchange, and regulatory approvals, with expected completion in August 2024. The company is an exploration-stage nickel sulphide developer advancing its Minago nickel project; no immediate production or supply changes are stated. | Flying Nickel Mining Corp. reports overwhelming shareholder approval of an arrangement with Nevada Vanadium and expects to complete closing in August 2024, subject to regulatory and court approvals. It also notes its Minago nickel project. The announcement is deal/process-focused with no stated immediate effect on nickel supply or demand.

London copper was subdued, slipping slightly as worries about demand in China, the top consumer, weighed on prices. Support came from better-than-expected U.S. data that reinforced expectations for a September Fed rate cut, keeping the dollar steady and lifting some other base metals including nickel and aluminium. | Indonesia is seeking to cut Chinese investment in new nickel mining and processing projects to help qualify for US tax breaks under the Inflation Reduction Act. The incentives exclude EV batteries and critical minerals sourced from entities of concern with substantial Chinese ownership, prompting Indonesia to adjust project participation. | The article says Chinese firms are trying to reduce their stakes in new Indonesian nickel smelters. Such moves can imply less committed investment and possible delays or adjustments in processing capacity, which may support tighter nickel supply expectations and help underpin nickel prices over the coming days to weeks. | Chinese firms are negotiating to cut stakes in Indonesian nickel smelters and HPAL projects so U.S. buyers can qualify for IRA EV tax credits under the 25% foreign-entity-of-concern rule. Indonesia is also working with Washington to include its nickel supply chain. These steps may boost near-term nickel demand sentiment. | Indonesia’s Luhut Pandjaitan says he will invite Tesla CEO Elon Musk to invest in Indonesia’s nickel industry during a US meeting next month. He provides no project specifics and notes Tesla has no near-term EV-plant plans, but Indonesia’s importance to Tesla’s nickel supply keeps sentiment supportive for nickel. | SMM reports NPI prices rose on tight supply: laterite ore shortages in Indonesia persist, smelter inventories remain low, and July NPI output is expected to decline. Chinese stainless demand is stable, with spot prices firm. Refined nickel fell amid US-election risk, yet cost support limits downside and narrows the NPI discount.

Copper prices were range-bound after three weeks of declines as investors assessed the global economic outlook ahead of the Fed meeting and multiple U.S./Eurozone data releases. Copper, aluminium, and nickel quotes in the article showed only small day-to-day moves, suggesting muted net price pressure in the near term. | New Caledonia’s mothballed Koniambo nickel plant (KNS) has begun mass layoffs as Glencore seeks an exit and the 31 August timeline nears without a finalized buyer. Ongoing unrest has targeted nickel sites, while New Caledonia’s nickel sector faces collapse amid a weak global nickel market dominated by low-cost Indonesian supply. | Indonesia is restructuring nickel mining and processing deals to reduce Chinese ownership in projects to qualify for US IRA incentives tied to critical minerals. The government is pushing new smelter investments with China holding minority stakes (<25%), aiming to sustain or expand nickel processing eligibility and production expectations. | New Caledonia’s Koniambo (KNS) nickel plant has begun mass lay-offs ahead of the end of a six-month respite, leaving only maintenance staff for a “cold” sleep. The decision follows Glencore’s earlier exit plans and persistent unrest that has damaged/targeted nickel sites, worsening the already weak global nickel market. | Indonesia is reportedly pushing for Chinese stakeholders to reduce their stakes in nickel projects. The move could alter project development and financing dynamics, influencing expectations for near-term nickel output and supply availability. That supply-effect potential can shift sentiment in nickel pricing over the coming days to weeks. | New Caledonia’s Koniambo (KNS) nickel plant began mass layoff processes after being mothballed, with a deadline for a “cold” sleep state as ownership and buyer options remain unresolved. The country’s unrest has also targeted nickel sites, adding supply disruption amid a broader nickel-price crisis. | Canada Nickel reported improved testwork at its Crawford East Zone, including a locked-cycle result delivering 47% total nickel recovery and 60% nickel sulphide concentrate, with open-circuit tests averaging 28% higher recovery than modelled. A 130-tonne pilot plant began commissioning and will run until September to generate concentrate samples. | First Atlantic Nickel reports progress at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland, including a completed investor site visit, a bridge installed for year-round access, permits filed for additional road work, and expanded geological activity targeting awaruite nickel occurrences. The updates support ongoing exploration that may bolster longer-term nickel supply expectations. | Reuters reports copper fell on Monday due to concerns over weak Chinese demand and elevated inventories. LME three-month copper dropped about 0.7% to $9,045/ton, with LME stocks more than doubling since early June. Macro expectations around Fed rate cuts also influenced sentiment across metals. | Nornickel reported a 1% nickel output drop in H1 due to furnace repairs at the Nadezhda smelter but kept full-year nickel guidance at 184,000–194,000 tonnes. It also noted lingering constraints from delayed Western-sourced equipment and the US/UK nickel/aluminium/copper trading and import bans, with repairs resuming early August. | Fastmarkets reports China nickel sulfate prices edging down, citing slow demand, weaker liquidity, and continued declines in the three-month LME nickel cash price. It also links softer international nickel sulfate activity to BHP’s suspension at Nickel West and West Musgrave, weighing on market sentiment. | Vedanta Nico, a Vedanta subsidiary, partnered with US battery technology firm AEsir Technologies to supply nickel for next-generation nickel-zinc batteries for EVs, 5G telecom and critical infrastructure. The move highlights growing nickel demand for battery cathodes, potentially supporting near-term nickel price sentiment.

Tanzania agreed to pay US$90 million in instalments to Australia’s Indiana Resources to settle an ICSID dispute over cancellation of its Ntaka Hill nickel licence. The article describes the project’s nickel sulphide potential but offers no concrete near-term production impact, implying minimal effect on global nickel pricing. | Kenorland Minerals entered a definitive agreement to sell 100% of its South Thompson nickel project to Evolution Nickel, receiving Evolution shares and a 2% net smelter return royalty. Closing is expected before end-2024 and depends on Evolution raising at least C$4m. The release is about exploration development, not production. | FPX Nickel reports value engineering at its Baptiste project reduced estimated carbon intensity by 50% (to about 1.2 tCO2e/tNi), driven by trolley-assist haul trucks cutting diesel use ~50%. It also secured BC Hydro funding for electrification studies, improving economics and expected nickel operating competitiveness.

PTX Metals reported additional near-surface drill results from its W2 Cu-Ni-PGE project in Ontario, citing higher confidence in continuity of mineralized zones and new intersections with disseminated to massive sulphides. The update is exploration-focused and does not signal near-term production impacts. Gold assays are also pending next week. | PTX Metals released additional diamond-drill assay results from its 100%-owned W2 Cu-Ni-PGE project, expanding and confirming near-surface sulphide mineralization across several zones. Results increase confidence in continuity of historical zones. The release is exploratory and does not mention production, shipments, or new supply disruptions affecting prices. | Indonesia expanded its Simbara mining tracking system to cover nickel (and tin), following a corruption case and to improve governance. The move links production, sales, exports, royalties, and port clearance, targeting Rp 5–10 trillion in additional nickel-related royalties/PNBP. It should tighten oversight and reduce illegal supply. | Merdeka Copper Gold (MDKA) is shifting its mining energy use from coal to renewables such as solar, alongside B35 biodiesel for vehicles and feasibility work for hydropower and wind. The transition is tied to operations including the Wetar copper mine and MDKA’s nickel assets, framed as cost-efficient ESG that may support output continuity. | Indonesia’s Nickel Industries managing director said Indonesia may limit further Chinese investment in its nickel sector to avoid reliance on China and attract more Western capital. This policy risk can influence expectations for Indonesia’s nickel supply and near-term project development, offering support to nickel prices over the next weeks.

Copper prices fell at the start of August as concerns grew over weakening Chinese demand, supported by a private survey showing manufacturing contracted and an official PMI hitting a five-month low. Despite some support from a narrower export parity and retreating inventories, higher exports boosted inventories. Powell’s rate-cut hint offered limited offset.

Indonesia’s Geological Agency says it has identified more than 100 additional potential nickel reserve sites for further exploration. The government’s findings reinforce Indonesia’s role as the leading nickel producer, important for EV batteries. With output and export revenues already large, new prospective supply may ease supply concerns. | Indonesia’s Geological Agency says it has identified over 100 additional potential nickel reserve sites for further exploration. The findings reinforce Indonesia’s role as the leading nickel producer used in EV batteries, but the report does not point to near-term production increases or export changes, suggesting limited immediate price pressure. | London copper rose 0.4% but was set for a flat week as investors await US jobs data while China’s demand outlook remains downbeat. Copper inventories stay high. LME aluminium edged down. Nickel prices were supported by tight Indonesian ore supply, but overall the week’s direction appears muted. | Indonesia’s Geological Agency says it has identified more than 100 new potential nickel reserve locations and will continue exploration. The update strengthens Indonesia’s position as the leading global nickel producer used in EV batteries. With large existing output already, new prospects may support expectations for longer-term supply, mildly bullish for nickel. | Goldrea Resources announced LOIs to acquire 50% interests in additional mineral claims adjoining its Cannonball Project, which hosts multiple porphyry copper-gold and copper-gold prospects. The company says the geology matches Seabridge’s Golden Triangle corridor and highlights recommended drilling. No production volumes, inventories, or near-term supply changes are reported.

London copper edged up 0.2% after prior sell-offs, as some dip-buying emerged, but global demand concerns capped upside. The U.S. jobs report and weaker China activity fueled recession worries while expectations of Fed cuts supported industrial demand. LME aluminium and nickel rose slightly, showing mixed sentiment. | Copper prices tumbled as worsening China and U.S. demand outlooks sparked a sell-off. Weaker U.S. jobs data and technology earnings reinforced recession fears, while rising LME warehouse inventories weighed near term. The risk-off move also pushed aluminium and nickel modestly lower alongside copper. | Indonesia’s Geological Agency reported at least 100 additional, currently unexplored sites with potential nickel reserves. It said the findings strengthen Indonesia’s position as the leading global nickel producer, with nickel being used in EV batteries, and that further exploration will continue. | Copper fell sharply (up to 3.8% on the LME) as global financial contagion triggered a risk-off rush to cash out and turn bearish amid fears of a US recession. The selloff pressured other industrial raw-materials, reflecting weaker growth expectations and tighter financial conditions.

Eramet Indonesia’s director says the company, via PT Eramet Bumi Sulawesi, is looking to expand its role in Indonesia’s nickel sector and build partnerships for an end-to-end EV battery value chain. Eramet plans new mining-site work and permits, positioning against Chinese dominance amid Western EV supply concerns. | Asian Battery Metals reported interim reconnaissance results from Mongolia’s Yambat project, including evidence of an expanded magmatic copper-nickel system and new targets such as Copper Ridge. Mapping and geophysics guided sampling and a Phase 1 drilling campaign. Results are preliminary and plan further scout drilling, implying future supply potential. | Indonesia’s nickel hydrometallurgy intermediate product output fell 12.06% MoM in June due to abnormal weather, which reduced transportation efficiency of ore and materials and caused delayed raw material supply. August output is forecast to rise 9.73% MoM as weather improves, though levels remain below normal.

London copper fell 0.5% to a more than four-and-a-half month low as a firm dollar and looming U.S. recession fears pressured prices, alongside weak China demand concerns. LME aluminium also slipped 0.3% and nickel fell 0.5%, while Shanghai contracts were mixed, reflecting catch-up activity. | Reuters reports LME three-month copper fell 0.5% to a four-and-a-half-month low due to a firm dollar and worries about U.S. and Chinese growth. Shanghai copper rose 0.3% as higher prices spurred Chinese exports and narrowing spreads attracted cargoes. LME aluminium and nickel slipped slightly while SHFE aluminium rose and nickel edged down. | ESG-focused coverage includes Macquarie’s notes that several nickel mines are being suspended/closed amid weak pricing, which may tighten near-term supply. It also reports South32 is appealing WA EPA conditions at its Worsley alumina mine, potentially influencing aluminium supply/costs. Other sections are largely macro/energy/ESG themes. | The piece argues Indonesia’s nickel boom—driven by EV and battery demand—has caused major rainforest and coastal damage, citing relentless smelting smoke, mining craters, and pollution at processing hubs like IMIP. It notes Indonesia’s leading nickel output and smelting capacity, raising the risk of disruption or tighter oversight affecting near-term nickel supply.

The article reviews Minnesota Governor Tim Walz’s approach to copper-nickel mining and highlights that Twin Metals’ leases were pulled by the Biden Administration, effectively halting a planned mine near the BWCA. Walz is portrayed as hands-off and emphasizing federal environmental review, keeping project timing uncertain. | Copper edged up slightly on a softer U.S. dollar, but broader sentiment is weak as LME inventories jumped and global growth expectations deteriorated. Commentary notes rising TC/RCs suggest improving concentrate supply. Aluminium and nickel prices were also lower in the same session, indicating mixed but generally pressured industrial metals. | Copper prices steadied but remained under pressure as pessimism on global growth and risk-off sentiment weighed on base metals. LME copper inventories surged nearly threefold to 294,750 tons. Aluminium and nickel also fell on the day, reflecting weaker demand expectations outweighing improving spot refining charges. | Golden Arrow reported new step-out drill intercepts at its San Pietro IOCG project in Chile, including long intervals grading about 0.43–0.44% Cu with associated gold and cobalt. The release is an exploration update with no production or reserve change stated, so any near-term effect on copper prices is likely limited.

Copper rose Friday after U.S. jobless claims came in better than expected, easing recession-demand fears and supporting rate-cut optimism. It still faces a fifth straight weekly drop, with LME inventories at a high since 2019 limiting upside catalysts. Aluminium and nickel also climbed amid the broader rebound. | Ormonde notes Peak Nickel has resumed a 2024 drill programme at its Rodburn Ni-Cu-Co project in Scotland, targeting further definition and potential expansion of mineralization. The announcement includes Ni, Cu and Co grades used in Ni-equivalent assumptions, but provides no new price-driving results or production impacts. | SMM reports high-grade NPI (8–12% nickel) prices rose WoW on tight nickel ore availability and higher ore-driven smelter costs, limiting short-term supply increases. Easing rainfall in Weda Bay supports logistics, while stainless-steel peak-season demand lifts inquiries. Nickel prices consolidated with a rebound and upside bias, though fundamentals remain weak.

Copper prices opened the week softer as investors weighed ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty and awaited data for demand signals. Reuters cites economic worries including recession fears, with copper down on both LME and Shanghai. Nickel also slipped, while aluminium was steadier, reflecting cautious industrial demand sentiment. | Copper prices rose as traders covered shorts and looked to upcoming China and U.S. data for demand and rate-cut timing. LME copper was up about 0.9%, with cancelled warrants increasing and warehouse inventories still elevated. The report also said aluminium and nickel climbed on the same risk-on backdrop. | US and Indonesia discussed a possible Critical Minerals Specific Free Trade Agreement to increase access to Indonesian nickel for EV batteries. However, nine US senators sent a bipartisan letter urging scrutiny over labor, environmental issues, and Chinese dominance, potentially slowing or complicating the agreement and dampening near-term nickel sentiment. | LME data show Russian-origin aluminium’s share in LME-approved warehouses rose to 65% in July (from 50% in June), while Indian-origin fell. Total on-warrant aluminium stocks fell 23% in July. Russian copper and nickel shares declined. The piece also references the LME’s earlier ban on sanctioned Russian output. | LME data show Russian-origin aluminium’s share of on-warrant stocks rose to 65% in July while Indian-origin fell to 33%, alongside a 23% decline in total on-warrant aluminium stocks. The May Russian ban remains cited, with similar share changes for copper and nickel inventories. | Knights Bay Oryx’s Oman subsidiary has completed initial drilling at its nickel laterite project in Mineral Block 21, targeting nickel continuity and staged resource definition. The company has not yet released lab results, but sampled ore near small mines showed ~1% nickel and shipped material for metallurgical testing. | Nickel prices continued to plunge through July, with early August stabilizing near early-2024 levels. Stainless steel distributors report weak demand, and lower nickel-driven stainless surcharges reduce buyer incentives. U.S. manufacturing PMI stays in contraction, reinforcing soft end-use conditions. Outokumpu sees growth QoQ but still down YoY.

Terrain Minerals reports airborne geophysics at its Lort River project in Western Australia, identifying five conductors that may indicate “Nova-style” magmatic nickel-copper sulphide mineralisation similar to IGO’s Nova-Bollinger deposits. The company plans ground EM, soil geochemistry, and reconnaissance drilling, seeking a new nickel discovery. | Morgan Stanley flags nickel may be near its pricing floor: inventories are surging and demand remains challenging, limiting upside and keeping prices range-bound. For copper, spot has retraced and Macquarie cuts short- to medium-term demand and price forecasts, pointing to renewed near-term downside pressure despite M&A activity. | Morgan Stanley says nickel’s “bottom” may be near but elevated nickel inventories and challenging demand constrain sustained rallies, implying range-bound pricing. Macquarie expects copper volatility, cuts 2025–27 forecasts on weaker demand, and lowers 2024 Chinese and North American growth estimates. Overall, neither metal faces a clear immediate breakout. | Reuters reports copper prices fell as LME inventories climbed to near five-year highs and China demand remained sluggish. Traders cited exports of surplus copper into Asian LME warehouses and weaker Chinese macro data. Purchases from some physical consumers and hopes of a BHP Escondida strike limited losses, but overall sentiment stayed bearish. | Grid Metals provided a permitting and exploration update for its Manitoba projects. Donner’s advanced exploration permit for lithium was approved but bulk sampling is deferred due to lower lithium prices. For MM, the company plans to resume a ~5,000 m drill program targeting Cu-rich sulfide mineralization near the New Manitoba deposit.

Australia’s FIRB approved Japanese investors (Sumitomo Metal Mining and Mitsubishi) to participate in Ardea Resources’ Goongarrie Hub within the Kalgoorlie Nickel Project. The decision removes a JV condition, enabling continued feasibility-study funding and engineering work, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel development. | NewRange Copper Nickel said it will run four studies to upgrade tailings storage design, modernize water treatment (with $100 million planned), improve efficient production to potentially raise throughput, and explore electric ore transport. The copper-nickel project uses existing processing infrastructure and faces permitting work, but most permits remain active. | The report says developers of a stalled Minnesota copper-nickel mine plan studies that could result in significant changes. Such actions can influence market expectations for near-term copper and nickel supply. However, the provided text lacks details on whether production will resume, delay, expand, or be canceled, limiting direction.

China’s July primary aluminium output rose 6% to 3.68m tonnes, the highest monthly total in over 20 years, as smelters ramped up due to still-profitable operations. While prices recently fell and demand stayed weak, analysts expect profits to remain steady and output to rise further, weighing on aluminium sentiment. | NewRange Copper Nickel (PolyMet) developers say they will run studies over the next year on redesign options to improve environmental safeguards, reduce carbon emissions, optimize waste storage and water treatment, and change ore transport. Environmental groups argue the plan is flawed. No decisions or production timing changes are announced. | NewRange Copper Nickel (PolyMet) developers said they will run studies on environmental safeguards and cost/energy efficiency, potentially changing the open-pit mine and processing design. Any major changes would require additional reviews and permitting. The project’s further delays/uncertainty are likely a modest negative for near-term copper and nickel sentiment. | Nickel prices have fallen sharply, reflecting a supply glut in Indonesia and, increasingly, weaker EV battery demand. Europe and the US are seeing EV incentive cutbacks and slower downstream uptake of nickel-rich chemistries (NCM/NCA). Producers are impairing and delaying battery materials investments, reinforcing bearish sentiment for nickel.

Copper prices are set for their first weekly gain in six, supported by mine supply worries after a strike at BHP’s Escondida in Chile and stronger-than-feared U.S. retail sales and jobs data easing recession fears. Aluminium and nickel also ticked higher, reflecting improved base-metals sentiment. | Philippine laterite nickel ore prices increased over the past week, with medium-grade ore rising from about $45/wmt to ~$48/wmt. Demand from Indonesia, tight ore supply, and smelter restocking needs are cited, while policy rewards and expectations of further quota tightness could keep prices rising short term. | Flying Nickel Mining completed its merger, acquiring 100% of Nevada Vanadium, creating a wholly owned subsidiary. The company is primarily focused on advancing its Minago nickel project and a vanadium project. The corporate combination may affect longer-term sentiment, but no near-term production or supply changes are stated.

Copper prices rose as U.S. recession fears eased after strong U.S. data and China’s copper inventories fell. LME three-month copper climbed 0.9% and SHFE September gained 0.5%. The report also cites easing supply concerns at BHP’s Escondida after a wage deal, alongside gains in aluminium and a modest nickel rise. | Flying Nickel Mining has finalized the acquisition of Nevada Vanadium, making it a wholly owned subsidiary. The company is an exploration-stage miner focused on nickel sulphide and vanadium, including development of its Minago nickel project in Manitoba. The article details share issuance and governance changes, with no stated output timing changes. | Power Nickel reported final results from a winter drilling program at its Nisk project’s “Lion Zone,” saying the mineralized zone increased over 94% and providing assay intercepts. The company is pursuing further summer drilling to test depth and continuity, aiming to expand high-grade nickel-copper mineralization.

London LME three-month copper fell 0.4% as the previous rally was attributed to short-covering rather than new demand. Open interest declined and analysts noted limited contract rollover. Chinese physical demand improved slightly, and an Escondida strike was averted, easing supply fears. LME aluminium and nickel also declined. | SMM cited China Customs data showing China’s nickel ore imports fell to 3.4851 million wmt in July (-30.6% YoY), with laterite down sharply. Philippines shipments dipped due to weather, though constraints are easing and supply is shifting toward Indonesia. August is expected to see room for laterite growth despite domestic demand. | Falcon Gold reported delays from high water levels at its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland and the need for additional permitting to build 8 km of road. Equipment mobilization is pending completion by week-end, after which drilling will test multiple copper geophysical anomalies. No nickel operational changes were reported. | Falcon Gold reports delays and permitting for drill access at its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland, with roadwork expected to finish by week-end. The property is contiguous to Benton/Homeland Nickel’s Great Burnt Copper Zone. Reported past and recent drilling shows high copper grades, but no immediate production impact.

Zeb Nickel Corp. said South Africa’s DMPR granted an Integrated Environmental Authorisation for its Limpopo Zeb Nickel Project, clearing a major permitting phase after a lengthy EIA process. The approval supports progressing toward a mining right for nickel extraction, with additional associated copper mineralisation mentioned. | Reports say BHP is discussing potential investment with Indonesia’s government to expand/participate in Indonesia’s rapidly growing nickel processing industry. The backdrop is BHP suspending WA nickel operations due to global nickel oversupply from Indonesia’s export ban. Such investment talks may support longer-term nickel supply balance expectations near term. | Eco-Business says Indonesia’s nickel push faces major hurdles: low-grade product focus, ore supply gaps, heavy captive-coal emissions, and environmental/labor controversies. EU and US rules may reduce appeal of Indonesian processed nickel, and a $2.6 billion BASF–Eramet project was cancelled. The article warns of China dependence and possible nickel price weakness. | Zeb Nickel received integrated environmental authorisation from South Africa’s Department of Minerals and Petroleum Resources for its Bushveld complex project. The approval supports progress toward a mining right and allows activities under the approved EIA, with plans for further drilling to improve nickel grade and project economics. | Flying Nickel Mining entered a definitive arrangement to sell its Minago nickel assets in Manitoba to Norway House Cree Nation for C$8 million cash plus shares. The transaction is expected by mid-October 2024 and requires shareholder and regulatory approvals. The announcement is asset-specific and not linked to broader nickel supply or pricing changes.

Flying Nickel Mining signed a definitive deal to sell its Minago nickel project in Manitoba to Norway House Cree Nation, receiving $8m cash and a 11.4% share stake transfer. The company plans a rebrand and remains active in its Nevada vanadium interests. The transaction is unlikely to meaningfully shift near-term global nickel balances. | NewRange Copper Nickel advances permitting-critical studies for a potential NorthMet copper-nickel mine near Hoyt Lakes, including tailings, water treatment, and efficiency upgrades. The company is examining higher throughput (32,000 to 40,000 tons/day) to increase domestic copper and nickel supply, which could lift near-term price sentiment.

SMM reports the coefficient for high-grade nickel matte versus LME nickel rose to 87–88 as nickel prices fell and refining costs increased. It also cites weak downstream demand, substitution pressure from MHP due to high coefficients, and producers shifting toward NPI for better margins, leaving transactions sluggish. | Nornickel reported a 22% profit drop, blaming weak nickel prices, logistics disruptions in the Red Sea, and harder cross-border payments. Maritime restrictions caused nickel and copper stock accumulation and reduced sales. The firm cited a 28% LME nickel price fall in H1 and forecast a 2024/25 nickel surplus.

First Atlantic Nickel reports completion of Phase 1 infrastructure upgrades and ongoing sampling over a 30 km awaruite (nickel-iron alloy) trend in Newfoundland, ahead of a planned 2024 drilling program. The release highlights awaruite’s easier concentration versus sulfide nickel and potential to ease North American nickel concentrate shortages. | Lifezone Metals said it is starting an evaluation with the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation for political risk insurance for future investments in the Kabanga nickel-copper-cobalt mine and Kahama hydromet refinery in Tanzania. The project is expected to produce LME-grade nickel and copper, with a feasibility study progressing. | DRC authorities have halted or recommended stopping Chemaf Resources’s planned sale of its copper-cobalt mines to China’s Norinco, citing violations of Gecamines’ lease and farm-out contract terms. The dispute may disrupt near-term copper supply expectations from the DRC, though the final outcome is uncertain. | First Atlantic Nickel reported Phase 1 infrastructure upgrades at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland, including road/bridge improvements and expanded nickel sampling along a 30 km trend. The firm is also using LiDAR for better geological targeting and sending initial samples for analysis, aimed at developing awaruite nickel-iron resources. | South Pacific Metals reports exceptional surface trench assay results at its Osena gold-copper Osena project in Papua New Guinea, including 79 m averaging ~981 ppm Cu and localized higher copper grades. The findings support potential expansion of a porphyry system, but are early-stage exploration with limited near-term impact on copper prices and none on nickel or aluminium. | Fastmarkets reports China nickel sulfate prices are rangebound but generally supported. Tight nickel MHP supply (and firm MHP payable terms) and low salt production inventories help hold prices firm, while slower NCM cathode precursor demand limits gains. Higher LME nickel cash prices lifted the all-in nickel sulfate range. | Nornickel said it plans to transfer part of its copper-plant capacities from Norilsk to China via a joint venture, aiming to resolve market-access and sanctions-related issues. Experts warn this could disrupt Russia’s industrial base and concentrate flows. The relocation can affect near-term expectations for copper supply and related nickel-linked outputs.

Anglesey Mining says it has submitted an initial Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to restart underground mining at Parys Mountain, historically a major copper producer. The plan would process concentrate (~30% copper) for export, but requires significant investment and further approvals, making near-term copper impact uncertain. | The UK nuclear regulator (ONR) issued improvement notices to Sellafield after breaches of hazardous chemical controls involving nickel nitrate in an effluent facility. Contamination outside a glovebox could have exposed workers. The issues must be corrected by late 2024 and March 2025, without compromising nuclear safety.

Rusal reported a 41.6% jump in first-half profit as it increased production of primary aluminium and alumina and boosted shipments to China to record levels. It also notes the LME broad ban on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel amid U.S./UK sanctions. Improved volumes and rerouting are mildly supportive for prices. | Rusal’s first-half adjusted net profit rose 41.6% as it increased production and shipped record volumes to China. The firm faced demand pressure as Western buyers avoid Russia-made metal after Ukraine. The article notes April LME bans on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel and links improved output to higher prices for aluminium and alumina. | Argentina’s minister says the pro-business mining incentive regime (RIGI) should attract major foreign investment and help new lithium and copper projects start later this year. Copper is framed as crucial for EVs and AI, with large capex needs for projects, implying improved near-term supply/export expectations.  | Sumitomo’s Ambatovy nickel mine in Madagascar missed output targets after malfunctions, prompting an 89 billion yen write-down and a restructuring plan with a British court. Annual output was 31,000 tonnes vs 40,000 targeted. The company expects costs and production to improve once operations stabilize.

Ardea Resources executed a binding shareholders’ agreement with a Japanese consortium (Sumitomo Metal Mining and Mitsubishi) to fund a $98.5m definitive feasibility study and advance FEED for the Goongarrie Hub nickel-cobalt project in Western Australia. The step supports potential FID but does not change near-term nickel output.

KNS, a New Caledonian nickel producer involving Glencore, shut its furnaces over the weekend, moving into care and maintenance and laying off most of its 1,200 staff. The wider local nickel sector has been hit by unrest, with Eramet’s SLN operating minimally and Prony Resources suspending operations.

Canada Nickel filed an NI 43-101 technical report for its Deloro nickel sulphide project, confirming an initial mineral resource estimate effective July 17, 2024 with no material differences from earlier results. The update is a regulatory disclosure tied to future exploration progress, but no near-term production changes are stated. | EV Nickel Inc. reported drill-hole results from its Langmuir #2 nickel zone, including intersections from 1.07% to 5.11% Ni and higher-than-expected nickel sulphide tenor (~12% to >40% Ni). The company plans further drilling to expand the mineralized envelope and advance its nickel strategy toward battery supply. | Mink Ventures reported positive results from a summer sampling program at its Warren nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario. Reference samples confirmed mineralized zones with meaningful nickel grades and significant copper assays, supporting drill targeting for a planned diamond drilling phase after freeze-up. | Mink Ventures reported positive grab-sample assays from the summer sampling program at its Warren nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario, highlighting multiple zones with copper and nickel grades. The company plans winter access and diamond drilling after freeze-up to test refined IP/EM targets. Near-term impact on metal prices appears limited. | Fastmarkets highlights Indonesia as the core of global nickel supply and processing, with expanding intermediate and refined capacity and rising ore imports. It links this supply build-out to much lower nickel prices, citing the LME three-month contract down 47% since January 2023 and Western producer closures in a low-price environment.

Koniambo Nickel SAS said negotiations for Glencore’s 49% stake are continuing, but its furnaces were shut over the weekend and operations were already halted. Employees were laid off, and other New Caledonia nickel players are running at minimal capacity or suspending. These disruptions point to near-term nickel supply weakness. | Indonesia’s deputy minister said nickel prices should remain stable near current levels as additional capacity from new plants offsets rising EV-driven demand. He noted ore supply risks from declining nickel content and potential policy tweaks to nickel tax holidays amid the 15% global minimum corporate tax regime. Overall, near-term pressure appears mixed.

Canada Nickel said it received a Letter of Interest from Export Development Canada for up to US$500 million of long-term debt financing to support the Crawford Nickel Sulphide Project in Ontario. The funding is subject to due diligence and permits, but the milestone improves prospects for future nickel supply. | Canada Nickel said Export Development Canada is considering a loan of up to $500 million for the Crawford project in Ontario and that the company is seeking remaining funding and permits for construction. It also plans a nickel-processing plant in Ontario to process about 80,000 tonnes annually, supporting nickel supply expectations. | The piece argues global copper demand is rising from electrification and energy transition while supply faces constraints: declining ore grades, labor disruptions, fewer high-quality projects, and a large projected deficit by 2030. It details BHP’s expansion in South Australia (including refining) but says uncertainty remains about fully closing the gap. | China has filed a WTO complaint against Canada over Canada’s new tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles and 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum, with Canada’s measures set to start next month. Analysts warn retaliation could raise costs and pressure markets, adding near-term uncertainty for aluminum supply and prices. | Canada Nickel said it received an LOI for up to US$500M in financing support from Export Development Canada to fund its Crawford nickel-sulphide project, alongside expectations of qualifying for additional critical minerals and carbon capture tax credits.
