Nickel Asia plans to sell its entire 15.63% stake in Coral Bay Nickel to Sumitomo, retaining a 10% interest in Taganito. Coral Bay is the Philippines’ sole HPAL processing plant. The move follows equity losses linked to higher costs and lower LME nickel prices, amid a nickel glut.

Indonesia is weighing cuts to nickel mine quotas for 2025, potentially reducing output from 272 mln to 150 mln tons. Macquarie said such cuts are unlikely, but if implemented they could remove up to 35% of global nickel supply, creating upside risk for prices amid prior Fitch downgrades. | The public can comment until Feb. 7 on Canada Nickel’s Crawford Nickel Project environmental impact statement, covering a long-running open-pit nickel-and-cobalt mine. The assessment flags habitat and water impacts and mitigation measures. It’s in phase 2 of a 5-phase process, with decisions still far off.

Indonesia’s mining ministry set a nickel ore mining quota of around 200 million metric tons for 2025. The quota may be reduced if miners fail performance checks, especially regarding reclamation and environmental management. A higher quota generally supports nickel supply expectations, weighing on prices near term. | Macquarie warns that proposed Indonesian nickel mine quota cuts could slash output from 272mt in 2024 to about 150mt, removing more than one-third of global supply. Even if a major cut is unlikely, lower-than-expected production is seen as an upside risk for nickel prices. | High-nickel pig iron prices in China and Indonesia weakened, with SMM NPI falling weekly and the Indonesian FOB index declining. Stainless steel maintenance reduced procurement, while Indonesia’s new capacity and supply variability increased. The NPI discount to electrolytic nickel widened, and macro/FX pressure plus possible oversupply sustain bearish nickel sentiment. | SMM reviews Indonesia’s nickel market: nickel ore domestic trade improved amid policy uncertainty, with supply stabilizing and ore premiums potentially rising. However, Indonesian high-grade NPI FOB fell $0.7/mtu WoW, with seasonal stainless demand weakness. Refined nickel production is ramping up, with more output expected in 2025.

Abaxx plans to launch a physically deliverable lithium carbonate contract within weeks after starting trading of its nickel sulphate futures. The exchange’s physically deliverable nickel contract begins in Singapore, with settlement linked to the LME nickel price and possible new delivery points. The story focuses on contract mechanics rather than nickel fundamentals. | MetalMiner reports bearish nickel market conditions, with Indonesia blamed for a supply glut. Nickel prices fell below $15k/mt to a multi-year low and exchange inventories rose sharply (LME and SHFE at highs). Indonesia is considering cutting mining quotas, but tighter supply may not be immediate, keeping prices under pressure. | Reuters notes the LME has fully recovered from its 2022 nickel crisis, with nickel inventories rising sharply and trading volumes rebounding. The exchange expanded approved nickel brands and broader stock/liquidity trends supported higher activity across base metals, while copper and aluminium are referenced largely as part of the overall inventory cycle.

Reuters says the LME has fully recovered from the 2022 nickel crisis, with 2024 trading volumes surging and LME nickel inventory rising to around 230,000 mt by end-November 2024 from under 40,000 in May 2023. Higher stocks and improved delivery options point to easing tightness. | Pan Global plans to accelerate 2025 exploration in Spain, including imminent drilling at the Escacena and CaÌrmenes projects. The program targets copper-tin-silver extensions at La Romana and copper-cobalt-nickel mineralization at CaÌrmenes. No immediate mine output or trade disruption is cited. | Reuters reports the LME has fully recovered from the 2022 nickel crisis, with nickel inventories rising to nearly 230,000 tonnes and new nickel brands approved, boosting confidence. It also cites higher exchange stocks across base metals (including aluminium) and fluctuating copper volumes tied to fund flows, implying mixed near-term price signals.

Hannan Metals reports a 1.2 km induced-polarization chargeability anomaly at its Sortilegio copper-gold porphyry prospect in Peru and states the target is drill-ready with permits for Q2 2025. This is positive for long-term copper optionality, but it does not affect current supply or inventories. | First Atlantic Nickel says Newfoundland’s Mineral Lands Division has granted permits for Phase 2 drilling and a southern access trail at its Atlantic Nickel Project. The program will expand the RPM discovery area and target extensions toward Chrome Pond and Pipestone. The firm is preparing for drilling and expects Phase 1 assay updates soon.

The article argues that Indonesia’s rising nickel output is driving higher deforestation, pollution, and mining-related disasters in Sulawesi. It warns that weak sustainability practices increase social and environmental risk, potentially leading to stricter oversight or cost burdens. The study suggests near-term sentiment risk for nickel. | Terrain Minerals has begun reverse-circulation drilling at its Lort River copper-nickel project, targeting Nova-Bollinger-style sulphide conductors based on EM data. The company expects RC drilling to finish in late January and assays in March, but the report is exploratory with no immediate supply effects for nickel or copper prices. | Port Kembla Copper has sold its former smelter site to developers, enabling rehabilitation of about 300 nearby homes after inspections found historical heavy-metal contamination. The smelter operated from 2000 to closure in 2003, and remediation work will begin post-sale. The news is localized and does not change current metal supply. | The piece argues that nickel mining in Indonesia’s Sulawesi is linked to nearly double deforestation rates near mines (2011–2018), plus higher pollution and mining-related disasters like landslides and flash floods. It frames these harms as potential drivers of stricter oversight and disruption risk for nickel operations, impacting supply expectations. | Zambia’s mines minister says Saudi Manara Minerals is exploring investments in the country’s critical-minerals projects, including copper and nickel assets, with discussions already held and a potential deal likely this year. The government also seeks up to 30% stakes in new projects and is expanding copper trading arrangements, boosting revenue expectations. | Petrosea won a $1bn contract from Vale Indonesia to mine nickel ore in Central Sulawesi, with a scheduled March signing and 10-year term. The deal sits within Indonesia’s ongoing restructuring of nickel investment, including HPAL capacity plans and potential battery-grade partnerships.

Ardea Resources is advancing the $98.5 million definitive feasibility study for its Goongarrie Hub nickel-laterite project in Western Australia, with multiple rigs and contracts for metallurgical and water studies. The DFS targets economics for long-term production, but it remains pre-FID with updates expected later in 2025. | Korea Zinc said its nickel sulfate manufacturing technology for cathode metal compounds was designated a South Korean national strategic technology. The move provides government tax and administrative support and supports investment in a large all-in-one nickel refinery planned to start in 2026, reducing costs and improving nickel supply readiness. | Global nickel production is expected to fall 7.4% in 2024 to about 3.4 million tonnes as major producers cut output amid declining prices and operational disruptions. Indonesia, the Philippines, and New Caledonia face notable declines, with regulatory approval delays contributing to reduced mine output. | SMM’s weekly Indonesia-1.17 nickel market review says Indonesian domestic laterite nickel ore premiums generally fluctuated higher, with ample supply and smelter inventories above two weeks. Demand improved on quota/stockpiling effects. NPI prices rebounded, losses eased, and nickel ore CIF prices are expected stable with an upward trend into the next weeks. | A US- and EU-backed Lobito Corridor rail project linking Angola’s Lobito port with the DRC and Zambia aims to speed shipments and expand freight capacity. It notes current copper concentrate flows from Ivanhoe Mines and expects much higher train throughput, supporting copper logistics. Nickel-linked investments are mentioned but are less immediate. | South Korea designated Korea Zinc’s nickel sulphate manufacturing technology as a national strategic technology, expanding eligible support and tax deductions. The company plans to start full-scale operations at a >500bn won all-in-one nickel refinery in 2026, aiming for up to 90bn won cost savings. The move strengthens domestic battery nickel supply competitiveness. | The opinion argues that mining consolidation may resume, with major buyers focused on copper assets such as Rio Tinto/BHP targets and Anglo’s Collahuasi. It emphasizes a global copper shortage from electrification, implying tighter supply. It also notes BHP’s plan to mothball nickel operations, potentially supporting nickel sentiment.

Anglo American plans to sell its Brazil nickel assets, Barro Alto and Niquelandia, by H1 2025 as part of a restructuring after BHP’s takeover bid. Analysts expect obtaining a good price will be hard given a weak nickel price, implying continued bearish sentiment for nickel.

BHP said copper production rose 17% year-on-year to 510,700 tonnes in the second quarter, with Chile’s Escondida delivering a 22% increase to a ten-year record. However, guidance for copper South Australia was lowered after a weather-related power outage. BHP also noted a USD300m impact from WA nickel following temporary suspension. | Noronex reported multiple strong copper-silver drill intercepts at its Fiesta project in Namibia, including 16m at 1.3% copper equivalent and other wider, higher-grade hits. The company also highlights silver’s potential to improve concentrate payability. Overall, the news is positive for copper sentiment but unlikely to affect near-term market prices materially. | Infinico Metals announced the start of a fixed loop fluxgate electromagnetic survey at its Nicobi Ni-Cu-Co property in Québec to refine conductive targets for future drilling. The company cites prior drilling results showing nickel-copper sulphide mineralization, but no production or operational changes are reported. | Infinico Metals began a fixed-loop fluxgate surface electromagnetic survey at its Nicobi property to refine conductive targets for potential Ni–Cu sulphide drilling. Prior drilling at Showing A intersected nickel and copper mineralization, but follow-up closed it off. The release focuses on exploration targeting rather than production or supply changes. | Alaska Energy Metals reported 2024 exploration results at its Angliers-Belleterre project in Quebec, outlining multiple undrilled VMS and Cu-Ni targets with anomalous copper and nickel in soil, rock, and VTEM results. The company plans further geophysical modeling and diamond drilling to test mineralization at depth. | Fathom Nickel reported 2024 exploration results expanding the historic Gochager Lake nickel sulphide footprint and validating magmatic mineralization at Albert Lake, plus plans for further EM surveys and drilling in 2025. The company expects improving nickel prices and notes Indonesia production curbs, but no near-term supply disruption occurs. | Indonesia’s mineral authorities are considering cutting domestic nickel production so supply better matches domestic and export needs and avoids further sustained price declines. As the world’s leading nickel miner, Indonesia’s potential quota reductions could tighten supply expectations and support international nickel prices, amid recent sharp losses.

Reuters column says Indonesia’s nickel sector has flooded markets and drove nickel to four-year lows, but Jakarta plans to cut 2025 ore mining quotas to 200 Mt from 240 Mt. The quota outlook has coincided with a modest LME 3-month nickel rebound (+3% since early January), though inventories remain elevated. | Trump’s administration launched an “America First Trade Policy” investigation under Section 232 focused on imported steel and aluminum. The review assesses exclusions/exemptions and could lead to adjusted tariffs or import restrictions after scheduled reports. The announcement is conditional and timing is uncertain, limiting immediate directional impact.

Reuters columnist says Indonesia is pivotal for nickel’s recovery after 2024’s price bust. The Indonesian mining ministry plans to cut nickel ore quotas to 200m tons from a planned 240m, helping lift LME 3-month nickel by ~3% since early January, though rising inventories and weaker EV battery demand limit follow-through. | The Trump administration has launched an investigation under Section 232 into whether to adjust tariff restrictions on imported steel and aluminum, reviewing exclusions and potential new import limitations tied to national security. Prior 232 tariffs included 10% aluminum in many cases, with possible further changes.

Canada Nickel announced positive exploration drill results from its Midlothian and Deloro properties, with multiple holes intersecting shallow, mineralized ultramafic rocks and reporting nickel grades (e.g., 0.27% over 371m including 0.30% over 39m at Deloro). It also describes ongoing infill work at Nesbitt. This supports bullish nickel sentiment for the near term. | SMM reports Indonesia’s 2025 RKAB quota for nickel ore at 298 million wmt and active ore trading, with smelters starting February procurement. High-grade NPI FOB/inland prices recovered on tighter circulation ahead of Chinese New Year, but quota approval and rising LME nickel inventories restrained refined nickel gains, implying mixed near-term direction. | High-nickel pig iron prices increased this week, with the Indonesian NPI FOB index also rising. Supply was slightly tightened by weaker domestic production due to smelter losses, while tight circulating resources and long-term contract coverage supported demand. The NPI-to-electrolytic nickel discount narrowed, implying buoyant nickel sentiment. | COBCO commissioned its first batch of NCM precursor cathode active materials lines in Jorf Lasfar, Morocco, targeting 120kT/year PCAM capacity. The NCM (nickel-containing) production ramp-up and planned closed-loop recycling expansion point to higher near-term demand for nickel materials used in battery cathodes. | An environmental group says Canada Nickel’s proposed Crawford Project could harm freshwater by releasing effluent into the North Driftwood River, a relatively small stream. The group is reviewing the large impact statement and has concerns about long-term effects over a 41-year mine life, prompting ongoing scrutiny.

Core Nickel Corp. published its 2024 year-end review and outlines 2025 exploration plans for its Thompson Nickel Belt projects, including planned VTEM, re-sampling, and a 4,000–5,000 m drill program at the Mel and Halfway Lake areas. The update targets resource expansion and does not affect near-term nickel supply directly.

Terra Metals says it will re-commission its copper and cobalt acid leach plant by end-Q1 2025 and commission a 1 million tpa flotation plant by end-Q2 2025 in Zambia. It also plans 400 MW solar capacity to power operations. The expansion improves copper production outlook and supports bullish near-term pricing.

Core Nickel Corp. started helicopter-borne VTEM electromagnetic surveys on its Mel and Odei River nickel projects in Manitoba and plans further drilling after survey results. The news updates exploration targeting deeper nickel sulphide potential, with no changes to production, exports, or supply affecting near-term nickel markets. | Tanzania and Burundi signed an agreement with two Chinese firms to build a $2.15 billion railway connecting the two countries and carrying metals, including battery-mineral nickel, to Dar es Salaam port. The 282 km line is expected to move about three million tonnes of minerals annually, improving nickel transport capacity.

A report and experts warn that expanding nickel mining in Raja Ampat, Indonesia, is damaging a UNESCO-protected marine region. Satellite data show mining pit area grew sharply since 2020. Despite a March 2024 court ruling requiring extra protection for small islands, permits continue, raising risks to nickel production timelines. | Glencore reported 2024 output declines across several commodities, including copper. Copper production fell to 951,600 tonnes, down 6% from 2023 and at the low end of its guidance. Management expects to provide 2025 guidance in February, with improved H2 performance supporting some offset. | Burundi and Tanzania agreed with Chinese firms to build a $2.15bn 282 km railway to Dar es Salaam to move minerals, including battery nickel. Financed with African Development Bank support, the line is expected to haul three million tonnes annually. Improved export logistics could lift near-term expectations for nickel supply. | A report says nickel mining expansion in Indonesia’s Raja Ampat region has rapidly increased land use and raised fears of deforestation and sediment pollution harming coral ecosystems. Although mining is still operating, growing scrutiny and backlash around nickel projects could threaten near-term supply continuity, supporting nickel prices. | NorthX Nickel Corp. announced a non-brokered subscription receipt financing to fund general working capital and to support a proposed acquisition of its Sudbury Basin nickel and base-metal assets. The escrow release depends on transaction conditions. The update is corporate/financing-focused with no direct production or pricing details, implying limited near-term nickel price effect.

The Nickel Institute comments on the EU “Competitiveness Compass,” arguing that EU regulatory inconsistencies—particularly revisions to nickel environmental quality standards proposed under the Water Framework Directive—could harm producers by constraining investment and competitiveness. It calls for streamlined permitting, reduced regulatory burden, and affordable energy to sustain EU nickel supply for batteries and the energy transition.

Reuters column citing a January base-metals analyst poll expects aluminium to lead in 2025 on a projected supply deficit, with cash prices set to rise. Copper upside is tempered by tariff-driven macro risk, especially for China demand. Nickel remains bearish on ongoing oversupply and rising stocks, limiting near-term net impact.

The article argues that rapid EV and renewable-storage growth is tightening global battery-metal supply chains. It highlights strong demand for nickel sulfate for NCM/NCA cathodes and notes irregular supply from producers such as Indonesia and the Philippines, alongside environmental and production-quote constraints. It implies continued nickel price volatility and upside bias. | The piece frames the energy transition as long-run support for copper, aluminum, and nickel and cites SMM’s January 20, 2025 price levels. It notes copper premiums weakening ahead of Chinese New Year due to softer consumption, while nickel premiums fall on abundant supply and reduced activity, implying mixed short-term pressure. | A report by C4ADS says Chinese firms control about 75% of Indonesia’s nickel refining capacity, dominated by Tsingshan and Delong. It raises concerns over supply-chain control, environmental risk, and possible disadvantages for automakers amid tighter U.S./EU trade policies. Indonesia’s response is not detailed, implying no immediate supply shock. | A New York judge allowed Trafigura to subpoena records from major banks in a nickel-related fraud case to trace where funds paid for purported nickel cargoes were transferred. The underlying claim is that worthless material was shipped instead, but the story contains no direct supply or inventory impact.

A report says Chinese firms control about 75% of Indonesia’s nickel refining capacity, with Tsingshan and Jiangsu Delong owning over 70%. Concerns focus on supply-chain resilience and possible disruptions from restrictive trade policies. Reuters also notes Chinese moves to cut stakes via partnerships and Indonesia’s push for more domestic control. | Podium Minerals completed the acquisition of EV Nickel’s Range Well nickel project, integrating it with its Parks Reef PGM project in Western Australia. The combined assets also reportedly include copper alongside nickel and cobalt. The deal involves issuing shares and a royalty to Johnson Matthey, but no near-term production impact is specified. | Fastmarkets’ technical note argues LME nickel’s bearish setup may be temporary. It describes a potential inverse head-and-shoulders pattern with a neckline near $16,000/t and suggests that only a monthly close below $14,500/t would invalidate the bullish bias. Otherwise, it expects a limited rebound/short-covering rally in coming weeks.

High-nickel pig iron (NPI) prices rose this week, with SMM 8–12% NPI averaging 944.2 RMB per nickel point and the NPI FOB index also increasing. Resumed smelter operations, limited Indonesian/Philippine output, and a Philippines raw-ore export ban strengthened sentiment, narrowing the discount to electrolytic nickel. | SMM reports Indonesian domestic trade laterite nickel ore activity rebounded post-holiday, with premiums rising (1.6 grade $17–18) amid ample supply and February restocking by pyrometallurgical plants. High-grade NPI prices strengthened, with the FOB index up WoW and improved smelter economics, supported by positive sentiment and export-ban news.

Trump announced a 25% tariff on all steel and aluminium imports to the US, likely including Australia, prompting diplomatic efforts for an exemption. The piece notes aluminium export volumes and likely competitive pressure on manufacturers. The tariff threat is a near-term negative for aluminium price expectations; copper and nickel aren’t directly addressed. | Asian markets were mixed, but LME base metals mostly rose after President Trump pledged 25% tariffs on all U.S. steel and aluminum imports. Aluminum futures gained 0.6% and copper 1.2% on the day, while nickel fell 0.5%. Analysts expected limited broader impact on emerging Asia exports. | Trump’s proposed 25% tariffs on all U.S. imports of steel and aluminium are rattling Asian producers by threatening higher U.S. import prices, disrupted trade flows, and lower volumes. Reports cite aluminium supply-tightening concerns and falling iron-ore sentiment, while copper and nickel are not directly addressed. | Trump says the US will impose a 25% tariff on steel and aluminium imports, with details expected soon. Australia’s industry urges a carve-out, citing past exemptions. Even with exemptions, the risk is weaker demand if tariffs slow China, pressuring overall industrial metal sentiment, especially aluminium. | Trump said the US will impose 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminium imports starting this week, with further “reciprocal” measures. The tariff backdrop includes potential retaliation from China and warnings that tariffs will raise US costs, likely pressuring near-term industrial demand and sentiment for copper, nickel, and aluminium. | Trump signed executive orders imposing a flat 25% tariff on all steel and aluminum imports into the US “without exceptions.” The piece also discusses possible US consideration of tariff exemptions for Australia and European warnings of retaliatory tariffs. Overall, aluminum faces direct near-term price support from higher import costs. | Donald Trump says the US will impose 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminium imports, including from Canada and Mexico, and is planning reciprocal tariffs almost immediately. The move escalates trade policy and could trigger retaliation, likely pressuring industrial metals demand expectations and prices near term. | Trump said the U.S. will impose 25% tariffs on all steel and “aluminum, too” imports, including from Canada and Mexico, with additional reciprocal duties later. Markets fell and partners warned of industrial impacts. Aluminum-focused expectations likely deteriorate near term; copper and nickel only face indirect macro/trade pressure. | Trump has signed executive orders imposing a flat 25% tariff on all US steel and aluminum imports “without exceptions or exemptions.” The news covers potential tariff exemptions for Australia, expected limited impact comments from ThyssenKrupp, and EU retaliation threats—driving near-term expectations for aluminum trade costs and prices, with only indirect effects for copper and nickel. | Trump announced executive actions raising US tariffs on aluminium and steel to 25% from 10% as China’s levies against the US take effect. Canada is the dominant source of primary aluminium imports to the US, while Mexico supplies much of the aluminium scrap and alloy. Higher tariffs likely pressure aluminium markets near term. | Trump plans to announce 25% tariffs on all imported steel and aluminum entering the U.S., including shipments from Canada and Mexico. Canada supplies major steel and is the top aluminum metal source. The move follows prior tariff threats and could tighten U.S. aluminum availability, but details remain limited. | Trump said 25% tariffs will be imposed on all steel and aluminium imports starting Monday, including shipments from Canada and Mexico. He referenced earlier 2018 tariffs and national security claims, as well as the 2019 deal that previously prevented supply surges. The new tariffs may raise costs and prices for aluminium. | Trump removed exemptions from U.S. steel tariffs so all steel imports face at least 25%, and raised 2018 aluminum tariffs to 25% from 10%. The policy change is meant to protect domestic industry and reset trade, likely increasing aluminum import costs and near-term price support. Other updates are unrelated to metal prices. | Norilsk Nickel said its 2024 IFRS net profit fell 37% to $1.8 bln as EBITDA declined and revenues dropped 13% amid weaker market prices for nickel and platinum group metals. The company reported lower free cash flow and higher net debt, while continuing investment and debt management plans. | Stocks in steel and aluminum producers jumped after President Trump said he plans 25% tariffs on imported steel and aluminum and will announce retaliatory duties on countries that impose tariffs on U.S. goods. The company noted payments/equipment access constraints, redirected sales to Asia, and faced repairs disruption at its Nadezhda smelter. It also warned it sees a nickel surplus in 2025. | Trump plans 25% tariffs on all US steel and aluminum imports, targeting China-linked trade flows and expanding an already tariff-heavy stance. The piece notes China’s overcapacity and rising exports depressing prices globally, which has triggered retaliation and further trade friction likely to pressure industrial metals pricing in the near term. | Trump signed executive orders imposing 25% tariffs on all US imports of steel and aluminum with no exceptions or exemptions. The US may also consider exemptions for Australian products. Europe and France signaled retaliatory measures. The tariff news is likely to raise near-term expectations for aluminum supply constraints in the US market. | President Trump orders 25% import duties on all steel and raises aluminium tariffs from 10% to 25%, covering about $50B of imports. Tariffs are expected to be passed through to U.S. buyers, pushing up prices for products using these metals and likely lifting nearby aluminium pricing pressure through higher import costs and substitution effects. | Trump signed executive orders imposing 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the U.S. from all countries, effective March 12, with Canada threatening “clear and calibrated” retaliation. The broad tariff scope includes downstream products and is expected to disrupt aluminum trade flows.

Trump says he will halt producing pennies. The article notes pennies have used mostly zinc since 1982, with only about 198 metric tons of copper embedded in 2024 pennies—around 0.02% of annual U.S. copper production. Any change should not meaningfully move copper prices. Nickel effects are speculative; aluminium is not mentioned. | Aluminium prices fell as investors worried about a possible global trade war after Trump imposed 25% tariffs on U.S. steel and aluminium imports. LME three-month aluminium dropped 0.6% and SHFE aluminium fell 0.5%. Industry leaders warn these duties could tighten short-term supply and raise input costs for automakers, likely supporting near-term aluminum prices, while copper and nickel impacts are not specified. | The AP reports new Trump tariffs: all steel imports face at least 25%, alongside a 25% aluminum tariff. Auto executives warn these duties will raise costs through supply-demand tightening and higher component prices. The higher-than-expected availability of nickel ore can influence market expectations for nickel supply in the coming weeks, generally creating downward pressure on nickel prices as traders adjust forecasts for future offtake and processing. | Canada and the EU retaliate against Trump’s increased steel and aluminum tariffs with reciprocal duties starting Thursday and additional EU measures from April 1 and April 13. The update is exploration-focused and can be mildly bullish for copper sentiment. | First Atlantic Nickel reported initial Davis Tube Recovery (DTR) results from drill hole AN-24-02 at its Atlantic Nickel Project, including a magnetic concentrate averaging 1.37% nickel over 383.1 meters with meaningful recoveries. The company frames this as a step toward smelter-free processing, supporting bullish nickel sentiment despite no near-term production impact. | U.S. 25% tariffs on imported steel and aluminum took effect, prompting EU, Canada and other partner backlash via retaliatory tariffs. Analysts warn higher input costs will hit U.S. downstream industries and consumers, while recession and inflation concerns add macro uncertainty. The piece focuses on steel/aluminum, with no clear copper/nickel specifics. | Indonesia’s recent nickel regulations—new HMA guidance, proposed higher/progressive royalties tied to nickel ore prices, and extended onshore export earnings—could lift production and operating costs for nickel products, especially NPI. The article notes rising LME nickel and higher China NPI ex-work prices, with HMA for late March expected higher, further pressuring costs. | The European Commission announced countermeasures against new US steel and aluminium tariffs, including letting prior 2018/2020 measures lapse and introducing new actions covering up to €26 billion of US-bound EU exports. The retaliation aims to offset tariff-driven price increases and protect EU producers and consumers.

High-nickel pig iron prices (SMM 8–12%) rose week-on-week amid tight supply, low smelter inventories, and limited capacity. Indonesian NPI FOB increased and stainless steel orders strengthened, boosting purchasing. With nickel ore expected to firm due to Philippines conditions and smelter losses easing, nickel prices are anticipated to trend higher. | SMM’s Indonesia nickel market review highlights policy-driven uncertainty and likely nickel ore royalty cost increases, expected to be passed to downstream buyers and support higher nickel ore prices. It reports an uptick in Indonesian high-grade NPI FOB prices, strong stainless procurement sentiment, tight ore supply, and expectations of continued short-term price firmness.

The paper examines the March 8, 2022 LME nickel market shock, when nickel prices nearly quadrupled in three days and threatened clearing defaults. The LME cancelled hours of nickel trades to ease distress, and UK courts upheld the authority. Losses occurred, but the event reduced clearing stress rather than signaling new fundamentals. | Homeland Nickel describes its consolidated portfolio of nine at-surface nickel laterite projects in Southern Oregon, highlighting historic resources and low-cost exploration potential. The company also notes partnerships to advance development while managing dilution. The update is nickel-specific, but no near-term output changes are cited.

The Philippines, a major nickel producer and exporter, is pitching nickel supply and deeper value-chain participation to India amid efforts to diversify away from Chinese buyers. India is also ramping up battery/EV production, creating potential new offtake channels. The planned preferential trade talks could gradually support nickel demand. | Miners and the Indonesian Mining Association urged the government to drop a planned increase in mining royalties, citing a sharp decline in tax revenue and rising operational costs. The rule would raise royalty rates for products including nickel, alongside VAT and export-receipt onshoring requirements. | Greater Sudbury’s mayor argues for building nickel sulfate processing capacity in Ontario to secure critical-mineral supply for U.S. defense and technology needs. He cites Ontario’s large share of U.S. nickel use and calls for midstream processing and battery-material production, which is directly tied to nickel demand and supply expectations.

Floods hit parts of Indonesia’s Morowali nickel processing industrial park, but the operator says operations were normal and plants were not halted. Heavy rain on Monday flooded areas under construction, yet work resumed. The complex produces about a fifth of Indonesia’s nickel pig iron; no supply disruption was reported. | SMM announced the launch of six additional weekly benchmark price points for Indonesian laterite nickel ore (1.3% to 1.5% grades, FOB and CIF), starting March 28, with prices maintained every Friday. The update improves quotation coverage but does not signal changes in nickel fundamentals or demand/supply conditions. | The Philippines is reportedly reassessing its heavy dependence on China for nickel sales, with China taking nearly 98% of imports. Analysts say this concentration is risky, prompting a push to broaden customer sources such as India. Diversifying export demand can affect short-term nickel market expectations. | Namibia–Zambia trade via the Walvis Bay–Ndola–Lubumbashi corridor rose 48% in 2024. Zambia exports copper and nickel (among other materials) through Walvis Bay, and stakeholders highlighted faster, cheaper logistics and growth of port/dry-port infrastructure to expand copperbelt export capacity, supporting short-term price sentiment.

The article argues Morowali’s economy depends heavily on Indonesia’s nickel smelters and their output of ferronickel/NPI shipped to China. It says PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry may face bankruptcy as its Chinese parent restructures debt, creating uncertainty over the Morowali processing plant’s future and supply outlook. | DRC introduces cobalt export quotas/processing push, mainly affecting cobalt but indirectly linked to copper supply via by-product dynamics. Copper meanwhile jumps to its highest level in over five months on LME as US tariff investigations spur early buying and widen Comex premiums. Faster US imports tighten other-market supply; Indonesia raises nickel-matte royalties. | Indonesia’s ESDM signals new PNBP/royalty rules before Eid, shifting nickel royalties from a 10% fixed rate to a variable 14%–19% based on HMA prices. The change is expected to raise nickel mining costs and nickel ore procurement costs for smelters, supporting higher nickel ore prices; copper may also face higher royalties. | The UK FCA fined the LME GBP9.2 million for inadequate systems and controls during an extreme nickel price spike in March 2022, when nickel more than doubled in little over an hour. The LME later cancelled trades and halted trading, and the FCA says the incident management was insufficient, though both parties claim risks were reduced since. | The FCA fined the LME £9.2m for inadequate systems during a March 2022 nickel spike that reached over $100,000/ton before trading was suspended and trades were cancelled. The FCA cited failures to escalate severe volatility. The LME says controls have since been strengthened, reducing recurrence risk. | Britain’s FCA fined the London Metal Exchange £9.2 million for handling failures during the March 2022 nickel crisis, when nickel prices surged beyond $100,000/t and trades were voided. The LME accepted findings and says new controls are in place. Nickel volumes later recovered, limiting immediate market disruption. | Britain’s financial regulator fined the LME about £9.2 million for its handling of the 2022 nickel crisis, saying the exchange failed to ensure its systems were adequately equipped. In March 2022, the LME halted nickel trading and canceled trades due to extreme pricing volatility. | The FCA fined the London Metal Exchange £9.2m for inadequate systems and controls during the March 2022 nickel market disruption, when Russia’s invasion of Ukraine triggered a rapid nickel price surge. The regulator found junior staffing and disabled price bands worsened the speed of the move, leading to investor risk exposure. | The UK FCA imposed a £9.2m fine on the London Metal Exchange over disorderly “nickel market chaos.” The regulatory action centers on how nickel trading was handled, raising near-term concerns about market functioning and price discovery that can weigh on nickel sentiment and liquidity.

SMM’s Indonesian market review says nickel ore supply remains tight as rainy season disruptions in Sulawesi/Philippines delay recovery, and NPI smelter inventories are low. Policy signals about earlier PNBP implementation may increase mine sales costs but be passed to downstream buyers, supporting prices. High-grade NPI averages edged up and refined nickel ramp-up continues. | High-grade nickel pig iron prices rose this week (SMM 8–12% up to 1016.5 yuan per nickel point; Indonesian NPI FOB up). Tight nickel ore/transport constraints, low smelter inventories, and improving smelter economics support higher pricing, even with weaker stainless steel demand. Overall nickel price downside is seen as limited. | The piece revisits the March 2022 LME nickel crisis, arguing regulators and the exchange lacked visibility into large OTC positions, especially linked to Tsingshan. It notes OTC and exchange interaction drove price volatility, alongside trade cancellations and legal actions. It concludes no current evidence of wrongdoing or new immediate market change.

Reuters reports hedge fund Elliott is looking at additional action against the LME after trades in nickel were cancelled. While details are limited in the excerpt, the dispute centers on nickel market operations at the exchange, which can raise near-term uncertainty and pressure nickel pricing. | Elliott says it may take additional steps regarding LME-voided nickel trades. The claim points to ongoing legal/market-recovery uncertainty after regulators voided transactions, which can weigh on nickel sentiment and liquidity in the immediate term as traders reassess risks and exposures.

Elliott Associates is considering further action against the LME after the FCA fined it about £9.2m for failing to handle severe nickel market stress during the March 2022 spike that saw trades cancelled. The FCA cited inadequate preparedness and staff training, while courts previously found LME acted lawfully. | The article (a Reuters member piece) states that the LME was fined for failing to act quickly during a nickel crisis. While this is clearly connected to nickel market disruption and oversight, the excerpt provides no specifics on follow-on measures or supply/demand changes that would drive a near-term directional price impact. | Indonesia’s EV push is threatened by regulatory complexity and ESG/safety concerns in nickel mining and processing. The piece cites investor withdrawals (e.g., BASF, Eramet) and safety incidents at nickel smelters, potentially reducing appetite for Indonesian nickel processing capacity. It also discusses looming tariff uncertainty affecting EV investments and downstream demand. | Brazilian Nickel’s CEO says the company will use heap leaching on its Piauí nickel laterite project, aiming for lower capex/opex than HPAL and stronger participation outside the China–Indonesia supply chain. The interview also highlights potential nickel deficits later this decade, but current news is mainly financing/offtake progress. | President Trump’s March 20 executive order expands U.S. support for domestic critical mineral mining using Defense Production Act financing, expedited permitting, and buyer/offtake facilitation. The order adds copper to the critical mineral list and AEMC links it to its nickel-dominant Nikolai project, improving near-term investment expectations for nickel and copper.

A sludge collapse at a Chinese-funded nickel plant in Indonesia has reportedly killed one person and left two missing. The accident is likely to disrupt operations at the nickel facility, increasing short-term supply uncertainty and potentially pressuring nickel prices lower over the coming days to weeks. | The article title reports a sludge collapse that killed one worker at a Chinese-funded nickel plant in Indonesia. Such an incident can halt or delay operations and maintenance, reducing near-term nickel output. That supply disruption risk is bearish for nickel prices over the coming days and weeks.

First Atlantic Nickel announced assays from drill hole AN-24-03 at its RPM Zone, reporting an average 0.25% nickel over 216 meters (including 0.27% nickel in the final 21 meters) with visible awaruite mineralization. The company plans higher-powered Phase 2 drilling to test deeper/westward extensions, implying favorable nickel potential. | Pan Global Resources reported underground channel samples at the Profunda target in Spain’s Cármenes project, including 5.2% copper over 5m and other high-grade Cu intervals. Nickel is also present in several samples (up to 0.53% Ni over 1m), supporting bullish sentiment for copper and some nickel. | Class 1 Nickel reported an updated NI 43-101 mineral resource estimate for the Dundonald North nickel sulphide deposit, citing higher tonnage and contained nickel, plus increases in contained copper and cobalt. The update is based on historical drilling and remains inferred/out-of-pit, with no near-term production timeline.

SMM launched new weekly price points for Indonesia local laterite nickel ore (1.3%, 1.4%, 1.5%), with values published every Friday. This week, multiple nickel ore grades increased WoW, including 1.4% and 1.5% ex-factory and FOB assessments, suggesting firmer nickel ore pricing. | SMM reports continued tight Indonesian nickel ore supply, with pyrometallurgical ore prices and April premiums expected to rise, aided by low smelter inventories and strong PNBP policy expectations that lift miners’ costs. Rainy-season disruptions and flooding constrain supply. NPI remains supported by higher costs, while refined nickel projects ramp up gradually. | China’s GEM becomes the majority owner of Bahodopi Nickel Smelting Indonesia (BNSI) via a Rp 1 trillion share subscription, diluting PT Vale Indonesia to 49%. BNSI develops a nickel processing facility in Morowali. The report says operations and business continuity won’t be affected. | High-nickel pig iron prices (SMM8-12%) rose WoW, supported by cost pressures from higher nickel ore and improved cost support amid tight feedstock availability. Indonesian flooding in Morowali is expected to increase pure nickel costs. The piece expects continued strong NPI and upward nickel price correction. | Fastmarkets reports alumina prices fell in 2025 amid excess supply and weaker demand, while bauxite remains influenced by Guinea supply security. It also notes a US move to impose 25% tariffs on imported aluminium, lifting domestic sentiment and aluminium premiums. Market participants watch further tariff signals and trade-flow impacts.

South Pacific Metals reports successful completion of a Papua New Guinea warden hearing renewing exploration licence EL 2310 for its Kili Teke Cu-Au project and plans to begin on-ground exploration. The update is copper-linked via project development, but no production or supply change is announced, limiting near-term price impact.

Bellezza claims a patented “fusion” process could enable graphene interconnects in CMOS chips at low temperatures, potentially replacing copper circuits as copper interconnects reach size limits. The method uses iron/nickel plating substrates treated to form martensite crystals. The article provides technology claims but no market-scale deployment. | 1844 Resources announced an extension of the outside date on its option agreement with Nickel North for the Hawk Ridge nickel/copper project, citing delays in preparing an updated geological report. The unit private placement details are also provided, subject to exchange approvals. No production or supply changes are described.

High-nickel pig iron prices edged up to 1030.25 yuan per nickel point, supported by low smelter inventories and cost support. However, weak downstream stainless demand and rising raw material costs reduce procurement willingness. Indonesia policy delays and a stronger USD pressure pure nickel sentiment. Next week discounts may widen, implying bearish risk for nickel. | MINE’s April 2025 issue reviews Indonesia’s rapid nickel growth and highlights upcoming challenges, including decreasing production quotas and plans to raise royalties. These policy shifts could reduce near-term nickel supply availability and influence market expectations, potentially providing upward support for nickel prices. No similar copper or aluminium signals are discussed.

A Singapore venture capitalist testified that he invested millions into an alleged nickel scheme run by Ng Yu Zhi after checking nickel prices and being assured returns. Prosecutors say there was no real physical nickel trading; investor funds paid earlier investors. The fraud unraveled in early 2021, damaging trust and nickel-related trading confidence. | Indonesia dominates global nickel supply, with rising production over 2018–2024 and major Chinese-linked investment. Despite a recent nickel price decline linked to faster supply growth and slower EV demand, the government is considering production quota cuts (and royalty increases), which could reduce supply and lift prices. Mentions recent support from tightening expectations.

7NEWS Spotlight reports that Indonesia’s nickel mines are unsafe and heavily polluting, funded by Chinese capital, and accuses them of harming Australia’s nickel industry. The debate follows over EV-driven nickel demand and Australia’s ‘Future Made in Australia’ nickel tax credits, with calls to reconsider Indonesia arrangements and improve standards. | SMM reports US tariff policy has driven nickel prices down, and delayed Indonesia’s PNBP-related royalty announcements. With weaker downstream nickel product prices, support for Indonesia’s April laterite nickel ore premiums is weakening. SMM expects mainstream April premiums of $24-25/wmt may fall in late April as renegotiations begin after Lebaran.

Avebury Nickel Mine’s boss says cheap Indonesian “dirty nickel” crushed nickel prices and shuttered Australian production. He urges policy changes and LME certification to separate “clean” and “uncertified” nickel for EV batteries and other buyers, which could revive higher-quality nickel demand and lift prices.

Rio Tinto says Trump’s administration could help unblock its Resolution Copper project in Arizona, citing plans to speed permitting and boost critical-mineral output. The article highlights potential upside for U.S. copper supply and notes another U.S. project (Twin Metals) that includes nickel, though details are secondary. | The EU member states approved retaliatory tariffs worth $23 billion in response to Trump’s 25% tariffs on imported US steel and aluminium. Retaliation will enter in stages starting April 15, May 15, and December 1. The EU prefers a negotiated “zero-for-zero” deal, but talks remain uncertain. | Nickel prices have fallen sharply to levels last seen since 2020, driven by reciprocal tariff announcements that spooked growth expectations and triggered a broad sell-off in base metals. The article warns that renewed recession fears could add volatility. It also notes lower nickel and copper would reduce the 304 stainless surcharge. | Canada’s Conference Board warns Sudbury’s economy is under pressure as nickel prices remain weak. It cites Indonesia-led oversupply, demand uncertainty around electric cars, and nickel-displacing lithium-iron-phosphate batteries. Vale’s layoffs and reduced output reflect soft nickel markets, with only slight price improvement expected.

First Atlantic Nickel closed a C$1.07M charity flow-through private placement to fund Phase 2 drilling at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland. The company highlights its nickel-iron alloy (awaruite) and proceeds timing/renunciation mechanics, but the financing itself is unlikely to materially move global nickel prices immediately. | New Caledonia’s Koniambo nickel mine and factory shut down in August after Glencore exited the operation, with smelters turned off last year. The closure halted production of high-grade nickel (up to ~60,000 tonnes/year) and leaves thousands unemployed. Buyers may not emerge soon, extending the supply shock.

High-nickel pig iron prices (SMM 8-12%) fell RMB 19.35 per nickel point week-on-week to RMB 1,010.9, the largest decline this year. The drop is linked to weaker stainless-steel demand on macro concerns from reciprocal tariffs, cautious procurement, and expectations of continued nickel price pressure despite some supply and cost factors. | SMM’s Indonesian nickel market review says global nickel prices fell sharply on renewed Trump tariff policy and PNBP expectations, dragging down high-grade NPI prices and pressuring profits. Indonesian nickel ore premiums are stable due to tight supply, but demand weakens. Refined nickel supply also continues ramp-up.

CNBC discusses whether Trump’s reciprocal tariffs could trigger a commodities “super-cycle.” It cites higher copper front-month prices but warns tariffs may raise slowdown risks that pressure copper short term. It says nickel is unlikely to surge, while aluminium is among materials expected to be repriced upward as trade tensions persist. | Ardea Resources completed 287 infill reverse circulation drill holes at its Goongarrie Hub nickel project in Western Australia, reporting multiple high-grade nickel intercepts at Big Four and Scotia Dam. Management says results support potential resource upgrades and pit optimization work in its ongoing definitive feasibility study, which can strengthen near-term nickel sentiment. | The piece outlines U.S. reinstatement of full tariffs on steel (25%) and aluminum (25%) with a higher China rate and a 90-day pause that excludes steel and aluminum. It emphasizes continued elevated tariff burdens until at least July 9, 2025, likely weighing on aluminum-linked demand and prices near term.

Clean Energy Transition Inc. filed an NI 43-101 Technical Report supporting an updated Mineral Resource Estimate for its Aurora Nickel Project in Ontario. The Indicated Resource totals 10.06 Mt at 0.42% nickel, with open-pit and underground deposits. The company frames the update as strengthening the low-carbon nickel opportunity. | Talon Metals said drill hole 25TK0561 at its Tamarack nickel-copper project intersected 16.09 meters of mixed massive sulphide with 10%–80% sulphide content, potentially extending mineralization at depth. Management links the result to earlier BHEM modeling success and suggests potential tonnage addition as feasibility work progresses. | Talon Metals announced a mixed massive sulphide intercept (16.09m) at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project, with sulphide content indicating higher nickel grades. The company frames results as extending the CGO East Zone below the current resource and supporting potential early tonnage, but it remains feasibility-stage exploration news. | Kincora Copper announced an expanded earn-in/JV with AngloGold Ashanti to broaden drilling across the Nyngan, Nevertire and Nevertire South projects in NSW, targeting copper-gold porphyry systems. The update notes ongoing scout drilling and prior anomalous copper results, but remains exploration-focused with no production or logistics changes. | Bloomberg reports a production halt and investigation around Indonesia’s Morowali nickel hub after a landslide at a tailings area tied to PT QMB New Energy. Fatalities occurred, traders expect reduced output, and nearby producers cut production. Indonesia’s large share of global nickel supply raises immediate bullish pricing pressure.

The piece describes repeated deadly incidents and alleged systematic safety shortcomings at Tsingshan’s IMIP nickel processing and stainless steel facilities in Indonesia, including a December 2023 nickel-plant explosion and another fatal smelter accident in February 2025. Despite production resuming quickly after one death, the accidents heighten perceived supply-risk and oversight concerns.

Indonesia’s nickel smelters association asked the government to delay higher mineral royalty fees until LME nickel prices reach $17,000/ton, above current levels (~$15,600). The new royalties (14%–19% on ore; higher rates on nickel pig iron and matte) would raise costs amid weakening nickel prices. | Indonesia plans higher nickel royalties, replacing a flat 10% levy on nickel ore with a variable rate of 14%-19% linked to government-set price levels. Lower-grade ore for battery-grade nickel faces a reduced 2% royalty. The change takes effect shortly, while miners warn of margin strain and output cuts. | Realtor.com reports that Trump’s 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum imports could raise construction costs for newly built residences. Builders may pass higher costs to consumers or delay finishing construction, reducing housing supply. This could push rents higher in multiple US metros as tariffs dampen development plans.

Indonesia dominates global nickel output and is pushing downstream processing after a raw ore export ban, building smelters and HPAL capacity. Nickel’s role in EV cathodes underpins demand growth from automakers. However, expanded refining could also increase refined supply, making the net near-term price effect uncertain. | Copper rose to a two-week high as a weaker U.S. dollar encouraged CTA/fund buying and traders covered shorts, breaching key moving averages. However, sentiment remains cautious because Trump’s import tariffs threaten growth and industrial metal demand. Aluminium and nickel were also up, but driven largely by macro flows. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. said Phase 1 construction is complete on an all-season restricted access road to its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project, replacing floatplane/ATV logistics with year-round road access. The company is starting Phase 2 and plans further upgrades and 2025 next steps to expand resources, potentially improving future supply prospects. | Glenstar Minerals announced it has hired Midnight Sun Drilling to conduct the first-ever drill program at its Green Monster nickel-copper-cobalt property in Nevada. The company plans to define the extent of mineralization after permitting and expects updates soon, citing earlier sampling with meaningful Cu and Ni values. | Glenstar Minerals hired Midnight Sun Drilling to run the first-ever drill program on its Green Monster nickel-copper-cobalt property in Nevada. The project is undrilled to date and targets shallow RC drilling based on prior surface sampling that included copper and nickel grades. Results are expected in coming days. | Alaska Energy Metals filed an updated NI 43-101 technical report for its Eureka deposit at the Nikolai nickel project in Alaska, raising tonnage, metal content, and nickel-equivalent grades versus the prior 2024 estimate. The company links the update to U.S. critical-mineral supply-chain efforts, but no near-term production changes are stated.

Talon Metals said feasibility study drilling at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project found massive sulphide mineralization extending laterally east of the prior southern resource boundary, including a 1.9m intercept logged at ~70% sulphides. The release continues drilling in Minnesota and references planned rigs in Michigan.

Vedanta is exploring a US IPO for its 80% owned Konkola Copper Mines in Zambia to raise about $1bn for mine development. The plan follows regained control after provisional liquidation and financing to restart operations, with production targets rising. More funding prospects can lift short-term copper supply expectations.

SMM’s weekly review finds Indonesian nickel ore premiums stable-to-slightly higher for pyrometallurgy, but hydrometallurgical ore prices may soften after a hydromet project accident. High-grade NPI FOB prices fell WoW, with sluggish downstream stainless demand and deeper smelter losses. Nickel ore supply stays tight, but NPI remains pressured. | High-nickel pig iron (8–12% NPI) prices fell further this week in China and Indonesia, with smelters showing low raw-material buying due to shrinking profits. Weak stainless steel demand, influenced by reciprocal tariffs, is expected to keep pressure on NPI. The discount to electrolytic nickel widened, signaling softer nickel demand.

Beijing-driven LFP dominance in Indonesia is eroding demand for nickel-based EV batteries. The article notes that about 90% of last year’s 43,000 EVs used nickel-free LFP chemistries, largely from Chinese automakers, leading LG Energy Solution to withdraw from a major Indonesia battery investment, signaling weaker nickel demand prospects. | The piece highlights a rebound case for commodities tied to infrastructure spending and electrification, claiming electrification will require sharply higher copper production and that materials demand will rise with supply-chain realignment. It also mentions nickel as part of electrification-related minerals and suggests pro-growth policies will support commodity prices. | Nickel 28 reported Q1 2025 Ramu operations: contained nickel in MHP fell to 6,970 tonnes and nickel sales to 6,133 tonnes, driven by production curtailment from an acid plant blower fan failure. Unit costs rose to $3.61/lb. It notes lower Q1 LME average nickel price at $7.06/lb.

Indonesia implemented new non-tax royalty (PNBP) rates for nickel products effective 26 April. Rates vary by HMA-based nickel reference price for ore and are set for NPI, ferronickel, matte, and newly introduced MHP. The change is expected to raise longer-term costs but likely has limited immediate price impact.

Power Metallic reported drill results from its Lion Zone at the Nisk polymetallic project, including copper-dominant massive sulphides and assays containing nickel. While the high-grade results may improve investor sentiment for future Cu/Ni resource potential, the article does not imply immediate production changes, so near-term price impact is likely limited. | DW reports leaked internal documents alleging systematic chromium-6 contamination from Harita Group’s Obi Island nickel mining and smelting complex, including claims made during the 2023 IPO due diligence. Residents lack clean water. IRMA is reviewing the site for certification, and legal scrutiny may follow, but no immediate supply cut is stated. | High-nickel pig iron prices in China fell again, though the weekly decline slowed, amid continued weak finished-product demand and cautious stainless steel buyers. Pure nickel also faced pressure from tariff/macro uncertainty and a surplus supply-demand balance, with downstream nickel plate transactions weak. Cost support limits downside somewhat. | First Atlantic Nickel says early drilling at Newfoundland ophiolite projects returned strong results, including a new “RPM zone” discovery, with metallurgical recovery testing underway. The company highlights processing awaruite nickel without smelting (lower energy, no sulfur removal). It also explores geologic hydrogen tied to the deposits, funded by researchers and U.S. support. | Glencore reported first-quarter copper output down 30% to 167,900 tonnes, despite keeping full-year 2025 production guidance unchanged. Nickel production fell 21% as well. The company cited tariff-driven uncertainty and potential trade-flow dislocation, while analysts reacted to the weaker quarterly physical supply.

Tartisan Nickel Corp. says it has started a 4-week airborne geophysical survey and a greenfields exploration program at its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project in northwestern Ontario. The work aims to refine subsurface targets for future prospecting, sampling, and drilling. No production or supply change is announced.

NewRange Copper Nickel announced the NorthMet project was designated a “Transparency Project” by a federal council aimed at expanding domestic critical-mineral supply. The move signals prioritized permitting progress for a major undeveloped copper and nickel resource. The company reports most state permits secured and plans to update Section 404 filings. | NewRange Copper Nickel said the federal NEDC designated its NorthMet project as a “Transparency Project,” prioritizing permitting for critical minerals. The firm holds most required permits and plans to update its Clean Water Act Section 404 application. The designation is intended to speed development of domestic copper and nickel supply with environmental safeguards.

Vale says it is evaluating strategic options for some nickel assets, including potential divestment or placing operations in care and maintenance, due to low nickel prices and short-term oversupply from Indonesia. The company is also exploring partnerships for Sudbury facilities to cut costs and maintain competitiveness.

Ivanhoe Mines said crews have entered the Platreef Flatreef orebody underground for the first time, with the first blast on May 7, 2025. The polymetallic deposit includes high-grade nickel and copper, and ore is being prepared for Phase 1 concentrator feed in Q4 2025. | Ivanhoe Mines said crews have entered the high-grade Flatreef orebody for the first time and completed initial underground development, including the first blast of ore on May 7, 2025. The polymetallic deposit includes nickel and copper as payable by-products, with stored ore ahead of Phase 1 concentrator feed in Q4 2025. | Nickel Asia Corp.’s Q1 profit rose sharply, supported by higher saprolite and limonite ore prices. Realized nickel ore sales prices increased year on year, and limonite pricing is linked to the London Metal Exchange. The company also sold part of its CBNC stake, while March deliveries were impacted by HPAL maintenance. | Green Bridge Metals reports a non-binding LOI to option the Serpentine copper-nickel sulphide project in Minnesota, with planned future option funding and continued drilling/permitting progress. The company highlights strong historic recoveries and infrastructure access. However, the LOI is not definitive and implies no immediate production impact for copper or nickel. | Green Bridge Metals entered a non-binding LOI to option the Serpentine copper-nickel sulfide project in Minnesota. The release links the plan to a U.S. executive order aimed at boosting American mineral production and cites permitting progress. It supports bullish sentiment for copper and nickel, though impact is likely limited near-term.

Canada Nickel closed a US$20 million bridge loan to fund the Crawford nickel sulphide project and repay part of its Auramet loan, plus a corporate update on acquiring additional Timmins-area claims. The financing reduces near-term project funding risk, which is generally supportive for expectations around nickel supply.

The US partially reversed “Liberation Day” tariffs on selected critical minerals, but Indonesia signals it could use critical minerals as leverage, citing its large nickel reserves (34% of global). The risk of non-tariff restrictions/export limits is highlighted, which would likely tighten near-term nickel supply expectations. | Green Bridge Metals announces the acquisition of the Copper–Nickel–PGE Serpentine project in Minnesota, USA. The deal is a mining-stage development move that may influence longer-term supply expectations for copper and nickel, but the article provides no near-term operational changes that would likely drive spot price shifts. | Green Bridge Metals says it has acquired the Copper-Nickel-PGE serpentine project in Minnesota. The deal links the company to copper and nickel resources, potentially shaping longer-term supply expectations, but there is no immediate production, permitting, or shipping change cited that would likely move near-term copper or nickel prices.

Glenstar Minerals says it will begin a 10–14 day reverse-circulation drilling program at its Green Monster Ni-Cu-Co property in Nevada. The target is within a district known for copper and also nickel anomalies. Sampling has shown high Cu and Ni grades, but the release contains no new assay results. | Glenstar Minerals says drilling will start May 14 on its Green Monster Ni-Cu-Co property in Nevada, using a reverse-circulation rig over 10–14 days with five drill holes. Prior sampling reported elevated copper and nickel values, which may modestly support sentiment for copper and nickel.

The piece says the US partially lifted tariffs on some critical minerals, while Indonesia (with 34% of global nickel reserves) may use nickel as bargaining leverage. It discusses possible policy concessions versus a continued ore export ban, implying near-term supply and market-access uncertainty for nickel. | Nickel’s decline is cited as the strongest driver behind falling 304 stainless steel surcharges, pulling down the Stainless Monthly Metals Index and pressuring service-center selling prices. Mills kept discounts high and struggled to raise prices as Asia remained the price floor and nickel-linked surcharge levels continued to fall.

Eurobattery Minerals’ subsidiary Finncobalt signed a non-binding letter of intent with Terrafame to refine nickel (and cobalt) from its Hautalampi project, with all production processed at Terrafame’s facilities. Terrafame owns Europe’s largest nickel mine. The framework may support expected nickel demand/offtake, though not binding. | ShFE is considering opening its domestic nickel futures contract to foreign investors under China’s QFII programme this year, rather than creating a separate INE contract. The plan aims to boost global accessibility and provide an alternative to LME nickel after the 2022 trading suspension, pending CSRC approval.

Reuters reports Europe’s large energy storage buildout is using lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries, accelerating a shift away from nickel-containing chemistries. CRU data show nickel battery-use intensity has fallen sharply, and analysts expect LFP dominance to further weigh on nickel prices, with oversupply already depressing benchmarks. | The IEA warns that critical-mineral supply chains are fragile due to export restrictions and geopolitical concentration, with copper facing particular stress as demand for electricity-network expansion rises. It projects worsening fundamentals for copper and highlights supplier concentration for nickel (notably Indonesia), supporting expectations of higher prices.

North Maluku police arrested 11 residents after protests against a nickel mining company in East Halmahera. Authorities said protesters carried sharp weapons, seized keys to mining vehicles, and obstructed legal operations. Protesters alleged environmental damage and forest clearing. Charges include threats, extortion, and obstruction, potentially disrupting nickel mining activity.

Indonesia’s nickel miners association says the industry faces major hurdles replacing coal with alternative energy sources despite global pressure for greener operations. The shift to new power plants is difficult in ore-rich regions lacking clean energy infrastructure. With growing processing capacity, uncertainty over coal transition may constrain nickel output or raise costs. | The European Commission’s steel & metals action plan is discussed through a nickel-industry lens. The article warns that overly stringent environmental rules (EQS/REACH), energy price disadvantages, and policy design could undermine nickel project permitting and competitiveness, while supporting state aid and circularity. Net near-term price impact is uncertain.

Indonesia’s Danantara Indonesia, INA, and Eramet signed an MoU to explore an investment platform for nickel, targeting an integrated EV battery raw-materials ecosystem from upstream to downstream. The parties will conduct preliminary assessments and prepare a collaboration roadmap, with Eramet contributing technical expertise and financing structures. | Lundin Mining and BHP’s Vicuña joint venture announced a major greenfield discovery at the Filo del Sol project in Argentina, estimating at least 13 million tons of copper plus sizable gold and silver resources, with expansion potential. While environmental concerns exist, the headline primarily supports copper supply expectations. | Glenstar Minerals reports completion of an initial reverse-circulation drill program on its Green Monster Ni-Cu-Cobalt property in Nevada under budget. Drilling found oxide and sulfide mineralization and dolomite veining hosting fine-grained sulfides across targets. Samples are being shipped for assay, with no new results released yet. | Glenstar Minerals reports completion of its initial reverse-circulation drill program on the Green Monster property in Nevada. Drilling intersected oxide and sulfide mineralized sequences and dolomite veining with local sulfide mineralization, targeting nickel-copper and anomalous cobalt. Assay results from these new samples were not yet provided. | Rumors that Indonesia may ease RKAB (mine quota) approvals have made nickel market sentiment volatile. Even so, ESDM officials reported no major supplemental quota approvals in two weeks, and Indonesian nickel ore prices remained firm. Weather disruptions and weak NPI economics cap upside for fire-grade ore, while hydromet demand improved after April outages. | The headline says a North Dakota coal mine will become a processing site for Minnesota nickel. That suggests changes to nickel processing operations that could affect near-term supply and refining economics. The article text is largely missing, so the magnitude and timing of any nickel price impact are uncertain.

A retired North Dakota coal mine is being repurposed into a nickel processing plant. The development suggests upcoming additional nickel processing capacity, which could gradually improve supply availability and weigh on nickel price expectations over the coming weeks, depending on commissioning timeline and throughput. | North Dakota’s Beulah will host a processing facility operated by Talon Metals to process Minnesota-mined nickel ore, shipped by rail to the site. Tesla is contracted to buy roughly half the processed nickel, with Defense Department use also expected. The project supports U.S. critical-mineral sourcing away from China. | Eramet and Indonesia’s Danantara/Indonesia Investment Authority signed an MoU to explore investments across the nickel supply chain, from upstream mining to downstream processing for EV battery materials. The deal, framed as part of a broader France-Indonesia economic push, centers on Indonesia’s large nickel reserves and production potential. | Lifezone Metals announced filing dates for technical report summaries for the Kabanga Nickel Project in Tanzania, including an Initial Assessment filing before market open June 2 and a Feasibility Study in July. The studies cover staged development and hydrometallurgical refinery plans, including the project’s first mineral reserves disclosure. | Talon Metals, owner of a taconite mine in Minnesota, plans to build the Beulah Minerals Processing Facility in North Dakota with Westmoreland Coal. The plant is designed to process nickel and copper minerals, using local energy and fly ash byproducts. Start is planned for 2027, with operations by 2029. | Talon Metals plans to build the Beulah Minerals Processing Facility on a former coal site in North Dakota, partnering with Westmoreland Coal. The project is described as advanced processing for nickel and copper minerals, using energy, rail transport, and coal fly ash. Construction is targeted for 2027, startup 2029.

Brazil’s ONS denied Vale’s request to increase power supply to its northern Onca Puma nickel complex after Vale missed a deadline to contract additional electricity. The second furnace for the $555 million expansion is still targeted for the second half of 2025, but approval uncertainty raises near-term nickel supply/ramp risks.

Danantara Indonesia, INA, and Eramet signed an MoU in Jakarta to explore an integrated nickel investment platform in Indonesia, spanning upstream mining to downstream EV battery raw materials. The partners will assess suitable projects and roadmap financing, aiming to strengthen Indonesia’s nickel supply chain for energy-transition demand. | First Atlantic Nickel says it will attend a Benchmark Mineral Intelligence conference and promote its Atlantic Nickel awaruite project, claiming low-energy, smelter-free processing and improved compliance with U.S. IRA FEOC rules. It also reports ongoing Phase 2 drilling and acquisition of additional licenses. The news is supportive but not production-impacting. | Lifezone Metals said it has released an initial assessment for its Kabanga nickel project in northwest Tanzania. The company expects to complete a feasibility-study technical report summary in July 2025. Results are preliminary and include resources not yet converted to reserves, with no certainty the economics will materialize. | Vale’s nickel output expansion at Brazil’s Onca Puma complex hit a hurdle after power-grid operator ONS declined Vale’s request to raise electricity consumption needed for a new furnace. Missing contract deadlines left power allocated elsewhere, potentially delaying added capacity of 15,200 tonnes annually and pressuring near-term nickel supply expectations. | Indonesia launched its first national ESG forum for the nickel industry, bringing together miners, policymakers and investors to develop locally tailored sustainability standards. With the EU tightening rules, the initiative aims to secure responsible supply chains for battery and clean-energy demand. Government backing suggests ESG integration into policy soon. | First Atlantic Nickel announces participation in Benchmark Mineral Intelligence’s GIGA USA conference, highlighting its Atlantic Nickel Project where awaruite (~75% nickel) can be concentrated via magnetic separation and flotation without overseas smelting. The company argues this supports North American nickel supply under U.S. IRA FEOC requirements, boosting nickel sentiment. | Trump’s planned policy doubles tariffs on imported steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% effective Wednesday. The article says higher costs for U.S. manufacturers and can/bottle users will be passed through, raising prices in sectors like autos, appliances, and canned foods. Aluminium demand-cost effects are most direct.

Benton Resources reported an early drill intercept of gold at its Great Burnt Copper-Gold project and noted visible chalcopyrite, with forthcoming base-metal assays. It also describes further drilling and EM surveys that expand targeting, with drilling paused for results. No production changes are announced; nickel is only referenced via a JV interest. | The article says Trump’s plan to double tariffs on steel and aluminum to 50% could lift grocery prices by increasing costs for can and packaging materials, including imported tinplate steel used for food cans. It also notes potential substitution from aluminum to plastic and higher logistics costs, pressuring retailer margins and consumer prices. | Talon Metals announced new developments for its Tamarack nickel-copper project in North Dakota, including an agreement giving it rights to buy the Beulah Mine for processing and recent higher-grade intercept results. The company updated its “mine in a box” design to address water and dust concerns and aims to begin the EIS process soon.

Trump’s executive order has put a 50% tariff on foreign steel and aluminum imports into effect in the US, with most partners except the UK. The move is aimed at countering low-priced, excess imports, notably from China, and is expected to burden exporters like Canada and Mexico immediately. | AFP reports that Indonesia’s Weda Bay nickel concession is encroaching on Indigenous forest lands, triggering protests, arrests, and increasing calls for protection measures. With Eramet seeking review of engagement protocols and government acknowledging isolated tribes, risks of operational or expansion delays could weigh on nickel sentiment. | Trump’s administration raised US steel and aluminium import tariffs, doubling them to as much as 50% effective Wednesday (UK imports exempted at 25% due to a trade deal). The move is expected to raise costs for US manufacturers and consumers and may support aluminium price expectations near term, with potential retaliatory risk. | Trump will raise US tariffs on nearly all imported steel and aluminium to 50% from Wednesday, doubling the current 25% rate (except for the UK). The policy is meant to protect domestic industry and counter excess foreign supply, likely lifting aluminium input costs and prices in the near term. | The article says US tariffs on steel and aluminum imports doubled to 50% and are likely to push up costs for construction, vehicles, appliances and cans. Aluminum prices had risen earlier, and the Aluminum Association warns higher tariffs could reduce Canadian raw-aluminum supply to US refiners, tightening availability and supporting higher aluminum prices. | Trump doubled US tariffs on imported steel and aluminum to 50% from 25%, citing national security and oversupply. The change threatens higher input costs for US manufacturers and drew retaliation threats from Canada and Mexico. The piece notes aluminum smelting’s electricity cost and Canada’s export impact, but no direct copper/nickel linkage. | The U.S. doubled import tariffs on steel and aluminum to 50% from 25%, citing national security and oversupply abroad. The move has triggered retaliation threats and cost concerns from automakers and homebuilders. For aluminum, higher import friction is likely to tighten U.S. supply and pressure prices upward over coming days. | Trump’s executive order doubles U.S. tariffs on imported steel and aluminum from 25% to 50%, effective immediately. Canadian officials warn of “mass disruption” and job losses, while negotiators express optimism. The OECD links tariffs to weaker growth, and producer prices for steel have risen, indicating near-term aluminum price pressure but timing is uncertain. | The U.S. will raise tariffs on most imported steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% beginning Wednesday, with a special 25% exception for U.K. metal.
