The move is intended to protect domestic producers and may raise costs for downstream manufacturers, likely supporting aluminium prices in the near term. | The U.S. plans to double most steel and aluminum import tariffs to 50%, effective after midnight Wednesday, while keeping a 25% rate for U.K. metal under a trade deal. Economists expect higher input costs across packaging, autos, and construction, with possible retaliation and demand pressure. | The U.S. increased tariffs on imported aluminium and steel from 25% to 50%, effective immediately, amid U.S.-EU trade talks. The move is already linked to aluminium market volatility and higher price premiums, with expectations that volumes may not fall quickly but prices could firm. Copper and nickel impacts are only indirect. | Trump’s proclamation will raise U.S. tariffs on nearly all imported steel and aluminum to 50% effective Wednesday, doubling from 25% for most sources. The move is intended to protect domestic producers and counter cheap excess imports, likely lifting landed aluminum costs and supporting higher aluminum prices. Copper and nickel are not directly discussed. | Trump hiked nearly all U.S. steel and aluminum import tariffs to 50%, ending the prior 25% rate (with the UK temporarily exempted at 25%). The move is expected to increase input costs for automakers, builders, and packaging, and likely support higher aluminum prices via reduced foreign supply and tighter market conditions. | Trump’s administration raised most US steel and aluminium import tariffs to 50% effective Wednesday, following prior increases to 25%. The move targets excess foreign supply and is expected to increase input costs for automakers and home builders. Analysts warn demand could soften, affecting metal prices, especially aluminium. | The article explains how a doubled U.S. tariff on steel and aluminum imports to 50% may raise costs for consumers across canned foods, cars, construction, and appliances. It notes high U.S. reliance on imports, potential supply tightening, and industry warnings that businesses will face higher input prices and uncertainty.

The U.S. doubled steel and aluminum tariffs to 50%, prompting calls in Canada for retaliation. Prime Minister Mark Carney says the tariffs are unjustified but is delaying reprisals while negotiations progress. Canadian industry argues aluminum demand will collapse and highlights job risks, even as a deal may be near. | Industry experts expect global nickel oversupply to persist as Indonesia’s production capacity expands and nickel demand growth slows. The article links weaker demand (including shift to LFP batteries) and compressed smelter margins to potential production cutbacks. Prices have halved over three years and remain pressured, reinforcing bearish sentiment. | Indonesia is investigating alleged nickel mining in Raja Ampat, West Papua. Greenpeace claims forests were cleared and sediments threaten coral reefs. Environment officials and the minerals minister plan reviews, identify the operator (Antam’s Gag Nikel), and say operations may be halted while documentation is checked. | Tsingshan reduced nickel pig iron output in Indonesia as profit margins shrank and the nickel market weakened. Indonesia’s NPI output is forecast to fall from 4.76mn tonnes in 2024 to 1.74mn this year, with further cuts possible if conditions persist, potentially tightening nickel supply. | Talon Metals reported record drill intercept assays at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project, including 34.9 meters at 28.88% NiEq and 57.76% CuEq. The results validate a BHEM-targeted zone and may improve long-run supply expectations, but no production timing is given. | Talon Metals reported record drill assays from drill hole 25TK0563 at the Tamarack nickel-copper project, including 34.9m grading 28.88% NiEq (and 57.76% CuEq). The company frames results as world-class and will guide further drilling, but there is no immediate production change. | President Trump raised Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum articles and derivative products from 25% to 50%, effective June 4, 2025 with strict customs declaration requirements. The change affects aluminum import competitiveness and can tighten near-term supply, supporting aluminum prices. The notice does not directly reference copper or nickel. | Trump’s 50% tariffs on imported steel and aluminum (double the prior rates) took effect June 4, raising protectionism concerns and inflation worries. Domestic producers praised the move, while Canada and the EU condemned it and promised countermeasures. Economists warn tariff-driven price hikes and demand pressure could follow. | Indonesia temporarily halted PT Gag Nickel’s operations in Raja Ampat due to allegations of environmental threats. The energy ministry will conduct field verification before deciding further actions, while the environment minister will review reports and may pursue legal action. The suspension creates immediate nickel supply risk. | Indonesia’s PT Ceria Nugraha Indotama plans to expand ferronickel capacity after starting production in late April. Its Rectangular Rotary Kiln Electric Furnace has initial annual capacity of 63,200 mt ferronickel. The company moves ahead despite expectations of nickel market oversupply in coming years, which can pressure prices. | BHP is weighing a return to nickel through a proposed $1 billion mine project in Tanzania, positioned as a lower-cost producer than Indonesia’s nickel industry. BHP exited nickel last year after suspending West Australian operations, and the backdrop includes Indonesia’s supply supported by Chinese backing.

Trump’s administration raised aluminum and steel import tariffs to 50%. A Mid-Michigan scrap firm expects tariffs to boost domestic production, tighten substitutes, and increase demand for recycled aluminum and steel scrap used in metalmaking. The CEO says recycled aluminum pricing is already strong and could rise if tariffs persist and are enforced. | Indonesia’s energy ministry has temporarily suspended Gag Nikel’s nickel mining on Gag Island in Raja Ampat while it verifies operations near a marine conservation area. The government says it is investigating potential violations with other agencies, creating a short-term nickel supply disruption risk for markets. | Indonesia’s environment ministry will review nickel mining in Raja Ampat after Greenpeace sparked public outcry over environmental damage. The energy ministry has suspended operations at a nickel mine pending inspection, with potential legal action and punishment for firms. Indonesia is a major nickel producer, so risks to output could support prices.

The piece warns that Indonesia’s expanding nickel mining in Raja Ampat—cited as necessary for the green transition—has cleared forests and coastal areas and threatens coral ecosystems. Multiple companies are under investigation for environmental permit violations and improper waste handling, raising the risk of enforcement or operational disruption and supply uncertainty for nickel. | Indonesia’s tighter domestic nickel ore supply has pushed up in-country nickel ore prices and premiums, with rainy weather disrupting Sulawesi operations and the government ordering Antam to suspend mining in a protected area. Ore buyers are seeking more imported supply. Downstream NPI and stainless prices fell, but upstream tightness is bullish for nickel. | Lawmakers and celebrities, backed by environmentalists, are urging Indonesia to permanently end nickel mining on Gag Island in Raja Ampat. The push follows outcry and Greenpeace Indonesia videos alleging environmental damage. Officials are calling for a moratorium or permanent ban and permit revocations, raising near-term nickel supply risk. | Indonesia’s energy minister ordered a temporary suspension of nickel mining in Raja Ampat, after inspections found serious environmental-law violations and protesters raised concerns about ecosystem damage. The halt applies pending field verification of PT Gag and relates to multiple mining sites on small islands, potentially tightening near-term nickel supply.

Indonesia revoked mining permits of four nickel mining firms in Raja Ampat, Papua, citing environmental regulation breaches and violations related to protected geopark areas. The minister said one company (PT GAG) remains unaffected due to its distance from the geopark and compliance planning, but others face permit loss. | Indonesia’s government revoked mining business licenses for four nickel operations in Raja Ampat (Southwest Papua) but allowed PT Gag Nikel to keep its permit. The move follows environmental concerns and regulatory violations, and comes before RKAB approvals are obtained for mining activities. | Indonesia suspended four nickel mining operations in Raja Ampat after an environmental investigation found permit violations. The companies had not started due to missing work-plan approval, while one remaining operator (Gag Nikel) continues outside the geopark. The move raises near-term concerns about nickel supply from Indonesia. | Copper prices fell as investors avoided the market amid uncertainty over U.S.-China trade talks and the trade war’s impact on demand. LME three-month copper was down 0.3%. Comex futures dropped too, while LME stocks continued to fall, and aluminium and nickel also eased. | Indonesia’s energy minister said the government revoked nickel ore mining permits for four firms in Raja Ampat, Papua, after environmental concerns triggered public protests. The decision risks cutting near-term nickel ore supply from a major producing country, which can support nickel prices over the next days to weeks.

A Jakarta Post report says nickel mining and processing at PT Indonesia Weda Bay Industrial Park (IWIP) near Gemaf village in North Maluku causes persistent health problems, with dust and ash blamed on the plant and its coal-fired power units. The social and regulatory risk could pressure nickel operations. | Indonesia’s energy minister denied claims that former President Joko Widodo and his wife were involved in nickel mining in Raja Ampat and said permits were not issued by the former administration. The episode followed viral allegations of nickel-ore shipping tied to vessel names resembling Jokowi’s initials. | Canada Nickel reported an initial resource estimate for its Mann West nickel sulphide project near Timmins, Ontario, including indicated and inferred nickel tonnes plus palladium/platinum resources. The company framed Mann West as a larger resource and part of multiple new nickel resource updates expected in 2025, boosting nickel supply expectations.

The Philippines dropped a planned ban on exporting unprocessed nickel ore, reversing a Senate-approved measure aimed at promoting domestic processing by 2030. Philippine ore exports rose in 2024, and dynamics in China/Indonesia are mixed, but the policy change supports continued ore supply and may weigh on nickel prices. | GT Resources provides exploration updates for its North Rock (Canada) and LK (Finland) copper–PGE–nickel projects, including BHEM survey results and discussion of historic and 2022 resource figures (notably large contained copper and nickel). The news is exploration-oriented with no immediate supply or demand change, limiting price impact.  | The Trump administration announced it will lift Biden-era restrictions on copper-nickel mining near Minnesota’s Boundary Waters, advancing the Twin Metals Minnesota project. The decision reverses a 20-year mineral withdrawal/moratorium upstream of the proposed mine site, improving prospects for additional domestic copper and nickel supply. | The Trump administration moved to lift Biden-era restrictions on copper-nickel mining near Minnesota’s Boundary Waters, reinstating/accelerating leases for the proposed Twin Metals Minnesota project. Supporters cite environmental safeguards and domestic critical-minerals supply; critics warn of acid drainage and plan legal challenges.

SMM’s weekly review reports a rise in Indonesian hydrometallurgical laterite nickel ore delivered prices (up ~3.8% WoW) amid weather-driven supply tightness and delayed quota approvals. Demand strengthened after Morowali resumed production, keeping hydrometallurgical ore prices firm. Meanwhile, NPI smelters face cost inversions as finished stainless prices fall.

India has sent geologists to Zambia to explore potential copper and cobalt deposits under a large government allocation. The initiative spans three years and aims to inform potential future mining leases. Because exploration is early and not affecting near-term supply, any impact on copper prices is likely limited. | CATL’s consortium with ANTAM and IBC broke ground on a $6 billion Indonesia project covering nickel mining, smelting, cathode materials, recycling, and battery manufacturing. Planned production includes about 142,000 tonnes of nickel annually. The integrated rollout should raise near-term expectations for nickel feedstock demand.

Indonesia’s nickel miners’ association APNI urged the government to keep mining quotas valid for three years rather than cutting RKABs to one year. The ministry said shorter quotas aim to better control supply and support prices amid revenue concerns. APNI warned shorter periods could cause approval bottlenecks for miners, raising supply uncertainty. | Mirasol reports a second drill hole at its Sobek (Vicuña, Chile) Potro SE target, extending a hydrothermal breccia system with gold, high silver, and zinc/lead plus copper indications. The company plans follow-up drilling to test the fault corridor southward, but no near-term production impact is given. | High-grade nickel pig iron prices (SMM 8–12% NPI and Indonesian FOB NPI) fell week-on-week and are expected to stay weak. Elevated Philippine nickel ore CIF costs and Indonesia smelter maintenance/retooling reduce output and worsen smelter losses. Demand is steady but inventories don’t destock strongly; macro support offers only limited lift. | SMM’s weekly review finds Indonesian nickel ore and NPI prices weakening: nickel ore delivered prices eased and NPI averages fell, while rain disrupts logistics and smelters struggle with high ore costs (cost inversion), prompting maintenance/production shifts and reduced procurement. Despite some potential quota approvals, short-term nickel prices face downward pressure.

South32 said it will record a $130 million impairment charge tied to selling its Cerro Matoso ferronickel operations in Colombia to CoreX. The move reflects a sustained nickel market downturn, with producers cutting spending as Indonesia boosts output and prices collapse. South32 also reports declining planned nickel grades. | NexMetals Mining said drilling at the Selebi North deposit in Botswana hit “rich ore,” including multiple intervals with copper grades up to 2.87% and nickel grades up to 1.85%, while extending the orebody down-dip by 315 meters beyond the modeled boundary. The update is generally supportive for copper and nickel sentiment. | NexMetals reported rich results from initial drill holes at its Selebi North copper-nickel deposit in Botswana, including 13.5 meters with 1.13% copper and 1.25% nickel and additional intercepts extending ore down-dip and beyond the modeled resource boundary. Improved resource potential supports bullish sentiment for copper and nickel. | A businessman in Singapore is on trial over an alleged S$1.5 billion nickel investment fraud, with claims that purported physical nickel trading was fictional and investor money was diverted to personal spending. The defence closed its case after the accused declined to testify, while the verdict is pending later. | A Singapore court trial ended for Ng Yu Zhi, accused of masterminding a $1.46b nickel-trading scam. Prosecutors said purported physical nickel trades never occurred and early investors were paid with funds from others. The case centers on alleged cheating and forged documents, not real nickel market flows. | Canada Nickel says 2025 drilling at its MacDiarmid project intersected sulphide mineralization with multiple multi-hundred-metre nickel intervals (e.g., 0.25% Ni over 363 m) and plans to resume drilling at Midlothian and publish additional resources. The update is exploration-focused and unlikely to change nickel supply near term. | Indonesia’s nickel miners (APNI) urged the government to keep mining quota validity at three years rather than shorten it to one. APNI argues yearly reapplications could create bureaucratic bottlenecks and reduce operational certainty. The ministry says quota adjustments aim to regulate supply and support commodity prices, alongside potential environmental sanctions at IMIP.

A Jakarta Post report details conflicts around nickel mining in Indonesia’s Raja Ampat region, where environmental and indigenous concerns have sparked controversy. The mining operation on Gag Island (PT Gag Nikel) was allowed to continue, but the ongoing dispute highlights potential disruption or uncertainty for nearby nickel activities, a factor that can weigh on nickel prices. | Supreme Critical Metals amended a uranium property option and, separately, relinquished its interest in the Saguenay Nickel Project in Quebec after a strategic review. While the release is not about nickel prices directly, dropping the nickel project can slightly weaken market expectations for future nickel supply. | The piece highlights that Indonesia’s government plans to reduce nickel-ore production from about 330Mt in 2025 to 250–260Mt in 2026. It notes nickel prices had swung and then surged, attributing the move to rising Chinese investor interest and growing risks to supply from the main producer.

Antofagasta’s CEO said a Trump 50% U.S. copper import tariff could create an opportunity for its stalled Twin Metals project in Minnesota. The article notes the tariff scope is still unclear but likely to be detailed soon, while U.S. firms are already increasing copper stockpiling ahead of possible charges. | Reuters reports LME data showing Russian-origin aluminium as a share of available warehouse stocks fell to 66% in June from 69%, while Indian origin rose to 34% from 30%. Russian-produced copper share declined as well, while nickel remained mostly China-origin. The sanctions ban on post-April-13 Russian warehousing is cited. | SMM’s semi-annual review says copper cathode rod operating rates rose slightly YoY in H1 due to early order release, but demand weakened by June, inventories built up, and processing fees were cut to zero or negative to maintain throughput. In July copper prices slipped below 80,000 yuan/mt and new orders/shipments stayed weak, with pessimistic outlook. | Antofagasta’s CEO says Trump’s planned 50% U.S. import tariffs on copper create an opportunity for the company’s stalled Twin Metals project in Minnesota. The article notes uncertainty over tariff scope and implementation timing. Chilean officials plan talks with industry to seek details, with U.S. firms already stockpiling copper.

URU Metals reported drilling results at its Zeb nickel project in South Africa, confirming thicker higher-grade Ni-Cu-PGE sulphide intervals and suggesting mineralisation remains open at depth and along strike. The company highlights nickel and associated copper grades, but this is a project update with limited immediate supply impact. | Lifezone Metals said it will file the Kabanga Nickel Project feasibility study technical report summary with the SEC on July 18, 2025, ahead of a webcast. The study focuses on the underground mine and concentrator and will include initial Proven and Probable reserves, supporting the project’s viability. | SMM’s Indonesian market review shows nickel ore prices declining and notes smelters remain unprofitable with weak production incentives, limiting acceptance of high-priced ore. NPI/ferronickel prices fell week-on-week, with only a narrowing of the decline. Overall, the report expects continued weak nickel ore and high-grade ferronickel pricing next week. | Zeb Nickel Corp. reported assay results from drilling at its Zeb Nickel Project in South Africa, confirming thicker, higher-grade Ni-Cu-PGE horizons and continuity beneath historic resources, with mineralisation open at depth and along strike. The update is supportive for nickel and modestly copper sentiment, but is exploration-stage and unlikely to move near-term prices materially.

Green Bridge Metals announced a definitive agreement to option up to a 70% interest in the Serpentine Cu-Ni project in Minnesota. The company cites historical copper and nickel resources, strong flotation recoveries, and new exploration permitting progress, positioning domestic policy tailwinds as supportive. The deal signals potential future supply, not immediate market balance. | Green Bridge Metals signed a definitive agreement to option up to 70% of the Serpentine bulk-tonnage copper-nickel project in Minnesota. The release cites historical resource grades, strong flotation recoveries, ongoing exploration permitting, and U.S. federal support for domestic copper supply, but provides no production timeline.

Green Bridge Metals reports newly received drill core assays from the Skibo prospect, including long intercepts and higher-grade zones with copper and nickel mineralization (plus PGE, cobalt, and titanium). The release is an exploration update and does not announce production, supply disruptions, or policy changes affecting near-term copper or nickel markets. | Australian Senator Matt Canavan says Chinese state-backed supply expansion has already hurt Australia’s nickel industry, costing thousands of jobs, citing the resources minister. He calls for a Senate inquiry and warns China may now target copper (and mentions lithium), implying extra supply could pressure prices. | Australian Nationals Senator Matt Canavan says China’s state-backed financing is expanding nickel supply and has already undermined Australia’s nickel industry, costing about 10,000 jobs. He calls for a Senate inquiry and warns China may now pressure copper and lithium markets as well, implying downside price risk for nickel and potentially copper.

Reports say a Trump–Prabowo trade deal would grant the U.S. tariff-free access to Indonesia’s copper resources. It also notes Indonesia’s nickel raw-ore export ban may be lifted for the U.S. improving critical-minerals trade flows, which could support near-term expectations for copper and nickel prices. | Gold Hart Copper Corp. announced the acquisition of three new gold-copper-silver properties in Chile’s Domeyko Belt, increasing its land position by ~30%. The company highlights surface copper grades and intends near-term drilling results from its flagship Tolita asset, which may be a mild copper sentiment support. | The piece argues the US failed to secure a nickel supply arrangement with Indonesia under the IRA, while China-led investment expanded Indonesian nickel refining. With over 80% of processing tied to Chinese investors and IRA benefits limited for Indonesia, market expectations may shift toward Indonesia-China nickel flows.

BHP flagged a delay and cost overrun at its Jansen potash project, but also reported record copper output in fiscal 2025. It expects copper output to fall in fiscal 2026 because Escondida will mine lower grades, potentially tightening supply expectations. It is reviewing Western Australia nickel assets for potential divestment. | BHP flagged higher costs and delays for its Canadian Jansen potash project. Separately, it is reviewing whether to divest its Australian Nickel West operations after earlier suspension. BHP also reported record annual copper production and provided a near-term copper output range, shaping short-term supply expectations for both nickel and copper. | BHP reported record annual copper production, including a 17-year high at Escondida, driven by higher throughput and recoveries, and noted improved copper performance after an outage at its South Australia operations. It also said its Western Australia Nickel unit is in temporary suspension with a divestment/restart review into early 2027. | BHP said its Jansen potash project faces higher costs and delays, but the update also included record annual iron ore and copper production and new copper output guidance. Separately, BHP is assessing selling its Nickel West business after shutting operations amid a global nickel supply glut, creating offsetting copper and nickel signals. | Lifezone Metals completed an agreement to acquire BHP’s 17% stake in Tanzania’s Kabanga Nickel Project, giving it 100% ownership through the operating subsidiary. The company says it will control 100% of existing offtake and pursue project financing and path to Final Investment Decision. | Lifezone Metals said it acquired BHP’s 17% stake in the Kabanga project in Tanzania, taking 100% control through the operating entity. The company terminated BHP-related agreements and assumed full offtake, while financing discussions progress toward Final Investment Decision.

Lifezone Metals agreed to buy BHP’s 17% stake to gain 100% ownership of Tanzania’s Kabanga Nickel project and full control of offtake. The project is advancing toward FID with feasibility-confirmed reserves and planned production. The deal is supportive for nickel supply expectations and modestly for copper byproduct sentiment. | Lifezone Metals published results from a feasibility study and first mineral reserve declaration for the Kabanga Nickel Project in Tanzania. It cites 52.2 Mt nickel-copper-cobalt sulfide mineral reserves and steady-state production economics, with stated costs and NPV based on nickel prices, potentially influencing near-term nickel supply expectations. | Lifezone Metals’ webcast details its Kabanga Nickel project feasibility study, including declared 52.2 million tons of reserves at 1.98% nickel, project economics (AISC $3.36/lb) and a staged development plan. It also highlights metallurgical recoveries for copper in the concentrate, improving confidence in future nickel output. | BHP agreed to sell its 17% stake in Tanzania’s Kabanga nickel project to Lifezone Metals for up to $83 million. The exit appears linked to the slump in nickel prices from ~$48,000/t in 2022 to ~$15,000/t amid oversupply, though no immediate Kabanga operational disruption is described.

BHP will sell its 17% stake in Tanzania’s Kabanga nickel project to Lifezone for up to $83 million. The deal reflects BHP’s concerns about an uncertain nickel market and difficulty funding new greenfield projects. Lifezone assumes full control of project off-take, while Kabanga remains pre-production.

Japan and the US agreed to keep a 50% tariff on steel and aluminium under their bilateral trade deal, NHK reported. Maintaining high duties can weigh on aluminium import demand and regional pricing expectations. The steel portion may add some broader industrial-metals sentiment, though aluminium is the direct link. | Lifezone agreed to buy BHP’s 17% stake in Tanzania’s Kabanga nickel project for up to $83m, bringing Lifezone to 100% ownership of the development vehicle. The project is budgeted at about $2.49bn and targets ~50,000 t/y nickel, with ramp-up taking six years and an FID due next year. | Lifezone Metals acquired BHP’s 17% stake in Tanzania’s Kabanga Nickel project, taking full control of the project entity and its offtake. Financing and an advancing path to FID are discussed for the flagship high-grade nickel sulphide deposit, with feasibility also projecting copper output as a by-product. | Vale reported growth in iron ore, copper and nickel output for Q2 2025. While details are not provided in the visible text, higher copper and nickel production generally increases near-term supply expectations, which can weigh on prices over days to weeks. Aluminium is not mentioned. | Westwin Elements said it has signed $1.4 billion in nickel Sales and Purchase Agreements with Traxys, including 10,000 tpa of Class 1 nickel briquettes and up to 17,000 tpa of contained nickel as MHP for seven years. The credit facility supports construction, and the deals total about $1.7 billion.

SMM reports nickel prices fell to a near four-year low in H1 2025, driven by macro uncertainty, surplus fundamentals, and rising LME inventories. China’s refined output and imports grew while demand recovery stayed sluggish. With H2 downside risks from US tariffs and ongoing Indonesia cost support erosion, bearish pressure persists. | Anglo American said first-half copper output fell 13% to 342,200 tonnes and cut its 2025 production outlook. It cited sluggish demand. The company is also restructuring and has agreed to sell nickel assets, but copper production guidance is the clearest potential near-term bearish pressure for prices. | Anglo American says its copper business remains on track for full-year guidance, citing higher Quellaveco throughput and improved performance at Collahuasi, alongside region-specific unit cost changes. Nickel production fell 5% due to lower grades, but 2025 guidance was unchanged. | Brixton Metals began a second drill at its Catalyst copper-gold porphyry target within the Thorn Project in NW British Columbia. The company reports a refined copper-in-soil anomaly, supportive IP geophysics, and encouraging initial rock assays, with results pending. It is exploration activity without near-term supply effects for metals.

A Jakarta Post piece reports health impacts and heavy-metal contamination tied to nickel mining on Kabaena island, citing elevated nickel and other metals in residents’ blood and urine. It highlights weak environmental safeguards and runoff of mining waste. The implied compliance and operational risk could weigh on near-term nickel supply expectations. | Ghana terminated Rocksure’s lease for the Nyinahin bauxite deposit, ending an operator role in a planned $1.2B mine and refinery with GIADEC. Authorities may seek a new foreign partner, with Emirates Global Aluminium mentioned. The reasons are undisclosed and near-term production impact is unclear.

Reuters reports that Indonesian coal producers face falling exports and peaking captive power demand from nickel smelters. Coal demand tied to nickel smelting is forecast to decline as nickel overcapacity and possible tougher emissions rules bite, while smelter inactivity rose and royalty/export pressures increased. | NewRange Copper Nickel in Minnesota launched a tribal observer program with members of the Bois Forte tribe to monitor plants, animals, and landscape during field work. The program aims to inform mine design and environmental management and includes a forthcoming full cultural survey. No production or cost changes are specified.

The SBS podcast says the US Fed held rates as tariffs remain a concern and discusses fresh US tariff announcements that triggered a sharp copper drop (reported as the biggest since 1988). It links the volatility to ad hoc tariff changes and uncertainty about inflation and growth impacts. | Palawan in the Philippines faces local opposition to new nickel mining, including a 50-year moratorium on mining permits. With the Philippines a key nickel exporter, delays to expansions and potential constraints on new projects could reduce near-term supply growth expectations. Environmental concerns and legal disputes add uncertainty. | Glencore said H1 2025 copper equivalent production rose 5% YoY, but own-sourced copper fell 26% due to lower grades and recoveries impacting several mines. The company narrowed its 2025 copper forecast range. Nickel output also declined 7% on maintenance at Murrin Murrin. Overall, net price impact is unclear. | Zeb Nickel Corp. reports completion of a SpectremPlus airborne electromagnetic survey over its Zeb Nickel Project in South Africa, with final processed datasets and 3D inversion models expected by end of August. The company will integrate EM with gravity and magnetic data to refine drill targets for high-grade nickel sulphide mineralisation.

SMM’s weekly Indonesia review finds nickel ore benchmarks slightly higher, while pyrometallurgical ore demand remains cautious amid smelter negative margins. In contrast, high-nickel pig iron (NPI) prices rose, activity improved, and discounts versus electrolytic nickel narrowed, supported by stainless steel sentiment, implying mildly bullish nickel near-term.

The Philippine Supreme Court partly rejected a VAT exemption argument by Coral Bay Nickel Corp., a PEZA-registered exporter. It ruled the cross-border VAT doctrine doesn’t apply to inputs consumed outside the ecozone and confirmed VAT applies within customs territory, while limiting refunds for unutilized input VAT.

Westwin Elements says it is building the first major US nickel refinery, signing two large long-term offtake agreements with Traxys and Golden Age and selling most of its projected commercial output. The deals secure feedstock and buyers for high-purity nickel over seven years, but near-term production impacts are not specified.

Canada’s exports of unwrought aluminum to the U.S. fell more than 11% in June after the U.S. doubled Section 232 tariffs on aluminum to 50%. The article notes aluminum accounted for about 60% of U.S.-collected duties on Canadian imports, signaling near-term pressure on aluminum trade flows. | Indonesia’s nickel downstream strategy is highlighted, including an MoU between INA, Danantara and Eramet to build a platform across the nickel value chain. The piece also notes heavy dependence on China for MHP and nickel sulphate exports and calls for fair rules and higher environmental standards, aiming to diversify risks. | Chhattisgarh’s Mahasamund district has reported a new Ni-Cu-PGE mineral deposit, following exploration and a licence award to Deccan Gold Mines Limited. A mineralised zone and sulphide mineralization are cited as promising. The government frames it as strategic supply progress, but no near-term production volumes are given. | Chhattisgarh’s government says a new Ni-Cu-PGE deposit was discovered in Mahasamund district, with preliminary exploration indicating a mineralised zone and sulphide potential. DGML received a composite licence to explore and mine the area. The development supports India’s critical-minerals self-reliance, but no near-term output is stated. | SMM’s daily review notes that Wanhua Chemical listed 64 mt of battery-grade nickel sulphate for sale on the Anhuida platform of the Shanghai Metals Market trading center. The new offer quantity can influence short-term expectations for nickel sulphate availability and thereby affect nickel-linked price sentiment. | Magna Mining reported new underground drill results from its McCreedy West mine, including high-grade copper and nickel intersections within the 700 Cu-PGE footwall zone. Management says the data supports production planning and potential up-dip/east-west expansion, but results are for definition drilling with no immediate market supply change described. | Guinea revoked Emirates Global Aluminium’s bauxite mining concession and moved assets to a state-backed entity, citing non-compliance with refinery-planning requirements. EGA’s Guinea operations had already seen export and mining suspensions. The action threatens bauxite supply from a major producer (about 2–3% globally), supporting aluminium prices. | The Trump administration issued a Copper Section 232 investigation and then a proclamation imposing universal 50% tariffs on over 70 categories of semi-finished copper products and copper-intensive derivatives effective Aug. 1, 2025. Copper ores, concentrates, cathodes, and anodes are exempt. Policies aim to strengthen US domestic copper supply.

Indonesia is urging EV manufacturers to switch from lithium-based batteries to nickel-based ones through proposed regulations and incentives. The government highlights SOE projects and partnerships to grow midstream battery investment and secure supply-chain efficiency. The strategy is framed around rising global EV battery demand through 2040. | Indonesia’s minister said the US will apply a 0% import tariff to Indonesian copper under the ongoing trade arrangement, improving export economics and near-term demand expectations. Negotiations continue to lower US tariffs on other commodities, including nickel, potentially from 19%. | SLAM Exploration reports additional IP survey results over its Goodwin copper-nickel-cobalt zones, extending deep conductive targets and identifying chargeability/resistivity anomalies east of the Granges and Logan zones. It also references prior drilling with 1.49% copper-equivalent over 64.9 meters. The update supports future potential but isn’t a current supply change. | Copper prices rose as China’s July trade data beat forecasts, lifting sentiment for industrial metals. Chinese exports and copper/copper concentrate imports increased, supporting smelter demand. Optimism around a potential U.S.-China trade deal also helped, while aluminium and nickel prices advanced in tandem. | The article forecasts refined nickel prices to remain range-bound in August, citing off-season downstream demand and stable yet constrained nickel supply. High-grade NPI is expected to hold steady with a slight rise as stainless steel fundamentals improve. The NPI-to-refined-nickel discount is expected to narrow due to shifting costs from Indonesia quotas. | Indonesia’s minister confirms Indonesian copper exports to the US will receive a zero-percent import tariff, with talks ongoing to extend tariff exemptions to other commodities. Negotiations with the USTR also target reductions for nickel (currently 19%). The US reduced reciprocal tariffs from 32% to 19% under the July agreement.

Indonesia’s EV battery ambition increasingly depends on Chinese firms such as CATL as other investors exit amid a slowing EV market and technology changes. The risk is that Indonesia’s large nickel reserves may not translate into an integrated battery supply chain, potentially reducing near-term expectations for nickel demand. | Grid Metals announced a grassroots surface discovery of semi-massive nickel sulfide (“Pavo” anomaly) at its Makwa Ni-Cu-PGE project, with grab samples up to 1.1% nickel and plans for follow-up EM surveys and drilling in Fall 2025. The update is exploration-stage with no immediate supply effect. | Winshear Gold signed an arms-length option agreement to earn 100% of the Portsoy Project in Scotland, targeting near-surface high-grade nickel–copper sulphide mineralisation. The plan includes TSX-V approval, a 1,000m drill program and metallurgical testwork. It’s an exploration milestone, not production.

Lifezone Metals secured a $60m bridge loan to fund early-stage infrastructure work at its Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, supporting progress from feasibility study completion toward final investment decision. The deal affects project development funding but provides no near-term supply or disruption signal. Nickel is mentioned most directly. | MMG reported a sharp rise in interim profit and cash flow, driven by higher copper output across its mines and stronger copper (and other metal) prices, alongside lower unit costs at Las Bambas. It also cited record payable copper sales. Nickel is referenced for an acquisition, with no immediate impact detailed. | MMG reported 2025 interim results with a large jump in profit driven primarily by higher copper production across its mines and improved costs at Las Bambas amid stronger market prices for copper. It also references work to complete a Nickel Brazil acquisition, but no near-term nickel operating impact is specified.

Lifezone Metals secured a $60mn senior bridge loan to fund early works and infrastructure at Tanzania’s Kabanga nickel project. The financing supports progress toward a mid-2026 FID, amid Lifezone’s consolidation of ownership. Development of a large planned nickel sulphide operation can lift expectations for future nickel supply. | Indonesia’s nickel downstreaming program has boosted exports, but the lack of a long-term plan has created a nickel ore supply shortage. The article notes that required demand based on capacity up to 2025 is 261.3 million tonnes, while the 2025 RKAB may be reduced from 270 to 150 million tonnes. | Ormonde Mining reports that Peak Nickel Limited has entered an option agreement allowing Winshear to earn a 100% interest in the Portsoy nickel (Rodburn) project in Scotland by funding drilling and metallurgical testwork and issuing shares over five years. Ormonde notes the deal offers value without additional exploration exposure. | Eagle Mine will pilot Allonnia’s D-Solve mobile unit at its Michigan nickel site to reduce magnesium impurities in concentrate and improve nickel recovery. Eagle expects the trial to test performance and scalability under continuous onsite processing. A prior test showed lower impurities and higher nickel grade, supporting nickel supply quality prospects.

SMM’s Indonesia nickel market review shows nickel ore prices slightly declining, with further RKAB-related uncertainty weighing on ore. However, high-nickel pig iron (NPI) prices rose on strong cost support from auxiliary materials and stable nickel ore, while stainless steel sentiment improved, limiting downside and supporting nickel sentiment next week.

Last week’s nickel intermediate product markets stayed tight. In the MHP spot market, limited available supply led some traders to stop quoting, while downstream external demand continued to emerge and trader inventories were low. Higher cobalt sulphate and payable indicators boosted MHP costs, and rising LME nickel prices provided additional support. | MCX announced revisions to the contract specifications for its Nickel futures contracts, aiming to enhance market efficiency. The notice is about contract design rather than nickel supply, demand, inventories, or policy shocks, so it is more likely to influence trading dynamics than prompt a clear directional nickel price move.

Tsingshan Holding Group will invest $800m to expand its Dinson Iron and Steel Company in Zimbabwe, building blast furnace and steel plants and a 50MW thermal power unit. The project aims to double carbon steel output and reduce steel imports. The nickel producer’s deeper presence may support nickel-related supply expectations, with weaker spillover to copper and aluminium. | Magna Mining reported initial drill assays from near-surface No. 1 Contact Ni-Cu zones at its Levack Mine, including grades around 2.3–2.4% Ni and 0.7–0.8% Cu over multiple metres, supporting a potential mine restart study. Near-term impact on metals prices appears limited as it is exploration-based.

Pan Global reports high-grade channel sample assays at its Providencia target in Spain, including 4.17% Cu and 2.68% Ni over 5m (plus cobalt and gold), with mineralization extending over wide intervals and remaining open. Drilling is underway for further expansion, but there is no immediate production impact. | Pan Global Resources announced channel-sampling results at the Providencia workings in Spain’s Cármenes project, reporting high-grade copper and nickel (along with cobalt and gold) over wide intervals. The mineralization remains open and drilling is testing expansion, which is modestly supportive for copper and nickel sentiment. | The piece says stainless mills received tariff support but demand remains weak at service centers. It emphasizes that nickel prices are still soft amid exceptionally high inventories and a global supply glut, with Indonesia’s strong output expected to face demand slowdown and some smelter idling. Overall, nickel recovery looks unlikely. | SMM reviews Indonesia’s 2025 nickel ore market, showing Q1 saprolite tightness and restocking supported prices, while Q2 diverged as royalties rose (PNBP nickel rate 10%→14%), lifting mining costs. Limonite demand weakened after a Morowali HPAL tailings accident, then recovered. 2H depends on RKAB revisions, weather, and HPAL/RKEF output changes, with volatility and limited upside. | Trump expanded U.S. steel and aluminum tariffs by adding 400 products effective Monday, extending 50% levies on aluminum/steel-containing items. The Commerce Department said it blocks workarounds and supports domestic metals industries. Analysts expect some cost pass-through into consumer and producer prices, modest so far.

SLAM reports new induced polarization (IP) survey results on its Goodwin claims in New Brunswick, suggesting potential extensions at depth of the Granges and Farquharson copper-nickel(-cobalt) zones. The company plans a 4,000m drilling program to test targets, with prior drill intervals reporting CuEq grades. | SLAM Exploration reported IP survey results identifying chargeability targets beneath its Farquharson and Granges copper-nickel-cobalt zones and plans a ~4,000 m drilling program to test extensions at depth. While the work may influence expectations for future resources, it provides no immediate production or inventory impact. | Azimut and KGHM have started a 4,000-metre diamond drilling program at the Kukamas property to expand the Perseus high-grade nickel-PGE zone (often >3% Ni) and test additional targets. The release discusses nickel-copper potential but provides no production, permitting, or inventory changes, implying limited short-term price pressure. | Trafigura denies allegations that it benefited from a nickel fraud scheme involving cargoes shipped as LME-grade nickel but reportedly containing worthless or unidentified materials. The dispute includes claims about boosting reported nickel holdings and trading/derivative activity. Proceedings are ongoing as the case heads toward a November trial.

The piece argues Indonesia should act to support a nickel-based EV battery supply chain, noting it is the world’s top nickel producer and that nickel-processed exports are a growing share of earnings. It highlights US tariff package negotiations and Trump comments about access to the Indonesian nickel market, implying near-term demand sensitivity. | SMM reports tightness in the nickel intermediate product market, with strengthening payables and rising MHP prices this week as supply remains constrained and refined producers actively procure. High-grade nickel matte is weaker on low downstream acceptance, while rising Indonesian sulfur prices support nickel input costs, underpinning broader nickel price support. | SMM’s weekly review shows a slight decline in Indonesian nickel ore reference prices, with premiums and delivered prices largely stable. RKAB quota progress and abundant ore supply keep ore fundamentals soft, while NPI rises on firm costs but end-user stainless demand is weaker, capping upside and limiting gains next week.

Tanzania’s minister laid the foundation for a USD 15m nickel and copper refining and processing plant in Dodoma, built by Zhongzhou Mining. The plant will need over 280,000 tonnes of raw material annually and is expected to start operations in Oct-Nov, boosting local processing demand for nickel and copper feedstock. | Tanzania’s Minerals Minister laid the foundation for Zhongzhou Mining’s first nickel and copper refining and processing plant in Dodoma, valued at over USD 15 million. The plant is set to start operations between October and November, requiring over 280,000 tonnes of raw material annually and creating jobs, with demand expected to support local mining supply.

SMM said it will adjust the weight coefficient methodology for its battery-grade nickel sulphate price index, revising enterprise sample weights based on updated capacity distribution, spot vs long-term contract structure, and quotation accuracy. The revised weighting scheme takes effect August 26, 2025. | FPX Nickel Corp. reports completion of the 2025 drilling program at its Baptiste Nickel Project in central British Columbia. Work supports feasibility study engineering and the environmental assessment process, including geomechanical, hydrogeological, and condemnation drilling, plus ongoing baseline studies. No near-term production or supply impact is specified.

Danantara signed a head of agreement with GEM to jointly develop a US$1.42bn HPAL smelting facility in Indonesia’s Morowali nickel hub, targeting 66,000 tonnes per year of nickel in mixed hydroxide precipitate. The project supports Indonesia’s EV downstream push alongside GEM’s Vale collaboration.

Core Nickel reports successful airborne electromagnetic surveys covering its Mel, Hunter, and Odei River nickel projects, identifying drill targets and confirming correlations between geophysical responses and nickel sulphide mineralization. It also notes a management change (VP Exploration resignation). The release is exploration-focused, with no near-term production impact.

The Philippines, the world’s second-largest nickel producer, is working on mine rehabilitation through reforestation and a bioremediation “greening” programme amid environmental concerns. Laws require restoration, but some abandoned mines remain unreclaimed. Risks like typhoons may hinder cleanup, affecting future nickel supply compliance. | Brazil’s CADE has opened an investigation into Anglo American’s planned sale of its nickel operations in the country to MMG for up to $500 million, following a complaint from CoreX. CADE said the probe does not imply the deal will be blocked. The sale is still pending. | NexMetals reports improved metallurgical test results from its past-producing Selebi Cu-Ni-Co mines in Botswana, showing it can produce separate saleable copper and nickel concentrates and potentially avoid a full smelter/hydromet plant. The company sees lower energy needs and faster restart optionality, but no near-term output change is stated. | Prime Minister Mark Carney said he’s in talks with President Trump for bilateral trade relief covering sectors facing U.S. tariffs, including aluminum. Canada recently cut retaliatory tariffs and U.S. officials discussed Canadian aluminum tariff levels, despite a reported 50% tariff. Easing barriers could lift near-term aluminum price expectations.

Brazil’s competition authority CADE has started an investigation into Anglo American’s planned sale of its Brazilian nickel operations to MMG for up to $500m. The probe follows a competitor complaint but CADE says it doesn’t automatically imply obstruction, leaving near-term nickel supply timing uncertain.

Zambia inaugurated First Quantum Minerals’ $1.25bn Kansanshi Sulphide-3 (S3) expansion, commissioning a new 25 mtpa processing plant that should lift Kansanshi output to about 280,000 tpa and increase total copper from Kansanshi/Sentinel toward 480,000 tpa by 2027. The project also extends mine life and highlights Zambia’s output ramp plans. | SMM reports stable Indonesian nickel ore benchmark and premiums for most grades, with some supply easing as weather improves and RKAB approvals near completion. Demand is slightly stronger, NPI prices continue rising, and high-nickel pig iron increases on cost support and seasonal demand recovery, implying upward near-term nickel pressure. | SMM reports nickel sulphate (battery-grade) prices rising in Q3 amid weak spot supply from maintenance and limited raw material availability, alongside strong demand/stockpiling for the September–October precursor peak season. Analysis indicates conversion between nickel sulphate and refined nickel is cost-uncompetitive now, so supply tightness should persist into September. | SMM analysis highlights a tight supply-demand balance for nickel intermediate products. This imbalance is described as the driver of an upward price trend, suggesting that market expectations for near-term nickel availability and conversion supply are strengthening and likely supporting nickel prices over the coming days.

Anglo American and Teck agreed to merge into a $53 billion copper-focused producer. The article frames copper as in rising demand from electrification/AI while supply may not keep up, implying support for copper prices. A related market talk adds Canadian government review risk for critical-minerals deals. | PwC urged the Philippines to move up the copper value chain by investing in processing, refining, and manufacturing rather than only exporting mined ore. The piece highlights large copper and some nickel reserves and mentions a need for regulation and infrastructure, but offers no immediate supply or demand change.

Alaska Energy Metals says senior EPA and FAST-41 officials toured its Nikolai nickel project and that Alaska signed an MOU with the Federal Permitting Improvement Steering Council to streamline permitting. The company frames this as improving predictability for developing domestic critical minerals, supporting sentiment for nickel and, to a lesser extent, copper. | Zambia’s government is seeking additional compensation after a Chinese-owned copper mine tailings dam collapse released highly acidic, heavy-metal pollution into the Kafue River system. While authorities imposed a fishing ban and used lime to curb acidity, firms dispute spill estimates. The event could affect expectations for copper output, with broader health impacts reported.

Indonesia’s task force seized 148 hectares of land from PT Weda Bay Nickel due to missing forestry permissions needed to use designated forest areas, despite holding a mining permit. The crackdown suggests potential delays or disruption to nickel output, tightening near-term supply expectations. | Indonesia’s authorities seized nearly 150 hectares of the Weda Bay Nickel concession over alleged forest and licensing violations. The company said the seized area is a quarry for construction materials and that mining operations are unaffected. While it raises compliance-related uncertainty, the small scale suggests limited immediate supply disruption. | Indonesian authorities seized nearly 150 hectares within the Weda Bay Nickel concession on Halmahera for encroaching forest areas without permits. Eramet said the seized section is a quarry for construction materials and mining operations were not affected. The company and authorities are clarifying permits while broader scrutiny of forestry rules continues. | Weekly SMM analysis says nickel intermediate products (MHP and high-grade nickel matte) fell as LME nickel prices declined. The drop is attributed to increased refined nickel supply, high inventories weighing on nickel, and weak downstream buying. Tight spot availability wasn’t enough to support prices, leading to lower absolute quotations. | SMM reports slightly lower Indonesian nickel ore prices, while higher-grade premiums remain steady amid improved weather and tighter enforcement of mining compliance. NPI prices rose on higher tenders, but mills’ weak acceptance and slim margins reduced transactions. Analysts expect upward momentum to fade next week.

Nickel prices rose after Indonesia seized about 148 hectares of PT Weda Bay Nickel, part of Tsingshan’s giant operation, citing alleged permit violations. While shareholders say operations may be unaffected initially, the action underscores supply risks as Indonesia tightens mining quotas and cracks down on illegal mining. | MMG, backed by China Minmetals, seeks EU approval for a $500m purchase of Anglo American’s nickel assets. EU antitrust concerns about Chinese ownership remain, but MMG expects clearance, citing strong European demand and reduced competitive overlap. US industry groups criticize the deal for increasing China’s nickel control.

Genomines announced an oversubscribed $45M Series A to scale genetically enhanced “hyperaccumulator” plants for battery-grade nickel via phytoextraction. The company says yields and productivity have improved and that it will accelerate South Africa pilot projects and seek commercial offtake. The raise could bolster expectations for nickel supply resilience.

Brazil’s antitrust authority CADE has dismissed its investigation into Anglo American’s planned sale of its nickel operations in the country to MMG’s unit. The approval clears a regulatory hurdle for the deal, but the article provides no clear, immediate signal of changes in nickel supply, production, or inventories. | NexMetals reported assay results from 2 of 12 metallurgical drill holes at the Selkirk Cu-Ni-Co-PGE mine in Botswana. Core supports ongoing metallurgical testwork, flowsheet optimization, and variability testing at labs in British Columbia. Additional assays from the 2025 program are expected on an ongoing basis.

SMM reports tight spot availability for nickel intermediate products (MHP) and scarce tradable high-grade nickel matte, boosting sellers’ bargaining power. Demand improved as downstream users entered peak procurement. Rising cobalt sulphate and sulphur prices increased cost coefficients, while macro factors lifted LME nickel. Overall, nickel prices rose week-on-week.

Indonesia’s ESDM issued administrative sanctions and temporarily suspended 190 mining companies over failure to provide reclamation guarantees, including multiple nickel producers. SMM says these specific firms’ quotas are small and current supply-chain impact is limited, but regulatory tightening and sentiment could support higher nickel ore prices near term. | Indonesia’s energy ministry sanctioned and temporarily suspended 25 nickel mining firms in southeast Sulawesi for inadequate reclamation and post-mining guarantees, with licence suspensions across other minerals as well. The action is expected to be limited for active output but increases concern over Indonesia nickel ore supply, pressuring nickel prices. | Nickel prices have been stuck near five-year lows amid a large oversupply from Indonesia, reflected in rising LME inventories. Despite the weak backdrop, investment funds have reduced shorts and built long positions since mid-April, betting on a potential recovery if Indonesia reins in production via regulation and quotas.

GreenLight Metals reports early drilling at the Bend deposit in Wisconsin confirms significant copper-bearing sulfides and notable gold, with results on additional holes expected in 3–4 weeks. The company plans further drilling and permits, but no economic development or production timeline is set, limiting near-term supply impact on metals markets. | A copper and nickel sorting/processing plant by Coast Nickel Industry Limited in Kikongo, Tanzania, is nearing completion and is expected to start operations by January. The $7m project supports local value addition and cites issues like power reliability and road infrastructure, but it is not yet producing, limiting near-term price impact. | Visionary Metals Corp. said TSXV approved a $1.22M private placement with Teck to fund initial exploration on its Granite Mountains nickel sulfide project in Wyoming. Teck subscribed for about 17.4M shares at $0.07, subject to a four-month hold. Proceeds will support early exploration activities. | Visionary Metals Corp. announced TSX Venture approval for a $1.22M private placement with Teck Resources to fund initial nickel sulfide exploration in Wyoming. Teck buys 17.39M shares at $0.07, subject to a four-month hold. Proceeds support early exploration only, without any immediate supply change.

Copper rose 0.1% on Tuesday as supply tensions (Grasberg production suspension in Indonesia) outweighed stock accumulation. US Comex reserves jumped on tariff expectations, keeping the outlook fragile. A Fed rate cut improved sentiment. Nickel gained on Indonesia permit suspensions, while aluminium eased slightly. | SLAM Exploration reports diamond drilling underway at its Goodwin Copper-Nickel-Cobalt Project in New Brunswick, targeting an eastward extension of the Granges copper-nickel zone. The company cites prior drilling grades (not new results) and IP anomaly follow-ups, but the release does not provide fresh assay outcomes.

The article warns the EU could halve the Environmental Quality Standard for nickel in surface waters, setting limits below natural background levels. It claims this would penalize compliant industries and deter mining, refining, and recycling permits, reducing nickel supply and increasing uncertainty during Europe’s EV and battery push. | NGOs warn that nickel mining in Indonesia’s Raja Ampat could damage coral reefs and that rehabilitation is poor, despite Indonesia revoking permits for four of five mining companies. One firm was allowed to restart, but activists fear mining could continue or resume, implying possible nickel supply disruption. | NGOs warn that nickel mining around Indonesia’s Raja Ampat threatens coral reefs via deforestation and sediment runoff, despite Indonesia revoking permits for four of five mining companies in Southwest Papua. One firm restarted after mitigation claims, but activists fear damage and possible further mining disruption, influencing nickel supply expectations. | First Atlantic Nickel reports a step-out drill hole (AN-25-08) extending the RPM Zone strike length to 800 meters and confirming long intervals of visibly disseminated awaruite. Metallurgical DTR testing shows magnetically recoverable nickel concentrates with stated average DTR nickel grade of 0.12%, supporting improved recovery expectations.

NGOs warn that nickel mining in Indonesia’s Raja Ampat threatens coral reefs despite permit revocations for four of five companies and a partial restart by PT Gag Nikel. They cite poor rehabilitation, sediment runoff, and fear mining could resume, implying potential disruption risk for near-term nickel supply. | SMM reports Indonesian nickel ore prices stable, but high-nickel pig iron (10–12% NPI) started trending downward. Demand for stainless steel is weaker with slowed destocking, while upstream inventories rise and smelter margins decline. With seasonal support fading and higher coking coal/electricity costs, NPI prices are expected to keep easing near China’s National Day.

A report by Auriga Nusantara and Earth Insight describes extensive environmental damage linked to Indonesia’s nickel mining in Raja Ampat, including deforestation, coral destruction, and sediment impacts. Groups argue permits and environmental assessments are often rubber-stamped, raising prospects of stronger scrutiny that could disrupt nickel output and weigh on prices.

Glencore is vetting BHP’s nickel assets in Western Australia as BHP prepares to sell Nickel West and West Musgrave, supported by due diligence materials opened via a data room. The motivation is weak profits and risks to iron ore exports, but no immediate nickel output impact is described.

Australia’s Liberals encourage potential buyers of BHP’s West Australian nickel assets to better use mothballed infrastructure. Glencore is flagged as a possible buyer for Nickel West and West Musgrave, which were put into care and maintenance after cheaper Chinese-backed Indonesian product contributed to a nickel price plunge.

Canadian North Resources reports high extraction rates from lab-scale bioleaching tests on its Ferguson Lake massive sulphides: ~90.6% copper and ~99% nickel (plus 98% cobalt). Tests show copper leach performance improves at 65°C with faster leaching, suggesting lower-cost processing, but no immediate production or market supply change. | A former Singapore chief investment officer was fined for not disclosing conflicts of interest linked to a S$1.46 billion nickel trading scam. The scheme involved buying nickel at a discounted price and reselling via forward contracts, but collapsed, causing investor losses. The case is regulatory and does not cite direct nickel market supply impacts. | Indonesia’s ESDM revised RKAB procedures and asked nickel mining licence holders to re-apply for 2026-2027 permits, cancelling previously approved RKABs for the next two years while leaving 2025 unchanged. The one-year validity change increases regulatory uncertainty around production and sales, potentially weighing on nickel sentiment.

Glencore’s Mount Isa copper smelter received a $600m bridging bailout, but its operation depends on Dyno Nobel’s Phosphate Hill fertiliser/acid plant. If Dyno Nobel cannot sell by March, the fertiliser acid supply could stop in September 2026, potentially ending smelter production despite government support. | Australia’s ASX200 rose slightly, with materials leading, as copper prices jumped overnight and lifted major copper miners such as BHP and Rio Tinto. The rally coincided with strong gains in several copper-related stocks. The top gainer was Nickel Industries, which also surged, indicating broader bullish sentiment. | Australia’s market rose as a copper-fuelled rally in big miners followed an overnight jump in copper prices. BHP, Rio Tinto, and other copper-linked producers gained. Nickel Industries also surged strongly on the day. Other sectors moved lower, suggesting copper and nickel sentiment were supportive. | China’s National Day holiday had minimal effect on Indonesian nickel ore operations. The Oct. 3 RKAB policy release saw few miners secure approvals under the new two-stage verification system. Smelters increased early procurement, while favorable weather and rising Indonesian NPI output support ore demand, keeping nickel ore prices elevated toward Q4 2025.

Ivanhoe Electric shares rose after the company reported a 57% year-on-year jump in copper output to 71,226 tons in Q3 and reiterated full-year guidance. The report also flags upcoming higher-grade mining at Kakula and first production at Platreef, which is linked to nickel and copper endowment. | SMM’s Indonesian nickel market weekly review shows upward nickel ore benchmarks but declining high-grade NPI prices. After the National Day holiday, NPI fell due to margin pressure at downstream stainless steel mills, sluggish sales, higher costs, and continued oversupply. Downward pressure is expected to persist next week. | Goldman Sachs said copper is likely to trade within $10,000–$11,000/t in 2026/27 due to a surplus, citing potential weaker Chinese buying and U.S. inventory effects. It expects nickel prices to fall as Indonesian supply grows, and aluminium surplus to increase as Indonesian output ramps up.

Verity Resources said it gained 100% control of a copper-silver exploration portfolio in Botswana by buying out its JV partner’s 66% stake for $206,060. The acquired assets include multiple high-grade copper prospects and a deposit reporting copper, nickel and PGM occurrences, but no new near-term production plans were announced.

Anglo American and MMG offered remedies to the EU Commission to secure approval for MMG’s purchase of MMG’s Brazilian nickel business from Anglo. The deal includes a five-year ferronickel offtake contract tied to current European sales. The commission set a Nov. 4 decision deadline.

A report says Indonesia’s rapidly expanding nickel industry, driven by EV and battery demand, is causing major environmental damage and health problems in mining areas. It criticizes captive coal use and calls for decarbonization and enforcement against abuses. The pressure could influence near-term expectations for nickel production and supply practices.

Marine activists and a report urge Indonesia to restore Raja Ampat and enforce a ban on coastal/small-island extraction, citing risks from expanding nickel mining. It describes overlapping concessions with reefs and forests and references June 2025 concession revocations and a temporary halt at Gag Nikel, resuming operations later. | The article says a deal has been reached to keep Glencore’s Mount Isa copper smelter and Townsville refinery operating, ending months of uncertainty for workers and local suppliers. It frames the outcome as safeguarding Australia’s refined-metal capacity, implying reduced immediate supply risk for refined copper.

Talon Metals said Kennecott Exploration granted a 12-month extension to deliver a feasibility study and make an additional US$10m payment to earn up to 60% of the Tamarack nickel-copper project. The company aims to align the study with Minnesota’s H1 2026 scoping environmental review, supporting permitting timelines. | Talon Metals says Kennecott Exploration granted a 12-month extension to deliver a feasibility study for the Tamarack nickel-copper project, plus a US$10 million payment for an additional 9% earn-in. The company aims to align the study release with Minnesota scoping environmental document timing in H1 2026.

London Metal Exchange copper contracts rose 0.9% to about $10,700/ton after better-than-expected Chinese industrial production data. The upbeat demand outlook and LME Week supply discussions supported broad base-metal buying, with nickel also up and aluminium slightly lower, reflecting tighter supply concerns.

Teck reported Q3 profits aided by higher copper prices, even as its Chile Quebrada Blanca copper mine struggles after an expansion. Tailings drainage problems raised costs and delayed ramp-up, prompting a production guidance cut. Analysts cite multiple mine disruptions totaling 5–6% of global supply, tightening copper expectations.

Magna Mining reported new assay results from drillhole FNX6083-W2 at the Levack Mine in Sudbury, including copper-rich chalcopyrite veins (up to 19.3% Cu) and nickel-rich pentlandite-pyrrhotite veins (up to 12.4% Ni), alongside PGM and silver. It is an exploration update with assays pending for other holes. | Fathom Nickel reported updated mapping and pXRF/geochemical results extending the Gochager Lake project’s Ni-Cu sulphide “container rock” along a 3.5 km strike length. The company highlights disseminated and vein-hosted pyrrhotite/pentlandite with potential for further high-grade discoveries, but provides no production or confirmed new reserves. | Nornickel said its nickel output fell 4% to 140,000 tons in January–September 2025 and copper output fell 4% to 313,000 tons. The company cited changes in ore quality and the need to replenish work-in-progress inventories. Q3 nickel rose but Q3 copper declined to 100,000 tons. | Nornickel said its nickel output fell 4% to 140,000 tons and copper output fell 4% to 313,000 tons in Jan–Sep 2025. The company attributed declines to a higher share of disseminated ores, fewer rich ores, and replenishing work-in-progress inventories. Q3 nickel rose vs Q2, while copper fell slightly.

Base metals coverage highlights aluminium strength, citing China’s cap on production growth and alumina disruptions that tighten supply toward a 2025 deficit, keeping prices elevated. It also notes zinc physical tightness and energy-cost smelting bottlenecks, while lead faces a forecast surplus. Copper and nickel impacts are not addressed.

SMM projects 2026 nickel intermediate products to face supply outpacing demand as Indonesia’s MHP capacity ramps and output rises sharply. Demand growth is described as weaker, with NEV policy changes slowing passenger NEV sales. Costs may rise via RKAB quota releases and higher sulfur, but imbalance suggests bearish nickel pressure.

Anglo American reported third-quarter copper output broadly flat at 183,500 tonnes, with guidance unchanged, despite some site-level grade and recovery offsets. Nickel production rose 2% to 10,100 tonnes. The major mine-fire disruption was to the company’s steelmaking coal business, not tracked metals. | Glencore says it will walk away from a $35 million government grant to fund a renewable power and battery hub for its Murrin Murrin nickel mine and refinery in Australia, citing losses and inability to justify investment. The grant cancellation raises questions about the asset’s future and clean-energy upgrade plans.

NexMetals announced a C$65 million equity raise to fund operations and milestone payments to advance the Selebi and Selkirk nickel projects in Botswana, where production has been halted since 2016. If development leads to commissioning, it could add future nickel supply, likely weighing on nickel prices.

Glencore plans to close Canada’s Horne smelter, citing high costs for environmental upgrades, with no closure date yet. Horne is Canada’s largest copper smelter and is estimated to produce over 300,000 tonnes annually. The shutdown would intensify expectations of refined copper shortages amid existing supply disruptions.

URU Metals announced South Africa granted and executed a 30-year mining right for its Zeb Nickel Project (Bushveld Complex, Limpopo). The company is completing registration formalities and will start ground EM and gravity surveys to refine drill targets. The update reduces project risk but does not indicate imminent nickel output. | Pacific Nickel Mines says it will sell its Kolosori nickel project to Green Rock. Such transactions can influence expectations for future nickel supply and development progress. However, the available article text provides no information on output, ramp-up timing, or operational changes, so near-term price impact is uncertain. | A report by Climate Rights International links nickel mining in Dinagat Islands and Surigao del Sur to water pollution, deforestation, mangrove loss, livelihood destruction, and alleged rights abuses. It urges the Philippine government to halt new nickel permits until standards are met, increasing near-term nickel supply and regulatory risk. | A report by Climate Rights International describes environmental and social impacts in Philippine towns hosting nickel mines, and calls on the government to halt new permits and suspend operations that violate regulations. With the Philippines a major nickel producer, the allegations could increase near-term supply risk and regulatory uncertainty. | The European Commission deepened its investigation into MMG’s $500 million purchase of Anglo American’s Brazilian nickel business, citing competition concerns. It fears the deal could divert ferronickel supplies from Europe, tightening availability for European stainless steel demand. A March 20 deadline follows earlier concession offers deemed insufficient. | The European Commission will deepen its review of MMG’s $500 million purchase of Anglo American’s Brazilian nickel business. Regulators have competition concerns that the deal could divert ferronickel supplies from Europe, raising prices and affecting stainless-steel quality. A March 20 deadline was set; concessions were deemed insufficient. | The EU Commission opened an in-depth antitrust probe into MMG’s planned purchase of Anglo American’s Brazilian nickel business. Regulators warned MMG could divert ferronickel supply from Europe, hurting stainless steel production and affecting price and quality due to limited alternative sources. A decision is expected by March 20. | Australian Gold and Copper (AGC) expanded its South Cobar Basin landholdings through acquisitions of EL9012 and the Browns Reef Project, including copper-bearing polymetallic targets and increased planned drilling. While this can support copper/bullish exploration sentiment, there is no immediate production, inventory, or logistics change, limiting short-term price impact.

South Africa granted Uru Metals a mining right for its Zeb nickel project. The development may improve the project’s path toward production and raise expectations of additional future nickel supply. Because it is not an immediate start of output, any near-term impact on nickel prices is likely modest. | The EU launched a Phase II merger investigation into MMG’s planned $500m purchase of Anglo American’s Brazilian nickel business, citing risks to continued, reliable ferro-nickel access for Europe’s stainless steel industry. Regulators could require remedies or block the deal by a March deadline, adding near-term supply uncertainty. | Hayasa Metals reports completion of a 2025 drilling campaign at the Vardenis copper-gold project in Armenia under Teck’s option/joint venture. Ten diamond holes intersected significant quartz-sulfide veining and potassic/sericite alteration. The company will receive assay results later, with no near-term production or supplies change disclosed. | A spokesperson urges the European Commission to honor an openness pledge amid an investigation into a nickel business acquisition by a China-backed MMG. Separately, the piece notes improving China–EU trade and talks nearing a solution on eliminating tariffs for Chinese EV imports. Details of any nickel outcome are not provided. | Vale executives and Fastmarkets sources say nickel’s battery demand has slowed, with LFP now about 50% cheaper and potentially taking share from NCM cathodes. Nickel supply is abundant, but demand growth has not followed. Copper is described as having solid demand, though the article’s key near-term risk centers on nickel.

Lundin Mining reported Q3 2025 results and increased/tightened full-year copper production guidance to 319,000–337,000 tonnes while lowering consolidated copper cash cost guidance to $1.85–$2.00/lb. The update reflects stronger performance at Caserones and Chapada, supporting near-term copper market expectations. | Indonesia’s PP 28/2025 adds OSS-based administrative moratoriums and declaration requirements limiting new nickel smelter projects producing NPI, FeNi, Nickel Matte and MHP. Firms face ambiguity over retroactive application, creating regulatory uncertainty and potential project delays, likely tightening Indonesian nickel supply and supporting prices. | The report describes environmental degradation and human-rights abuses linked to ongoing nickel mining in Caraga, Dinagat Islands and Surigao del Sur. It notes nickel ore largely goes to China and cites changes in export destinations tied to nickel shortages. Despite risks from typhoons and pollution, no immediate supply stoppage is reported.

Stellantis will end its battery-grade nickel and cobalt supply deal with Alliance Nickel’s NiWest project from Dec 3 after missed milestones. Alliance attributes delays to difficult global nickel market conditions and financing constraints as prices have stayed under pressure. The termination supports renegotiation, but implies limited immediate market-wide nickel shock. | A report says nickel mining in the Caraga region (Dinagat Islands and Surigao del Sur) has caused severe environmental degradation and community impacts, while describing nickel ore shipments largely going to China. It does not mention immediate mine closures or policy actions, so near-term nickel price effects are uncertain.

Guinea is expanding alumina refining to capture more value from its bauxite, including a major SPIC plant and stricter local-processing enforcement that previously triggered a jump in alumina prices. While greater processed output could eventually affect aluminium sentiment, the article also cites a 2025–2027 global alumina oversupply, limiting near-term price clarity.

Tartisan Nickel Corp. said it is preparing a ~3,600-meter Phase 1 diamond drill program at its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario. The work targets extensions of high-grade Ni-Cu mineralization and upgrading inferred resources toward measured/indicated categories to support a potential PFS. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. announced it is preparing a ~3,600-meter Phase 1 diamond drill program at its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario. The drilling aims to convert inferred resources to Measured/Indicated categories and test down-plunge extensions of high-grade Ni-Cu mineralization, supporting potential future PFS work. No production impacts are stated. | Nickel Asia reported a sharp Q3 profit increase driven by higher nickel ore prices and strong exports. The company cites elevated nickel ore pricing due to tight supply, expecting persistence as Indonesia enforces stricter production-permit policies. Export volumes and prices also rose notably in the quarter.
