St-Georges Eco-Mining announced first revenue from its subsidiary and identified a new high-grade nickel-copper-PGE target zone at its Manicouagan Project in Québec. Surface grab samples returned up to 2.28% Ni and 0.37% Cu, prompting plans for further surveys and future drilling, but results are early and selective. | Australia and Indonesia highlight critical minerals cooperation after the Australia-Indonesia Climate and Infrastructure Partnership, with strong focus on nickel. Indonesia is described as the dominant, surplus-producing nickel supplier, but policy moves on nickel quotas and continued downstreaming support shape expectations for near-term supply, investment, and pricing. | SMM outlines Indonesia’s PP 28/2025 risk-based licensing rules affecting nickel smelters. OSS notices require firms to submit statements that they will not produce nickel intermediates unless exemptions are granted. System instability and difficulties for projects not yet under construction add uncertainty, potentially weighing on near-term nickel supply expectations. | Prime Minister Mark Carney listed new energy and mining projects for expedited review, including Ontario’s Crawford nickel project, described as the world’s second-largest nickel-sulphide reserve. Officials say the referral can speed approvals, attract investors, and potentially support construction next year, improving near-term nickel supply outlook. | Canada Nickel CEO Mark Selby tours the proposed Crawford nickel mine site near Timmins as the federal government’s Major Projects Office fast-tracks the project. The article frames it as a step toward creating a new nickel district amid stronger EV battery demand. It suggests improved prospects for future nickel supply.

The report says nickel prices stayed under pressure this week: SHFE fell below 120,000 yuan/mt and closed at 117,080 yuan/mt, while LME weakened to about $14,860/mt. Spot nickel also declined and inventories built domestically, with end-users more cautious as prices kept dropping. | Canada Nickel said the Government of Canada has referred its Crawford Nickel Project to the Major Projects Office, a step aimed at accelerating national-significance permitting and financing. The company expects progress toward construction by end-2026, supporting near-term optimism for future nickel supply.

Indonesia’s 2020 nickel ore export ban has driven downstreaming, requiring companies to build smelters and process nickel into battery materials. The article cites Indonesia’s large nickel reserves and notes PT Vale’s nickel matte production and emission-reduction investments. Overall, it supports expectations for tighter nickel supply and stronger processing demand. | A bridge collapsed at the Kalando copper and cobalt mine in DR Congo after wildcat miners entered despite a rain/landslide ban, killing at least 32 (possibly 40). Authorities suspended operations on Sunday amid reports of military gunfire and panic. Disruption is localized, with no production figures given. | A bridge collapse at DRC’s Kalando copper-and-cobalt mine killed dozens, with authorities citing wildcat miners forcing entry despite a ban due to heavy rain. Gunfire sparked panic, and the resulting rush caused fatalities. Operations were suspended, raising immediate supply and operational-risk concerns for copper markets. | SMM reports nickel sulphate prices easing in early/mid-November as refined nickel prices weaken and producers expect refined output cuts. While demand from ternary cathode makers stays high, cost pressure eases and integrated/smelters ramp nickel sulphate supply. Potential conversion of refined nickel into nickel sulphate could further loosen the market, weighing on prices into December. | Nickel prices have fallen sharply since late October, breaking multiple SHFE and LME support levels. The report cites weak China data, delayed US easing expectations, large refined nickel and LME inventories, and Indonesian regulatory actions affecting smelter investment/ore quotas. Near-term signals suggest oversold conditions, but surplus pressures dominate, keeping downside bias. | Indonesia introduced PP 28/2025, enforcing an administrative moratorium on new smelter projects producing intermediate nickel products via the OSS licensing system. Industry groups debate exceptions for already-investing firms, but the policy is justified as a response to expected 2025 nickel oversupply. The market effect is supportive for nickel prices. | Tribeca Investment Partners invested AUD 4 million in Cobre Limited to help fund a copper demonstration plant at the Ngami project in Botswana’s Kalahari Copper Belt. The financing targets well-field development and exploration using In-Situ Copper Recovery, supporting expectations for future copper supply growth. | The U.S. Mint ended penny production due to metal costs exceeding face value and warned it may target the nickel next. Nickels are made from a copper-nickel alloy, and rising copper and nickel prices have widened Mint losses, while advocates push for a cheaper “new” nickel composition within a year.

Ivanhoe Mines announced that South Africa’s president officially opened the Platreef mine and that the Phase 1 concentrator produced first concentrate on Nov. 18, 2025. The project targets significant platinum-group output plus payable nickel and copper by-products. Faster ramp-up supports incremental supply expectations for copper and nickel. | Ivanhoe Mines reports the official opening of its Platreef mine in South Africa and that Phase 1 concentrate production began on November 18, 2025. The orebody is platinum-palladium-nickel-rhodium-gold-copper, with nickel and copper expected as by-products, indicating incremental near-term supply support for those metals.

Nickel prices fell on both SHFE and LME as the nickel market’s surplus widened: inventories rose to near five-year highs and SHFE broke key support around 114,050 yuan/mt. Weaker rate-cut expectations, a stronger USD, and risk-off sentiment further pressured prices, outweighing some oversold signals.

MMG and Anglo American agreed to extend the deadline for MMG’s purchase of Anglo’s Brazilian nickel business after the European Commission prolonged its competition review and escalated it to Phase II. The review could delay closing from Nov. 18 to June 30, 2026, with concerns about ferronickel supply diversion to Europe. | SMM analysis argues that rising Indonesian MHP supply (and sulphur-driven cost variability) will likely loosen MHP payable indicators and weaken nickel-sulphate cost support. It highlights refined nickel oversupply suppressing prices and notes conversion to nickel sulphate may further drive expectations of a looser nickel sulphate market, pressuring nickel. | Tanzania says construction of a new nickel and copper processing and smelting plant is 85% complete and targets first production in February 2026. The government wants value-add before export and urges small miners to supply ore, aiming for a structured market and finished exports. Near-term price impact is limited by the delayed start. | Tanzania’s nickel and copper processing/smelting plant is reported to be 85% complete, with first production line commissioning expected in February 2026 and initial capacity to process 300 tonnes of ore per day. The government aims to add value domestically before export, creating a more reliable local supply channel for feedstock. | The piece forecasts aluminum moving toward a 2025 deficit due to China capacity constraints, recovery in demand from renewables and EVs, and US tariffs supporting domestic prices. It also expects nickel prices to face pressure from a large 2025 surplus driven by Indonesian production and softer battery-driven demand, leading to mixed metal signals. | The piece forecasts aluminium trending toward a 2025 deficit, supported by China capacity constraints, recovering demand from renewables and EVs, and US steel/aluminum tariffs boosting domestic prices. It also expects nickel prices to face pressure from a large 2025 surplus driven by Indonesia and softer battery-sector demand.

The U.S. Department of Energy issued an expression of interest to commercially extract about 9,700 one-ton ingots of contaminated nickel stored at the Paducah site. If pursued, the project would convert hazardous nickel into sellable commodity and help fund cleanup, potentially influencing expectations for nickel supply. | SLAM Exploration announced an additional 100 m extension of the Farquharson copper-nickel-cobalt zone at its Goodwin project in New Brunswick, reporting a 51.2 m mineralized interval with pyrrhotite and chalcopyrite. Assays are pending, and the company is preparing geophysics for 2026 drilling. | SLAM Exploration reports drilling intersected a 51.20 m interval of mineralized gabbro with pyrrhotite and chalcopyrite, extending the Farquharson copper-nickel-cobalt zone to 300 m at its Goodwin project in New Brunswick. Assays are pending and there are no immediate supply or demand shocks.

Villagers near Indonesia’s Morowali IMIP nickel processing complex report severe air and water pollution and tailings-related disasters. Indonesia’s environment ministry found violations and announced administrative sanctions and fines, but locals and NGOs say enforcement and transparency remain lacking. Ongoing risks could increase near-term operational and compliance costs for nickel. | Inomin Mines reported initial 2025 drilling at its Beaver-Lynx project, intersecting substantial near-surface polymetallic mineralization including grades around 0.19–0.20% nickel across large drill intercepts. The release also notes copper among other metals seen in prior work, but provides no production or reserve updates. | Trafigura’s lawyers accuse Prateek Gupta of suppressing or destroying messages tied to a fraudulent nickel trading scheme, alleging containers marketed as LME-grade nickel contained near-worthless material, causing large losses. Gupta denies involvement, claiming he followed instructions from Trafigura’s nickel desk and knew little of the details.

Trafigura alleges Prateek Gupta’s team tried to use a “friendly” inspection agency for nickel cargoes, after which inspections revealed worthless material instead of LME-grade nickel. The dispute cites failed repurchases, port container buildup, and large losses, but no clear immediate supply shock.

SMM reports Indonesian nickel ore prices largely stable but notes potential downward pressure for pyrometallurgical ore as early-December HMA prices are expected to weaken. Nickel pig iron (10–12% NPI) prices fell sharply, with weak end-user stainless demand. Indonesian Navy intercepted vessels shipping nickel ore, signaling tighter oversight. | The piece highlights divergence where oil falls but industrial metals rise. It says copper is leading, with aluminum and nickel also supported by supply disruptions and years of underinvestment in mining, alongside demand from electrification, grid upgrades and data centers. It forecasts continued strength into early 2026. | BHP withdrew from a proposed cash-and-share bid for Anglo American after previously attempting to buy it with conditions. The focus of the talks was consolidating Anglo–Teck copper operations in northern Chile ahead of a Dec. 9 vote. Outcomes could shape near-term copper supply expectations, but no new capacity change is confirmed.

PTX Metals began a 5,000m diamond drilling program at its W2 Cu-Ni-PGE project in Ontario. The company aims to test and expand sulfide-hosted targets using updated geophysics and preliminary metallurgy, while also citing regional infrastructure and metallurgy progress. No production or shipment figures are provided. | PTX Metals launched a 5,000m diamond drilling program at its W2 Cu-Ni-PGE project in Ontario, aiming to delineate mineralized zones and support resource and metallurgical work. It highlights ongoing surveys, geophysics, and infrastructure progress, but provides no immediate production or supply disruptions. | Copper is portrayed as facing a widening structural deficit driven by rapid demand growth from EVs, renewables and AI data centers, while mine supply gains remain limited. Disruptions, low ore grades, concentrate shortages and smelter output cuts (due to weak TCRCs) are tightening physical markets, keeping prices elevated.

Vale Base Metals and Glencore Canada agreed to evaluate a brownfield copper development at Sudbury Basin properties using existing Glencore underground infrastructure. The project is proposed to produce about 880 kt of copper over 21 years, with final investment decision expected in 1H 2027 and engineering/permitting in 2026. Nickel would also be produced as part of polymetallic output. | Vale Base Metals and Glencore Canada agreed to jointly evaluate a brownfield copper project in Ontario’s Sudbury Basin. The plan would use existing shaft and infrastructure at Glencore’s Nickel Rim South Mine to access nearby copper deposits. Engineering and permitting are expected next year, with a final investment decision in 1H 2027. | Vale and Glencore are studying a framework for a potential $2-billion copper joint venture in Sudbury, using infrastructure at Glencore’s Nickel Rim South Mine. The plan could produce substantial copper over decades, but engineering and final investment are not until 2027, implying limited immediate supply impact.

Vale and Glencore agreed on a framework for a $1.6–$2.0 billion brownfield copper project in the Sudbury Basin using Glencore’s existing infrastructure, targeting 880 kt of copper over 21 years. The work is set for 2026 with a final investment decision in 2027, implying limited immediate near-term supply impact. | Vale says it is in advanced discussions with Glencore to form a copper-focused joint venture in Canada’s Sudbury basin, targeting ~900,000 tonnes of copper over 21 years. Investment is estimated at $1.6–$2bn with a decision expected in H1 2027. Nickel is mentioned via Vale’s broader base-metals strategy. | Copper fell from an all-time peak on the LME, down 0.8% as a stronger dollar and risk-off caution weighed on dollar-priced commodities. Other base metals also declined, including aluminium and nickel. The retreat is viewed as temporary, with supply-squeeze worries and copper support above $11,000. | SLAM Exploration announced assay results from its Goodwin copper-nickel-cobalt project in New Brunswick, including copper-equivalent intercepts and an extension of the Farquharson Zone strike length. The company is still drilling and planning further geophysical work; no production or inventory changes are reported. | SLAM Exploration reported early 2025 drilling results from its Goodwin copper-nickel-cobalt project, extending the Farquharson Zone to 360 meters and highlighting copper-equivalent grades. As copper/nickel prices are used to compute Cu Eq, the news is relevant but remains exploratory with no production or resource update.

London LME copper surged to record highs as a weaker dollar and tight on-warrant availability drove prices higher. The LME showed large net warehouse cancellations in Asia, pushing stocks to the lowest since July. Supply worries were reinforced by Glencore cutting 2026 copper guidance, alongside gains in other metals.

SMM reports SHFE nickel moving sideways after failing to hold a brief 118,000 yuan/mt push, closing near 117,790 yuan/mt. LME nickel ranged roughly $14,700-$14,900/mt. Spot prices and premiums rose modestly, but downstream demand stayed weak and inventories were mixed (domestic buildup, LME still high). Prices expected to remain range-bound. | SMM’s Indonesia nickel market review shows domestic nickel ore benchmarks and some delivered laterite prices falling, while NPI prices (10–12% grade) eased week-on-week. Upstream smelters/traders supported prices amid contract talks, but stainless demand is weak with production cuts, limiting upside momentum. Premiums provide some support; near-term declines are restrained. | Burundi’s President met Lifezone Metals to discuss expanding investment in the country’s mineral sector, focusing on large-scale nickel extraction. Burundi is estimated to hold about 6% of global known nickel reserves, particularly the Musongati laterite deposit. The government stressed transparency and local value creation amid rising nickel demand for batteries.

KAIST researchers found that the electrolyte additive succinonitrile (CN4), used to improve battery stability, excessively bonds to nickel ions on high-nickel cathodes. This disrupts the protective electrical double layer and accelerates cathode damage and nickel migration, worsening battery degradation and motivating new additives. | Weda Bay Industrial Park (IWIP) rejected claims of nickel smuggling through its private airport in North Maluku. The firm said the shipments were permitted alumina samples sent to Jakarta for lab testing, detained temporarily due to missing documents. No seizures or investigations occurred, and samples remain under aviation security supervision. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. said drilling has started at its 100%-owned Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario. The program aims to convert resources, test extensions of nickel-copper sulphide zones, and gather data for engineering and development studies. It is an exploration milestone with no production figures or immediate supply change. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. says drilling has commenced at its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario. The program aims to convert resources, test potential extensions of high-grade nickel-copper sulphide zones, and gather technical data for future engineering and development studies. No results or production changes are reported. | Gungnir Resources said Sweden’s Geological Survey (SGU) designated its Lappvattnet nickel project as a “Property of National Interest,” offering protection from competing land uses and potentially easing future permitting. The release details nickel resources at Lappvattnet and Rormyrberget but provides no near-term production impact.

SLAM Exploration announced a 74.80m drill core interval at its Goodwin copper-nickel-cobalt project, grading 0.96% copper equivalent, and noted the Farquharson zone remains open for extension. The release outlines upcoming geophysical EM work for future drilling, supporting incremental bullish sentiment for copper/nickel. | SLAM Exploration announced a 74.80 m core interval grading 0.96% copper equivalent from the Goodwin copper-nickel-cobalt project in New Brunswick, extending the Farquharson zone to 360 m strike length. The company plans further geophysical surveys for 2026 drilling; the update is primarily sentiment-level for copper and nickel.

Nornickel forecasts the global nickel market will stay in surplus at over 200,000 tonnes of Ni in both 2025 and 2026, driven by rising Indonesian supply outpacing demand growth. Indonesia’s recent licensing and quota adjustments may help but are expected to have limited short-term effect, keeping bearish pressure on nickel prices likely.

Nickel sold off sharply, with SHFE nickel down about 2.36% and LME nickel down about 2.22% to around $14,295/mt. Spot premiums stayed high but trading was sluggish as downstream buyers turned cautious after prior stockpiling. Social inventories rose and production cuts failed to relieve inventory pressure.

Indonesian nickel industry groups APNI and FINI object to a new ministerial decree imposing nearly $400,000 per hectare per year in administrative fines for illegal mining in forest areas, specifically targeting nickel. They argue the fines are unfair and could distort costs, prompting pushback amid regulatory enforcement changes. | The piece links U.S. policy to near-term metals price pressure, citing higher aluminium import tariffs and proposed copper tariffs, plus sanctions that prohibit Russian-origin aluminium, copper and nickel imports from mid-April 2024. It also highlights infrastructure “Buy America” demand for copper and aluminium, boosting expectations of tighter supply and higher prices. | The piece describes a “great divergence” in commodities where industrial metals tied to the green transition outperform. It highlights copper’s strong 2025 rally driven by EVs, renewables and data centers, notes a structural aluminium deficit, and points to rising nickel demand from EV and storage growth, all supporting bullish near-term price sentiment. | First Atlantic Nickel Corp. announced a non-brokered, fully allocated private placement of flow-through shares to raise up to about C$2.94m, with proceeds earmarked for drilling and metallurgical testing at its Pipestone XL Nickel Alloy Project in Newfoundland. The company says work will advance exploration targets and processing recovery.

A CRI–Empower investigation says politically connected Philippine nickel exports are hard to trace after leaving the country, with limited safeguards at Chinese processing hubs in Indonesia. Communities report pollution-linked harm amid ASEAN EV growth and rising nickel demand. The piece raises compliance and reputational risks rather than immediate nickel supply disruption. | Canada Nickel announced initial mineral resource estimates for its Midlothian and Bannockburn nickel sulphide projects near Timmins, Ontario, reporting inferred and indicated resources totaling substantial contained nickel. While this improves the company’s long-term resource base, it is unlikely to materially alter near-term nickel supply expectations within weeks. | The ILO warns the Philippines could miss green-transition benefits without reforms to improve “decent work” and domestic value creation in its nickel supply chain. While nickel demand should rise, the industry says further processing is limited by low LME nickel prices and higher costs versus Indonesia/China.

Lundin Mining agreed to sell its Eagle Mine and Humboldt Mill to Talon Metals for Talon shares, creating a U.S. nickel-copper producer. Eagle is described as the only operating primary U.S. nickel mine and historically produces nickel and copper. The transaction aims to leverage shared processing infrastructure rather than stop output. | Talon Metals will acquire 100% of Lundin Mining’s Eagle Mine and Humboldt Mill, combining them with Talon’s Tamarack nickel-copper project and its Beulah processing facility. The agreement is positioned to extend mine life and accelerate exploration, supporting domestic nickel-copper supply narratives. | Lundin Mining agreed to sell its Eagle Mine and Humboldt Mill to Talon Metals in exchange for Talon shares, creating a U.S. nickel-copper pure play. Eagle is the only operating U.S. primary nickel mine. Humboldt mill processing and potential mine-life extension are highlighted, with no immediate output shutdown stated. | Talon Metals has signed an agreement to acquire 100% of Lundin Mining’s producing Eagle Mine and Humboldt Mill in Michigan. The combined entity would be a primary nickel-copper producer with expansion potential, aiming to extend mine life, advance permitting, and progress Tamarack project development. | Talon Metals agreed to acquire 100% of Lundin Mining’s producing Eagle Mine and Humboldt Mill in Michigan. The company says the combination creates the only operating primary nickel-copper producer in the U.S. and will use Eagle’s cash flow to support mine-life extension and advance its Tamarack nickel-copper project. | Indonesia is expected to expand mixed-hydroxide-precipitate (MHP) capacity further in 2026, deepening an already oversupplied nickel market. Argus estimates show falling MHP and Class 1 nickel prices in 2025, while EV demand growth cools and non-nickel battery chemistries gain. Nickel surplus is projected to rise in 2026. | Indonesia’s domestic nickel laterite ore benchmarks and delivered prices edged down, with rainy-season disruptions at mines and softer procurement from NPI smelters. NPI prices fell again amid weak end-user stainless demand, high inventories, and sparse transactions. While 2026 benchmark/RKAB revisions are discussed, near-term tone stays bearish. | Copper rose toward record highs as analysts pointed to tight mine supply constraints, with Goldman reiterating a bullish long-run view and Commerzbank expecting a move toward $12,000 over the next month. Nickel gained on Indonesia’s proposed ore output reduction, while aluminium climbed after South32 said it would shut Mozal. | Nickel 28 reported Q3 2025 financials and Ramu production/sales (contained nickel and MHP) and stated quarterly results were interruption-free. It notes nickel prices remain flat and payables are improving, while Indonesia’s announced fines for illegal mining may tighten ore supply in the short term. | Anglo American reported it is on track to complete the sale of its nickel business, restarting/continuing processes to dispose of remaining steelmaking coal assets after a failed bid. The company is simplifying its portfolio and expects regulatory approvals in the nickel/coal transactions, but near-term nickel output changes appear limited. | Talon Metals agreed to acquire Lundin Mining’s Eagle Mine and Humboldt processing mill in Michigan, consolidating U.S. nickel-copper production and refining capacity. The purchase supports domestic “critical minerals” supply focus and may improve near-term expectations for nickel and, to a lesser extent, copper feedstock and processing. | Kutcho Copper Corp. announced a non-brokered $1,000,000 flow-through financing to fund exploration on its Kutcho copper-zinc property in British Columbia. Proceeds will be used for Canadian exploration and critical mineral mining expenditures through Dec. 31, 2026. The release focuses on capital raising rather than near-term production changes.

The piece argues AI and data-center expansion are driving heavy copper demand (including claims of up to 50,000 tons per hyperscale site) and links rising prices to supply pressure from copper theft and infrastructure disruptions. It also discusses silver shortages and battery-related silver use, but only copper is directly price-relevant here.

SMM reports Bloomberg citing APNI sources that Indonesia may cut its 2026 nickel ore RKAB quota by about 34% to ~250 million tons to tighten supply and support nickel prices. While Indonesia’s 2025 output already ran below quota, rising HPAL/processing demand and declining ore grades could lift ore needs. | The Democratic Republic of Congo suspended artisanal copper processing and ordered entities to halt operations and certify mineral origins amid a corruption crackdown. The decree aims to improve transparency and prevent illegal exports. While cobalt supply issues are prominent, the article provides no quantified estimate of copper output changes or price effects.

Producers of raw materials rose as recent weakness in the U.S. dollar pushed metals futures to record highs. Copper topped $12,000 a ton in London for the first time, linked to tariffs and currency-market moves. The roundup also highlights record-testing gold and higher silver, citing geopolitical hedging demand.

Nickel prices rose to a seven-month high after Indonesia signaled it would cut nickel supply. Indonesian media cited Mineral Resources Minister Bahlil Lahadalia confirming production cuts, while an industry group expected Jakarta to enforce a 34% reduction in volumes next year, supporting higher prices. | Copper surged to record levels near $13,000/t before paring gains on Monday as investors took profits and the LME reopened after a holiday. The rally reflects shortage worries, weaker dollar, and risk-on sentiment, with tighter US stocks and mine disruptions cited. Aluminium and nickel moved only marginally. | Copper prices surged to near record highs as London caught up with China and US gains. The rally was attributed to shortage worries, tighter US inventories, mine disruptions, a weaker dollar, and risk-on sentiment. SHFE also got support from reports China will curb copper capacity growth. Aluminium slightly dipped; nickel rose. | Mining-related stocks fell as gold, silver and copper prices reversed lower late in 2025. The article also says CME Group increased margin requirements for metals contracts after a normal volatility review, effective Monday. Shares of Newmont, Barrick, Freeport-McMoRan and Southern Copper declined as copper dropped 4.6%.

Bloomberg profiles Arif Kurniawan, a dominant Indonesian nickel ore trader with stakes across multiple concessions and an estimated large share of domestic ore trade. It links Indonesia’s rising role in global nickel supply to battery demand and notes potential political shakeups and export-banning risks, but provides no fresh immediate policy action.

Nickel prices rose to a nine-month high after Indonesia signaled it will cut nickel supply to support prices. The Energy and Mineral Resources Minister said output in 2026 will be reduced and mining quotas (RKABs) tightened. Indonesia’s rising production has pressured LME stocks, but policy guidance is bullish for nickel. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. closed a C$1.0m flow-through financing at $0.24/share to fund Canadian exploration expenses and continue drilling at its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project in northwestern Ontario. The firm says assays are pending from a first completed hole and expects to update results as available. | Indonesia’s Energy and Mineral Resources minister said the government will adjust nickel miners’ production quotas in 2026 to curb an oversupplied market and reduce environmental impacts. Following the signal, LME nickel inventories rose but nickel prices jumped, with spot and three-month contracts climbing sharply. | Tanzania and the U.S. held talks in Dar es Salaam focused on deepening economic cooperation, including U.S. interest in investing in the Kabanga Nickel project. While LNG and graphite are also mentioned, the nickel element could improve sentiment around future nickel supply, supporting near-term price expectations.

Sphere Corp, a key nickel supplier to SpaceX, will take a 10% stake in Nickel Industries’ Excelsior nickel-cobalt project in Indonesia, valuing it at $3.6 billion. Sphere has also signed a 10-year SpaceX supply deal worth about $1 billion and will receive nickel cathode output with volume options at market prices. | ING expects 2026 to be characterized by tightness in copper and aluminium. Copper is forecast in a large deficit due to disruptions at Grasberg, Kamoa-Kakula, and El Teniente plus tariff-related trade distortions. Aluminium should also remain in deficit from China’s capacity cap and power constraints, while nickel stays surplus. | ING’s Metals Outlook for 2026 expects a refined copper deficit (~600kt) from high demand and outages/disruptions in Indonesia, DRC and Chile, alongside tariff-driven trade flow distortions. Aluminium is also projected in deficit (~200kt) due to China’s capacity cap and power constraints. Nickel is seen in surplus, with regulatory scrutiny and softer demand. | Sphere Corp bought a 10% stake in Indonesia’s Excelsior Nickel Cobalt (ENC) HPAL project for about $2.4B, securing proportional offtake tied to high-purity nickel cathode. The deal reinforces tighter Indonesian quota expectations and could affect market pricing if commissioning or output is delayed. | Aluminium rose above $3,000/ton for the first time since 2022 as investors priced tighter supply after China capped smelting capacity and Europe faced output constraints from higher electricity prices, drawing down inventories. The piece also notes copper gains on tight supply and nickel strength following a Vale Indonesia mining halt.

The DRC Ministry of Mines partially and temporarily lifted a Dec. 19 suspension on copper-cobalt processing after a compliance review in Lualaba Province. Processing entities operating exclusively in Lualaba can resume, and Haut-Katanga firms may process stockpiled minerals under supervision, pending inspections. The move targets OECD due-diligence compliance and traceability.

Copper hit record highs and nickel jumped to a 19-month peak as supply disruptions and delays raised concerns about near-term tightness. A strike at Capstone’s Mantoverde mine in Chile and delays to Tongling’s Ecuador project were cited. Nickel gains were reinforced by Indonesia plans to cut ore output. Aluminium rose as well. | Aluminum surged above $3,000/t on the LME, with buyers citing enforced China primary output caps, high European smelter energy costs, and “green aluminum” premiums tightening LME-grade availability. The rally is echoed by record copper and improved nickel prices, reflecting electrification-driven demand and a carbon-border-driven supply vacuum.

Nickel prices surged to a 15-month high after Vale halted operations in Indonesia. The stoppage tightens short-term supply from Indonesia, a key low-cost nickel producer used in stainless steel. With oversupply previously pressuring the market, the disruption provides immediate support, boosting price expectations for nearby weeks. | Nickel prices surged on 6 January, with LME nickel above $18,000/t and SHFE hitting its daily limit, supported by robust sentiment and possible changes to Indonesia’s mining plans. Market discussion centers on an RKAB cut and potential export-benchmark/tariff mechanisms, while copper strength also lifted nickel.

SMM reports nickel and SHFE nickel futures have risen, creating structural arbitrage versus non-standard high-grade NPI prices. Despite off-season stainless demand and high NPI inventories, upstream and downstream are executing more high-priced trades (980–995 yuan/mtu). With ore costs lower and potential stainless price recovery, NPI acceptance may rise toward 980 yuan/mtu within a month. | Perseverance Metals reported additional drill assays from the Baseline Zone at the Lac Gayot project, including a 2.1m massive sulphide intersection with high nickel and copper grades, alongside cobalt and PGE values. Management highlights expanding conductive targets for follow-up drilling. The update may modestly support sentiment for nickel and copper. | Core Silver reported drill hole LAV25-012 intersected additional copper-bearing intervals, confirming a 450-metre southern extension of the Laverdiere Cu-Mo-Ag porphyry system. While the results may modestly support copper-focused market sentiment, there is no indication of near-term changes to mined output, inventories, or refined metal flows.

The report describes sharp volatility in global nickel prices driven by expectations around Indonesia’s 2026 nickel ore quotas, then undermined by weak downstream demand and a sudden LME inventory surge (+20,000 mt in one day). Nickel prices corrected late in the week as sentiment shifted to caution. | Talon Metals completed its acquisition of Lundin Mining’s producing Eagle Mine and associated Humboldt Mill in the U.S., positioning Talon as an operating primary nickel-copper producer. The release highlights ongoing mine life extension, permitting, and exploration acceleration, which could support near-term expectations for nickel and copper supply. | Talon Metals announced completion of its acquisition of Lundin Mining’s producing Eagle Mine and the associated Humboldt Mill in Michigan. The deal positions Talon as a multi-asset U.S. primary nickel-copper operator and supports ongoing production and processing, potentially improving short-term expectations for nickel and copper supply. | SMM’s weekly review says Indonesian nickel ore prices eased slightly, but logistics and regulatory constraints disrupted mining/shipping, while demand from NPI smelters remained soft. LME nickel strength is expected to boost the next HPM cycle and support ore premiums. NPI prices rose on stronger high-grade sentiment and arbitrage that tightens supply short-term.

Rio Tinto and Glencore have confirmed preliminary talks for a mega-merger, with a Feb. 5 deadline to announce a firm offer. The deal is portrayed as consolidating Glencore’s large copper pipeline and expanding dominance in copper and nickel, potentially tightening near-term supply expectations. Antitrust scrutiny in China is a key risk.

Clean Air Metals announced 2026 objectives for its Thunder Bay North Critical Minerals Project, citing substantial increases in platinum and copper spot prices versus prior study assumptions. It also highlights resource grades and contained copper and nickel quantities, but provides no immediate production or supply disruption details that would likely move metals in days-to-weeks. | Ontario is fast-tracking permitting for Canada Nickel’s Crawford mine, which holds the world’s second-largest nickel reserve, to reduce regulatory friction linked to U.S. trade-war concerns. The company plans construction before year-end and a processing plant in Timmins, but financing gaps remain, suggesting limited immediate supply relief for nickel markets.

An investigative report describes alleged environmental and social damage from Marcventures nickel mining in Cantilan, Philippines, and claims political connections helped sidestep regulations. It notes a court ruling in favor of the company after years of litigation. The outcome may reduce near-term fears of supply disruption for nickel. | Bryan County, Georgia residents and leaders heard support for Westwin Elements considering Richmond Hill as a site for a major nickel refinery. Few specifics were shared, but backers cited 800 jobs and economic benefits. Commissioners want more research due to pending lawsuits and national security concerns. | Ontario selected Canada Nickel’s Crawford Nickel Project to proceed under its 1P1P streamlined permitting framework. The nickel-cobalt sulphide mine is advanced and Ontario expects construction to start by year-end. Faster regulatory progression improves confidence in future nickel supply, offering modest near-term support to nickel prices.

First Atlantic Nickel links its Pipestone XL awaruite nickel-cobalt alloy discovery to a January 14 White House proclamation targeting U.S. processed critical-mineral import vulnerabilities, highlighting nickel’s downstream processing dependence. The company argues awaruite could be beneficiated without smelting into nickel sulfate feed, potentially easing near-term nickel processing constraints.

Prospect Resources reported new exploration intercepts at its Mumbezhi copper project in Zambia, confirming gold by-product potential. The article notes copper prices have firmed above $13,000/t and gold is near records, with plans to update resources and begin early studies. It signals potential longer-term value, not immediate supply changes. | Nickel prices rose sharply after Indonesian officials reiterated expectations of tighter nickel ore production quotas (RKAB) for 2026, boosting bullish sentiment and driving a rebound in SHFE nickel toward its weekly high. This was tempered by high global inventories and weak downstream demand, keeping volatility elevated despite China easing liquidity support.

Chinese regulators’ crackdown on high-frequency trading aimed at reducing volatility and leveling access for retail investors led to a weekend speed bump in base-metal prices on the LME. The article says copper and nickel’s early-year rally hit a pause, indicating near-term sentiment/liquidity effects rather than fundamentals. | Chinese regulators have cracked down on high-frequency traders in base metals on the London Metal Exchange, aiming to reduce market imbalance and volatility. After a strong early-year run, the policy shift caused a weekend speed bump, weighing on copper and nickel prices and potentially altering short-term trading conditions.

PT Vale Indonesia is appealing Indonesia’s approval of its annual production plan (RKAB) after receiving only about 30% of the nickel ore quota it requested. The shortfall may jeopardize supply commitments for three HPAL plants, including a major Pomalaa project, creating potential near-term nickel supply uncertainty. | First Quantum reported 2025 copper production in Zambia fell 8% to 370,000 tons but set out a steady rebound. Kansanshi volumes are guided higher into 2026–2028, driven by ramp-up of the S3 processing circuit and improved ore grades. Sentinel remains weaker on lower-grade ore and maintenance. | Sarytogan Graphite reports shallow drilling at Kazakhstan’s Baynazar project found a 600m-wide copper bedrock anomaly up to 0.5% Cu, with copper mineralisation confirmed by malachite and sulphide veinlets. Alteration patterns fit a copper porphyry system, supporting expectations for potential future copper resources.

MacroAsia’s subsidiary received Mines and Geosciences Bureau clearance to develop and operate the Infanta Nickel Project in Palawan. The approval authorizes extraction and commercial disposition of nickel (and associated minerals) under MPSA terms, subject to environmental and regulatory conditions, after an ECC was issued in December 2023.

Westwin Elements is seeking a site in Richmond Hill, Georgia, to build a large nickel refinery producing Class 1 nickel for defense and energy security. The plan faces environmental, legal, and community opposition, and a previous similar effort stalled amid weaker nickel prices. Still, U.S. supply support is bullish for nickel sentiment. | Fathom Nickel announced a C$3 million private placement to fund resumed drilling at its Gochager Lake Ni-Cu-Co project, with Crescat taking a strategic stake. The company cites a strengthening nickel market and plans a 3,000–4,000 meter program, but provides no immediate supply or inventory impact. | Abitibi Metals announced additional high-grade copper-gold intercepts from Phase 3 drilling at the B26 polymetallic deposit in Québec, including 12.83% CuEq over 5.0 metres. The company is expanding drilling into Phase 4 and highlights resource growth potential, implying incremental bullish sentiment for copper, but no confirmed near-term supply change.

Nickel prices extended gains, with SHFE 2602 up 2.52% WoW and LME 3M up 4.07%, supported by positive macro news and Indonesian developments. Spot trading was sluggish and average spot prices edged down, while domestic social inventory rose, yet the outlook says nickel should hold up next week. | SLAM Exploration began an 852 km VTEM-Plus airborne survey over its Goodwin copper-nickel-cobalt project in New Brunswick. The company aims to map potential copper-nickel targets based on prior drill intercepts. Assays are pending for a separate gold program; no production or inventory changes are announced. | SLAM Exploration started an 852 km VTEM-Plus survey over its Goodwin copper-nickel-cobalt project in New Brunswick to refine targets for potential ovoid-type Cu-Ni deposits. The release cites strong historical copper-nickel-equivalent drilling but provides no new assay results or production/inventory impacts. | Prospect Ridge reports final IP survey results identifying a large chargeability/resistivity/magnetic target at its Excalibur copper-gold porphyry project and expects to finalize drill targets and begin a maiden diamond program in June 2026, subject to permits. The update is sentiment-supportive for copper but no near-term supply effect is stated.

Westwin Elements is seeking to build what it says would be the only major U.S. nickel refinery in coastal Georgia, scaling from 18,000 to 68,000 tons annually and producing Class 1 nickel for defense and energy security. The project faces local environmental and legal opposition, but could boost expectations for domestic nickel supply. | Bryan County, Georgia residents are split over Westwin Elements’ proposal to build a nickel refinery at an abandoned industrial site. Opponents cite environmental and safety risks; the company argues strong containment and compliance, using nickel carbonyl in a sealed process. A town hall is scheduled to review project data. | First Atlantic Nickel reports expanded RPM Zone drilling at its Pipestone XL project, with all holes returning positive magnetically recoverable nickel (awaruite) results and continued growth of the nickel-bearing footprint. It also references a White House proclamation on US imports of processed critical minerals, naming nickel and cobalt amid limited domestic processing capacity. | Quimbaya Gold reports independent confirmation of an extensive Tahami porphyry copper-gold-molybdenum system, with visible copper and anomalous Cu/Mo in preliminary sampling. The company plans maiden drilling in Q2 2026, but there is no near-term production or supply change, limiting immediate price pressure. | Benton Resources announced new VMS-style copper intersections at its South Pond project in Newfoundland, including up to 1.6% Cu over 0.70 m, and additional gold results. One drill hole also found highly anomalous nickel, chromium, and cobalt intervals, with follow-up drilling planned. This is exploration news with limited near-term price impact.

True North Copper increased its Wallace North copper-gold mineral resource in Queensland by adding results from two RC drillholes, taking contained copper to about 25,000 tonnes and gold to about 50,000 ounces. The update supports ongoing studies and extension drilling, reinforcing bullish sentiment around near-term copper supply expectations. | EV Nickel announced positive interim flotation test results from its Gemini North nickel sulphide zone. Tests show nickel recoveries of ~81–85% and final concentrate grades meeting project targets (up to ~5.83% Ni, with variability to 9.70% noted), using repeatable conditions suitable for bioleaching. | Fathom Nickel upsized a private placement to fund a planned 3,000–4,000 m drill program at its Gochager Lake Ni-Cu-Co project, targeting sulphide mineralization and using BHEM and soil geochemistry. The company provides nickel and copper tenors but no near-term production or supply changes.

Copper jumped to record highs on speculative buying, supported by a weaker dollar and geopolitical risk, though physical demand signals in China remain soft and inventories are high. LME and Shanghai moves were volatile, with aluminium rising then retreating and nickel slightly higher, suggesting broader risk-driven trading rather than stable fundamentals. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulphate prices at 32,857 yuan/mt, slightly higher than the prior day, alongside rising nickel prices. The cost impact stems from nickel price fluctuations tied to Indonesian policy disruptions, while producers show more willingness to hold prices firm. Demand is mixed as Chinese New Year stocking is cautious. | Copper surged to record highs above $14,000/t on heavy speculative buying in China, aided by a weak dollar and geopolitical concerns and expectations of stronger U.S. growth and infrastructure demand. The move lifted other LME base metals too, with aluminium rising and nickel up modestly, though physical demand in China remained weak. | A Westwin Elements proposal for Richmond Hill, Georgia to host a new nickel refinery has drawn intense debate over environmental risks and legal issues. The company cites national-security demand for high-purity nickel, but a similar Oklahoma project stalled citing logistical issues and declining nickel prices.

SMM’s weekly review says Indonesian nickel ore prices were mostly flat week-on-week, but shipping obstacles from MOMS verification delays and RKAB uncertainty reduce selling, keeping a steady-to-firm tone. NPI prices rose week-on-week, with higher LME nickel seen as supporting a floor for future ore premiums. | A 60th anniversary feature on Kambalda’s nickel discovery underscores how the region’s nickel industry collapsed in 2024. The Foster mine remains shut due to flooding, and restarting would cost up to $60 million. BHP’s nickel concentrator suspension and a cooling sector drive uncertainty and negative sentiment around nickel prices.

Commodities slumped after Kevin Warsh was selected as next US Fed chair, seen as signaling a more hawkish Fed. That strengthened the dollar and raised the opportunity cost of risk and non-yielding assets. Copper fell ~3% and industrial metals weakened amid high inventories and softer near-term demand. | BHP expanded its Xplor exploration program, selecting Orion Minerals and other energy-transition mineral-focused juniors, and highlighted subsequent copper partnerships such as its potential acquisition of Botswana copper projects via Cobre Limited. The piece notes BHP’s absence in DRC copper but continued African exploration activity.

Future Mineral Resources agreed to acquire the remaining 52% of its Poland polymetallic project, aiming for 100% control. The project includes the Szklary nickel deposit (historic production and reported Ni grades). The company frames the deal amid strengthening base-metal and electrification-linked demand for critical minerals. | EV Nickel reported continuous pilot bioleaching results on W4 nickel concentrates, achieving ~90%+ nickel extraction and >99% recovery of nickel as mixed hydroxide precipitate, with successful production of high-purity nickel sulphate (up to ~98.2%). The company says the flowsheet is scalable toward full-scale operations, supporting future nickel supply. | Glencore said it is in talks to sell a 40% stake in its Democratic Republic of Congo copper and cobalt assets to Orion Critical Mineral Consortium. The deal is based on a nonbinding memorandum tied to regulatory approvals, and Orion would gain rights related to mine production, potentially affecting short-term supply expectations for copper. | Westwin Elements told residents at a town hall that its proposed nickel refinery in Richmond Hill, Georgia will likely proceed, using a retrofit and closed-loop approach. Environmental and incentive disputes continue, with residents skeptical about wastewater trucking and safety. The project supports expectations for U.S. nickel refining capacity.

Bryan County attorney says a proposed nickel refinery site sale could be stopped if contamination is found at the abandoned Caesarstone facility. Officials plan to investigate, and if contaminants are confirmed, remediation would be required before any transfer. The potential delay could effectively postpone the refinery project. | Westwin Elements’ proposed nickel refinery in Richmond Hill, Georgia, is advancing amid significant local environmental and safety concerns and disputes over incentives. The company says it will retrofit an existing site using closed-loop processing and third-party monitoring, but residents fear wastewater transport and community risks, leaving near-term execution uncertain. | Merdeka Copper Gold said it targets higher gold output for 2026, while reporting Wetar copper production of 2,990 tonnes in Q4 2025 and total annual copper of 10,454 tonnes. It also mentions nickel expansion this year, suggesting incremental supply that could weigh on copper and nickel prices in the near term. | Merdeka Copper Gold targets nearly doubling its gold output in 2026 and reports Wetar mine copper production of 2,990 tonnes in Q4 2025, lifting annual copper to 10,454 tonnes. The company also aims to expand nickel output this year. The news supports a better supply outlook and sentiment.

Ardea Resources received up to about A$1 billion equivalent in letters of support from EFA and US EXIM under a critical minerals program for its Goongarrie nickel hub in Western Australia. The funding underpins progress toward a DFS and potential FID, strengthening bullish sentiment for nickel supply development. | Anglo American said copper production fell 10% last year to 695,000 tonnes and cut 2026 copper guidance to 700,000–760,000 tonnes, while maintaining later-year expectations. It also warned of further losses at De Beers and is restructuring, including planning to sell some nickel assets. | Kalgoorlie Nickel’s Goongarrie Hub Project secured non-binding interest letters for roughly A$1bn total backing from Australia’s EFA and the US EXIM, tied to eligibility and approvals. The funding would support development of a key nickel-cobalt resource for stainless steel and battery cathodes, a positive signal for nickel demand/supply outlook. | A letter discusses Westwin Elements’ proposed nickel refinery in Richmond Hill, citing community concerns and past feasibility issues. It notes an earlier expansion was dropped partly due to “decreasing nickel values,” implying nickel price levels affected project economics. The rest focuses on lawsuits and local governance without clear near-term market impact. | The editor discusses Westwin Elements’ plan to build a nickel refinery in Richmond Hill, Georgia, and notes that an emergency County Commission meeting blocked the project’s advances while an investigation examines contamination concerns under public nuisance laws. The local controversy could delay refining capacity, supporting nickel supply expectations.

Hindustan Copper said it is expanding into critical minerals, supported by its hard-rock mining and beneficiation capabilities. It signed agreements to explore and develop critical minerals including copper and nickel, and plans to triple ore production capacity from 4 MTPA to 12.2 MTPA by 2030-31, boosting future supply expectations. | Sandfire and Havilah executed agreements to progress the Kalkaroo copper-gold project in South Australia, allowing Sandfire to earn an 80% stake after a shareholder vote. The A$117.6m transaction includes further drilling and a new pre-feasibility study, alongside A$30m regional exploration over two years.

The U.S. Senate is considering legislation (H.J. Res. 140) to overturn a 20-year mining moratorium and allow copper-nickel mining near Minnesota’s Boundary Waters Wilderness. The proposal could raise expectations of additional metal supply, likely weighing on short-term copper and nickel sentiment, despite environmental concerns.

Canada Nickel closed a US$32 million bridge loan with Auramet. Proceeds will fund additional work on the Crawford nickel sulphide project and repay an existing loan. The financing lowers near-term funding risk for the nickel project, supporting nickel-related sentiment while the loan is due May 9, 2026.

Indonesia’s ESDM will reduce the 2026 nickel RKAB work plan quota to 260–270mn t, compared with 379mn t approved for 2025 and below estimated 2026 ore consumption (~330mn t). Some expect imports or lower smelter production, with changes taking full effect from April. | Fitch downgraded Eramet’s rating and highlighted weak earnings and high leverage, but the key market item is Indonesia’s nickel quota framework. Eramet’s Weda Bay Nickel received an initial annual production/sales cap of 12 Mt (down from 32 Mt in 2025). The firm plans to request higher quotas. | Eramet said its Indonesia nickel venture received a 2026 initial production allowance of 12 million wet metric tons, far below the 32 million initial allowance for 2025 (later raised to 42 million). Indonesia’s 2026 RKAB quotas are still being discussed, implying tighter nickel supply expectations. | Nickel futures jumped after Indonesia instructed the world’s largest nickel mine, Eramet and Tsingshan’s Weda Bay Nickel, to slash output. Authorities capped 2026 nickel-ore production at 12 million tons versus 42 million tons in 2025, tightening supply. LME nickel rose about 2.8% on the move. | The article argues the U.S. embargo on Cuba—especially cutting energy and financial channels—has caused widespread blackouts and has “crippled” Cuba’s mining sector, with nickel production falling sharply. It highlights Cuba’s major nickel reserves and claims the disruption weakens battery-material supply chains tied to China and Russia.

Indonesia’s regulators ordered PT Weda Bay Nickel to cut ore production quotas sharply, from 42 million tonnes last year to 12 million tonnes for 2026. The move is intended to correct a nickel supply glut, and nickel reportedly rallied in London following the announcement.

Vale reported a widened Q4 net loss, including a $3.5 billion impairment on its Canadian nickel assets. The impairment was triggered by downward revisions to long-term nickel price assumptions based on market estimates. While core EBITDA beat expectations, the nickel outlook deterioration is likely negative for nickel sentiment near term. | Vale reported a US$3.8 billion fourth-quarter net loss, citing weak nickel prices as a key driver. The company also booked a $3.5 billion impairment on Canadian nickel assets after downward revisions to long-term nickel price assumptions linked to a nickel surplus and softer EV-linked demand.

Avalon’s nickel bond default has the Western Australia Mining Rehab Fund preparing for a record $300m clean-up bill. The news highlights financial distress tied to nickel assets, increasing perceived short-term risk of operational disruption and potential supply constraints, which can support nickel price expectations over coming weeks.

Antofagasta reported a 52% jump in 2025 core profit as benchmark copper prices surged more than 40% last year. The miner said higher capital spending at its Centinela concentrator supports production and noted potential progress on its Twin Metals copper-nickel project in Minnesota. Shares fell despite the earnings beat. | Erdene reported new drilling intersections at its Zuun Mod molybdenum-copper project in Mongolia, extending near-continuous Mo and Cu mineralization and planning a resource update and PEA in 2026. The update may modestly affect sentiment on future copper supply, but no immediate production changes were announced. | Erdene reported drill results and plans to update resources and complete a PEA for its Zuun Mod molybdenum-copper project in Mongolia. Near-surface drilling intersected high-grade Mo and copper, including further testing of copper porphyry targets. The news is pre-development, limiting short-term metal price effects.

Indonesia is tightening state control over its nickel sector through crackdowns on illegal exploitation, with large seizures and fines across mines and processing sites. With EV makers increasingly shifting toward LFP batteries that use less nickel, the move raises near-term supply uncertainty and lowers nickel demand expectations. | Indonesia is tightening state control over the nickel industry after crackdowns on illegal mining and large seizures/fines. The move raises uncertainty for supply and downstream processing at a time EV demand is shifting toward lower-nickel battery chemistries (LFP). China-U.S. competition for critical minerals adds market focus. | Indonesia ordered major reductions in nickel ore production for 2026, including a 71% cut at the PT Weda Bay Mine, to address global oversupply and stabilize prices. The announcement is cited with LME benchmark nickel jumping more than 2% and analysts upgrading nickel forecasts, indicating near-term bullish pressure. | Indonesia is tightening state control over its nickel industry, seizing large areas and imposing significant fines over illegal or improper operations. The crackdown comes as EV makers increasingly adopt LFP battery chemistries that use less nickel. Together, supply and demand uncertainties in the largest nickel supplier weigh on nickel expectations. | Enduro Metals reports 2025 sampling that identified strongly mineralized gold skarn in bedrock at its October Dome project and expanded a gold-copper target corridor to 5.5 km, with soil copper anomalies up to 0.13%. The release is exploration-focused with no production impact, so near-term copper price effects are likely limited. | Indonesia is tightening state control over nickel, seizing large areas of mines and processing sites, imposing fines, and targeting illegal exploitation. As EV makers increasingly shift to lithium iron phosphate batteries that use less nickel, the market faces both higher supply uncertainty and weaker near-term demand outlook for nickel. | Cyclone Gezani has halted operations at Sumitomo’s Ambatovy nickel and cobalt mine in Madagascar. The company suspended production ahead of the storm and says the mine cannot restart immediately due to damage assessment and rebuilding needs. With Ambatovy already below capacity, the stop increases near-term nickel supply uncertainty. | A UK logistics intermediary is linked to a London court finding of fraudulent duplicate bills of lading tied to Trafigura’s nickel claims. The court found Gupta-controlled entities used the agent to duplicate documents and that some cargoes weren’t nickel at all. The disputes disrupt delivery and raise counterparty and trade-finance risk for nickel. | The Trump DOJ defended the Biden EPA’s 2023 veto blocking the Pebble Mine in Alaska. The project was projected to be the US’s largest copper source, alongside other metals. Blocking it raises near-term copper supply uncertainty, contributing to a sharp drop in Northern Dynasty Minerals’ shares.

A landslide hit a tailings area at PT Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park (IMIP) in Sulawesi, killing one contractor and halting operations at the affected zone. The incident damaged equipment and is under investigation, potentially disrupting Indonesia’s nickel processing supply in the near term. | Vale Base Metals and partners formed a consortium to invest up to US$200 million to secure the Thompson Mine Complex in Manitoba, including the Thompson Nickel Belt. The deal includes an offtake agreement for nickel concentrate and aims to maintain long-term nickel production, supporting employment and operational continuity into the future. | Nickel surged 5.2% in one session despite an oversupplied backdrop as Indonesia cut RKAB mining quotas, cracked down on illegal operations, and delayed compliance systems—limiting high-grade ore flows to smelters. The move was reinforced by U.S. strategic stockpiling targeting nickel, boosting speculative and institutional demand. | Vale Base Metals agreed to sell a majority stake in Manitoba’s Thompson Nickel Belt mine to a Canadian-led consortium, creating Exiro Nickel. The buyers plan $200 million to revive the operation, with Vale retaining 18.9% and signing a five-year offtake. Nickel prices have been pressured by higher supply and weak demand. | Exiro Nickel agreed to acquire Vale Base Metals’ Thompson nickel mine and concentrator in Manitoba, planning to restore production rates toward prior peaks and process ore at the on-site mill. The deal is expected to close by end-2026, with Vale retaining off-take rights, supporting a constructive nickel outlook.

Newmont reported end-2025 mineral reserves of 118.2M ounces of gold and 12.5M tonnes of copper, with copper reserves slightly down from 2024 mainly due to reclassification of Yanacocha Sulfides from reserves to resources. The update does not mention operational changes that would materially affect near-term copper flows or prices. | Hudbay reported record 2025 revenue and EBITDA and said it met its consolidated copper production guidance, with copper output of 118,188 tonnes. It also outlined 2026 copper guidance and cited temporary operational interruptions. The update may slightly affect near-term copper supply expectations, but likely not move the wider market meaningfully.

Koryx Copper reported assay results from 13 infill/expansion drill holes at the Haib copper project in Namibia, highlighting generally stronger-than-expected copper intercepts and continued advancement toward an updated MRE and a PFS in late 2026. The news is supportive for project fundamentals but unlikely to shift near-term copper markets materially. | Indonesia is assessing revoking the environmental permit of PT QMB New Energy Materials after a landslide struck mine waste at its nickel processing hub in Sulawesi. The minister cited repeated fatal incidents; QMB already suspended most production after a 2025 landslide. Indonesia’s action could further disrupt nickel supply. | Nickel Industries’ H2 earnings call highlighted record NPI and mine production but “challenging” nickel pricing, citing LME nickel down 9.8% and NPI down 2.8% versus 2024. Indonesia RKAB quota timing caused standby costs and lost volumes. Management also flagged ENC commissioning and higher early-2026 margins.

Indonesia’s environmental minister is considering revoking PT QMB New Energy Materials’ environmental approval after a fatal landslide in a tailings area at the Morowali Industrial Park. The incident occurred at an HPAL unit processing low-grade nickel ore to produce MHP for EV batteries. Tailings operations were halted and investigations continue. | A watchdog complaint filed with SGX alleges OCBC may have misled investors about its financing exposure to Harita Nickel Group, whose Obi Island nickel operations rely almost entirely on captive coal power. The challenge raises climate-transition and disclosure risks, potentially increasing near-term risk premia for nickel. | Fathom Nickel reported completion of a winter access trail and mobilization for a 3,000–4,000 m drilling program at the Gochager Lake Ni-Cu project, expected to start in early March. The release outlines multiple high-priority targets and geochemical results, but provides no production or finalized resource changes. | The piece attributes nickel’s mid-December rise to supply-tightening signals from Indonesia and outlines large Indonesian growth and lower production costs. It separates Class 1 vs Class 2 nickel and notes EV demand headwinds from LFP. Despite some tightening in niches, it projects surpluses through 2026, keeping nickel range-bound. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. reported assay results from its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project, including 3.0m grading 2.17% nickel and 1.45% copper. The company is continuing an infill/drill program to expand and improve deposit continuity, with additional assays pending. No production or supply changes were announced.

Indonesia, the dominant nickel producer, is expected to boost nickel supply in 2026, with production projected to rise about 11.9% to 2.9mt. Capacity increases are supported by higher RKAB-permitted volumes and ongoing NPI/HPAL plant commissioning despite policy efforts that may tighten quotas.

Indonesia’s Nickel Industry Forum warns that implementing a US-Indonesia Agreement on Reciprocal Trade (ART) should not disrupt the “nickel ecosystem” of existing smelter and refining contracts. The US has a small processing footprint but seeks influence in critical mineral supply chains, while China dominates current refining investment. | Canada Nickel announced mineralogical and metallurgical results from its Midlothian Project in Ontario, highlighting widespread awaruite (about 79% of nickel) with initial grades around 0.28% nickel and Davis Tube Recoverable nickel roughly 0.07–0.15%. Higher-quality recoverable nickel expectations are incremental positive for nickel sentiment. | Tinka reported initial diamond drilling results from its Silvia copper-gold project, highlighting copper-bearing intervals and planning follow-up geophysics over a wider target area. The company also updated progress at its Ayawilca zinc-silver-tin project. The copper exploration news may sway sentiment, but no immediate supply impact is indicated. | London’s High Court refused an Indian businessman permission to appeal a ruling that he defrauded Trafigura over fake nickel cargoes. Trafigura said it is owed about $640 million, while the judge found Trafigura was induced by false representations. The dispute affects credibility and settlement risk more than near-term nickel fundamentals. | Kutcho Copper announced the closing of a C$3.0 million flow-through financing to fund exploration on its Kutcho copper-zinc property in British Columbia. Proceeds are intended for Canadian exploration and flow-through critical mineral expenditures. The release includes standard financing and regulatory language, with no direct near-term production or supply change. | EV Nickel announced a maiden mineral resource estimate for the Gemini North nickel zone within its Shaw Dome project, reporting indicated and inferred tonnes/grades and contained nickel using a pit-constrained approach. The company highlights nickel-copper sulphide potential but provides no near-term production timeline, so short-term nickel price impact is limited.

Indonesia (ESDM) cut PT Weda Bay Nickel’s 2026 production quota to 12 million Mwmt, a ~71% drop from the prior year, aiming to constrain supply and stabilize prices. LME nickel surged over 2% after the announcement, shifting the market narrative from surplus to imminent shortage.

Aluminium ended lower as a stronger dollar and a widening Middle East conflict against Iran pressured commodity prices. The closure of Hormuz earlier had boosted aluminium, but risk persisted and producers paused premiums. Copper and nickel also weakened, reflecting broad base-metal pressure from higher oil/gas inflation fears.

Copper’s historic rally abruptly reversed on March 4, with LME prices falling nearly 10% as investors de-risk amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions. The blockade boosts energy costs and interrupts sulfur supply for SX-EW, raising costs while geopolitics lifts war-risk premiums. Demand-growth fears and de-grossing deepen the bearish move. | Debswana approved plans to diversify beyond diamonds amid a demand slump, including expanding copper and platinum projects via Naledi Mining Services and developing a solar farm near the Jwaneng mine. The article suggests strategic redirection of investment and potential long-run supply signals, but no immediate production changes are specified.

Canada Nickel says the federal Impact Assessment Agency of Canada has ended the Impact Statement phase and formally started the Impact Assessment for the Crawford nickel sulphide project in Ontario. The company expects a federal permitting decision by summer 2026 after draft report and public comment.

Copper slumped 1.6% on the LME to around $13,131/t after a surprise build-up of LME-registered inventories and speculative profit-taking. The move reflects macro pressure from higher rates and energy-driven inflation, while aluminium and nickel also dipped modestly as traders shifted toward tighter conditions. | Reuters reports Gulf shipping disruptions from the Iran conflict are squeezing Middle East sulphur supplies used for sulphuric acid in HPAL nickel refining and copper processing. Indonesia imports most sulphur and has limited stockpiles, with analysts warning HPAL output cuts by next month. African copper miners may face similar shortages and higher acid costs. | Boliden said its Kevitsa mine expansion in Finland is on hold after Finland quadrupled mining taxes on metal ores from 0.6% to 2.5%. Boliden Kevitsa, producing copper and nickel, has initiated change negotiations and is reviewing operations, potentially affecting future supply expectations. | Boliden halted a €1 billion expansion at its Kevitsa open-pit mine in Finland after the government quadrupled mining taxes on metal ores from 0.6% to 2.5%. The changes could affect operations at the Kevitsa copper and nickel producer, raising supply-growth risk. | Research cited by TASS says Russian shipments to China surged in 2025: aluminium up 94% to 2.1 mln tons, nickel up 51% to 88,000 tons, and refined copper up 58% with copper concentrate up 2.1-fold. Increased inflows raise supply expectations and can weigh on aluminium, nickel, and copper prices. | Research cited by TASS says Russia’s shipments to China surged in 2025: aluminium exports rose 94% to 2.1 mln tons, nickel to 88,000 tons (+51%). It also notes higher refined copper (+58%) and copper concentrate (+2.1x) supplies. Greater availability likely pressures prices.

Four GEM-linked Chinese-run HPAL nickel plants at Indonesia’s Morowali park have shut temporarily after a deadly landslide at a waste site. The biggest QMB unit may stay offline up to three months. The disruptions hit roughly 30% of local HPAL capacity as Indonesia increases environmental enforcement.

Indonesia is expanding nickel ore processing, including saprolite and limonite chains, to move toward higher-purity nickel and battery intermediates. However, EV demand is shifting from nickel-based chemistries to LFP, which has much lower pricing and rising market share. This combination suggests weaker near-term nickel battery demand versus expanding supply capacity. | Greenridge Exploration says it has earned a 100% interest in the Electra Nickel Project in Ontario, which is prospective for nickel-copper-cobalt (and PGEs). The article details project geology, past-producing grades, and exploration results, but provides no operational output or near-term supply changes affecting nickel or copper prices. | PTX Metals reports assay results from W2 step-out drilling, extending the CA1 zone with long intercepts of copper-nickel-PGE mineralization. Grades are highlighted using copper and nickel inputs into CuEq calculations. While supportive for future copper/nickel resource potential, it is mainly an exploration update with limited immediate market impact. | PTX Metals reports step-out drill results from its W2 Cu-Ni-PGE project, including 213.85m of mineralization and high-grade intervals with 0.90% Cu over 7.0m and 0.41% Ni over 0.5m. The findings expand the CA1 zone footprint, but do not imply near-term production changes. | Zambia is seeking global investors to fund a major expansion of copper production, aiming to more than triple output to 3 million tonnes by 2031. The US is mentioned as a potential partner amid efforts to diversify supply chains. Zambia’s copper performance missed last year’s target.

Indonesia’s government has cut coal and nickel production quotas to bolster prices, but miners and related industries complain about possible shortages, including power plant operators. The piece links the policy to a broader price jump after the Iran war, suggesting tighter nickel supply could raise near-term nickel prices. | SLAM Exploration said it acquired the Mount Raymond claim adjoining its Goodwin copper-nickel-cobalt project, expanding coverage within a 25 km belt in New Brunswick. Historical drilling reported chalcopyrite/pyrrhotite mineralization with grades up to 0.43% copper and 0.12% nickel, plus cobalt. It also reports completion of VTEM surveying. | Canada Nickel Company says the Crawford Project has received major federal and Ontario permitting boosts, enabling faster financing, engineering, and the aim to break ground before year-end. The CEO reiterates it will become the western world’s largest nickel sulphide operation and outlines additional deposits and electric arc furnace processing. | Macquarie says nickel prices could rise further this year as Indonesia, the top producer, limits production starts. The resulting supply shortage may deepen into a market deficit, tightening near-term availability and supporting higher nickel prices as the production limits feed through to output and supply.

A Guardian report describes nickel laterite mining on Palawan, Philippines, with communities alleging silt runoff harms fisheries, rice fields and coral. The mine (Ipilan) has faced protests, license challenges and cease-and-desist orders that were later overturned, keeping output uncertain and potentially constraining nickel supply. | Market Forces says Indonesia’s Harita Group removed specific figures on coal power capacity tied to electricity supply for its nickel smelting operations after an SGX-linked complaint involving OCBC. The scrutiny raises transparency and potential sustainability-breach concerns, increasing perceived compliance/financing risk for nickel producers. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. announced a non-brokered flow-through financing of up to $1,000,000 at $0.38/share to fund eligible Canadian exploration expenses. Proceeds will support further work at its Kenbridge Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Northwestern Ontario. The release is financing-focused, implying limited immediate impact on nickel/copper prices. | Tennessee has passed a bill allowing cash purchases to round to the nearest nickel as pennies fade after the U.S. Treasury suspended penny production due to cost. The state aims to reduce liability for lawful rounding, while noting Treasury expects rounding to boost nickel demand.

EV Nickel announced a maiden mineral resource estimate for the Gemini North Nickel Zone at its Shaw Dome project. The release provides nickel grades and contained nickel (including pit-constrained starter resources) and notes metallurgical recovery assumptions for a future open pit. It is a resource update, not an immediate supply disruption.

Lifezone Metals signed a 14-month exclusivity deal with Burundi to assess the Musongati nickel deposit, with planned finished nickel production using hydromet technology at the Kahama multi-metal facility in Tanzania. The facility is expected to generate nickel with copper and cobalt by-products, supporting regional beneficiation and logistics. | Glencore signed a binding five-year offtake agreement worth about $450 million for nickel output from Centaurus Metals’ Jaguar project in Brazil. The deal supports progress toward a final investment decision, improving visibility on contracted nickel supply and demand expectations, which can be mildly supportive for nickel prices.

Litchfield Minerals reports continued drilling momentum at its Oonagalabi project in Northern Territory, including completed diamond and RC holes, new drill pads, and pending assays. The company previously reported thick, stacked copper and zinc mineralization and plans additional RC holes if weather and rig access improve. | Centaurus and Glencore reportedly signed a nickel supply deal in Brazil. Such agreements can affect near-term nickel supply expectations and contract availability. In the absence of details on volumes, pricing, or disruptions, the likely near-term effect is modest bearish pressure on nickel as the market anticipates steadier supply.

First Atlantic Nickel announces discovery of a second large awaruite (Ni3Fe) target area (“Alloy Max”) at its Pipestone XL nickel-cobalt project, extending 7 km from the RPM zone. The zone is fully permitted and funded for drilling, supported by favorable DTR surface grades and geophysics, reinforcing district-scale nickel potential. | Copper prices have reversed sharply from January record highs as China’s manufacturing cools (PMI ~49), a ‘buyer strike’ emerges, and LME/SHFE inventories build. Goldman Sachs warns a mid-2026 copper surplus and a looming US refined-copper tariff decision could trigger further downside; aluminium and nickel show correlated weakness. | KoBold Metals began development of its Mingomba copper project in Zambia, moving into permitting with shaft sinking expected in 2027 and production in the early 2030s. The project is designed for 300,000 tonnes of annual copper. KoBold also conducts nickel exploration in Namibia, but effects appear long-dated.

SMM reports Indonesian nickel ore prices and premiums rising, supported by smelter restocking demand and pessimism around RKAB quota reductions, with Eid holidays expected to delay approvals. At the same time, high-grade nickel pig iron shows periodic retracement as stainless steel and futures prices weaken, limiting upside but also containing downside. | Ardea Resources’ Kalgoorlie nickel project was included in a Japan-US Critical Minerals joint fact sheet, backed by policy cooperation after a Trump-Japan summit. The company is progressing its nickel project DFS with Japanese consortium funding and potential EFA/EXIM support, boosting confidence in future nickel supply prospects for Western markets.

Researchers at Cornell report a nickel-on-carbon catalyst that enables high-performance alkaline fuel cells without precious metals like platinum. By improving nickel’s stability in alkaline media, the work could eventually expand fuel-cell applications using nickel. However, it does not signal near-term deployment, so short-term price impact is likely limited.

The US initiated a Section 301 investigation into Indonesia for alleged forced labor tied to the country’s expanding nickel industry, specifically products used for EV batteries. With forced-labor accusations and potential trade consequences, the probe increases near-term supply and export risk, which is typically bearish for nickel. | Indonesia’s energy minister said Indonesia could implement a “measured relaxation” of production plans for nickel (RKAB) and coal if prices remain high. Indonesia has already set tighter nickel ore quotas below demand, with quotas subject to revision, implying potential supply increases. | Indonesia’s energy ministry signaled it may “measuredly relax” coal and nickel production planning if global commodity prices remain high, though no change has been implemented yet. The statement frames this as maintaining supply-demand balance, after Indonesia set a much lower 2026 nickel quota versus 2025. | Philippine nickel producers are bracing for disruptions from a Middle East-related fuel shock, citing limited 15–30 day fuel buffers and higher costs. Fuel prices nearly doubled, adding about $3/wet MT to production costs. While higher nickel prices offset margins, deeper fuel rationing could reduce profitability and exports. | Short interest in the iShares Copper and Metals Mining ETF (ICOP) fell 55.2% in March, with the ETF trading down 4.8% on the day. The piece focuses on ETF positioning and trading metrics, without citing new fundamentals affecting copper, nickel, or aluminium markets directly.

Vale Base Metals reported 2025 increases in copper and nickel mineral reserves and resources and outlined 2026 exploration plans, including doubled drilling intensity in Brazil’s Carajás copper district. The update is supportive for long-term supply potential but contains no immediate production, export, or cost shock.

Reuters data referenced in the article indicate nickel prices increased as markets bet Indonesia may curb output growth, amid uncertainty over quotas and supply tightness. It also reports aluminium rising on supply concerns following Iranian strikes on major Middle Eastern aluminium facilities, alongside ongoing nickel and base-metal volatility. | Spot copper treatment charges in China have fallen to historic lows (about -70 USD/dmt), threatening smelter profitability. Smelters are still running high output as surging sulfuric acid prices offset TC losses. Geopolitical risks around Hormuz/Red Sea may keep acid prices high, supporting copper supply and near-term price tone. | Glenstar Minerals received BLM approval for a 13-hole reverse circulation drill program and 2,200 feet of road construction at its Wildhorse copper-rich, polymetallic project in Nevada. The company highlights prior anomalous nickel-copper mineralization and copper oxide/sulfide zones, but no new assay results are provided.
